english_verse: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ a, Seba Lɛgãtsole aayidza ɛdza ɛkɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɛtsã osi gɛlɛɛlɛ eto ye ɔmaĩ kamɛ ɛyatse Lɛgã Salomo nyansã fɔtsa katsa, ade mitɛ yĩ mido kĩ odi deĩ nɛ awĩ kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Salomo. Hũhũ Kpa Kabuki Kamuniki Kaya.

english_verse: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
translation_verse: yabi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Maamuniki nnaa yĩ kɔla kɔɛ a kamɛ.’ Foesũ ntɛ ɛyamɔ kĩ bɛyɔ koe kamɛ bɛlɔla koe kamɛ yededeede a,

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu kalɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ bati a, ɔma mɔ bawaɛ̃nyole yawo awã. Bɛdza nɛ kanya bɛwɛ kĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ ye.

english_verse: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
translation_verse: Foesũ bati a kamɛ odi bi ye kĩ, “Dã, ama mɔ awaɛ̃nyole amɛ dzɛ̃ nɛ kanya bawɛ kĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ nɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Owei nyɛ mma? Bawei nyɛ nwaɛ̃ amɛ?”

english_verse: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
translation_verse: Ade eyo awɛ ɛtsa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ɛlɛ kĩ, “Midã, ade mma mɔ nwaɛ̃ amɛ nɛ awĩ a!

english_verse: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɛna nɛ Nda nɛ ode kawɛ akũ ni, ade ye nyɛ nwaɛ̃nyole, mɔ nwaɛ̃tsole mɔ mma a.”

english_verse: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ bati kɔdabu a kĩ kɔtsɛna nɛ ye akũ a tɛ́nɛ kapɔ sũ ni, ewo edzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ ade kɔdabu a dza nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ a.

english_verse: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ɛtsa be fɔtsa pii nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Luwi a onyole odi naasã kamɔnɛ.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Babi a badi tɔ nɛ apibɔ akũ awã kĩ tite lɛpɔ́nɛ nɛ boe a anɛ. Babi a mɛ mɔatsɛ kitonɛkĩ tite a kamɛ tádzũ.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Babi a badi tɔ nɛ fu tuwĩ kamɛ ade kĩ bɛmɛ bade a tuwĩ a bo anɛ tɛmɛna be.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ abi bati a fɔlɛtsa nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ?”

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ oti a kĩ edeĩ fɔtsa ni, baakpa ye fudi bibu lɔkɔa fɔpɔ fubu, lɛmɛ oti a kĩ ɛlá lɛtsama ni, baatɛ foe kabii a kĩ edeĩ a malo nɛ ye awɛ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: Foesũ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a ya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, ‘Bati kĩĩ aanu bibuki binu lɛmɛ bɛláanu foe kayɔ. Ade baamɔ bibuki bɛmɔ́ lɛmɛ bɛláamɔ biyi ɔsũ.

english_verse: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
translation_verse: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ, Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ mɔ bati kpĩĩ pii wɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bamɔ lɛtsa a kĩ miamɔ a, lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛmɔ́ foe, ade bɛwɛ lɛmɛ kĩ banu lɛtsa a kĩ mianu a lɛmɛ bɛtánu foe. Yesu Nyɛ Lɛgbã Nɛ Kamɔnɛ a Odunɛ Akũ a Kayɔ.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
translation_verse: Babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ apibɔ akũ a nyɛ oti a kĩ ntɛ esila enu fɔlɛtsa a, yatɛ foe mɔatsɛ mɔ kawɔlɛ,

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message about the kingdom, but don't understand it. Then the evil one comes and snatches the message from their hearts.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yanu ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ fɔlɛtsa lɛmɛ yaánu foe kayɔ a, la ndɛ babi a kĩ biwula nɛ osi ɔɔwɛ̃ a. Oti Kpa a ayawãnɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bidu nɛ ye kamɛ a.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ fu tuwĩ kamɛ a nyɛ oti a kĩ yanu fɔlɛtsa a, lɛmɛ katinya kamɛ ɔhau mɔ akũ kana fɔtsa liti katomɛna anya fɔlɛtsa a akũ, foesũ fɔáwũ babi.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
translation_verse: Ade babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ tite wĩ akũ a nyɛ oti a kĩ yanu fɔlɛtsa a, kanu foe kayɔ lɛmɛ. Bawũ babi, badi ɔha, badi aduesia babɛbã lɛmɛ be taalɛ.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Tuwĩ Wuluwulu Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
translation_verse: Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã bɛbã kĩ, “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ oti odi kĩ ɛsã kamɔnɛ babi wĩ nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ.

english_verse: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɛnyɛ mɔa lidi, kĩ odi nyɛ odi wa tida a, ye okesĩnɛ nasã tuwĩ wuluwulu nɛ kamɔnɛ a kamɛ ɛdzakũ.

english_verse: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
translation_verse: Kĩ kamɔnɛ a bo anɛ kido kayɛlɛ awɛ a, tuwĩ a lɛmɛ nɛ́ tɛdza nɛ ke kamɛ.

english_verse: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
translation_verse: “Oti a basumunɛ ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bibi ye kĩ, ‘Boe ɔlate, nnɛ́ funitsã wĩ odu nɛ kɔba a kamɛ, mɔ ndia tuwĩ kpa to tɛyamɛ nɛ ke kamɛ?’

english_verse: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
translation_verse: “Ɔlate a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Okesĩnɛ odi lɛkɛna fukĩna.’ “Adeke bivia ye kĩ, ‘Awɛ kĩ bɔnaabɔlɛ tuwĩ a nɛ ke kamɛ?’

english_verse: “No!” he answered. “You might also pull up the wheat.
translation_verse: “Ebi be kĩ, ‘Oowo! Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ minaabɔlɛ tuwĩ a ni, míabɔlɛ kamɔnɛ a kadi mɔ te.

english_verse: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ kutse libi kabii flee nɛ kutse babi kamɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ libo anɛ ni, kɔapɔ lububu kaba futse flee, ade kɔvɔlabi ayakɛna fɔtsɔɛ nɛ koe tɛnanɛ kamɛ a.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Oyila Fɔtsa Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
translation_verse: Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã bɛbã kĩ, “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ kutse libi kabii kĩ oti odi nadu nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ.

translation_verse: Yesu bi bati kɔdabu a fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ, ade yaálɛ fɔlɛtsa fudima ntɛ nnɛ́ nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
translation_verse: Ɛkɛna fukĩĩ kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Maalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ, ade maanyɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla kito anɛ katinya kayɔkayi a ntsa a.” Yesu Nyɛ Lɛgbã Nɛ Tuwĩ Wuluwulu Akũ a Kayɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”

english_verse: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.

translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a aado ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛyaakpɔta be a kĩ bɛfɔ̃ bati kɛna tɔkpa mɔ be a kĩ bɛkɛna tɔkpa a flee bɛnɛmɛna nɛ Baguma lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Baata be bido nɛ fi luwotu kamɛ, ade awã baabe bɛba adɔ̃ a.

translation_verse: Baguma bade a kɔ̃ akũ aakpalɛ ndɛ suwa aye nɛ be Ɔlɛga lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ. Oti a kĩ edeĩ sɔtɔ ni, etse! Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Akũ Kana Fɔtsa Kɔlawa Akũ.

translation_verse: “Ode lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ akũ kana fɔtsa fudi kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla nɛ kɔba kamɛ. Oti odi nake foe, ɛdani ɛkɔla foe. Ɛmɔ́ kawɔlɛ paa, foesũ ɛnanɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ a emuniki ɛnaya kɔba a.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Litsabi Kĩ Latɛ Kɔya Akũ.

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ kɔyaatɔ ɔnyanɛ odi kĩ ɛtsɛ̃ yawɛ fulitsa babi wĩ.

translation_verse: Kĩ ɛnatsɛlamɔ lidi kĩ lideĩ kɔya paa a, ɛnanɔ fɔtsa a kĩ edeĩ a flee ɛkɔa ɛya fulitsa libi a. Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Asawu Kĩ Bɛta Akũ.

english_verse: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.

english_verse: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
translation_verse: Ade aye fɔ́ana nɛ katinya kawũna lɔkɔ a. Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a aayasa bati kpa bɛnyɛ nɛ bati wĩ kamɛ,

translation_verse: Foesũ ebi be kĩ, “Fukĩĩ atsa kĩ mbla ɔtsanɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdani ɛkɛna fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ la ndɛ ɔtɔlate odi kĩ yanyɛ fɔtsa vɔɛ̃ mɔ fɔkɔɛ kanya katomɛna ye fɔtsa kakɔlakɔ̃.” Bisĩ Yesu Nɛ Nazaret.

translation_verse: Yohanes fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nabɔɛ ɔnɔkpa a bɛnabila, ade bɛnabɔa Yesu amandiɛ a. Yesu Kpa Bati Mpim Elo Funitsã Bɛnya.

translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mikɔa foe mima yĩ nɛ awĩ.”

english_verse: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu tálɛ kadima. Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nake ye aduli bɛlɛ kĩ, “Blɔmɛna ye ɛdzakũ, kitonɛkĩ etomɔ boe mɔ fɔwɔla!”

english_verse: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ lɛtsa kĩ lawo nɛ oti onukpɛ̃ kamɛ a ado ye efĩĩ, mbom lɛtsa a kĩ lanɛ kato ye onukpɛ̃ kamɛ a, ado ye efĩĩ.”

english_verse: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
translation_verse: Adeke ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɛyabi ye kĩ, “Onyina kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɔlɛ a wo Farisifɔ a kalɔɛ?”

english_verse: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
translation_verse: Petro bi ye kĩ, “Nyɛ lɛgbã kĩĩ kayɔ katsa boe.”

english_verse: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mbɔɛɛ mi malo bati kĩ baánu fɔlɛtsa kayɔ mibuki mianɛ?

english_verse: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
translation_verse: Mitánu foe kayɔ kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lawo nɛ oti onukpɛ̃ kamɛ a kanaa ye lupu kamɛ, ke liti yanaanyɛ nɛ kɔbɔa kayo kamɛ?

english_verse: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔanɛ kato oti onukpɛ̃ a, ato ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ade foe ado oti efĩĩ a.

english_verse: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ oti ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ adzuni kpa, oti kakɔ, adɔpaa kabɔa, bayitsɔ̃ mɔ bapopo kawa katɛ̃, kaye, fɔvã adansiɛ kadi, mɔ bati buĩ kanyɛ anɛ kato.

english_verse: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ ado oti efĩĩ a, lɛmɛ kĩ ɔkɔa awɛ kĩ ɔtávɔlɛ anya ndɛ ayekĩ Farisifɔ atsa a, ládo oti efĩĩ.” Otsole Odi Katɛkado.

english_verse: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Baveli a kĩ bɛyɛla nɛ Israelfɔ kamɛ odi sɛkɛ̃ bido yĩ.”

english_verse: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
translation_verse: Ade bati kɔdabu baũ kpã abafã amɛ, banɛkunɛ, bamumu, mɔ basɔnɛ bɛbã pii bɛyamɛna nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ a. Bɛkɔa be biko nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ ade ɛtsa be a.

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa kayite a kĩ kifuã Tiro mɔ Sidon tɛmatɔ kpale a kamɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
translation_verse: Awã naa, Kanan otsole odi kĩ edzi nɛ kayite a na ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ mɔ fɔwɔla kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Dawid Obi, mɔ yĩ kɔnya! Yĩ obitsole lamɛna lubo kamɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ nɛ hũhũ kpa awɛ kamɛ!”

english_verse: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ otsole a yatɔ nɛ Yesu abɔa kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, bumɔ yĩ!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Fɔnɛ́nɛ kĩ bata babisɛ̃ funitsã biko bakpete.”

english_verse: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
translation_verse: Otsole a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, fudeĩ lɔmɔ kɔ̃, lɛmɛ bakpete a malo anya funitsã wuliwa a kĩ futo be ɔlate opunu akũ fɔtɔ a.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nu foe a, ɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “O, boe ɔma, nɔ katɛkado pɔnɛ! Lɛtsa a kĩ awɛ a lɛya lɛkpa nɔ.” Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ a obitsole a akũ de ɔsĩ. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee.

english_verse: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛtsã Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a abuĩ. Ede ɛnaa katɔbii kadi akũ ade ɛkɛna akũ edzi a.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a mɔ́ kĩ bamumu kalɛ kɔdzɛla, abafã amɛ kayidza katsã, bikĩ bɛnɛ lɛkpɛ̃ akũ dani fuyo, banɛkunɛ kamɔ fɔkɔ a, fɔkɛna be wãwã, ade bimu Israel Baguma a. Yesu Kpa Bati Mpim Ɛna Funitsã Bɛnya.

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Yesu vɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi be kĩ, “Bati kĩĩ akũ akɔ yĩ kɔnya, kitonɛkĩ bɛna nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ fuwi ɛlalɛ flee, lɛmɛ bɛlá lɛtsama kĩ banya. Ntáwɛ kĩ manyɛ be osi bɛnaa mɔ kɔlɛ, anɛ lɛnaasinya be nɛ osi.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye kĩ, “Nɛ ndia bɔ́ana funitsã nɛ kɔsa kamɛ nɛ awĩ kĩ fɔ́ato bati kɔdabu kĩĩ odu bɛnya?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Yesu via be kĩ, “Abodoo eni minɛɛ?” Bibi ye kĩ, “Abodoo evũkɔnɔ mɔ kɔfɔabi wuluwulu sukuloo deĩ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
translation_verse: Ade Yesu fɔ̃ bati kɔdabu a kɛna akũ bidzi nɛ tite a.

english_verse: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ ɛbɔɛ abodoo evũkɔnɔ mɔ kɔfɔabi a ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ a, ebulibuli ye mɔ koe ɛkɔa ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a, ade be malo bɛsɛ̃ bɛkpa bati a.

english_verse: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
translation_verse: Be flee bɛnya biwĩ. Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fila fuwuliwa a kĩ fute a, fuyi tɛtsɛtsɛ evũkɔnɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu nyɛ bati a osi ade edzi ɔkɔlɔ ɛnaa Magadan amantam kamɛ a.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Nde sũ mɔ miámɔ kĩ nnɛ́ abodoo fɔlɛtsa nlamɛna mi kabi? Midã kadã wĩ nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fuyilatsa akũ!”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu wo kakɔ a kĩ kifuã Kaisarea Filipi ɔmatɔ a, evia ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Owei bati alɛ kĩ Otidziwa Obi a anɛ?”

english_verse: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
translation_verse: Ade finyaa fɔmɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yanu foe kayɔ kĩ nnɛ́ fuyilatsa a kĩ bakɔa kado nɛ mawɛ kamɛ a akũ yadɛ be, mbom kadɛ ɛdɛ be nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fɔtsa katsa a akũ. Petro Nyɛ Yesu Ɛtsa Ndɛ Oti a Kĩ Bɛpau a.

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ suwa anaa katomukɔ̃ a, mialɛ kĩ, ‘Fɔkɔ aakpalɛ kunya, kitonɛkĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ yɔɔlɛ.’

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ɔnɔnɛ lɛmɛ mialɛ kĩ, ‘Nviã oni aako kitonɛkĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ yɔɔlɛ fɔkɔ lɛmɛ kɛna tũũ.’ Miayi anɛ kadã odetɔ́ kamɛ kapuli kalɛ ayekĩ fɔkɔ aana, lɛmɛ miápuli minyi nviã nsɛntsɛlɛni a kĩ yaaya a kayɔ.

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Badi alɛ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a anɛ. Babɛbã alɛ kĩ Eliya, ade badi lɛmɛ alɛ kĩ Yeremia ĩye Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa anɛ.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Kito luwi a, Yesu do ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kabi awɛ pefee kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ nnaa Yerusalem, nnaamɔ lubo paa nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a awɛ. Baakɔ yĩ, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ ni, Baguma aadzɛla yĩ ndzi nkpã.”

english_verse: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mite anɛ, midã kadã wĩ nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ fuyilatsa a akũ.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Ade bido kalɛ nɛ be nɛɛnɛɛ kamɛ kĩ, “Ayekĩ bɔtábɔɛ abodoo adima bɔlɛ sũ mɔ ɛlɛ foe.”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a aaya nɛ Ɔlɛga kukũkpalɛ kamɛ mɔ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ, lɔkɔa yaase odi nyɛ odi kɔtɔ nɛ ayekĩ edzi nkpã a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ, Petro, mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ nɔ dĩ kayɔ nyɛ de mɔ ɔsĩ a, ade de kĩĩ akũ maayi yĩ asɔli a. Wuda malo lɛláapuli ɛnya ye akũ da.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Yesu via be kĩ, “Adeke mi malo, owei mialɛ kĩ ye manɛ?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Ade Simon Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Kristo a, Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a Obi.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Simon, Yona obi, Baguma hila nɔ, kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ otidziwa odima lɛtsa nɔ foe, mbom Nda nɛ ode a lɛnyɛ foe ɛtsa nɔ.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Maakɔa ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a tɛsafi ndo nɔ nɛ awɛ. Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ osĩ nɛ tite akũ ni, baasĩ foe nɛ ode, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ ɔkpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, baakpa de osi nɛ ode.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Adeke Yesu si ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mbla kĩ bɛtabi odima kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo a. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Ye Lubokamɔ Mɔ Wuda Akũ.

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Petro kpã Yesu bɛnaadza nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃, ade edo ye anɛ kakã awɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Baguma esua foe osi. Fukĩnaa lɛláaya nɛ nɔ akũ da!”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Yesu dani ɛtsɛlamɔ Petro ebi ye kĩ, “Satana, dzakũ nɛ yĩ akũ! Ɔnyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lasua yĩ osi kitonɛkĩ nɔ kakɔna táto Baguma sɛkɛ̃ mbom futo otidziwa sɛkɛ̃.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Ntɛ odi wɛ kĩ yatomɔ yĩ ni, ɛtatsɛla ye mɔawɛ akũ lɔkɔa ɛbɔɛ ye asendua etomɔ yĩ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛyɛla ye nkpã nɛ yĩ sũ ni, yaana ye.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Ntɛ katinya kamɛ fɔtsa flee dani odi fude mɔ ɛyɛla ye nkpã ni, litsa nfasɔ yaana? Mbɔɛɛ litsedi deĩ kĩ oti aakɔa ɛvɛmɛna mɔ ye nkpã?

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ badi deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ nɛ awĩ kĩ bɛláaku kɛnaawo kĩ bɛmɔ́ Otidziwa Obi a kĩ ɛya ndɛ Lɛgã aye.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkadã ye a, Yesu akũ dani nɛ be anɛ kamɛ. Ye anɛ kamɛ kata ndɛ suwa aye, ade ye tɛtadiɛ lɛmɛ kɛna fitaa ndɛ ɔhaĩ aye a.

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Ade awã mɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu foe kayɔ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a akũ kɔdzɛla ɛkalɛ. Yesu Tsa Kanyolebi Kadi Kĩ Hũhũ Kpa Na Nɛ Ke Kamɛ.

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi evũ liti a, Yesu kpã Petro, Yakobo mɔ ɔwaɛ̃ Yohanes bide bɛnaa ɔtɔ geĩ odi kalete, awã kĩ be odi bɛnadzi.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Adeke fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a mɔ́ Mose mɔ Eliya kĩ bɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ Yesu a.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Foesũ Petro dzɛla odo ebi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, fɔnɛnɛ paa kĩ budeĩ nɛ awĩ! Ntɛ awɛ ni, maabɔa fɔpɛ ɛlalɛ nɛ awĩ, nɔ lide lɔmɔa, Mose lide lɔmɔa lɔkɔa Eliya lɛmɛ lide lɔmɔa.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a, luflututu fitaa ka lɛtsa be, ade odo nɛ́ eto de kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ Obi kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ a, yawo yĩ anɛ. Mitse ye!”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu odo a libe wo be paa, foesũ bɛka bɛtsa anɛ nɛ tite.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Yesu ya nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛkɔa awɛ ɛta be, ade ebi be kĩ, “Miyidza! Mitavila!”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛdzɛla anɛ nɛ ode, lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ odima nɛ awã kɛba Yesu.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Adeke fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via Yesu kĩ, “Nde sũ mbla batsanɛ a alɛ kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya, ɛyaalɔla lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa eyi.

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ Eliya ya kɔkɔɛ lɛmɛ bati támɔ ye biyi ɔsũ, foesũ bɛkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ bawɛ nɛ ye akũ. Ade ayea baakɛna Otidziwa Obi a lɛmɛ awɔda a.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a muniki bɛya nɛ bati kɔdabu a sɛkɛ̃ a, onyole odi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ɛyake aduli nɛ ye anɛ,

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, mɔ yĩ obinyole kɔnya! Odetɔ́ fɔsɔ deĩ nɛ ye akũ fɔamɔna ye lubo mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Fɔfɔ̃ yawɛ katɔ nɛ fi ĩye mui kamɛ.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: Nkpã ye mma nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛtsa ye.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “O mi nviã babi kpa kĩ milá katɛkado. Mbɔɛɛ maana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ daa? Lɔkɔ lɔɔdabe kamɛ maana ɔwɔlɛ nvɛlɛ mi nnaawo? Mikpã kanyolebi a mima yĩ nɛ awĩ!”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Adeke Yesu wũni nɛ hũhũ kpa a akũ mɔ ɛnɛ nɛ kanyolebi a kamɛ, ade kɛna akũ ɔsĩ nɛ lɔkɔ mɔapɛ a na kamɛ a.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kabɛla kamɛ bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ boe kɔ̃ bɔtápuli bɔblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a bɔnɛmɛna?”

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a flee yakpɔta nɛ Galilea a, Yesu bi be kĩ, “Baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ bati awɛ.

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: Baakɔ ye, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ a, Baguma aadzɛla ye edzi nkpã.” Nɛ foesũ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo ɔblɔ paa. Yesu Mɔ Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Se Asɔli Kɔla Ɔgɔlɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo Kapernaum a, bikĩ batɛ Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ a ya nɛ Petro sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Mi fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ a ase Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ?”

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, yase.” Kĩ Petro wo ɛnaa tɔtɔ kamɛ a, Yesu nya lɔtɔ evia ye kĩ, “Simon, litsa nyɛ nɔ adzuni? Baáde ase dutɛ ĩye tɔgɔlɛ kakpa katinya kamɛ fɔgã? Kade a bati ase mbɔɛɛ kĩ banɔvɔɛ̃?”

english_verse: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
translation_verse: Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Banɔvɔɛ̃ a.” Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ fɔatsa kĩ kade bati a wɛya.

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔáwɛ kĩ bɔafum bati kĩĩ, foesũ naata dalɛwa nɛ ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a kamɛ. Kafɔabi katɔnyade a kĩ aanyɛ a ni, daɛ ke onukpɛ̃, aamɔ sika ovoe kĩ saato bɔmɔ nɔ Asɔli Kɔla ɔgɔlɛ kase. Kɔa se kanaase be bɔmɔ nɔ tɔgɔlɛ.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ nɔ linebi aafɔ̃ ɔkɛna tɔkpa ni, bɔlɛ de kanyɛ kata kamuni! Fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ kĩ aana linebi mɔa odzi nkpã, kɛba kĩ aana banebi nviã lɛmɛ bɛta nɔ bido nɛ fi a kĩ fɔádĩ da a kamɛ a. Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Kuveli Kĩ Kɔyɛla Akũ.

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: Kɛtáfɛta kĩ amɔ nɔ ɔkɔba ɔsumunɛ kɔnya ndɛ ayekĩ mmɔ́ nɔ kɔnya a?’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Oti kĩ ɛbɛ̃ flee nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a nyɛ oti a kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ, ɛdani ɛkɛna ndɛ obisɛ̃ kĩĩ aye.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ ɛkpa obisɛ̃ kĩĩ odu nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, yĩ ɛkɛna ɛkpa. Takpã Odima Odo Nɛ Tɔkpa Kamɛ.

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ esĩ ɛtátse be malo ni, kɔa fɔlɛtsa a kama asɔli a, ade ntɛ ɛtátse asɔli bati a lɛmɛ ni, bu ye ndɛ ɔmamuni ĩye ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ aye. Yesu Kpa Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Otumi.

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: “Ntɛ oti odi aakpã babisɛ̃ kĩĩ, kĩ bɛtɛ yĩ bido a kamɛ ɔmɔa edo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ ni, fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa ye fɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛkɔa kɔwɔlɛ kpale bɛvɛla nɛ ye sɔmɔ bɛta bido nɛ mpo kamɛ ɛmɔa mui eku.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: “Foesũ ntɛ nɔ awɛ ĩye nɔ kɔbɔa aafɔ̃ ɔkɛna tɔkpa ni, tsua koe kata kamuni! Fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa nɔ kĩ aana nkpã mɔ awɛ mɔa ĩye kɔbɔa mɔa, kɛba kĩ aana akpɔ mɔ abɔa lɛmɛ bɛta nɔ bido nɛ fi a kĩ fɔádĩ da a kamɛ.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ awaɛ̃ fum nɔ ni, naa ye sɛkɛ̃ kanaanyɛ ye mfumsu katsa ye, lɛmɛ kɛna foe nɛ kabɛla kamɛ nɛ mimɔ ye odi kayite. Ntɛ enu foe kayɔ ɛkpa nɔ ni, ɔdani ɔtɛ awaɛ̃.

english_verse: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a ya kĩ yaatɛ bikĩ bɛyɛla a bɛna nkpã.

english_verse: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛmɔ́ koe ni, yamɔ kawɔlɛ paa nɛ kɔmɔa a akũ kaba be aduekrɔũ evedi a kĩ bɛtáyɛla a.

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Ade ayea mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a láwɛ kĩ babisɛ̃ kĩĩ kamɛ odima ɛyɛla a. Kɛna Ɔmɔa Mɔ Awaɛ̃ Kĩ Efum Nɔ.

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛtánu ye akũ kayɔ ni, kpã oti mɔa ĩye bati nviã kabumɔ nɔ akũ minaa ye sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a flee aanɛ fɔwa nɛ adansifɔ anɛ.

english_verse: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: “Foesũ mabi mi flee kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ mitákpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, bɛláakpa de osi nɛ ode, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míakpa osi nɛ tite akũ ni, baakpa de osi nɛ ode.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: “Mabi mi mbu kĩ ntɛ mi kamɛ bati nviã mikɛna ɔmɔa nɛ litsedi akũ nɛ tite akũ, mɔ mibɔa mpaɛ mivia de ni, Nda nɛ ode a aakɛna foe ɛkpa mi.

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ awã kĩ bati nviã ĩye be ɛlalɛ tsɛna nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, ndeĩ nɛ be kayite.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Ɔsumunɛ Kĩ Ɛtámɔ Ɔkɔba Kɔnya a Akũ.

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Petro yavia Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, ntɛ nwaɛ̃ dzɛmɛna tɔkpa kakɛna nɛ yĩ akũ ni, titi eni kɛnɛ kĩ nkɔa nte ye? Titi evũkɔnɔ mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
translation_verse: Kĩ esila eyi kayɔ a, bɛkpã be kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ edzimɔ ye sika mpim mpim kɔtɔ bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: Ɔsumunɛ a lɛlá sika kĩ yaase kɔtɔ a, foesũ lɛgã a kpa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛnɔ ye mɔawɛ a ndɛ ɔsate, ye ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ babi, mɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ a bɛkɔa bise kɔtɔ a.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: “Ɔsumunɛ a ke aduli nɛ lɛgã a anɛ eke ye aduli kĩ, ‘Na ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa yĩ. Maase nɔ se flee.’

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Ye akũ kɔ lɛgã a kɔnya foesũ ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ ade ɛkɔa kɔtɔ a kĩ edzimɛna a flee ete ye a.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: “Ɔkɔba a tɔ nɛ ye abɔa kamɛ eke ye aduli ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Na ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa yĩ! Maase nɔ kɔtɔ a!’

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ ɛtátunɔ, mbom ɛfɔ̃ bido ye nɛ kɔla kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ yaase kɔtɔ a.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Kĩ basumunɛ bɛbã a mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a, fuwo be kalɔɛ ade bɛnabi lɛgã a lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a flee a.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: “Foesũ lɛgã a vɛ ɔsumunɛ a ɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi ye kĩ, ‘Nɔ ɔsumunɛ kpa! Nkɔa kɔtɔ beblebee a kĩ odzimɔ yĩ a nte nɔ nɛ kĩ oke yĩ aduli sũ.

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Ade lɛgã a na kalɔkpa mɔ ɔsĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnado ye nɛ kɔla kĩ bɛmɔna ye lubo kɛnaawo kĩ ese kɔtɔ a flee.”

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Yesu wũna kĩ, “Ade aye Nda nɛ ode a aakɛna mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtákɔa ete ɔwaɛ̃ etomɛna ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu lɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ eyua a, ɛdzakũ nɛ Galilea ɛnaa Yudea amantam nɛ Yordan ɔwɔ liti a.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kayɔkanyɛ futefute deĩ kĩ foe sũ banyole lɛláapuli bɛkɔã batsɔ̃. Badi bɛma be basaɛ, babɛbã bati lɛkɛna be basaɛ, ade badi lɛmɛ lákɔã batsɔ̃ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a sũ. Oti a kĩ yaapuli ɛtɛ fɔtsa katsa kĩĩ ni, ɛtɛ foe.” Yesu Hila Kubisɛ̃ɛ̃.

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
translation_verse: Bati kɔdabu pii tomɔ ye bɛnaa ade ɛtsa be fɔtsa nɛ awã a.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Farisifɔ a badi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade bɛwɛ kĩ basɔ ye bɛdã, foesũ bivia ye kĩ, “Boe mbla kpa onyole osi kĩ esĩ ɔtsɔ̃ nɛ kalɛ nyɛ kalɛ kĩ yawɛ a akũ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mitákã nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ nɛ kayɔkayi a, otsole mɔ onyole Ɔbɔanɛ bɔa?

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: Ade Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ onyole aafɔnɛ ɔlɛga mɔ ɔma eyi ɛkɔa akũ ɛmɛna mɔ ɔtsɔ̃, lɔkɔa be nviã a baakɛna oti mɔa.’

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Foesũ bibuki bɛnyɛ́ bati nviã mbom bɛkɛna oti mɔa. Nɛ foesũ otidziwa ɛtasɛ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma boomu a kamɛ.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Ade Farisifɔ a via ye kĩ, “Mɔ nde sũ Mose kpa osi kĩ onyole ɛkpa ye ɔtsɔ̃ kasĩ ovoli lɔkɔa ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ade mi atɔ ɔsĩ sũ mɔ Mose kpa mi osi kĩ misĩ mi batsɔ̃ a, lɛmɛ fɔtána lɔmɔ nɛ kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa mabi mi kĩ ntɛ onyole odi sĩ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ nnɛ́ kanɛ ɛnɛ ye liti sũ, mɔ ɛnakɔã otsole bɛbã ni, ɛbɔa adɔpaa.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nnɛ́ odi nyɛ odi aapuli enu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kayɔ, mbɔɛntɛ bikĩ Baguma kpa be foe a.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Bati badi kpã babisɛ̃ bɛma Yesu kĩ ete awɛ nɛ be akũ, ɛbɔa mpaɛ ɛkpa be, lɛmɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wũni nɛ be akũ.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Luwi a onyole odi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ɛvia ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, lɛtsa wĩ lɔɔdabe kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna mɔ maana daa nkpã a?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Oti a via ye kĩ, “Tɛbla tɛwɔdabe?” Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Takɔ oti, tabɔa adɔpaa, taye, tadi adansiɛ klumu,

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Adeke Yesu te awɛ nɛ be akũ ehila be, ade ɛdzakũ nɛ awã a. Opopo Osikani a.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ avia yĩ kalɛ nɛ fɔtsa wĩ kakɛna akũ? Oti mɔapɛ nyɛ owĩ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ awɛ kĩ awo nɛ nkpã kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, kɛna nɛ tɛbla a akũ.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ babisɛ̃ a bɛya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mitasua be osi, kitonɛkĩ ade bikĩĩ bamɛ odu lá ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati pii kĩ bɛnyɛ batɔnyade finyaa a, aakɛna liti bade, ade liti bade a aakɛna batɔnyade a.”

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: bu aga mɔ ama, kawɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ akũ kalɛ a.”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Ade opopo a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nkɛna nɛ tɛbla kĩĩ flee akũ. Litsa libuki lisĩ kĩ nkɛna?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ awɛ kĩ akpɔla ni, naa kanaanɔ nɔ akũ fɔtsa flee, kakɔa sika a kate ahiãfɔ, lɔkɔa aana akũ kana fɔtsa nɛ ode. Ke liti muniki kayaatomɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Kĩ opopo a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ɛdzakũ mɔ ayɔlɔhɔ, kitonɛkĩ edeĩ akũ fɔtsa beblebee.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Adeke Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ fɔ́ana ɔsĩ paa fɔkpa osikani kĩ yaawo nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mbuki mabi mi kĩ fɔadã bɔɛɛ fɔkpa ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kanɛ mɔ lɔpɔ kĩ yaatsã ɔnɔbɔ̃ ɔkɛ kamɛ ɛnɛ́ kɛba kĩ osikani aawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu kalɛ kĩĩ a, akũ dzuli be, ade bivia kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ owei aapuli ɛna katɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu dã be kɛnanaana ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Otidziwa lɛláapuli ɛkɛna fukĩĩ, lɛmɛ Baguma kɔ̃ apuli kakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
translation_verse: Ade Petro dzɛla odo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Dã kĩ bɔfɔnɛ boe fɔtsa flee awɛ bɔya butomɔ nɔ, litsa koũ aanya boe fude?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ ntɛ Otidziwa Obi a kɛna akũ edzi nɛ ye lɛgãkpo kpalɛwa a akũ nɛ katinya vɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ni, mi bati ewua-nviã a kĩ mitomɔ yĩ a lɛmɛ míadzi nɛ fɔgãkpo ewua-nviã akũ midzɛmɔ Israel futete ewua-nviã a.

english_verse: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
translation_verse: Ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛfɔnɛ fɔla, bawaɛ̃nyole, ĩye bawaɛ̃tsole, balɛga ĩye bama ĩye babi, ĩye fɔba biyi nɛ yĩ sũ ni, Baguma aakpa ye foe titi ɔha mɔ nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ okuani odi kĩ eyidza mɔ ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu ɛnabɔɛ apaafɔ badi kĩ bɛyaakɛna adzuma bɛkpa ye nɛ ye waĩ kɔba kamɛ.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Bɛmɔ apaafɔ a bɛnya kɔya, etunɔ kĩ yaase be sika ovoe sɔmɔa sɔmɔa kĩ fɔnyɛ luwi mɔa kɔtɔkase, ade edo be nɛ ye waĩ kɔba a kamɛ a.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: “Kĩ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ evedi bɔa a, onyole a buki ɛnɛ ɛnaa kɔya ade ɛnamɔ banyole badi kĩ bɛdzɛ̃, kĩ bɛlá adzuma kɛnawa adima.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Foesũ ebi be kĩ, ‘Mi malo minaakɛna adzuma nɛ yĩ waĩ kɔba kamɛ, maase mi kɔtɔ kĩ kɛfɛta a.’

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Ade bɛnaa kɔba a. “Ebuki ɛnɛ nɛ tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã mɔ olobe tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ ɛnakɛna ndɛ fɔtɔnyade a.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Kĩ tɔdɔũ elo anaa kabɔakɔ̃ a, ɛnaa kɔya ɛnamɔ banyole bɛbã kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ pupulidza. Evia be kĩ, ‘Nde sũ midzɛ̃ nɛ awĩ luwi a flee kĩ miákɛna lɛtsama?’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Odima tábɔɛ boe kĩ bɔkɛna adzuma bɔkpa ye.’ “Foesũ ebi be kĩ, ‘Ntɛ aye ni, mi malo minaakɛna adzuma nɛ yĩ kɔba a kamɛ.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: “Kĩ olobe wo ɔtɔ a, kɔba ɔlate a bi ye adzuma akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a kĩ, ‘Vɛ adzuma bakɛnanɛ a kase be kɔtɔ, kayi kayɔ katomɛna bikĩ bɛya nkaɛ a akũ kanaawũna nɛ batɔnyade a akũ.’

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: “Bikĩ biyi adzuma kayɔ nɛ olobe tɔdɔũ elo kamɛ a kamɛ odi nyɛ odi tɛ sika ovoe sɔmɔa sɔmɔa.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtɛ be sika a biyua a, bɛna kalɔkpa bɛkakɛna hũhũhũhũ nɛ ɔlate a akũ.

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: Ade bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Bati a kĩ ɔbɔɛ nkaɛ a kɛna adzuma dɔũhuilɛ mɔapɛ, ade boe kɔ̃ bɔdza nɛ suwa kpaa kamɛ luwi a flee bɔkɛna adzuma a, lɛmɛ ose boe flee kɔtɔ tsɛtsɛɛ!’

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: “Kɔba ɔlate a kpa be kamɛ ɔmɔa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔkɔba, dã ntásisi nɔ. Nnɛ́ sika ovoe sɔmɔa akũ bɔnya kɔya?

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Kɔa nɔ kɔtɔkase kanaa kɔla. Yĩ lɛwɛ kĩ mase bati nkaɛ kĩĩ kɔtɔ ndɛ ayekĩ nse nɔ a.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: Ndeĩ osi kĩ makɛna yĩ sika akũ adzuma ndɛ ayekĩ mawɛ. Mbɔɛɛ ayekĩ nkɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ a sũ anɛ abɛɛlɛ nɔ?’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Ade Yesu wũna kĩ, “Foesũ bati a kĩ bɛkpa liti a, aadani batɔnyade, ade bikĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ a aadani batide a.” Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Lɛlalɛde Nɛ Ye Wuda Akũ.

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Yesu fɔ̃ ɛdza ɛvɛ be kĩ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃. Evia be kĩ, “Litsa miawɛ kĩ nkɛna nkpa mi?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Be akũ kɔ Yesu kɔnya, ade ɛkɔa awɛ ɛta be banebi a. Deedimɔapɛ be anɛ binya bɛmɔ́ fɔkɔ ade bitomɔ ye a.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Bati kɔdabu a wũni nɛ be akũ kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fɔwɔla kabɔa, lɛmɛ bɛbɔa fɔwɔla a kɔɔba bɛba bɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Dawid Obi, mɔ́ boe kɔnya ni!”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu yɛlɛ yanaa Yerusalem a, ɛvɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃,

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: bɛkɔa ye bido bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a nɛ awɛ kĩ bidi ye nsekuo. Baabo ye, ke liti baabɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ a, Baguma aadzɛla ye edzi nkpã.” Obima Odi Yakɔa Ye Kawɛ Eyi Nɛ Yesu Anɛ.

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Adeke Zebedeo ɔtsɔ̃ kpã ye babinyole nviã ɛyamɛna nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃, ɛyake aduli nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ evia kabumɛna a.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Yesu via ye kĩ, “Litsa awɛ?” Otsole a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kpa yĩ bɔhɛ kĩ aafɔ̃ yĩ babinyole nviã kĩĩ kamɛ ɔmɔa lidzi nɛ nɔ sɔmɔna, ɔyade a lɛmɛ lidzi nɛ nɔ sɛba, nɛ nɔ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Minyína lɛtsa a kĩ de milamɛna kavia a. Míapuli mimɔa lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu a kĩ maamɔa a?” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, bɔ́apuli.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ míapuli mimɔa foe, lɛmɛ nlá osi kĩ manyɛ oti a kĩ ye aadzi nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna ĩye yĩ sɛba. Fudzikɔ̃ a na nyɛ bikĩ Nda manɛ foe eyi a fude.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nkaɛ ewua a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, bɛna kalɔkpa nɛ bawaɛ̃ nviã a akũ.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu vɛ be flee ɛkpɔta ade ebi be kĩ, “Minyina kĩ fɔgã a kĩ fɔanya amamufɔ akũ a deĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ be akũ, ade be banɔkɔɛ̃ lɛmɛ deĩ otumi baũ a nɛ be akũ a.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kɛtánɛ kĩ fɔana lɔmɔ nɛ mi kamɛ. Ntɛ mi kamɛ odi awɛ kĩ yanya ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛnya mi ɔsumunɛ.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ mi kamɛ odi awɛ kĩ yanya ɔtɔnyade ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛnya mi ɔsumunɛ,

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: ndɛ Otidziwa Obi a kĩ ɛtáya kĩ bisum ye, mbom kĩ yaasum ɛkɔa ye nkpã ɛkpa ɛnyɛ bati pii.” Yesu Tsa Banɛkunɛ Nviã.

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nɛ́ bito Yeriko ɔmatɔ a kamɛ banaa ni, bati kɔdabu baũ tomɔ be.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Banɛkunɛ nviã kĩ bidzi nɛ osi ɔɔwɛ̃ nu kĩ Yesu yaatɛ̃, foesũ biyi fɔwɔla kabɔa kayɔ kĩ, “Lɛgã Dawid Obi, mɔ́ boe kɔnya!”

english_verse: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, fɔ̃ boe anɛ ebinya.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: “Nde mikɔna nɛ kalɛ kĩĩ akũ? Onyole odi deĩ babinyole nviã. Ɛnaa ɔnɔkɔɛ̃de a sɛkɛ̃ ebi ye kĩ, ‘Yĩ obi, naa yĩ waĩ kɔba kamɛ nviã kanaakɛna adzuma kakpa yĩ.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ kĩ kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ a mɔ́ obi a, bibi akũ kĩ, ‘Ade fɔtsa ɔnyanɛ a nɛ awĩ a. Miya bɔkɔ ye, ye adzapadiɛ a ɛdani boe ade.’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi via mi kadi ni, milɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate a sĩ be,’ lɔkɔa yaafɔnɛ be awɛ ɛkpa mi lɛtalɛta.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Bɛkɛna fukĩĩ ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ lɛ eyi a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: “Bi Zion ɔmatɔ a kĩ, ‘Dã kĩ nɔ lɛgã aya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃! Ɛdɔla ye akũ, edzi nɛ aflimu mɔ obi akũ yaya.’ ”

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a naa bɛnakɛna ndɛ ayekĩ Yesu bi be kĩ bɛkɛna a.

english_verse: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Bɛkpã aflimu mɔ obi a bɛyamɛna, bɛkɔa be tilima bɛtɛɛtɛɛ nɛ be akũ ade Yesu de edzi a.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu wo nɛ Yerusalem ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, ɔmatɔ a flee bɔa obũ, ade bati via kĩ, “Owei nɛ awĩ a?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a mɔ́ awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna a, mɔ ayekĩ babisɛ̃ kabɔa fɔwɔla nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ kĩ, “Mitu osee nɛ Lɛgã Dawid Obinyole a akũ!” a ni, bɛna kalɔkpa paa.

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: Kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Farisifɔ a nu Yesu fɔgbã kĩĩ a, bɛmɔ́ kĩ be akũ fɔlɛtsa ɛkalɛ,

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: ade ebi be kĩ, “Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Baavɛ Yĩ Asɔli Kɔla a kĩ mpaɛ kabɔakɔ̃’ lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ mikɔa koe mikɛna bayekpanɛ kabɛlakɔ̃!”

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
translation_verse: Ntɛ mideĩ katɛkado ni, lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míavia nɛ mpaɛbɔ kamɛ ni, míana foe.” Bati Via Yesu Awã Kĩ Etomɛna Ye Otumi.

english_verse: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
translation_verse: Foesũ bivia Yesu nɛ kalɔkpa kamɛ kĩ, “Anu lɛtsa a kĩ bɛlamɛna kalɛ a?” Ade Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, manu. Mbɔɛɛ mitákã tɔ̃ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, ‘Babisɛ̃ mɔ bakɔkɔa onukpɛ̃ kamɛ kamu kĩ kɛkpɔla anɛ kato’ a?”

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ be, ɛnɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ade ɛnaa Betania ɛnawa nɛ awã a. Yesu Dumu Kutse Kudi Kĩ Kɔtáwũ Babi.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Maavia mi kalɛ mɔapɛ ade ntɛ mikpa yĩ ke mbuayɛ ni, maabi mi otumi a kĩ nnɛɛ nkɔa makɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Nɛ kadekanyina ɔnɔnɛ a, kĩ emuniki yanaa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, kɔlɛ kɔ ye.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ ntɛ mitɛ mido, mɔ mitátɛ atsindze ni, míapuli mikɛna lɛtsa a kĩ nkɛna kutse kĩĩ. Nnɛ́ fukĩĩ odi malo, míapuli mibi ɔtɔ kĩĩ kĩ, ‘Bɔlɛ kanaatɔ nɛ mpo kamɛ,’ ade yaabɔlɛ a.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛkpa Yesu mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bunyína.” Ade Yesu lɛmɛ bi be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ yĩ malo nláabi mi awã kĩ ntomɛna otumi nnɛɛ makɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Babinyole Nviã Badi Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitse lɛgbã bɛbã lidi lɛmɛ. Kɔba ɔnɛnɛ odi nɛ́ waĩ kɔba, ade edzi kɔbã emini koe a. Ɛnyɛ lɛkɛ ɛkpa waĩ mui a kakã kako, ade eke kɔla geĩ kĩ oti aana nɛ awã ɛkadã kɔba a akũ. Ke liti ɛkɔa kɔba a edo nɛ waĩ kɔba banɛnɛ awɛ ɛdzakũ ɛnaa osi akũ.

english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu muniki ɛya nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ ɛkatsa fɔtsa a, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Otumi ɔdabe ɔnɛɛ akɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ? Owei lɛkpa nɔ osi a?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Owei sɛkɛ̃ Yohanes tɛ otumi etomɛna mɔ ɛwɔla bati mui? Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ɛtɛ ye etomɛna mbɔɛɛ kĩ batidziwa sɛkɛ̃?” Ade bido foe kamɛ kasa awɛ nɛ be kamɛ kĩ, “Litsa bɔ́alɛ? Ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ, ‘Eto Baguma sɛkɛ̃’ ni, yaavia boe kĩ, ‘Mɔ nde sũ mitátɛ Yohanes mido?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ, ‘Eto bati sɛkɛ̃’ ni, bɔavila lɛtsa a kĩ bati a aakɛna, kitonɛkĩ be flee bɛtɛ bido kĩ Yohanes nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: “Ye obi a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Nláanaa,’ lɛmɛ ke liti a, fuwo ye kalɔɛ ade ɛnaa kɔba a.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: “Ɔlɛga a naa obi ɔyade a sɛkɛ̃ ebi ye kalɛ mɔapɛ a. Ɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Awoo, papa maanaa,’ lɛmɛ ɛtánaa.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: “Bati nviã kĩĩ kamɛ ɔɔdabe lɛkɛna nɛ ɔlɛga a kawɛ akũ?” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃de a.” Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ adɔpaafɔ nya mi lɔtɔ bɛnaa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a ya kĩ yatsa mi osi wĩ a kĩ míatsã midzi nkpã a, mitátɛ ye mido. Mbom tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ adɔpaafɔ kɔ̃ tɛ ye bido. Ade titɔ kĩ mimɔ́ fukĩĩ malo ni, ke liti a mitádani adzuni mitɛ ye mido.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Waĩ Kɔba Akũ Badãmɛnanɛ a Akũ.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Kĩ kutse babi a kana a wo ɔtɔ a, edo ye basumunɛ nɛ waĩ kɔba banɛnɛ a sɛkɛ̃ kĩ bɛnaatɛ ye kanyakɔ̃ bɛma ye.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: “Kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ a pi ye basumunɛ a, bibo ɔmɔa, bɛkɔ ɔbɛbã ade bɛtalɛ ɔlalɛde a boe a.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kɔba ɔlate a buki edo basumunɛ bɛbã kĩ bɛpɔnɛ bɛbɛ̃ batɔnyade a, lɛmɛ kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ a kɛna be lɛtsa mɔapɛ a.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe kawũna kamɛ a, edo ye obi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃, ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Matɛ kado kĩ baanyɔã yĩ obi.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Foesũ bipi ye bɛvɛlɛ bɛnɛmɛna nɛ kɔba a kamɛ, ade bɛkɔ ye a.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: Yesu via kĩ, “Finyaa, ntɛ waĩ kɔba ɔlate a ya ni, litsa yaakɛna kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ kĩĩ a?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, yaawɔla bati kpa a na, lɔkɔa ɛkɔa kɔba a edo nɛ kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ bɛbã awɛ, bikĩ baakɔa ye kanyakɔ̃ bɛma ye nɛ fudutsa kakpɔta lɔkɔ kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitákã tɔ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ a? ‘De a kĩ fɔla bakenɛ sĩ a, ade de lɛdani kɔla akũ de titiliwu a. Boe Ɔlate a lɛkɛna fukĩĩ. Fɔkɛna boe wãwã!’ ”

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ebu kĩ, “Foesũ mabi mi kĩ, baatɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a nɛ mi awɛ bɛkɔa bɛkpa kade a kĩ kaawũ babi wĩ a.

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaanɛ ɛtɔ nɛ de kĩĩ akũ ni, yaaveli gblɛgblɛgblɛ, lɛmɛ owula kĩ ye akũ laaatɔ ni, laabubuli ye kpɛtɛkpɛtɛkpɛtɛ.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: foesũ bɛwɛ kĩ bapi ye. Lɛmɛ bivila bati kɔdabu a, kitonɛkĩ bati a bu Yesu ndɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ aye.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Yesu wũna kĩ, “Bati beblebee bavɛ, lɛmɛ be sukuloomɛ bapau.” Bivia Yesu Kalɛ Nɛ Abãã Ɔgɔlɛ Kase Akũ.

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ a nanaa adzina ayekĩ banaĩ Yesu bɛtsãmɛna fɔlɛtsa kavia kamɛ, bɛna ye nɛ ye kɔdzɛla kalɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, Mose bi boe kĩ ntɛ onyole odi kĩ ɛtáma ku ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɔwaɛ̃nyole ɛkɔã okunawa a lɔkɔa bɛma bido ye lɔlɔ.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ be nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ kĩ,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ fukĩĩ. Lɛgã lidi te opunu ɛkpa obinyole nɛ ye obolitsɔ̃ kapi luwi akũ.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ edo ye basumunɛ kĩ bɛnaavɛ bati a kĩ ɛkpa kanyi a kĩ bɛya nɛ kanyakɔ̃, bisĩ kaya.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: “Foesũ ebuki edo basumunɛ bɛbã nɛ bikĩ bɛvɛ a sɛkɛ̃ mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, ‘Bɛtɔ yĩ funitsã a biyua, bɛkɔ yĩ banantsuenyole mɔ banantsue babi dɔliwa, bɛmanɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa biyua. Miya nɛ obolitsɔ̃ luwi kanyakɔ̃ a!’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ bati a kĩ bɛvɛ a tákpeti ye kavɛ a, mbom bɛdzakũ bɛnaa be tudzuma kamɛ. Oti mɔa naa ye kɔba, ɔbɛbã naa kɔyaatɔ,

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: bati nkaɛ a pili basumunɛ a bɛkɛna be tɛnya ade bɛkɔ be a.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Lɛgã a na kalɔkpa paa, foesũ edo ye sodzafɔ bɛnakɔ awudifɔ a, bipila be ɔmatɔ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: “Ke liti a, ɛvɛ ye basumunɛ ebi be kĩ, ‘Biyua obolitsɔ̃ kapi funitsã a katɔ, lɛmɛ bati a kĩ nvɛ a táfɛta obu a kĩ nkpa be a.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Foesũ finyaa minaa ɔmatɔ a tɔblɔnu kpale akũ minaavɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ míamɔ ɛya nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: “Kĩ lɛgã a nawo kĩ yadã bikĩ bɛvɛ a, ɛmɔ́ onyole odi kĩ ɛtádo obolitsɔ̃ kapi atadiɛ nɛ awã.

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Foesũ bi boe nɔ adzuni. Boe mbla kpa osi kĩ bute tɔgɔlɛ bɔkpa Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃, mbɔɛɛ kĩ ɛtákpa osi?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Ade lɛtsa mɔapɛ a lɛya nɛ ɔyade a, mɔ ɔlalɛde a, kɛnawo ovũkɔnɔde a akũ a.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Foesũ bido be fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ Herodes a kamɛ badi nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɛlɛ kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bunyina kĩ odeĩ ɔnɔkɔali, atsa Baguma osi a nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ, ade ɔtsɛ́la odima akũ, kitonɛkĩ aádã oti anɛ.

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Mikɔa sika a kĩ miakɔa kase ɔgɔlɛ a mitsa yĩ.” Bɛkɔa sika a bɛma ye,

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ fude fɔanɛ.” Foesũ Yesu bi be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ mikɔa lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ lide mikpa ye, ade mikɔa lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ Baguma lide lɛmɛ mikpa Baguma.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Kĩ binu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a fɔkɛna be saa, ade bɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ bɛdzakũ a. Bivia Yesu Fɔlɛtsa Nɛ Banɔkpa Kayidza Kadzi Nkpã Akũ.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe kawũna kamɛ a, otsole a lɛmɛ ku.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Ade tɛbla nviã kĩĩ akũ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔtsa katsa a flee dzɛ̃ a.” Owei Nyɛ Kristo a?

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Luwi mɔapɛ a, Zadukifɔ kĩ balɛ kĩ banɔkpa kayidza lɛláa kamɛ badi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bɛyavia ye

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mikɛna mfumsu, kitonɛkĩ minyína Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a fɔlɛtsa mɔ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ a.

english_verse: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nɛ banɔkpa kayidza kadzi nkpã kamɛ ni, bɛláakɔã batsɔ̃ ĩye bidzi fɔnyɔ, mbom baana ndɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ aye.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ mitákã tɔ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ nɛ banɔkpa kayidza kadzi nkpã akũ a? Ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: ‘Yĩ nyɛ Abraham Baguma, mɔ Isak Baguma mɔ Yakob Baguma.’ Nnɛ́ banɔkpa Baguma yanɛ, mbom batidziwa ade yanɛ.”

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, akũ dzuli be nɛ ye fɔtsa katsa a akũ. Yesu Tsa Mbla a Kĩ Ɛbɛ̃ Flefle a.

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Kĩ Farisifɔ a nu kĩ Zadukifɔ a tápuli bɛdza nɛ Yesu anɛ a, bɛnakpɔta nɛ kakɔ mɔa,

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, Mose mbla aádabe bɛ̃ nɛ te kamɛ?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Ade mbla kĩĩ nyɛ atɔnyade mɔ ekĩ ɛbɛ̃ flefle a.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Mbla ayade a la ndɛ ye, ‘Wɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ mɔawɛ akũ kalɛ.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Kĩ Farisifɔ a yakpɔta a, Yesu via be kĩ,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘Boe Ɔlate bi yĩ Ɔlate a kĩ, “Dzi nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna kɛnaawo kĩ maakɔa nɔ bakesĩnɛ ndo nɛ nɔ abɔa kayɔ.” ’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Litsa nyɛ mi adzuni nɛ Kristo akũ? Owei obinyole yanɛ?” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Lɛgã Dawid kasinu kamɛ ɔwa yanɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Ade Yesu via be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ nde sũ Dawid tsã Hũhũ kamɛ ɛvɛ ye kĩ, ‘Ɔlate’? Kitonɛkĩ Dawid lɛ kĩ,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Ntɛ Dawid vɛ Kristo kĩ Ɔlate a ni, nde Kristo aapuli ɛnya Dawid obinyole?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Be kamɛ odima tápuli ɛkpa Yesu mbuayɛ, ade kito luwi a kanaa a, odima tádo ɔwɔlɛ ebuki evia ye kalɛ kadima.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yadɛla ye akũ nɛ ode ni, baakɛna ye nɛ kayɔ, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ ni, baadɛla ye nɛ ode. Yesu Kɔ́ Mbla Batsanɛ Mɔ Farisifɔ Kɔtɔ.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Dã, baafɔnɛ nɔ tɔtɔ awɛ biyi tɛdza luto.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ nɛ mi kamɛ ni, ɛnya mi ɔsumunɛ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem, nɔ a kĩ ɔkɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ, ade ɔnɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kĩ Baguma do nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ a boe a! Titi eni nwɛ kufɛ kĩ makpɔta nɔ babi ndɛ ayekĩ ɔkɔkɔ bima akpɔta ye babi kado nɛ fupoki kayɔ, lɛmɛ ɔtátunɔ!

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ odi tani afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a ni, ɛtani ye mɔ futetsa a kĩ futeĩ nɛ atɛnyɛ a akũ a flee.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu bi bati kɔdabu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fɔlɛtsa kĩ,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ a bɛkpa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ banyɛ Mose mbla a kayɔ.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Foesũ mikɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ bibi mi kĩ mikɛna a mipi foe midomɔ akũ. Lɛmɛ mitadã be fɔkɛlatsa mikɛna, kitonɛkĩ baákɛna nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ balɛ a akũ.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: “Bisuli fɔtsa mɔ ɔlɔmɛ kĩ fudeĩ ɔsĩ mɔ kalɔ bɛlɔla bati, lɛmɛ be mɔawɛ a kɔ̃ baáwɛ kĩ bayo lawobi malo bibumɛna bati foe kalɔ.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: Bakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa ayekĩ bati bɛmɔ be kɛkɛ. Bakɔa fumakɛ kĩ fɔsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa bite kabɔa nɛ nnyɛ mɔ akpɔ. Ade bakɛna be tɛtadiɛ wulaa atɔ tɛsa, tɛvanɛ mɔ mpɛsɛmpɛsɛ gɛlɛɛlɛgɛlɛɛlɛ a!

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Bawɛ kĩ badzi nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ nɛ kanyakɔ̃, mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Bawɛ kĩ bati bɛwɔ̃na be mɔ obu nɛ kɔyaatɔ, bɛvɛ be kĩ Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ mɔ obu.

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: “Kɛtánɛ kĩ bavɛ mi kɔ̃ kĩ Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, kitonɛkĩ Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ mɔapɛ mideĩ, ade mi flee minyɛ bawaɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Ade ayea mitavɛ odima nɛ tite akũ kĩ mi Ɔlɛga, kitonɛkĩ Ɔlɛga mɔapɛ mideĩ nɛ ode.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Mitafɔ̃ odima lɛvɛ mi kĩ Ɔkpãnɛ malo, kitonɛkĩ ɔkpãnɛ mɔapɛ mideĩ, kĩ ɛnyɛ Kristo.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ bɔbɔɛ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Miatɛ bakunawa tɔtɔ nɛ be awɛ ade miabɔa mpaɛ gɛlɛɛlɛ kakɔa tsa akũ a. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ míana sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ mɔ ɔsĩ sɛba!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: “Makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ bɔbɔɛ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Mitsã mpo akũ minyina minaa fude fude kĩ miawɛ oti mɔapɛ ɛdani Yudani, lɛmɛ ntɛ midani ye a, miakɛna ye titi nviã ɛkpɔla ɛkpa fi luwotu kamɛ kanaa ndɛ mi aye!

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Makpa mi banɛkunɛ a kĩ bakpã bati a bɔbɔɛ! Mialɛ kĩ, ‘Ntɛ odi tani Asɔli Kɔla ni, fɔnyɛ́ lɛtsama, lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛtani sika ngbã nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, fubo a pi ye.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Banɛkunɛ bakɔasia! Fɔɔdabe bɛ̃ kɔya? Sika ngbã a mbɔɛɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kĩ kɔfɔ̃ sika a akũ kpalɛ a?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Miatsa fɔtsa lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Ntɛ odi tani Baguma afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a ni, fɔnyɛ́ lɛtsama, lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛtani afɔliɛ fɔtsa a nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a akũ kɔ̃ ni, ɛlɔ fubo.’
