english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Banɛkunɛ mianɛ nuwĩ! Fɔɔdabe bɛ̃ kɔya? Futetsa a mbɔɛɛ kĩ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kĩ ye lɛkpalɛ futetsa a akũ a?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ odi tani Asɔli Kɔla a ni, ɛtani koe mɔ Baguma a kĩ edzi nɛ koe kamɛ a.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ odi tani ode ni, ɛtani Baguma lɛgãkpo mɔ Baguma a kĩ edzi nɛ de akũ a.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ mianɛ! Miakpa Baguma tuwĩ kĩ bakɔa kado funitsã ɔlɛ̃ te kamɛ kasɛ̃ katsu kawuade a pɛpɛɛpɛ, lɛmɛ mifɔnɛ mbla fɔtsa katsa sĩwa, kĩ fɔnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛ nɛ osi kpĩĩ, kɔnyakamɔ mɔ ɔnɔkɔali kadi awɛ. Ade fukĩĩ kufɛ kɛfɛta kĩ miakɛna lɔkɔa mikɛna fɔbɛbã a mibu a.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Banɛkunɛ bati bakpãnɛ! Miasɔni kɔyabi kanyɛ nɛ bavɛ kamɛ, lɛmɛ miamɛna ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kanɛ mɔ lɔpɔ!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Miavɔlɛ mi kuyei mɔ titsensi liti yededeede, lɛmɛ foe kamɛ yi mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ mikɔa kɔbɛ̃ mɔ anɛngbã mitɛ a.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Nɔ Farisini ɔnɛkunɛ, nya lɔtɔ kavɔlɛ kayei a kamɛ, lɔkɔa ke liti lɛmɛ aakpalɛ!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Mila ndɛ funikɛ kĩ bikputa mɔ tite ovoe kĩ fɔalɛ kadã, lɛmɛ foe kamɛ yi mɔ banɔkpa fɔtsɔ̃ mɔ sukɛna vɔ̃wa.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Ade ayea miatsa akũ nɛ bati anɛ ndɛ bati wĩ, lɛmɛ mi kamɛ kɔ̃ yi mɔ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa kakɛna mɔ tɔkpa. Yesu Lɛ Mbla Batsanɛ Mɔ Farisifɔ Sɔtɔ Kavɛlɛ Eyi.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ mɔ Farisifɔ! Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Mike funi mɔ kalɛ mikpa Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a ade mihɛhɛ bikĩ bidzi nkpã wĩ a anɛ kate fɔtsa a.

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: Ade mialɛ kĩ, ‘Ntɛ budzi nkpã nɛ boe bawanyɔ lɔkɔ a ni, kufɛ bɔtákɛna ɔmɔa mɔ be bɔkɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ mikɔa midi adansiɛ nɛ mi akũ kĩ minyɛ bati a kĩ bɛkɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a bawa!

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: “Mi bati kĩ bɛla ndɛ bɔɛ babi! Kakɔna mikɔna kĩ míapuli milo, kĩ bɛláakɔ mi kɔtɔ bɛkpa fi luwotu kamɛ kanaa?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ nɛ wuda kĩĩ flee akũ aaya nɛ mi nviã babi akũ! Yesu Ɔdɔ Kĩ Ɛna Ɛkpa Yerusalem.

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Mabi nɔ kĩ kito finyaa kanaa ni, ɔláabuki ɔmɔ́ yĩ kɛnaawo kĩ aalɛ kĩ, ‘Baguma hila oti a kĩ yaya nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ a.’ ”

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nɛ́ nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ yanaa a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ kĩ batsa ye ayekĩ bike kɔla a flee a.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Midã fukĩĩ flee kadã wĩ. Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ bɛláatina de lidima nɛ lɔkɔba akũ kĩ bɛláadu bimuni.” Yesu Bi Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Kĩ Baana Ɔhau Mɔ Liti Katomɛna.

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati pii aaya nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Kristo a.’ Baakɛ bati pii.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Míanu kawũ kanɔ mɔ kawũ akũ fɔlɛtsa mɔ lugo. Lɛmɛ mitafɔ̃ fɔtsa kĩĩ lɛbɔlɛ mi tɔwɔlɛ. Kɛnɛ kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ, lɛmɛ fɔátsa kĩ katinya kamɛ kawũna a wo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nɛ edzi nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛla bɔɛɛ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe fɔtsa kĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ. Bi boe lɛtsa a kĩ laanya nɔ kaya mɔ katinya kamɛ kawũna a nsɛntsɛlɛni.”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Ɔmaĩ aanɔ mɔ akũ, ade fɔgã kanyakɔ̃ aayidza bɛnɔ mɔ fɔgã kanyakɔ̃ a. Kɔlɛ aana, ade tite aakuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ nɛ fɔkɔ pii a.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔbɔɛ makpa bamatsɔ̃ mɔ bikĩ babi amɛmɛ nɛ awɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ ayekĩ odima lɛláakɛ mi.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ flee la ndɛ ɔmatsɔ̃ sɛmɛ kapi kawo a kayɔkayi aye.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: “Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, baanyɛ mi bɛkpa kĩ bɛkɛna mi awɔda, bɛkɔ mi malo. Batidziwa flee aakesĩ mi nɛ yĩ sũ.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Bati pii aamuniki nɛ be katɛkado liti. Baanyɛ be akũ akũ abɔa, bikesĩ akũ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Fɔvã onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ aanɛ bɛkpã bati pii bɛyɛla osi.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: “Foesũ ntɛ odi bi mi kĩ, ‘Dã, Kristo deĩ nɛ awĩ,’ ĩye ‘Amɔ ye nɛ akĩĩ’ ni, mitatɛ foe mido.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Mibɔa mpaɛ mikpa Baguma kĩ mi kalo lɔkɔ lɛtana nɛ osili lɔkɔ ĩye Kawɛya Luwi akũ!

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: “Ntɛ mimɔ́ Fɔtsa mɔ libe kĩ fɔakɔ Ɔmaĩ, kĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Daniel lɛ foe akũ fɔlɛtsa eyi a kĩ fɔdzɛ̃ nɛ kakɔ kpalɛwa a, nɔ ovoli kĩĩ ɔkãnɛ, nu foe kayɔ dzununuunu,

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: lɔkɔa bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yudea, bilo bɛnaa tɔtɔ a akũ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Ade ayea oti a kĩ edeĩ nɛ kɔba lɛmɛ, ɛtamuniki ɛya kĩ yanaabɔɛ ye atadiɛ.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Ntɛ Baguma ɛtatinya lubokamɔ fuwi a na akũ kɔkɔɛ ni, kufɛ otidziwa odima lɛláana nkpã. Lɛmɛ nɛ bati a kĩ ɛpau eyi a sũ ni, Baguma aatinya fuwi a akũ.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ mimɔ́ fɔtsa a kĩ nlɛ flee a ni, minyi kĩ katinya kawũna a lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ fuyua.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a kaya aana ndɛ ayekĩ fɔya nɛ Noa lɔkɔ a.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Odima linyína luwi ĩye dɔũhuilɛ a kĩ fɔ́aya nɛ foe kamɛ. Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ĩye Obi mɔawɛ a malo linyína, mbɔɛntɛ Ɔlɛga odi keĩ nyina.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi a kĩ fɔnya mui kayili nɛ tite akũ lɔtɔ kamɛ a, bati kanya bɛkamɔa, bɛkakɔã batsɔ̃, bɛkakɔã banyole kɛnawo luwi a kĩ Noa wo nɛ adaka ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ,

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: lɛmɛ bɛtányi lɛtsa a kĩ lɛkaya kɛnawo kĩ mui kayili a yalɔ be flee ɛnamɛna. Ade aye lɛmɛ Otidziwa Obi a kaya aana a.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, banyole nviã aakakɛna adzuma nɛ kɔba, baabɔɛ ɔmɔa bɛnamɛna, bɛfɔnɛ ɔmɔa awɛ biyi.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Batsole nviã aakawɔlɛ onuvoe nɛ kɔwɔlɛ akũ, baabɔɛ ɔmɔa bɛfɔnɛ ɔyade a biyi.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: “Foesũ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ minyína luwi a kĩ de akũ mi Ɔlate a aaya.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kanyi tɔtɔ ɔlate nyina lɔkɔ a kĩ oyekpanɛ aawo nɛ ye akũ ni, kufɛ esi lipe kĩ ɛtákpa oyekpanɛ a osi ewo nɛ ye kɔla kamɛ.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ mi malo mimanɛ akũ miyi, kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a aaya nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ miádã ye anɛ a akũ. Ɔsumunɛ Kĩ Edi Ɔnɔkɔali Mɔ Ɔsumunɛ Kĩ Ɛtádi Ɔnɔkɔali.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛnyɛ ɔsumunɛ kpa ni, yaabi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Yĩ ɔlate a lɛláaya mɔatsɛ!’

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “Foesũ owei nyɛ ɔsumunɛ ɔnɔkɔalini mɔ kadi onyinɛ? Ade ye nyɛ oti a kĩ ye ɔlate kɔa ɛdzɛla nɛ ye basumunɛ bɛbã onukpɛ̃ kĩ ɛkpa be funitsã nɛ lɔkɔ wĩ akũ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Bihila ɔsumunɛ a kĩ ye Ɔlate aato osi akũ ɛyaamɔ kĩ ɛlamɛna adzuma a kĩ ɛkpa ye a kakɛna!

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali mabi mi kĩ, ɔlate a aakɔa ye ɛdzɛla nɛ ye fɔtsa flee onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: mɔ edo ye bakɔba basumunɛ a kabo awɛ, yanya kamɔa mɔ bavɛ bawuganɛ ni,

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: ye ɔlate a aamuniki ɛya nɛ luwi a kĩ ɛtáte anɛ mɔ lɔkɔ kĩ enyína akũ.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Ye ɔlate a aablɔmɛna ye, ɛvɛlɛ ye sɔtɔ ɛfɔ̃ ɛna tsɛfã mɔ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ. Ade awã yaabe ɛba adɔ̃ a.

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Kĩ badzimbifɔ a bɔɛ be tɛsɛdza a, bɛtábɔɛ kugua bibu.

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Anyansãfɔ a kɔ̃ ko kugua nɛ titontolowa kamɛ bɛlɛ bibu mɔ be tɛsɛdza a.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: “Nɛ lɛnyɛ kayite a, binu fɔwɔla kĩ, ‘Amɔ obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a nɛ awĩ tsoo! Minɛ miyaatsɛla mɔ ye!’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: “Ade bayitsɔ̃ ewua a yidza bɛtɛna be tɛsɛdza a.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Badzimbifɔ a bi anyansãfɔ a kĩ, ‘Mikpa boe mi kugua a kudi, kitonɛkĩ boe tɛsɛdza lamɔ kadĩ.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ anyansãfɔ a kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Oowo, kukĩ bɔnɛɛ a lɛláato bɔmɔ mi. Minaa bikĩ banɔ a sɛkɛ̃ minaaya mi kude.’

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: “Obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Oowo! Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, ĩnyína mi!’ ”

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ewũna kĩ, “Nɛ foesũ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ minyína luwi ĩye dɔũhuilɛ a. Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Basumunɛ Ɛlalɛ a Akũ.

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: “Nɛ lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ liti a, be ɔlate a muniki ɛya, ade ɛkɛna akunta mɔ be a.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: kufɛ kakɔa akɔa sika a ɔnaado nɛ sika kakɔlakɔ̃ ayekĩ ntɛ mmuniki ĩya, maatɛ se mɔ se akũ kute a.

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: “Ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ. Onyole odi manɛ akũ kĩ yanaa osi akũ. Ɛvɛ ye basumunɛ ɛkpɔta ade ɛkɔa ye fɔtsa kĩ edeĩ a edo nɛ be awɛ a.

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: Ɔsumunɛ a kĩ ɛtɛ sika mpim elo a, nadani awɛ nɛ se akũ ɛna mpim elo nfasɔ ete nɛ se akũ.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Ye ɔlate a bi ye kĩ, ‘Mbo, ɔsumunɛ wĩ mɔ ɔnɔkɔalini! Odi ɔnɔkɔali nɛ fɔtsa kabii akũ, foesũ maakɔa nɔ ndzɛla nɛ fɔtsa pii onukpɛ̃. Yaawo nɛ yĩ kawɔlɛkamɔ kamɛ!’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Ye ɔlate bi ye kĩ, ‘Mbo, ɔsumunɛ wĩ mɔ ɔnɔkɔalini! Odi ɔnɔkɔali nɛ fɔtsa kabii akũ foesũ maakɔa nɔ ndzɛla nɛ fɔtsa pii onukpɛ̃. Yaawo nɛ yĩ kawɔlɛkamɔ kamɛ!’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ nvila, ade nnakɔa nɔ sika a nnabila nɛ tite kamɛ a. Amɔ nɔ fɔtsa nɛ awĩ.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: “Ye ɔlate kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Nɔ ɔsumunɛ kpa mɔ ɔnyɔnɛpo! Ntɛ onyina kĩ mana fɔtsa nɛ awã kĩ ntádu foe, ade makpɔta fɔtsa nɛ awã kĩ ntásã foe a ni,

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: “ ‘Mitɛ sika a nɛ ye awɛ, mikɔa se mikpa oti a kĩ edeĩ sika mpim ewua a.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edeĩ fɔtsa ni, baakɔa fɔtsa pii bibu ye, ade yaana foe fublusu a. Lɛmɛ oti a kĩ ɛlá lɛtsama ni, foe kabii a kĩ edeĩ a malo baatɛ foe nɛ ye awɛ.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Ɔsumunɛ kĩ ye akũ lɛlá nfasɔ kĩĩ lɛnɛ́nɛ ɛkpa lɛtsama kakɛna. Foesũ mita ye mimuni nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ. Ade awã yaana ebe ɛba adɔ̃ a.’ Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Bati Kadzɛmɛna Nkaɛ a Akũ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ Otidziwa Obi a to ode ɛya nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ kamɛ, kĩ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ flee a tomɔ ye a, yaadzi nɛ ye lɛgãkpo a akũ.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: Ade baakpɔta katinya kamɛ bati flee nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ a. Adeke, yaasɛ̃ be nɛ kutsu nviã kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ baveli ɔkpãnɛ asɛ̃ baveli mɔ kutukpabi kamɛ a.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Yaakɔa bati wĩ a ɛdzala nɛ ye sɔmɔna, lɔkɔa bati kpa a anna nɛ ye sɛba.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: “Lɛgã a aabi bati nɛ ye sɔmɔna a kĩ, ‘Miya, mi a kĩ Nda hila a. Miyaawo nɛ lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a kĩ bɛmanɛ biyi bɛkpa mi a kamɛ, kito anɛ kĩ bɛbɔa katinya kamɛ a,

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ kĩ kɔlɛ kɔ yĩ a, mikpa yĩ litsedi ĩnya. Kĩ ɔmɔado kɔ̃ yĩ a, mikpa yĩ mui mmɔa, ade kĩ ĩnyɛ ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ a, mitɛ yĩ nɛ mi tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ndzi ɔdzɔ a, mido yĩ atadiɛ, kĩ masɔ a midã yĩ akũ, ade kĩ nwaa kɔla a, miyasɛla yĩ.’

english_verse: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
translation_verse: “Ade bati wĩ a aavia ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, luwei bɔmɔ́ nɔ kĩ kɔlɛ kɔ nɔ mɔ bɔkpa nɔ funitsã, ĩye kĩ ɔmɔado kɔ nɔ mɔ bɔkpa nɔ mui ɔmɔa?

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Luwei bɔmɔ́ nɔ ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ mɔ bɔtɛ nɔ nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ, ĩye luwei ɔtsã ɔdzɔ mɔ bɔkpa nɔ atadiɛ odo?

english_verse: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Luwei ɔsɔ mɔ bɔdã nɔ akũ, ĩye luwei ɔwa kɔla mɔ bɔyasɛla nɔ?’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: “Lɛgã a aakpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ mikɛna mikpa nwaɛ̃ amɛ babisɛ̃ kĩĩ kĩ bɛlá obu a kamɛ odi ni, yĩ mikɛna foe mikpa!’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: “Adeke mɔ Lɛgã a aabi bikĩ nɛ ye sɛba a kĩ, ‘Minɛ liti nɛ yĩ akũ, mi bati kĩ Baguma dumu! Mitɔ minaa fi kĩ fɔádĩ da a kĩ bɛmanɛ bɛkpa Abonsam mɔ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ!

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Kɔlɛ kɔ yĩ lɛmɛ mitákpa yĩ ĩnya, ɔmɔado kɔ yĩ lɛmɛ mitákpa yĩ mui mmɔa.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: Ĩnya ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ lɛmɛ mitátɛ yĩ nɛ mi tɔtɔ kamɛ. Ndzi ɔdzɔ lɛmɛ mitádo yĩ atadiɛ. Nsɔ mitádã yĩ akũ, ade nwa kɔla lɛmɛ mitáyaasɛla yĩ a.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: “Ade be malo baakpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, luwei tɔ̃ kɔlɛ kɔ nɔ, ĩye ɔmɔado kɔnɔ, ĩye ɔnya ɔnɔvɔɛ̃, ĩye odzi ɔdzɔ, ĩye ɔsɔ, ĩye kĩ ɔwa kɔla mɔ bɔtákɛna lɛtsama bɔkpa nɔ?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: “Lɛgã a aakpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ mitákɛna mikpa nwaɛ̃ amɛ babisɛ̃ kĩ bɛlá obu kĩĩ kamɛ odi ni, yĩ mitákɛna foe mikpa.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: “Foesũ baanamɔ bati kĩĩ lubokamɔ a kĩ lɛlá kawũna a kamɛ, lɛmɛ bati wĩ a aanaa nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a kamɛ.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Minyina kĩ kite fuwi nviã kĩ banya Wuda Katɛ̃ luwi a. Nɛ fuwi a na kamɛ, baanyɛ Otidziwa Obi a bɛkpa kĩ bɛbɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, emuniki ɛyabi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Tida miwaa miawɛya? Midã, lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ kĩ baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ kɔbɛ̃ awɛ.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: Bɛkɛna nhihiɛ kĩ baapi Yesu nɛ kabɛla kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛkɔ ye.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ kĩ, “Kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔakɛna foe nɛ fuwi kanya a kamɛ, ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, bati a aado ɔtutũ nɛ ɔmaĩ a kamɛ.” Bɛtsɛla Yesu Kugua Mɔ Ɔlɛ̃ Nɛ Betania.

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: Baapuli bɛnɔ koe mɔ kɔya baũ lɔkɔa bɛkɔa se bite ahiãfɔ!”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mɔ́ fukĩĩ a, bɛna kalɔkpa ade bivia kĩ, “Nde sũ yabula kugua a kade lɔmɔ?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛsã kɔlɛkanyɛ ɔnɔ biyua a, bɛnɛ bɛnaa Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ. Yesu Lɛ Eyi Kĩ Petro Aasĩ Kĩ Enyína Ye.

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: Kito lɔkɔ a naa, ɛkawɛ osi wĩ a kĩ yaatsã ɛnyɛ Yesu ɛkpa. Yesu Mɔ Ye Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ a Nya Wuda Katɛ̃ Funitsã a.

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a, Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ mi kamɛ ɔmɔa aanyɛ yĩ ɛkpa.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Nɛ Abodoo Kĩ Ɛtáyila Luwi kanya lɔtɔnyade akũ a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Nɛ ndia awɛ kĩ bɔnaamanɛ bɔkpa nɔ Wuda Katɛ̃ funitsã a kanya?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkanya a, Yesu bɔɛ abodoo, ade kĩ ɛbɔa mpaɛ nɛ ye akũ a, ebulibuli ye ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a, ebi be kĩ, “Mitɛ minya, ade yĩ sukɛna nɛ awĩ a.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ nyɛ yĩ babɔanɛ kĩ bɛkɔa bisipi Baguma apam mɔ bati a. Fɔnyɛ yĩ babɔanɛ kĩ biko nɛ tite nɛ bati pii lɔlɔ kĩ Baguma ɛkɔa be tɔkpa ete be.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ nláabuki mmɔa waĩ kĩĩ kɛnaawo luwi a kĩ bɔmɔ mi bɔmɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ Nda Lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nviã lɛnyɛ, mi flee míalo mifɔnɛ yĩ, kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, ‘Baguma aakɔ baveli ɔkpãnɛ a ade baveli a aagba bɛsã a.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi Petro kĩ, “Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ lɛnyɛ kĩĩ fɔmɔ ɔkɔkɔ aabe ni, aalɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ onyína yĩ.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a naa kakɔ kadi kĩ bavɛ Getsemane. Ebi be kĩ, “Midzi nɛ awĩ nnaa lɔtɔ nnaabɔa mpaɛ tɔ̃.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: ade ebi be kĩ, “Ayɔlɔhɔ tɛ yĩ kamɛ flee ndɛ maku. Midzi nɛ awĩ bɔmɔ mi bɔkaadzɔlɛ fɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Petro bi Yesu kĩ, “Ntɛ be flee aalo bɛfɔnɛ nɔ malo ni, yĩ kɔ̃ nláalo nfɔnɛ nɔ da!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nláalo nfɔnɛ nɔ da ntɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔmɔ nɔ aaku malo!” Ade ayea fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nkaɛ a flee lɛ kalɛ mɔapɛ a. Yesu Bɔa Mpaɛ Nɛ Getsemane.

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛnaa lɛyade ɛnabɔa mpaɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nda, ntɛ fɔnyɛ kĩ lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu kĩĩ lɛláapuli ɛtɛ̃ nɛ yĩ akũ mbɔɛntɛ mmɔa ye ni, kekɔ̃ kɛna nɛ nɔ kawɛ akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛnaa lɔtɔ sukuloo a, ɛtsa anɛ nɛ tite ɛbɔa mpaɛ kĩ, “Nda, ntɛ fɔ́adã bɔɛɛ fɔ̃ lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu kĩĩ ɛtɛ̃ nɛ yĩ akũ! Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ nɛ ayekĩ mawɛ, mbom ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Miyidza, mifɔ̃ bɔnaa. Midã, oti a kĩ yaanyɛ yĩ ɛkpa a, fuã.” Yudafɔ Banɔkɔɛ̃ a Pi Yesu.

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Ɛkpã Petro mɔ Zebedeo babi nviã ebumɔ ye akũ. Nɛ awã naa, ayɔlɔhɔ pi ye edo ɛkatila bliblibli awɛ,

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Emuniki ɛya nɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a sɛkɛ̃ ɛyamɔ kĩ bɛwaa tida, ade ebi Petro kĩ, “Nde sũ mi ɛlalɛ a miápuli midzi midzɔlɛ dɔũhuilɛ mɔapɛ malo mibumɔ yĩ?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Mikaadzɔlɛ akũ, mikaabɔa mpaɛ ayekĩ miláawo nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ. Hũhũ a awɛ lɛmɛ sukɛna a kɔ̃ kɛna kpɛtɛɛ.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ be ɛnabɔa mpaɛ lɛlade, ɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔapɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔkɔba, wɛ sɔwa nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɔyaakɛna a akũ.” Adeke bati a fuã bipi Yesu, bitimtim ye kɔɔba a.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu buki ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a, Yuda kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa a yawo awã. Bati kɔdabu baũ a kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ do a tomɔ ye, bɛnɛɛ fɔpamɛ mɔ futsekpa nɛ awɛ.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ ye ɛkpa a, kpa be nsɛntsɛlɛni kĩ, “Oti a kĩ ye onukpɛ̃ maadzɛmɛ a, ade ye nɛ awã a. Mipi ye.”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Yuda yɛlɛ tĩĩ ɛnaa Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, lukudɔ litomɔ nɔ,” ade ɛdzɛmɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bitomɔ Yesu a kamɛ ɔmɔa vɛlɛ kɔpã ɛnyɛ nɛ sɛpapo kamɛ, etsua Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a ɔsumunɛ sɔtɔ emuni.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Kɔa nɔ kɔpã a kado nɛ koe kadokɔ̃, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛbɔɛ kɔpã nɛ oti akũ ni, kɔpã onukpɛ̃ yaatɔ.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Mɔ ye onukpɛ̃ katɔ pɛ, Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a yɛlɛ ye atadiɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Onyole kĩĩ lɛ fubusuotsa! Bɔtábuki busĩ adansifɔ badima! Minu fubusuotsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a!

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu tábinya onukpɛ̃. Ade Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a buki ebi ye kĩ, “Ntani Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a kĩ, bi boe ntɛ nɔ nyɛ Kristo, Baguma Obi a.”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Kĩ eyidza ɛtsã onyukpɛ̃ yanɛ nɛ kanya a, katsolebi ɔsumunɛ bɛbã mɔ ye, ade ebi bikĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kĩ, “Onyole kĩĩ lɛmɛ na nɛ Yesu Galileani a sɛkɛ̃ tɔ̃.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bipi Yesu a, kɔa ye bɛnamɛna Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee Kaiafa sɛkɛ̃, awã kĩ mbla batsanɛ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a natsɛna.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Petro tomɔ be nɛ liti kɔlaa ɛnawo nɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a tɔtɔ kamɛ. Ewo ɛnadzi kĩ yadã fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔya a kawũna.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Fɔlɛtsa Badzɛnɛ a flee wɛ kĩ bama fɔvã bite nɛ Yesu akũ ayekĩ baakɔ ye.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bati pii nɛ bɛyama fɔvã nɛ ye akũ malo ni, bɛtámɔ lɛtsama nɛ ye akũ. Nɛ ke kawũna kamɛ a, bati nviã nɛ bɛyadza,

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Onyole kĩĩ lɛ kĩ, ‘Maapuli nveli Asɔli Kɔla a, mbuki nke koe nɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ liti.’ ”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ade aye ɔlɛ a. Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ kito finyaa kanaa ni, míamɔ Otidziwa Obi a kĩ edzi nɛ Baguma Kpale sɔmɔna, ade yaya nɛ ode luflututu akũ a!”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him

translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu dĩ gba nɛ Galilea tite akũ flee. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee Fɔsɔ.

english_verse: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
translation_verse: Anɛ kɔkɔɛ a mɔ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya tsɛlɛ eyi kĩ Baguma bi ye obi kĩ, “Maado yĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɛnya nɔ lɔtɔ, ɛyaalɔla osi eyi ɛkpa nɔ.

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, Yohanes do ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kanɛ mɔ lɔpɔ tɔlɔ tɛtadiɛ, ɛkɔa ɔyaɛ ovoli ɔwɛ̃ ɛbɔa kate. Ye funitsã nyɛ kɔsã mɔ koolo.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Ede lɛtsã kĩ, “Oti a kĩ yaaya nɛ yĩ liti a, ɛbɛ̃ yĩ kɔlaa kĩ ntáfɛta kĩ makɛna akũ nwũnya mpabua nɛ ye abɔa malo.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a nawo ɔmatɔ kĩ bavɛ Kapernaum. Nɛ Yudafɔ Kawɛya Luwi a kĩ litomɔ be awã kawo luwi a, Yesu naa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ɛnatsa fɔtsa.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ binu ye fɔtsa katsa a kɛna saa, kitonɛkĩ fɔvɛmɛna nɛ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a fude akũ, mbom ye ɛtsa fɔtsa mɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Owi tábuna mɔ Yesu to Nazaret ɔmatɔ nɛ Galilea tite akũ, ɛyatɛ Yohanes mui kawɔla amaniɛ a nɛ Yordan ɔwɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu sila ɛnɛ́ eto mui a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ binya ade Hũhũ a ka eko nɛ ye akũ ndɛ ayekĩ mblɔduma aka a.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Oti odi kĩ yasɔ fɔsɔ ngbã kĩ bati avila ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃. Ɛkɛna akũ ade eke Yesu aduli kĩ ebumɔ ye a. Onyole a bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ awɛ ni, aapuli ɔfɔ̃ fɔsɔ kĩĩ lɛwɔ nɛ yĩ akũ.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛkalɛ nɛ awã kĩ, “Lɔkɔ wĩ a wo ɔtɔ ade Baguma lɛgãkanya lɔkɔ a fuã. Mimuniki nɛ mi tɔkpa liti lɔkɔa mitɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a mido!”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ be a, ɛvɛ be deedimɔapɛ kĩ bitomɔ ye. Be malo biyidza lɛtalɛta, bɛfɔnɛ be ɔlɛga Zebedeo mɔ ye adzuma bakɛnanɛ a odi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, ade bitomɔ Yesu a. Yesu Blɔmɛna Hũhũ Kpa Etomɛna Oti Odi Kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu wo nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, ade epi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ ɛdzɛla ye kĩ eyidza. Deedimɔapɛ fɔsɔ a dɔ ye, ade eyidza esum be a.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a, bibi ye kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi lamɛna nɔ kawɛ!”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, onyole odi kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ na nɛ be katsɛnakɔ̃ a,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: ade ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Yesu Nazaretni, litsa deĩ nɛ bɔmɔ nɔ kayite? Ɔya kĩ ayaawɔla boe mbɔɛɛ? Ĩnyina oti a kĩ anɛ. Nɔ nyɛ Baguma Oti Kpalɛwa Kĩ Bido a.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Yesu wũni nɛ hũhũ a akũ kĩ, “Sua onukpɛ̃, kanɛ nɛ oti a kamɛ.”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Hũhũ a fufulinya oti a mɔ ɔsĩ, ɛfɔ̃ ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ade ɛnɛ nɛ ye kamɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna bati a flee kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã saa, ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Litsa nɛ awĩ a? Ei! Fɔtsa katsa vɔɛ̃ ɔdabe nɛ awĩ a? Oti kĩĩ deĩ kɔbɛ̃ yawũni nɛ hũhũ kpa akũ ade yatse ye ɔlɛdo a!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bɔnaa tukula bɛbã kĩ tifuã a lɛmɛ kamɛ. Kɛfɛta kĩ mabi be malo Baguma fɔlɛtsa, kitonɛkĩ ade foesũ ĩya nɛ katinya kamɛ a.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Ye fɔlɛtsa kɔ Yesu kɔnya, foesũ eyo awɛ ɛta ye. Ebi ye kĩ, “Mawɛ lɔmɔ. Nɔ akũ ɛkpalɛ.”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ, Yakobo mɔ Yohanes dzakũ nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade bɛnaa Simon mɔ Andrea tɔtɔ kamɛ a.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo awã a, badi bi Yesu kĩ Simon nyina waa yasɔ, ye akũ bo hlihlihli.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Kĩ suwa tomu a, bati kpã basɔnɛ pii mɔ bikĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ be kamɛ a bɛyamɛna kĩ Yesu ɛtsa be.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Bati nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ yabɔa obũ nɛ onyukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Yesu tsa bati pii kĩ basɔ fɔsɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ, ade ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa etomɛna bati pii lɛmɛ kamɛ a. Yesu tákpa hũhũ a osi bɛlɛ kɔdzɛla kitonɛkĩ binyina ye. Yesu Lɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a Nɛ Galilea Tite Akũ.

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Odenyinawi a, Yesu yidza nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ, ɛnɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛnaa awã kĩ oti odima táfuã, ɛnabɔa mpaɛ.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Simon mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a nɛ bɛtsɛ̃ bawɛ Yesu.

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛnamini nɛ Galilea flee. Ɛtsã ɛkalɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃, ade ɛkablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa kanɛmɛna nɛ bati kamɛ a. Yesu Tsa Onyole Odi Kĩ Yasɔ Fɔsɔ Ngbã.

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ fɔsɔ a wɔ nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Awã naa, Yesu bi ye kĩ ɛdzakũ mɔatsɛ ɛnaa, lɛmɛ esi ye mbla mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ,

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
translation_verse: “Tafɔ̃ ɔdza nɛ osi ɔkabi bati lɛtsa a kĩ de lɛya, mbom naa kpĩĩ, kakɔa nɔ akũ kanaatsa ɔsɔfɔ. Bɔa afɔliɛ a kĩ Mose mbla tsa a, ayekĩ odi nyɛ odi aanyi kĩ nɔ akũ kpalɛ.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ oti kĩĩ dzakũ a, ɛnado fɔlɛtsa a kagbamɛna awɛ, ɛkabi bati flee ayekĩ Yesu tsa ye a. Nɛ foesũ, Yesu tápuli ebuki ewo nɛ ɔmatɔ odima kamɛ, ade ɛkana nɛ tuwĩ kamɛ a. Lɛmɛ bati buki bɛkato kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kaya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
translation_verse: Awã naa Yesu via be kĩ, “Mitákã lɛtsa a kĩ Lɛgã Dawid kɛna a? Lɔkɔ a kĩ kɔlɛ kɔ ye mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a, Dawid wo nɛ Baguma kɔla a kamɛ a, nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Abiata anya Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a. Ɛnanyɛ afɔlibɔ abodoo kĩ Yudafɔ basɔfɔ odi deĩ osi kĩ banya a ɛnya, ade ɛkɔa adi ɛkpa bikĩ bitomɛna ye a lɛmɛ bɛnya a.”

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Foesũ bati yayi tɔtɔ a kĩ te kamɛ edeĩ a mɔ kanya flee, ade ɛkatsa be Baguma fɔlɛtsa a.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Ade awã naa mɔ bati ɛna lɔ oti odi kĩ eveliveli bɛma ye a.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtápuli bɛtsã onyukpɛ̃ nɛ bati lidede sũ ni, bide nɛ kɔla ode bɛnyɛ ɔkɛ nɛ kakɔ kadi, ade bɛkɛna ɔsɔnɛ a mɔ ɔkɛna biyi nɛ Yesu anɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Nde sũ yalɛ fubusuotsa lɔmɔ? Baguma odi aapuli ɛkɔa titikpa ete.”

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: Kĩ míanyi kĩ Otidziwa Obi a deĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kĩ yakɔa bati titikpa kate sũ ni,” Yesu bi oti a kĩ eveliveli a kĩ,

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: “Yidza, bɔɛ nɔ ɔkɛna kanaa kɔla.”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛdzakũ ɛnaa Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ abuĩ a. Bati pii ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awã ade ɛtsa be Baguma fɔlɛtsa a.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Otidziwa sũ bɛbɔa Kawɛya Luwi a, nnɛ́ Kawɛya Luwi a sũ bɛbɔa otidziwa.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mɔ bati pii kĩ bitomɔ ye a naanya nɛ Levi awã. Tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ pii lɛmɛ na nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a mɔ be.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Ade Farisifɔ kĩ bɛnyɛ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi lɛmɛ na nɛ awã a. Kĩ Farisifɔ mbla batsanɛ a mɔ́ kĩ Yesu anya mɔ titikpa bakɛnanɛ mɔ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ a, bivia ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Litsa sũ yanya kamɔa mɔ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bɛbã bakɛnanɛ?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ lidi kamɛ, kĩ Yohanes mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ mɔ Farisifɔ kasui onukpɛ̃ a, bati badi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Nde sũ nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lásui onukpɛ̃ ndɛ Yohanes bade mɔ Farisifɔ a aye?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mbɔɛɛ obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ bakɔba aapuli bidzi kɔlɛ nɛ obolitsɔ̃ kapikɔ̃ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a deĩ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ a?

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ luwi lidi aaya kĩ baakpã ye nɛ ye bakɔba a sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa baasui onukpɛ̃ nɛ fuwi a kamɛ.

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Odima lákɔa limakũ vɔɛ̃ kataɛ ɔkɛ nɛ lima kɔɛ akũ. Limakũ vɔɛ̃ a aabɛ̃ lɔkɔɛ lidomɔ akũ ntɛ bɛvɔlɛ de, ade fɔ́afɔ̃ ɔkɛ baũ lɛwa ɛba ayekĩ fɔna kɔkɔɛ a.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Bio, odima lákɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ kako nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ kɔɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ waĩ vɔɛ̃ aabɛ̃ kuvũ kɔɛ a, lɔkɔa waĩ a aawula, kuvũ a lɛmɛ aabu kade. Mbom bakɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ kako nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ vɔɛ̃ kamɛ.” Fɔlɛtsa Kavia Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi a Akũ.

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lidi akũ a, kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ tsɛ̃ batɛ̃ nɛ fugbanɔ kɔba kudi kamɛ a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kave fudi.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Foesũ Farisifɔ a bi Yesu kĩ, “Dã! Boe mbla tákpa osi kĩ bakɛna fukĩnaa nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ.”

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Foesũ Otidziwa Obi a nyɛ Kawɛya Luwi a Ɔlate.”

english_verse: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na Yesu bakesĩnɛ badi na nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, bɛkadã ntɛ yaatsa oti a nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ, ayekĩ baabɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Yesu vɛ oti a kĩ ye kɔkpɔ ku a kĩ ɛnɛ ɛyaadza nɛ bati a flee anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Yesu dã be ɛtɛ̃mɛna nɛ kalɔkpa kamɛ, ade fɔha ye paa nɛ be lɔlɔ kamɛ ɔsĩ sũ a. Ade ebi ɔsɔnɛ a kĩ, “Yo nɔ awɛ.” Eyo awɛ a ade ye awɛ a de ɔsĩ a.

english_verse: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu de ɛnaa ɔtɔ kalete. Ade awã ɛvɛ bikĩ yawɛ kĩ bɛnya ye bade a.

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a kĩ bito Yerusalem bɛya a lɛ kĩ, “Hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ. Hũhũ kpa lɛgã a lɛkpa ye kɔbɛ̃ yablɔmɛna hũhũ a katomɛna bati kamɛ.”

english_verse: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bidzi bɛtsɛna Yesu a bi ye kĩ, “Dã, ama mɔ awaɛ̃ amɛ dzɛ̃ nɛ kanya bawɛ nɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Ade Yesu vɛ bati a ɛyamɛna nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ be nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ kĩ, “Nde fɔ́aya kĩ Abonsam aapuli ɛblɔmɛna ye mɔawɛ akũ etomɛna oti kamɛ?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa tɔtɔ kamɛ, ade bati pii buki bɛya nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awã. Nɛ foesũ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lána aya bɛnya malo.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Foe nfantuhũ nyɛ kĩ ntɛ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ kadi sɛ̃ ke akũ kutsu kutsu kĩ kɔanɔ akũ ni, kanyakɔ̃ a na lɛláapuli kitim.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: “Odima lɛláapuli ewo nɛ oti kĩ edeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kɔla kamɛ ɛnaafila ye fɔtsa, mbɔɛntɛ ɛnya lɔtɔ esuli ɔbɛlɛbɛ a fɔmɔ yaawo efila ye fɔtsa a.

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: “Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ Baguma aakɔa bati tɔkpa tidi nyɛ tidi kĩ bɛkɛna mɔ fubusuotsa fudi nyɛ fudi kĩ bɛlɛ ete be.

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaalɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a akũ ni, Baguma lɛláakɔa ete ye da. Kitonɛkĩ ɛkɛna tɔkpa kĩ taawa daa.”

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ fukĩĩ kitonɛkĩ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a badi lɛ kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ. Bawei Liti Bɛnyɛ Yesu Akũ Bati?

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu ɔma mɔ bawaɛ̃nyole ya bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ kanya ade bido lɔtɔ kĩ bɛvɛ ye ɛya.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu via be kĩ, “Owei nyɛ mma? Bawei nyɛ nwaɛ̃ amɛ?”

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛdã bati a kĩ bidzi bisinya ye ɛnake a, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Midã, ade mma mɔ nwaɛ̃ amɛ nɛ awĩ a!

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ ni, ade ye nyɛ nwaɛ̃nyole mɔ nwaɛ̃tsole, mɔ mma a.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛnaa Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a ɔwɛ̃, ade ɛkatsa Baguma fɔlɛtsa a. Ayekĩ bati pii fɔli nɛ ye akũ sũ ni, ewo edzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kĩ eteĩ nɛ mui anɛ kamɛ. Bati dza nɛ ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a abuĩ ade Yesu katsa be fɔtsa nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ a.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkasã foe a fudi tɔ nɛ osi ɔɔwɛ̃, ade kɔvɔlabi yafila be bɛmɛna a.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ pii nɛ ye fɔtsa katsa kamɛ a, ɛkɔa fɔgbã ɛtsa fɔtsa a kĩ yawɛ kĩ ɛtsa be a. Fɔtsa a kĩ ɛtsa be a nyɛ fukĩĩ.

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Mitse! Luwi a onyole odi naa kɔba ɛnaasã funitsã babi.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Badi tɔ nɛ lɛkpakpalabode akũ, kĩ tite lɛláa pii. Foesũ babi a mɛ mɔatsɛ.

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ suwa ta kpaa pɛ bɛvɛ biku, kitonɛkĩ be tunu tána funitsã, nɛ boe a sũ.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Badi tɔ nɛ tuwĩ mɔ fu ndɛ ɔgɔabi kamɛ. Tuwĩ mɔ fu mɛ fusuli be, kĩ bɛtápuli bibo anɛ. Nɛ foesũ bɛtáwũ babi.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Yesu via kĩ, “Litsa bɔ́akɔa bɔkɔnamɔ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a? Lɛgbã lɛwɔdabe makɔa ĩnyɛ kayɔ ntsa?

english_verse: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu odi na nɛ kakɔ mɔa a, bati a kĩ binu ye lɛgbã a mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ mɔ ye fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ya kĩ ɛnyɛ de kayɔ ɛtsa be.

english_verse: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kalɛ a ke tunu láwo nɛ tite kamɛ kawo wĩ. Be katɛkado adã lukũ. Ntɛ bati liti katomɛna yawo nɛ kalɛ kĩĩ akũ pɛ, baamuniki nɛ ke liti.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Foesũ fɔ́aya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ kamɛ odi tsɛlɛ eyi kĩ, ‘Baakadã, lɛmɛ bɛláamɔ biyi ɔsũ. Baakatse lɛmɛ bɛláanu foe kayɔ. Kitonɛkĩ titɔ kĩ banu ĩye bamɔ malo ni, bɛláanu kayɔ mɔ baadani bɛdã Baguma, lɔkɔa ɛkɔa be titikpa ete be.’ ”

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
translation_verse: Bati badi la ndɛ babi a kĩ bɛtɔ nɛ osi ɔwɛ̃ a. Ntɛ binu pɛ lɔkɔa Abonsam yanyɛ foe nɛ be fɔlɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa odunɛ a nyɛ oti kĩ ɛtsɛ̃ yalɛ Baguma kalɛ.

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Yesu buki evia be kĩ, “Owei atɛna ɔsɛdza kakɔa lɔɔda katsa ye ĩye kakɔa ye kado nɛ mpa kayɔ? Nnɛ́ ɔsɛdza otse akũ bakɔa ye kate?

english_verse: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
translation_verse: Bio, Yesu bi be kĩ, “Miyi atɔ yededeede mitse lɛtsa a kĩ mianu a. Kitonɛkĩ fɔtsa kakɔna odu a kĩ mikɔa mikɔna mikpa bati bɛbã a, ade foe odu Baguma lɛmɛ aakɔa ɛkɔna ɛkpa mi a.

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ebi be kĩ, “Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la ndɛ oti kĩ yasã kamɔnɛ.

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Tite afɔ̃ fudutsa alɛ nɛ foe akũ akũ. Lanya lɔtɔ lɔkɔa lɛnyɛ tɔɔta fɔmɔ kutse a anɛ. Ntɛ kubo anɛ kɔawũ babi beblebee.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Ntɛ babi a yɔɔlɛ a banɛna be, kitonɛkĩ kɔba funitsã kakpɔta lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Kutse Libi Kabii Akũ.

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Fɔla ndɛ kutse libi kabii nɛ katinya kamɛ flee kĩ oti bɔɛ ɛnadu.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Kutse kĩĩ yapɔ kɔkɛna futse baũ a kamɛ kɔmɔa nɛ kɔba kamɛ. Kɔanɛ tɛnanɛ pii ade kɔakpa ɔna a. Kɔvɔlabi akɛna fɔtsɔɛ nɛ koe kamɛ.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: Yaábi be kadima kɛba fɔgbã kamɛ. Ntɛ bɛmɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a odi bidzi a, lɔkɔa yanyɛ foe kayɔ katsa be. Yesu Fɔ̃ Ɔwɛ Kɔbɛ̃ Onukpɛ̃ Dɔ.

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Luwi a ke olobe a, Yesu vɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bunyina ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a bɔnaa de abuĩ atide a.”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ nɛ be kapuli kanu kayɔ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛfɔnɛ bati a biwo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ. Yesu nawo ɛkɛna akũ edzi, ade biyi ɔwɔ a kanyina kayɔ a. Tɔkɔlɔ bɛbã lɛmɛ na nɛ mui a akũ.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Owi tábuna ɔwɛ kpale odi do kavũ awɛ, ɛbɔa mui eko nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ. Ɔkɔlɔ a ka kɔlaa kĩ kite sukuloo kufɛ yayi mɔ mui ɛyɛla.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Yesu wa nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a liti. Ɛkɔa sumuyɛ ete lɔlɔ ɛlamɛna tida kawa. Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nadzɛla ye ade bibi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, fɔtsɛ́la nɔ kĩ bɔwɔ buyua?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu yidza ɛdza a, ade ewũni kĩ, “Ɔwɛ, fɔ̃ kavũ!” Ebi mui a lɛmɛ kĩ, “Mui, kɛna dĩĩ.” Ɔwɛ a fɔ̃ kavũ ade mui a anɛ flee kɛna dĩĩ a.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Yesu via ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nde sũ libe wo mi lɔmɔ? Mitátɛ yĩ mido?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Nɛ libe a kĩ libuki liwo be a sũ ni, bɛkavia be akũ kĩ, “Owei koũ nyɛ oti kĩĩ? Mui mɔ ɔwɛ malo atse ye kalɛ!”

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Ade ebi ye akũ kĩ, “Ntɛ maapuli nta titɔ ye atadiɛ malo ni, kufɛ maasaɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ obitsole, nɔ katɛkado fɔ̃ ɔsaɛ. Naa nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ. Nɔ lubo kamɔ a lɛláabuki lɛya nɛ nɔ akũ dadaada.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: Otsole odi kĩ babɔanɛ kawula nɛ ye akũ futeli ewua-nviã flee mɔ lubo kamɔ paa, titɔ kĩ bawanɛ kadã ye a malo na nɛ be kamɛ. Ebula sika flee kade nɛ fɔsɔ kĩĩ katsa akũ, lɛmɛ fɔsɔ a tádɔ ye, mbom kade fɔkade nɛ ode.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: ewo nɛ be kamɛ, evia be kĩ, “Litsa tsɛ̃mɔ fɔwɔla kĩĩ odu? Nde sũ miabe? Kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ kĩĩ táku. Tida kawa!”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a naa Galilea lutubu baũ sɛkɛ kĩ a nɛ Gerasefɔ tite akũ.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Onyole kĩ kadzikɔ̃ nyɛ asie a kĩ efuã nɛ awã. Bati a flee lápuli kapi ye kado nɛ nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ basuli ye akpɔ mɔ abɔa ni, yaveli nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ a kĩ tipini ye a. Oti kĩ deĩ kɔbɛ̃ kudi odu kĩ odima lápuli kapi ye.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee yamini nɛ asie mɔ tɔtɔ kamɛ. Ɛtsɛ̃ yabɔa fɔwɔla lɔkɔa yakɔa boe kakpeli ye akũ.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Onyole kĩĩ dzɛ̃ mɔ lugo fɔmɔ ɛmɔ Yesu. Foesũ ewula atsɛ ɛnake aduli nɛ Yesu nnyɛ.

english_verse: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
translation_verse: Ade ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ kĩ, “Yesu, Baguma Nɛ Ode Flee Obi! Bɔake nɔ aduli, nɛ Baguma sũ, tunɔ kĩ ɔláavɛlɛ yĩ sɔtɔ!”

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, baplakuo pii dzɛ̃ badɛ nɛ katɔbii kayɔ.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Ade bike Yesu aduli kĩ, “Blɔmɛna boe bɔnaawo nɛ baplakuo a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ akĩĩ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ badãmɛna be akũ a wula atsɛ bɛnaa ɔmatɔ a mɔ tikĩ tifuã a kamɛ, bɛnalɛ lɛtsa flee a kĩ lɛya a. Bati wula atsɛ bɛnaa awã kĩ banaadã lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a.

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Yesu sɛkɛ̃ a, bɛmɔ́ oti a kĩ hũhũ kpa beblebee na nɛ ye kamɛ a nɛ awã. Ɛkɛna akũ edzi ade edo atadiɛ a, ye anɛ kamɛ lɛmɛ kpalɛ. Libe wo bati a flee!

english_verse: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛmɔ foe a, lɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ onyole a mɔ baplakuo a akũ.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Foesũ bike Yesu aduli kĩ ɛdzakũ nɛ be tite akũ.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu tátunɔ. Mbom ebi ye kĩ, “Naa kɔla nɛ nɔ bati sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa kabi be lɛtsa a kĩ Ɔlate a kɛna ɛkpa nɔ, mɔ ayekĩ ɛmɔ nɔ kɔnya a.”

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Foesũ onyole kĩĩ naa ɛnamini nɛ tɛmatɔ ewua kamɛkamɛ a, ade ebi be fɔlɛtsa flee a kĩ Yesu kɛna ɛkpa ye a. Fɔkɛna bikĩ binu foe a saa. Yesu Tsa Yairo Kĩ Ɛnyɛ Yudafɔ Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Obitsole Mɔ Otsole Ɔsɔnɛ Odi.

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛlɛ foe lɔmɔ pɛ, bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã támɔ ye kamɛmɛ. Ade Yesu fɔ̃ bɛnyɛ bati a flee bɛnaa kanya, ade ɛfɔ̃ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a ɔlɛga mɔ ɔma mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a biwo nɛ awã kĩ ɔnɔkpa a waa a.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛdani ɛnaa lutubu baũ a sɛkɛ̃ kĩ a. Kĩ ewo de abuĩ a, bati pii yayi ye akũ.

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: Onyole odi nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ bavɛ Yairo ya nɛ awã. Kĩ ɛmɔ Yesu a, ɛkɛna akũ ɛkpa ye.

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: Ade ebi ye mɔ kudulike kĩ, “Yĩ obitsole kabii kĩ kɛtɛ futeli ewua-nviã asɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Foesũ ya kayate awɛ nɛ ye akũ lɔkɔa yaana akũ ɔsĩ edzi nkpã.”

english_verse: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu yidza etomɔ ye a. Nɛ ayekĩ bati a kĩ bitomɔ Yesu pɔnɛ sũ, ɛna nɛ be kayite kĩ banaa.

english_verse: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Enu Yesu nkã foesũ ɛna nɛ bati a kĩ bitomɔ Yesu nɛ liti a kamɛ.

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛta Yesu atadiɛ a, ade akũ fɔ̃ ye kawula deedimɔapɛ a. Ɛmɔ́ eyi ɔsũ nɛ ye kamɛ kĩ ɛsaɛ ade ɛnɛ nɛ ye lubo kamɔ kamɛ a.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Lɛtalɛta Yesu nyi kĩ kɔbɛ̃ kudi nɛ́ nɛ ye kamɛ. Foesũ ɛfɔ̃ ɛdza, ɛdani evia bati a kĩ bitomɔ ye a kĩ, “Owei nyɛ ekĩ ɛta yĩ atadiɛ?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Dã ayekĩ bati beblebee sinya nɔ. Nde sũ avia kĩ owei lɛta nɔ?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu dzɛmɛna anɛ kako kadã kĩ yaamɔ oti a kĩ ye lɛta ye a.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Otsole a mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ ye akũ. Foesũ nɛ libe mɔ kukũtila kamɛ ni, ɛnɛ ɛyake aduli nɛ Yesu anɛ kamɛ. Ebi ye lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a yededeede.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu kalɛ fukĩĩ a, batɔ to Yairo awã bɛyabi ye kĩ, “Nɔ obi a ku kɔkɔɛ. Nde sũ obuki aha fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ kĩĩ?”

english_verse: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
translation_verse: Yesu tátse be kalɛ a, mbom ebi Yairo kĩ, “Tavila, nɔ kɔ̃ tɛ kado.”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: Yesu kpã Petro, Yakobo mɔ ɔwaɛ̃ Yohanes be odi bɛnaa Yairo awã.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo tɔtɔ a kamɛ a, ade kĩ Yesu mɔ́ ayekĩ bati dzɛ̃ mɔ kɔnya mɔ fɔwɔla a,

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ katsolebi kĩĩ yidza kɛdza ade kido kato katɛ̃ awɛ a. Kĩ fɔya lɔmɔ a, fɔkɛna be a flee wãwã.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu si be mbla mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bɛtabi odima kadimanɔ. Ebuki ebi be kĩ, “Mikpa ke litsedi kɛnya.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a sɛkɛ sitide a, biduma nɛ Genezaret. Ade awã bɛbla be ɔkɔlɔ bɛdzɛla a.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ tɛ dĩ nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, lɛmɛ nɛ ye mɔawɛ ɔmatɔ, mɔ ye mɔawɛ bati kamɛ ni, ɛlá dĩ wĩ lidima.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Lɛgã Herodes nu fɔlɛtsa kĩ a ye malo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Yohanes a kĩ nfɔ̃ bitsua ye lɔlɔ a, ade ye liyidza ebuki edzi nkpã a.”

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi a akũ a, ɛnatsa Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a. Ye ɔmatɔ bati pii yatse ye fɔtsa katsa, ade fɔkɛna be saa a. Bivia be akũ kĩ, “Ndia oti kĩĩ kɔlɛ fɔtsa kĩĩ odu etomɛna? Kadikanyi mɔ kɔbɛ̃ wɔdabe bɛkpa ye sũ mɔ epuli yakɛna awãwã fɔtsa kĩĩ odu?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ nnɛ́ Maria obi kĩ yamɛ fɔtsa a yanɛ? Ĩye nnɛ́ bawaɛ̃nyole nyɛ Yakobo, Yose, Yuda mɔ Simon? Miádã! Nnɛ́ bawaɛ̃tsole lɛmɛ nɛ awĩ a?” Foesũ bati kĩĩ sĩ ye katɛ kado.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Esi be mbla kĩ, “Odima ɛtabɔɛ lɛtsama ɛlɛ nɛ ye osi akũ, mbɔɛntɛ otse kĩ bake katsã odi. Mitalɛ funitsã ĩye kuvũ ndɛ ayekĩ fɔtsa bawãnɛ alɛ a. Mitabɔɛ sika milɛ nɛ mi fuvũ kamɛ.

english_verse: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
translation_verse: Awã kĩ bati tátɛ mi ni, minɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ lɔkɔa mikpa mi abɔa kudu miko nɛ be akũ, fɔnya nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔkpa be kĩ Baguma aavɛlɛ be sɔtɔ.”

english_verse: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
translation_verse: Foesũ Yohanes fɔlɛtsa a wo Herodia kalɔɛ, ade ɛbɔɛ ye likesĩ a. Ɛwɛ kĩ yakɔ ye lɛmɛ ɛláapuli ɛkɛna fukĩĩ nɛ Lɛgã Herodes liti.

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Lɛgã Herodes avila Yohanes, ade enyina kĩ Yohanes nyɛ oti wĩ kĩ ye akũ kpalɛ. Ɛkana kɔnɛyi nɛ ye akũ. Yawɛ katse ye fɔtsa katsa a katse fɔkɔ fudi, titɔ kĩ ye fɔlɛtsa awɛ ye ɔwɔlɛ kaha ntɛ enu foe.

english_verse: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
translation_verse: Ɛlɔ fubo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ aavia yĩ ni, maakpa nɔ, ntɛ fɔ́anya yĩ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a sɛkɛ malo!”

english_verse: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Foesũ oyitsɔ̃ kĩĩ nɛ ɛnavia ɔma kĩ, “Litsa nvia?” Ade ɔma bi ye kĩ, “Via Yohanes, mui ɔwɔlanɛ a, lɔlɔ.”

english_verse: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Oyitsɔ̃ a wɛ sɔwa ɛnaa kɔla a kamɛ, ade ebi Lɛgã Herodes kĩ, “Mawɛ kĩ tsua Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a lɔlɔ kate nɛ tsensi akũ kakpa yĩ finyaa finyaakĩĩ.”

english_verse: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ wo Lɛgã Herodes kalɔɛ paa lɛmɛ ɛláapuli ɛdani ye sɛmɛ nɛ fubo a kĩ ɛlɔ nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ banɔkɔɛ̃ anɛ sũ.

english_verse: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
translation_verse: Foesũ awã naa edo ye ɔblafɔ kĩ ɛnaatsua Yohanes lɔlɔ ɛyamɛna. Ɔblafɔ a natsua naa kɔla kĩ bado bati a ɛnatsua Yohanes lɔlɔ.

english_verse: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Ɛkɔa lɔlɔ a ete nɛ tsensi akũ ade ɛkɔa ɛma oyitsɔ̃ a. Ye malo ɛtɛ ɛkɔa de ɛma ɔma.

english_verse: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ Yohanes fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu fɔlɛtsa a, bɛnaa bɛnabɔɛ ye ɔnɔkpa a bɛnabila. Yesu Kpa Bati Mpim Elo Funitsã.

english_verse: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
translation_verse: Awã kĩ Yesu mɔ be bɛna a bati a yapɔ lidede, bikĩĩ anaa ni bikĩĩ aya, foesũ bɛtána osi mɔ banya malo. Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ boe odi bɔnaa awã kĩ kadã dĩĩ bɔnaawɛya sukuloo.”

english_verse: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Biwo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ banaa awã kĩ bati lɛláa.

english_verse: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
translation_verse: Kĩ olobe wo ɔtɔ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ya bɛyabi ye kĩ, “Kade dɔ kiyua. Ayekĩ awĩ la a bɛláana lɛtsama bɛnya.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mi mɔawɛ a miwɛ litsedi mikpa be bɛnya.” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Be kanya aatɛ ndɛ oti mɔa tidetɔ ɛlɛ̃ kɔtɔkase. Awɛ kĩ bubula sika kade nɛ be kanya odi sũ?”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu via be kĩ, “Abodoo eni minɛɛ? Minaa minaadã.” Kĩ bɛnadã a bibi ye kĩ, “Abodoo elo mɔ kɔfɔabi nviã pɛ deĩ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛfɔ̃ bati a flee bidzi nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ nɛ tuwĩ blɔblɔ akũ.

english_verse: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Libe wo be flee. Lɛmɛ lɛtalɛta Yesu lɛmɔ be kɔdzɛla kĩ, “Mitavila! Yĩ manɛ. Mina ɔwɔlɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
translation_verse: Yesu bɔɛ abodoo elo mɔ kɔfɔabi nviã a ade kĩ ɛdã odetɔ́ kamɛ fɛɛ a, ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ ade ɛyɛlɛ abodoo a. Ɛkɔa foe ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛsɛ̃ bɛkpa kutsu a flee. Ade ɛkɛna kɔfɔabi a lɛmɛ lɔmɔ a.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
translation_verse: Bati a flee nya biwĩ.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
translation_verse: Kĩ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fila funitsã a kĩ fute bɛkpɔta a, fuyi kɔba tɛtsɛtsɛ ewua-nviã.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Awã naa Yesu fɔ̃ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ bɛnaa ɔmatɔ kĩ bavɛ Betsaida nɛ lutubu baũ sɛkɛ̃ kĩ a. Ɛfɔ̃ bati beblebee a lɛmɛ pansam.

english_verse: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ye malo ede ɛnaa ɔtɔ kalete ɛnabɔa mpaɛ.

english_verse: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
translation_verse: Ke lɛnyɛ a, ɔkɔlɔ a wo ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a kayite, ade kite Yesu odi nɛ awã a.

english_verse: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ́ kĩ kɛkapiã ye bati a mɔ ɔkɔlɔ a kahali, kitonɛkĩ ɔwɛ kavũ katsɛlamɔ be. Foesũ fɔmɔ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ elo aabɔa a, Yesu tsã mui anɛ ɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃. Kĩ efuã be a, ɛkɛna ndɛ yatɛ̃ be,

english_verse: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
translation_verse: Ewo nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, ade ɔwɛ a fɔ̃ kavũ a. Fukĩĩ fɔ̃ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kɛna saa,

english_verse: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ bɛtányi ayekĩ fɔkɛna fɔmɔ ɛkpa bati mpim elo funitsã bɛnya bitina. Be kakɔna nɛ fukĩĩ akũ malo tákpalɛ, foesũ bɛtátɛ bido. Yesu Tsa Basɔnɛ Nɛ Genezaret Tite Akũ.

english_verse: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɔ be kanɛ nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ pɛ bati mɔ Yesu biyi ɔsũ.

english_verse: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
translation_verse: Foesũ biwula atsɛ bɛnakpa kanyi nɛ be tite flee akũ. Awã kĩ binu kĩ Yesu na a, bɛlɔ basɔnɛ bɛma ye.

english_verse: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Ade kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ Yesu tsã, ntɛ ɔmatɔ kamɛ o-o, ĩye akula kamɛ o-o, bati kayamɛna basɔnɛ nɛ kɔyaatɔ ade bɛkawã ye kĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛta ye atadiɛ atɔ. Bikĩ bɛta ye a na akũ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi to Yerusalem bɛyatsɛna nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mideĩ tɛdake tidi kĩ mitomɛna ade mikɔa Baguma mbla miyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ nɛ ayekĩ míakɛna nɛ mi bawanyɔ amaniɛ akũ sũ.

english_verse: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
translation_verse: Bɛmɔ́ kĩ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a dzi banya, ade badi távɔlɛ awɛ ndɛ ayekĩ be Farisifɔ bɛtsa bati kĩ bɛkaakɛna a.

english_verse: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
translation_verse: Biyi foe ɔsũ kitonɛkĩ Farisifɔ a mɔ Yudafɔ a flee tomɔ be bawanyɔ amaniɛ a kĩ bɛkɔa biyi be a. Foesũ ntɛ bɛtávɔlɛ awɛ kavɔlɛ wĩ ni, baánya.

english_verse: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɛya litsedi bitomɛna kɔyaatɔ malo, kɛnɛ kĩ bavɔlɛ awɛ fɔmɔ banya. Bitomɛna mbla kumɛkumɛ lɛmɛ kĩ bibo anɛ bɛyamɔ a, ndɛ kuyei, fɔtɔtsa mɔ fugumi kavɔlɛ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
translation_verse: Foesũ Farisifɔ mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a via Yesu kĩ, “Nde sũ bikĩ bitomɛna nɔ a lákɛna nɛ boe bawanyɔ amaniɛ akũ? Ade ntɛ banya malo baávɔlɛ awɛ!”
