english_verse: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mi anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ! Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya lɛ nɛ mi akũ a ya nɛ foe kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ. Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Bati kĩĩ asum yĩ mɔ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa mɛnɛmɛnɛ odi, lɛmɛ be tɔwɔlɛ deĩ lugo nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
translation_verse: Pupulidza basum yĩ, kitonɛkĩ batsa batidziwa mbla nɛ Baguma mbla a kadokɔ̃.’

english_verse: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
translation_verse: Fɔatsa kĩ bɛkɔa yĩ mbla biyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ ade bɛkɔa batidziwa amaniɛ a batsa a!”

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ miatsa kĩ ntɛ odi deĩ litsedi kĩ yaakɔa ebumɔ ye bamanɛ ni, yaapuli ɛlɛ kĩ ɛkɔa foe eyi lite ndɛ fɔtsa kpalɛwa ɛkpa Baguma lɔkɔa fɔatsa kĩ owula kɔa fɔtsa tewa a eyi kĩ yaabɔa afɔliɛ ɛkpa Baguma.

english_verse: You won't let those people help their parents.
translation_verse: Ade ke mitákpa ye osi malo kĩ yabumɛna ye bamanɛ a mɔ fɔtsa tewa a!

english_verse: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade aye mikɔa mi bawanyɔ amaniɛ mibula Baguma mbla kade a. Miakɛna fɔtsa bɛbã ndɛ fukĩĩ odu.” Fɔtsa Kĩ Fɔato Oti Ɔwɔlɛ Kamɛ Ni, Ade Foe Ado Ye Efĩĩ a.

english_verse: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ ɛnamɛna lɔtɔ kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ lanɛ kato oti ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ni, ade foe ado ye efĩĩ a.

english_verse: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu buki ɛvɛ bati a flee ɛkpɔta nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃. Ebi be kĩ,

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛdzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛnaa kɔla a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye kĩ ɛnyɛ lɛgbã a kayɔ ɛtsa be.

english_verse: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Ntɛ mitánu kalɛ kĩĩ kayɔ kekɔ̃ mi adzuni lɛláapuli epi lɛtsama. Fɔwaa yedede kĩ lɛtsa kĩ lawo nɛ oti kamɛ ni, fɔádo ye efĩĩ.

english_verse: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ fɔtáwo nɛ ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Mbom fɔnaa ye sɛmɛ kamɛ, ade fɔtsa ye kamɛ fɔnɛ ndɛ fɔtsa kpa a.” Lɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lɛ nɛ awĩ tsa kĩ funitsã fudi nyɛ fudi nɛnɛ fɔkpa kanya.

english_verse: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
translation_verse: anɛ ngbã, ablɔ kakɛna, fɔvã kama, sukɛna fɔwɛtsa kĩ fɔnɛnɛ, likesĩ, fɔvã kama kate nɛ bati akũ, lukũta mɔ kadzi nɛ tidzimbi kamɛ.

english_verse: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
translation_verse: Fɔtsa kĩĩ flee to oti kamɛ ade foe ado ye efĩĩ a.” Otsole Odi Kĩ Ɛnyɛ́ Yudani Tɛ Yesu Edo.

english_verse: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
translation_verse: Awã naa otsole a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Awoo, boe Ɔlɛga, lɛmɛ bakpete a kĩ bikoni nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a ana osi kanya babisɛ̃ funitsã a kĩ fuwuli fuko nɛ tite a.”

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ ɛnaa tite a kĩ tifuã ɔmatɔ kĩ bavɛ Tiro a kamɛ. Ɛwo nɛ tɔtɔ tidi kamɛ. Ɛtáwɛ kĩ odima enyi kĩ edeĩ nɛ awã, lɛmɛ ɛtápuli ɛkɔla ye akũ.

english_verse: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
translation_verse: Otsole odi kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye obi kamɛ nu kĩ Yesu na nɛ awã, ade ɛya ɛyake aduli nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
translation_verse: Otsole kĩĩ nyɛ ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛmɔ Yudafɔ baákã. Ɛnyɛ Foenikeni kĩ eto Siria tite akũ. Eke Yesu aduli kĩ ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a etomɛna obitsole kamɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Fɔ̃ bɛkpa babisɛ̃ funitsã tɔ̃. Fɔnɛ́nɛ kĩ bakɔa babisɛ̃ funitsã bɛkpa bakpete.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɔlɛ nɛ awĩ sũ ni, kaanaa kɔla. Aamɔ kĩ hũhũ kpa a nɛ́ nɛ nɔ obitsole a kamɛ.”

english_verse: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
translation_verse: Otsole kĩĩ naa kɔla, ade ɛmɔ kĩ obi a waa nɛ ɔkɛna akũ, lɔkɔa hũhũ kpa a nɛ nɛ ye kamɛ. Yesu Tsa Oti Kĩ Ɛnyɛ Ɔtɔsĩnɛ Mɔ Omumu.

english_verse: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu dzakũ nɛ Tiro kayite a ade ɛtsã Sidon ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, ɛtsã Galilea ɔwɔ lutubu baũ abuĩ, ɛnaa tɛmatɔ ewua a kamɛkamɛ a.

english_verse: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a, bati badi yamɛna odi kĩ esĩ atɔ ade ɛnyɛ omumu lɛmɛ a. Bike ye aduli kĩ ete awɛ nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu kpã ye ɛnamɛna ɔɔwɛ̃, ade ɛkɔa bawobi edo nɛ ye atɔ kamɛ a, ade etua tanɛ lɛmɛ ete nɛ lawobi akũ ɛkɔa ekputa onyole a sɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu dzɛla anɛ ɛdã ode, ɛwɛya eke fɛɛ ade ebi onyole a kĩ, “Hefata,” foe kayɔ nyɛ kĩ, “Binya!”

english_verse: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ onyole a atɔ binya ade ye sɛmɛ tɔwɛ̃ wũnya, epuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla a.

english_verse: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
translation_verse: Yesu sua bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã osi kĩ bɛtabi odima lɛtsama nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛmɔ́ a akũ. Lɛmɛ pupulidza esi be mbla, kitonɛkĩ be flee bɛnaa bɛnalɛ lɛtsa kĩ lɛya a.

english_verse: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
translation_verse: Bati a flee kĩ binu fɔlɛtsa kĩ a, fɔkɛna be saa! Ade bibi akũ kĩ, “Yakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kakɛna wĩ. Ɛfɔ̃ omumu alɛ kɔdzɛla, ade ɔtɔsĩnɛ lɛmɛ anu fɔkɔ a!”

english_verse: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ nɛ Lɛgã Herodes mɔ Farisifɔ oyila a akũ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
translation_verse: Ebi be fukĩĩ yededeede binu. Petro kpã ye ɛnamɛna ɔɔwɛ̃, ade ɛtsa kĩ bɛmɔ ye bɛtákɛna ɔmɔa nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a akũ.

english_verse: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
translation_verse: “Bati kĩĩ fɔlɛtsa akɔ yĩ kɔnya kitonɛkĩ bɛna nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ fuwi ɛlalɛ flee. Ade finyaakĩĩ bɛlá lɛtsama kĩ baanya.

english_verse: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Ntɛ nfɔnɛ be awɛ mɔ bɛdzakũ mɔ kɔlɛ ni, baanaa lɔkɔa anɛ aasinya be nɛ osi, kitonɛkĩ badi to mɔ lugo.”

english_verse: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
translation_verse: Yesu bi bati a kĩ bɛkɛna akũ bidzi. Ade ɛbɔɛ abodoo evũkɔnɔ a. Ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ ade ɛyɛlɛ ye fɔkɛfɔkɛ a, ɛkɔa ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkpa bati a flee. Be malo bɛtɛ, ade bɛsɛ̃ bɛkpa be a.

english_verse: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
translation_verse: Bɛnɛɛ kɔfɔabi kubii kudi lɛmɛ nɛ awã, ade Yesu bɔɛ koe lɛmɛ ɛnyɛ kɔlɛ a. Ɛkɔa koe ɛkpa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛsɛ̃ koe lɛmɛ bɛkpa be.

english_verse: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ binyina bɛnaa Dalmanuta tite akũ. Farisifɔ Via Kĩ Yesu Ɛkɛna Awãwã Fɔtsa Ɛtsa Be.

english_verse: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ badi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ade bɛkavia ye fɔlɛtsa kĩ basɔ ye bɛdã. Foesũ bibi ye kĩ ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa fudi lɔkɔa bɛmɔ kĩ eto Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
translation_verse: Kĩ banaa a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lɛla abodoo kabɔɛ kabu, foesũ abodoo a amɔapɛ bɛna nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu nyi lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkalɛ a, ade evia be kĩ, “Nde sũ mialɛ abodoo katabɔɛ fɔlɛtsa? Foesũ kɛyawo lɔkɔ kĩĩ a mitáyi ɔsũ ĩye mitánu foe kayɔ tɔ̃? Mi atɔ deĩ ɔsĩ paa! Miáte lɛtsama anɛ?

english_verse: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
translation_verse: Yesu pi oti a nɛ kɔkpɔ ade ɛkpã ye ɛnamɛna ɔmatɔ a ɔbaɛ̃ a. Kĩ evũma tanɛ eko nɛ oti a anɛ kamɛ a, ɛkɔa awɛ ete nɛ ye akũ ade evia ye kĩ, “Amɔ litsedi?”

english_verse: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
translation_verse: Ade oti a dzɛla anɛ ɛdã ode ɛlɛ kĩ, “Oo! Mamɔ bati, lɛmɛ badã ndɛ futse. Lɛmɛ bɛtsɛ̃ bamini.”

english_verse: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu buki ɛta ye anɛ a. Lɛyade kĩ kɔ̃ oti a binya ye anɛ yededeede, ye anɛ kamɛ kpalɛ ade ɛmɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɔ wĩ a.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu nyɛ ye osi ebi ye kĩ ɛtabuki ɛtsã ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛnaa kɔla. Petro Tsa Kĩ Yesu Nyɛ Oti a Kĩ Bɛbɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu buki evia be kĩ, “Nde nyɛ mi lɛmɛ? Owei mialɛ kĩ ye manɛ?” Petro bi ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Kristo a Kĩ Bɛbɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu sua be osi kĩ bɛtabi odima lɛtsa a kĩ binyi nɛ ye akũ nɛ awĩ a. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Etomɛna Ye Wuda Mɔ Kayidza Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu yi kayɔ mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fɔtsa fudi katsa a. Ebi be kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ fɔya kĩ Otidziwa Obi a ɛmɔ lubo etomɛna ɔmatɔ banɔkɔɛ̃, Yudafɔ basɔfɔ banɔkɔɛ̃, mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a awɛ kamɛ. Ade baasĩ ye, bɛkɔ ye, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ ni, yaayidza edzi nkpã.”

english_verse: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yesu dani ɛdã ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a, ɛkã Petro anɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Dzakũ nɛ yĩ akũ Satana! Aáwɛ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma awɛ a, mbom awɛ lɛtsa a kĩ batidziwa awɛ a odi!”

english_verse: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu vɛ bati kɔdabu a mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi be kĩ, “Ntɛ odi awɛ kĩ yatomɔ yĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ yafɔnɛ ye apɛdiɛ awɛ, ɛbɔɛ ye lubo kamɔ fɔlɔtsa luwi nyɛ luwi lɔkɔa etomɔ yĩ nɛ liti.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ oti kĩ ɛwɛ kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye. Lɛmɛ oti kĩ ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛkpa nɛ yĩ mɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a sũ ni, yaana nkpã daa.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
translation_verse: Litsa nfasɔ oti aana ntɛ ɛna katinya kamɛ fɔtsa flee mɔ ɛyɛla ye nkpã mɔawɛ a?

english_verse: What could you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Ĩye litsa oti aakɔa ɛvɛmɛna mɔ nkpã mɔawɛ a?

english_verse: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ yĩ mɔ yĩ fɔtsa katsa a flee nyɛ fɔnyɔãtsa fɔkpa odi nɛ ablɔ katinya kpa kĩĩ kamɛ ni, ye malo yaanya fɔnyɔãtsa ɛkpa Yĩ Otidziwa Obi, nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ bɔmɔ́ Baguma fɔtɔ banyanɛ kpalɛwa a bɔ́aya nɛ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a flee kamɛ.”

english_verse: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ Eliya ya kɔkɔɛ ade bɛkɛna ye lɛtsa a kĩ bɛwɛ a, ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ biyi nɛ ye akũ a.” Yesu Tsa Kanyolebi Kadi Kĩ Hũhũ Kpa Deĩ Nɛ Ke Kamɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kĩ Yesu naa tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye pɛtɛɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ boe kɔ̃ bɔtápuli bɔblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a ɛnɛ́ nɛ ye kamɛ?”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ɛkpã kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ kadi kɛyadza nɛ be anɛ kamɛ, ade kĩ etu eboomu ke a, ebi be kĩ,

english_verse: And his clothes became much whiter than any bleach on earth could make them.
translation_verse: ade ye tɛtadiɛ dani tivoe kɛba ayekĩ otidziwa aapuli ɛvɔlɛ te tɛnɛ́ lɔmɔ.

english_verse: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Lufulututu lidi ka lɛyasua be akũ, ade odo nɛ́ eto de kamɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ obi kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ a, mitse ye kalɛ!”

english_verse: At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛtalɛta bɛdã fɔkɔ bisinya lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ odima kɛba Yesu kĩ ɛdzɛ nɛ be ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
translation_verse: Bɛfɔ̃ kalɛ kĩĩ kahɔ nɛ be lɔlɔ kamɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ be odi bidzi a balɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ foe akũ kavia akũ kĩ, “Kayidza kato kanɔkpa a kayɔ nyɛ litsa?”

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Adeke bivia Yesu kĩ, “Litsa sũ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a alɛ kĩ, kɛnɛ kĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Nuwĩ, kɛnɛ kĩ Eliya ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya ayekĩ yaamanɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa eyi. Litsa sũ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ Otidziwa Obi a aayamɔ lubo pii, bisupa ye lɛmɛ a?

english_verse: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a muniki bɛya nɛ be nkaɛ a sɛkɛ̃ a, bɛmɔ́ bati kɔdabu baũ kĩ bisinya be, ade mbla batsanɛ a badi kavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ be a.

english_verse: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
translation_verse: Bɛkpã ke bɛma Yesu. Kĩ hũhũ a sila ɛmɔ Yesu a, efufulinya kanyolebi a kɛka kɛtɔ, kɛkamilinya nɛ tite kanɛ kuvũ nɛ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaatɛ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ kĩĩ kamɛ ɔmɔa nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, yĩ ɛtɛ. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ yĩ ni, nnɛ́ yi odi ɛtɛ, mbom ɛtɛ oti a kĩ edo yĩ a lɛmɛ.” Odi Nyɛ Odi Kĩ Ɛtáyidza Ɛdza Nɛ Boe Akũ Ni, Etomɔ Boe.

english_verse: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a kɔdabu a mɔ́ Yesu a, akũ dzuli be, ade biwula atsɛ bɛnawɔ̃na ye a.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What are you arguing about?”
translation_verse: Yesu via ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Litsa fɔlɛtsa milamɔ kavɛlɛ mɔ be?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “O! Nviã babi kĩ milá katɛkado! Maana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ daa? Lɔkɔ lɔɔdabe mado ɔwɔlɛ nvɛlɛ mi nnaawo? Mikpã kanyolebi a mima yĩ!”

english_verse: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu via ɔlɛga kĩ, “Lɔkɔ ɔdabe fuyi ye kayɔ?” Ɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kito anɛ ye tibisɛ̃ kamɛ.

english_verse: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a ɔlɛga bɔa fɔwɔla kĩ, “Ndeĩ katɛkado, lɛmɛ kɛpɔ́nɛ, bumɔ yĩ nna katɛkado mbu!”

english_verse: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ́ kĩ bati kɔdabu a kadu kafuã be a, ewũni nɛ hũhũ kpa a akũ kĩ, “Nɔ hũhũ kpa ɔtɔsĩnɛ mɔ omumu, mabi nɔ kĩ nɛ nɛ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a kamɛ, tabuki ɔyaawo nɛ ke kamɛ dadaada!”

english_verse: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
translation_verse: Hũhũ kpa a bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba, efufulinya kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a mɔ ɔsĩ, ɛbɔɛ ke emuni nɛ tite, ade ɛnɛ́ nɛ ke kamɛ a. Kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a kɛna ndɛ kaku kiku, foesũ bati a pii lɛ kĩ, “Eku!”

english_verse: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu pi kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a nɛ kɔkpɔ ade kiyidza kɛdza a.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Bɛláapuli bɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kĩĩ odu, mbɔɛntɛ bɛtsãmɛna foe mpaɛbɔ kamɛ.” Yesu Buki Ɛlɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Ye Wuda Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a dzakũ nɛ awã bɛnatsã Galilea. Yesu táwɛ kĩ odima anyi awã kĩ ɛnaa,

english_verse: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ ɛkatsa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a fɔtsa. Ebi be kĩ, “Baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ bati awɛ ade baakɔ ye a, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde liti a, yaayidza edzi nkpã.”

english_verse: The disciples did not understand what Jesus meant, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtánu lɛtsa kĩ ebi be a kayɔ, ade bivila kĩ bavia ye a. Owei Nyɛ Oti a Kĩ Ɛbɛ̃ Flee a?

english_verse: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ nɔ kɔbɔa akpã nɔ kado nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ ni, tsua koe kamuni. Fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ kĩ odeĩ kɔbɔa mɔa ɔna nkpã mɔawɛ a, kɛba kĩ odeĩ abɔa nviã ade baata nɔ bido nɛ fi kamɛ a.

english_verse: The worms there never die, and the fire never stops burning.
translation_verse: ‘Awã kĩ batebi a kĩ bawo be a láku dadaada, ade fi a kĩ fɔapila be a lɛmɛ ládĩ da a.’

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
translation_verse: Bɛyawo Kapernaum ɔmatɔ a kamɛ, ade kĩ biwo nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ a, Yesu via ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Litsa kalɛ mivɛlɛ nɛ osi a?”

english_verse: They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtákpa ye mbuayɛ, kitonɛkĩ nɛ osi akũ a, bɛkavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ owula kĩ ɛbɛ̃ flee nɛ be kamɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
translation_verse: Yesu kɛna akũ edzi ɛvɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a, ebi be kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ lɔtɔ ɛnya ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛnya bati a flee nkaɛ mɔ ɔsumunɛ.”

english_verse: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
translation_verse: “Lɛdɛmɛ nɛnɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ de ɔmɛnɛ nɛ́ nɛ de kamɛ ni, nde aakɛna obuki ɔkɔa ɔmɛnɛ odo nɛ de kamɛ? Foesũ mina tɔkɔba kakɛna lɛdɛmɛ nɛ mi kamɛ, lɔkɔa midzi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ mɔ akũ akũ.”

english_verse: John said, “Teacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.”
translation_verse: Ade Yohanes bi Yesu kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bɔmɔ́ onyole odi kĩ ɛkablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa nɛ nɔ dĩ akũ, ade bubi ye kĩ ɛfɔ̃, kitonɛkĩ ɛláa boe kamɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitasua ye osi, kitonɛkĩ oti kĩ yakɛna awãwã fɔtsa nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, ɛláadani ɛlɛ kalɛ kpa nɛ yĩ akũ.

english_verse: Anyone who isn't against us is for us.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaákɛna lɛtsama katia boe ni, ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ boe liti.

english_verse: And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded. Temptations To Sin (Matthew
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔkɔali a mabi mi kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaakpa mi mui mimɔa kĩ minyɛ yĩ bade sũ ni, yaana ye kɔtɔkase kokooko.” Bati Kakpã Kado Nɛ Tɔkpa Kamɛ.

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ oti odi aafɔ̃ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ kĩĩ kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ ɛtɛ yĩ edo lɛyɛla ye katɛkado ni, fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ fɔkpa oti a ntɛ bɛkɔa kɔwɔlɛ bɛvɛla nɛ ye sɔmɔ, bɛta ye bido nɛ mpo kamɛ.

english_verse: So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out.
translation_verse: “Foesũ ntɛ nɔ awɛ afɔ̃ akɛna tɔkpa ni, tsua ye kamuni. Fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ fɔkpa nɔ kĩ aakɔa awɛ mɔa owo nɛ nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna kamɛ, kɛba kĩ odeĩ awɛ nviã lɔkɔa bɛkɔa nɔ bido nɛ fi luwotu kĩ laádĩ a kamɛ.

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: Ntɛ nɔ linebi akpã nɔ kado nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ ni, bɔlɛ de kanyɛ. Fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa nɔ kĩ aakɔa linebi mɔa owo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a, kɛba kĩ odeĩ banebi nviã lɛmɛ baata nɔ bido nɛ fi luwotu kamɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa Yudea amantam kamɛ mɔ Yordan Ɔwɔ liti. Bati kɔdabu buki bɛyatsɛna nɛ ye akũ nɛ awã, ade ɛtsa be fɔtsa ndɛ ayekĩ yawɛ kakɛna a.

english_verse: A woman who divorces her husband and marries again is also unfaithful.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ otsole fɔnɛ ɔnyɔ awɛ mɔ ɛnadzi mɔ onyole bɛbã ni, ye malo ɛbɔa adɔpaa.” Yesu Hila Babisɛ̃ Kubii.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ badi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɛwɛ kĩ basɔ ye bɛdã, foesũ bivia Yesu kĩ, “Boe mbla a kpa osi kĩ onyole esĩ ɔtsɔ̃?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
translation_verse: Yesu kɔa kavia ɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mbla adabe Mose kpa mi?”

english_verse: They answered, “Moses allows a man to write out divorce papers and send his wife away.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mose kpa osi kĩ onyole ɛtsɛlɛ kasĩ ovoli ɛkpa ye kanyi lɔkɔa esĩ ye.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Moses gave you this law because you are so heartless.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mose tsɛlɛ mbla kĩĩ nɛ mi atɔ ɔsĩ sũ.

english_verse: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
translation_verse: Kito katinya kayɔkayi a, onyole mɔ otsole Baguma kɛna.

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ onyole aafɔnɛ ɔlɛga mɔ ɔma awɛ ɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ɔtsɔ̃,

english_verse: He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one.
translation_verse: lɔkɔa be nviã a baakɛna oti ɔmɔa. Foesũ bibuki bɛnyɛ́ bati nviã, mbom bɛkɛna oti mɔa.

english_verse: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Foesũ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma boomu ni, otidziwa ɛtasɛ̃ foe kamɛ!”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kĩ bɛya nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye fɔlɛtsa nɛ fukĩ ɛlɛ a akũ.

english_verse: He told them, “A man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Onyole kĩ esĩ ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ ebuki ɛkɔã ɔbɛbã ni, ɛbɔa adɔpaa nɛ ɔtsɔ̃ akũ.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, bati badi kpã babisɛ̃ bɛma Yesu kĩ ete awɛ nɛ be akũ, lɛmɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kã be anɛ.

english_verse: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali a mabi mi kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtátɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a ndɛ obisɛ̃ aye ni, ɛláawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ da.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ fukĩĩ a ni, ɛna kalɔkpa ade ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ babisɛ̃ bɛya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mitasua be osi, kitonɛkĩ ade bati kĩĩ odu lá Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”
translation_verse: Yesu dã ye kɛnaana nɛ ɔdɔ kamɛ, ade ebi ye kĩ, “Kite nɔ lɛtsa mɔa. Naa kanaafila fɔtsa a kĩ odeĩ a flee kanɔ, kakɔa sika a kakpa ahiãfɔ, lɔkɔa aadani ɔkɛna osikani nɛ ode. Ke liti a, ya kayaatomɔ yĩ!”

english_verse: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Kĩ onyole a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, emuna anɛ ade ɛdzakũ mɔ ayɔlɔhɔ a, kitonɛkĩ ɛna akũ.

english_verse: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Yesu dã ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ɛtsɛna ade ebi be kĩ, “Fɔ́ana ɔsĩ paa fɔkpa osikafɔ kĩ baawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ dzuli ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a akũ. Lɛmɛ Yesu buki ebi be kĩ, “Yĩ babi, Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ kawo deĩ ɔsĩ paa fɔkpa asikafɔ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Fɔ́adã bɔɛɛ fɔkpa ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kapu kĩ yaatsã ɔnɔbɔ̃ ɔkɛ kamɛ ɛnɛ kɛba kĩ osikani aawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a kɛna fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wãwã paa. Ade bɛkavia akũ kĩ, “Lɔkɔa owei koũ aapuli ɛna nkpã katɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu dã be kɛnanaana ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Batidziwa sɛkɛ̃ mɔ fɔláapuli fɔya, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ mɔ Baguma, kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa adã bɔɛɛ lɛkpa Baguma.”

english_verse: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
translation_verse: Finyaa Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yɛlɛ banaa Yerusalem. Yesu nyaa be lɔtɔ ade fɔkɛna ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a saa a, bati a kĩ bitomɔ be nɛ liti a kavila. Yesu buki ɛvɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a ɛnamɛna ɔɔwɛ̃ ade ɛkabi be lɛtsa a kĩ laaya nɛ ye akũ a.

english_verse: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Mitse! Bɔanaa Yerusalem awã kĩ baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a awɛ. Baakɔ ye kɔtɔ bɛkpa wuda lɔkɔa bɛkɔa ye bido nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ awɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked them what they wanted,
translation_verse: Yesu via be kĩ, “Litsa fɔanɛ?”

english_verse: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Bibi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ ɔnɛ odzi nɛ nɔ lɛgãkpo akũ nɛ nɔ kukũkpalɛ kamɛ ni, bɔawɛ kĩ budzi nɛ nɔ ɔwɛ̃, boe kamɛ ɔmɔa nɛ nɔ sɔmɔna, ɔmɔa lɛmɛ nɛ nɔ sɛbã.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Minyína lɛtsa kĩ de milamɛna kavia a! Mitɛ mido kĩ míapuli mimɔa lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ mmɔa? Ĩye míapuli mitɛ mui kawɔla a kĩ maatɛ a?”

english_verse: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
translation_verse: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bɔ́apuli.” Yesu bi be kĩ, “Oo, míapuli mimɔa kɔɔpu a kĩ maamɔa a, mɔ mui kawɔla amaniɛ a kĩ maatɛ a.

english_verse: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nlá osi kĩ mapau oti a kĩ ye aadzi nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna ĩye sɛbã. Baguma nyɛ oti a kĩ yaakɔa foe ɛkpa bati a kĩ ɛmanɛ foe eyi a.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua nkaɛ nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, bɛna kalɔkpa nɛ Yakobo mɔ Yohanes akũ.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu vɛ be ɛkpɔta ebi be kĩ, “Minyina kĩ bati a kĩ babu kĩ bɛnya tɔnɔkɔɛ̃ nɛ amamufɔ akũ a, deĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ be akũ, ade banɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ deĩ otumi nɛ be akũ a.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mi kamɛ odi wɛ kĩ yanya ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛnya be nkaɛ a ɔsumunɛ.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a malo táya kĩ bisum ye, mbom ɛya kĩ yasum bati ɛkɔa ye nkpã ɛkpa lɔkɔa ɛtɛ bati pii nkpã.” Yesu Tsa Bartimeo Ɔnɛkunɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yawo Yeriko. Kĩ banɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, bati kɔdabu baũ tomɔ be. Kĩ banaa nɛ osi a, bɛmɔ́ ɔnɛkunɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Bartimeo, Timeo obi kĩ edzi nɛ osi ɔɔwɛ̃ yawã fɔtsa.

english_verse: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Kĩ enu kĩ Yesu Nazaretni a lɛyaatɛ̃ a, edo fɔwɔla kabɔa awɛ kĩ, “Yesu! Dawid obi! Mɔ yĩ kɔnya!”

english_verse: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Bati pii wũni nɛ ye akũ kĩ esua onukpɛ̃. Lɛmɛ ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛba kĩ, “Dawid obi! Mɔ yĩ kɔnya!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
translation_verse: Yesu fɔ̃ ɛdza ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mivɛ ye mikpa yĩ.” Foesũ bɛvɛ ɔnɛkunɛ kĩĩ bibi ye kĩ, “Fɔ̃ ɔwɔlɛ ɛka edzi nɔ! Yidza mɔ atsɛ, yavɛ nɔ.”

english_verse: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
translation_verse: Awã naa ɛta ye atadiɛ wulaa eyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ eyidza ɛdza, ɛnaa Yesu sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
translation_verse: Yesu via ye kĩ, “Litsa awɛ kĩ nkɛna nkpa nɔ?” Ɔnɛkunɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, fɔ̃ mbuki mmɔ́ fɔkɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Naa. Nɔ katɛkado tsa nɔ.” Deedimɔapɛ ye anɛ binya ɛmɔ́ fɔkɔ, ade etomɔ Yesu nɛ osi a akũ a.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu wo Yerusalem a, ɛnaa Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, ade eko anɛ ɛdã lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa ɛtɛ̃mɛna a. Lɛmɛ kĩ kade lamɛna kadɔ sũ ni, bɛmɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ewua-nviã dzakũ bɛnaa Betania. Yesu Dumu Kutse Kudi Kĩ Kɔtáwũ Babi.

english_verse: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ kiwo olobe lɔkɔ a, Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Nɛ Mpaɛbɔ Mɔ Katɛkado Akũ.

english_verse: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: ade bivia ye kĩ, “Kɔbɛ̃ ɔdabe ɔnɛɛ akɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ? Owei lɛkpa nɔ osi kĩ kɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ?”

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi via mi kĩ, ‘Nde sũ miawũnya ye’ ni, mibi ye kĩ mi Ɔlate sĩ ye, ade yaakɔa ye ɛmɛna mɔatsɛ a.”

english_verse: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
translation_verse: Foesũ bati a naa, ade bɛnamɔ kĩ bido aflim obi ɔwɛ̃ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ ɔblɔnu akũ nɛ kɔla onyukpɛ̃. Ade kĩ bɛkawũnya ye nɛ ɔwɛ̃ kamɛ a,

english_verse: some of the people standing there asked, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
translation_verse: bati badi kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã a via be kĩ, “Litsa miakɛna nɛ awã? Litsa sũ miawũnya aflim obi a?”

english_verse: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
translation_verse: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Yesu bi be a, ade bati a kpa be osi bɛnyɛ ye a.

english_verse: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkɔa ɔyaɛ a bɛma Yesu a, bɛdzɛlɛ be tilima biko nɛ ye akũ, ade Yesu de edzi nɛ ye akũ a.

english_verse: In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ mɔ bikĩ bitomɛna ye a do fɔwɔla kabɔa kalɛ kĩ, “Hosiana! Mimu Baguma! Baguma hila oti a kĩ yaya nɛ Ɔlate a dĩ akũ!

english_verse: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
translation_verse: Baguma hila Boe Ɔlɛga Dawid lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kĩ kaya a! Hosiana deĩ nɛ ode flee flee!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
translation_verse: Basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a nu fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lɛ a, foesũ bɛwɛ osi a kĩ baatsã bɛkɔ ye. Lɛmɛ bivila ye kitonɛkĩ ye fɔtsa katsa a nyɛnɛ bati kɔdabu a flee akũ.

english_verse: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi kutse a kĩ, “Odimanɔ lɛláabuki ɛnya nɔ babi dadaada!” Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nu foe. Yesu Blɔmɛna Kɔyaatɔ Banyanɛ Nɛ Baguma Asɔli Kɔla a Kamɛ.

english_verse: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ɛtsa bati a fɔtsa ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Baavɛ yĩ Asɔli Kɔla kĩ kamɛ katinya kamɛ bati flee mpaɛ kabɔakɔ̃.’ Lɛmɛ midani koe mikɛna bayekpanɛ kabɛlakɔ̃!”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitɛ Baguma mido.

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples reached Jerusalem, he went into the temple and began chasing out everyone who was selling and buying. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and the benches of those who were selling doves.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Yerusalem a, Yesu naa Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ ade edo bati a kĩ bɛkanɔ fɔtsa, mɔ bikĩ bɛkaya fɔtsa a kablɔmɛna kanɛmɛna awɛ a. Edu sika babɛ̃nɛ a tupunu ewula, ade edu bikĩ bidzi banɔ mblɔduma a lɛmɛ fukpo ewula a.

english_verse: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
translation_verse: Ade ɛtákpa osi malo kĩ odima abɔɛ lɛtsama ɛtsã kɔla kamɛ ɛtɛ̃mɛna a.

english_verse: Everything you ask for in prayer will be yours, if you only have faith.
translation_verse: Foesũ mabi mi kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míavia Baguma nɛ mpaɛbɔ kamɛ ni, mitɛ mido kĩ mina foe, ade Baguma aakpa mi foe a.

english_verse: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ mitákɔa bati tɔkpa mite be, mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a lɛmɛ lɛláakɔa mi tɔkpa a ete mi!” Bati Via Yesu Ye Kɔbɛ̃ Katokɔ̃.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a buki bɛnaa Yerusalem a. Kĩ Yesu nɛ ɛtsɛ̃ yamini nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃, mbla batsanɛ a mɔ ɔmatɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ badi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃,

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Maanya lɔtɔ nvia mi kalɛ mɔapɛ. Ntɛ mikpa ke mbuayɛ ni, maabi mi kɔbɛ̃ a odu kĩ nnɛɛ makɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Mibi yĩ Yohanes bati mui kawɔla a ni, Baguma sɛkɛ̃ futo mbɔɛɛ kĩ batidziwa sɛkɛ̃ futo? Mikpa yĩ mbuayɛ.”

english_verse: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Biyi fukĩĩ kavɛlɛ nɛ be kamɛ awɛ ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Litsa bɔ́alɛ? Ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ kɔbɛ̃ a to Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ni, yaavia boe kĩ, ‘Kekɔ̃ litsa sũ mitátɛ Yohanes mido?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.” They were afraid of the crowd
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ bɔ́alɛ kĩ futo batidziwa sɛkɛ̃?” Lɛmɛ bavila bati kɔdabu a, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi abu Yohanes kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ.

english_verse: and told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.”
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛkpa Yesu mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bunyína.” Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Yĩ malo nláabi mi awã kĩ kɔbɛ̃ a to nnɛɛ makɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ!”

english_verse: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
translation_verse: Foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ oti ɛwɛ Baguma kalɛ mɔ ye ɔwɔlɛ flee, ye adzuni flee mɔ ye kɔbɛ̃ flee. Ade kɛnɛ kĩ ɛwɛ ye ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ yawɛ ye akũ kalɛ a. Fɔnɛnɛ kĩ oti ɛkɛna nɛ tɛbla nviã kĩĩ akũ kɛba kĩ yaabɔa bayaɛ mɔ afɔliɛ bɛbã ɛkpa Baguma.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a, Yesu via bati a kĩ, “Litsa waĩ kɔba ɔlate a aakɛna? Yaaya ɛyaakɔ apaafɔ a ɛkɔa waĩ kɔba a edo nɛ bati bɛbã awɛ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, Mose tsɛlɛ mbla kĩĩ eyi ɛkpa boe kĩ ntɛ onyole odi ku ɛfɔnɛ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ bɛmɔ ye bɛtáma ni, ɔwaɛ̃nyole ɛkɔã okunawa a, lɔkɔa bɛma babi bɛkpa ɔtɔnyade a.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, he sent a servant to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Kĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ baana babi a, onyole a do ye ɔsumunɛ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ kĩ ɛnaatɛ ye kanyakɔ̃ ɛma ye.

english_verse: The renters grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him away without a thing.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ye apaafɔ a pi fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bibo ye ade bɛblɔmɔ ye mɔ awɛ pupulidza a.

english_verse: Then the man sent another servant, and they killed him. He kept sending servant after servant. They beat some of them and killed some.
translation_verse: Kɔba ɔlate a buki edo fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã, adeke bɛkɔ ye a. Ade aye bɛkɛna babɛbã pii a. Bibo badi, bɛkɔ babɛbã lɛmɛ.

english_verse: The owner had a son he loved very much. Finally, he sent his son to the renters because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: “Oti kĩ ete kĩ yaado nyɛ ye mɔawɛ obi kĩ yaátunɔ kalɛ. Nɛ kawũna kamɛ a, edo ye obi a kĩ yawɛ kalɛ paa a, ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Matɛ kado kĩ baakpa yĩ obi kĩĩ kɔ̃ obu.’

english_verse: But they said to themselves, “Someday he will own this vineyard. Let's kill him! That way we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ kĩĩ bi be akũ kĩ, ‘Ade adzapadiɛ ɔnyanɛ a nɛ awĩ a. Miya bɔkɔ ye ayekĩ ye adzapadiɛ a aakɛna boe ade!’

english_verse: So they grabbed the owner's son, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Foesũ, bipi obi a bɛkɔ ye, bɛta bimuni nɛ waĩ kɔbã a liti.”

english_verse: This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’ ”
translation_verse: Baguma lɛkɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ, ade fɔnyɛ awãwã fɔtsa nɛ boe anɛ a!’ ”

english_verse: The leaders knew that Jesus was really talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him. But because they were afraid of the crowd, they let him alone and left. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a wɛ kĩ bapi Yesu, kitonɛkĩ bɛmɔ́ kĩ ɛbɔa lɛgbã a etia be, lɛmɛ bivila bati kɔdabu a, foesũ bɛfɔnɛ ye ade bɛdzakũ a. Bivia Yesu Ɔgɔlɛ Kate Akũ Fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu mɔ́ be tɛdake a, foesũ evia be kĩ, “Nde sũ miawɛ kĩ miasɔ yĩ midã? Mikɔa sika a miyamɛna ndã!”

english_verse: You surely know about people being raised to life. You know that in the story about Moses and the burning bush, God said, “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ banɔkpa kayidza akũ ni, mitákã nɛ Mose ovoli a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ nɛ kutse a kĩ kɔkatɛ̃ a akũ? Baguma bi Mose kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Abraham Baguma, Isak Baguma mɔ Yakob Baguma.’

english_verse: The Pharisees got together with Herod's followers. Then they sent some men to trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bido Farisifɔ badi mɔ bikĩ bitomɛna Herodes a kĩ bɛnaa Yesu sɛkɛ̃, bɛnaanaĩ ye nɛ tɛdake kamɛ mɔ fɔlɛtsa kavia bɛkɔa bipi ye.

english_verse: They went to him and said, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. And you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ a, bibi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bunyina kĩ alɛ ɔnɔkɔali daa, ade ɔtsɛ́la lɛtsa a kĩ bati akɔna nɛ nɔ akũ a. Aádã oti anɛ, mbom atsa Baguma akũ ɔnɔkɔali osi a. Bi boe ntɛ boe Mbla kpa osi kĩ bute ɔgɔlɛ bɔkpa Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃. Bute mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔtate?”

english_verse: They brought him a silver coin, and he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa sɔmɔa bɛma ye, ade evia be kĩ, “Owei lɔlɔ mɔ dĩ teĩ nɛ se akũ?” Awã naa bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.” The men were amazed at Jesus. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ mikɔa lɛtsa kĩ lɛnyɛ Kaisare lide a mikpa ye, ade lɛtsa kĩ lɛnyɛ Baguma lide a lɛmɛ ni, mikɔa mikpa Baguma.” Fukĩĩ fɔ̃ akũ nyɛnɛ be nɛ Yesu akũ. Fɔlɛtsa Kavia Nɛ Kayidza Kato Kanɔkpa Akũ.

english_verse: He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. You Sadducees are all wrong. The Most Important Commandment (Matthew
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ atsa kĩ ɛnyɛ bati kĩ bidzi nkpã Baguma, nnɛ́ banɔkpa Baguma yanɛ. Mikɛna mfumsu paa!” Mbla Wɔdabe Nyɛ Atɔnyade Flefle?

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and pray long prayers just to show off. They will be punished most of all. A Widow's Offering (Luke
translation_verse: Basisi bakunawa katɛ be tɔtɔ nɛ be awɛ. Babɔa mpaɛ gɛlɛɛlɛ kakɔa kapia be akũ. Be sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ aana ɔsĩ sɛba bati bɛbã fude.” Okunawa Ohiãni Odi Te Ɔgɔlɛ.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Ade Zadukifɔ kĩ balɛ kĩ bati kayidza kato kanɔkpa lɛláa a kamɛ badi ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃, bɛyavia ye kĩ,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mikɛna mfumsu, kitonɛkĩ minyína Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a fɔlɛtsa ĩye Baguma kɔbɛ̃ a.

english_verse: When Jesus saw that the man had given a sensible answer, he told him, “You are not far from God's kingdom.” After this, no one dared ask Jesus any more questions. About David's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ kĩ onyole a kpa ye mbuayɛ nɛ nyansã kamɛ a ni, ebi ye kĩ, “Mimɔ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kayite lɛlá lugo.” Nɛ fukĩĩ liti a, odimanɔ tápuli edo ɔwɔlɛ evia Yesu kalɛ kadima. Fɔlɛtsa Kavia Nɛ Oti a Kĩ Ɛnyɛ Kristo a Akũ.

english_verse: One of the teachers of the Law of Moses came up while Jesus and the Sadducees were arguing. When he heard Jesus give a good answer, he asked him, “What is the most important commandment?”
translation_verse: Ade Yudani mbla ɔtsanɛ odi kĩ ɛna nɛ awã a, nu fɔlɛtsa kamɛ kasa a. Ɛmɔ́ kĩ Yesu kpa Zadukifɔ a mbuayɛ wĩ, foesũ efuã Yesu evia ye kĩ, “Mbla wɔdabe nyaa Baguma tɛbla a flee lɔtɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The most important one says: ‘People of Israel, you have only one Lord and God.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bɛtsɛlɛ mbla a kĩ ɛbɛ̃ a bɛtsa kĩ, ‘Israelfɔ, mitse! Ɔlate a nyɛ boe Baguma, ade ye odi lɛmɛ deĩ a.

english_verse: You must love him with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength.’
translation_verse: Foesũ awɛ Ɔlate, nɔ Baguma a, mɔ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ flee, mɔ nɔ ɔkla flee, mɔ nɔ adzuni flee mɔ nɔ kɔbɛ̃ flee.’

english_verse: The second most important commandment says: ‘Love others as much as you love yourself.’ No other commandment is more important than these.”
translation_verse: Ade mbla sĩwa ayade nyɛ kĩ, ‘Wɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ akũ a.’ Mbla adima buki ɛbɛ́ tɛbla kĩĩ.”

english_verse: Jesus was sitting in the temple near the offering box and watching people put in their gifts. He noticed that many rich people were giving a lot of money.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu nadzi nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ efuã ɔgɔlɛ kate adakã a, ɛkadã ayekĩ bati lamɔ sika kata kado a. Asikafɔ pii ta sika baũ baũ bido nɛ ye kamɛ.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had. Now she doesn't have a cent to live on.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati bɛbã a kɔa akũ kana fɔtsa a kĩ fute a fudi bɛyate ɔgɔlɛ, lɛmɛ otsole ohiãni kosokoso kĩĩ kɔ̃, lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ a flee ɛkɔa ete. Ɛkɔa lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ yaadzi nkpã a ɛyate ɔgɔlɛ.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the teachers of the Law of Moses say the Messiah will come from the family of King David?
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu katsa fɔtsa nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ a, evia be kalɛ kĩ, “Nde fɔla sũ mbla batsanɛ a aapuli bɛlɛ kĩ Kristo a aanya Lɛgã Dawid kasinu obi?

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be his son?” The large crowd enjoyed listening to Jesus teach. Jesus Condemns the Pharisees and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ Lɛgã Dawid mɔawɛ vɛ ye kĩ Ɔlate a ni, nde fɔ́apuli fɔya kĩ Kristo a aanya Dawid kasinu obi?” Bati kɔdabu baũ dza nɛ awã bɛkatse Yesu fɔlɛtsa a mɔ kawɔlɛ. Yesu Dɛ Bati Nɛ Mbla Batsanɛ a Akũ.

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching, he said: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes and be greeted in the market.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu katsa bati a fɔtsa a ebi be kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ nɛ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a akũ. Bado tɛtadiɛ wulaa katsã kawɛ kĩ bati bɛwɔ̃na be nɛ obu kamɛ nɛ kɔyaatɔ.

english_verse: They like the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: Bawɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ kadzi nɛ be fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ mɔ obu fudzikɔ̃ kadzi nɛ kanyakɔ̃.

english_verse: Finally, a poor widow came up and put in two coins worth only a few pennies.
translation_verse: Adeke okunawa ohiãni odi lɛmɛ ya ade ɛkɔa kaplɛ nviã edo nɛ adakã a kamɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to gather around him. Then he said: I tell you that this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a Yesu vɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a ɛkpɔta ebi be kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali mabi mi kĩ okunawa ohiãni kĩĩ lɛtsa a kĩ edo nɛ ɔgɔlɛ adakã kamɛ a pɔnɛ fɔbɛ̃ bati a flee fude.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look at these beautiful stones and wonderful buildings!”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nɛ eto Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ yanaa a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa bi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, dã fɔla mɔ kalɛ mɔ boe kpale kĩĩ!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these huge buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Amɔ fɔla kpale kpale kĩĩ. Bɛlátina de a kamɛ lidima nɛ lɔkɔba akũ kĩ bɛláadu biwula! Baatinya de lidi nyɛ lidi biwula.” Lubokamɔ Mɔ Bati Liti Katomɛna.

english_verse: Jesus answered: Watch out and don't let anyone fool you!
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ, ade mitafɔ̃ odima lɛkɛ mi a.

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will use my name and fool many people.
translation_verse: Bati pii aaya nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ bibi mi kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Kristo a!’ ade baakɛ bati pii a.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him in private.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nɛ edzi nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ, ɛtsɛlamɔ Asɔli Kɔla a, Petro, mɔ Yakobo, mɔ Yohanes mɔ Andrea ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɛyavia ye pɛtɛɛ kĩ,

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” where it should not be. Everyone who reads this must try to understand! If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ mimɔ́ fukesĩtsa mɔ libe kĩ fɔakɔ ɔmaĩ kĩ fɔdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kĩ kɛtánɛ kĩ fɔna a ni, ovoli ɔkãnɛ a enu foe kayɔ kanu wĩ, lɔkɔa bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yudea a bilo bɛnaa tɔtɔ akũ.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and threats of wars, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Ɔwɔlɛ ɛtabɔlɛ mi ntɛ minu kawũkanɔ nkã nɛ mi ɔwɛ̃ ĩye kawũkanɔ akũ fɔlɛtsa mɔ lugo. Kɛnɛ kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ odu fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ, lɛmɛ fɔátsa kĩ katinya kamɛ kawũna a wo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: This will be the worst time of suffering since God created the world, and nothing this terrible will ever happen again.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lubo kamɔ a kĩ laaya nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ aana ɔsĩ lɛba lidi nyɛ lidi kĩ lɛya tɔ nɛ katinya kamɛ, kito nɛ kayɔkayi kĩ Baguma bɔa katinya a kɛyawo finyaa. Adeke lubo kamɔ kĩĩ odu lɛláabuki lɛya dadaada a.

english_verse: They asked, “When will these things happen? What will be the sign that they are about to take place?”
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate, lɔkɔ wɔdabe fukĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ? Bi boe fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́aya fɔkɔa fɔtsa kĩ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ.”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. There will be earthquakes in many places, and people will starve to death. But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Ɔmaĩ mɔ ɔmaĩ aanɔ akũ. Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ aayidza nɛ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ akũ. Tite aakuna nɛ fɔkɔ kumɛkumɛ, ade kɔlɛ aaya a. Lɛmɛ fukĩĩ flee la ndɛ sɛmɛ kapi kayɔkayi lubo kamɔ a aye.

english_verse: Be on your guard! You will be taken to courts and beaten with whips in their synagogues. And because of me, you will have to stand before rulers and kings to tell about your faith.
translation_verse: “Kɛnɛ kĩ mi mɔawɛ a miana nɛ kɔnɛte kamɛ. Baapili mi lɔkɔa bɛkɔa mi bɛnamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃. Baabo mi nɛ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃. Baafɔ̃ midza nɛ fɔgã mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ anɛ nɛ yĩ sũ, lɔkɔa mibi be Kalɛ Wĩ a.

english_verse: But before the end comes, the good news must be preached to all nations.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔmɔ katinya kawũna a aaya ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ bide lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ wĩ a akũ bɛkpa fude fude a flee.

english_verse: When you are arrested, don't worry about what you will say. You will be given the right words when the time comes. But you will not really be the ones speaking. Your words will come from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bipi mi bɛkɔa mi bɛnamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ ni, mitanya lɔtɔ mibo akũ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ a akũ. Ntɛ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ ni, milɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ baakpa mi kĩ milɛ a. Kitonɛkĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ a lɛláanya mi fude, mbom fɔ́ato Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray each other and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: “Bati aakɔa be mɔawɛ bawaɛ̃ bɛkpa kĩ bɛkɔ, ade balɛga lɛmɛ aakɛna foe mɔapɛ a nɛ babi akũ. Babi aayidza bɛdza nɛ be bamanɛ akũ, bɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔ be.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved. The Horrible Thing (Matthew
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi aakesĩ mi nɛ yĩ sũ. Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaadza pintii kɛnaawo ye nkpã kawũna ni, Baguma aakpa ye nkpã.” Yesu Tsa Ayekĩ Bikĩ Bitomɔ Ye a Aamɔ Lubo Mɔ Liti Katomɛna.

english_verse: If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything.
translation_verse: Oti kĩ edzi nɛ ye kɔla lɛtsã a, ɛtaduma ɛya nɛ tite kĩ yaabɔɛ litsedi etomɛna ye kɔla kamɛ ɛkpa ye akũ.

english_verse: If the Lord doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of his chosen and special ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Ɔlate a tinya lubo kamɔ fuwi a akũ. Ntɛ nɛ aye ni, kufɛ odima lɛláana nkpã. Lɛmɛ nɛ ye bati a kĩ ɛpau a sũ ni, etinya fuwi a akũ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Oti kĩ edeĩ nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ ɛtamuniki ɛnaa kɔla kĩ yanaabɔɛ ye lima.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Bɔbɔɛ makpa batsole a kĩ bɛtsɛmɔ fɔma mɔ bikĩ bɛnɛɛ babi kĩ bamɛmɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ.

english_verse: Pray that it won't happen in winter.
translation_verse: Mibɔa mpaɛ mikpa Baguma kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ fɔtaya nɛ osili lɔkɔ!

english_verse: False messiahs and false prophets will come and work miracles and signs. They will even try to fool God's chosen ones.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ fɔvã Kristo amɛ, mɔ fɔvã Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ aanɛ. Baakɛna awãwã fɔtsa mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni pii kĩ ntɛ baapuli ni, baakɔa bɛkɛ bati a kĩ Baguma pau a malo.

english_verse: But be on your guard! That's why I am telling you these things now. When the Son of Man Appears (Matthew
translation_verse: Mi kɔ̃ midã kadã wĩ! Mbi mi lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa ĩnyamɛna lɔtɔ fɔmɔ lɔkɔ a aawo ɔtɔ.” Otidziwa Obi a Kaya.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know that the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Ade aye ntɛ mimɔ́ kĩ fɔtsa a kĩĩ do kaya awɛ pɛ, minyi kĩ lɔkɔ a fuã, ade liti liwo onyukpɛ̃ malo a.

english_verse: The sky and the earth will not last forever, but my words will. No One Knows the Day or Time (Matthew
translation_verse: Tite mɔ ode flee aayatɛ̃, lɛmɛ yĩ kalɛ a kɔ̃ lɛláatɛ̃ da.

english_verse: The stars will fall, and the powers in the sky will be shaken.”
translation_verse: Fusibii aato ode fɔtɔ, ade odetɔ́ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ kupini ode a aakuna akũ.’

english_verse: Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near.
translation_verse: “Mikɔlɛ fɔtsa nɛ kutse kĩ kɔawũ babi a akũ. Ntɛ fɔya kĩ mimɔ́ kĩ kutse a na nɛ́ tɛnanɛ kɔnyɛ tɔɔta vɔɛ̃ ni, minyi kĩ ovio lɔkɔ fuã.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
translation_verse: “Ke liti a, baamɔ Otidziwa Obi a, kĩ ɛnɛ yaya nɛ liflututu kamɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ mɔ kukũkpalɛ baũ.

english_verse: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Adeke yaado ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kĩ bɛnaakpɔta bati a kĩ ɛpau eyi a, bitomɛna katinya kamɛ tukonto ɛna a kamɛ kito tite kakpulakɔ̃ kɛnaawo ode kakpulakɔ̃. Fɔtsa Kakɔlɛ Nɛ Kutse Kĩ Kɔawũ Babi a Akũ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ luwi a ĩye ɔdɔũ a, odima linyína de. Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a mɔ Baguma Obi mɔawɛ a malo linyína, mbɔɛntɛ Ɔlɛga a odi keĩ nyina.

english_verse: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.
translation_verse: “Nɛ foesũ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ minyína lɔkɔ a kĩ tɔtɔ ɔlate a aaya. Fɔ́apuli fɔnya olobe lɔkɔ ĩye lɛnyɛ kayite ĩye odenyinawi ĩye ɔnɔnɛ.

english_verse: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.
translation_verse: Midã kadã wĩ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ minyína luwi a kĩ de akũ lɔkɔ a aawo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali a mabi mi kĩ mi lɛtsã babi a flee lɛláaku fɔmɔ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛyafuta mi ni, ɛtayaamɔ mi kĩ miwaa tida.

english_verse: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
translation_verse: Fɔ́ana ndɛ ayekĩ onyole odi naa osi akũ, ade ɛfɔnɛ ye tɔtɔ eyi ye basumunɛ a. Ɛkpa odi nyɛ odi ye kɔbɛ̃ mɔ adzuma kĩ ɛkɛna. Ke liti a, esi onyukpɛ̃ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a mbla kĩ ɛna nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: I tell everyone just what I have told you. Be alert!
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ mabi mi a, ade foe mɔapɛ a mabi bati flee kĩ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ.”

english_verse: They were saying, “We must not do it during the festival, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Matthew
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ kĩ, “Kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔkɛna foe nɛ fuwi kanya a kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ bati aado ɔtutũ nɛ ɔmaĩ a kamɛ.” Otsole Odi Tsɛla Yesu Kugua Nɛ Betania.

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Teacher!” Then Judas kissed him,
translation_verse: Kĩ Yuda sila ewo awã a, ɛnaa Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ɛvɛ ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ!” ade ɛdzɛmɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together.
translation_verse: Ade bɛkpã Yesu bɛnamɛna Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a tɔtɔ kamɛ, awã kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃, ɔmatɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a kpɔta a.

english_verse: She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial.
translation_verse: Otsole a kɛna lɛtsa kĩ yaapuli. Ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛtsɛla yĩ sukɛna kugua kĩ yamanɛ yĩ eyi ɛkpa yĩ kabila.

english_verse: This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Ade bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã a badi na kalɔkpa bɛlɛ kĩ, “Litsa sũ yabula kugua mɔ kɔya kĩĩ kade?

english_verse: You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others. Judas and the Chief Priests (Matthew
translation_verse: Mabi mi nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ baade Kalɛ Wĩ a lɛtsã nɛ katinya kamɛ flee ni, baalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ lɛtsa kĩ otsole kĩĩ kɛna a akũ bɛkɔa bite ye anɛ.” Yuda Tunɔ Kĩ Yaanyɛ Yesu Ɛkpa.

english_verse: The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a dzakũ bɛnaa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ, ade bɛnamɔ fɔtsa flee ndɛ ayekĩ Yesu bi be a. Ade bɛtɔ Wuda Katɛ̃ funitsã a nɛ awã a.

english_verse: Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yuda Iskariot kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa naa Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a sɛkɛ̃ kĩ yaanyɛ Yesu ɛkpa be.

english_verse: The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.” The Lord's Supper (Matthew
translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a anaaku ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ nɛ ye akũ a. Lɛmɛ bɔbɔɛ makpa oti a kĩ yaanyɛ Otidziwa Obi a ɛkpa a. Ntɛ bɛtama oti a na ni, kufɛ fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa ye.” Boe Ɔlate a Olobe Funitsã Kanya.

english_verse: Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ɛbɔɛ kɔɔpu mɔ waĩ, ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ, ɛkɔa ɛkpa be flee bɛkate kamɔa.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi Petro kĩ, “Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ fɔmɔ ɔkɔkɔ aabe titi nviã lɛnyɛ kĩĩ ni, aalɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ onyína yĩ.”

english_verse: He answered, “It is one of you twelve men who is eating from this dish with me.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɛnyɛ mi ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa, oti a kĩ ye awɛ deĩ nɛ sɔɔdaɛ kamɛ mɔ yĩ a.

english_verse: But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro buki ebi ye mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ, “Ntɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔmɔ nɔ buku malo ni, nláalɛ kĩ ĩnyína nɔ!” Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a flee lɛ kalɛ mɔapɛ a. Yesu Bɔa Mpaɛ Nɛ Getsemane.

english_verse: told them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: ade ɛdani ebi be kĩ, “Ayɔlɔhɔ tɛ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ paa ndɛ maku. Midzi nɛ awĩ, mikaadã fɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, “Father, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nda, yĩ Ɔlɛga, apuli kakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa. Nyɛ lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu kĩĩ nɛ yĩ akũ. Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ lɛtsa a kĩ mawɛ, mbom lɛtsa a kĩ nɔ awɛ.”

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ lɔkɔa mikaabɔa mpaɛ ayekĩ miláawo nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ. Hũhũ a awɛ lɛmɛ sukɛna a kɔ̃ lɛlá kɔbɛ̃.”

english_verse: But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛdzɛla yĩ ndzi nkpã ni, maanya mi lɔtɔ nɛ Galilea.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Ade Petro bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ kɔ̃, nláalo nfɔnɛ nɔ da, ntɛ be nkaɛ a flee lo malo!”

english_verse: and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Foesũ bipi Yesu, ade bipini ye kɔɔba a.

english_verse: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
translation_verse: Yesu buki emuniki ɛnabɔa mpaɛ ɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔapɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
translation_verse: Bɛyawo kakɔ kadi kĩ bavɛ Getsemane, ade Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Midzi nɛ awĩ lɔkɔa nnaabɔa mpaɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter, James, and John. He was sad and troubled and
translation_verse: Ade ɛkpã Petro, Yakobo mɔ Yohanes ebumɔ ye akũ a. Kalɔɛ mɔ libe wo nɛ ye kamɛ, ɛkatila bliblibli,

english_verse: When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, “Are you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour?
translation_verse: Ke liti a, emuniki ɛyamɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ɛlalɛ a kĩ bɛwa tida. Ebi Simon Petro kĩ, “Simon, tida ɔwaa? Ɔtápuli odzi ɔdã fɔkɔ dɔũhuilɛ mɔapɛ malo?”

english_verse: But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
translation_verse: Ebuki emuniki ɛnaa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a sɛkɛ̃ ɛnamɔ kĩ bɛwa tida. Bɛtápuli bibinya anɛ, tida deĩ nɛ be anɛ ade bɛtányi lɛtsa kĩ balɛ bɛnyɛ be akũ onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Kĩ ebuki emuniki ɛya lɛlalɛde a, evia be kĩ, “Tida mibuki miwaa miawɛya? Fɔlɛ! Ɔdɔũ a wo ɔtɔ! Midã, bɛkɔa Otidziwa Obi a, bado nɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ awɛ.

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Miyidza mifɔ̃ bɔnaa. Midã oti a kĩ yaanyɛ yĩ ɛkpa a fuã.” Bipi Yesu.

english_verse: Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kamɛ ɔmɔa vɛlɛ ye kɔpã ɛnyɛ ɛbaa Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a ɔsumunɛ sɔtɔ emuni.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlamɔ kɔdzɛla fɔmɔ Yuda kĩ ɛnyɛ be ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa yanɛ ɛta. Bati kɔdabu a kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ mɔ ɔmatɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ nyɛ bibu a tomɔ ye, ade bɛnɛɛ fɔpamɛ glɛlɛ glɛlɛ mɔ titse fukũ a.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.”
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ ye ɛkpa a kpa be nsɛntsɛlɛni eyi kĩ, “Oti a kĩ míamɔ kĩ ndzɛmɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a ni, ade ye nɛ awã a. Mipi ye mikpã minamɛna, lɔkɔa mi anɛ ɛna nɛ ye akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the mob, “Why do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Fusĩ kĩ mialɛ apã mɔ titse fukũ miya kĩ miaayapi yĩ ndɛ tɔkpa ɔkɛnanɛ manɛ?

english_verse: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
translation_verse: Luwi daa mana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, katsa fɔtsa, lɛmɛ mitápi yĩ. Lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa fɔlɛtsa a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ.”

english_verse: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
translation_verse: Ade ke ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a flee lo bɛfɔnɛ ye a.

english_verse: One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him,
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a kamɛ a, opopobi odi kĩ esua aklala lima odi akũ tomɛna Yesu. Bati a wɛ kĩ bapi ye,

english_verse: he left the cloth behind and ran away naked. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: lɛmɛ ɛwɔlɔ ye lima edo be nɛ awɛ, ade elo mɔ ɔdzɔ a. Yesu Dza Nɛ Yudafɔ Ɔmaĩ Fɔlɛtsa Badzɛnɛ Anɛ.

english_verse: Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said,
translation_verse: Ade bati badi yidza bɛdza bɛma fɔvã kĩ,

english_verse: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Ade Petro sĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ĩnyína lɛtsama, ade maánu lɛtsa a kĩ alɛ a malo a!” Ade ɛnɛ ɛnaa onyukpɛ̃ a. Mɔ ye awã kawo pɛ mɔ ɔkɔkɔ nyɛ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
translation_verse: Petro tomɛna nɛ liti flee, ɛyawo nɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a lɔwabe. Ɛkɛna akũ edzi nɛ basumunɛ a ɔwɛ̃ bɛkawa fi.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council tried to find someone to accuse Jesus of a crime, so they could put him to death. But they could not find anyone to accuse him.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a kĩ bidzi nɛ awã wɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩ batsua bite nɛ Yesu akũ bɛkɔa bɛkɔ ye wuda kɔtɔ, lɛmɛ bɛtána fudima nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, “Why do we need more witnesses?
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a, Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a bɛ̃ ye atadiɛ kamɛ nɛ kalɔkpa kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɔtábuki busĩ ɔdansini odima!

english_verse: This time Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't even know the man you're talking about!”
translation_verse: Ade Petro do kadum mɔ katani awɛ kĩ, “Ntani kĩ ɔnɔkɔali malɛ, Baguma ɛvɛlɛ yĩ sɔtɔ ntɛ fɔvã mama. Ĩnyína oti a kĩ ye fɔlɛtsa milamɛna kalɛ a.”

english_verse: “We heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.”
translation_verse: “Bunu kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Maabubuli Asɔli Kɔla kĩĩ kĩ bike mɔ awɛ a, ade nɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ liti ni, maake kɔbɛbã kĩ nnɛ́ awɛ bɛkɔa bike.’ ”

english_verse: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati kĩĩ adansiɛ malo táketi mɔ akũ.

english_verse: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
translation_verse: Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a yidza ɛdza nɛ bati a flee anɛ, ade evia Yesu kĩ, “Ɔláanyɛ onukpɛ̃ nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlamɔ kalɛ katia nɔ a?”

english_verse: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu sua onukpɛ̃ ɛtálɛ kadima. Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a buki evia ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Kristo, Nhila Ɔlate a Obi a?”

english_verse: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
translation_verse: Minu ye mbusuo fɔlɛtsa a. Litsa nyɛ mi adzuni?” Adeke be flee bitunɔ yɔ kĩ eku kɔtɔ ade ɛfɛta ɛkpa wuda a.

english_verse: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Be kamɛ badi yi kayɔ mɔ tanɛ katua kako nɛ Yesu akũ, badi sua ye anɛ, bɛkake ye fukpeni kavia ye kĩ, “Bi boe owula kĩ ɛbɔa nɔ!” Ade badãmɛnanɛ a kpã Yesu bɛbalɛbalɛ ye awɛ nɛ tɔlɔtɔ a. Petro Sĩ Kĩ Enyína Yesu.

english_verse: The servant girl saw Peter again and said to the people standing there, “This man is one of them!”
translation_verse: Abawa a buki ɛmɔ́ ye nɛ awã, ade edo bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kabi awɛ kĩ, “Ɛnyɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa!”

english_verse: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro buki edzi nɛ lɔwabe a, abawa adi nɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a tɔtɔ kamɛ a ya nɛ awã.

english_verse: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, “You were with Jesus from Nazareth!”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛmɔ Petro kĩ edzi yawa fi a, ɛbɛ̃ anɛ ɛdã ye ade ebi ye kĩ, “Nɔ malo otomɛna Yesu Nazaretni a!”

english_verse: “No, I'm not!” Peter replied. A little while later some of the people said to Peter, “You certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro buki esĩ. Owi tábuna mɔ bikĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã bi ye kĩ, “Ɔláapuli osi kĩ ɔnyɛ́ be kamɛ ɔmɔa, kitonɛkĩ nɔ malo Galileani anɛ.”

english_verse: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ɔkɔkɔ be lɛyade, ade Petro te anɛ nɛ kalɛ a kĩ Yesu bi ye a akũ kĩ, “Fɔmɔ ɔkɔkɔ aabe titi nviã ni, aalɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ onyína yĩ.” Kĩ ɛmɔ ye akũ eyi ɔsũ a, ebe blobloblo.

english_verse: Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu a basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a, ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a, mbla batsanɛ a mɔ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a tsɛna mɔatsɛ bɛnaa adzina nɛ Yesu akũ. Bisui Yesu nɛ tɔwɛ̃ kamɛ, bɛkpã ye bɛnado nɛ Pilato awɛ.

english_verse: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kɔa bido nɛ bati kɔdabu a kamɛ kĩ bivia Pilato ɛfɔnɛ Baraba mbom awɛ ɛkpa be.

english_verse: Pilate asked, “But what crime has he done?” “Nail him to a cross!” they yelled even louder.
translation_verse: Pilato via be kĩ, “Litsa tɔkpa ɛkɛna?” Ade be flee bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Bɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ!”

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu tábuki ebinya onukpɛ̃ ade Pilato kɛna saa a. Pilato Kɔ Yesu Wuda Kɔtɔ.

english_verse: They made fun of Jesus and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Adeke bɛkawɔ̃na ye kĩ, “Yiiduu! Yudafɔ Lɛgã, lidzi nkpã gɛlɛɛlɛ!”

english_verse: Then Pilate questioned him again, “Don't you have anything to say? Don't you hear what crimes they say you have done?”
translation_verse: Foesũ Pilato buki evia ye kĩ, “Ɔláakpa kalɛ a mbuayɛ? Tse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlamɛna katsua kate nɛ nɔ akũ a.”

english_verse: Pilate wanted to please the crowd, so he set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Pilato wɛ kĩ yakɛna nɛ bati kɔdabu a kawɛ akũ ɛkpa be, foesũ ɛfɔnɛ Baraba awɛ ɛkpa be, ade kĩ ɛfɔ̃ sodzafɔ bo Yesu paa mɔ mpile biyu a, ɛkɔa ye ɛkpa sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛnaabɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ. Sodzafɔ a Di Yesu Nsekuo.

english_verse: On it was a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Ade bɛtsɛlɛ ye onukpɛ̃ katsua fɔlɛtsa bɛbɔa nɛ ye lɔlɔ nyangɛ kĩ, “Yudafɔ Lɛgã a.”

english_verse: They yelled, “Nail him to a cross!”
translation_verse: Ade bɛbɔa fɔwɔla bibi ye kĩ, “Bɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ!”

english_verse: It was about nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross.
translation_verse: Bɛbɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ nɛ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ evedi kamɛ.

english_verse: A Roman army officer was standing in front of Jesus. When the officer saw how Jesus died, he said, “This man really was the Son of God!”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ ɛdza ɛtsɛlamɛna asendua a nu Yesu fɔwɔla kabɔa mɔ ayekĩ eku a, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nuwĩ fɔanɛ kĩ oti kĩĩ nyɛ Baguma Obi.”

english_verse: The soldiers led Jesus inside the courtyard of the fortress and called together the rest of the troops.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ a kpã Yesu biwomɛna nɛ abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a tɔtɔ a lɔwabe, ade bɛvɛ sodzafɔ nkaɛ a bɛyakpɔta a.

english_verse: And at that time there was a prisoner named Barabbas. He and some others had been arrested for murder during a riot.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Baraba waa kɔla mɔ bati bɛbã badi. Bati a yidza bɛdza nɛ Roma Abãã akũ ade bɛkɔ bati nɛ foe kamɛ a.

english_verse: The crowd now came and asked Pilate to set a prisoner free, just as he usually did.
translation_verse: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a kpɔta a, bivia Pilato kĩ ɛkɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ kĩ yawɛ kakɛna kateli nyɛ kateli a ɛkpa be.

english_verse: Pilate asked them, “Do you want me to free the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Evia be kĩ, “Miáwɛ kĩ nfɔnɛ Yudafɔ Lɛgã a awɛ nkpa mi?”

english_verse: Pilate knew that the chief priests had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Pilato via fukĩĩ kitonɛkĩ enyina yededeede kĩ lineke sũ mɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kɔa Yesu bido nɛ ye awɛ.
