english_verse: Then Pilate asked the crowd, “What do you want me to do with this man you say is the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Pilato buki evia bati kɔdabu a kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ litsa miawɛ kĩ nkɛna oti a kĩ miavɛ kĩ Yudafɔ Lɛgã a?”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the purple robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Kĩ bidi ye nsekuo biyua a, bɛnyɛ atadiɛ ngbã a nɛ ye akũ, bimuniki mɔ ye mɔawɛ tide bido ye. Ke liti a, bɛkpã ye bɛnɛmɛna kĩ banaabɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ. Bɛbɔa Yesu Nɛ Otse Akũ.

english_verse: Simon from Cyrene happened to be coming in from a farm, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross. Simon was the father of Alexander and Rufus. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ banaa a, bɛtsɛlamɛna onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Simon kĩ eto akula adi kamɛ yanaa ɔmatɔ kamɛ. Ade sodzafɔ a hɛ ye kĩ ɛtɛ Yesu asendua a ɛlɔ a. Simon kĩĩ to Kireneni ɔmatɔ a, ade ɛnyɛ Aleksandro mɔ Rufo ɔlɛga a.

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses also made fun of Jesus. They said to each other, “He saved others, but he can't save himself.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a lɛmɛ di ye nsekuo bɛkabi akũ kĩ, “Ɛtɛ bati bɛbã nkpã, lɛmɛ yaápuli ɛtɛ ye mɔawɛ akũ.

english_verse: Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, and he called in the army officer to find out if Jesus had been dead very long.
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna Pilato wãwã kĩ enu kĩ Yesu ku kɔkɔɛ. Foesũ ɛvɛ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ evia ye kĩ nuwĩ Yesu ku kɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: A man named Joseph from Arimathea was brave enough to ask Pilate for the body of Jesus. Joseph was a highly respected member of the Jewish council, and he was also waiting for God's kingdom to come.
translation_verse: Yosef kĩ eto Arimatia ɔmatɔ a ya. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ edeĩ obu nɛ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a kamɛ, ade edzi yadã Baguma Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kaya anɛ a. Yosef do ɔwɔlɛ ɛnaa Pilato sɛkɛ̃ eke ye aduli evia kĩ ɛfɔnɛ Yesu ɔnɔkpa a awɛ ɛkpa ye.

english_verse: The soldiers took Jesus to Golgotha, which means “Place of a Skull.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpã Yesu bɛnamɛna kakɔ kadi kĩ bavɛ Golgata, kĩ foe kayɔ nyɛ oti lɔlɔ fɔtsɔ̃.

english_verse: They nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ a bɔa Yesu nɛ asendua akũ ade bɛta bavulibi nɛ ye tɛtadiɛ akũ bɛmɔ́ ekĩ yaanya odi nyɛ odi ade a.

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and shouted, “Ha! So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛkatɛ̃ a kpɔkpa fɔlɔ ade bivũna Yesu kĩ, “Ao! Nɔ a kĩ ɔlɛ kĩ aaveli Asɔli Kɔla a obuki oke kɔbɛbã nɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ liti a nɛ awĩ a?

english_verse: If he is the Messiah, the king of Israel, let him come down from the cross! Then we will see and believe.” The two criminals also said cruel things to Jesus. The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ Kristo, Israelfɔ Lɛgã a eduma nɛ asendua akũ finyaa bɔmɔ lɔkɔa bɔtɛ ye budo” Ade bati nviã a kĩ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ bibumɔ Yesu a, lɛmɛ vũna ye a. Yesu Ku.

english_verse: After the officer told him, Pilate let Joseph have Jesus' body.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a bi ye ɔnɔkɔali a, Pilato kpa Yosef osi kĩ ɛnaavɛnya ɔnɔkpa a.

english_verse: About noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until around three o'clock.
translation_verse: Kĩ suwa tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã bɔa a, katinya kamɛ flee ke ɔtũ, kɛnawo tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Nɛ tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ a, Yesu bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ nɛ Hebri tide kamɛ kĩ, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabaktani?” Foe kayɔ nyɛ, “Yĩ Baguma! Yĩ Baguma! Litsa sũ ɔfɔnɛ yĩ awɛ?”

english_verse: Some of the people standing there heard Jesus and said, “He is calling for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã a badi nu lɛtsa kĩ ɛlɛ a, ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Mitse! Yavɛ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Eliya.”

english_verse: One of them ran and grabbed a sponge. After he had soaked it in wine, he put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus. He said, “Let's wait and see if Elijah will come and take him down!”
translation_verse: Be kamɛ ɔmɔa wula atsɛ ɛnabɔɛ kanyinya butubu ɔsapɔ kĩ edo nɛ waĩ mɔ ɔnyɛ kamɛ ade ɛdɛla mɔ otse ɛkpa Yesu kĩ ɛmɔa a. Ade oti a kalɛ kĩ, “Midza, mifɔ̃ bɔdã ntɛ Eliya aaka ɛyaanyɛ ye nɛ asendua a akũ!”

english_verse: Jesus shouted and then died.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ade ɛmɛna onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: It was now the evening before the Sabbath, and the Jewish people were getting ready for that sacred day.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa kĩĩ flee ya nɛ luwi kĩ lɛnya Kawɛya Luwi a lɔtɔ. Nɛ olobe lɔkɔ a,

english_verse: Joseph bought a linen cloth and took the body down from the cross. He had it wrapped in the cloth, and he put it in a tomb that had been cut into solid rock. Then he rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb.
translation_verse: Ade Yosef naya aklala, ade kĩ ɛnavɛnya Yesu ɔnɔkpa a, esuli ye nɛ lima a kamɛ. Ɛkɔa ye ɛwɔla nɛ linikɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ nɛ de ɔkɛ kamɛ, ade emilinya de kpale ɛbalɛ linikɛ a onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph were watching and saw where the body was placed.
translation_verse: Maria Magdalani mɔ Maria kĩ ɛnyɛ Yose ɔma a dza bɛkadã, ade bɛmɔ́ awã kĩ bɛkɔa Yesu ɔnɔkpa a bibila a.

english_verse: Later, Jesus appeared in another form to two disciples, as they were on their way out of the city.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, Yesu nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nviã kĩ Bito Yerusalem banaa akula, lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ ye biyi ɔsũ nɛ kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: Then the disciples left and preached everywhere. The Lord was with them, and the miracles they worked proved that their message was true. ANOTHER OLD ENDING TO MARK'S GOSPEL
translation_verse: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nalɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ. Boe Ɔlate a kɛna adzuma mɔ be, ɛkɔa awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkakɛna a ete de kĩ be fɔlɛtsa a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali.

english_verse: She left and told his friends, who were crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Ade ye lɛnabi bikĩ bitomɔ Yesu tɔ̃ a fɔlɛtsa a. Bati kĩĩ dzi bɛkabe ayɔlɔhɔ nɛ ye wuda a akũ.

english_verse: Very early on Sunday morning, just as the sun was coming up, they went to the tomb.
translation_verse: Nɛ Ɛkwansieda ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu a, kĩ suwa de a, biyidza bɛnaa linikɛ a akũ.

english_verse: But when they looked, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away. And it was a huge stone!
translation_verse: Kĩ banaa a, bɛkavia akũ kĩ, “Owei aamilinya de kpale a nɛ linikɛ a onukpɛ̃ ɛkpa boe?” De a pɔnɛ lububu paa. Kĩ bɛdzɛla anɛ bɛdã a, bɛmɔ́ kĩ bimilinya de a biyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ kɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: The women went into the tomb, and on the right side they saw a young man in a white robe sitting there. They were alarmed.
translation_verse: Foesũ biwo nɛ linikɛ a kamɛ. Adeke bɛmɔ́ opopo odi kĩ edo atadiɛ wulaa fitaa edzi nɛ sɔmɔna sɛmakɛ, ade bɛbɔa oblia.

english_verse: Now go and tell his disciples, and especially Peter, that he will go ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.”
translation_verse: Minaabi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mɔ Petro lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Yesu nyaa mi lɔtɔ yanaa Galilea, ade awã míamɔ ye ndɛ ayekĩ ebi mi eyi a.’ ”

english_verse: They will handle snakes and will drink poison and not be hurt. They will also heal sick people by placing their hands on them. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ bipi kɔɛ ĩye bɛmɔa otsonu kpa ni, fɔláakɛna be lɛtsama. Ntɛ bite awɛ nɛ basɔnɛ akũ ni, baana akũ ɔsĩ.” Baguma Bɔɛ Yesu Ɛnamɛna Ode.

english_verse: But when these disciples told what had happened, the others would not believe either. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Bimuniki bɛnabi be nkaɛ a, lɛmɛ be malo bɛtátɛ bido. Yesu Nyɛ Ye Akũ Ɛtsa Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ Ewua-odi a.

english_verse: Anyone who believes me and is baptized will be saved. But anyone who refuses to believe me will be condemned.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ edo, ɛtɛ mui kawɔla lɛmɛ ni, baatɛ ye nkpã, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtátɛ edo ni, baakɔ ye kɔtɔ.

english_verse: After the Lord Jesus had said these things to the disciples, he was taken back up to heaven where he sat down at the right side of God.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kĩ boe Ɔlate Yesu lɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ be eyua a, bɛbɔɛ ye bɛnamɛna nɛ ode ade ɛnadzi nɛ Baguma sɔmɔna a.

english_verse: Afterwards, Jesus appeared to his eleven disciples as they were eating. He scolded them because they were too stubborn to believe the ones who had seen him after he had been raised to life.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-odi a kĩ bidzi banya. Lɛmɛ ɛkã be anɛ kĩ bɛtána katɛkado, mɔ kĩ bɛna atɔ ɔsĩ, bɛtátɛ bati a kĩ bɛmɔ́ kĩ ye edzi nkpã a lɛmɛ bido a.

english_verse: Then he told them: Go and preach the good news to everyone in the world.
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Minaa katinya kamɛ flee minaalɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a mikpa batidziwa flee.

english_verse: Everyone who believes me will be able to do wonderful things. By using my name they will force out demons, and they will speak new languages.
translation_verse: Baguma aakpa batɛ badonɛ a kɔbɛ̃ bɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa. Baablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa bɛnɛmɛna nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ, baalɛ tide vɔɛ̃.

english_verse: Many people have tried to tell the story of what God has done among us.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Tiofilo, bati pii bɔa mbɔdi ayekĩ baapuli, bɛtsɛlɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ boe kamɛ a biyi.

english_verse: They wrote what we had been told by the ones who were there in the beginning and saw what happened.
translation_verse: Bɛtsɛlɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bibi boe a, ndɛ ayekĩ binu bitomɛna bikĩ bɛmɔ́ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ bamɛ kito anɛ kayɔkayi a, ade bide lɛtsã nɛ foe akũ a.

english_verse: So I made a careful study of everything and then decided to write and tell you exactly what took place. Honorable Theophilus,
translation_verse: Foesũ, Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Tiofilo, nɛ ayekĩ yĩ mɔawɛ a ntsã fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee kamɛ kito kayɔkayi a sũ ni, nkɔna kĩ fɔnɛnɛ kĩ matsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa a pɛpɛɛpɛ mma nɔ.

english_verse: I have done this to let you know the truth about what you have heard. An Angel Tells about the Birth of John
translation_verse: Nkɛna fukĩĩ ayekĩ aanyi ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsa nɔ a flee kamɛ. Bɛkpa Kanyi Nɛ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ Kama Akũ.

english_verse: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest by the name of Zechariah from the priestly group of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was from the family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Herodes kanya lɛgã nɛ Yudea tite akũ a, Yudafɔ ɔsɔfɔ odi na kĩ bavɛ Zakaria. Eto ɔsɔfɔ katsu a kĩ bavɛ Abiya a kamɛ. Ɔtsɔ̃ dĩ nyɛ Elizabet kĩ ye malo eto ɔsɔfɔ Aaron kasinu a kamɛ.

english_verse: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.
translation_verse: Ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ ɔnyɔ kĩĩ dzi nkpã wĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, ade bɛkadi ye tɛbla mɔ nhihiɛ akũ pɛpɛɛpɛ a.

english_verse: But they did not have children. Elizabeth could not have any, and both Zechariah and Elizabeth were already old.
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ táma obi odima kitonɛkĩ Elizabet nya oduko, ade bati nviã a lɛmɛ dɛ a.

english_verse: One day Zechariah's group of priests were on duty, and he was serving God as a priest.
translation_verse: Luwi a Zakaria kakɛna ye tɔsɔfɔ adzuma nɛ Yudafɔ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃, kitonɛkĩ be katsu a akũ fuke.

english_verse: According to the custom of the priests, he had been chosen to go into the Lord's temple that day and to burn incense,
translation_verse: Nɛ be basɔfɔ adzuma kakɛna onukpɛ̃ a, biwole ade fɔyake nɛ Zakaria akũ, kĩ ɛnaate ɔnyɔ mɔ ɔlɛ̃ ɛkpa Baguma nɛ ye afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a akũ. Foesũ ewo ɛnaa Yudafɔ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: All at once an angel from the Lord appeared to Zechariah at the right side of the altar.
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye nɛ ɔnyɔ katekɔ̃ a sɔmɔna sɛmakɛ.

english_verse: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
translation_verse: Kĩ Zakaria mɔ́ ye a, ɛbɔa obili ade libe wo ye paa a.

english_verse: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Zakaria, tavila! Baguma nu nɔ mpaɛbɔ, nɔ ɔtsɔ̃ Elizabet aama obinyole ɛkpa nɔ. Aate ye dĩ kĩ Yohanes.

english_verse: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ aade nɔ paa, ade ayea kawɔlɛ aade bati pii lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛma ye a.

english_verse: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
translation_verse: Yaanya oti kpale nɛ Baguma anɛ. Kɛtánɛ kĩ yamɔa waĩ ĩye bavɛ mɔ ɔsĩ. Ade kito ye kama kamɛ ni, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa aatɛ ye kamɛ.

english_verse: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
translation_verse: Ade ye aanya oti kĩ yaamunikimɔ Israelfɔ bati pii ɛyamɛna nɛ be Ɔlate Baguma a sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
translation_verse: Yaanya boe Ɔlate kaya a lɔtɔ ɛna hũhũ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Eliya na a. Yaalɔla babi mɔ balɛga kayite. Yaadani bikĩ bideĩ atɔ ɔsĩ bɛna adzuni ndɛ bikĩ bakɛna tuwĩ. Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ boe Ɔlate bati lɛna nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ bɛkpa ye.”

english_verse: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
translation_verse: Ade Zakaria via ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, “Nde maakɛna ĩnyi kĩ fukĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ? Kitonɛkĩ ndɛ, ade yĩ ɔtsɔ̃ lɛmɛ dɛ a.”

english_verse: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
translation_verse: Baguma fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yĩ nyɛ Gabriel kĩ madza nɛ Baguma anɛ. Ade ye lido yĩ kĩ ĩyaabi nɔ ɔlɛdo wĩ kĩĩ a.

english_verse: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɔtátɛ yĩ ɔlɛdo odo, kĩ yaaya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma hihiɛ eyi a. Nɛ foesũ aatɔ lumumu, ɔláapuli ɔlɛ kɔdzɛla kɛnaawo luwi a kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ nlɛ ĩyi kĩĩ a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.”

english_verse: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a kamɛ a, bati a kĩ bɛna nɛ kanya bɛkadzɔlɛ Zakaria kɛna saa, ade bɛkabu lɔlɔ kamɛ nɛ lɛtsa a sũ kĩ ebuna owi beblebee nɛ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Kĩ Zakaria nɛ ɛya a, ɛtábuki epuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ be. Foesũ bɛmɔ́ biyi ɔsũ kĩ ɛmɔ kanyɛkatsa kadi nɛ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a. Kĩ ɛtɔ lumumu sũ ni awɛ ɛkɔa ɛkakɛna nsɛntsɛlɛni katsa be.

english_verse: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
translation_verse: Kĩ Zakaria yua ye adzuma nɛ Yudafɔ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a, ɛdzakũ ɛnaa ɔtɔ.

english_verse: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Elizabet yatsãmɔ kɔma ade ɛtábuki ɛnɛ kanya a. Ɛkɔla ye akũ tidetɔ elo flee.

english_verse: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
translation_verse: Adeke Elizabet lɛ kĩ, “Finyaakĩĩ ni, Baguma bumɔ yĩ ade ɛnyɛ yĩ kɔnyɔã nɛ bati anɛ a.” Bɛlɛ Yesu Kama Biyi.

english_verse: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
translation_verse: Nɛ tidetɔ evũ liti a, Baguma do ye ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ Gabriel ɛnaa Galilea ɔmatɔ odi kĩ bavɛ Nazaret kamɛ.

english_verse: with a message for a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to Joseph from the family of King David.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ kĩĩ kɔa ɔlɛdo ɛma oyitsɔ̃ kĩ bitinya bɛkpa onyole kĩ bavɛ Yosef, kĩ ɛnyɛ Lɛgã Dawid kasinu a kamɛ obi. Oyitsɔ̃ a dĩ nyɛ Maria.

english_verse: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a ya nɛ Maria sɛkɛ̃ ade ebi ye kĩ, “Oyitsɔ̃, mawɔ̃na nɔ nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ! Nɔ oti kĩ bɛmɔ́ kɔnya, boe Ɔlate tomɔ nɔ!”

english_verse: Mary was confused by the angel's words and wondered what they meant.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔlɛdo a fɔ̃ Maria kɛna basaa, ade evia ye akũ kĩ, “Litsa kawɔ̃na nɛ awĩ a?”

english_verse: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Maria, tavila. Baguma mɔ́ nɔ kɔnya.

english_verse: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
translation_verse: Aatsãmɔ kɔma ɔma obinyole, lɔkɔa aate ye dĩ kĩ Yesu.

english_verse: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
translation_verse: Yaatɛ dĩ paa kĩ bati aavɛ ye kĩ Baguma Nɛ Ode Flee Obi. Boe Ɔlate aadzina ye lɛgã ndɛ ɔwanyɔ Dawid aye.

english_verse: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
translation_verse: Ade yaanya lɛgã nɛ Yakob babi mɔ bawa akũ daa a. Ye lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a lɛláawũna dadaada.”

english_verse: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
translation_verse: Awã naa Maria bi Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, “Nde fukĩĩ aapuli fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ lɔkɔ kĩ ĩnyína onyole a?”

english_verse: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
translation_verse: Ade ke ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ayako nɛ nɔ akũ ade Baguma kɔbɛ̃ aatɛ nɔ kamɛ a. Foesũ obi kpalɛwa a kĩ aama a ni, baavɛ ye kĩ Baguma Obinyole.

english_verse: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
translation_verse: Mawɛ kĩ kai nɔ akũ oti kĩ bavɛ Elizabet. Bati alɛ kĩ ɛláama, lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ ɛtsɛ̃mɔ kɔma. Edeĩ nɛ odetɔ ovũde kamɛ, titɔ kĩ ɛdɛ posoposo malo.

english_verse: Nothing is impossible for God!”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsama lɛláa kĩ Baguma lɛláapuli ɛkɛna.”

english_verse: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
translation_verse: Ade Maria bi ye kĩ, “Dã, boe Ɔlate ɔsumunɛ manɛ. Fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ fɔkpa yĩ ndɛ ayekĩ ɔlɛ a.” Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ ye a. Maria Nasɛla Elizabet.

english_verse: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Kĩ Elizabet nu Maria kawɔ̃na a, ye obi nɛ sɛmɛ kamɛ a kuna akũ, ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a tɛ Elizabet kamɛ a.

english_verse: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
translation_verse: Ade awã na ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bihila nɔ nɛ batsole flee kamɛ, ade bihila nɔ sɛmɛ kamɛ obi a lɛmɛ a!

english_verse: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
translation_verse: Yĩ nyɛ owei kĩ boe Ɔlate ɔma ya kĩ yayaawɔ̃na yĩ?

english_verse: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ nsila nnu nɔ kawɔ̃na pɛ, obi nɛ yĩ sɛmɛ kamɛ a kuna akũ mɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
translation_verse: Bihila nɔ paa kitonɛkĩ ɔtɛ odo kĩ Baguma ɔlɛdo a kĩ ɛlɛ nɛ nɔ akũ a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.” Maria Sã Kamu Ɔnɔ.

english_verse: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
translation_verse: Awã naa Maria lɛ kĩ, “Yĩ ɔwɔlɛ amu Baguma.

english_verse: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
translation_verse: Ade kawɔlɛ ade yĩ hũhũ a, nɛ Baguma yĩ Ɔtɛnɛ a sũ,

english_verse: God cares for me, his humble servant. From now on, all people will say God has blessed me.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ ete anɛ nɛ, yĩ ye ɔsumunɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ́ lɛtsama akũ. Kito finyaa kanaa ni, babi mɔ bawa flee aavɛ yĩ oti kĩ bihila ye,

english_verse: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ Baguma Otumifɔ kɛna fɔtsa kpale ɛkpa yĩ. Ye dĩ akũ kpalɛ.

english_verse: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
translation_verse: Ye kɔnyakamɔ waa, kɛkpa babi mɔ bawa kĩ bavila ye a.

english_verse: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛna fɔtsa kpale, ade ɛyalɛ bati a kĩ bɛdɛla be akũ nɛ ode mɔ be kakɔna flee a.

english_verse: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
translation_verse: Ɛdzɛla fɔgã kĩ fudeĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ be fɔgãkpo akũ, ade ɛdɛla bikĩ bɛdɔla be akũ a nɛ ode a.

english_verse: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
translation_verse: Ɛkpa bikĩ kɔlɛ akɔ be a fɔtsa wĩ pii, ade ɛfɔ̃ bikĩ bideĩ akũ a dzakũ mɔ awɛ pupulidza a.

english_verse: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
translation_verse: Maria dzi nɛ Elizabet sɛkɛ̃ ndɛ tidetɔ ɛlalɛ fɔmɔ emuniki ɛnaa ɔtɔ. Bɛma Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a.

english_verse: When Elizabeth's son was born,
translation_verse: Kĩ Elizabet aama a, ɛma obinyole.

english_verse: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
translation_verse: Ye akũ bati mɔ banunɛ flee nu ayekĩ Baguma kɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ ɛkpa ye, foesũ be flee bɛmɔ́ kawɔlɛ paa mɔ ye.

english_verse: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
translation_verse: Kĩ akɔkɔa a tɛ fuwi ɛlɛ̃ a, ye akũ bati ya kĩ batsua ye lɛkpati, ade bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔa ye ɔlɛga dĩ Zakaria bite ye a.

english_verse: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɔma tátunɔ mɔ be, ade ebi be kĩ, “Ye dĩ aanya Yohanes.”

english_verse: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
translation_verse: Ade awã mɔ bibi ye kĩ, “Odima táto nɔ kɔla kamɛ kĩ yanya dĩ a na.”

english_verse: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bɛkɔa awɛ bɛkɛna nsɛntsɛlɛni bivia ɔlɛga kĩ ɛtsa dĩ a kĩ ɛwɛ kĩ yate obi.

english_verse: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
translation_verse: Awã naa Zakaria via sileti ade ɛtsɛlɛ ete kĩ, “Ye dĩ nyɛ Yohanes.” Fɔkɛna be a flee saa.

english_verse: At once, Zechariah started speaking and praising God.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ Zakaria buki edo kɔdzɛla kalɛ awɛ ade emu Baguma a.

english_verse: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
translation_verse: Libe wo ye banunɛ a flee ade fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ tɛmatɔ a kĩ tideĩ nɛ Yudea tɔtɔ kamɛ a flee a.

english_verse: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kpa bati a kĩ binu foe a lɔlɔ kamɛ kabu, ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Litsa odu obisɛ̃ kĩĩ aadani ɛkɛna?” Bivia foe kitonɛkĩ fɔwaa yedede kĩ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ tomɔ ye. Zakaria Lɛ Baguma Fɔlɛtsa Eyi.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa tɛ obisɛ̃ a ɔlɛga Zakaria kamɛ ade ɛlɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa eyi kĩ,

english_verse: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
translation_verse: “Mifɔ̃ bumu boe Ɔlate, Israel Baguma, kitonɛkĩ ɛyabumɔ ye kade bati, ade ɛnyɛ be nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ a.

english_verse: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
translation_verse: Ɛkpa boe Ɔtɛnɛ kĩ edeĩ kɔbɛ̃, oti kĩ ɛnyɛ ye osumunɛ Lɛgã Dawid kasinu a kamɛ obi.

english_verse: Long ago the Lord promised by the words of his holy prophets
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ eyi anɛ kɔkɔɛ ɛtsãmɛna ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ kpalɛwa a akũ,

english_verse: to save us from our enemies and from everyone who hates us.
translation_verse: kĩ yaanyɛ boe etomɛna bikĩ bapi boe osumbɔ mɔ boe bakesĩnɛ flee awɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: God said he would be kind to our people and keep his sacred promise.
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ kĩ yaamɔ boe bawanyɔ kɔnya, ade ete anɛ nɛ ye nhihiɛ kpalɛwa a kĩ ɛkɛna eyi a akũ a.

english_verse: that he would rescue us from our enemies. Then we could serve him without fear,
translation_verse: Yaakɛna nɛ fubo a kĩ ɛlɔ ɛkpa boe ɔwanyɔ Abraham, kĩ yaanyɛ boe etomɛna boe bakesĩnɛ awɛ kamɛ a akũ, ayekĩ bɔ́apuli busum Baguma nɛ katavila kamɛ,

english_verse: by being holy and good as long as we live.
translation_verse: boe akũ lɛkpalɛ, budzi kadzi wĩ nɛ ye anɛ, nɛ boe nkpã fuwi flee kamɛ.

english_verse: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
translation_verse: Nɔ, yĩ obi, bati aavɛ nɔ Baguma Nɛ Ode Flee Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ. Ade nɔ aanya boe Ɔlate lɔtɔ lɔkɔa ɔlɔla osi ɔkpa ye kaya a.

english_verse: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
translation_verse: Aabi ye bati kĩ baana katɛ nɛ be titikpa kakɔakate kamɛ.

english_verse: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
translation_verse: Boe Baguma deĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ mɔ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ, foesũ yaafɔ̃ katɛ ɔhaĩ a lide ɛkpa boe.

english_verse: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
translation_verse: Yaafɔ̃ ɔhaĩ kĩĩ lɛyaako nɛ bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ, mɔ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ wuda kuyo kamɛ a akũ, lɔkɔa ɛkpã boe ɛtsãmɛna lukudɔ osi akũ.”

english_verse: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Obisɛ̃ a bo anɛ nɛ sukɛna mɔ hũhũ kamɛ. Edzi nɛ kɔsa kamɛ, kɛnawo luwi a kĩ ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Israel kanade a.

english_verse: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare Augusto si mbla kĩ bɛkã, bɛtsɛlɛ bati flee a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Roma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ tite akũ a buĩ.

english_verse: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Simeon dzi nɛ Yerusalem. Ɛnyɛ oti wĩ kĩ yavila Baguma ade edzi ɛkadã osi kakpa Israel katɛ a. Hũhũ Kpalɛwa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ,

english_verse: These first records were made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
translation_verse: Nɛ bati buĩ katsɛlɛ katɔnyade kĩĩ kamɛ a, abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩ bavɛ Kirenio lɛnya Siria tite akũ.

english_verse: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
translation_verse: Nɛ mbla a sũ odi nyɛ odi naa ye ɔtɔ kĩ yanaatsɛlɛ dĩ.

english_verse: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
translation_verse: Ade ke Yosef to Nazaret ɔmatɔ nɛ Galilea ɛnaa Betlehem ɔmatɔ nɛ Yudea tite akũ, awã kĩ bɛma Lɛgã Dawid lɛmɛ a. Yosef naa awã kitonɛkĩ ɛnya Lɛgã Dawid kasinu a kamɛ obi.

english_verse: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
translation_verse: Bɛmɔ Maria kĩ bitinya bɛkpa ye a kĩ ɛtsɛ̃mɔ kɔma lɛmɛ a lɛnaa kĩ banaatsɛlɛ buĩ.

english_verse: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
translation_verse: Ɛma ye obinyole ɔtɔnyade, ade esuli obi a edo nɛ fumakũ kamɛ, ɛkɔa ɛwɔla nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de kamɛ bayaɛ anya a kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ bɛtána kawakɔ̃ kadima nɛ banɔvɔɛ̃ kasekɔ̃ a. Ode Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ Yabi Baveli Bakpãnɛ Fɔlɛtsa Wĩ.

english_verse: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
translation_verse: Baveli bakpãnɛ badi na nɛ tite a kakɔ kadi lɛnyɛ a flee bɛkadã be bayaɛ akũ.

english_verse: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nɛ ɛta nɛ be kayite ade Baguma kukũkpalɛ ko nɛ be akũ a. Nɛ foesũ bivila paa,

english_verse: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
translation_verse: lɛmɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi be kĩ, “Mitavila! Mma mi fɔlɛtsa wĩ kĩ fɔ́ayamɛna kawɔlɛ mɔ lukudɔ pii fɔkpa bati flee.

english_verse: This very day in King David's hometown a Savior was born for you. He is Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɛ nviã luwi kĩĩ akũ, bɛma Ɔtɛnɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ boe Ɔlate Kristo bɛkpa mi nɛ Lɛgã Dawid ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ míakɔa mimɔ́ miyi ɔsũ nyɛ kĩ míamɔ waawa a kĩ busuli ye bido nɛ fumakũ kamɛ ɛwaa nɛ bayaɛ kanyakɔ̃.”

english_verse: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ kɔdza baũ kudi to ode kɔka kɔyabumɔ ɔtɔnyade a, ade be a flee bɛkamu Baguma mɔ kawɔlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “Praise God in heaven! Peace on earth to everyone who pleases God.”
translation_verse: “Obu mɔ kamu kɛkpa Baguma nɛ ode flefle, lɔkɔa lukudɔ litomɛna katinya kamɛ bati flee a kĩ Baguma awɛ a!”

english_verse: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a dzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ bɛdani bɛnaa ode a, baveli bakpãnɛ a bi be akũ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bɔnaa Betlehem bɔnaadã lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a kĩ boe Ɔlate bi boe a.”

english_verse: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
translation_verse: Ade bɛwɛ sɔwa bɛnaa bɛnamɔ Maria, Yosef mɔ waawa a kĩ ɛwaa nɛ bayaɛ kanyakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a, bɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa a flee kĩ Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi be nɛ obisɛ̃ a akũ.

english_verse: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Maria pi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee edo nɛ lɔlɔ kamɛ, ɛkakɔna foe akũ dzununuunu.

english_verse: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
translation_verse: Baveli bakpãnɛ a muniki bɛnaa mɔ Baguma kamu nɛ fɔlɛtsa flee a kĩ binu, bɛmɔ́ lɛmɛ a akũ, kitonɛkĩ foe flee fɔya pɛpɛɛpɛ ndɛ ayekĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi be a. Bite Waawa a Di.

english_verse: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Nɛ luwi lɛlɛ̃de akũ kĩ batsua obisɛ̃ a lɛkpati a, bite ye dĩ kĩ Yesu ndɛ ayekĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a lɛ eyi fɔmɔ bɛyatsãmɔ ye kɔma a. Bɛkɔa Yesu Bɛnamɛna Yudafɔ Afɔliɛ Kabɔakɔ̃.

english_verse: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ Yosef mɔ Maria bɛkɛna akũ kakpalɛ yedede amaniɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Mose mbla tsa a. Foesũ bɛkɔa waawa a bɛnamɛna Yerusalem kĩ banaakɔa ye bido nɛ boe Ɔlate awɛ,

english_verse: just as the Law of the Lord says, “Each first-born baby boy belongs to the Lord.”
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ bido nɛ boe Ɔlate mbla ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, “Obinyole ɔtɔnyade odi nyɛ odi ni kɛnɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ ye biyi bɛkpa boe Ɔlate.”

english_verse: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
translation_verse: Ade bɛnaa bɛnakɛna afɔlibɔ fɔtsa a nɛ boe Ɔlate mbla a onukpɛ̃ kĩ bɛkɔa mblɔduma nviã ĩye fɔblɔblɔm nviã bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa ye.

english_verse: and told him that he would not die until he had seen Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: ade ɛnyɛ ɛtsa ye lɛmɛ kĩ ɛláaku kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛmɔ boe Ɔlate, Kristo a kĩ bɛlɛ biyi a.

english_verse: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
translation_verse: Ade ke Hũhũ a kpã Simeon ɛnamɛna Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a. Kĩ bamanɛ a kɔa waawa Yesu a bɛyamɛna kĩ bayaakɛna amaniɛ nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ a,

english_verse: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
translation_verse: Simeon tɛ waawa a ɛlɛ nɛ awɛ, ade ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate, finyaa, ɔkɛna nɛ nɔ kalɛ onukpɛ̃ akũ, aapuli ɔnyɛ yĩ, nɔ ɔsumunɛ, osi nnaa nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: and foreign nations will also see this.
translation_verse: kĩ ɔkɔa ɔma bati flee a.

english_verse: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ ɔhaĩ a kĩ yaanyɛ nɔ fɔwɛtsa ɛtsa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ, ade yaanya obu fɔtsa ɛkpa nɔ bati Israelfɔ a.”

english_verse: Jesus' parents were surprised at what Simeon had said.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Simeon lɛ nɛ obisɛ̃ a akũ kɛna Yosef mɔ Maria saa.

english_verse: Then he blessed them and told Mary, “This child of yours will cause many people in Israel to fall and others to stand. The child will be like a warning sign. Many people will reject him,
translation_verse: Simeon hila be ade ebi Maria kĩ, “Baguma nyɛ obisɛ̃ kĩĩ kĩ fɔtsã ye akũ Israelfɔ pii bɛna katɛ ĩye kawɔ. Yaanya nsɛntsɛlɛni kĩ eto Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ade bati pii aayidza bɛdza nɛ ye akũ a.

english_verse: and you, Mary, will suffer as though you had been stabbed by a dagger. But all this will show what people are really thinking.” Anna Speaks about the Child Jesus
translation_verse: Ade yaanyɛ bati kakɔna a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla a kanya a. Fɔtsɛ̃ fukĩĩ akũ sũ ni, aatɛ kalɔɛ ndɛ ayekĩ lɛpamɛ atsɔɛ nɔ du.”

english_verse: At this time Anna came in and praised God. She spoke about the child Jesus to everyone who hoped for Jerusalem to be set free. The Return to Nazareth
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ ye malo ɛyawo awã ade ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ, ɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ obisɛ̃ a akũ ɛkpa bati flee a kĩ bidzi badã Baguma anɛ kĩ yaanyɛ Yerusalem a. Bimuniki Bɛnaa Nazaret.

english_verse: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yosef mɔ Maria yua be amaniɛ kakɛna ndɛ ayekĩ boe Ɔlate mbla tsa a, bimuniki bɛnaa be ɔtɔ Nazaret nɛ Galilea tite akũ.

english_verse: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
translation_verse: Obisɛ̃ a bo anɛ, ɛna akũ ɔsĩ, ade enyi kadi paa a. Baguma nhila lɛmɛ tomɔ ye. Yesu Naa Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla Kamɛ.

english_verse: Every year Jesus' parents went to Jerusalem for Passover.
translation_verse: Kateli nyɛ kateli, Yesu bamanɛ kanaa Yerusalem kanaanya luwi kĩ bavɛ Wuda Katɛ̃ Luwi.

english_verse: And when Jesus was twelve years old, they all went there as usual for the celebration.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu tɛ futeli ewua-nviã a, bibuki bɛnaa luwi a kanyakɔ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ bɛkakɛna kɔkɔɛ a.

english_verse: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
translation_verse: Kĩ luwi a kanya wũna a, bimuniki banaa ɔtɔ, lɛmɛ Yesu kɔ̃ kahɔ nɛ liti nɛ Yerusalem nɛ ye bamanɛ katanyi kamɛ.

english_verse: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
translation_verse: Bibu kĩ ɛna nɛ bati kamɛ, foesũ bɛtsã luwi mɔa flee fɔmɔ bido ye kawɛ awɛ nɛ ye akũ bati mɔ bakɔba kamɛ.

english_verse: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtámɔ ye sũ ni, bimuniki bɛnaa Yerusalem bɛnɛ bɛtsɛ̃ bawɛ ye.

english_verse: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
translation_verse: Nɛ luwi lɛlade a bɛnamɔ ye nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a kĩ ye mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi bidzi, ɛkatse be fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛkalɛ, ade ɛkavia be fɔlɛtsa nɛ foe akũ a.

english_verse: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
translation_verse: Be flee a kĩ binu ye fɔlɛtsa kamɛ kakpalɛ a, kɛna saa nɛ kadi kanyi baũ odu a kĩ ɛkɔa ɛkpa mbuayɛ a.

english_verse: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
translation_verse: Kĩ ye bamanɛ mɔ ye a, akũ yɛla be, ade ɔma via ye kĩ, “Obi, litsa sũ ɔkɛna boe fukĩĩ odu? Bɔmɔ aga flee bɔkɔa kɔnɛyɔɔlɛ bɔtsã bɔwɛ nɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ mitsã miwɛ yĩ? Minyína kĩ yĩ Ɔlɛga awa kɛnɛ kĩ nna mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: But they did not understand what he meant.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtánu lɛtsa a kĩ de ebi be a kayɔ.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
translation_verse: Yesu tomɔ be bɛdzakũ bɛnaa Nazaret, ade ɛdɔla ye akũ ɛkpa be a. Ɔma kɔa fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee edo nɛ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ dzununuunu.

english_verse: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bo anɛ nɛ sukɛna mɔ kadikanyi kamɛ, ade Baguma mɔ bati flee mɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ ye akũ a.

english_verse: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ badi malo ya kĩ ɛwɔla be mui, ade bivia ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna?”

english_verse: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
translation_verse: Minyɛ mitsa nɛ mi fɔtsakɛlawa kamɛ bɛmɔ kĩ midani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Ade mitakalɛ kĩ minyɛ Abraham babi mɔ bawa sũ ni, Baguma lɛláavɛlɛ mi sɔtɔ. Kitonɛkĩ mabi mi kĩ Baguma aapuli ɛkɔa boe kĩĩ ɛkɛna babi mɔ bawa ɛkpa Abraham.

english_verse: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
translation_verse: Yohanes kã abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Herodes anɛ, kitonɛkĩ ɛtɛ ɔwaɛ̃ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ bavɛ Herodia ɛkɔã, ade Herodes kɛna fɔtsa kpa bɛbã pii lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Annas and Caiaphas were the Jewish high priests. At that time God spoke to Zechariah's son John, who was living in the desert.
translation_verse: Hana mɔ Kayafa lɛmɛ nya Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ flefle a. Ade lɔkɔ a na kamɛ lɛmɛ mɔ Baguma ɔlɛdo ya ɛkpa Yohanes kĩ ɛnyɛ Zakaria obi a kĩ ɛna nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a.

english_verse: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
translation_verse: Ade aye bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, “Oti odi tsɛ̃ yabɔa fɔwɔla nɛ kɔsa kamɛ kalɛ kĩ, ‘Milɔla osi mikpa boe Ɔlate kaya. Mikɛna osi kpĩĩ miyi ye.

english_verse: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
translation_verse: Miyula fɔkɔ a kĩ fɔpɛlɛ pɛlɛ fuwo a, ade mibubuli tɔtɔ mɔ kɔtɔbii a flee fɔtɛɛ a. Miyo tisi balibaliwa a, ade mitɛɛtɛɛ fɔkɔ a kĩ fude fute a.

english_verse: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
translation_verse: Katinya kamɛ bati flee aamɔ Ɔtɔnɛ a kĩ Baguma do a!’ ”

english_verse: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Bati kɔdabu kɔdabu pii ya nɛ Yohanes sɛkɛ̃ kĩ ɛkɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ, ade ebi be kĩ, “Mi bati kĩ mila ndɛ bɔɛ kpa! Owei lɛkpa mi kɔdɛ kĩ míapuli milo nɛ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a kĩ Baguma aayamɛna a kamɛ?

english_verse: The crowds asked John, “What should we do?”
translation_verse: Awã naa bati a via Yohanes kĩ, “Foesũ litsa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna?”

english_verse: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ badi lɛmɛ yavia ye kĩ, “Boe malo litsa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna?” Yohanes bi be kĩ “Mitakɔa kɔbɛ̃ mitɛ sika nɛ bati awɛ, ade mitatsua odima onukpɛ̃ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ minyina kĩ ɛtákɛna a akũ. Mifɔ̃ mi kɔtɔkase kuwo mi anɛ.”

english_verse: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yohanes bi be kĩ, “Yĩ kɔ̃ nkɔa mui mawɔla mi mui, lɛmɛ oti odi kĩ ɛbɛ̃ yĩ aya nɛ liti. Ntáfɛta kĩ manyɛ ye mpabua nɛ ye abɔa malo. Oti kĩĩ aakɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔ fi ɛwɔla mi mui.

english_verse: In many different ways John preached the good news to the people.
translation_verse: Ade Yohanes tsã kɔdɛ tisi futefute pii akũ ede lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ kĩ bati bɛdani be kadzi kamɛ a.

english_verse: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade ebuki ɛkɛna tɔkpa kĩ tɛbɛ̃ kɔlaa kĩ epi Yohanes edo nɛ abãã kɔla kamɛ a. Yohanes Wɔla Yesu Mui.

english_verse: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
translation_verse: Kĩ Yohanes wɔla bati a flee mui eyua a, ɛwɔla Yesu lɛmɛ mui. Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu kabɔa mpaɛ a, odetɔ́ kamɛ binya,

english_verse: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ka ndɛ mblɔduma aye ɛyadzi nɛ ye akũ a. Awã naa odo odi to ode ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ yĩ obi kĩ maátunɔ kalɛ. Kawɔlɛ ade yĩ nɛ nɔ akũ.” Yesu Bawanyɔ Kasinu a Kamɛ Bati.

english_verse: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
translation_verse: Kainan ɔlɛga nyɛ Enok, Enok ɔlɛga nyɛ Set. Set ɔlɛga nyɛ Adam, ade Adam ɔlɛga nyɛ Baguma a.

english_verse: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔkɔ ka a, bati kpã be bakɔba a kĩ basɔ fɔsɔ kumɛkumɛ a bɛma Yesu. Ete awɛ nɛ be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi akũ ɛtsa be flee.

english_verse: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Abonsam bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ Baguma obi anɛ ni, kekɔ̃ bi de kĩĩ lɛdani lɛkɛna abodoo.”

english_verse: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Abonsam kpã ye ɛnamɛna kakɔ kadi nɛ ode, ade nɛ sinepo mɔa kabɔa kamɛ ni, ɛnyɛ katinya kamɛ mɔ ke fɔgãtsa a flee ɛtsa Yesu.

english_verse: Just worship me, and you can have it all.”
translation_verse: Foesũ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee aanya nɔ fude ntɛ ɔkɛna akũ ɔkpa yĩ.”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Abonsam kpã Yesu ɛnamɛna Yerusalem nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a ode flee, ade ebi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ Baguma obi anɛ ni, kekɔ̃ vɛnya kaka kanaadza nɛ tite.

english_verse: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛtsa fɔtsa nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade odi nyɛ odi mu ye fɔtsa katsa a. Bisĩ Yesu Nɛ Nazaret.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu naa Nazaret awã kĩ bɛdɔla ye a. Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ a, ɛnaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ yawɛ kakɛna a. Eyidza ɛdza kĩ yakã Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa,

english_verse: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate Hũhũ a tɛ yĩ kamɛ flee, kitonɛkĩ ɛpau yĩ, kĩ nlɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a ntsa ahiãfɔ. Ade edo yĩ kĩ nde lɛtsã kĩ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ awɔba kamɛ aana be akũ akũ kadzi, kĩ banɛkunɛ aamɔ fɔkɔ, bikĩ bideĩ nɛ ɔhau mɔ lubo kamɔ kamɛ a, aana kawɛya.

english_verse: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
translation_verse: Ade nkpa kanyi kĩ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma aamɔ ye bati kɔnya a wo ɔtɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu sua ovoli a emunikimɔ ye ɛkpa fɔtsa akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a, ade ɛkɛna akũ edzi a. Bati a flee ke anɛ nɛ ye akũ, bɛkadã ye kɛnanaana.

english_verse: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
translation_verse: Bati a flee kĩ bɛna nɛ awã a mu ye paa mɔ ye kɔdzɛla kalɛ, ade ye kɔnyakamɔ fɔlɛtsa a kɛna be saa lɛmɛ a. Ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Mbɔɛɛ nnɛ́ Yosef obi a nɛ awĩ a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Nti ĩnyina kĩ míabɔa yĩ lɛgbã kĩĩ kĩ, ‘Ɔwanɛ tsa nɔ akũ,’ ade míabi yĩ lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Fɔtsa a kĩ bunu kĩ ɔkɛna nɛ Kapernaum a ni, kɛna foe odu nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ ɔmatɔ lɛmɛ.’ ”

english_verse: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ odima látɛ dĩ wĩ nɛ ye mɔawɛ ɔmatɔ.

english_verse: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ bakunawa pii na nɛ Israel tite akũ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Eliya dzi nkpã a, kĩ oni fɔ̃ kako futeli ɛlalɛ mɔ sɛkɛ, ade kɔlɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ya nɛ tite a na akũ a.

english_verse: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma tádo Eliya nɛ Israelfɔ bakunawa a kamɛ odima sɛkɛ̃. Mbom okunawa odi kĩ ɛna nɛ Zarepta ɔmatɔ kĩ edeĩ nɛ Sidon tite akũ a sɛkɛ̃ edo ye.

english_verse: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
translation_verse: Bati pii lɛmɛ kasɔ fɔsɔ ngbã nɛ Israel, nɛ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Elisa lɔkɔ, lɛmɛ bɛtátsa be kamɛ odima, kɛba Naeman kĩ eto Siria tite akũ.”

english_verse: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
translation_verse: lɛmɛ Yesu tsã bati a kayite ɛdzakũ. Yesu Blɔmɛna Hũhũ Kpa Nɛ Odi Kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu dzakũ ɛnaa Kapernaum ɔmatɔ kamɛ nɛ Galilea tite akũ, ade ɛkatsa bati fɔtsa nɛ Kawɛya Fuwi akũ a.

english_verse: His teaching amazed them because he spoke with power.
translation_verse: Ye fɔtsa katsa kɛna bati a saa, kitonɛkĩ kɔbɛ̃ na nɛ ye kɔdzɛla kalɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
translation_verse: Luwi a, odi kĩ hũhũ kpa na nɛ ye kamɛ na nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: Jesus ordered the evil spirit to be quiet and come out. The demon threw the man to the ground in front of everyone and left without harming him.
translation_verse: Awã naa, Yesu do hũhũ a onukpɛ̃ kĩ, “Tse kɔdzɛla kanɛ nɛ oti a kamɛ!” Hũhũ a bɔɛ oti a emuni nɛ tite nɛ bati a anɛ kamɛ na, ɛnɛ nɛ ye kamɛ, ade lɛtsama tákɛna ye a.

english_verse: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kɛna bati a flee wãwã ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Litsa fɔlɛtsa odu nɛ awĩ a? Oti kĩĩ deĩ otumi mɔ kɔbɛ̃ yawũni nɛ hũhũ kpa akũ ade batse a.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu akũ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ kayite a na flee. Yesu Tsa Bati Pii.

english_verse: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
translation_verse: Yesu to Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a ɛnaa Simon awã. Lɔkɔ a na Simon nyina waa yasɔ atilili mɔ ɔsĩ, foesũ bɛkpa Yesu kanyi kĩ ɛyaadã ye.

english_verse: So Jesus went over to her and ordered the fever to go away. Right then she was able to get up and serve them a meal.
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu nadza nɛ ye mpa ɔwɛ̃, ewũni nɛ fɔsɔ a akũ, ade fɔnɛ nɛ ye kamɛ eyidza mɔatsɛ ɛkɛna fɔdɛtsa ɛkpa be a.

english_verse: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Hũhũ kpa nɛ́ nɛ bati pii kamɛ mɔ fɔwɔla kabɔa kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Baguma obi a!” Lɛmɛ Yesu bɔa hũhũ kpa a onukpɛ̃ ade ɛtákpa be osi kĩ bɛlɛ kɔdzɛla kitonɛkĩ binyina kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo a. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Nɛ Yudafɔ Fɔtsɛnakɔ̃.

english_verse: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
translation_verse: Odenyinawi a, Yesu dzakũ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛnaa kakɔ dĩĩ kadi, ade bati a do ye kawɛ awɛ a. Kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye a bike ye aduli kĩ ɛtadzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ebi be kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ nnaalɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ akũ a nɛ tɛmatɔ bɛbã lɛmɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ ade foe Baguma do yĩ kĩ ĩyaakɛna a.”

english_verse: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛkalɛ Baguma kalɛ a nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a nɛ tite a na akũ flee.

english_verse: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔkɔ fudi Yesu adzakũ kanaa kakɔ kĩ kɛkɛna dĩĩ kanaabɔa mpaɛ. Yesu Tsa Onyole Odi Kĩ Eveliveli.

english_verse: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ be katɛkado a, ebi onyole a kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃, bɛkɔa nɔ titikpa bite nɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
translation_verse: Yesu wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kĩ ɛnya Simon ode a kamɛ ebi ye kĩ edu ɔkɔlɔ a ɛnamɛna mui anɛ sukuloo. Ɛkɛna akũ edzi nɛ ye kamɛ ɛtsa bati a fɔtsa.

english_verse: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
translation_verse: Ade Simon kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, bɔkɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kadzɔ lɛnyɛ flee lɛmɛ bɔtána lɛtsama. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔlɛ lɔmɔ ni maafɔ̃ bɔta tɛsau a.”

english_verse: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛta tɛsau a bɛnyɛ kɔfɔabi beblebee kĩ fɔfɔ̃ be tɛsau a do kakpeli awɛ.

english_verse: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛbɔa awɛ bɛvɛ be bakɔba nɛ ɔkɔlɔ ɔyade a kamɛ kĩ bɛyaabumɔ be. Bɛya bɛyanyɛ kɔfɔabi biyula tɔkɔlɔ nviã a tiyi limii kĩ tido kayɛla awɛ.

english_verse: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
translation_verse: Ade ayea fɔkɛna ye adzuma bakɔba Yakobo mɔ Yohanes kĩ bɛnyɛ Zebedeo babi a lɛmɛ wãwã a. Ade Yesu bi Simon kĩ, “Tavila. Kito finyaakĩĩ kanaa ni, bati aakanyɛ.”

english_verse: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bɛta be tɔkɔlɔ bɛnadzɛla nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ, bɛfɔnɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa awɛ biyi ade bitomɔ Yesu nɛ liti a. Yesu Tsa Ɔkɔatani Odi.

english_verse: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu akũ fɔlɛtsa gba fɔnaa mɔ lugo, ade bati pii kaya kĩ bayaatse ye fɔtsa katsa, bɛna fɔsɔ katsa lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ bati lidede sũ ni, bɛtána osi biwomɔ ye. Foesũ bɛbɔɛ oti a bide nɛ lɛtsã. Ade bitinya fɔkpã bɛnyɛ ɔkɛ bɛkamɛna ɔsɔnɛ a mɔ ye ɔkɛna biyi nɛ Yesu mɔ bati a flee anɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the experts began arguing, “Jesus must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: Ade Farisifɔ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a do akũ kavia awɛ kĩ, “Owei nyɛ oti kĩĩ kĩ yalɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Baguma akũ? Baguma odi keĩ aapuli ɛkɔa titikpa ete!”

english_verse: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
translation_verse: Fɔɔdabe kalɛ deĩ ɔsĩ fɔbɛ̃, ‘Bɛkɔa nɔ titikpa bite nɔ’ mbɔɛɛ kĩ, ‘Yidza kadza, katsã’?

english_verse: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ míamɔ miyi ɔsũ kĩ yĩ a kĩ ĩnyɛ Otidziwa Obi a ndeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ makɔa bati titikpa kate nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ sũ ni, midã o-o!” Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ eyua a, ɛdani ebi oti a kĩ eveliveli a kĩ, “Mabi nɔ kĩ yidza kabɔɛ nɔ ɔkɛna kanaa kɔla!”

english_verse: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
translation_verse: Lɛtalɛta onyole kĩĩ ta eyidza nɛ bati a flee anɛ kamɛ, ɛbɔɛ ye ɔkɛna a kĩ ye akũ ɛwa a, ɛnaa kɔla mɔ Baguma kamu.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ kɛna bati a flee wãwã. Akũ nyɛnɛ be flee, ade bimu Baguma, bɛkalɛ kĩ, “Litsa awãwã fɔtsa bɔmɔ́ nviã kĩĩ a!” Yesu Vɛ Levi.

english_verse: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kĩ Yesu anɛ nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ́ ɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Levi edzi nɛ ye adzumamu. Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Tomɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Levi yidza ɛfɔnɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa eyi ade etomɔ ye a.

english_verse: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
translation_verse: Owi tábuna mɔ Levi te opunu kpale ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ. Tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ pii mɔ bati bɛbã lɛmɛ na nɛ awã.

english_verse: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Farisifɔ mɔ be bakɔba Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a badi kɛna hũhũhũhũ bivia Yesu fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nde sũ mianya kamɔa mɔ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Basɔnɛ lisĩ bɛkpa tutsonu ɔkɛnanɛ, nnɛ́ bikĩ bideĩ akũ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
translation_verse: Ntáya kĩ mayaavɛ bati kĩ bidzi kpĩĩ, mbom ĩya kĩ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.” Yesu Lɛ Kalɛ Nɛ Baguma Osi Vɔɛ̃ Mɔ Ɔkɔɛ Akũ.

english_verse: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
translation_verse: Bati badi bi Yesu kĩ, “Yohanes fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a awɛ onukpɛ̃ kasui mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ kabɔa mpaɛ ade ayea Farisifɔ bade a lɛmɛ akɛna a. Nde sũ nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ anya, kamɔa?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Miakɔna kĩ bati a kĩ bɛvɛ nɛ obolitsɔ̃ kapikɔ̃ a adzi kɔlɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a deĩ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃? Oowo! Fɔlá lɔmɔ!

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ luwi aaya kĩ baakpã obolitsɔ̃ ɔnyɔ a nɛ be sɛkɛ̃. Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, baasui onukpɛ̃.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛbɔa be lɛgbã kĩ, “Odima lábɛ̃ lima vɔɛ̃ kakɔa katalɛ lima kɔɛ ɔkɛ. Ntɛ ɛkɛna foe lɔmɔ ni, yaabɛ̃ lima vɔɛ̃ a, lɛmɛ lɔvɔɛ̃ mɔ lɔkɔɛ a lɛláaketimɔ akũ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
translation_verse: Ade ayea baáko waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ kɔɛ kamɛ a, kitonɛkĩ waĩ vɔɛ̃ a aafɔ̃ kuvũ a lɛbɛ̃ lɔkɔa waĩ a lɛmɛ aawula.

english_verse: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ biko waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ vɔɛ̃ kamɛ.

english_verse: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
translation_verse: Finyaa, ntɛ odi mɔa waĩ kɔɛ ni, ɛláawɛ kĩ yamɔa bavɔɛ̃. Lɛtsa a kĩ yaalɛ nyɛ kĩ, ‘Waĩ kɔɛ a nɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu nyi be kakɔna ade ebi onyole a kĩ, “Yidza kayaadza nɛ bati anɛ kamɛ nɛ awĩ.” Onyole a nɛ ɛyadza nɛ awã.

english_verse: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Ade ke Farisifɔ a badi via be kĩ, “Nde sũ miakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ boe mbla tákpa osi kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ?”

english_verse: Long ago your own people did these same things to the prophets. So when this happens to you, be happy and jump for joy! You will have a great reward in heaven.
translation_verse: “Mimɔ kawɔlɛ, ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ a, mito, mimɔ kawɔlɛ kitonɛkĩ mideĩ kɔtɔkase kpale nɛ ode. Mite anɛ lɛmɛ kĩ ade fukĩĩ odu be bawanyɔ kɛna Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ kɔɛ a.

english_verse: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
translation_verse: Mikpa bikĩ badumu mi a nhila. Mibɔa mpaɛ mikpa bikĩ bakɛna mi tɛnya a.

english_verse: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
translation_verse: Ade be nyɛ Simon kĩ ete dĩ kĩ Petro mɔ ɔwaɛ̃ Andrea, Yakobo mɔ Yohanes, Filipo mɔ Bartolomeo.

english_verse: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi bɛbã akũ ni, Yesu naa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a ɛnaatsa fɔtsa. Onyole odi kĩ ye kɔkpɔ ku na nɛ awã.

english_verse: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
translation_verse: Yudafɔ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a na kalɔkpa mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ade bɛkapi adzuni nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ baakɛna ye akũ a. Yesu Nyɛ Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ Ewua-nviã.

english_verse: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, ɛvɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a flee ɛkpɔta ade ɛnyɛ be kamɛ bati ewua-nviã ɛvɛ be kĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
translation_verse: Bati a flee kabɔa mbɔdi kĩ bata Yesu kitonɛkĩ kɔbɛ̃ kudi kanɛ kato ye kamɛ katsa be flee. Bati Badi Aamɔ Kawɔlɛ Ade Badi Lɛmɛ Aamɔ Lubo a.

english_verse: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Babɛbã nyɛ Mateo mɔ Toma, Yakobo kĩ ɛnyɛ Alfeo obi mɔ Simon a kĩ epili ye kade lɔlɔ.

english_verse: Jude, who was the son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Matthew
translation_verse: Be nkaɛ nyɛ Yuda kĩ ɛnyɛ Yakobo obi mɔ Yuda Iskariot kĩ ye lɛyatsa Yesu liti osi. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Ɛtsa Bati Lɛmɛ.

english_verse: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
translation_verse: Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi a kĩ miwĩ finyaakĩĩ a, kitonɛkĩ kɔlɛ aakɔ mi! Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi a kĩ miamɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ a, kitonɛkĩ míawo ɔblɔ, mibe.

english_verse: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
translation_verse: Bɛya kĩ bayaatse Yesu fɔtsa katsa bɛna fɔsɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ katsa. Bikĩ hũhũ kpa kaha be a lɛmɛ ya ade ɛtsa be a.

english_verse: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
translation_verse: Baguma aahila mi a kĩ kɔlɛ akɔ mi finyaakĩĩ a, kitonɛkĩ míawĩ! Baguma aahila mi a kĩ miabe finyaa a, kitonɛkĩ miaya kamɛmɛ kɔ̃!

english_verse: God will bless you when others hate you and won't have anything to do with you. God will bless you when people insult you and say cruel things about you, all because you are a follower of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Baguma aahila mi ntɛ bati akesĩ mi, bisĩ mi, bavũna mi, ĩye balɛ kĩ minyɛ bati kpa nɛ Otidziwa Obi a sũ!

english_verse: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
translation_verse: Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi ntɛ bati flee abɔa mi buĩ wĩ, kitonɛkĩ ade aye be bawanyɔ mu fɔvã onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a. Miwɛ Mi Bakesĩnɛ Kalɛ.

english_verse: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
translation_verse: Mikpa bati fɔtsa lɔkɔa Baguma lɛmɛ aakpa mi. Nuwĩnuwĩ Baguma aakɔna, edo ɛmɛnana fuyi tsɔnɔnɔɔnɔ fuwula nɛ ye sɛmɛ kalɛ onukpɛ̃. Fɔtsa kakɔna odu a kĩ mikɔa mikɔna mikpa bati bɛbã a, ade foe odu Baguma lɛmɛ aakɔa ɛkɔna ɛkpa mi a.”

english_verse: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
translation_verse: Ɛla ndɛ oti kĩ ntɛ eyi kɔla kake kayɔ etu ɔkã dzununuunu eke litekũ lɛyɔde nɛ boe akũ. Ntɛ ɔwɔ yili mɔ mui yalɔmɔ kɔla a ni, lɛtsama lɛláakɛna kɔla a, kitonɛkĩ bike koe kake wĩ.

english_verse: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi balɛ nɔ awɛ nɛ ɔlɔtɔ mɔa ni, fɔ̃ ɛbalɛ nɔ nɛ ɔyade a lɛmɛ. Ntɛ odi tɛ nɔ atadiɛ wulaa ni kɔa nɔ atadiɛ anyanglede a lɛmɛ kakpa ye.

english_verse: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaawã nɔ fɔtsa ni, kpa ye, ade ntɛ odi bɔɛ nɔ litsedi ni, tabuki ɔlɛ kĩ atɛ foe.

english_verse: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ miawɛ kĩ bati bɛbã bɛkɛna bɛkpa mi ni, mikɛna foe lɔmɔ mikpa be.

english_verse: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ miawɛ bati a kĩ bawɛ mi kalɛ odi ni, nde sũ kɛnɛ kĩ bihila mi? Tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ malo awɛ bikĩ bawɛ be kalɛ a.

english_verse: If you are kind only to someone who is kind to you, will God be pleased with you for that? Even sinners are kind to people who are kind to them.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ bati a kĩ bakɛna mi sɛmɛ kalɛ odi mɔ miakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ kakpa a ni, nde sũ kɛnɛ kĩ bihila mi? Tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ malo akɛna lɔmɔ.

english_verse: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
translation_verse: Mimɔ bati kɔnya ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma amɔ kɔnya. Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Nɛ Bati Bɛbã Kadzɛmɛna Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
translation_verse: “Mitadzɛmɔ bati bɛbã ayekĩ Baguma lɛmɛ lɛláadzɛmɔ mi. Mitakɔ bati bɛbã kɔtɔ lɔkɔa Baguma lɛmɛ lɛláakɔ mi kɔtɔ. Mikɔa bati tɔkpa mite be, lɔkɔa Baguma lɛmɛ aakɔa ete mi.

english_verse: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã kĩ, “Ɔnɛkunɛ lɛláapuli ɛkpã ɔkɔba ɔnɛkunɛ. Ntɛ ɛkɛna foe ni be nviã a flee baatɔ lɛkɛ.

english_verse: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ lɛbɛ́ ye fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛkɔlɛ fɔtsa eyua ni, yaana ndɛ ye fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ aye.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: “Nde sũ adã sukuluku kĩ sideĩ nɛ awaɛ̃ linebi kamɛ lɛmɛ ɔtsɛ́la luvokũ a kĩ lideĩ nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ linebi kamɛ a?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
translation_verse: Nde aapuli obi awaɛ̃ kĩ, ‘Make nɔ aduli, fɔ̃ ĩnyɛ sukuluku nɛ nɔ linebi kamɛ’ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ luvokũ deĩ nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ linebi kamɛ a? Anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa ɔkɛnanɛ anɛ! Nya lɔtɔ kanyɛ luvokũ a nɛ nɔ linebi kamɛ lɔkɔa aapuli ɔmɔ́ yededeede ɔnyɛ sukuluku nɛ awaɛ̃ linebi kamɛ. Kutse Kudi Nyɛ Kudi Mɔ Koe Libi Kĩ Kɔawũ.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: “Kutse wĩ láwũ babi kpa, ade kutse kĩ kɔnɛ́nɛ lɛmɛ láwũ babi wĩ a.

english_verse: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Babi a kĩ kutse nyɛ kutse awũ a bakɔa kamɔ koe kayi ɔsũ. Baána figi babi nɛ fu kutse kamɛ ade baána akutu nɛ ɔgɔabi akũ a.

english_verse: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
translation_verse: Oti wĩ anyɛ fɔtsa wĩ a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a kanya, ade aye oti kpa lɛmɛ anyɛ fɔtsa kpa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a kanya a. Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ lideĩ nɛ oti ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a, ade de anɛ nɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a. Fɔla Kake Nɛ Litekũ Lɛyɔde Mɔ Ɔsĩ Akũ.

english_verse: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
translation_verse: “Nde sũ miavɛ yĩ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, boe Ɔlate’ lɛmɛ miákɛna nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ matsa mi a akũ?

english_verse: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaátse yĩ fɔlɛtsa, ade yaákɛna nɛ foe akũ lɛmɛ ni, ɛla ndɛ oti kĩ eke kɔla kĩ koe kayɔ litekũ a lɛla ɔsĩ. Ntɛ ɔwɔ yili ɛyalɔmɔ kɔla a na ni, kɔ́aveli kɔtɔ gbloo!”

english_verse: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
translation_verse: Yĩ malo ĩnyɛ oti kĩ ndeĩ nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛbɛ̃ yĩ lɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kayɔ, ade ndeĩ sodzafɔ nɛ yĩ kayɔ a. Ntɛ mbi odi kĩ, ‘Naa ni’ yanaa, ntɛ mbi ɔbɛbã kĩ, ‘Ya ni’ yaya, ade ntɛ mbi yĩ ɔsate lɛmɛ kĩ ‘Kɛna fukĩĩ ni’ yakɛna foe.”

english_verse: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ yawɛ boe kade bati kalɛ, ade ye like boe katsɛnakɔ̃ a ɛkpa boe a.”

english_verse: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
translation_verse: Kĩ boe Ɔlate mɔ́ otsole a, ye fɔlɛtsa kɔ ye kɔnya ade ke ebi ye kĩ, “Fɔ̃ kabe.”

english_verse: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
translation_verse: Bɛya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ ade bike ye aduli mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ, “Oti kĩĩ fɛta ɛkpa nɔ kabumɛna paa,

english_verse: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Ade Simon kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Matɛ kado kĩ oti a kĩ bɛkɔa kɔtɔ kpale bite ye a.” Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Ɔlɛ ɔnɔkɔali.”

english_verse: In this town an army officer's servant was sick and about to die. The officer liked his servant very much.
translation_verse: Awã naa, Romafɔ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi nɛ awã na ɔsumunɛ kĩ yaátunɔ kalɛ. Ɔsumunɛ kĩĩ kasɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ ewo wuda onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ nu Yesu nkã a, edo Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ badi kĩ bɛnaabi ye ɛyaatsa ye ɔsumunɛ a.

english_verse: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe sũ Yesu tomɔ be ɛnaa. Ki kite sukuloo kĩ Yesu awo nɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a do ye bakɔba kĩ bibi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, tabuki ɔha nɔ akũ. Ntákpɔla nkpa kĩ awo nɛ yĩ tɔtɔ kamɛ,

english_verse: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
translation_verse: ade ke ntákpɔla lɛmɛ kĩ maya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ a. Foesũ lɛ kalɛ mɔapɛ lɔkɔa yĩ ɔsumunɛ a aasaɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “In all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nu fukĩĩ a fɔkɛna ye saa. Ɛdani ebi bati kɔdabu a kĩ bitomɔ ye a kĩ, “Mabi mi kĩ ntámɔ tɔ̃ katɛkado kĩĩ odu nɛ Israelfɔ malo kamɛ da!”

english_verse: The officer's friends returned and found the servant well. A Widow's Son
translation_verse: Batɔ a kĩ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a do a muniki bɛnaa tɔtɔ a kamɛ, ade bɛnamɔ kĩ ɔsumunɛ a saɛ a. Yesu Nyina Okunawa Odi Obinyole.

english_verse: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
translation_verse: Kĩ ewo nɛ ɔmatɔ a koletia a, ɛtsɛlamɔ bati kɔdza kĩ bɛnɔnɛ ɔnɔkpa banaabila. Ɔnɔkpa a nyɛ otsole odi kĩ ɛnyɛ okunawa obinyole mɔapɛ, ade ke ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bati pii lɛmɛ tomɔ okunawa a.

english_verse: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
translation_verse: Awã naa Yesu fuã ɛnata ɔnɔkpa apaka a ade bikĩ bɛnɔnɛ ye a fɔ̃ bɛdza a. Ade Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Opopo! Mabi nɔ kĩ yidza!”

english_verse: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkpa a yidza edzi edo kɔdzɛla kalɛ awɛ, ade Yesu kɔa ye edo nɛ ɔma awɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
translation_verse: Libe wo bati a flee ade bimu Baguma bɛlɛ kĩ, “Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ kpale odi nɛ́ ɛta nɛ boe kamɛ! Baguma ka ɛya kĩ yayaanyɛ ye bati!”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu akũ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ Yudea tite mɔ fɔkɔ a kĩ fufuã a flee kamɛ. Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ Do Batɔ Nɛ Yesu Sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: ade edo be nɛ boe Ɔlate sɛkɛ̃ kĩ bɛnaavia ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ oti a kĩ bɛlɛ kĩ yaaya a, mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔdã oti bɛbã anɛ?”

english_verse: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Yesu sɛkɛ̃ a bibi ye kĩ, “Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a do boe kĩ bɔyaavia nɔ kĩ, ‘Nɔ nyɛ oti a kĩ bɛlɛ kĩ yaaya mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔdã oti bɛbã anɛ?’ ”

english_verse: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ a, Yesu tsa bati beblebee fɔsɔ, ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa etomɛna bati kamɛ ade ɛfɔ̃ banɛkunɛ pii lɛmɛ mɔ́ fɔkɔ a.

english_verse: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Ade ɛkpa Yohanes batɔ a mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mimuniki minaabi Yohanes lɛtsa a kĩ mimɔ́ mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ minu a, banɛkunɛ mɔ́ fɔkɔ, bikĩ biveliveli yidza bɛtsɛ̃, bikĩ basɔ fɔsɔ ngbã akũ kpalɛ, batɔsĩnɛ anu fɔkɔ, bɛdzɛla banɔkpa bidzi nkpã, ade bɛlamɛna ahiãfɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a kabi a.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Baguma hila oti a kĩ ɛtamɔ́ nfumusu adima nɛ yĩ akũ.”

english_verse: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Kĩ Yohanes batɔ a dzakũ a, Yesu yi kayɔ mɔ kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ Yohanes akũ kabi bati kɔdabu a kĩ, “Kĩ minaa Yohanes sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a, litsa mikɔna kĩ míamɔ? Mimɔ́ ye ndɛ kɔkaɛ sɔɔta kĩ ɔwɛ abɔa katomɛna katɛ̃mɛna?

english_verse: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Litsa koũ minɛ minaadã? Oti kĩ ɛkɛɛlɛ ye akũ mɔ fɔtsa mɔ kalɛ yanɛ? Bikĩ bɛkɛɛlɛ be akũ mɔ fɔtsa mɔkalɛ bidzi nɛ ɔmɛnɛ kamɛ ni, fɔgã tɔtɔ kamɛ bamɔ be.

english_verse: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Mibi yĩ lɛtsa a koũ sũ miti mina a? Kĩ míamɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ mbɔɛɛ? Mifɔ̃ nti mbi mi kĩ mimɔ́ oti kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ kɔlaa.

english_verse: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
translation_verse: Ade Yohanes akũ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, ‘Maado yĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɛnya nɔ lɔtɔ, ɛyaalɔla osi eyi ɛkpa nɔ.’ ”

english_verse: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ebi be kĩ, “Yohanes bɛ̃ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edzi tɔ̃ nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ. Lɛmɛ odi kĩ ɛnyɛ obisɛ̃ kɔlaa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ bɛ̃ Yohanes.”

english_verse: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
translation_verse: Fɔmɔ fukĩĩ aayanɛ a, bati a flee nu Yohanes fɔlɛtsa a, bɛmɔ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ a titiliwu nyɛ bikĩ bɛkɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ, ade bɛfɔ̃ Yohanes kɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ a.

english_verse: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a sĩ Baguma nhihiɛ a kĩ ɛkɛna eyi ɛkpa be a, ade bɛtátunɔ kĩ Yohanes ɛwɔla be mui a.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ɛnamɛna lɔtɔ kĩ, “Finyaakĩĩ litsa maakɔa nviã babi nkɔna mɔ?

english_verse: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
translation_verse: Bɛla ndɛ babisɛ̃ kĩ bikoni nɛ kɔya bagulo. Babɔa fɔwɔla kabi bakɔba kĩ, ‘Bɔsã obolitsɔ̃ kapi tɔnɔ bɔkpa mi, lɛmɛ mitáto! Bɔsã tukutɔnɔ bɔkpa mi, lɛmɛ mitábe.’

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a ya, ɛkasui onukpɛ̃, ɛtámɔa waĩ, lɛmɛ milɛ kĩ, ‘Hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ!’

english_verse: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a lɛmɛ ya, ɛnya, ɛmɔa lɛmɛ, ade mialɛ kĩ, ‘Midã ɔnyakpanɛ mɔ bavɛ ɔmɔanɛ kĩĩ! Ɛnyɛ tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ ɔkɔba!’

english_verse: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati flee a kĩ bɛtɛ Baguma kadikanyi ni, be fɔkɛlatsa atsa kĩ fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa.” Yesu Naa Simon Farisini a Awã.

english_verse: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
translation_verse: Farisini odi vɛ Yesu kĩ ɛyaanya nɛ ye awã. Yesu tunɔ ɛnaa ɛnadzi bɛkanya.

english_verse: When a sinful woman in that town found out that Jesus was there, she bought an expensive bottle of perfume.
translation_verse: Otsole odi kĩ ɛtábɔa ɔbla wĩ na nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ. Kĩ enu kĩ Yesu na nɛ Farisini a tɔtɔ kamɛ ɛkanya a, ɛbɔɛ tumpã mɔkalɛ mɔ kugua mɔ ɔlɛ̃ mɔlɛ̃ ɛyamɛna nɛ awã.

english_verse: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
translation_verse: Ɛdza nɛ Yesu liti nɛ ye abɔa ɔwɛ̃ ade ɛkabe kako nɛ ye fɔnɛta akũ a. Otsole kĩĩ kɔa ye lɔlɔ tɔlɔ ɛyɔ babe a nɛ Yesu fɔnɛta, ade efifi foe akũ, ɛkɔa kugua a lɛmɛ eko nɛ foe akũ a.
