english_verse: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Farisini a mɔ fukĩĩ a, ɛlɛ nɛ ye lɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ obi kĩĩ mɔ nuwĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ yanɛ ni, kufɛ yaanyi kĩ otsole a odu kĩ ɛlamɛna ye katalɛ a nyɛ titikpa ɔkɛnanɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi Farisini a kĩ, “Simon, ndeĩ kalɛ kĩ mabi nɔ.” Simon kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bi yĩ lɛtsa a.”

english_verse: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi otsole a kĩ, “Nɔ katɛkado tɛ nɔ. Naa nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.”

english_verse: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ tápuli bise ye fɔtɔ kĩĩ, foesũ ɛkɔa ete be. Bati nviã kĩĩ kamɛ ɔwɔdabe aawɛ sika ɔvɛ ɔkpanɛ a kalɛ ɛba?”

english_verse: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu dani ɛdã otsole a ebi Simon kĩ, “Amɔ otsole kĩĩ? Kĩ nwo nɛ nɔ tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ɔtákpa yĩ mui kĩ nvɔlɛ fɔnɛta, lɛmɛ otsole kĩĩ kɔa ye babe ɛvɔlɛ yĩ fɔnɛta, ɛkɔa ye tɔlɔ lɛmɛ ɛyɔ be.

english_verse: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
translation_verse: Ɔtákɛna yĩ atuu, lɛmɛ kito lɔkɔ a kĩ ĩya nɛ awĩ a, otsole kĩĩ táfɔ̃ yĩ fɔnɛta kafifi.

english_verse: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
translation_verse: Ɔtákpa yĩ kugua ntsɛla nɛ lɔlɔ kamɛ, lɛmɛ ɛkɔa kugua mɔ ɔlɛ̃ mɔ ɔlɛ̃ ɛtsɛla yĩ fɔnɛta.

english_verse: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
translation_verse: Foesũ mabi nɔ kĩ nɛ ɔdɔ a kĩ ɛnyɛ ɛtsa kĩĩ a sũ ni, bɛkɔa ye titikpa beblebee a bite ye. Lɛmɛ oti kĩ bɛkɔa fɔtsa kabii bite ni, ɔdɔ kabii yanyɛ katsa.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi otsole a kĩ, “Bɛkɔa nɔ titikpa bite nɔ.”

english_verse: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛna nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a kavia be akũ kĩ, “Owei nɛ awĩ a kĩ yakɔa bati tɔkpa malo kate?”

english_verse: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
translation_verse: Batsole badi kĩ Yesu blɔmɛna hũhũ kpa nɛ be kamɛ, mɔ bikĩ ɛtsa be fɔsɔ lɛmɛ tomɔ ye. Be kamɛ ɔmɔa nyɛ Maria Magdalani kĩ ye kamɛ ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa evũkɔnɔ etomɛna a.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
translation_verse: Bikĩ biwula nɛ apibɔ akũ a nyɛ bati a kĩ binu kalɛ a, bɛtɛ ke mɔ kawɔlɛ, lɛmɛ kɛtáwo nɛ be kamɛ dzununuunu. Bɛtɛ bido nɛ lɔkɔ lukũ odi kamɛ, ade ntɛ sɔhuiɛ adi ya ni, bamuniki liti.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
translation_verse: Badi wula nɛ apibɔ akũ, ade kĩ bɛmɛ a bɛvɛ biku, kitonɛkĩ apibɔ a kamɛ lɛla mui.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Badi wula nɛ tuwĩ mɔ fu kamɛ. Tuwĩ mɔ fu a mɛ tipi be.

english_verse: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Bɛnyɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ fɔtsa kɔlawa a bɛtsa mi, lɛmɛ bati bɛbã a kɔ̃ ni, fɔgbã kamɛ fɔaya kakpa be ayekĩ, ‘Baadã lɛmɛ bɛláamɔ biyi ɔsũ, baanu lɛmɛ bɛláa nu foe kayɔ.’ Yesu Nyɛ Lɛgbã Nɛ Okuani Akũ a Kayɔ.

english_verse: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
translation_verse: “Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla ni, baanyɛ de kanya. Ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ bisua de ni, baamɔ de bɛnyɛ bɛyamɛna nɛ ɔhaĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
translation_verse: Kĩ biteĩ nɛ mui anɛ banaa a, Yesu wa tida. Deedimɔapɛ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ odi vũ nɛ lutubu baũ a akũ, ade ɛkabɔa mui kako nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, fɔfɔ̃ bɛna nɛ wuda mɔ nkpã kayite a.

english_verse: This is what the story means: The seed is God's message,
translation_verse: “Lɛgbã a kayɔ nyɛ fukĩĩ. Funitsã babi a nyɛ Baguma kalɛ a.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Babi a kĩ biwula nɛ tuwĩ mɔ fu kamɛ a nyɛ bati a kĩ binu kalɛ a bɛtɛ ke lɛmɛ katinya kamɛ ɔhau, akũ fɔtsa kawɛ mɔ katinya kamɛ kawɔlɛkamɔ anya be akũ, kafɔ̃ baábo anɛ kawũ babi kayɔɔlɛ.

english_verse: Those seeds that fell on good ground are the people who listen to the message and keep it in good and honest hearts. They last and produce a harvest. Light (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ babi a kĩ biwula nɛ tite wĩ akũ a nyɛ bati a kĩ binu kalɛ a, bɛtɛ ke nɛ tɔwɔlɛ wĩ mɔ obu kamɛ ade bipini ke mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kɛnawo kĩ biwũ babi a. Fɔ̃ Ɔsɛdza Ɛkpalɛ Fɔkɔ Ɛkpa Bati.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade evia ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nde mi katɛkado?” Bɛkɛna saa, libe wo be lɛmɛ, ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Owei nyɛ oti kĩĩ? Yado ɔwɛ mɔ mui onukpɛ̃ ade batse ye a!” Yesu Blɔmɛna Hũhũ Kpa Nɛ Onyole Odi Kamɛ.

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
translation_verse: “Odima látɛna ɔsɛdza kakɔa lɔɔda kasua ye, ĩye kakɔa ye kado nɛ mpa kayɔ. Mbom yadɛla ye kate nɛ ɔsɛdza otse akũ ayekĩ bati aamɔ fɔkɔ ntɛ bawo nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Mmɔ́ nɛ yĩ kamɛ kĩ odi ta yĩ ni, kitonɛkĩ kɔbɛ̃ kudi nɛ kuto yĩ kamɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yesu nu fɔlɛtsa a ebi Yairo kĩ, “Tavila. Nɔ kɔ̃ tɛ kado, obisɛ̃ a aasaɛ.”

english_verse: They begged Jesus not to send them to the deep pit, where they would be punished.
translation_verse: Hũhũ kpa a ke Yesu aduli kĩ ɛtablɔmɛna be bɛnaawo nɛ lɛkɛ dzununuunu a kĩ lɛlá kakpulakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
translation_verse: Bati nɛ bɛyaadã lɛtsa kĩ lɛya. Ade kĩ biwo Yesu sɛkɛ̃ a bɛmɔ́ onyole a kĩ ye kamɛ hũhũ kpa a nɛ a kĩ edzi nɛ ye abɔa kayɔ. Edo atadiɛ ade ye anɛ kamɛ lɛmɛ kpalɛ. Libe wo bati a flee.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
translation_verse: Luwi a, Yesu ɔma mɔ bawaɛ̃ ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bifuã ye nɛ bati lidede sũ.

english_verse: As Jesus was getting out of the boat, he was met by a man from this town. The man had demons in him. He had gone naked for a long time and no longer lived in a house, but in the graveyard.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nɛ́ nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ a, onyole odi kĩ hũhũ kpa na nɛ ye kamɛ nɛ eto ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛyatsɛlamɔ ye. Owi buna kĩ onyole kĩĩ tsã ɔdzɔ, ade yaádzi nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ, mbom asie mɔ funikɛ kamɛ yadzi.

english_verse: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
translation_verse: Ade ke onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Yairo kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ ɔmɔa nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ya nɛ awã a. Ɛyake aduli nɛ Yesu abɔa kamɛ ɛwã ye kĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnaa ye tɔtɔ kamɛ,

english_verse: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
translation_verse: Luwi a Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ ade ebi be kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bunyina ɔwɔ lutubu baũ a bɔnaa abuĩ atide a.” Ade biyi ɔwɔ a kanyina kayɔ a.

english_verse: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a nadzɛla Yesu bibi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate! Boe Ɔlate! Buku buyua!” Awã naa Yesu yidza ɛdza ewũni nɛ ɔwɛ mɔ mui a akũ. Ɔwɛ mɔ mui a onukpɛ̃ dɔ, ade awã flee kɛna dĩĩ a.

english_verse: The man saw Jesus and screamed. He knelt down in front of him and shouted, “Jesus, Son of God Most High, what do you want with me? I beg you not to torture me!”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛmɔ Yesu a, ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ade ɛbɔɛ ye akũ emuni nɛ Yesu abɔa kamɛ mɔ fɔwɔla kĩ, “Yesu Baguma Kpale Nɛ Ode Obi! Litsa akamɔ yĩ? Make nɔ aduli, takɛna yĩ awɔda!”

english_verse: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
translation_verse: Kĩ bikĩ badãmɛna baplakuo akũ a mɔ́ lɛtsa kĩ lɛya a, bilo bɛnaa ɔmatɔ mɔ tukula kamɛ bɛkabi bati lɛtsa kĩ lɛya a.

english_verse: He said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to go out of him. The man had often been attacked by the demon. And even though he had been bound with chains and leg irons and kept under guard, he smashed whatever bound him. Then the demon would force him out into lonely places.
translation_verse: Onyole a lɛ fukĩĩ kitonɛkĩ Yesu wũni nɛ hũhũ kpa a akũ kĩ ɛnɛ nɛ oti a kamɛ. Lɔkɔ pii hũhũ kpa a adzi nɛ ye akũ ade ntɛ bido ye nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ nɛ akpɔ mɔ abɔa malo a, yaveliveli ye lɔkɔa hũhũ kpa a akpã ye kanamɛna kɔsa kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
translation_verse: Yesu via ye kĩ, “Nde bavɛ nɔ?” Ade ɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bavɛ yĩ Lidede,” kitonɛkĩ hũhũ kpa beblebee wo nɛ ye kamɛ.

english_verse: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
translation_verse: Baplakuo beblebee na nɛ ɔtɔ odi akũ bɛkadɛ. Foesũ hũhũ kpa a ke Yesu aduli kĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnɛ bɛnawo nɛ be kamɛ, ade Yesu lɛmɛ kpa osi a.

english_verse: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Ade bɛnɛ nɛ onyole a kamɛ bɛnawo nɛ baplakuo a kamɛ a. Be kɔdabu a flee wula atsɛ bɛka ɔtɔ sɛkɛ geĩ a biwo nɛ mui kamɛ, bɛmɔa mui biku.

english_verse: Then all who had seen the man healed told about it.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛmɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a bi bati ayekĩ bɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa nɛ oti a kamɛ.

english_verse: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ bati a flee nɛ Gadara kayite a na ke Yesu aduli kĩ ɛdzakũ nɛ be tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ libe wo be paa. Nɛ foesũ ewo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ ɛdzakũ.

english_verse: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
translation_verse: Onyole a kĩ ye kamɛ hũhũ kpa a nɛ a ke Yesu aduli kĩ, “Fɔ̃ ntomɔ nɔ bɔnaa.” Lɛmɛ Yesu nyɛ ye osi, ebi ye kĩ,

english_verse: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: “Muniki kanaa ɔtɔ kanaalɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma kɛna ɛkpa nɔ a.” Onyole a tsã nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛkalɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Yesu kɛna ɛkpa ye a. Yesu Tsa Yairo Obitsole Mɔ Otsole Odi.

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
translation_verse: Otsole odi kĩ akũ awula ye futeli ewua-nviã flee lɛmɛ na nɛ bati a kamɛ. Otsole kĩĩ bula ye sika flee kade nɛ fɔsɔ batsanɛ sɛkɛ̃ lɛmɛ odima tápuli ɛtsa ye.

english_verse: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu muniki ɛnaa ɔwɔ lutubu baũ sɛkɛ sitide a, bati a tɛ ye mɔ kawɔlɛ kitonɛkĩ be flee bidzi bɛkadzɔlɛ ye.

english_verse: because his twelve-year-old child was dying. She was his only daughter. While Jesus was on his way, people were crowding all around him.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ ye obitsole mɔapɛ kĩ ɛtɛ futeli ewua-nviã waa nɛ wuda mɔ nkpã kayite. Kĩ Yesu kanaa a, bati pii yi ye akũ.

english_verse: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛna bɔɛɛ ɛtsã bati a kamɛ nɛ Yesu liti ɛta ye atadiɛ sɔtɔ. Deedimɔapɛ babɔanɛ a fɔ̃ kawula.

english_verse: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu via kĩ, “Owei lɛta yĩ?” Lɛmɛ odima tákpeti. Foesũ Petro bi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, dã ayekĩ bati beblebee yi nɔ akũ!”

english_verse: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
translation_verse: Kĩ otsole a mɔ eyi ɔsũ kĩ bɛmɔ́ ye sũ ni, ɛna nɛ kukũtila kamɛ ɛnɛ ɛyake aduli nɛ Yesu abɔa kamɛ. Nɛ bati a flee anɛ kamɛ na, ebi ye lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa. Ebi ye lɛtsa a kĩ de sũ ɛta ye, mɔ ayekĩ ɛsaɛ lɛtalɛta a.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ obitsole, nɔ katɛkado kpa nɔ akũ ɔsĩ. Naa nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.”

english_verse: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu kalɛ fukĩĩ a, ɔtɔ to Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a awã ɛyabi Yairo kĩ, “Nɔ obitsole a ku, fɔtábuki fusĩ kĩ aha fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ a.”

english_verse: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu wo Yairo tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ɛtákpa odima osi ewo nɛ kɔla a kamɛ, mbɔɛntɛ Petro mɔ Yohanes mɔ Yakobo mɔ obisɛ̃ a ɔlɛga mɔ ɔma odi.

english_verse: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Bati pii dza nɛ awã bɛkabe mɔ kɔnya nɛ obisɛ̃ a wuda sũ. Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ kabe. Obisɛ̃ a táku. Tida kɛkɛ ɛwaa.”

english_verse: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati a flee mɛmɛ ye kitonɛkĩ binyina kĩ obisɛ̃ a ku.

english_verse: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu pi obisɛ̃ a nɛ kɔkpɔ ɛvɛ ye kɔɔba kĩ, “Yĩ obi, yidza kadza!”

english_verse: She came back to life and got right up. Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
translation_verse: Ade ke katsolebi a nyinya kiyidza kɛdza deedimɔapɛ a. Yesu bi be kĩ bɛkpa ke funitsã kɛnya.

english_verse: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ nyɛnɛ bamanɛ a akũ, lɛmɛ Yesu si be mbla kĩ bɛtabi odima lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a.

english_verse: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a muniki bɛya a, bibi Yesu lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkɛna. Ade ɛkpã be odi bɛnɛ bɔɛɛ bɛnaa Betsaida ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: They did this, and all the people sat down.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kɛna foe lɔmɔ biyua a,

english_verse: Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life.
translation_verse: Kĩ Lɛgã Herodes, Galilea tite akũ ɔnyanɛ a nu fɔtsa kĩ fɔlamɛna kaya a, ɛkɛna basaa, kitonɛkĩ bati badi kalɛ kĩ Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a libuki eyidza edzi nkpã.

english_verse: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
translation_verse: Fɔmɔ baanaa ni, Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mitabɔɛ lɛtsama ndɛ otse kĩ bake katsã, fɔtsa bawãnɛ kuvũ, funitsã ĩye sika milɛ. Mitabɔɛ atadiɛ ayade malo mibu.

english_verse: When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town.
translation_verse: Kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ mise ni, mina nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ míadzakũ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, “Send the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!”
translation_verse: Kĩ suwa anaa katomukɔ̃ a, Yesu fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã yabi ye kĩ, “Fɔ̃ bati a bɛdzakũ bɛnaa tukula mɔ fɔpɛ a kĩ fufuã a kamɛ bɛnaawɛ funitsã mɔ fɔwakɔ̃ kitonɛkĩ awĩ lɛnyɛ́ bati kadzikɔ̃.”

english_verse: While he was praying, his face changed, and his clothes became shining white.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkabɔa mpaɛ a ye anɛ kamɛ dani, ade ye tɛtadiɛ kɛna fita fita, tɛkata muamua a.

english_verse: Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Yesu si be mbla mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bɛtabi odima fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ. Yesu Lɛ Kalɛ Nɛ Ye Lubokamɔ Mɔ Wuda Akũ.

english_verse: “Pay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.”
translation_verse: “Mitalɛla lɛtsa a kĩ maabi mi a! Baakɔa Otidziwa Obi a bido nɛ bati awɛ.”

english_verse: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ bati kɔdabu a nu foe a, bitomɔ ye bɛnaa awã. Yesu tɛ be ade ebi be fɔlɛtsa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ akũ, ɛtsa bikĩ bisĩ fɔsɔ katsa lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye mɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ bɛbã a kĩ, “Mitasua ye osi, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdzɛ́ nɛ mi akũ ni, mi etomɛna.” Samariafɔ Badi Sĩ Yesu.

english_verse: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ɛkwansieda mɔa liti kĩ Yesu lɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ɛkpã Petro, Yohanes mɔ Yakobo bide bɛnaa ɔtɔ odi akũ kĩ banaabɔa mpaɛ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛfɔnɛ ye nkpã awɛ ɛkpa nɛ yĩ katomɛna sũ ni, yaana nkpã wĩ a kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
translation_verse: Litsa nfasɔ oti aana ntɛ ɛna katinya kamɛ fɔtsa flee lɛmɛ ɛyɛla ye mɔawɛ nkpã?

english_verse: If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels.
translation_verse: Ntɛ yĩ mɔ yĩ fɔtsa a kĩ matsa a nyɛ fɔnyɔãtsa fɔkpa odi ni, ye malo yaanya fɔnyɔãtsa ɛkpa Yĩ Otidziwa Obi a, ntɛ ĩya nɛ yĩ kukũkpalɛ mɔ Nda mɔ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ kpalɛwa kukũkpalɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
translation_verse: Bɛna nɛ ode kukũkpalɛ kamɛ ade bɛkalɔmɛna mɔ Yesu nɛ osi a kĩ ye akũ yaatsã ɛkɛna nɛ Baguma nhihiɛ a kĩ ɛkɛna eyi kĩ yaaku nɛ Yerusalem akũ a.

english_verse: ) While everyone was still amazed at what Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ Petro mɔ ye bade a wa tida kɔlaa malo ni, binyinya bɛmɔ́ Yesu kukũkpalɛ mɔ bati nviã a kĩ bɛdza nɛ ye ɔwɛ̃ a.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a, luflututu ka ade de kuyo ko nɛ be akũ a. Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a vila nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ luflututu a ka lisua be akũ a.

english_verse: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Ade odo odi nɛ eto luflututu a kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ obi a kĩ mpau a. Mitse ye kalɛ.”

english_verse: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Kĩ odo a tsua a, Yesu odi lite nɛ awã. Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a sua onukpɛ̃ nɛ fukĩĩ flee akũ ade bɛtábi odima kadima nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛmɔ́ a akũ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a. Yesu Tsa Kanyolebi Kĩ Hũhũ Kpa Deĩ Nɛ Ke Kamɛ.

english_verse: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
translation_verse: Hũhũ kpa adi adzi nɛ ye akũ lɔkɔa yabɔa fɔwɔla. Odetɔ́ fɔsɔ ade kadzi nɛ ye akũ, kabɔɛ ye kamuni, lɔkɔa kuvũ anɛ nɛ ye onukpɛ̃. Fɔafɔ̃ yapila ade hũhũ a lɛvá ye awɛ kafɔnɛ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “O nviã babi mɔ atɔ ɔsĩ kĩ milá katɛkado! Lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe maana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃, nna ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ lɛmɛ nkpa mi?” Ade ebi onyole a kĩ, “Kpã nɔ obi a kayamɛna nɛ awĩ.”

english_verse: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
translation_verse: Kĩ kanyolebi a yɛlɛ kaya a, hũhũ kpa a bɔɛ ye emuni nɛ tite ade ke odetɔ́ fɔsɔ de fudzi nɛ ke akũ a. Awã naa Yesu do hũhũ kpa a onukpɛ̃, ade ɛtsa kanyolebi a ɛkɔa ke ɛkpa ɔlɛga a.

english_verse: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a tánu foe kayɔ. Bɛkɔa foe bɛkɔla be kĩ bɛtanu foe kayɔ ade bivila kĩ bavia ye foe kayɔ a. Yesu Tsa Oti a Odu Kĩ Ɛbɛ̃ Flee a.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kavɛlɛ ya nɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ nɛ owula a kĩ ye bɛ̃ flee nɛ be kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nyi lɛtsa kĩ bɛkakɔna a, ɛvɛ obisɛ̃ odi ɛyadzɛla nɛ ye ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ obisɛ̃ kĩĩ nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, ade yĩ ɛtɛ a, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ yĩ ni, ɛtɛ owula a kĩ edo yĩ fɔtɔ a. Kitonɛkĩ owula kĩ ɛnyɛ obisɛ̃ kɔlaa nɛ mi kamɛ a, ade ye lɛtɛ kɔya ɛbɛ̃ a.” Oti Kĩ Ɛdzɛ́ Nɛ Mi Akũ Ni, Etomɔ Mi.

english_verse: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
translation_verse: Yohanes bi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, bɔmɔ́ onyole odi kĩ ɛkablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa nɛ nɔ dĩ akũ, lɛmɛ busua ye osi, kitonɛkĩ ɛláa boe kamɛ.”

english_verse: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kĩ lɔkɔ kawo ɔtɔ kĩ Baguma aabɔɛ Yesu ɛnamɛna ode a, esipi kĩ yanaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
translation_verse: Edo fɔtɔ banyanɛ badi bɛnya ye lɔtɔ bɛnaa Samaria akula adi kamɛ kĩ bɛnaamanɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa bɛkpa ye kaya.

english_verse: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ akula a kamɛ bati sĩ kĩ baátɛ Yesu, kitonɛkĩ binyina kĩ ɛtɛ̃ yanaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yohanes mɔ Yakobo nu fɔlɛtsa a, bivia Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, awɛ kĩ bɔvɛ fi butomɛna ode fɔyaawɔla be?”

english_verse: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a tɛ̃ bɛnaa akula bɛbã kamɛ. Bati Badi Wɛ Kĩ Batomɔ Yesu.

english_verse: Along the way someone said to Jesus, “I'll follow you anywhere!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtsɛ̃ banaa a, onyole odi bi Yesu kĩ, “Maatomɔ nɔ nnaa kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ aanaa.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Kulikii deĩ fudzikɔ̃, kɔvɔlabi lɛmɛ deĩ fɔtsɔɛ, lɛmɛ Otidziwa Obi a lɛlá awã kĩ yate lɔlɔ malo.”

english_verse: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Ebi oti bɛbã kĩ, “Tomɔ yĩ.” Lɛmɛ oti a lɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, maatomɔ nɔ, lɛmɛ fɔ̃ nnaabila nda tɔ̃.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Let the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Fɔnɛ bikĩ bɛlá Baguma nkpã mɔawɛ a bibila be banɔkpa. Nɔ kɔ̃ naa kanaade lɛtsã nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ.”

english_verse: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
translation_verse: Oti bɛbã kɔ̃ lɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, maatomɔ nɔ, lɛmɛ fɔ̃ nnaakla yĩ kɔla bati tɔ̃.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Oti kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ yapupu, mɔ yadani kadã liti ni, ɛtáfɛta ɛkpa Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ adzuma a.”

english_verse: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Fudutsa a kĩ míave a pɔnɛ lɛmɛ adzuma bakɛnanɛ a lɛpɔ́nɛ. Mibɔa mpaɛ mikpa kɔba ɔlate a kĩ edo adzuma bakɛnanɛ pii bɛyaave fudutsa a.

english_verse: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
translation_verse: Minaa! Mado mi fɔtɔ ndɛ bavelibi nɛ kulikii kamɛ.

english_verse: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
translation_verse: Mitabɔɛ sika kuvũ, ĩye osi katsã fuvũ, ĩye mpabua milɛ, ade mitafɔ̃ midza nɛ osi kĩ miawɔ̃na odima a.

english_verse: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
translation_verse: “Ntɛ miwo tɔtɔ tidi kamɛ ni, minya lɔtɔ milɛ kĩ, ‘Lukudɔ lɛya nɛ tɔtɔ kĩĩ kamɛ.’

english_verse: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti kĩ yawɛ lukudɔ deĩ nɛ awã ni, mifɔ̃ mi lukudɔ a litomɔ ye. Lɛmɛ ntɛ odima lɛláa ni, mi nhila a aamuniki ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Mina nɛ tɔtɔ mɔapɛ a kamɛ, minya mimɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkpa mi. Kitonɛkĩ adzuma ɔkɛnanɛ odi nyɛ odi sĩ kɔtɔkase. Mitatu nɛ tɔtɔ mɔa kamɛ minaa tɔtɔ bɛbã kamɛ.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ miwo ɔmatɔ odi kamɛ mɔ bɛtɛ mi ni, minya lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkpa mi.

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: Mitsa basɔnɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ lɔkɔa mibi bati a kĩ, ‘Baguma lɛgãkanya lɔkɔ a fuã mi.’

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ miwo ɔmatɔ odi kamɛ mɔ bɛtátɛ mi ni, midzakũ minaa ye tɔblɔnu akũ lɔkɔa milɛ kĩ,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade mabi mi pipiipi kĩ nɛ bati kadzɛmɛna luwi a akũ ni, Baguma aamɔ Sodom ɔmatɔ a kɔnya ɛba ɔmatɔ a na! Tɛmatɔ Tidi Kĩ Tisĩ Kalɛ a.

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kawɔlɛ kɛtade mi kĩ hũhũ kpa tse mi kalɛ, mbom mimɔ kawɔlɛ kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ mi buĩ biyi nɛ ode.” Yesu Mu Baguma.

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ pii mɔ fɔgã wɛ kĩ bamɔ lɛtsa a kĩ mimɔ́ finyaa, lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛmɔ́, ade bɛwɛ kĩ banu lɛtsa a kĩ mianu a, lɛmɛ bɛtánu foe.” Yesu Bi Be Kalɛ Nɛ Samariani Sɛmɛ Kalɛ Ɔkɛnanɛ Odi Akũ.

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Baguma aamɔ Tiro mɔ Sidon kɔnya nɛ kadzɛmɛna luwi a akũ ɛba mi.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Ade nɔ Kapernaum ɔmatɔ! Kawɛ ɔwɛ kĩ adɛla nɔ akũ nɛ ode? Baakɛna nɔ nɛ kayɔ ɔnaa kanɔkpade!”

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mmɔ́ Abonsam ndɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ kamɔanɛ aye kĩ eto ode ɛyatɔ.

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: Mitse! Nkpa mi kɔbɛ̃ ayekĩ míapuli misuma bɔɛ mɔ kɔkpɔ̃na, ade míanya Okesĩnɛ a kɔbɛ̃ akũ kĩ lɛtsama lɛláakɛna mi a.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Luwi a, Yudani mbla ɔtsanɛ a odi ya kĩ yasɔ Yesu ɛdã. Evia ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna nna nkpã a kĩ ɛlá kawũna a?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Onyole a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Wɛ nɔ Ɔlate, nɔ Baguma kalɛ mɔ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ flee, nɔ ɔkla flee nɔ kɔbɛ̃ flee mɔ nɔ kakɔna flee, kawɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ mɔawɛ akũ a.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kawɔlɛ wo nɛ Yesu kamɛ ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Yĩ Ɔlɛga, tite mɔ ode Ɔlate, manyɛ nɔ kɔlɛ kitonɛkĩ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla kadi banyinɛ mɔ tuvoli banyinɛ a, ɔnyɛ foe ɔtsa bikĩ binyína tuvoli ndɛ babisɛ̃ aye. Oo, Nda, ade aye koũ ɔwɛ kĩ fɔna a.”

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nda kɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa edo nɛ yĩ awɛ. Odima linyína oti a kĩ Baguma Obi a anɛ mbɔɛntɛ Ɔlɛga a odi keĩ. Ade aye odima lɛmɛ linyína oti a kĩ Ɔlɛga a anɛ, mbɔɛntɛ Obi odi mɔ bikĩ Obi aawɛ kĩ yanyɛ Ɔlɛga ɛtsa a.”

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Ade Yesu dani ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a odi bɔɛɛ kĩ, “Lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ Baguma hila mi! Mi anɛ amɔ ade mi atɔ lɛmɛ anu fɔkɔ a.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Litsa Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ? Nde onu foe kayɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Ɔlɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Kɛna fukĩĩ lɔkɔa aana nkpã.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mbla ɔtsanɛ kĩĩ wɛ kĩ yakpa ye akũ ɔtɔbɛ, foesũ evia Yesu kĩ, “Owei nyɛ yĩ ɔkɔba?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Luwi a, onyole odi to Yerusalem yanaa Yeriko ɔmatɔ kamɛ, ade bayekpanɛ napi ye nɛ osi a. Bibo ye bɛyɛlɛ ye akũ tɛtadiɛ, ade bɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ biyi nɛ wuda mɔ nkpã kayite a.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Fɔyaya kĩ ɔsɔfɔ odi yatsã nɛ osi a na akũ yatɛ̃, lɛmɛ kĩ ɛmɔ oti kĩĩ a ɛtsã ɔɔwɛ̃ ɛtɛ̃.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Levini lɛmɛ yatɛ̃ a. Efuã ɛnadã onyole a, ade ye malo ɛtsã ɔɔwɛ̃ ɛtɛ̃ a.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Samariani odi kĩ ɛtsɛ̃ osi a akũ yamɔ oti a. Kĩ ɛmɔ oti a, ye akũ kɔ ye kɔnya.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Efuã ye ade ɛkɔa kugua mɔ bavɛ eko nɛ ye fɔɔkpa kamɛ esui foe a. Ade ɛbɔɛ onyole a ete nɛ ye mɔawɛ ɔyaɛ akũ, ɛkɔa ye ɛnamɛna banɔvɔɛ̃ kasɛkɔ̃ ade ɛdã ye akũ a.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, ɛnyɛ sika ɛkpa kasɛkɔ̃ a akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a ebi ye kĩ, ‘Dã ye akũ. Ntɛ mmuniki matɛ̃ ni maase nɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ aabuki obula kade nɛ ye akũ a kɔtɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu wũna evia ye kĩ, “Nɛ nɔ kakɔna onukpɛ̃ ni, bati ɛlalɛ kĩĩ kamɛ a, ɔwɔdabe lɛnyɛ ye tɔkɔba kakɛna ɛtsa oti a kĩ ewo nɛ bayekpanɛ awɛ kamɛ a?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Ade mbla ɔtsanɛ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oti a kĩ ɛmɔ ye kɔnya a.” Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ nɔ malo naa kanaakɛna foe lɔmɔ.” Yesu Nasɛla Marta Mɔ Maria.

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Luwi a, Yesu mɔ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a tsɛ̃ nɛ osi ade bɛnawo akula adi kamɛ a. Otsole odi kĩ bavɛ Marta tɛ ye nɛ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Marta deĩ ɔwaɛ̃tsole kĩ bavɛ Maria. Edzi nɛ boe Ɔlate ɔwɛ̃ ɛkatse ye fɔtsa katsa.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Fɔha Marta kĩ tɔtɔ kamɛ adzuma ko nɛ ye odi akũ, foesũ ɛnabi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, fɔtsɛ́la nɔ akũ kĩ nwaɛ̃ kɔa tɔtɔ kamɛ tudzuma a flee eko nɛ yĩ odi akũ? Bi ye ɛyaabumɛna yĩ!”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Ade boe Ɔlate kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “O Marta! Dã, aha nɔ akũ nɛ fɔtsa pii akũ,

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: lɛmɛ lɛtsa mɔapɛ lisĩ. Maria nyɛ lɛtsa wĩ a kĩ bɛláatɛ nɛ ye awɛ.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Foesũ mabi mi kĩ mivia fɔtsa lɔkɔa baakpa mi, miwɛ lɔkɔa míamɔ foe, mibɔa opunu lɔkɔa baabinya bɛkpa mi.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ minyɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ malo ni, minyina ayekĩ miate mi babi fɔtsa wĩ. Nde fɔ́aya mɔ boe Ɔlɛga nɛ ode lɛláakɔa ye Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ɛkpa bikĩ baavia ye a!” Bati Badi Tɛ Atsindze Nɛ Yesu Kɔbɛ̃ Akũ.

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: “Bɔɛ foe kĩ nɔ ɔkɔba a nɛ́ nɛ kɔla kamɛ ɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Taha yĩ kɔlaa! Ndo kɔla safi ade bɔmɔ yĩ babi bɔwa kɔkɔɛ. Nláapuli mbuki ĩyidza mbɔɛ lɛtsama nkpa nɔ finyaa.’

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: “Mi kamɛ ɔwɔdabe kĩ ɛnyɛ obilɛga, aakɔa kɔɛ ɛkpa obi ntɛ evia ye kafɔabi?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Ntɛ miabɔa mpaɛ ni, milɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlɛga, fɔ̃ bɛkpalɛ nɔ dĩ akũ. Nɔ lɛgãkanya a kɛya. Bɛkɛna nɛ nɔ kawɛ akũ, nɛ tite akũ ndɛ ayekĩ bakɛna nɛ ode a.

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Kɔa boe tɔkpa kate boe kitonɛkĩ boe malo bɔakɔa odi nyɛ odi tɔkpa kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ boe akũ a kate. Tafɔ̃ buwo nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ, mbom nyɛ boe katomɛna tɔkpa kamɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwã fɔtsa ni, yaana foe. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwɛ litsedi ni, yaamɔ de, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ yabɔa opunu ni, baabinya bɛkpa ye.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Yona nya nsɛntsɛlɛni ɛkpa Ninivefɔ a, ade aye Otidziwa Obi a lɛmɛ aanya nsɛntsɛlɛni nkpa nviã babi a.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu kalɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, otsole odi bɔa fɔwɔla nɛ bati kɔdabu a kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Baguma hila otsole a kĩ ɛma nɔ, mɔ lɛmɛ a kĩ de ɔmɛmɛ a!”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Luwi a, Yesu kablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa kĩ yaálɛ kɔdzɛla etomɛna odi kamɛ. Kĩ hũhũ kpa a nɛ a, oti a yi kɔdzɛla kalɛ kayɔ. Fukĩĩ kɛna bati a kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã a wãwã.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Ade badi lɛmɛ wɛ kĩ basɔ Yesu bɛdã, foesũ bibi ye kĩ ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa ɛkɔa ɛtsa kĩ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ eto.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu nyi lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkakɔna a, foesũ ebi be kĩ, “Ɔmaĩ kĩ ɛsɛ̃ nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ, mɔ lɛma nɛ́ nɛ ye kamɛ ni, ɔmaĩ a na aawɔ. Ade aye tɔtɔ nyɛ tɔtɔ kĩ te kamɛ bati sɛ̃ be akũ ni, tayalɛ.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ Abonsam lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a sɛ̃ nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ mɔ banɔ akũ lɛma ni, nde kanyakɔ̃ a na aapuli kɛdza? Mialɛ kĩ mablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa kitonɛkĩ Beelzebul lɛkpa yĩ kɔbɛ̃ sũ.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ aye mablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa a ni, nde bikĩ bitomɔ mi a ablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa? Mi mɔawɛ bati a nyɛ mi kayɔ bɛtsa kĩ fɔvã miama.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ odi kĩ edeĩ kɔbɛ̃ dzi akpadza, edzi yadzɔlɛ ye tɔtɔ ni, enyina kĩ ye akũ fɔtsa waa pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi Farisifɔ! Miawɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ nɛ mi katsɛnakɔ̃ ade miawɛ kĩ bati bɛwɔ̃na mi mɔ obu nɛ kɔya a.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Nɛ awã naa Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa bi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, ntɛ ɔlɛ fukĩĩ ni, ovũna boe malo.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Ntɛ hũhũ kpa nɛ́ nɛ oti kamɛ ni yanaatsã kamini nɛ fɔkɔ wɔlɛwa kawɛ kasekɔ̃. Ntɛ ɛtána kasekɔ̃ ni, yabi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Maamuniki nnaa yĩ tɔtɔ a kĩ te kamɛ nnɛ́ nto a kamɛ.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Foesũ yamuniki kanaamɔ kĩ bɛyɔ tɔtɔ a kamɛ bɛmanɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa yamuniki kanaawɛ hũhũ kpa evũkɔnɔ kĩ bideĩ ablɔ bɛbɛ̃ ye kabumɔ ye akũ bayaadzi nɛ awã. Ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ a, oti a kadzi kamɛ abu kade kaba ayekĩ fɔna nɛ kayɔkayi a.” Yesu Tsa Fɔtsa Nɛ Kawɔlɛ Wĩ a Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mbom bihila bikĩ batse Baguma kalɛ, kĩ bakɛna nɛ ke akũ lɛmɛ a!” Yesu Sĩ Kĩ Yaákɛna Awãwã Fɔtsa Ɛtsa.

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna Farisini a wãwã kĩ ɛmɔ kĩ Yesu távɔlɛ awɛ ndɛ ayekĩ mbla tsa a fɔmɔ eyi kanya kayɔ.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi Farisifɔ! Miakɔa tɛnyɛ kumɛkumɛ kĩ tabɔa mɔ ɔlɛ̃ mɔ ɔlɛ̃ a te katsu kawuade kakpa Baguma, lɛmɛ misĩ ɔtɔbɛ kakpa bati ade miáwɛ Baguma kalɛ lɛmɛ a. Ade fukĩĩ bamɛ kɛnɛ kĩ miakɛna mibumɔ foe nkaɛ a.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Kĩ bati kɔdabu a bɔa opim nɛ Yesu akũ a, ɛlɛ ɛnaa lɔtɔ kĩ, “Nviã babi nyɛ bati kpa paa! Bawɛ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa bɛmɔ́ lɛmɛ bɛláamɔ fudima kɛba Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yona fude a.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ ni, Seba Lɛgãtsole a aayidza ɛdza ɛkɔ nviã babi kɔtɔ kitonɛkĩ eto ye tite akũ nɛ kakɔ mɔ lugo ɛnatse Lɛgã Salomo kadikanyi fɔtsa katsa a, lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ odi kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Salomo deĩ nɛ awĩ.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Ninivefɔ lɛmɛ aayidza bɛkɔ mi kɔtɔ nɛ Kadzɛ Luwi a akũ, kitonɛkĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Yona bi be Baguma kalɛ a. Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ odi kĩ ɛbɛ̃ Yona deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ nɛ awĩ. Yesu Tsa Lɛtsa a Kĩ Lakpalɛ Oti Akũ.

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: “Odima látɛna ɔsɛdza kakɔla ye ĩye kakɔa lɔɔda kasua ye. Mbom bakɔa ye kate nɛ ɔsɛdza otse akũ ayekĩ ntɛ bati awo nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ ni, baamɔ fɔkɔ yededeede.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Oti banebi kɛna ndɛ ɔsɛdza ɛkpa ye kutitse. Ntɛ nɔ anɛ amɔ kamɔ wĩ ni, nɔ kutitse a flee deĩ nɛ ɔhaĩ kamɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ nɔ anɛ lɛnɛ́nɛ ni, nɔ kutitse a flee aana nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Bakɔasia! Nnɛ́ Baguma a kĩ ɛkɛna fɔtsa fɔmɛde a lɛkɛna kanya fude a lɛmɛ mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi paa kitonɛkĩ mike funi mɔkalɛ mikpa Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a. Ade Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kĩ mi bawanyɔ kɔ a.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu yua kɔdzɛla a, Farisini odi tsɛlamɔ ye kĩ ɛyaanya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃. Foesũ ɛnaa awã ɛnɛ edzi kĩ yanya.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Foesũ boe Ɔlate bi ye kĩ, “Finyaa ni, mi Farisifɔ miavɔlɛ mi tɔkɔɔpu mɔ fɔɔdaɛ liti, lɛmɛ anɛngbã mɔ ablɔ yi mi kamɛ tɛtɛɛtɛ.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Mikɔa fɔtsa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ tɔkɔɔpu mɔ fɔɔdaɛ kamɛ a mikpa ahiãfɔ lɔkɔa mi lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa akũ aakpalɛ nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi! Mila ndɛ funi kĩ bɛtáyi foe ɔsũ, kĩ bati tsɛ̃ nɛ foe akũ nɛ katanyi kamɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ a lɛmɛ. Milɔ́la bati fɔlɔtsa kĩ foe kalɔ deĩ ɔlɔmɛ, lɛmɛ mi mɔawɛ miáyo awɛ kĩ míabumɔ be fɔtsa a kalɔ.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Mi mɔawɛ a mitunɔ kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ mi bawanyɔ kɛna a nɛnɛ. Bɛkɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a ade mi mike be funi a.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ Baguma nɛ ye kadikanyi kamɛ lɛ kĩ, ‘Maado Yĩ onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃. Baakɔ badi, bitomɔ babɛbã lɛmɛ liti.’

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Foesũ baavɛlɛ nviã bati sɔtɔ nɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ flee a kĩ bɛkɔ, kito anɛ katinya kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: Kito Abel kakɔ kɛnawo Zakaria kĩ bɛkɔ nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ mɔ kakɔ kpalɛwa kayite a. Nuwĩ paa kĩ, baavɛlɛ nviã babi sɔtɔ nɛ fukĩĩ flee sũ!

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Bɔbɔɛ makpa mi mbla batsanɛ a. Kitonɛkĩ mikɔla safi a kĩ sabinya kadikanyi kɔla a. Mi mɔawɛ misĩ kĩ miáwo nɛ koe kamɛ ade miasua bikĩ babɔa mbɔdi kĩ bawo a lɛmɛ osi a!”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu dzakũ nɛ awã a, Farisifɔ mɔ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ yi fɔlɛtsa kavɛlɛ kayɔ mɔ Yesu mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ade bɛkavia ye fɔlɛtsa nɛ fɔtsa pii akũ,

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: bɛkabɔa mbɔdi kanaĩ kĩ bapi ye nɛ ye kɔdzɛla kalɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Bati mpim mpim yakpɔta limii kĩ bɛkasumini akũ malo. Yesu nya lɔtɔ ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ paa nɛ Farisifɔ oyila fɔtsa a kĩ fɔnyɛ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa katsa a akũ.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Baguma akɛɛlɛ lupo kamɛ tuwĩ. Tuwĩ adza nviã, kunya tiku, lɔkɔa bipila te. Nde sũ Baguma lɛláadã kĩ ɛkpa mi lɛtsa a kĩ míasua akũ ɛba tuwĩ a? Mi katɛkado adã kabii koũ!

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Mbom miwɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ osi lɔkɔa yaakpa mi fɔtsa kĩĩ flee lɛmɛ. Mite Anɛ Nɛ Ode Lɛgãkanya Fɔtsa Akũ.

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mabi mi kĩ mitavila bikĩ baakɔ sukɛna lɛmɛ ke liti bɛláapuli bɛkɛna lɛtsama fɔba foe.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Mi lɔlɔ tɔlɔ malo bɛkã te flee, foesũ mitavila, kitonɛkĩ mitɛ kɔya mibɛ̃ funitataɛ beblebee. Midza Nɛ Kristo Liti Pintii.

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: “Mi sukuloo a kĩ mitomɔ yĩ a, mitavila, kitonɛkĩ fɔnyɛ Baguma kawɛ kĩ yaakpa mi fudzikɔ̃ nɛ ye lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Minɔ mi akũ kana fɔtsa mikɔa foe sika a mite ahiãfɔ. Minɔ sika fuvulumi a kĩ fɔláakpeli a mikpa mi akũ, lɔkɔa mikɔla mi akũ kana fɔtsa nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a. Awã naa, foe akũ lɛláatinya kitonɛkĩ oyekpanɛ lɛláapuli ɛta foe ade lupuka lidima lɛmɛ lɛláapuli libula foe kade a.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa aatsa mi lɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kamɛ.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Osikani Ɔkɔasia Odi Akũ.

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ Baguma bi ye kĩ, ‘Ɔkɔasia! Lɛnyɛ mɔapɛ kĩĩ baatɛ nɔ nkpã nɛ nɔ awɛ, lɔkɔa owei aanya fɔtsa a kĩ ɔmanɛ oyi ɔkpa nɔ akũ a?’ ”

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Mabi mi yededeede kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛlɛ nɛ bati anɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ yĩ ode ni, Otidziwa Obi lɛmɛ aakɛna lɔmɔ ɛkpa ye nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a anɛ.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ esĩ yĩ nɛ bati anɛ ni, Otidziwa Obi lɛmɛ aasĩ ye nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a anɛ.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: “Foesũ ntɛ bɛkpã mi banaadzɛmɔ mi nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃, ĩye abãã banɔkɔɛ̃ anɛ ĩye fɔgã anɛ ni, mitaha akũ nɛ ayekĩ míapili mi lɔlɔ ĩye lɛtsa a kĩ míalɛ a akũ.

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Onyole odi nɛ bati kɔdabu a kamɛ bi Yesu kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bi nwaɛ̃nyole ɛfɔ̃ bɔsɛ̃ boe ɔlɛga adzapadiɛ a kĩ ɛkɔa eyi boe a.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃, owei lɛkpa yĩ osi kĩ ndzɛ mi fɔlɛtsa ĩye nsɛ̃ adzapadiɛ nkpa mi bati nviã kĩĩ?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Ade ebi be flee kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ, lɔkɔa mipi mi akũ nɛ anɛngbã akũ, kitonɛkĩ oti nkpã lɛláa akũ fɔtsa kamɛ.”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ nɛ ye kamɛ kĩ, ‘Nlá awã kĩ mako yĩ funitsã kĩĩ flee. Litsa maapuli nkɛna?’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: “Ebi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Ade fukĩĩ maakɛna a. Maapansam yĩ tɛpata lɔkɔa nke tɛbaũbaũ awã kĩ maatɛɛ yĩ fugbanɔ mɔ yĩ fɔtsa bɛbã flee.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Foesũ mabi mi kĩ mitaha akũ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ míanya midzi nkpã, ĩye lɛtsa a kĩ míasua akũ a akũ.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: “Midã ayekĩ flawasi abo anɛ. Fɔákɛna adzuma ade fɔánɔ tilima kakpa foe akũ a. Lɛmɛ mabi mi kĩ Lɛgã Salomo mɔ ye akũ kana fɔtsa a flee malo táhɛhɛ fɔlɛ kadã ndɛ nhuilɛ kĩĩ kamɛ fɔmɔa.

english_verse: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
translation_verse: Foesũ mitakaha akũ kɔlaa, mitakabu lɔlɔ kamɛ luwi daa nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ míanya mɔ likĩ míamɔa akũ.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bikĩ bɛtátɛ Baguma bido a, ade be adzi kabu lɔlɔ kamɛ nɛ fukĩĩ bamɛ odu akũ a. Mi Ɔlɛga nyina kĩ misĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ osumunɛ a bi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Yĩ ɔlate buna owi nɛ osi akũ.’ mɔ eyi ye bakɔba basumunɛ batsole mɔ banyole kabo kayɔ, kanya pɔtɔɔ, kamɔa bavɛ kawuga lɛmɛ ni,

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Miakɔna kĩ lukudɔ nkɔa ĩyamɛna nɛ katinya kamɛ mbɔɛɛ? Oowo! Nnɛ́ lukudɔ mbom ɔdzɛ mɔ kasɛ̃ ĩyamɛna.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ aade basumunɛ ntɛ be Ɔlate a yamɔ kĩ bɛmanɛ akũ bidzi badzɔlɛ ye kaya. Mabi mi kĩ yaakɔa ye lima efili nɛ kate, ɛfɔ̃ bidzi lɔkɔa ɛsɛ̃ funitsã ɛkpa be bɛnya.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ aade bikĩ baana nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ titiliwu ntɛ ɛya nɛ lɛnyɛ kayite ĩye odenyinawi.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Ade minyina kĩ ntɛ tɔtɔ ɔlate a enyi lɔkɔ a kĩ oyekpanɛ aya ni, kufɛ ɛláakpa ye osi ebubuli ye kɔla ewo.

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Ade Petro via Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, boe odi ɔbɔa lɛgbã kĩĩ ɔkpa mbɔɛɛ kĩ latamɔ odi nyɛ odi?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Owei nyɛ osumunɛ ɔnɔkɔalini mɔ kadi onyinɛ? Ade ye nyɛ ekĩ ye ɔlate kɔa ɛdzɛla kĩ ɛkaadã tɔtɔ akũ, ɛkpa basumunɛ bɛbã a funitsã nɛ lɔkɔ wĩ akũ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ aade osumunɛ a kĩ ye ɔlate ayamɔ ye kĩ ɛlamɛna fukĩĩ kakɛna!

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali, mabi mi kĩ Ɔlate a aakɔa osumunɛ kĩĩ ɛdzɛla nɛ ye fɔtsa a kĩ edeĩ flee a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: ye ɔlate ayafuta ye luwi mɔa nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛtáte anɛ ĩye ɛláanyi kamɛ. Ɔlate a aavɛlɛ ye sɔtɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ndɛ bikĩ bideĩ atɔ ɔsĩ aye.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ a kĩ enyina ye adzuma kĩ ye ɔlate kpa ye lɛmɛ ɛtámanɛ akũ ɛkpa foe kakɛna ni, yaatɛ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ mɔ ɔsĩ paa.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ osumunɛ kĩ enyína lɛtsa kĩ ye ɔlate awɛ, lɛmɛ ɛkɛna lɛtsa kĩ a lɛfɛta sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ ni, baavɛlɛ ye sɔtɔ sukuloo. Oti kĩ bɛkɔa fɔtsa pii bido nɛ ye awɛ ni, baavia foe pii bitomɛna ye awɛ. Ade oti kĩ bɛkɔa fɔtsa pii kĩ fublusu bido nɛ ye awɛ ni, baavia foe pii kĩ fublusu bitomɛna ye awɛ. Bati Aasɛ̃ Be Akũ Nɛ Yesu Sũ.

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: “Nkɔa kadani vɔɛ̃ kĩ fɔnyɛ fi ĩyaatɛna nɛ katinya kamɛ ade nwɛ kĩ kufɛ fi a fuyi katɛ̃ kayɔ kɔkɔɛ a.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Ndeĩ mui kawɔla lubo kamɔ adi kĩ maatɛ ade ke fɔ́akaha yĩ ɔwɔlɛ flee kɛnaawo luwi a kĩ lubo kamɔ a atɛ̃ nɛ yĩ akũ a.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Babilɛga aayidza bɛdza nɛ be babinyole akũ, ade babinyole aayidza bɛdza nɛ be balɛga akũ a. Babima aayidza bɛdza nɛ be babitsole akũ ade babitsole aayidza bɛdza nɛ be bama akũ a. Nyina amɛ aayidza bɛdza nɛ be babi batsɔ̃ akũ ade babi batsɔ̃ aayidza bɛdza nɛ be nyina amɛ akũ a.” Miyi Lɔkɔ a Ɔsũ.

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ebi bati a kĩ, “Ntɛ mimɔ́ kĩ oni dza nɛ suwa katomukɔ̃ ni mialɛ kĩ oni aako, ade yako lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Ade ke ntɛ mimɔ́ kĩ ɔwɛ bɔa eto kayɔ sɛmakɛ ni, mialɛ kĩ ovio ya, ade fɔaya lɔmɔ a.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Mi anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ, miapuli kadã tite mɔ odetɔ́ kamɛ kamɔ kalɛ lɛtsa a kĩ laaya. Nde sũ miápuli minyɛ nviã lɔkɔ kĩĩ kayɔ? Kɛna Ɔmɔa Mɔ Nɔ Okesĩnɛ.

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Nde sũ miápuli mike otse mikpa mi akũ nɛ lɛtsa wĩ a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ mikɛna akũ?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi bɔɛ kalɛ nɛ nɔ akũ ni, tsã osi nyɛ osi akũ mimɔ ye milɛ foe fɔmɔ yanaadza nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni yaavɛlɛ nɔ ɛma fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ ade ye malo yaakɔa nɔ ɛkpa polisifɔ bɛnaado nɔ nɛ kɔla a.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: Mabi nɔ kĩ ade awã aana flee kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ose kɔtɔ a flee oyua a.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mikɔna kĩ Galileafɔ a kĩ bɛkɔ a tɛnɛ titikpa kakɛna kaba Galileafɔ bɛbã flee a sũ?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Oowo, nnɛ́ aye kɔlaa! Mabi mi kĩ ntɛ mitádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ni, mi a flee míaku ndɛ be aye.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Ade Yesu via kĩ, “Nde Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a la? Litsa maakɔa nkɔnamɔ de?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Oowo! Nnɛ́ aye fɔla kɔlaa! Mabi mi kĩ ntɛ mitádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ni, míaku ndɛ be aye.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Atɔ Kutse Kĩ Kɔáwũ Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã ɛlɛ kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lidi onyole odi du atɔ kutse nɛ ye kɔba kamɛ. Ɛkasɛla kadã ntɛ yaana atɔ babi nɛ koe akũ lɛmɛ ɛtána badima.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ kɔba akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, fɔ̃ kɔdza, kpa yĩ kateli mɔa kabu. Maatu nsinya koe lɔkɔa nko fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́akpa koe tite kanɔ.

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Ntɛ kuwũ atɔ babi kateliva ni, kekɔ̃ fɔnɛnɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ kɔtáwũ babi ni, aapuli ɔfɔ̃ bitsua koe bimuni.’ ” Yesu Tsa Otsole Odi Kĩ Eveliveli Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi Akũ.

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lidi akũ ni, Yesu katsa fɔtsa nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ kadi.

english_verse: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
translation_verse: Otsole odi kĩ hũhũ kpa do ye fɔsɔ mɔ akũ futeli ewua-alɛ̃ flee na nɛ awã. Fɔsɔ a fɔ̃ ekũũkũ ebuki yaápuli eyo akũ kpĩĩ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ ye a ɛvɛ ye ebi ye kĩ, “Boe ɔma, bɛnyɛ nɔ nɛ fɔsɔ kamɛ!”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mi anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ, mianyɛ mi bayaɛ tɔwɛ̃ banaamɔa mui nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ.

english_verse: This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ amɔ Abraham kasinu kamɛ obi kĩ Abonsam do ye fɔsɔ mɔ akũ futeli ewua-alɛ̃ flee. Fɔnɛ́nɛ kĩ banyɛ ye nɛ tɔwɛ̃ kamɛ nɛ Kawɛya Luwi a akũ?”

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Mbuayɛ a kĩ Yesu kpa a fɔ̃ kɔnyɔã wo ye bakesĩnɛ a, lɛmɛ kawɔlɛ de bati a nɛ awãwã fɔtsa flee a kĩ ɛkɛna a sũ. Yesu Kɔa Ode Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a Ɛkɔnamɔ Kutse Libi Kabii Lidi.

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Farisifɔ badi yabi Yesu kĩ, “Wɛ sɔwa kadzakũ nɛ awĩ kĩĩ kanaa kakɔ bɛbã, kitonɛkĩ lɛgã Herodes wɛ kĩ yaakɔ nɔ.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ mpi osi, nviã, kunya mɔ kukunya, kitonɛkĩ fɔtáfɛta kĩ bakɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ nɛ kakɔma kɛba Yerusalem.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Ade bati a kĩ bɛkpa liti finyaa a, badi aadani batɔnyade, ade bikĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ finyaa a, badi aadani liti bakpanɛ.” Yesu Deĩ Ɔdɔ Ɛkpa Yerusalem.

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu bi be kĩ, “Minaabi kalikii a kĩ, ‘Nviã mɔ kunya flee maakablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa, maatsa fɔsɔ lɛmɛ, ade nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde a akũ ni, maayua yĩ adzuma.’

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Fɔla ndɛ fudutsa libi kabii ki onyole odi bɔɛ ɛnadu nɛ ye kɔba. Libi a mɛ libo anɛ lɛkɛna kutse, ade kɔvɔlabi yakɛna fɔtsɔɛ nɛ koe tɛnanɛ kamɛ a.” Yesu Kɔa Ode Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a Ɛkɔnamɔ Oyila Fɔtsa.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Yesu buki evia kĩ, “Litsa maakɔa Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a nkɔnamɔ?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu anaa Yerusalem a, ɛnatsã tɛmatɔ mɔ tukula kamɛ ɛkatsa bati fɔtsa.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Bɔa mbɔdi katsã onyukpɛ̃ a kĩ ɛtása a kamɛ kawo, kitonɛkĩ bati pii abɔa mbɔdi kĩ bawo lɛmɛ bɛláapuli.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Kɔla ɔlate a aayidza ɛyaabalɛ kɔla a, ade ntɛ midza nɛ kanya mikabɔa opunu kalɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, binya kakpa boe ni.’ “Ɔlate a aakpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Ĩnyína mi mɔ awã kĩ mito!’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: “Ade ke míalɛ kĩ, ‘Bɔmɔ nɔ lɛnya bɔmɔa ade ɔtsa fɔtsa nɛ boe ɔmatɔ kamɛ lɛmɛ a!’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ yaakpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Ĩnyína awã kĩ mito! Midzakũ nɛ yĩ ɔwɛ̃, mi tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ.’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: “Awã naa, kabe kadi kĩ míabe, miba adɔ̃ ntɛ mimɔ́ Abraham, Isak mɔ Yakob mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a flee nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ bɛblɔmɛna mi kĩ mitawo.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Bati aato suwa kadekɔ̃ mɔ suwa katomukɔ̃, kayɔ mɔ nyangɛ bɛyaanya bɛmɔa nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem! Nɔ bati kɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a ade bɛtalɛ bikĩ Baguma do nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ a boe bɛkɔ be a. Titi eni ntáwɛ kĩ makpɔta nɔ babi ndɛ ayekĩ ɔkɔkɔbima api ye babi kado nɛ ye fupoki kayɔ, lɛmɛ ɔtákpa yĩ osi.

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ finyaa nɔ Asɔli Kɔla aawaa luto. Ade mabi mi kĩ miláabuki mimɔ́ yĩ kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ míalɛ kĩ, ‘Baguma hila ekĩ yaya nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ a!’ ”

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Onyole odi kĩ ye akpɔ mɔ abɔa wumi dzi nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu via Farisifɔ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a kĩ, “Boe mbla kpa osi kĩ bɛtsa oti nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ mbɔɛɛ kĩ ɛtákpa osi?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bisĩ bɛtákpa ye mbuayɛ. Ade Yesu ta oti a ɛtsa ye, ɛnyɛ ye osi ɛdzakũ a.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtápuli bɛkpa ye foe mbuayɛ. Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Akũ Kadɔla Mɔ Sɛmɛ Kalɛ Kakɛna Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ́ ayekĩ bati a kĩ bɛvɛ nɛ kanyakɔ̃ kawãnɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ a, ɛbɔa be lɛgbã kĩĩ kĩ,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ odi vɛ nɔ nɛ obolitsɔ̃ kapikɔ̃ ni, tanaadzi nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ kadzikɔ̃, kitonɛkĩ fɔ́apuli fɔya kĩ ɛvɛ odi kĩ edeĩ obu ɛbɛ̃ nɔ,

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdɛla ye akũ nɛ ode ni, baakɛna ye nɛ kayɔ, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ ni, baadɛla ye nɛ ode!”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi oti a kĩ ɛtsɛlamɔ ye nɛ kanyakɔ̃ a kĩ, “Ntɛ ote opunu ɔkpa bati ni, tavɛ nɔ bakɔba, awaɛ̃ amɛ, nɔ kɔla babi ĩye nɔ badzi kɔba a kĩ bideĩ akũ a, ayekĩ baavɛ nɔ malo ɔnaanya nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ bise kɔtɔ.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ Baguma lihila nɔ kusɛkusɛ, kitonɛkĩ bɛláapuli bise nɔ foe kɔtɔ. Lɛmɛ Baguma aase nɔ kɔtɔ nɛ luwi a kĩ bati wĩ aayidza bito kanɔkpa a.” Lɛgbã Nɛ Ode Opunu Kpale Kate Akũ.

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: “Owi tábuna mɔ osumunɛ a yabi ye kĩ, ‘Yĩ ɔlate nkɛna nɛ nɔ ɔlɛdo akũ, lɛmɛ fudzikɔ̃ buki fute.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a kĩ bidzi nɛ opunu akũ bɛkanya kamɛ ɔmɔa nu fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lɛ a, ebi ye kĩ, “Kawɔlɛ aade bati a kĩ baadzi nɛ opunu akũ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Luwi a onyole odi te opunu ɛvɛ bati beblebee.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Kĩ kanya lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ a edo ye osumunɛ kĩ ɛnaavɛ bikĩ ɛtsɛlamɛna a kĩ, ‘Miya. Buyua Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa!’ ”

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ bati a kĩ ɛtsɛlamɛna a flee kamɛ odi nyɛ odi nyɛ onukpɛ̃. Oti ɔtɔnyade bi osumunɛ a kĩ, ‘Nnaya tite finyaa, foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ nnaadã te, make nɔ aduli nláapuli ĩya.’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Oti bɛbã lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ, ‘Ĩya banantsue ewua, kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ nkpã be nnaasɔ be ndã ntɛ baapuli bɛkɛna adzuma kakɛna wĩ. Makɛ nɔ aduli, nláapuli ĩya.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ a muniki ɛnabi ye ɔlate lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a flee. Awã naa ɔlate a na kalɔkpa paa ade ebi ye osumunɛ a kĩ, ‘Wɛ sɔwa kanaa tɔblɔnu mɔ fɔla fuyite nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ, kakpã ahiãfɔ, lɛkpɛ̃ banɛnɛ, banɛkunɛ mɔ bikĩ biveliveli a kayamɛna.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: “Ade ke ɔlate a bi ye kĩ, ‘Naa tɔblɔnu mɔ fusikũ akũ nɛ tukula kamɛ, kabi be kĩ bɛya ayekĩ kɔla a kamɛ aayi.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: Mabi mi a flee kĩ, bikĩ be ĩnya lɔtɔ nvɛ a kamɛ odima lɛláata yĩ funitsã a onukpɛ̃!’ ” Kɔdɛ Nɛ Fɔtsa Ɔkɔlɛnɛ Lubokamɔ Akũ.

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Luwi a, kĩ bati kɔdabu tomɔ Yesu a, ɛdani ebi be kĩ,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ odi ya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mɔ yawɛ ye ɔlɛga, ɔma, ɔtsɔ̃, babi, bawaɛ̃nyole mɔ bawaɛ̃tsole, mɔ ye mɔawɛ akũ kalɛ kaba yĩ ni, ɛláapuli ɛnya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtábɔɛ ye asendua ɛlɔ etomɔ yĩ ni, ɛláapuli ɛnya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: “Ntɛ mi kamɛ odi awɛ kĩ yake kɔla baũ kudi ni, yaánya lɔtɔ kadzi kabu kɔla a kake akũ akunta kadã, kamɔ ntɛ yaana sika epuli eke kɔla a ewũna?

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛtákɛna foe lɔmɔ ni, ɛláapuli eyua kɔla a titɔ kĩ eke kayɔkayi futekũ malo. Ade ke bati flee a kĩ bamɔ foe a aadi ye nsekuo bɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: ‘Oti kĩĩ yi kɔla kake kayɔ lɛmɛ ɛtápuli eke koe ewũna.’

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛláapuli ni, yaanya lɔtɔ edo batɔ nɛ lɛgã kĩ a sɛkɛ̃ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛtáfuã akũ lɔkɔa ɛkpa lɔlɔ.”

english_verse: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ewũna kĩ, “Ade aye mi kamɛ odima lɛláapuli ɛnya yĩ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ ntɛ ɛtáfɔnɛ ye lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ awɛ a. Fɔtsa Kĩ Fɔlá Nfasɔ Ni, Baata Foe Biwula.

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: “Lɛdɛmɛ nɛnɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ lɛyɛla de ɔmɛnɛ ni, osi odima lɛláa kĩ aatsã ye akũ obuki odo de ɔmɛnɛ.

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: Lɛnɛ́nɛ lɛkpa ndɛ tite kanɔ, ĩye lɛkpa fukluku kawulakɔ̃ malo. Kata bata de kawula. “Okĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse katse wĩ!”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: Maayidza nnaa nda sɛkɛ̃ nnaabi ye kĩ, “Papa, nkɛna tɔkpa nɛ Baguma mɔ nɔ akũ.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Ade obisɛ̃de a bi ɔlɛga kĩ, ‘Papa, kɔa adzapadiɛ a kĩ yaanya yĩ kanyakɔ̃ a kakpa yĩ finyaa.’ Foesũ onyole a sɛ̃ ye adzapadiɛ kamɛ ɛkpa ye babinyole nviã a.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Ebula lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ edeĩ a kade. Ke liti a kɔlɛ kudi nɛ nɛ tite a na flee akũ ade ohiã pi ye a.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: Luwi a, tɔgɔlɛ batɛnɛ mɔ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ bɛbã ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃ bɛyaatse ye fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Nɛ awã naa, Farisifɔ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a kakɛna hũhũhũhũ kalɛ kĩ, “Oti kĩĩ atɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ ade yado awɛ kanya mɔ be malo a!”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã kĩĩ kĩ,

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛmɔ koe a, yaamɔ kawɔlɛ paa lɔkɔa ɛbɔɛ koe ete nɛ lɔbɔtɛɛ ɛyamɛna nɛ kɔla.

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Ade yaavɛ ye bakɔba mɔ banunɛ ebi be kĩ, ‘Kawɔlɛ de yĩ kĩ mmɔ́ yĩ kuveli a kĩ kɔyɛla a, foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔmɔ́ kawɔlɛ!’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way there is more happiness in heaven because of one sinner who turns to God than over
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ ade aye baamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a nɛ oti mɔa kĩ ɛdani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ akũ, kɛba bati aduekrɔũ-evedi kĩ be akũ kpalɛ kĩ bɛtásĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kadani. Bɛmɔ́ Sika Ovoe a Kĩ Sɛyɛla a.
