english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: “Ntɛ otsole odi deĩ sika ovoe babi ewua mɔ ɛyɛla libi mɔa ni, litsa yaakɛna? Yaatɛna ɔsɛdza ɛyɔ kɔla kamɛ ɛdã kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ yededeede kɛnawo kĩ ɛmɔ sika libi a.

english_verse: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛmɔ de a, yaavɛ ye bakɔba mɔ banunɛ ɛkpɔta ebi be kĩ, ‘Kawɔlɛ de yĩ paa kitonɛkĩ mmɔ́ yĩ sika libi a kĩ lɛyɛla a. Mifɔ̃ bɔmɔ́ kawɔlɛ!’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ ade aye Baguma ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ aamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ tɔkpa ɔkɛnanɛ mɔa kĩ ɛdani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ akũ a.” Obinyole a Kĩ Ɛyɛla a Muniki Ɛya.

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ɛnaa lɔtɔ kĩ, “Onyole odi na babinyole nviã.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: “Nɛ fuwi fudi liti ni, obisɛ̃de a nɔ ye kanyakɔ̃ fɔtsa a flee ade ɛdzakũ mɔ foe sika a. Ɛnaa katide mɔ lugo ɛnabula sika a kade nɛ nkpã basabasa kadzi kamɛ.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ ɛnaa onyole odi nɛ tite a akũ sɛkɛ̃ kĩ yanaakɛna adzuma ɛkpa ye. Onyole a kpa ye baplakuo akũ kadãmɛna nɛ ye kɔba.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɔlɛ sũ ɛwɛ kĩ yanya funitsã a kĩ bɛkako baplakuo a fudi lɛmɛ odima tákpa ye lɛtsama.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: “Nɛ ke kawũna a, ɛyamɔ ye akũ eyi ɔsũ, ade ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Bati a kĩ nda bɔɛ bakɛna tudzuma a ana kanya kawĩ pɔtɔɔ fɔate malo. Amɔ yĩ dzi nɛ awĩ kɔlɛ ayua yĩ kakɔ.

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Ntáfɛta kĩ abuki ɔvɛ yĩ kĩ nɔ obi, mbom kɔa yĩ kakɛna ndɛ nɔ tudzuma bakɛnanɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa.” ’

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Foesũ eyidza epi osi yanaa ɔlɛga sɛkɛ̃. “Ɔlɛga mɔ́ ye mɔ lugo kĩ yaya. Obi akũ kɔ ye kɔnya ade ewula atsɛ ɛnatu eboomu ye a.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: “Ade ke obi a bi ye kĩ, ‘Papa, nkɛna tɔkpa nɛ Baguma mɔ nɔ akũ. Ntáfɛta kĩ abuki ɔvɛ yĩ kĩ nɔ obi.’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ ɔlɛga a vɛ ye basumunɛ a ebi be kĩ, ‘Miwɛ sɔwa mibɔɛ tɛtadiɛ kĩ tɛnɛnɛ paa mido ye. Mido ye lipetia nɛ lawobi mɔ mpabua nɛ abɔa.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Ke liti ni, minaanyɛ ɔnantsue kĩ ɛdɔli paa mikɔ, lɔkɔa bɔnya bɔmɔ́ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɔbɔɛ foe kĩ yĩ obi kĩĩ ku lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ edzi nkpã. Ɛyɛla lɛmɛ bɛmɔ́ ye.’ Foesũ biyi luwi a kanya kayɔ.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: “Nɛ lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ a, obinyolenɔkɔɛ̃ a na nɛ kɔba. Kĩ eto yade, efuã tɔtɔ kamɛ a, enu tɔnɔ mɔ kuto nkã.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛvɛ basumunɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa evia ye kĩ, ‘Litsa lámɔ kaya nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ?’

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ a kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Awaɛ̃ muniki ɛya nɛ ɔtɔ ade ke aga fɔ̃ bɛkɔ ɔnantsue dɔliwa a, kitonɛkĩ emuniki ɛya mɔ akũ ɔsĩ.’

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: “Tuwũna ba ɔnɔkɔɛ̃de a, ade esĩ kĩ yaáwo nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ, foesũ ɔlɛga naa ye sɛkɛ̃ ɛnake ye aduli kĩ ewo ɛya nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ obi bi ɔlɛga kĩ, ‘Dã, nkɛna adzuma ndɛ ɔsate nɛ futeli pii kĩĩ kamɛ nkpa nɔ, kĩ ntáyidza ndza nɛ nɔ akũ da. Ade ɔtákpa tɔ̃ yĩ midi katukpabi malo kĩ nkɔ bɔmɔ yĩ bakɔba bɔmɔ́ kawɔlɛ a.

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɔ obi kĩĩ nabula nɔ akũ kana fɔtsa flee kade nɛ adɔpaafɔ akũ ade kĩ emuniki ɛya ni, ɔkɔ ɔnantsue dɔliwa akpa ye!’

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: “Awã naa, ɔlɛga bi ye kĩ, ‘Yĩ obi, odeĩ nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ luwi daa, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ ndeĩ a, nɔ lide lanɛ.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔanya luwi bɔmɔ́ kawɔlɛ, kitonɛkĩ bɔbɔɛ foe kĩ awaɛ̃ kĩĩ ku lɛmɛ finyaa edzi nkpã, ɛyɛla lɛmɛ bɔmɔ́ ye.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Osikani odi na adzuma ɔkɛnanɛ kĩ edzi nɛ ye tudzuma onukpɛ̃. Bati yabi osikani a kĩ ye tudzuma onukpɛ̃ ɔdzanɛ a abula ye sika kade.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Foesũ osikani a vɛ tudzuma onukpɛ̃ ɔdzanɛ a evia ye kĩ, ‘Litsa fɔlɛtsa manu kĩ balɛ nɛ nɔ akũ a? Kɛna akunta yededeede nɛ ayekĩ ɔnɛɛ yĩ tudzuma akũ, kitonɛkĩ ɔláapuli obuki ɔnya yĩ fɔtsa akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: “Ade ke tudzuma akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a bi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Yĩ ɔlate aablɔmɛna yĩ nɛ adzuma kamɛ. Litsa maakɛna? Nlá kɔbɛ̃ kĩ maatu tɔbɔnkam ade ke kɔnyɔã akɔ yĩ mɔ fɔtsa kawã lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: Ahãã! Ĩnyina lɛtsa a kĩ maakɛna. Ntɛ adzuma nɛ́ nɛ yĩ awɛ ni, maana bakɔba kĩ baatɛ yĩ mɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ be awã!’

english_verse: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
translation_verse: “Ekĩ edzimɔ kɔtɔ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Bɔ̃ tɛkantsi ɔha.’ “Ade tudzuma akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a bi ye kĩ, ‘Amɔ nɔ akunta nɛ awĩ. Kɛna akũ kadzi katsɛlɛ aduenum.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: “Ade evia ɔbɛbã lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Nɔ malo ani odzimɛna?’ “Kɔtɔ odzimɛnanɛ a lɛ kĩ, ‘Fugbanɔ fuvũ baũbaũ ɔha mɔa.’ “Oti a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ tudzuma onukpɛ̃ a bi ye kĩ, ‘Amɔ nɔ akunta nɛ awĩ. Tsɛlɛ aduesɔũ kayi.’

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: “Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ɔlate a mu ye tudzuma ɔdãmɛnanɛ a nɛ nyansã tɛdake tisi a kĩ ɛbɔɛ a sũ. Ɔnɔkɔali ni, katinya kamɛ bati deĩ nyansã tisi nɛ be fɔtsakɛlawa kamɛ bɛbɛ̃ bikĩ batse Baguma kalɛ a.”

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu lɛ ɛnaa lɔtɔ kĩ, “Mikɔa katinya kamɛ akũ kana fɔtsa mikɛna tɔkɔba, ayekĩ ntɛ fuwoli ni, baatɛ mi nɛ daa nkpã ɔtɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ Abraham bi ye kĩ, ‘Ntɛ bɛláatse Mose mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a fɔlɛtsa ni, ntɛ odi to kanɔkpa ɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃ malo a, bɛláatɛ ye fɔlɛtsa a bido.’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Kĩ Farisifɔ a nu fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lɛ a, bɛnyɛ ye ahĩsɛm kitonɛkĩ sika bawɛnɛ banɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Minyɛ bati kĩ bakɛna be akũ ndɛ badã yedede nɛ bati bɛbã anɛ, lɛmɛ Baguma nyina mi tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Kitonɛkĩ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔtɛ kɔya nɛ otidziwa anɛ a, fɔnyɛ kɔwɛ̃ fɔtsa nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: “Mose mbla mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ biyi a kɛna adzuma kɛyawo Yohanes Mui Ɔwɔlanɛ a lɔkɔ. Kito ke a, balɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ, ade odi nyɛ odi lamɛna kapili kĩ yawo nɛ de kamɛ.

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔadã bɔɛɛ kĩ tite mɔ ode aawɔ kɛba kĩ baafɔnɛ fɔlɛtsa kabiiwɛ awɛ nɛ mbla a kamɛ.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: “Onyole nyɛ onyole kĩ esĩ ɔtsɔ̃, mɔ ɛnakɔã ɔbɛbã ni, ɛbɔa adɔpaa. Ade onyole kĩ ɛkɔã otsole kĩ bisĩ ni, ye malo ɛbɔa adɔpaa. Yesu Lɛ Kalɛ Nɛ Osikani Odi Mɔ Lazaro Akũ.

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: “Osikani odi dzi nkpã. Ɛkahihiɛ ye akũ nɛ tilima kĩ tɛtɛ kɔya kamɛ, ade ɛkanya nkpã mɛnɛmɛnɛ luwi daa a.

english_verse: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
translation_verse: Ohiãni odi lɛmɛ kĩ bavɛ Lazaro lɛmɛ dzi nkpã tɔ̃. Fɔɔkpa nɛ́ nɛ ye akũ flee ade bɛkabɔɛ ye kanaadzina nɛ osikani a kɔla onyukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Ɛkakɔna kĩ yaana funitsã wuliwa a kĩ fɔato osikani opunu akũ katɔ nɛ tite a ɛnya. Bakpete malo kayadzɛmɛ ye fɔɔkpa a kamɛ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: “Ohiãni a ku, ade ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ yabɔɛ ye bɛnamɛna ode bɛnadzina nɛ Abraham ɔwɛ̃ a. Osikani a lɛmɛ yaku, ade ke bibila ye a.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: Nɛ kanɔkpade awã kĩ ɛkamɔ lubo paa a, ɛdzɛla anɛ ɛmɔ Abraham mɔ lugo nɛ ode, mɔ Lazaro lɛmɛ kĩ edzi nɛ ye ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Ade osikani a bɔa fɔwɔla ɛvɛ ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlɛga Abraham! Mɔ yĩ kɔnya kafɔ̃ Lazaro edo lawobi nɛ mui kamɛ ɛta nɛ yĩ sɛmɛ akũ, ayekĩ fɔ́adɔla yĩ ɔmɔado, kitonɛkĩ nlamɛna lubo kamɔ nɛ fi kĩĩ kamɛ!’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ Abraham bi ye kĩ, ‘Yĩ obi, te anɛ kĩ bɛkpa nɔ fɔtsa wĩ flee nɛ nɔ nkpã kamɛ, lɛmɛ Lazaro kɔ̃ ni, fɔtsa kpa nɛnɛ ɛna. Lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ a, ɛlamɛna kawɔlɛ kamɔ, ade nɔ malo ɔlamɛna lubo kamɔ a.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: “Awã naa, osikani a bi ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlɛga Abraham, make nɔ aduli kĩ do Lazaro ɛnaa nda tɔtɔ kamɛ,

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: awã kĩ ndeĩ bawaɛ̃nyole elo. Fɔ̃ ɛnaabɔa be kɔkɔ ayekĩ be malo bɛláayawo nɛ lubo kamɔ kĩĩ kamɛ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: “Ade Abraham bi ye kĩ, ‘Awaɛ̃ amɛ deĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Mose mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a tsɛlɛ biyi a. Kɛnɛ kĩ bitse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ a lɔkɔa bɛkɛna nɛ foe akũ.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: “Osikani a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlɛga Abraham, fɔláawĩ be! Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi aayidza eto kanɔkpa ɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃ ni, baadani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Nde sũ ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ kĩĩ odi limuniki ɛyaanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ odeĩ katɛkado kĩ kɛpɔnɛ lububu ndɛ kutse libi kabiiwɛ flee ni, aapuli obi kutse kĩĩ kĩ, ‘Bɔlɛ mɔ tunu kanaadza nɛ mpo kamɛ’ ade kɔ́atse nɔ a. Yesu Lɛ Ayekĩ Osumunɛ Wĩ Akɛna Adzuma.

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: “Mibɔɛ foe kĩ mi kamɛ odi deĩ ɔpaani kĩ yadã nɔ kɔba akũ, ĩye ekĩ yadãmɛna nɔ baveli akũ, ntɛ eto ede a, aabi ye kĩ ɛwɛ sɔwa ɛyadzi ɛnya?

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Odima lɛláalɛ kĩ, ‘Dã, amɔ foe nɛ awĩ’ ĩye ‘amɔ foe nɛ akĩĩ,’ kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛgãkanya a deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Ke liti a ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lidi aaya kĩ míawɛ kĩ miamɔ Yĩ Otidziwa Obi fuwi a kamɛ lɔmɔa lɛmɛ miláamɔ de.

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Osumunɛ tásĩ ɛkpa kɔlɛkanyɛ ntɛ ɛkɛna nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkɛna akũ. Mbɔɛɛ esĩ?

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Fɔtsa a kĩ foe aafɔ̃ bati liwo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ a aaya kokooko, lɛmɛ bɔbɔɛ makpa oti a kĩ ye akũ fɔ́atsã fɔya a!

english_verse: would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their neck.
translation_verse: Fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa ye ntɛ bɛkɔa kɔwɔlɛ kpale bɛvɛla ye nɛ sɔmɔ bɛta bido nɛ mpo kamɛ, kɛba kĩ yaafɔ̃ babisɛ̃ kamɛ ɔmɔa liwo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ.

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Foesũ midã kadã wĩ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ miakɛna a akũ. “Ntɛ awaɛ̃ kɛna nɔ tɔkpa ni, kã ye anɛ. Ntɛ etunɔ kĩ eku kɔtɔ ni, kɔa kate ye.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛkɛna tɔkpa nɛ nɔ akũ titi evũkɔnɔ nɛ luwi mɔa kamɛ mɔ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ ɛyabi nɔ kĩ eku kɔtɔ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ akɔa ote ye.” Bivia Yesu Katɛkado Kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, fɔ̃ boe katɛkado kide nɛ ode kibu.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Oowo! Mbom aabi ye kĩ, ‘Manɛ akũ katɔ funitsã, kadza kasum yĩ, ĩnya, mmɔa ĩyua tɔ̃ fɔmɔ nɔ malo aanya.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Ade ayea fɔla fɔkpa mi a. Ntɛ mikɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ mbi mi kĩ mikɛna ni, milɛ kĩ, ‘Basumunɛ odi bɔanɛ, lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ de bɔakɛna a, ade de bɔkɛna a.’ ” Yesu Tsa Banyole Ewua Badi Fɔsɔ Ngbã.

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu anaa Yerusalem a, ɛnatsã Samaria mɔ Galilea ɔbɛ̃ akũ.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkɔa yawo akula adi kamɛ a, banyole ewua kĩ basɔ fɔsɔ ngbã yatsɛlamɔ ye.

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Bɛdza mɔ lugo ade bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kĩ, “Boe Ɔlɛga Yesu, mɔ boe kɔnya!”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Yesu dã be ade ebi be kĩ, “Minaa, minaakɔa mi akũ mitsa basɔfɔ bɛsɛla mi akũ bɛdã.” Kĩ bɛyɛlɛ banaa nɛ osi a, fɔsɔ a wɔ nɛ be akũ.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Kĩ be kamɛ ɔmɔa mɔ kĩ fɔsɔ a wɔ nɛ ye akũ a, emuniki ɛya mɔ Baguma kamu mɔ fɔwɔla kɔɔba.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Ɛyatɔ nɛ Yesu abɔa kamɛ ɛnyɛ ye kɔlɛ. Onyole kĩĩ nyɛ Samariani.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Yesu via ye kĩ, “Nnɛ́ banyole ewua ntsa fɔsɔ a? Nde be evedi nkaɛ a?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Yidza kanaa. Nɔ katɛkado fɔ̃ ɔsaɛ.” Yesu Lɛ Ayekĩ Baguma Lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a Aana.

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ayekĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ amɔanɛ kato sɛkɛ kĩĩ kanaa sikĩĩ a, ade aye Otidziwa Obi a kaya aana a.

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Bati badi aana, ade baabi mi kĩ, ‘Midã ye nɛ akĩĩ!’ ĩye ‘Midã ye nɛ awĩ!’ Lɛmɛ mitatomɔ foe liti.

english_verse: When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building.
translation_verse: “Ade ayea fɔ́ana ndɛ ayekĩ fɔna nɛ Lot lɔkɔ lɛmɛ a. Bati kanya, bɛkamɔa, bɛkanya kɔyaatɔ, kadu fɔtsa ade badi lɛmɛ kake fɔla a.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a via ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nɛ ndia?” Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Awã kĩ fɔtsa kuwa waa ni, ade awã fɔpɛtɛ akpɔta a.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, “God's kingdom isn't something you can see.
translation_verse: Farisifɔ badi via Yesu lɔkɔ a kĩ de kamɛ Baguma lɛgãkanya a aaya. Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Baguma lɛgãkanya a lɛlaaya nɛ osi a kĩ bati amɔ mɔ anɛ a.

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛmɔ lubo pii, ade nviã bati lɛmɛ aasĩ ye a.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: “Ayekĩ fɔna nɛ Noa lɔkɔ a, ade aye fɔ́ana nɛ Otidziwa Obi a kabuki kaya fuwi a kamɛ a.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Bati flee kanya bɛkamɔa, banyole mɔ batsole kakɔã akũ, kɛnawo luwi a kĩ Noa de ewo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ, ade oni ko eyili fɔkɔ ɛwɔla bati flee a.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Ade luwi a kĩ Lot nɛ́ nɛ Sodom ɔmatɔ kamɛ a, fi mɔ fukpokpo kĩ fudeĩ nfiã kpitii wula futo ode fɔyawɔla be a flee.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: “Ade aye fɔ́ana nɛ luwi a kĩ Otidziwa Obi a aanɛ ɛta a.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Nɛ luwi a na, oti kĩ edeĩ nɛ kɔla ode ɛtabuki eduma kĩ yanaafila ye akũ fɔtsa nɛ kɔla kamɛ. Ade ayea odi kĩ edeĩ nɛ kɔba kamɛ ni, ɛtabuki emuniki ɛnaa tɔtɔ kamɛ kĩ yanaabɔɛ litsedi.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Mite anɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ Lot ɔtsɔ̃ akũ a!

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛbɔa mbɔdi kĩ yapili ye nkpã ni, yaayɛla ye, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛyɛla ye nkpã ni yaana nkpã a bio.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Luwi a lɛnyɛ ni, mabi mi kĩ bati nviã aawa nɛ ɔkɛna mɔa akũ, baabɔɛ ɔmɔa bɛdzakũmɛna lɔkɔa bɛfɔnɛ ɔyade a biyi.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Banyole nviã aakakɛna adzuma nɛ kɔba. Baabɔɛ ɔmɔa bɛnamɛna, bɛfɔnɛ ɔmɔa biyi.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Yesu bɔa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lɛgbã lidi, ɛkɔa yatsa be kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkaabɔa mpaɛ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ, kĩ akpɔ ɛtaveli be da.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ odi na nɛ ɔmatɔ odi kamɛ kĩ ɛtávila Baguma ĩye ɛkɔa obu ɛkpa odima lɛmɛ.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Okunawa odi lɛmɛ na nɛ ɔmatɔ mɔapɛ a kamɛ, ade ɛkayake ye aduli kĩ, ‘Bumɔ yĩ kanyɛ yĩ nɛ yĩ okesĩnɛ awɛ kamɛ.’

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: “Fɔtɛ lɔkɔ pii kĩ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ a sĩ ɛtákɛna lɛtsama nɛ foe akũ. Lɛmɛ nɛ ke kawũna kamɛ a, ebi ye akũ kĩ, ‘Titɔ kĩ maávila Baguma ade maákɔa obu kakpa otidziwa malo ni,

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: nɛ ɔhau beblebee kĩ okunawa kĩĩ akpa yĩ sũ ni, maadã kĩ ɛna ye ɔtɔbɛ. Ntɛ ntákɛna lɔmɔ ni, yaakaya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ lɔkɔa ɛláafɔ̃ nna kawɛya.’ ”

english_verse: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
translation_verse: Ade boe Ɔlate lɛ ɛnaa lɔtɔ kĩ, “Mitse lɛtsa a kĩ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ kĩ yaádi ɔnɔkɔali kĩĩ lɛ.

english_verse: Won't God protect his chosen ones who pray to him day and night? Won't he be concerned for them?
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ a, miatɛ kado kĩ Baguma lɛláadzɛ ɛkpa ye bati a kĩ babe kavɛ ye lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa, kavia kabumɛna a ɔtɔbɛ? Mbɔɛɛ miatɛ kado kĩ ɛláabumɛna be mɔatsɛ?

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ yaadzɛ ɛkpa be ɔtɔbɛ mɔatsɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ Otidziwa Obi a ya nɛ katinya kamɛ ni, yaamɔ kĩ bati deĩ katɛkado?” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Farisini Mɔ Tɔgɔlɛ Ɔtɛnɛ Odi Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Yesu bɔa lɛgbã kĩĩ ɛkpa bati a kĩ bɛtɛ bido kĩ bɛkpalɛ yedede ade basupa bati nkaɛ a.

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Farisini a kpɛ̃ ye akũ ɛnadza ɛbɔa mpaɛ kĩ, ‘Baguma, manyɛ nɔ kɔlɛ, kitonɛkĩ nlá anɛngbã. Maádi bati ablɔ ade maákɛna adɔpaa ndɛ ayekĩ bati bɛbã akɛna a. Manyɛ nɔ kɔlɛ lɛmɛ kĩ nlá ndɛ tɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ a kĩ ɛdzɛ nɛ akĩĩ a aye.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: Masui onukpɛ̃ fuwi nviã nɛ ɛkwansieda mɔa kamɛ, ade makɔa lɛtsa a kĩ mana a foe katsu kawuade a kakpa nɔ a.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mabi mi kĩ tɔgɔlɛ ɔtɛnɛ a akũ lɛkpalɛ yedede nɛ Baguma anɛ kĩ emuniki ɛnaa kɔla a, nnɛ́ Farisini a. Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdɛla ye akũ nɛ ode ni, baakɛna ye nɛ kayɔ, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ ni, baadɛla ye nɛ ode.” Yesu Hila Babisɛ̃.

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Bati badi kɔa be babi bɛyamɛna kĩ Yesu ete awɛ nɛ be akũ. Kĩ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mɔ́ fukĩĩ a, bɛkã be anɛ.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: baase ye fɔtsa kĩĩ fublusu nɛ nviã katinya kĩĩ kamɛ mɔ nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a nɛ kikĩ kaaya a kamɛ.” Yesu Buki Ɛlɛ Kalɛ Nɛ Ye Wuda Akũ.

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Yesu kpã ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a ɛnamɛna ɔɔwɛ̃ ade ebi be kĩ, “Mitse! Bɔanaa Yerusalem, awã kĩ fɔtsa flee a kĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ tsɛlɛ nɛ Otidziwa Obi akũ biyi a, aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Yudafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi via Yesu kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ wĩ, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna fɔmɔ maana nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna dadaada a?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Oti a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kito anɛ yĩ tibisɛ̃ kamɛ a, nkɛna nɛ tɛbla kĩĩ flee akũ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nu foe a, ebi ye kĩ, “Lɛtsa mɔa te nɔ kakɛna. Naanɔ nɔ fɔtsa flee a kĩ odeĩ a kakɔa foe sika a kakpa ahiãfɔ, lɔkɔa aana akũ kana fɔtsa nɛ ode. Ke liti muniki kayaatomɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ onyole a nu fɔlɛtsa a ewo ɔblɔ paa, kitonɛkĩ ɛna akũ kana fɔtsa beblebee.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ ayekĩ ewo ɔblɔ a, ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Fɔ́ana ɔsĩ fɔkpa asikafɔ kĩ baapuli biwo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ!

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Fɔadã bɔɛɛ kĩ ɔpɔnkɔ mɔ kapu aatsã ɔnɔbɔ̃ ɔkɛ kamɛ ɛnɛ, kɛba kĩ osikani aawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Ade bati a kĩ binu kalɛ kĩĩ a via Yesu kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ owei koũ aana katɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ otidziwa lɛláapuli ɛkɛna dadaada ni, Baguma kɔ̃ aapuli ɛkɛna.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
translation_verse: Awã naa Petro bi Yesu kĩ, “Dã, bɔfɔnɛ boe tɔtɔ awɛ buyi bɔya butomɔ nɔ.”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Baakɔa ye bido nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ awɛ, bidi ye nsekuo, bivũna ye, bitua tanɛ biko nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Baabo ye lɔkɔa bɛkɔ ye, lɛmɛ nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ ni, yaabuki eyidza edzi nkpã.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu fuã Yeriko ɔmatɔ a, ɔnɛkunɛ odi dzi nɛ osi ɔɔwɛ̃ ɛkawã fɔtsa.

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Kĩ enu bati kɔdabu nkã kĩ batɛ̃ a, evia kĩ, “Litsa lamɔ kaya?”

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Ade bibi ye kĩ, “Yesu Nazaretni a lɛyaatɛ̃.”

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Awã naa, ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Yesu, Lɛgã Dawid Obi! Mɔ yĩ kɔnya!”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ a wũni nɛ ye akũ kĩ esua onukpɛ̃. Lɛmɛ ɛdanɛ fɔwɔla kabɔa kɔɔba kalɛ kĩ, “Lɛgã Dawid Obi! Mɔ yĩ kɔnya!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu fɔ̃ ɛdza ade ɛlɛ kĩ bɛkpã ɔnɛkunɛ a bɛma ye a. Kĩ ewo Yesu sɛkɛ̃ a, evia ye kĩ,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Litsa awɛ kĩ nkɛna nkpa nɔ?” Ɔnɛkunɛ a akpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, mawɛ kĩ mbuki mmɔ́ fɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ mɔ fɔkɔ. Nɔ katɛkado fɔ̃ ɔsaɛ.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ epuli ɛmɔ fɔkɔ ade ke etomɔ Yesu mɔ Baguma kɔlɛ kanyɛ a. Kĩ bati kɔdabu a mɔ fukĩĩ a, be flee bimu Baguma.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Foesũ ewula atsɛ ɛnya bati kɔdabu a lɔtɔ ɛnade nɛ kutse gɛlɛɛlɛ kudi kamɛ ayekĩ yaamɔ Yesu kitonɛkĩ enyina kĩ yaatsã awã ɛtɛ̃.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu wo kutse a kayɔ a, ɛdzɛla anɛ ɛmɔ Zakeo ade ɛvɛ ye kĩ, “Zakeo, duma mɔatsɛ, kɛnɛ kĩ mase nɛ nɔ awã nviã.”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Zakeo wɛ sɔwa eduma ade ɛyatɛ Yesu mɔ kawɔlɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Bati a flee kĩ bɛmɔ́ fukĩĩ a kɛna hũhũhũhũ nɛ Yesu akũ bɛlɛ kĩ, “Oti kĩĩ nase nɛ titikpa ɔkɛnanɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ.”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Zakeo yidza ɛdza ebi boe Ɔlate a kĩ, “Boe Ɔlɛga, dã! Maakɔa yĩ akũ fɔtsa sɛkɛ mɔa nte ahiãfɔ, ade ntɛ nsisi odi ni, maase ye foe titi ɛna kɔtɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Nkpã katɛ ya nɛ tɔtɔ kĩĩ kamɛ nviã, kitonɛkĩ oti kĩĩ lɛmɛ nyɛ Abraham kasinu a kamɛ obi.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a ya kĩ yawɛ bikĩ bɛyɛla ɛnyɛ be lɛmɛ.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Sika Akũ.

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ bati a bi ye kĩ, ‘Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, oti a na deĩ sika sɔmɔa sɔmɔa se ewua kɔkɔɛ!’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ ye ɔtɔ bati akesĩ ye foesũ bido batɔ bitomɔ ye liti kĩ bɛnaalɛ kĩ, ‘Bɔáwɛ onyole kĩĩ kĩ ɛnya boe lɛgã.’

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Minaa ɔmatɔ kabii a kĩ ɛwa nɛ mi lɔtɔ a kamɛ. Ntɛ miawo nɛ ye kamɛ ni, míamɔ aflim obi kĩ odima tádzi nɛ ye akũ tɔ̃ kĩ edeĩ nɛ ɔwɛ̃ kamɛ. Miwũnya ye lɔkɔa mikɔa miyamɛna nɛ awĩ.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ ɔtɔnyade a bi ye kĩ, ‘Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, nna sika a sɔmɔa sɔmɔa foe ewua nte nɛ sikĩ ɔkpa yĩ a akũ.’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ ɔyade a lɛmɛ yalɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, nna sika sɔmɔa sɔmɔa foe elo nte nɛ sikĩ ɔkpa yĩ a akũ!’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: “Osumunɛ ɔlalɛde a yalɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, amɔ nɔ sika sɔmɔa kĩ ɔkɔa ɔkpa yĩ a. Nsui se nɛ limakũ kamɛ nkɔa nkɔla.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Kĩ onyina lɔmɔ a, nde sũ ɔtákɔa yĩ sika ɔnaado nɛ sika kakɔlakɔ̃ kĩ ntɛ mmuniki ĩya ni, maatɛ se mɔ se akũ kute?’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Ade ebi bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kĩ, ‘Mitɛ sika sɔmɔa nɛ ye awɛ mikɔa mikpa osumunɛ a kĩ ɛna sika sɔmɔa sɔmɔa ewua a.’

english_verse: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: “Ade Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Mabi mi kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edeĩ litsedi ni, ade ye baakɔa foe pii bibu a, lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛlá lɛtsama a ni, foe kabii a kĩ edeĩ a malo baatɛ foe nɛ ye awɛ.

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Finyaa ni, mivɛlɛ yĩ bakesĩnɛ a kĩ bɛtáwɛ kĩ ĩnya be lɛgã a miyamɛna nɛ awĩ, lɔkɔa mikɔ be nɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ.’ ” Yesu Wo Nɛ Yerusalem Ɔmatɔ Kamɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu bi be fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ eyua a, ɛnya ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lɔtɔ epi osi yanaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Kĩ efuã Betfage mɔ Betania tɛmatɔ a nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ ɔwɛ̃ a, edo ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nviã ɛnyamɛna lɔtɔ, ebi be kĩ,

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi via mi kĩ litsa sũ miawũnya ye ni, mibi ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate sĩ ye.’ ”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a naa ade bɛmɔ́ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa ndɛ ayekĩ Yesu bi be a.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkawũnya aflim obi a, ye balate via be kĩ, “Nde sũ miawũnya ye?”

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nviã a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate sĩ ye.”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa aflim obi a bɛma Yesu ade bɛdzɛlɛ be tɛtadiɛ wulaa biko nɛ ye akũ bibumɛna Yesu ede edzi nɛ ye akũ a.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Kĩ edzi nɛ ye akũ banaa a, bati dzɛlɛ be tɛtadiɛ biko nɛ tite.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu fuã Yerusalem nɛ awã kĩ osi a ka eto Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ akũ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ kɔdabu flee a yi kayɔ mɔ Baguma kɔlɛ kanyɛ, mɔ ye kahulu mɔ fɔwɔla kɔɔba nɛ awãwã fɔtsa flee a kĩ bɛmɔ́ a sũ.

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: Ade ke bɛlɛ kĩ, “Baguma ehila Lɛgã a kĩ laya nɛ Boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ! Lukudɔ lɛna nɛ ode, obu ɛnya Baguma nɛ ode flefle fude!”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Farisifɔ badi kĩ bideĩ nɛ bati kɔdabu a kamɛ bi Yesu kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bi nɔ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bisua onukpɛ̃!”

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu fuã Yerusalem ɛkɔa anɛ ɛmɔ ɔmatɔ kĩĩ a, ewula babe nɛ ye lɔlɔ.

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: Ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mawɛ kĩ nviã kĩĩ malo ni, aanyi lɛtsa a kĩ laakpa nɔ lukudɔ. Lɛmɛ finyaa kĩĩ a, ɔláapuli ɔmɔ́ de!

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Baawɔla nɔ mɔ bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ nɔ kamɛ a, ade bɛláafɔnɛ de lidima lɛwa nɛ de kawakɔ̃ malo a, kitonɛkĩ ɔtámɔ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma yatɛ nɔ a oyi ɔsũ!” Yesu Blɔmɛna Kɔyaatɔ Banyanɛ Nɛ Asɔli Kɔla Kamɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu nawo nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a, eyi kayɔ mɔ fɔtsa banɔnɛ kablɔmɛna awɛ nɛ awã.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Baavɛ yĩ asɔli kɔla kĩ mpaɛ kabɔakɔ̃.’ Lɛmɛ mikɔa koe mikɛna bayekpanɛ kabɛlakɔ̃!”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Luwi nyɛ luwi ni, Yesu katsa fɔtsa nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃, mbla batsanɛ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ wɛ kĩ bakɔ ye.

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ osi a kĩ ye akũ baatsã, kitonɛkĩ bati a flee dzɛmɛna ye fɔlɛtsa kayaatse, ade bɛtáwɛ kĩ ye kalɛ kadima kɛtɛ̃ nɛ be akũ a.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Bikĩ banaĩ Yesu a ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, bibi ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, bunyina kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ alɛ mɔ fukĩ atsa a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Bunyina lɛmɛ kĩ aádã oti anɛ, mbom atsa Baguma tisi a nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Ade Zadukifɔ kĩ balɛ kĩ kayidza kato kanɔkpa lɛláa a, ya nɛ Yesu sɛkɛ̃, bibi ye kĩ,

english_verse: When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa nɛ luwi a kĩ banɔkpa aabuki biyidza bidzi nkpã a ni, be kamɛ owei ɔtsɔ̃ yaanya, nɛ kĩ be evũkɔnɔ a flee bɛkɔã tɔ̃ ye a?”

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Ade bibi ye kĩ, “Bi boe osi a kĩ odeĩ fɔmɔ akɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ a. Owei lɛkpa nɔ osi?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I want to ask you a question.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ ĩnya lɔtɔ nvia mi kalɛ mɔa. Mibi yĩ,

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Yohanes bati mui kawɔla amaniɛ a ni, Baguma sɛkɛ̃ futo mbɔɛɛ kĩ bati sɛkɛ̃ futo?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a, biyi fɔlɛtsa kavɛlɛ mɔ be akũ kĩ, “Litsa bɔ́alɛ? Ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ futo Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ni, yaavia kĩ, ‘Nde sũ mitátɛ Yohanes mido?’

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ bati sɛkɛ̃ futo ni, bati kɔdabu a flee aanɔ boe boe, kitonɛkĩ be flee bɛtɛ bido kĩ Yohanes nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bunyína awã kĩ futo.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Yesu lɛmɛ bi be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ yĩ malo nláabi mi awã kĩ ntomɛna kɔbɛ̃ nnɛɛ makɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ.” Yesu Bɔa Lɛgbã Nɛ Apaafɔ Akũ.

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Midã kadã wĩ nɛ Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a akũ. Bati kĩĩ ado tɛtadiɛ wulaa katsã kamini, ade bawɛ kĩ bati bɛwɔ̃na be nɛ obu kamɛ nɛ kɔya a. Bawɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fudzikɔ̃ nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃, mɔ fudzikɔ̃ wĩ nɛ fuwi kanyakɔ̃.

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story: A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bɔa bati a lɛgbã kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lidi kamɛ ni, onyole odi nɛ waĩ kɔba. Ɛkɔa koe edo nɛ apaafɔ awɛ ɛnaa osi akũ, ɛnadzi nɛ awã ebuna owi.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Kĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ bana waĩ babi a, edo ye osumunɛ odi kĩ ɛnaatɛ ye kanyakɔ̃ nɛ apaafɔ a sɛkɛ̃ ɛma ye. Lɛmɛ apaafɔ a bo osumunɛ a, bɛlɔla ye mɔ awɛ pupulidza.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ kĩ apaafɔ a mɔ́ obi a, bibi akũ kĩ, ‘Ade kɔba ɔlate a obi nɛ awĩ a. Mifɔ̃ bɔkɔ ye lɔkɔa ye adzapadiɛ ɛkɛna boe ade.’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ yaayakɔ apaafɔ a na flee lɔkɔa ɛkɔa kɔba a edo nɛ apaafɔ bɛbã awɛ.” Kĩ bati a nu fukĩĩ a, bɛlɛ kĩ, “Kusɛ! Baguma lɛláakpa osi fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ lɔmɔ da!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a mɔ Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a bɔa mbɔdi kĩ bapi Yesu nɛ awã, kitonɛkĩ bɛmɔ́ biyi ɔsũ kĩ be sũ mɔ ɛbɔa lɛgbã a, lɛmɛ bivila bati a.

english_verse: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bɛwɛ osi bɛbã kĩ ye akũ baatsã bipi ye. Bɛkpa banyole badi sika kĩ bɛkɛna be akũ ndɛ bati kĩ badi ɔnɔkɔali. Bido be kĩ bɛnaanaĩ Yesu nɛ fɔlɛtsa kavia katse kamɛ, lɔkɔa bɛna osi bɛkɔa ye bido nɛ banɔkɔɛ̃, mɔ Roma abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ awɛ.

english_verse: “Show me a coin.” Then he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: “Miyamɛna sika ovoe a sidi ndã. Owei lɔlɔ mɔ dĩ teĩ nɛ se akũ?”

english_verse: Then he told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare fude fɔanɛ.” Yesu bi be kĩ, “Kekɔ̃ mikɔa fukĩ fɔnyɛ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ fude a mikpa Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃, ade mikɔa fukĩ fɔnyɛ Baguma fude lɛmɛ mikpa Baguma a.”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɛ bati kɔdabu a anɛ kamɛ na a, bɛtápuli bipi ye nɛ osi odima akũ, foesũ bisua be tunukpɛ̃ nɛ Yesu mbuayɛ a kĩ ɛkɛna be wãwã sũ. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Banɔkpa Kabuki Kayidza Akũ.

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, Mose tsɛlɛ mbla ɛkpa boe kĩ, ‘Ntɛ onyole odi ku ɛfɔnɛ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ bɛmɔ ye bɛtáma ni, onyole a ɔwaɛ̃ ɛkɔã otsole a lɔkɔa bɛmɔ ye bɛma babi bɛnya ekĩ eku a bade.’

english_verse: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ lidi ni, bawaɛ̃nyole evũkɔnɔ badi dzi nkpã. Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃de a kɔã ɔtsɔ̃ lɛmɛ eku, kĩ bɛtáma obi odima.

english_verse: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
translation_verse: Ɔwaɛ̃ ɔyade a mɔ ɔlalɛde a lɛmɛ kɔã okunawa, ade be malo biku kĩ bɛtáma babi badima a.

english_verse: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
translation_verse: Ade lɛtsa mɔapɛ a lɛkɛna bawaɛ̃nyole evũkɔnɔ a flee a. Be flee biku nɛ kĩ otsole a táma obi ɛkpa be kamɛ odima.

translation_verse: Nɛ foe kawũna kamɛ a, otsole a mɔawɛ yaku.

english_verse: Jesus answered: The people in this world get married.
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Katinya kĩĩ kamɛ mɔ́ otsole mɔ onyole akɔã akũ,

translation_verse: Nɛ awã a Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi lɛ kĩ. “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, ɔkpa mbuayɛ kakpa wĩ paa!”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Why do people say that the Messiah will be the son of King David?
translation_verse: Yesu via be kĩ, “Nde sũ bati alɛ kĩ Kristo a aanya Lɛgã Dawid kasinu kamɛ obi?

english_verse: In the book of Psalms, David himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side
translation_verse: Ade Dawid mɔawɛ a lɛ nɛ Tɔnɔ Ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate bi yĩ Ɔlate kĩ, “Dzi nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna

english_verse: until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ nkɔa nɔ bakesĩnɛ nkɛna nɔ abɔa katekɔ̃.” ’

english_verse: David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?” Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ Dawid vɛ ye kĩ boe Ɔlate a, nde fɔya mɔ Kristo a aanya Dawid kasinu kamɛ obi?” Yesu Bɔa Bati Kɔkɔ Nɛ Yudafɔ Mbla Batsanɛ Akũ.

english_verse: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a flee dza bɛkatse Yesu fɔlɛtsa a, ɛdani ebi ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ,

english_verse: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
translation_verse: “Fɔmɔ fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ flee aaya ni, baatomɔ mi liti, bipili mi bɛnamɛna Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ bɛdzɛ̃mɔ mi, bibo mi lɔkɔa bido mi nɛ kɔla. Ade baakpã mi bɛnaadzɛla nɛ fɔgã mɔ abãã banɔkɔɛ̃ anɛ nɛ yĩ sũ.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had.” The Temple Will Be Destroyed (Matthew
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati bɛbã a kɔ̃ ni, be akũ kana fɔtsa a kĩ fublusu a kamɛ fudi sukuloo bite, lɛmɛ otsole ohiãni kĩĩ kɔa lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ yaadzi nkpã a flee ɛyate nɛ awĩ.” Yesu Lɛ Lɔkɔ a Kĩ Baabubuli Asɔli Kɔla a.

english_verse: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ badi kalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a akũ, mɔ boe mɔ kalɛ a kĩ bɛkɔa bike koe, mɔ ayekĩ bɛkɛɛlɛ kɔla foebi a, mɔ fɔtsatewa a kĩ bakpa Baguma a. Lɛmɛ Yesu bi be kĩ,

translation_verse: “Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ miamɔ nɛ awĩ a, lɔkɔ aaya kĩ de lidima lɛláabuki lite nɛ lɔkɔba akũ. Baadzuli be flee biwula.” Lubokamɔ Mɔ Bati Liti Katomɛna Aaya.

english_verse: Some people asked, “Teacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Ade ke bivia ye kĩ, “Fɔtsa Ɔtsanɛ, lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe fukĩĩ aaya? Ade litsa bɔ́akɔa buyi ɔsũ kĩ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, “I am Christ!” and “Now is the time!” But don't follow them.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Midã kadã wĩ, mitafɔ̃ bɛkɛ mi. Bati pii aaya nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ bɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Ɔtɛnɛ a,’ mɔ ‘Lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ.’ Lɛmɛ mitatomɔ be.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
translation_verse: Mitafɔ̃ libe liwo mi ntɛ minu kawũkanɔ mɔ bati kayidza kadza nɛ bati akũ nkã. Kɛnɛ kĩ fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ flee fɔnya lɔtɔ fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ, lɛmɛ fɔátsa kĩ katinya kawũna a fuã kiyua.”

english_verse: Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ɛnamɛna lɔtɔ kĩ, “Ɔmaĩ mɔ ɔmaĩ aanɔ akũ, fɔgã mɔ fɔgã lɛmɛ aanɔ akũ.

english_verse: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
translation_verse: Tite kakuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, kɔlɛ mɔ fɔsɔ kpa kumɛkumɛ lɛmɛ aaya nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, ade libe mɔ awãwã fɔtsa aanɛ nɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ a.

english_verse: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
translation_verse: Mi bamanɛ, bawaɛ̃, akũ bati mɔ bakɔba aanyɛ mi bɛkpa ade baakɔ mi kamɛ badi a.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi aakesĩ mi nɛ yĩ sũ.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yudea bilo bɛnaa tɔtɔ akũ. Bikĩ bideĩ nɛ Yerusalem ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bilo bɛnɛ, ade bikĩ bɛláa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bɛtabuki bimuniki bɛya nɛ ye kamɛ.

english_verse: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ mimɔ́ kĩ sodzafɔ dza bisinya Yerusalem ni, minyi kĩ ɔmatɔ a kawɔla wo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ a na aanya Sɔtɔ Kavɛlɛ fuwi fɔkpa bati, ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa fɔlɛtsa a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
translation_verse: Fɔ́ana ɔsĩ paa fɔkpa bamatsɔ̃ mɔ babima a kĩ babi amɛmɛ be a. Lubokamɔ baũ aaya nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ, ade Baguma sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ aaya nɛ bati kĩĩ akũ a.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bɔa be lɛgbã kĩ, “Midã atɔ kutse mɔ futse bɛbã a.

english_verse: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ mimɔ́ fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ kĩ fɔlamɛna kaya ni, míanyi kĩ Baguma lɛgãkanya a fuã.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
translation_verse: Ke liti a Otidziwa Obi a aanɛ ɛta, ɛya nɛ luflututu kamɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ kpale mɔ ɔhaĩ.

english_verse: Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do.
translation_verse: “Awãwã fɔtsa aanɛ nɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ fɔkatsa nsɛntsɛlɛni nɛ suwa, odetɔ mɔ fusibii anɛ. Katinya kamɛ tɛmaĩ akũ aayɛla be, libe liwo be nɛ mpo kayili mɔ kavũ basabasa sũ.

english_verse: People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken.
translation_verse: Libe aafɔ̃ anɛ lisinya bati nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ baakadã anɛ kakpa lɛtsa a kĩ de aaya nɛ katinya kamɛ flee akũ a, kitonɛkĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ fɔtsa lɛláabuki fɔdza nɛ foe kadzakɔ̃.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place.
translation_verse: “Mabi mi pipiipi kĩ bati a kĩ bidzi finyaa lɛláaku biwoli fɔmɔ fɔtsa kĩĩ flee aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: like a trap. This day will surprise everyone on earth.
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ ɔyaɛ atɔ kpeĩ, kitonɛkĩ Luwi a aaketimɔ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edzi nkpã.

english_verse: putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mimɔ́ kĩ kɔnyɛ tɔɔta vɔɛ̃ ni, minyina kĩ kanɔde lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: The sky and the earth won't last forever, but my words will. A Warning
translation_verse: Tite mɔ ode flee onukpɛ̃ aatɛ̃ lɛmɛ yĩ fɔlɛtsa a kɔ̃ aana daa. Kɛnɛ Kĩ Miana Nɛ Kukũmanɛ Kamɛ.

english_verse: Don't spend all of your time thinking about eating or drinking or worrying about life. If you do, the final day will suddenly catch you
translation_verse: “Midã akũ kadã wĩ. Mitakɔa mi akũ mikpa kanya mɔ kamɔa pɔtɔɔ, mɔ katinya kamɛ ɔhau. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, Luwi a aayafuta mi,

english_verse: Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
translation_verse: Foesũ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, mikaabɔa mpaɛ luwi nyɛ luwi ayekĩ míana kɔbɛ̃ mitsã yedede nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́aya a flee kamɛ, lɔkɔa miyaadza nɛ Otidziwa Obi a anɛ kamɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Yesu kanaatsa bati fɔtsa luwi nyɛ luwi nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, ade ntɛ olobe wo ɔtɔ lɔkɔa yadzakũ kanaa Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ kawa nɛ awã.

english_verse: Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɛ nyɛ ɔnɔnɛ ni, bati kɔdabu atsɛna nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ katse ye fɔtsa katsa.

english_verse: They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ de be ade bike otse kĩ baakpa ye sika.

english_verse: I tell you I will not eat another Passover meal until it is finally eaten in God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ nláabuki ĩnya funitsã kĩĩ kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ foe kayɔkanu flee a ya nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanya a kamɛ.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were looking for a way to get rid of Jesus, because they were afraid of what the people might do.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a kavila bati a, foesũ bɛkabɔa mbɔdi kawɛ osi kĩ baatsã bɛna Yesu bɛkɔ nɛ kabɛla kamɛ. Yuda Tunɔ Kĩ Yaanyɛ Yesu Ɛkpa.

english_verse: Jesus told them, “As you go into the city, you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house
translation_verse: Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ miawo nɛ Yerusalem ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ni, onyole odi kĩ ɛnɔnɛ kugumi mɔ mui aatsɛlamɔ mi. Mitomɔ ye miwo nɛ tɔtɔ a kĩ te kamɛ yaawo a.

english_verse: Jesus got up from praying and went over to his disciples. They were asleep and worn out from being so sad.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu yidza nɛ mpaɛbɔ kamɛ a, emuniki ɛnaa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a sɛkɛ̃, ɛnamɔ be kĩ bɛwaa tida nɛ ayɔlɔhɔ a kĩ epi be a sũ.

english_verse: Judas went to talk with the chief priests and the officers of the temple police about how he could help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yuda naa Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Yudafɔ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ badãmɛna Asɔli Kɔla akũ a sɛkɛ̃, ade bipi adzuni nɛ osi wĩ a kĩ ye akũ yaatsã ɛnyɛ Yesu ɛkpa a.

english_verse: He agreed and started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus when the crowds were not around. Jesus Eats with His Disciples (Matthew
translation_verse: Yuda tunɔ nɛ foe akũ, foesũ ɛkawɛ lɔkɔ wĩ kĩ yaanyɛ Yesu ɛkpa nɛ bati kɔdabu a katanyi kamɛ. Yesu Manɛ Akũ Ɛkpa Wuda Katɛ̃ Luwi Funitsã a Kanya.

english_verse: Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he told the apostles, “Take this wine and share it with each other.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu bɔɛ kɔɔpu, ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mitɛ fukĩĩ misɛ̃ mikpa akũ.

english_verse: The one who will betray me is here at the table with me!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ midã, oti a kĩ yaanyɛ yĩ ɛkpa a, bɔmɔ ye budzi nɛ kanyakɔ̃ nɛ awĩ.

english_verse: You will eat and drink with me in my kingdom, and you will each sit on a throne to judge the twelve tribes of Israel. Jesus' Disciples Will Be Tested (Matthew
translation_verse: Míanya mimɔa mɔ yĩ nɛ opunu akũ nɛ yĩ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ, ade míadzi nɛ fɔgãkpo akũ minya Israel futete ewua-nviã a akũ a.” Yesu Lɛ Eyi Kĩ Petro Aasĩ Ye.

english_verse: The day had come for the Festival of Thin Bread, and it was time to kill the Passover lambs.
translation_verse: Luwi a kĩ bakɔ Wuda Katɛ̃ kavelibi nɛ Abodoo Kĩ Ɛtáyila Luwi Kanya kamɛ a wo ɔtɔ.

english_verse: So Jesus said to Peter and John, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us to eat.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Yesu do Petro mɔ Yohanes kĩ, “Minaa lɔkɔa minaamanɛ Wuda Katɛ̃ funitsã a miyi mikpa boe kanya.”

english_verse: But they asked, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a bivia ye kĩ, “Nɛ ndia awɛ kĩ bɔnaamanɛ foe buyi?”

english_verse: The Son of Man will die in the way that has been decided for him, but it will be terrible for the one who betrays him!”
translation_verse: Otidziwa Obi a kɔ̃, aaku ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma hihiɛ eyi a. Lɛmɛ bɔbɔɛ bakpa owula a kĩ yaanyɛ ye ɛkpa a.”

english_verse: Then the apostles started arguing about who would ever do such a thing. An Argument about Greatness
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a biyi akũ kavia kayɔ kĩ be kamɛ owei aakɛna fukĩĩ odu. Owei Bɛ̃ Flee Nɛ Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ a Kamɛ?

english_verse: he said to them, “I have very much wanted to eat this Passover meal with you before I suffer.
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Fɔnyɛ yĩ kawɛ kɔkɔɛ kĩ bɔmɔ mi bɔnya Wuda Katɛ̃ Funitsã kĩĩ fɔmɔ maamɔ lubo.

english_verse: After the meal he took another cup of wine in his hands. Then he said, “This is my blood. It is poured out for you, and with it God makes his new agreement.
translation_verse: Ade ayea kĩ bɛnya biyua a, ɛbɔɛ kɔɔpu mɔ waĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Kɔɔpu kĩĩ nyɛ Baguma nhihiɛ vɔɛ̃ kĩ esipi nɛ foe akũ mɔ yĩ babɔanɛ a kĩ maako nɛ tite nɛ mi lɔlɔ.

english_verse: You have stayed with me in all my troubles.
translation_verse: Mina nɛ yĩ akũ nɛ yĩ sɔhuiɛ flee kamɛ.

english_verse: So I will give you the right to rule as kings, just as my Father has given me the right to rule as a king.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Nda kpa yĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ ĩnya bati akũ a, ade ayea maakpa mi kɔbɛ̃ a na odu a.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Simon, listen to me! Satan has demanded the right to test each one of you, as a farmer does when he separates wheat from the husks.
translation_verse: Yesu vɛ Simon Petro ɛlɛ kĩ, “Simon, Simon! Tse katse wĩ! Bɛkpa Abonsam osi kĩ ɛsɔ mi ɛdã lɔkɔa ɛsa bati wĩ a nɛ bakpa kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ okuani awɛ afafa kanyɛ nɛ kamɔnɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “When I sent you out without a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals, did you need anything?” “No!” they answered.
translation_verse: Yesu via ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Lɔkɔ a kĩ ndo mi fɔtɔ kĩ ntáfɔ̃ mibɔɛ sika kuvũ, ĩye kuvũ kĩ babɛla ĩye mpabua a ni, litsedi nasĩ mi?” Bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsama tásĩ boe.”

english_verse: The disciples said, “Lord, here are two swords!” “Enough of that!” Jesus replied. Jesus Prays (Matthew
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, amɔ, fɔpamɛ gɛlɛɛlɛ nviã nɛ awĩ!” Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Fɔlɛ!” Yesu Bɔa Mpaɛ Nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ Akũ.

english_verse: Peter said, “Lord, I am ready to go with you to jail and even to die with you.”
translation_verse: Awã naa Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe ɔlate, mmanɛ akũ nkpa kĩ bɔmɔ nɔ aawa kɔla ĩye kĩ bɔmɔ nɔ aaku!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Peter, I tell you that before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.” Moneybags, Traveling Bags, and Swords
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Petro, mabi nɔ kĩ fɔmɔ ɔkɔkɔ aanyɛ onukpɛ̃ nviã lɛnyɛ kĩĩ ni, aalɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ onyína yĩ.” Sika Kuvũ Mɔ Kuvũ Kĩ Babɛla Mɔ Lɛpamɛ Gɛlɛɛlɛ.

english_verse: Do this because the Scriptures say, ‘He was considered a criminal.’ This was written about me, and it will soon come true.”
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔlɛ kĩ, ‘Bibu ye bido nɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ kamɛ,’ a kɛnɛ kĩ fɔya nɛ yĩ akũ. Kitonɛkĩ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ yĩ akũ a lamɛna kaya nɛ foe kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ.”

english_verse: He said to them, “Why are you asleep? Wake up and pray that you won't be tested.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Ebi be kĩ, “Nde sũ miawa tida? Miyidza mibɔa mpaɛ ayekĩ miláawo nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ.” Yesu Kapi.

english_verse: “Father, if you will, please don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nda, ntɛ fɔnyɛ nɔ kawɛ ni, tinya lubo kamɔ kɔɔpu kĩĩ nɛ yĩ akũ. Lɛmɛ takɛna nɛ yĩ kawɛ akũ, mbom kɛna nɛ nɔ kawɛ akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus went out to the Mount of Olives, as he often did, and his disciples went with him.
translation_verse: Yesu dzakũ ɛnɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ɛnaa Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ akũ ndɛ ayekĩ yawɛ kakɛna a, ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ lɛmɛ to mɔ ye a.

english_verse: When they got there, he told them, “Pray that you won't be tested.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ewo awã a, ebi be kĩ, “Mibɔa mpaɛ ayekĩ sɔhuiɛ lɛláanya nɛ mi akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way before he knelt down and prayed,
translation_verse: Ɛvɛlɛ akũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛnaa lɔtɔ sukuloo, ade ɛnake aduli ɛbɔa mpaɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus was in great pain and prayed so sincerely that his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood.
translation_verse: Ɛna nɛ ayɔlɔhɔ mɔ kɔnɛyɔɔlɛ kamɛ, ɛbɔa mpaɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ebu ade ye olulu na ndɛ babɔanɛ fɔbɔ, kako nɛ tite a.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu lamɛna kɔdzɛla kalɛ a, bati kɔdabu kudi de kɔnɛ. Yuda kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa nyaa be lɔtɔ. Efuã Yesu, etu eboomu ye.

english_verse: and a servant girl saw him. Then after she had looked at him carefully, she said, “This man was with Jesus!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bikĩ banya abawa kamɛ adi yamɔ Petro kĩ edzi yawa fi a, eke anɛ nɛ ye akũ ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Obi kĩĩ malo tomɔ Yesu.”

english_verse: One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
translation_verse: Lɛtalɛta be kamɛ ɔmɔa tsua Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a osumunɛ sɔmɔna sɔtɔ emuni.

english_verse: Jesus asked Judas, “Are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Yuda, ɔkɔa onukpɛ̃ kadzɛmɛ anyɛ Otidziwa Obi a ɔkpa bati?”

english_verse: When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we attack them with a sword?”
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛna nɛ ye akũ a mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ laya nɛ Yesu akũ a, bivia ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, bɔkɛna boe fɔpamɛ gɛlɛɛlɛ a akũ adzuma?”

english_verse: “Enough of that!” Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yesu lɛ kĩ, “Oowo! Fukpula nɛ awã!” Ade ke ɛta awã kĩ bitsua sɔtɔ a ɛtsa ye a.

english_verse: Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, “Why do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yesu bi basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ badãmɛna Asɔli Kɔla akũ a mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bɛyaapi ye a kĩ, “Nde sũ minɛɛ futsekpa mɔ fɔpamɛ miyaapi yĩ ndɛ tɔkpa ɔkɛnanɛ aye?

english_verse: Peter said, “Woman, I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro sĩ ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Otsole dã, ĩnyina ye kɔlaa!”

english_verse: A little later someone else saw Peter and said, “You are one of them!” “No, I'm not!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Owi tábuna mɔ onyole odi mɔ́ Petro eyi ɔsũ a ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nɔ malo ɔnyɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa!” Lɛmɛ Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Onyole dã, nnɛ́ yĩ manɛ!”

english_verse: They said, “Tell us! Are you the Messiah?” Jesus replied, “If I said so, you wouldn't believe me.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a, bivia Yesu kĩ, “Bi boe ntɛ nɔ nyɛ Kristo a.” Ade Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ nlɛ kadi malo miláatɛ yĩ mido.

english_verse: Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance.
translation_verse: Foesũ bipi Yesu ade bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a awã a. Petro lɛmɛ tomɔ be ɛna nɛ liti flee.

english_verse: At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a yakpɔta ade bɛkpã Yesu bɛyadzɛla nɛ be anɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Peter replied, “I don't know what you are talking about!” Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ “Onyole dã, ĩnyína lɛtsa a kĩ de ɔlamɛna kalɛ!” Mɔ Petro onukpɛ̃ katɔ pɛ, ɔkɔkɔ nyɛ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, “Before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate dani ɛdã Petro, ade Petro te anɛ kĩ boe Ɔlate bi ye kĩ, “Fɔmɔ ɔkɔkɔ aanyɛ onukpɛ̃ lɛnyɛ kĩĩ ni, aalɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ onyína yĩ.”

english_verse: Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Ade ke Petro nɛ ɛnaa kanya ɛnabe blobloblo a. Bidi Yesu Nsekuo.

english_verse: The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛdãmɛna Yesu akũ a di ye nsekuo bibo ye lɛmɛ.

english_verse: They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade bibi ye fuvũnatsa pii lɛmɛ a. Yesu Dza Nɛ Ɔmaĩ Banɔkɔɛ̃ Fɔlɛtsa Badzɛnɛ Anɛ.

english_verse: And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ nvia mi kadi malo ni, miláakpa mbuayɛ.

english_verse: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kito nviã kĩĩ kanaa ni, baadzina Otidziwa Obi a nɛ Baguma Kpale Nɛ Ode a sɔmɔna.”

english_verse: Then they asked, “Are you the Son of God?” Jesus answered, “You say I am!”
translation_verse: Ade ke be a flee bivia ye kĩ, “Foesũ Baguma obi anɛ?” Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mi lamɛna kalɛ kĩ ye manɛ.”

english_verse: They replied, “Why do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!”
translation_verse: Ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɔtásĩ adansifɔ badima. Boe mɔawɛ a bunu lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a.”

english_verse: Everyone in the council got up and led Jesus off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Ade bati kɔdabu a flee yidza bɛkpã Yesu bɛnamɛna Pilato sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: They started accusing him and said, “We caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.”
translation_verse: Ade bido ye onukpɛ̃ katsua awɛ kalɛ kĩ, “Bupi obi kĩĩ kĩ ɛlamɔ boe kade bati adzuni kadani. Yabi be kĩ bɛtate ɔgɔlɛ bɛkpa Romafɔ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃. Ɛbɔɛ ye akũ kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo, lɛgã a.”

english_verse: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
translation_verse: Lɛgã Herodes mɔ ye sodzafɔ a di Yesu nsekuo bisupa ye. Bido ye atadiɛ wulaa mɔ kalɛ ndɛ lɛgã aye, bimuniki mɔ ye bɛma Pilato.

english_verse: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Mikpã oti kĩĩ mima yĩ ade milɛ kĩ ɛlamɔ bati adzuni kadani a. Finyaa nɛ mi anɛ kamɛ nɛ awĩ a, nvia ye onukpɛ̃ kamɛ tuwuli, ade fɔlɛtsa a kĩ mibɔɛ nɛ ye akũ a, ntámɔ tɔkpa tidima nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ makɔ ye kɔtɔ.

english_verse: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛdzɛmɛna kalɛ kabuli kĩ, “Ɛnɛɛ ye fɔtsa katsa yado ɔtutũ nɛ bati kamɛ nɛ Yudea tite flee akũ. Eyi fukĩĩ kayɔ nɛ Galilea ade finyaa ɛkɔa foe ɛyamɛna nɛ awĩ lɛmɛ a.” Herodes Dzɛmɔ Yesu.

english_verse: But the whole crowd shouted, “Kill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!”
translation_verse: Bati kɔdabu a flee bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Kɔ ye! Fɔnɛ Baraba awɛ kakpa boe!”

english_verse: Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words,” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Pilato via ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Yudafɔ Lɛgã a?” Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, fɔla ndɛ aye kĩ ɔlɛ a.”

english_verse: Pilate told the chief priests and the crowd, “I don't find him guilty of anything.”
translation_verse: Ade Pilato bi Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ bati kɔdabu a kĩ, “Ntámɔ lɛtsama nɛ ye akũ kĩ madza nɛ foe akũ nkɔ oti kĩĩ kɔtɔ.”

english_verse: After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ enyi kĩ Yesu to Galilea nɛ awã kĩ Lɛgã Herodes anya akũ a, ɛkɔa ye ɛma Herodes, kĩ ɛna nɛ Yerusalem nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ.

english_verse: For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ de Herodes paa kĩ ɛmɔ Yesu, kitonɛkĩ enu ye nkã ade ɛkawɛ kĩ yamɔ ye anɛ kɔkɔkɔɛ a. Ɛkadã anɛ kĩ Yesu aakɛna awãwã fɔtsa fudi ɛtsa ye.

english_verse: Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Foesũ Herodes via Yesu fɔlɛtsa pii lɛmɛ Yesu tábinya onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
translation_verse: Awã naa Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a nɛ bɛyadza nɛ lɔtɔ bitsua fɔlɛtsa mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ bite nɛ Yesu akũ.

english_verse: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends, even though they had been enemies before this. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Luwi a na, Herodes mɔ Pilato dani bɛkɛna bakɔba, kufɛ kɔkɔɛ a bakesĩ akũ. Pilato Kɔ́ Yesu Kɔtɔ Ɛkpa Wuda.

english_verse: As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ a kpã Yesu bɛnamɛna. Ade kĩ banaa nɛ osi a, bɛtsɛlamɛna Kireneni onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Simon, kĩ eto akula adi kamɛ yanna Yerusalem. Bipi ye bɛhɛ ye kĩ ɛlɔ asendua a etomɔ Yesu nɛ liti.

english_verse: One of the criminals hanging there also insulted Jesus by saying, “Aren't you the Messiah? Save yourself and save us!”
translation_verse: Tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ a kĩ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ a kamɛ ɔmɔa vũna Yesu kĩ, “Nnɛ́ nɔ nyɛ Kristo a? Tɛ nɔ akũ katɛ boe malo!”

english_verse: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people.
translation_verse: Pilato vɛ Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃, ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ bati a ɛkpɔta.

english_verse: Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds.
translation_verse: Pilato wɛ kĩ yafɔnɛ Yesu awɛ foesũ ebuki ɛnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ ɛtsa bati kɔdabu a.

english_verse: Pilate spoke to them a third time, “But what crime has he done? I have not found him guilty of anything for which he should be put to death. I will have him beaten with a whip and set free.”
translation_verse: Pilato buki ebi be lɛlalɛde kĩ, “Mɔ litsa tɔkpa oti kĩĩ kɛna? Ntámɔ lɛtsa kĩ ɛkɛna kĩ yana foe akũ wuda! Maafɔ̃ bibo ye lɔkɔa nfɔnɛ ye awɛ.”

english_verse: The people kept on shouting as loud as they could for Jesus to be put to death.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati a dzɛmɛna fɔwɔla kabɔa kɔɔba kĩ bɛbɔa Yesu nɛ asendua akũ. Nɛ foe kawũna kamɛ a, bati a fɔwɔla kabɔa nya Pilato akũ.

english_verse: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade ɛfɔnɛ oti a kĩ bawɛ a awɛ ɛkpa be a, oti kĩ bido nɛ kɔla nɛ ɔtutũ mɔ bati kakɔ sũ, ade ɛkɔa Yesu edo nɛ be awɛ kĩ bɛkɛna ye ndɛ ayekĩ bɛwɛ a. Sodzafɔ Bɔa Yesu Nɛ Otse Akũ.

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bɛkɛna fukĩĩ nɛ kutse gɔ̃ akũ ni, nde fɔána fɔkpa kutse wɔlɛwa?”

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Kĩ kite sukuloo suwa tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã abɔa a, suwa fɔ̃ kata, ade ɔtũ ke nɛ katinya flee kamɛ kɛnawo tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
translation_verse: Bɛkpã banyole bɛbã nviã kĩ bɛnyɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ bibumɔ Yesu kĩ bɛnaakɔ be.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Bati a flee dza nɛ awã bɛkadã foe. Ade Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a di Yesu nsekuo bɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɛtɛ bati bɛbã nkpã foesũ ntɛ ye nyɛ Kristo a kĩ Baguma pau a ni, ɛnyɛ ye akũ!”

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Ade ayea sodzafɔ a lɛmɛ di Yesu nsekuo a. Bifuã ye bɛkɔa waĩ mɔ ɔnyɛ mɔ ɔnyɛ bɛkpa ye kĩ ɛmɔa.

english_verse: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
translation_verse: Ade bibi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ nɔ nyɛ Yudafɔ lɛgã a ni, tɛ nɔ akũ!”

english_verse: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɔyade a wũni nɛ ye akũ kĩ, “Aávila Baguma mbɔɛɛ? Ɔtɛ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ mɔapɛ a kĩ ɛtɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Awã naa Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ nviã kĩĩ aana nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Paradiso kamɛ.” Bɛkɔ Yesu.

english_verse: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle.
translation_verse: Ade lima a kĩ lɛvanɛ nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a bɛ̃ nɛ kayite a.

english_verse: Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bimuniki bɛnaa kɔla bɛnamanɛ tutsonu mɔ fugua mɔ ɔlɛ̃ mɔ ɔlɛ̃ kĩ baakɔa bɛtsɛla ɔnɔkpa a biyi. Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ a, bidzi bɛwɛya nɛ be mbla a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Jesus shouted, “Father, I put myself in your hands!” Then he died.
translation_verse: Yesu bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Nda, nkɔa yĩ kawɛya ndo nɛ nɔ awɛ.” Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ a, eku.

english_verse: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a kĩ bɛkpɔta nɛ awã bɛkadã foe flee a mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a, bɛdzakũ bɛnaa kɔla mɔ ayɔlɔhɔ mɔ akpɔ kako nɛ lɔlɔ mɔ ɔwɔlɛ.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ binyina Yesu kanyina wĩ a, mɔ batsole a kĩ bitomɔ ye bito Galilea a, dza mɔ lugo bɛkadã fɔtsa kĩĩ. Yosef Kĩ Eto Arimatia a Bila Yesu.

english_verse: There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ ɛna nɛ Yudafɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ katsɛna a kamɛ malo ni, ɛtátunɔ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ bike otse, mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkɛna a akũ.

english_verse: Joseph went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body.
translation_verse: Ɛnaa Pilato anɛ kamɛ ade ɛnawã Yesu ɔnɔkpa a.

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
translation_verse: Ade ke ɛvɛnya ɔnɔkpa a ɛkɔa lima ovoe esuli ye a. Ɛkɔa ye ɛwɔla nɛ linikɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ nɛ de kamɛ, kĩ bɛtábila odima nɛ de kamɛ tɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: It was Friday, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
translation_verse: Luwi a nya Efieda ade Kawɛya Luwi a lamɔ ɔtɔ kawo a.

english_verse: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
translation_verse: Batsole a kĩ bitómɔ Yesu bito Galilea tomɔ Yosef bɛnadã linikɛ a, mɔ ayekĩ bɛkɔa Yesu ɔnɔkpa bɛwɔla nɛ de kamɛ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Batsole a nyɛ Maria Magdalani, Yohana mɔ Maria kĩ ɛnyɛ Yakobo ɔma. Bɛmɔ batsole bɛbã a bɛnabi fɔtɔ banyanɛ a fɔlɛtsa a flee.

english_verse: When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance,
translation_verse: Awã naa bɛmɔ́ kĩ bimininya de kpale a kĩ bɛkɔa bisua linikɛ onukpɛ̃ a biyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃.
