english_verse: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
translation_verse: Ɔnanɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edeĩ nɛ yĩ akũ kĩ yaáwũ babi ni, yatsua ye kamuni, ade ɔnanɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawũ babi ni, yanyɛ ye akũ tɔɔta, ayekĩ yaawũ babi pii.

english_verse: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
translation_verse: Finyaa yĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ mbi mi a kpalɛ mi akũ kɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
translation_verse: Mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ, yĩ malo nkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ mi. Ndɛ ayekĩ kutse ɔnanɛ lɛláapuli kuwũ nɛ koe akũ, mbɔɛntɛ kɔmanɛ nɛ kutse ɔma akũ a, ade aye mi malo miláapuli miwũ babi ntɛ mitákɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ a.

english_verse: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
translation_verse: “Yĩ nyɛ kutse a, ade mi minyɛ tɛnanɛ a. Oti a kĩ ɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ, mɔ yĩ malo nkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye ni, ade ye aawũ babi pii, kitonɛkĩ ntɛ nláa mi kamɛ ni, miláapuli mikɛna lɛtsama.

english_verse: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛláa yĩ kamɛ ni, ɛla ndɛ ɔnanɛ kĩ bitsua bɛta bimuni ade yawɔlɛ a. Bakpɔta tɛnanɛ a kafila kado nɛ fi kamɛ tavio.

english_verse: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ, mɔ yĩ fɔlɛtsa a deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ ni, míavia lɛtsa a kĩ miawɛ ade baakɛna foe bɛkpa mi a.

english_verse: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
translation_verse: Ntɛ miwũ babi beblebee ni, foe fɔnyɛ Nda kukũkpalɛ fɔtsa, ade fusipi kĩ yĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ mianɛ.

english_verse: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ Nda awɛ yĩ kalɛ a ni, ade aye yĩ malo mawɛ mi kalɛ a. Foesũ mifɔ̃ yĩ ɔdɔ a ɛna nkɔsu nɛ mi kamɛ lɔmɔ.

english_verse: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mipi yĩ tɛbla a midomɔ akũ ni, míana nɛ yĩ ɔdɔ a kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ malo mpi Nda mbla ndomɔ akũ fɔfɔ̃ ye ɔdɔ deĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ a.

english_verse: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
translation_verse: “Mbi mi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ ayekĩ kawɔlɛ a kĩ kideĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ a odu kɛna nɛ mi kamɛ, lɔkɔa mi kawɔlɛ a kɛkpɔla.

english_verse: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
translation_verse: Ade yĩ mbla kĩ makpa mi nyɛ kĩ, miwɛ mi akũ akũ kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ nwɛ mi kalɛ a.

english_verse: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔ titiliwu adima lɛláa kĩ oti akɔa ye nkpã ɛbɔa afɔliɛ nɛ bakɔba lɔlɔ.

english_verse: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mikɛna nɛ yĩ mbla a kĩ nkpa mi a akũ a ni, lɔkɔa minyɛ yĩ bakɔba.

english_verse: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
translation_verse: Nláabuki nvɛ mi kĩ basumunɛ, kitonɛkĩ ɔsumunɛ linyína lɛtsa a kĩ ye ɔlate akɛna. Lɛmɛ mavɛ mi kĩ bakɔba kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ nnu ntomɛna Nda sɛkɛ̃ ni, nfɔ̃ minyi foe.

english_verse: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ mi lɛpau yĩ, mbom yĩ lɛpau mi ndzina mi kĩ minaawũ babi beblebee kĩ baawa daa. Nɛ foesũ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míavia Ɔlɛga a nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ ni, yaakpa mi foe.

english_verse: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
translation_verse: Foesũ ade mbla kĩ makpa mi kĩ miwɛ mi akũ kalɛ.” Katinya Kamɛ Likesĩ.

english_verse: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
translation_verse: Yesu buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ katinya kamɛ bati akesĩ mi ni, minyi kĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ bikesĩ yĩ.

english_verse: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ katinya kamɛ bade mianɛ ni, kufɛ baawɛ mi kalɛ ndɛ be bade aye. Lɛmɛ kĩ mpau mi ntomɛna katinya bati kamɛ ade mibuki minyɛ́ be bade a sũ ni, katinya kamɛ bati akesĩ mi.

english_verse: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
translation_verse: Mite anɛ nɛ kalɛ a kĩ mbi mi akũ a kĩ, ‘Ɔsumunɛ lɛbɛ́ ye ɔlate.’ Ntɛ bati yidza bɛdza nɛ yĩ akũ ni, baayidza bɛdza nɛ mi malo akũ. Ntɛ bipi yĩ fɔlɛtsa bidomɔ akũ ni, baapi mi fude lɛmɛ bidomɔ akũ.

english_verse: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ baakɛna fukĩĩ flee nɛ mi akũ nɛ yĩ dĩ sũ, kitonɛkĩ binyína oti a kĩ edo yĩ a.

english_verse: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ntayaabi be fɔlɛtsa ni, kufɛ bɛtáku tɔkpa kɔtɔ. Lɛmɛ finyaa bɛkɛna tɔkpa ade bɛlá onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ kadima a.

english_verse: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakesĩ yĩ ni, yakesĩ Nda lɛmɛ.

english_verse: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ntakɛna fɔtsa kĩ odima tákɛna tɔ̃ nɛ be kamɛ ni, kufɛ bɛtákɛna tɔkpa, lɛmɛ kĩ finyaa bɛmɔ́ fɔtsa a kĩ nkɛna a malo ni, bakesĩ bɔmɔ Nda.

english_verse: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fukĩĩ flee ya nɛ foe kamɛ ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ be mbla ovoli kamɛ a, fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, ‘Bikesĩ yĩ pupulidza.’

english_verse: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
translation_verse: “Maado Obumɛnanɛ eto Ɔlɛga a sɛkɛ̃ ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃. Ade ye nyɛ Hũhũ a kĩ yaanyɛ ɔnɔkɔali a nɛ Baguma akũ ɛtsa a. Ntɛ eto Ɔlɛga a sɛkɛ̃ ɛya ni, yaadi adansiɛ nɛ yĩ akũ.

english_verse: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
translation_verse: Mi malo míadi adansiɛ, kitonɛkĩ mina nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ kito kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
translation_verse: “Mbi mi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee ayekĩ bati lɛláafɔ̃ akpɔ liveli mi nɛ mi katɛkado kamɛ.

english_verse: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
translation_verse: Baablɔmɛna mi nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃. Lɔkɔ aya kĩ ntɛ oti odi kɔ mi ni, yaakɔna kĩ Baguma adzuma ɛkɛna.

english_verse: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
translation_verse: Bati aakɛna fukĩĩ nɛ mi akũ kitonɛkĩ bɛtányi Ɔlɛga a ĩye yĩ.

english_verse: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mabi mi fukĩĩ ayekĩ ntɛ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ ni, míate anɛ kĩ mbi mi foe. Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a Adzuma. “Ntábi mi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ nɛ kayɔkayi a, kitonɛkĩ nna nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa kĩ madzakũ nnaa oti a kĩ edo yĩ a sɛkɛ̃ a, mi kamɛ odima távia yĩ awã kĩ manaa a.

english_verse: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
translation_verse: Finyaa, kĩ mbi mi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, mi flee miwo ɔblɔ.

english_verse: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mabi mi ɔnɔkɔali a kĩ fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa mi kĩ ndzakũ, kitonɛkĩ ntɛ ntánaa ni, Obumɛnanɛ a lɛláaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ndzakũ nnaa ni, maado ye ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ ɛya ni, yaakɔ katinya kamɛ bati kɔtɔ ɛfɔ̃ binyi lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ tɔkpa, mɔ kadzi kpĩĩ, mɔ Baguma kɔtɔkakɔ.

english_verse: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
translation_verse: Yaakɔ be kɔtɔ nɛ tɔkpa sũ, kitonɛkĩ bɛtátɛ yĩ bido.

english_verse: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
translation_verse: Yaakɔ be kɔtɔ bɛkɔa binyi kadzi kpĩĩ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ manaa Nda sɛkɛ̃, kĩ miláabuki mimɔ́ yĩ,

english_verse: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
translation_verse: ade baanyi lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ kɔtɔkaku, kitonɛkĩ bɛkɔ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ akũ ɔnyanɛ a kɔtɔ kɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
translation_verse: “Ndeĩ fɔlɛtsa pii kĩ maabi mi, lɛmɛ fɔ́aba mi kalɔ finyaa.

english_verse: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Hũhũ a kĩ yanyɛ ɔnɔkɔali nɛ Baguma akũ katsa a ya ni, yaafɔ̃ minyi Baguma akũ ɔnɔkɔali a flee a. Ɛláalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ ye mɔawɛ otumi akũ, mbom lɛtsa a kĩ enu a odi mɔ yaalɛ, ade yaafɔ̃ minyi fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́ayaya a.

english_verse: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
translation_verse: Yaado yĩ obu ɛnyɛ yĩ kukũkpalɛ ɛtsa, kitonɛkĩ yaabi mi lɛtsa a kĩ ɛtɛ etomɛna yĩ sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kĩ fɔnyɛ Nda fude a flee, yĩ fude fɔanɛ. Ade foesũ nlɛ kĩ Hũhũ a aanu etomɛna yĩ sɛkɛ̃ ɛyaabi mi a.” Fɔtsa Bakɔlɛnɛ Ɔblɔ Kawo Mɔ Kawɔlɛ Kamɔ.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ ɛnamɛna lɔtɔ kĩ, “Kite sukuloo kĩ miláabuki mimɔ́ yĩ, lɛmɛ owi lɛláabuna kĩ míabuki mimɔ́ yĩ.”

english_verse: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
translation_verse: Adeke ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a badi via akũ kĩ, “Foe kayɔ nyɛ litsa kĩ yabi boe kĩ, ‘Kite sukuloo miláabuki mimɔ́ yĩ, lɛmɛ owi lɛláabuna kĩ míabuki mimɔ́ yĩ.’ Ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Kitonɛkĩ manaa Nda sɛkɛ̃.’

english_verse: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Kite sukuloo’ a kayɔ nyɛ litsa? Bɔánu lɛtsa a kĩ yalɛ a kayɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
translation_verse: Yesu mɔ́ kĩ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a wɛ kĩ bavia ye kalɛ kadi, foesũ ebi be kĩ, “Nlɛ kĩ kite sukuloo miláabuki mimɔ́ yĩ, lɛmɛ owi lɛláabuna kĩ míabuki mimɔ́ yĩ. Mbɔɛɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ milamɛna kasa mɔ akũ a?

english_verse: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
translation_verse: Mabi mi ɔnɔkɔali a kĩ míabe miwo ɔblɔ, lɛmɛ katinya kamɛ bati aamɔ kawɔlɛ. Míadi ayɔlɔhɔ lɛmɛ mi ayɔlɔhɔ a aadani kawɔlɛkamɔ.

english_verse: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
translation_verse: Ntɛ sɛmɛ pi otsole ni, yawo ɔblɔ kitonɛkĩ ye lubo kamɔ ɔdɔũ a bɔa. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɛma eyua a, yalɛla ye lubo kamɔ a, kitonɛkĩ yamɔ kawɔlɛ kĩ ɛma obi edo nɛ katinya kamɛ.

english_verse: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
translation_verse: Ade aye fɔla fɔkpa mi a. Finyaa miwo ɔblɔ, lɛmɛ maabuki mmɔ́ mi, lɔkɔa mi tɔwɔlɛ lɛka tidzi mi mɔ kawɔlɛ paa a kĩ odima lɛláapuli ɛtɛ nɛ mi awɛ a.

english_verse: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
translation_verse: “Ntɛ luwi a wo ɔtɔ ni, miláabuki mivia yĩ lɛtsama. Ɔnɔkɔali a mabi mi kĩ, Nda aakpa mi lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ míavia ye nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ.

english_verse: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
translation_verse: Kito kɛyawo finyaa kĩĩ a, mitávia tɔ̃ lɛtsama nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ. Mivia, lɔkɔa míatɛ, ayekĩ mi kawɔlɛkamɔ aakpɔla. Yesu Nya Katinya Kamɛ Akũ.

english_verse: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
translation_verse: “Mabi mi fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ. Lɛmɛ lɔkɔ aaya kĩ nláabuki mbi mi fɔlɛtsa nɛ fɔgbã kamɛ, mbom maabi mi Nda akũ fɔlɛtsa pefee.

english_verse: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ luwi a wo ɔtɔ ni, mivia ye fɔtsa nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ. Maátsa kĩ yĩ maavia ye nkpa mi,

english_verse: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ Nda mɔawɛ a awɛ mi kalɛ. Yawɛ mi kalɛ kitonɛkĩ miawɛ yĩ kalɛ ade mitɛ mido kĩ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ nto ĩya.

english_verse: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
translation_verse: Nuwĩ, Ɔlɛga a sɛkɛ̃ nto ĩya nɛ katinya kamɛ, ade finyaa madzakũ nɛ katinya kamɛ nnaa Nda sɛkɛ̃ a.”

english_verse: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
translation_verse: Ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Dã, finyaa kɔ̃ alɛ kɔdzɛla pefee, kĩ obuki aábɔa fɔgbã.

english_verse: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
translation_verse: Finyaa kɔ̃ bunyina kĩ onyina lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa, ade fɔtásĩ kĩ odi abuki evia nɔ kalɛ kadima a. Fukĩĩ fɔ̃ bɔtɛ budo kĩ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ oto.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
translation_verse: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Finyaa kɔ̃ mitɛ mido?

english_verse: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ aya ade liwo ɔtɔ kɔkɔɛ malo, kĩ baapasam mi flee odi nyɛ odi lɛnaa ye tɔtɔ kamɛ kite yĩ odi. Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ yĩ odi aate, kitonɛkĩ Nda deĩ nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
translation_verse: Mbi mi fukĩĩ kĩĩ flee ayekĩ míakɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ bɔna lukudɔ. Katinya kamɛ bati aafɔ̃ mimɔ́ lubo. Lɛmɛ mido ɔwɔlɛ! Ĩnya katinya kamɛ akũ!”

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu lɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ eyua a, ɛdɛla anɛ ɛdã ode, ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nda, ɔdɔũ a bɔa. Dɛla nɔ Obi dĩ nɛ ode lɔkɔa nɔ Obi lɛmɛ ɛdɛla nɔ dĩ nɛ ode.

english_verse: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ɔkpa ye otumi nɛ batidziwa flee akũ ayekĩ yaakpa bati a kĩ ɔkɔa odo nɛ ye awɛ a nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
translation_verse: Nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a nyɛ kĩ baanyi nɔ ɔnɔkɔali Baguma keĩ a mɔ Yesu Kristo a kĩ odo fɔtɔ a.

english_verse: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
translation_verse: Ndɛla nɔ dĩ nɛ ode nɛ tite akũ, ade ĩyua adzuma a kĩ ɔkpa yĩ kĩ nkɛna a.

english_verse: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Foesũ Nda, dɛla yĩ dĩ nɛ ode nɛ nɔ anɛ kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ nna nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ fɔmɔ bɛbɔa katinya a.

english_verse: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
translation_verse: “Ĩnyɛ nɔ ntsa bati a kĩ ɔnyɛ nɛ katinya kamɛ ɔkpa yĩ a. Nɔ bade banɛ, lɛmɛ ɔkɔa ɔkpa yĩ. Bɛkɛna nɛ nɔ kalɛ akũ.

english_verse: They know that you gave me everything I have.
translation_verse: Finyaa kɔ̃ binyina kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ ɔkɔa ɔkpa yĩ a flee to nɔ sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Nkɔa ɔlɛdo a kĩ ɔkpa yĩ a nkpa be, ade bɛtɛ ye a. Binyina kĩ ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ nto, ade bɛtɛ bido kĩ nɔ lido yĩ a.

english_verse: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
translation_verse: “Make aduli nɛ be lɔlɔ. Nlámɛna aduli kake nɛ katinya kamɛ bati lɔlɔ, mbom nɛ bati a kĩ ɔkɔa odo nɛ yĩ awɛ a lɔlɔ, kitonɛkĩ nɔ bade banɛ.

english_verse: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
translation_verse: Yĩ bati a flee nɔ bade banɛ, ade ayea nɔ bade flee lɛmɛ nyɛ yĩ bade a, ade bɛdɛla yĩ dĩ nɛ ode bɛtsãmɛna be akũ a.

english_verse: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
translation_verse: Ade finyaa maya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃, mbuki nláa katinya kamɛ, lɛmɛ be kɔ̃ bideĩ nɛ katinya kamɛ. Ɔlɛga Kpalɛwa, kɔa nɔ dĩ akũ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ ɔkpa yĩ a kadãmɛna be akũ ayekĩ baakɛna ɔmɔa ndɛ ayekĩ bɔmɔ nɔ bɔnyɛ ɔmɔa a.

english_verse: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
translation_verse: Kĩ nna nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ a, nkɔa nɔ dĩ akũ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ ɔkpa yĩ a ndãmɛna be akũ. Ndã be akũ kĩ be kamɛ odima táyɛla, mbɔɛntɛ oti a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛyɛla a, ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa fɔlɛtsa a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa maya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃, ade malɛ fukĩĩ bamɛ nɛ katinya kamɛ ayekĩ baamɔ kawɔlɛ kɛkpɔla nɛ be kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ malo mamɔ ke a.

english_verse: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
translation_verse: “Nkɔa nɔ ɔlɛdo a nkpa be lɛmɛ katinya kamɛ bati kesĩ be, kitonɛkĩ bɛtáto katinya kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ malo ntáto katinya kamɛ a.

english_verse: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
translation_verse: Ntávia nɔ kĩ nyɛ be nɛ katinya kamɛ, mbom mawã nɔ kĩ nyɛ be nɛ Okesĩnɛ a awɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ ntáto katinya kamɛ sũ ni, be malo bɛnyɛ́ katinya kamɛ bade.

english_verse: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
translation_verse: Kɛna be bati kpalɛwa kakpa nɔ akũ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali a kamɛ, nɔ kalɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali.

english_verse: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
translation_verse: Ndo be nɛ katinya kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ nɔ malo odo yĩ nɛ katinya kamɛ a.

english_verse: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
translation_verse: Ade nɛ be sũ ni, nkpalɛ yĩ akũ nkpa nɔ ayekĩ be malo baakpalɛ be malo akũ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali a kamɛ bɛkpa nɔ. Yesu Bɔa Mpaɛ Nɛ Batɛ Badonɛ Flee Lɔlɔ.

english_verse: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
translation_verse: “Nnɛ́ bati kĩĩ odi lɔlɔ mabɔa mpaɛ, mbom mabɔa nkpa bati a kĩ baatɛ yĩ bido nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ baabi be a sũ.

english_verse: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
translation_verse: Make nɔ aduli kĩ fɔ̃ be flee bɛkɛna ɔmɔa. Nda, fɔ̃ be malo bɛna nɛ boe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ odeĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ, ade yĩ malo ndeĩ nɛ nɔ kamɛ a. Fɔ̃ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa ayekĩ katinya kamɛ bati aatɛ bido kĩ nɔ lido yĩ.

english_verse: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
translation_verse: “Ndɛla be buĩ nɛ ode ndɛ ayekĩ ɔdɛla yĩ dĩ nɛ ode, ayekĩ baakɛna ɔmɔa ndɛ ayekĩ bɔmɔ nɔ bɔnyɛ ɔmɔa a.

english_verse: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
translation_verse: Yĩ ndeĩ nɛ be kamɛ ade nɔ malo odeĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ, kĩ baakɛna ɔmɔa bɛkpɔla, ayekĩ katinya kamɛ bati aanyi kĩ nɔ lido yĩ. Ade baanyi kĩ awɛ be kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ yĩ kalɛ a.

english_verse: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
translation_verse: “Nda, mawɛ kĩ bati a kĩ ɔkɔa ɔkpa yĩ a bɛna nɛ awã kĩ ndeĩ a, ayekĩ baamɔ yĩ kukũkpalɛ a kĩ ɔkpa yĩ a, kitonɛkĩ ɔwɛ yĩ kalɛ fɔmɔ biyi katinya kayɔ.

english_verse: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
translation_verse: “Nda, Oti Kpĩĩ, katinya kamɛ bati linyína nɔ lɛmɛ yĩ kɔ̃ ĩnyina nɔ, ade bati kĩĩ lɛmɛ nyina kĩ nɔ lido yĩ a.

english_verse: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
translation_verse: Ĩnyɛ nɔ ntsa be yededeede, ade maakanyɛ nɔ nkatsa be, ayekĩ ɔdɔ a kĩ odeĩ ɔkpa yĩ a aana nɛ be kamɛ, lɔkɔa yĩ malo maana nɛ be kamɛ.”

english_verse: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
translation_verse: Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a basumunɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ ye akũ oti sɔtɔ Petro baa emuni a via ye kĩ, “Ntámɔ nɔ nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ nɛ kɔbã a kamɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu buki evia be kĩ, “Owei miawɛ?” Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yesu Nazaretni a.”

english_verse: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mbi mi kɔkɔɛ kĩ yĩ nyɛ ye, foesũ ntɛ yĩ miawɛ kɔ̃ ni, mifɔ̃ bati kĩĩ bɛdzakũ.”

english_verse: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ fukĩĩ ayekĩ kalɛ a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ kĩ, “Nda, bati a kĩ ɔkɔa odo nɛ yĩ awɛ a kamɛ odima táyɛla.”

english_verse: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
translation_verse: Ade Simon Petro nyɛ kɔpã a kĩ ɛnɛɛ a ɛbaa Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a ɔsumunɛ sɔmɔna sɔtɔ emuni a. Ɔsumunɛ a dĩ nyɛ Malko.

english_verse: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Roma sodzafɔ mɔ be banɔkɔɛ̃, mɔ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla akũ badãmɛnanɛ a pi Yesu ade bisui ye a.

english_verse: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro fɔ̃ ɛdza nɛ kanya nɛ onyukpɛ̃. Ade fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ bɛbã a nɛ́, ɛnaa kanya ɛnalɛmɔ oyitsɔ̃ a kĩ yadã onyukpɛ̃ akũ a kɔdzɛla, ade ɛkpã Petro ewomɛna nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
translation_verse: Oyitsɔ̃ a kĩ yadã onyukpɛ̃ akũ a via Petro kĩ, “Nɔ malo nnɛ́ onyole kĩĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa anɛ?” Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oowo, ĩnyɛ́ ye fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ.”

english_verse: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
translation_verse: Osili na, foesũ basumunɛ mɔ bikĩ badzɔlɛ awã akũ a tɛna futuã fi bɛdza bisinya bawa foe. Petro lɛmɛ nadza nɛ be ɔwɛ̃ ɛkawa fi a. Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a Via Yesu Fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
translation_verse: Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a via Yesu fɔlɛtsa nɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ mɔ ye fɔtsa katsa a akũ.

english_verse: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
translation_verse: Mɔ nde sũ avia yĩ fɔlɛtsa? Via bikĩ binu yĩ fɔlɛtsa a. Ade be nyina lɛtsa a kĩ nlɛ a.”

english_verse: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ade Hana fɔ̃ bɛkpã Yesu kĩ ebuki edeĩ nɛ tɔwɛ̃ kamɛ a bɛma Kayafa, Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a. Petro Buki Enunya Kĩ Enyína Yesu.

english_verse: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu a, bɛkpã Yesu bitomɛna Kayafa sɛkɛ̃ bɛnamɛna abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a tɔtɔ kamɛ. Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔawɛ táwo nɛ abãã tɔtɔ a kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ bɛwɛ kĩ be akũ ɛkpalɛ nɛ be amaniɛ onukpɛ̃ ɛkpa Wuda Katɛ̃ funitsã a kanya.

english_verse: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
translation_verse: Foesũ Pilato nɛ́ ɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃ evia be kĩ, “Litsa fɔlɛtsa mibɔɛ nɛ oti kĩĩ akũ?”

english_verse: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ nnɛ́ tɔkpa ɔkɛnanɛ yanɛ ni, kufɛ bɔtákpã ye bɔma nɔ.”

english_verse: And so what Jesus said about his death would soon come true.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ ya lɔmɔ ayekĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yesu lɛ eyi nɛ wuda odu kĩ yaaku a akũ a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Pilato dani ewo nɛ abãã tɔtɔ kamɛ ade ɛvɛ Yesu evia ye kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ Yudafɔ lɛgã a mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɔ mɔawɛ avia yĩ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ, mbɔɛɛ kĩ bati bɛbã libi nɔ yĩ akũ fɔlɛtsa?”

english_verse: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
translation_verse: Pilato kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Akɔna kĩ Yudani manɛ? Nɔ mɔawɛ kade bati mɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a lɛkpã nɔ bɛmɛ yĩ. Litsa ɔkɛna?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yĩ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ táto katinya kĩĩ kamɛ. Ntɛ yĩ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ kito katinya kĩĩ kamɛ ni, kufɛ yĩ basumunɛ a pili yĩ lɔlɔ, mɔ baakɔa yĩ bido nɛ Yudafɔ awɛ kamɛ. Lɛmɛ yĩ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a táto katinya kĩĩ kamɛ.”

english_verse: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
translation_verse: Pilato via ye kĩ, “Foesũ lɛgã anɛ kɔ̃?” Yesu kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɔ lɛlɛ kĩ lɛgã manɛ. Yĩ kɔ̃ ni, ade fukĩĩ sũ bɛma yĩ a, ade foesũ ĩya nɛ katinya kamɛ kĩ mayaalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a akũ. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ eto ɔnɔkɔali a kamɛ ni, yatse yĩ.”

english_verse: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ mi amaniɛ onukpɛ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ nfɔnɛ kɔla bawanɛ a kamɛ ɔmɔa nkpa mi nɛ Wuda Katɛ̃ Luwi kanya kamɛ. Miawɛ kĩ nfɔnɛ Yudafɔ lɛgã a awɛ nkpa mi mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: They shouted, “No, not him! We want Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
translation_verse: Ade bɛbɔa fɔwɔla bɛlɛ kĩ, “Oowo, nnɛ́ ye bɔawɛ! Baraba bɔawɛ!” Baraba nyɛ oti kĩ eyidza ɛdza nɛ abãã akũ.

english_verse: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Ade Pilato fɔ̃ bɛkpã Yesu bɛdzakũmɛna bɛnabo ye mɔ mple.

english_verse: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ kɔa fɔtsɔlɛwɛ bɛnɔ lɛgãbɔɛ bɛtsa ye. Kĩ bido ye atadiɛ ngbã biyua a,

english_verse: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
translation_verse: bifuã ye bibi ye kĩ, “Yiiduu! Yudafɔ Lɛgã o-o!” Ade bɛbalɛbalɛ ye awɛ nɛ tɔlɔtɔ a.

english_verse: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Pilato buki ɛnɛ́ ɛnaa kanya ɛnabi bati kɔdabu a kĩ, “Midã, maakpã ye nnɛmɛna mma mi mimɔ́ kĩ ntámɔ́ mfumsu adima nɛ ye akũ kĩ makɔ ye kɔtɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
translation_verse: Adeke Yesu nɛ́ ɛya nɛ kanya, fɔtsɔlɛwɛ lɔbɔɛ a tsanɛ nɛ ye lɔlɔ, edeĩ nɛ atadiɛ ngbã a kamɛ. Pilato bi bati a kĩ, “Midã! Amɔ oti a nɛ awĩ!”

english_verse: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
translation_verse: Kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Asɔli Kɔla akũ badzɔlɛnɛ a mɔ́ ye a, bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kĩ, “Bɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ! Bɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ!” Ade Pilato bi be kĩ “Mi mɔawɛ mikpã ye minaabɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ. Yĩ kɔ̃ ntámɔ kĩ ɛkɛna lɛtsa kpa lidima.”

english_verse: The crowd replied, “He claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.”
translation_verse: Yudafɔ a kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe kɔ̃ budeĩ mbla, ade nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ eku, kitonɛkĩ ɛkɛna ye akũ Baguma Obi.”

english_verse: When Pilate heard this, he was terrified.
translation_verse: Kĩ Pilato nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ebuki evila ɛba kɔkɔɛ a.

english_verse: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Foesũ emuniki ɛnaa abãã tɔtɔ a kamɛ ade evia Yesu kĩ, “Ndia oto?” Lɛmɛ Yesu tákpa ye mbuayɛ adima.

english_verse: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
translation_verse: Ade Pilato bi ye kĩ, “Hei! Aákpa yĩ kalɛ a mbuayɛ? Mbɔɛɛ onyína kĩ ndeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ mafɔnɛ nɔ awɛ, ĩye kĩ mafɔ̃ bɛbɔa nɔ nɛ asendua akũ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ bɛtakpa nɔ kɔbɛ̃ bitomɛna ode ni, kufɛ ɔláana kɔbɛ̃ nɛ yĩ akũ. Foesũ oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ yĩ ɛkpa nɔ a tɔkpa bɛ̃ nɔ tide.”

english_verse: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Pilato nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ɛwɛ kĩ yatsã osi nyɛ osi akũ ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ, lɛmɛ Yudafɔ a bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Ntɛ ɔfɔnɛ ye awɛ ni, fɔatsa kĩ ɔnyɛ́ Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ ɔkɔba! Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛkɛna ye akũ Lɛgã ni, eyidza ɛdza nɛ Roma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ akũ!”

english_verse: When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as “The Stone Pavement.” In Aramaic this pavement is called “Gabbatha.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Pilato nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, ɛkpã Yesu ɛnamɛna kanya ade ye mɔawɛ ɛnadzi nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛ lukpo kamɛ nɛ kakɔ a kĩ bavɛ kĩ Boe Ɔda a. Bavɛ kakɔ a nɛ Hebri tide kamɛ kĩ Gabata.

english_verse: It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, “Look at your king!”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a nyɛ Wuda Katɛ̃ kukũmanɛ luwi, ade fɔ́ana ndɛ suwa tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã a. Ade Pilato bi Yudafɔ a kĩ, “Amɔ mi Lɛgã a nɛ awĩ!”

english_verse: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be nailed to a cross. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Adeke Pilato fɔnɛ Yesu awɛ ɛkpa sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛnaabɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ a. Bɛbɔa Yesu Nɛ Asendua Akũ. Foesũ bɛkpã Yesu bɛdzakũmɛna.

english_verse: The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there.
translation_verse: Foesũ sodzafɔ a yaveli oti ɔtɔnyade mɔ oti bɛbã a kĩ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ bibumɔ Yesu a abɔa.

english_verse: So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, “Why did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‘He claimed to be King of the Jews.’ ”
translation_verse: Ade basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a sĩ fɔlɛtsa a, ade bibi Pilato kĩ, “Tatsɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Yudafɔ Lɛgã,’ mbom ‘Oti kĩĩ lɛ kĩ, “Yĩ nyɛ Yudafɔ Lɛgã a.” ’ ”

english_verse: When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, “This man is now your son.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔ́ ɔma mɔ ye fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ a kĩ yaátunɔ kalɛ a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã a, ebi ɔma kĩ, “Mma, dã ade nɔ obi nɛ awĩ a!”

english_verse: and he carried his cross to a place known as “The Skull.” In Aramaic this place is called “Golgotha.”
translation_verse: Ye mɔawɛ ɛlɔ ye asendua ɛnawo awã kĩ bavɛ Oti Lɔlɔ Fɔtsɔ̃. Bavɛ awã nɛ Hebri tide kamɛ kĩ Golgata.

english_verse: There Jesus was nailed to the cross, and on each side of him a man was also nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ade awã naa, bɛbɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ a. Bɛbɔa bati nviã bɛbã nɛ asendua akũ bibumɔ ye, ɔmɔa nɛ ye sɛba, ɔyade nɛ ye sɔmɔna, ade Yesu deĩ nɛ be kayite a.

english_verse: Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Pilato tsɛlɛ ye onukpɛ̃ katsua fɔlɛtsa bɛbɔa nɛ asendua a lɔlɔ nyangɛ. Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛtsɛlɛ nyɛ, “Yesu Nazaretni, Yudafɔ Lɛgã a.”

english_verse: The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek. The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ pii kã fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ a, kitonɛkĩ awã kĩ bɛbɔa Yesu a fuãmɔ ɔmatɔ kamɛ. Pilato tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa a nɛ Hebri, Roma mɔ Hela lɛmɛ tide kamɛ.

english_verse: But Pilate told them, “What is written will not be changed!”
translation_verse: Pilato kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ ntsɛlɛ ni, ntsɛlɛ de kɔkɔɛ.”

english_verse: The soldiers said to each other, “Let's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.” This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say, “They divided up my clothes and gambled for my garments.” The soldiers then did what they had decided.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ a bi akũ kĩ, “Mitafɔ̃ bɔyɛlɛ atadiɛ a, mbom mifɔ̃ bɔta bavulibi bɔmɔ́ oti a kĩ yaatɛ ye.” Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ ya ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Bɛsɛ̃ yĩ tɛtadiɛ bɛkpa akũ. Ade bɛta bavulibi nɛ yĩ atadiɛ akũ a.” Ade lɛtsa a kĩ sodzafɔ a kɛna a.

english_verse: Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩiĩ a, Yesu ɔma, ɔma ɔwaɛ̃tsole, Maria kĩ ɛnyɛ Clopa ɔtsɔ̃, mɔ Maria Magdalani dzɛ̃ nɛ Yesu asendua a ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ biwo Yesu akũ a, bɛmɔ́ kĩ eku kɔkɔɛ, foesũ bɛtáveli ye abɔa.

english_verse: In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used.
translation_verse: Kɔbã kudi deĩ nɛ awã kĩ bɛbɔa Yesu nɛ asendua akũ a. Linikɛ vɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛtábila oti tɔ̃ deĩ nɛ kɔbã a kamɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, “I am thirsty!”
translation_verse: Finyaa Yesu nyi kĩ biyua lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kakɛna. Ayekĩ fɔ́afɔ̃ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛya nɛ foe kamɛ sũ ni, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɔmɔado akɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant.
translation_verse: Kugumi kudi keni nɛ awã kĩ kuyi mɔ waĩ yilawa. Ade bɛkɔa ɔsapɔ bɛta waĩ a bɛkɔa bido nɛ otse akũ bɛnatamɔ Yesu onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: kilograms of spices made from myrrh and aloes. This was the same Nicodemus who had visited Jesus one night.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu mɔa bavɛ a eyua a, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Fuwoli!” Ɛkɛna lɔlɔ ade ye kawɛya nɛ a. Bɛtsɔɛ Yesu Lɔtsɔɛ Nɛ Sɛklabi.

english_verse: The next day would be both a Sabbath and the Passover. It was a special day for the Jewish people, and they did not want the bodies to stay on the crosses during this day. So they asked Pilate to break the men's legs and take their bodies down.
translation_verse: Luwi a nyɛ Efieda, ade Kawɛya Luwi kĩ litomɔ de a nyɛ luwi kpalɛwa lɛkpa Yudafɔ, foesũ bɛtáwɛ kĩ banɔkpa a bɛkahɔ nɛ asendua akũ Kawɛya Luwi a liwo ɔtɔ. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a navia Pilato kĩ ɛkpa be osi bɛnaaveliveli bati a kĩ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ a abɔa, lɔkɔa bɛnyɛ be banɔkpa a.

english_verse: One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa kɔa lɔtsɔɛ ɛtsɔɛ ye sɛklabi, ade deedimɔapɛ babɔanɛ mɔ mui nɛ a.

english_verse: We know this is true, because it was told by someone who saw it happen. Now you can have faith too.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛmɔ́ fɔtsa kĩĩ lidi adansiɛ nɛ foe akũ ayekĩ nɔ malo aatɛ odo. Lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, ade enyina pefee kĩ ɔnɔkɔali ɛlɛ a.

english_verse: All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, “No bone of his body will be broken”
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ ya ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa fɔlɛtsa a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Bɛláave ye lɔtsɔ̃ lidima.”

english_verse: and “They will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.” Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa bɛbã nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ, “Bati aako anɛ bɛdã oti a kĩ bɛtsɔɛ ye lɔtsɔɛ a.” Yesu Kabila.

english_verse: The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ luwi a nyɛ Kukũmanɛ luwi lɛkpa Kawɛya Luwi a, ade kĩ awã lɛmɛ fuã be sũ ni, bɛkɔa Yesu ɔnɔkpa a bibila nɛ awã.

english_verse: Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Yosef kĩ eto Arimatia yavia Pilato kĩ ɛkpa ye osi ɛbɔɛ Yesu ɔnɔkpa a. Yosef kĩĩ nyɛ Yesu fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ odi nɛ kabɛla kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ ɛkavila Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a. Pilato kpa osi, ade Yosef yabɔɛ ɔnɔkpa a ɛdzakumɛna a.

english_verse: The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead.
translation_verse: Banyole nviã a kɔa tutsonu a bikputa Yesu ɔnɔkpa a, ade bɛkɔa aklala ovoe bisuli ye ndɛ ayekĩ Yudafɔ akɛna ɔnɔkpa fɔmɔ babila ye a.

english_verse: Thomas replied, “You are my Lord and my God!”
translation_verse: Toma bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ Ɔlate mɔ yĩ Baguma!”

english_verse: On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
translation_verse: Ɛkwansieda ɔnɔnɛ tutuutu a, kĩ fɔkɔ tákpalɛ tɔ̃ a, Maria Magdalani naa linikɛ a ɔwɛ̃, ade ɛmɔ́ kĩ bitinya de a nɛ linikɛ a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: So the two of them went back to the other disciples. Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene (Mark
translation_verse: Ke liti a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a dzakũ bɛnaa be tɔtɔ kamɛ. Yesu Nyɛ Ye Akũ Ɛtsa Maria Magdalani

english_verse: The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ bɛbã a kĩ ɛnya lɔtɔ ewo linikɛ a ɔwɛ̃ a, lɛmɛ wo nɛ de kamɛ. Ɛmɔ́ aklala mɔ lɔlɔ kasui lima a, ade ɛtɛ edo a.

english_verse: Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu ya a, Toma kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ ewua-nviã a kamɛ ɔmɔa, kĩ bibuki bavɛ Ata a, tána nɛ ye bade a kamɛ.

english_verse: and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot.
translation_verse: ade ɛmɔ́ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ nviã kĩ bideĩ nɛ tɛtadiɛ fitaa kamɛ, kĩ bidzi nɛ awã kĩ bɛwɔ́la Yesu a. Ɔmɔa dzi nɛ ye lɔlɔ nyangɛ, ɔbɛbã dzi nɛ ye abɔa kayɔ.

english_verse: She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Foesũ ewula atsɛ ɛnaa Simon Petro mɔ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ a kĩ Yesu látunɔ kalɛ a sɛkɛ̃, ebi be kĩ, “Bɛyabɔɛ boe Ɔlate nɛ linikɛ a kamɛ bɛdzakũmɛna, ade bunyína awã kĩ bɛkɔa ye biyi a.”

english_verse: Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
translation_verse: Ade Petro mɔ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ kĩ a yidza bɛnaa linikɛ a ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first.
translation_verse: Be nviã a flee bɛkawula atsɛ, lɛmɛ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ kĩ a wula atsɛ ɛtɛ̃ Petro ɛnya lɔtɔ ewo linikɛ a ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛna akũ ɛdã de kamɛ ɛmɔ́ aklala a kĩ bɛkɔa bisuli ɔnɔkpa a, lɛmɛ ɛtáwo nɛ linikɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth.
translation_verse: Simon Petro kĩ etomɔ ye nɛ liti a, ya ade ye kɔ̃ ewo nɛ linikɛ a kamɛ a. Ɛmɔ́ aklala ovoe a kĩ ɛwaa nɛ awã.

english_verse: He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ́ lima a kĩ bɛkɔa bisui Yesu lɔlɔ a lɛmɛ nɛ awã, lɛmɛ lɛwáa nɛ aklala a ɔwɛ̃, mbom bibuta de biyi de lite.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Maria dza nɛ linikɛ a ɔwɛ̃ ɛkabe. Kĩ ɛkabe a, ɛkɛna akũ ɛdã linikɛ a kamɛ,

english_verse: The angels asked Mary, “Why are you crying?” She answered, “They have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Bivia Maria kĩ, “Boe ɔma, litsa sũ abe?” Maria kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Bɛbɔɛ yĩ Ɔlate bɛnamɛna, ade ĩnyína awã kĩ bɛkɔa ye bɛnamɛna a.”

english_verse: Jesus asked her, “Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener and said, “Sir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.”
translation_verse: Yesu via ye kĩ, “Boe ɔma, nde sũ abe? Owei awɛ?” Lɛmɛ Maria kɔna kĩ oti a kĩ yadãmɛna awã a yanɛ, foesũ ebi ye kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, ntɛ nɔ lɛbɔɛ ye ni, bi yĩ awã kĩ oyi ye lɔkɔa nnaabɔɛ ye.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Don't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.”
translation_verse: Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Tata yĩ, kitonɛkĩ ntámuniki nnaa ode nɛ Nda sɛkɛ̃ tɔ̃. Lɛmɛ naa nwaɛ̃ amɛ sɛkɛ̃ kabi be kĩ mmuniki manaa oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ Nda mɔ mi Ɔlɛga, yĩ Baguma mɔ mi Baguma a sɛkɛ̃.”

english_verse: and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ eyua a, ɛkɔa ye awɛ mɔ sɛklabi ɛtsa be. Adeke kawɔlɛ de fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ bɛmɔ́ Ɔlate a.

english_verse: The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them
translation_verse: Nɛ Ɛkwansieda a na olobe a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kpɔta nɛ kɔla kamɛ, ade bɛbalɛ onyukpɛ̃ tupunu a flee, kitonɛkĩ bɛkavila Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a. Lɛtalɛta Yesu yanɛ ɛdza nɛ be kayite, ade ebi be kĩ, “Lukudɔ litomɔ mi.”

english_verse: After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, “I am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.”
translation_verse: Yesu buki ebi be kĩ, “Lukudɔ litomɔ mi! Ndɛ ayekĩ Nda do yĩ a, ade aye mado mi a.”

english_verse: Then he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Adeke ɛwɛya eko nɛ be akũ, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mitɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a.

english_verse: If you forgive anyone's sins, they will be forgiven. But if you don't forgive their sins, they will not be forgiven.” Jesus and Thomas
translation_verse: Ntɛ mikɔa bati tɔkpa mite be ni, bɛkɔa bite be, lɛmɛ ntɛ mitákɔa be tɔkpa mite be a ni, bɛtákɔa te bite be.” Yesu Nyɛ Ye Akũ Ɛtsa Toma.

english_verse: So they told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said, “First, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!”
translation_verse: Foesũ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ bɛbã a bi ye kĩ, “Bɔmɔ́ boe Ɔlate a!” Ade Toma bi be kĩ, “Ntɛ ntámɔ ye awɛ kamɛ tiplego fɔɔkpa a, nkɔa lawobi nte nɛ fɔɔkpa a akũ ndo awɛ nɛ ye sɛklabi ni, nláatɛ ndo da.”

english_verse: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi ɛlɛ̃ liti a, fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a buki bɛkpɔta nɛ kɔla a kamɛ, ade Toma lɛmɛ na nɛ be kamɛ a. Bɛbalɛ tupunu a flee, lɛmɛ Yesu yadza nɛ be kayite ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Lukudɔ litomɔ mi!”

english_verse: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
translation_verse: Adeke ebi Toma kĩ, “Kɔa nɔ lawobi kate nɛ awĩ, kadã yĩ awɛ kamɛ. Yo kɔkpɔ kado awɛ nɛ yĩ sɛklabi kamɛ. Fɔ̃ atsindze katɛ, lɔkɔa katɛ kado!”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Ayekĩ ɔmɔ́ yĩ sũ ɔtɛ odo. Baguma hila bikĩ bɛtámɔ yĩ, lɛmɛ bɛtɛ bido a!” Lɛtsa a Kĩ Lɛnyɛ Ovoli Kĩĩ Botaɛ.

english_verse: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, Yesu kɛna awãwã fɔtsa bɛbã pii nɛ ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a anɛ, kĩ bɛtátsɛlɛ bido nɛ ovoli kĩĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛtsɛlɛ fukĩĩ biyi ayekĩ míatɛ mido kĩ Yesu nyɛ Kristo, Baguma Obi, ade mitɛ ye mido ni, míatsã ye akũ mina nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a.

english_verse: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
translation_verse: Luwi a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ badi kpɔta. Ade be nyɛ Simon Petro, Toma kĩ bavɛ Ata obi, mɔ Nataniel kĩ eto Kana nɛ Galilea tite akũ, mɔ Zebedeo babi mɔ Yesu fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ bɛbã nviã.

english_verse: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
translation_verse: Ade Simon Petro bi be kĩ, “Manaata asau.” Bibi ye kĩ, “Bɔ́atomɔ nɔ bɔnaa.” Foesũ bɛnawo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ bɛnaa, lɛmɛ lɛnyɛ a flee bɛtányɛ lɛtsama.

english_verse: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade aya kanyina kɔ̃ a, Yesu ya ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ, lɛmɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a linyína kĩ Yesu yanɛ.

english_verse: Jesus shouted, “Friends, have you caught anything?” “No!” they answered.
translation_verse: Ade evia be kĩ, “Bapopo, mbɔɛɛ mitányɛ kɔfɔabi kudima?” Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oowo!”

english_verse: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
translation_verse: Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mita asau nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a sɔmɔna sɛmakɛ lɔkɔa míana kɔfɔabi a kudi.” Ade bɛta asau a bɛnyɛ kɔfɔabi beblebee kĩ bɛtápuli bɛvɛlɛ bɛnɛmɛna.

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ a kĩ Yesu látunɔ kalɛ a bi Petro kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate a yanɛ!” Kĩ Petro nu kĩ boe Ɔlate yanɛ a, ɛbɔɛ ye atadiɛ anyanglede efili nɛ kate, kitonɛkĩ edzi ɔdzɔ, ade ɛvɛnya ewo nɛ mui a kamɛ a.

english_verse: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
translation_verse: Kĩ biduma nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ a, bɛmɔ́ kĩ bɛtɛna futuã fi kĩ kɔfɔabi teĩ nɛ foe akũ, ade abodoo adi lɛmɛ waa nɛ awã a.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mikɔa kɔfɔabi a kĩ minyɛ finyaa kudi miyamɛna.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Miyaanya.” Lɛmɛ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kamɛ odima tádo ɔwɔlɛ evia ye kĩ, “Owei anɛ?” kitonɛkĩ binyina kĩ Boe Ɔlate a yanɛ.

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu nabɔɛ abodoo a ɛkɔa ɛkpa be, ade ayea ɛbɔɛ kɔfɔabi a lɛmɛ ɛkpa be a.

english_verse: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ nyɛ lɛlade kĩ Yesu nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ, kĩ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna kanɔkpa a. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Mɔ Petro.

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛnya biyua a, Yesu via Simon Petro kĩ, “Simon, Yona obi, awɛ yĩ kalɛ kaba ayekĩ bati bɛbã kĩĩ a awɛ yĩ kalɛ a?” Ɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, Boe Ɔlate! Onyina kĩ mawɛ nɔ kalɛ.” Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Dãmɛna yĩ bavelibi akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
translation_verse: Yesu buki evia ye lɛyade kĩ, “Simon, Yona obi, awɛ yĩ kalɛ?” Petro kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oo, Boe Ɔlate! Nɔ mɔawɛ onyina kĩ mawɛ nɔ kalɛ.” Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Dãmɛna yĩ baveli a akũ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
translation_verse: Yesu buki evia Petro lɛlalɛde kĩ, “Simon, Yona obi, awɛ yĩ kalɛ?” Petro wo ɔblɔ kitonɛkĩ Yesu via ye lɛlade kĩ, “Awɛ yĩ kalɛ?” Foesũ ebi Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nɔ mɔawɛ onyina lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa. Nɔ mɔawɛ onyina kĩ mawɛ nɔ kalɛ!” Ade Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Dãmɛna yĩ baveli a akũ.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
translation_verse: Mabi nɔ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ kĩ lɔkɔ a kĩ ɔnyɛ opopo a, apuli kafili lima, kanaa kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ awɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔdɛ a ni, aadzɛla akpɔ, oti bɛbã lifili nɔ lima, ɛkpã nɔ ɛnamɛna awã kĩ aáwɛ kĩ anaa.”

english_verse: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
translation_verse: Yesu lɛ fukĩĩ ɛkɔa ɛtsa wuda odu a kĩ Petro aaku, ɛkɔa ɛnyɛ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a ɛtsa. Ke liti a, Yesu bi ye kĩ, “Tomɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”
translation_verse: Petro dani ade ɛmɔ́ fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ a kĩ Yesu látunɔ kalɛ a kĩ etomɔ be nɛ liti yaya. Ade fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ kĩĩ lidzi ɛmɛnamɔ Yesu kĩ bidzi bɛkanya, ade evia ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, owei aanyɛ nɔ ɛkpa a?”

english_verse: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro mɔ́ ye a, evia Yesu kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, litsa aaya nɛ oti kĩĩ akũ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ mawɛ kĩ edzi nkpã kɛnaawo kĩ maaya a ni, fɔwɔdabe tsɛla nɔ akũ? Nɔ kɔ̃, tomɔ yĩ!”

english_verse: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ Yesu batomɛnanɛ a kamɛ kĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ kĩĩ lɛláaku. Lɛmɛ Yesu tálɛ kĩ ɛláaku, mbom kalɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ mawɛ kĩ edzi nkpã kɛnaawo kĩ maaya a ni, fɔɔdabe tsɛla nɔ akũ?”

english_verse: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa ɔkɔlɛnɛ kĩĩ adi adansiɛ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ akũ a, ade ye lɛmɛ lɛtsɛlɛ foe eyi a. Foesũ bunyina kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Fɔlɛtsa Fuwũnade.

english_verse: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa bɛbã pii deĩ kĩ Yesu kɛna. Ntɛ katsɛlɛ bɛtsɛlɛ foe flee biyi fɔmɔa fɔmɔa ni, nkɔna kĩ katinya kĩĩ flee kamɛ malo lɛláana kakokɔ̃ kɛkpa tuvoli a kĩ baatsɛlɛ a.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛkpɔta a, esi be mbla kĩ, “Mitadzakũ nɛ Yerusalem, mbom midzi midzɔlɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kĩ mbi mi kĩ yĩ Ɔlɛga lɛ eyi kĩ yaakpa mi a kaya.

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: Yohanes kɔ̃ ni, mui ɛkɔa ɛwɔla bati. Lɛmɛ nɛ fuwi lukũ kamɛ ni, Baguma aakɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ɛwɔla mi.” Baguma Bɔɛ Yesu Ɛnamɛna Ode.

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Yesu mɔ ye fɔtɔ banyanɛ a buki bɛkpɔta a, bivia ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, mbɔɛɛ finyaakĩĩ ɔwɛ kĩ ayaamunikimɔ Israelfɔ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a ɔkpa be?”

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Fɔtásĩ kĩ mianyi lɔkɔ ĩye fuwi a kĩ yĩ ɔlɛga lɛ eyi ɛkpa kĩ litsedi lɛya a nɛ ye kɔbɛ̃ akũ a.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a yawo nɛ mi kamɛ ni, yaado mi kɔbɛ̃ lɔkɔa milɛ yĩ fɔlɛtsa a nɛ Yerusalem, mɔ Yudea flee mɔ Samaria, kɛnaawo katinya kamɛ tite kawũnakɔ̃.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ eyua a nɛ be anɛ kamɛ naa, Baguma bɔɛ ye ɛnamɛna ode, ade ɛyɛla nɛ luflututu kamɛ a.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛdza bɛkadã koee kĩ yanaa odetɔ́ kamɛ a, lɛtalɛta banyole nviã badi kĩ bideĩ nɛ fɔtsa ovoe kamɛ nyɛ be akũ bɛtsa be,

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: ade bibi be kĩ, “Galileafɔ, nde sũ midzɛ̃ miadã odetɔ́ kamɛ lɔmɔ? Yesu a kĩ Baguma bɔɛ nɛ mi ɔwɛ̃ nɛ awĩ ɛnamɛna ode a, aabuki ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ mimɔ́ kĩ yanaa ode a.” Matias Yatɛ Yuda Kadzakɔ̃.

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, bɛnaa be kadzikɔ̃ nɛ abansulu kɔla ode. Bati a kĩ bɛna nɛ awã nyɛ Petro, Yohanes, Yakobo, Andrea, Filipo, Toma, Bartolomeo, Mateo, Yakobo kĩ ɛnyɛ Alfeo obi, mɔ Simon kĩ epili ye kade lɔlɔ, mɔ Yuda kĩ ɛnyɛ Yakobo obi.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ flee katsɛna lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ mɔ kakɔna mɔa, kabɔa mpaɛ kakpa Baguma. Bati a badi lɛmɛ nyɛ batsole mɔ Yesu ɔma Maria, mɔ Yesu bawaɛ̃nyole.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɛkɛna tɔkpa kĩ ɛnatɛ sika kĩ se bɛkɔa bɛya tite a kĩ te akũ eku a. Ade awã na kĩ eto ode ɛtɔ a, ye lupu tui lɛbɛ̃, ade ke ye fɔmɛtsa flee nɛ fuko nɛ tite a.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Ye wuda a akũ fɔlɛtsa gba nɛ Yerusalem kĩ odi nyɛ odi nu foe. Foesũ awã kĩ ɛnaku ɛwa a bavɛ ke kĩ Akeldamak, foe kayɔ nyɛ Babɔanɛ Tite.

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Foesũ Petro buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Dawid Tɔnɔ Ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, ‘Mifɔ̃ ye tɔtɔ tɛwa luto, odima ɛtabuki edzi nɛ te kamɛ.’ Bɛtsɛlɛ lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Mifɔ̃ oti bɛbã ɛtɛ ye kadzakɔ̃ a.’

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: Foesũ fusĩ kĩ oti bɛbã ɛyaawo nɛ boe kamɛ ndɛ ɔdansini ɛkpa boe Ɔlate Yesu kayidza kato kanɔkpa a. Ade fusĩ lɛmɛ kĩ ɛnya oti kĩ ɛnya boe bati a kĩ bɔtsã butomɛna boe Ɔlate Yesu a kamɛ ɔmɔa, kito lɔkɔ a kĩ Yohanes de lɛtsã nɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ akũ kɛyawo lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma bɔɛ Yesu nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃ ɛnamɛna ode a.”

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛnyɛ bati nviã ade be nyɛ Matias mɔ Yosef kĩ bavɛ Barsaba kĩ babuki kavɛ Yusto a.

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛta bavulibi nɛ bati nviã kĩĩ fɔlɔ a, fɔyake nɛ Matias akũ, ade bɛkɔa ye bibumɛna fɔtɔ banyanɛ ewua-odi a akũ a.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Kĩ Pentekoste luwi a wo ɔtɔ a, batɛ badonɛ a flee natsɛna nɛ kakɔ mɔapɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ, binu litsedi nkã ndɛ ayekĩ ɔwɛ kpale avũ a nɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ, ade ɔwɛ kĩĩ tɛ tɔtɔ a flee kĩ te kamɛ bɛna a kamɛ a.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa tɛ be kamɛ flee, ade bido kɔdzɛla kalɛ awɛ nɛ tide futefute kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ Hũhũ a kpa be a.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ade nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Yudafɔ kĩ bavila Baguma kĩ bito fude kumɛkumɛ nɛ katinya kamɛ flee a ya bideĩ nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ binu ɔwɛ baũ kĩĩ nkã a, bati pii bɔa obũ bɛnakpɔta nɛ awã. Kĩ binu kĩ batɛ badonɛ kĩĩ kalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi tide kamɛ a, fɔkɛna be wãwã.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ libe wo be, bɛkɛna saa, ade bido akũ kavia awɛ kĩ, “Midã, mbɔɛɛ nnɛ́ Galileafɔ bati kĩĩ flee anɛ?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Mɔ nde fɔya mɔ bɔanu kĩ bɛlamɛna kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ boe tide kamɛ?

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: kĩ bɔnyɛ Yudafɔ ĩye bati kĩ bɛdani akũ bɛkɛna Yudafɔ, boe badi bɔnyɛ Kretafɔ mɔ Arabiafɔ. Ade boe flee bɔanu kĩ bɛlamɛna Baguma awãwã fɔtsa kalɛ nɛ boe tide kamɛ a.”

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ fɔkɛna be saa mɔ basaa lɔmɔ a, bɛkavia be akũ kĩ, “Litsa fɔlɛtsa nɛ awĩ a?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bikĩ bɛdza nɛ awã badi di be nsekuo bɛlɛ kĩ, “Bavɛ lamɛna be kawugɛla.” Petro Bi Yudafɔ Yesu Akũ Fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Ade awã mɔ Petro yidza ɛdza mɔ be ewua-odi a, ɛdzɛla odo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mi Yudafɔ mɔ mi a flee kĩ midzi nɛ Yerusalem, mawɛ kĩ manyɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩ flee kayɔ ntsa mi, foesũ mitse katse wĩ.

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ bavɛ bati kĩĩ wuga ndɛ ayekĩ mikɔna a. Midã kĩ ade owi fɔmɔ ɔnɔnɛ tɔdɔũ evedi bɔa a.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Mbom lɛtsa a kĩ de milamɛna kamɔ nɛ awĩ a nyɛ fukĩ Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yoel lɛ eyi anɛ kɔkɔɛ a kĩ,

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: ‘Baguma lɛ kĩ, “Nɛ fuwi nkaɛ a kamɛ ni, maado yĩ Hũhũ ɛyaawo nɛ bati flee kamɛ, lɔkɔa mi babinyole mɔ babitsole aade lɛtsã nɛ yĩ kalɛ a akũ. Mi bapopo aamɔ ode fɔtsa kɛnawa nɛ hũhũ kamɛ, ade mi banɔkɔɛ̃ lɛmɛ aawa fɔda a.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali, nɛ fuwi a na kamɛ ni, yĩ Hũhũ aatɛ mi basumunɛ batsole mɔ banyole flee kamɛ, lɔkɔa baadɛ lɛtsã nɛ yĩ fɔlɛtsa akũ.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: Ade maafɔ̃ awãwã fɔtsa lɛnɛ nɛ odetɔ kamɛ mɔ tite akũ. Míamɔ babɔanɛ, mɔ fi mɔ ɔnyɔ kusuu kĩ fɔgba nɛ tite akũ.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Suwa aake ɔtũ, odetɔ anɛ aadani ngbã ndɛ babɔanɛ aye, ade owi fɔmɔ Baguma Luwi kpale mɔ kukũkpalɛ a aaya a.

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: Ade Petro buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Israelfɔ, mitse fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ. Yesu Nazaretni nyɛ Oti a kĩ Baguma pau, kĩ ye deĩ Baguma kɔbɛ̃. Ɛnyɛ kɔbɛ̃ kĩĩ ɛtsa mi nɛ ye awãwã mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa kɛnawa kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ mi mɔawɛ minyina lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ Baguma kawɛ mɔ nhihiɛ onukpɛ̃ ni ɛfɔnɛ ye awɛ ɛkpa mi, ade mitsã tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ akũ mikɔ ye, nɛ kĩ mibɔa ye nɛ asendua akũ a.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma buki ɛdzɛla ye etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ ade ɛnɛ́ nɛ wuda awɛ kamɛ a, kitonɛkĩ wuda tápuli ɛfɔ̃ ɛkahɔ nɛ kanɔkpa da.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: Ade lɛgã Dawid lɛ eyi nɛ ye akũ kĩ, ‘Mamɔ kĩ yĩ Ɔlate dzɛ̃ nɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ Nɛ kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna ndɛ yĩ obumɛnanɛ sũ maávila.

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ kawɔlɛ de yĩ ade fɔfɔ̃ mamu ye luwi daa. Titɔ kĩ sukɛna pupulidza manɛ malo ni, maadzi nɛ kɔnɛte kamɛ.

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ɔláafɔ̃nɛ yĩ ɔkla oyi nɛ kanɔkpade, ade ɔláafɔ̃ nɔ Obi Kpalɛwa sukɛna lɛvɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Nɔ lɛtsa yĩ nkpã osi a, ade nɔ sɛkɛ̃ kana aafɔ̃ mmɔ́ kawɔlɛ paa.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: “Foesũ, nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mabi mi yededeede kĩ boe ɔwanyɔ Lɛgã Dawid ku, bibila ye, ade ye kuni waa nɛ boe kamɛ kɛyawo nviã kĩ a.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ ɛnya Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ sũ ni, enyi lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaakɛna ɛkpa ye. Baguma lɔ fubo kĩ yaakɛna Dawid kasinu a kamɛ obi mɔa odi ɛyaadzi ɛnya lɛgã ndɛ Dawid aye.

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ Dawid nyi fukĩĩ ɛnyamɛna lɔtɔ sũ ni, ɛlɛ eyi kĩ Kristo aayidza eto banɔkpa kamɛ. Ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Baguma táfɔnɛ ye awɛ ɛkahɔ nɛ kanɔkpa, ade ye sukɛna lɛmɛ távɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ a.’

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kĩĩ Baguma dzɛla etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ a, ade boe a flee bɔnyɛ foe akũ adansifɔ a.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ni, Ɔlɛga Baguma kpa ye lɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kadzikɔ̃ nɛ ye sɔmɔna, ade ɛkɔa ye Hũhũ ɛkpa ye ndɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ eyi a. Foesũ ye malo ɛkɔa ye Hũhũ a, eko nɛ boe akũ. Ade foesũ mimɔ́, minu lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛlamɛna kaya nɛ awĩ a.

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Foesũ mawɛ kĩ mi Israelfɔ flee minyi kĩ Yesu a kĩ mibɔa nɛ asendua akũ a, ade ye Baguma kɛna Ɔlate mɔ Kristo Oti a Kĩ Bɛbɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Kĩ bati kĩĩ nu Petro fɔlɛtsa a, fɔha be paa. Foesũ bivia ye mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛbã a kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ade Petro bi be kĩ, “Fusĩ kĩ mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi ɛdani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ɛtɛ Baguma edo, lɔkɔa ɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo dĩ akũ ayekĩ Baguma aakɔa ye titikpa ete ye, ɛtɛ Baguma futetsa a kĩ fɔnyɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩĩ a waa fɔkpa mi, mɔ mi babi mɔ bikĩ bito mɔ lugo, mɔ bikĩ boe Ɔlate Baguma aavɛ kĩ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ a.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Ade Petro dɛ be pii ɛlɛ kĩ, “Minyɛ mi akũ mitomɛna sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a kĩ saaya nɛ nviã katinya bati kpa kĩĩ akũ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Bɛfɔnɛ be akũ awɛ bɛkpa fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bɛkatsa be fɔtsa, ade bɛkabɔa mpaɛ mɔ kanya kakpɔta mɔ ɔmɔa kakɛna a. Batɛ Badonɛ a Dzi Mɔ Onukpɛ̃ Mɔa.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kakɛna awãwã fɔtsa mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni pii sũ ni, fɔkɛna bati pii wãwã.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ kĩĩ flee katsɛna luwi daa kakɛna lɛtsa mɔa, ade bɛkasɛ̃ be fɔtsa kakpa akũ a.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɔsa bɛnɔ be akũ fɔtsa fudi ni, basɛ̃ sika a kakpa akũ nɛ be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kasĩ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Bɛkanaa Baguma afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a luwi daa, ade bɛkatsɛna nɛ kutsu kutsu kamɛ nɛ be tɔtɔ kamɛ kanya kakpɔta nɛ kawɔlɛ mɔ akũ kadɔla kamɛ a.

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: Ade bɛkanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ, kamu ye lɛmɛ a. Bati flee kɔa obu bɛkpa be. Luwi daa boe Ɔlate akɔa bikĩ bɛtɛ bido a kĩ ɛtɛ a kayaabumɔ be.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Luwi a Petro mɔ Yohanes naa Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Yudafɔ abɔa mpaɛ a kamɛ. Fɔ́ana ndɛ olobe tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Asɔli Kɔla onyukpɛ̃ a kĩ bavɛ Onyukpɛ̃ Mɔ Kalɛ a, bɛmɔ́ onyole odi kĩ eveliveli kito ye lɛmawi, kĩ ɛwaa nɛ awã. Ye bati abɔɛ ye kanaayi nɛ awã luwi daa ayekĩ yaakawã sika katomɛna bikĩ batɛ̃ nɛ awã a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro mɔ Yohanes atɛ̃ a, ɛwã be sika.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Bɛfɔ̃ bɛdza bɛdã ye koee ade Petro bi ye kĩ, “Dã boe anɛ kamɛ!”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Ade oti a dzɛla anɛ ɛdã be mɔ kakɔna kĩ baakpa ye litsedi.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Petro bi ye kĩ, “Nla sika sidima kĩ makpa nɔ, lɛmɛ maakpa nɔ lɛtsa a kĩ ndeĩ a! Nɛ Yesu Kristo Nazaretni dĩ akũ ni, mabi nɔ kĩ yidza kadza, katsã!”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Ade Petro pi oti a nɛ sɔmɔna kɔkpɔ ɛdzɛla ye a. Deedimɔapɛ ye abɔa mɔ anya flee de ɔsĩ,

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: ade ɛvɛnya ɛdza nɛ abɔa akũ, edo katokatɛ̃ awɛ a. Etomɔ be biwo nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ ade ɛkavɛnya kamu Baguma lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛmɔ́ biyi ɔsũ kĩ ye lɛkadzi nɛ Onyukpɛ̃ Mɔ Kalɛ ɔwɛ̃ kawã fɔtsa a, fɔnyɛnɛ be flee akũ ade bɛkɛna saa nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ ye akũ a. Petro Lɛ Yesu Kɔbɛ̃ Akũ Fɔlɛtsa Nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a Kamɛ.

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Bati pii wula atsɛ bɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awã kĩ bavɛ Lɛgã Salomo Mblada nɛ Asɔli kɔla kamɛ a. Bɛyamɔ oti a kĩ ɛmanɛmɔ Petro mɔ Yohanes tsɛmɛtsɛmɛ ade ye kasaɛ a kɛna be saa a.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro mɔ́ bati kĩĩ a ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Israelfɔ, litsa koũ liti lakɛna mi wãwã nɛ fɔtsa kĩĩ akũ? Nde sũ miadã boe bãã lɔmɔ ndɛ bati kpalɛwa bɔanɛ? Mitakɔna kĩ boe awɛ kɔbɛ̃ bɔkɔa bɔtsa oti kĩĩ!

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ɛnyɛ Abraham, Isak mɔ Yakob mɔ boe bawanyɔ Baguma a do ye Ɔsumunɛ Yesu obu. Ade ye obi Yesu mikɔa mikpa bɛkɔ a. Misĩ ye nɛ Pilato anɛ kamɛ, titɔ kĩ minyina ye nɛ kĩ Pilato wɛ kĩ yafɔnɛ ye awɛ kufɛ.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Misĩ oti kpĩĩ mɔ oti kpalɛwa a, ade mitunɔ kĩ ɛfɔnɛ owudini awɛ ɛkpa mi nɛ ye kadokɔ̃ a.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bɛkɔ oti a kĩ yakpa nkpã a, lɛmɛ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ. Boe awɛ a bɔmɔ́ ye mɔ anɛ.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Yesu dĩ kɔbɛ̃ akũ futo mɔ oti kĩ miamɔ kĩĩ, minyina lɛmɛ a na akũ ɔsĩ. Nɛ kĩ ɛtɛ Yesu edo sũ ni, boe Ɔlate tsa ye nɛ mi anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, finyaakĩĩ ĩnyina kĩ mi mɔ mi banɔkɔɛ̃ mikɛna fukĩĩ nɛ Yesu akũ nɛ katanyi kamɛ.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma lɛ eyi anɛ kɔkɔɛ ɛtsãmɛna ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ flee akũ kĩ ye Kristo a aatsã lubo kamɔ kamɛ. Ade foesũ ɛfɔ̃ fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ lɔmɔ a.

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Foesũ midani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ mimuniki miyaatómɔ Baguma ayekĩ yaakɔa mi tɔkpa ete mi. Ntɛ mikɛna lɔmɔ ni, fɔ́afɔ̃ mina hũhũ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ mitomɛna boe Ɔlate sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ yaado Yesu a kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo a nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ Yesu ɛna nɛ ode kɛnawo lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma lɔla lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa nɛ de osi akũ ndɛ ayekĩ ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ kpalɛwa a kĩ bidzi nkpã kɔkɔɛ a lɛ biyi a.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Mose lɛ kĩ, ‘Mi Ɔlate Baguma aanyɛ onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ odi ndɛ yĩ aye etomɛna mi kamɛ. Foesũ mitse lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ yaabi mi.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛláatse ye kalɛ ni, baanyɛ ye nɛ Baguma bati a kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛwɔla ye mɔ odu mɔ odu.’

english_verse: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
translation_verse: “Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ flee a, kito Samuel akũ kɛyawo bikĩ bitomɔ ye a, lɛ biyi bɛnyamɛna lɔtɔ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔlamɛna kaya nɛ nviã fuwi kĩĩ kamɛ a akũ.

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Baguma apam a kĩ ɛkɛna mɔ mi bawanyɔ ɛtsãmɛna ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ akũ a nyɛ mi fude, ade mi aanya tsɛfã nɛ apam kĩĩ fɔtsa akũ a. Ebi Abraham kĩ fɔ́atsã ye ɔwa akũ yaakɔa nhila ɛkpa katinya kamɛ bati flee.

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ Baguma pau ye Ɔsumunɛ a ni, mi sɛkɛ̃ ɛnya lɔtɔ edo ye kĩ ɛkɔa nhila ɛkpa mi, lɔkɔa ɛfɔ̃ mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi ɛfɔ̃ tɔkpa kakɛna.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Petro mɔ Yohanes dza bɛkabi bati a fɔlɛtsa a, Yudafɔ basɔfɔ badi, Asɔli kɔla a akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Zadukifɔ badi ya nɛ awã.
