english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Kĩ binu kĩ Petro mɔ Yohanes katsa fɔtsa nɛ Yesu kayidza fɔlɛtsa akũ, katsa kĩ banɔkpa aayidza bidzi nkpã a, bɛna kalɔkpa paa!

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Foesũ bipi be, lɛmɛ kĩ kade dɔ sũ ni, bɛkɔa be bido nɛ kɔla kamɛ kɛnawo kĩ kade nyina.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati pii kĩ binu be fɔlɛtsa a badi tɛ kalɛ wĩ a bido. Banyole batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛtɛ bido a pɔ biwo ndɛ mpim elo.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ kade nyina a, Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ mbla batsanɛ a yakpɔta nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Bɛyatsa fɔlɔ mɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee Hana mɔ Kayafa, Yohanes mɔ Aleksandro mɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a akũ bati badi.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Ade bɛnyɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bɛyadzɛla nɛ be anɛ kamɛ a. Bivia be kĩ, “Osi ɔwɔdabe akũ mikɛna fukĩĩ? Owei dĩ akũ ĩye ɔwa kɔbɛ̃ akũ mikɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Ade Petro kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa tɛ ye kamɛ a yidza ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe lɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃,

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: ntɛ miwɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ kanyɛ nɛ lɛtsa wĩ a kĩ bɔkɛna bɔkpa oti kĩ eveliveli mɔ ayekĩ bɛtsa ye a akũ ni,

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: mitse mbi mi, lɔkɔa Israelfɔ flee binyi kĩ, Yesu Kristo Nazaretni a kĩ mibɔa nɛ asendua akũ, lɛmɛ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ a, ye dĩ akũ fɔtsã sũ mɔ oti kĩĩ na akũ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: Ade Yesu kĩĩ akũ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa lɛ kĩ, ‘De a kĩ fɔla bakenɛ sĩ a, ade de lɛdani kɔla akũ de titiliwu a.’

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Ye odi nyɛ bati Ɔtɛnɛ, ade otidziwa odima lɛláa tite akũ kĩ ye akũ bɔ́atsã bɔna nkpã a.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Kĩ lɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔ́ ayekĩ Petro mɔ Yohanes lɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ kɔbɛ̃, titɔ kĩ bɛtánaa sukuu malo ni, fɔkɛna be saa. Ade bɛmɔ́ biyi ɔsũ kĩ bɛtsã bitomɔ Yesu a.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ bɛmɔ́ oti a kĩ bɛtsa a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ be ɔwɛ̃ sũ ni, bɛtápuli bɛlɛ kadima.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Bibi be kĩ bɛnɛ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, kite be odi bɛkapi adzuni.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Badi via kĩ, “Litsa bɔakɛna bati kĩĩ? Bɔláapuli busĩ kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkɛna a lɛnyɛ́ awãwã fɔtsa, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi nɛ Yerusalem nyina, enu kalɛ kĩĩ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ fɔlɛtsa kĩ fɔtagba sũ ni, mifɔ̃ budo be libe kĩ bɛtabi odima kalɛ nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ.”

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ade bibuki bɛvɛ be bɛnamɛna katsɛnakɔ̃ a bisĩ be mbla kĩ bɛtabuki bibi odima kalɛ ĩye bɛtsa fɔtsa nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ a.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro mɔ Yohanes kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mi awɛ mibu fukĩ fɔfɛta nɛ Baguma anɛ kĩ bɔkɛna, mi ɔlɛdo butse, mbɔɛɛ kĩ Baguma ode?

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ de bɔmɔ́, bunu lɛmɛ a, bɔláapuli busua onukpɛ̃ nɛ de akũ budzi kulokulo.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ye bɛtsa a, tɛ fɔba futeli tɛna. Batɛ Badonɛ a Bɔa Mpaɛ Bɛkpa Ɔwɔlɛ Kado.

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛfɔnɛ Petro mɔ Yohanes awɛ bɛnaa be bati sɛkɛ̃ a, bɛbɔa be amandiɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a bi be a akũ.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Ade batɛ badonɛ a flee kpɔta bɛbɔa mpaɛ bɛvɛ Baguma bɛlɛ kĩ, “O boe Ɔlate, nɔ kĩ ɔkɛna tite mɔ ode, mpo mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ ye kamɛ,

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: nɔ lɛtsã Hũhũ Kpalɛwa akũ ɔfɔ̃ boe ɔwanyɔ Dawid, nɔ ɔsumunɛ lɛ eyi kĩ, ‘Nde sũ katinya kamɛ bati de adɔ̃ mɔ bati tsa fɔlɔ nɛ tɔkpa akũ pupulidza?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Katinya kamɛ fɔgã flee kɛna ɔmɔa ade biyidza bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ boe Ɔlate mɔ ye Kristo akũ a.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ Herodes mɔ Pontio Pilato mɔ Israelfɔ, mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Israelfɔ a tsɛna nɛ ɔmatɔ kĩĩ kamɛ, ade biyidza bɛdza nɛ Yesu nɔ Ɔsumunɛ kpalɛwa a kĩ ɔbɔa tɔdɔ̃ a akũ a.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ nɔ kɔbɛ̃, mɔ nɔ kawɛ, ɔkpa osi bɛkpɔta bɛkɛna lɛtsa kĩ ohihiɛ oyi a fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, O boe Ɔlate, dã ɔhau a kĩ ye kamɛ bido boe, lɔkɔa kado boe nɔ adzuma bakɛnanɛ a ɔwɔlɛ, ayekĩ bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ nɔ kalɛ wĩ a nɛ katavila kamɛ.

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: Te awɛ nɛ nɔ bati akũ bɛna kasaɛ, kafɔ̃ awãwã fɔtsa mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni pii fɔya nɛ nɔ Ɔsumunɛ Kpalɛwa Yesu dĩ akũ.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛbɔa mpaɛ biyua a, kɔla kĩ koe kamɛ bɛna a kuna. Hũhũ Kpalɛwa wo nɛ be flee kamɛ ade bido Baguma fɔlɛtsa kalɛ awɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ a. Batɛ Badonɛ a Sɛ̃ Be Akũ Fɔtsa.

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi kĩĩ kamɛ a, batɛ badonɛ a flee kɛna ɔmɔa nɛ be fɔkɛnatsa mɔ adzuni kamɛ. Be kamɛ odima tákɔna kĩ ye odi la ye fɔtsa kĩ ɛna a, mbom fɔtsa kumɛkumɛ a nya be flee fude.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Fɔtɔ banyanɛ kĩĩ de lɛtsã mɔ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ boe Ɔlate Yesu kayidza kato banɔkpa kamɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, ade Baguma lukudɔ mɔ nhila na nɛ be flee akũ a.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Odima tánya ohiãni kitonɛkĩ bikĩ bɛna fɔla mɔ tite a nɔ foe.

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa sika a bɛma fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ade bɛsɛ̃ se bɛkpa odi nyɛ odi nɛ ye kasĩ onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Anania mɔ ɔtsɔ̃ Safira na nɛ be kamɛ, be malo bɛnɔ be tite tidi.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Anania kɔa sika a sidi ɛkɔla ade ɛkɔa se nkaɛ ɛma fɔtɔ banyanɛ a. Ɔtsɔ̃ Safira nyina foe akũ litsedi ade etunɔ kĩ bakɛna lɔmɔ a.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Ade Petro via ye kĩ, “Anania, nde sũ ɔfɔ̃ Abonsam nya nɔ akũ, ɔma fɔvã ɔkpa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa, fɔfɔ̃ ɔkɔla tite a kĩ ɔnɔ a sika a sidi?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Nɔ tide nyɛ tite a fɔmɔ ɔnɔ te, sika a kĩ ɔtɛ a lɛmɛ nɔ side sanɛ. Litsa lɛfɔ̃ ɔbɔɛ adzuni kpa kĩĩ odu? Nnɛ́ boe batidziwa ɔkɛ, mbom Baguma ɔkɛ!”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Kĩ Anania sila enu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ pɛ, esinya ɛtɔ nɛ tite eku. Libe wo bati flee a kĩ binu fɔlɛtsa a paa.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Bapopo a kĩ bɛna nɛ be kamɛ a kɔa lima bisuli ye bɛbɔɛ bɛnabila.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Ɔtsɔ̃ yanɛ ɛta nɛ awã ndɛ ndɔũhuilɛ ɛlalɛ liti, kĩ enyína lɛtsa kĩ lɛya lɛtɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Foesũ Petro bi ye kĩ, “Nde sũ mimɔ nɔ ɔnyɔ mikɛna onukpɛ̃ mɔa miasɔ boe Ɔlate Hũhũ midã? Dã, bapopo a kĩ bɛbɔɛ nɔ ɔnyɔ bɛnabila a dzɛ̃ nɛ onyukpɛ̃ finyaakĩĩ. Baabɔɛ nɔ malo bɛnaabila.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ otsole a ka ɛtɔ nɛ tite, eku. Ade kĩ bapopo a yamɔ kĩ eku ɛwaa a, bɛbɔɛ ye malo bɛnabila nɛ ɔnyɔ ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Foesũ libe paa wo batɛ badonɛ a flee mɔ be flee a kĩ binu lɛtsa kĩ lɛya nɛ awã a. Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ a Kɛna Awãwã Mɔ Nsɛntsɛlɛni Fɔtsa Pii.

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ a fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kakɛna awãwã mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa pii nɛ bati a kamɛ. Batɛ badonɛ a flee katsɛna nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli kɔla kakɔa kĩ bavɛ Salomo Mblada a kamɛ.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛtána nɛ batɛ badonɛ a kamɛ a tádo ɔwɔlɛ kĩ bafuã be, titɔ kĩ bakɔa obu kakpa be malo.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Bati pii, batsole mɔ banyole, dani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ bɛtɛ boe Ɔlate bido.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Ade bati pii bɔɛ be basɔnɛ mɔ tɛpa mɔ tɛkɛna bɛnayi nɛ kɔya nɛ awã kĩ ntɛ Petro atɛ̃ mɔ ye kuyo ako nɛ be akũ ni, lɔkɔa bɛsaɛ.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Ade bɛkɔa basɔnɛ mɔ bikĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ be kamɛ a bitomɛna tɛmatɔ a kĩ tisinya Yerusalem a bɛyamɛna bɛtsa be flee a. Yudafɔ Ɔmaĩ Banɔkɔɛ̃ Yidza Bɛdza Nɛ Yesu Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ a Akũ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bipi be bɛnado nɛ kɔla kamɛ.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ke lɛnyɛ a, boe Ɔlate ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi yabinya opunu a, ɛnyɛ be, ade ebi be kĩ,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Minaa Yudafɔ Asɔli kɔla a kamɛ lɔkɔa mibi bati fɔlɛtsa a flee nɛ nkpã vɔɛ̃ kĩĩ akũ!”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ kade nyina a bɛnawo nɛ Asɔli kɔla a kamɛ ade biyi fɔtsa katsa kayɔ nɛ awã a. Lɔkɔa na kamɛ, Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a, mɔ Yudafɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a tsɛna, ade bido bikĩ badã asɔli kɔla akũ kĩ bɛnaanyɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a nɛ kɔla a kamɛ bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ bati a naa awã a, bɛtámɔ be, foesũ bimuniki bɛyabi be kĩ,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Kĩ bɔnaa a, bɛbalɛ kɔla a kpakplakpa ade bikĩ badã kɔla a akũ lɛmɛ dzɛ̃ nɛ onyukpɛ̃ a pɛpɛɛpɛ a. Lɛmɛ kĩ bubinya kɔla a bati a kamɛ odima lɛláa koe kamɛ.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Kĩ asɔli kɔla akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a nu kalɛ kĩĩ a, akũ dzuli be ade bɛkakɔna lɛtsa kĩ lɛya nɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a akũ a.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Ade odi yabi be kĩ, “Bati a kĩ mipi mido nɛ kɔla kamɛ a, deĩ nɛ asɔli kɔla kamɛ ade bɛlamɛna bati fɔtsa katsa nɛ awã a.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a Asɔli Kɔla ɔdãmɛnanɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ye bati yidza bɛnakpã be bɛyamɛna. Nɛ kĩ bivila kĩ bati aanɔ be boe sũ ni, bɛtákpã be nɛ ɔhɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkpã fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bɛyamɛna a, bɛfɔ̃ bɛdza nɛ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a anɛ kamɛ. Ade Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a via be kĩ,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Bɔtábi mi kĩ mitabuki milɛ kadima nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ a? Lɛmɛ migbamɛna fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ flee nɛ Yerusalem, ade miawɛ kĩ miakɔa ye wuda sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a mite nɛ boe akũ a.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Awã na Petro mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛbã a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ bɔabu Baguma, nnɛ́ batidziwa.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Yesu a kĩ mifɔ̃ bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ mikɔ ye a, boe bawanyɔ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna kanɔkpa, edzi nkpã.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Baguma kpa ye kadzikɔ̃nɔkɔɛ̃ nɛ ye sɔmɔna, ade ɛkɛna ye Ɔkpãnɛ mɔ Ɔtɛnɛ, ayekĩ Israelfɔ aana osi bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛna be titikpa kakɔakate.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Ade boe mɔ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a, kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma futetsa kĩ ɛkpa bikĩ babu ye a, bɔnyɛ foe akũ adansifɔ a.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, fɔfɔ̃ bɛna kalɔkpa paa ade bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Farisifɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ bavɛ Gamaliel sua be osi. Ɛnyɛ fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ nɛ Mose mbla akũ, ade bati a flee akɔa obu baũ kakpa ye a. Eyidza ɛdza ade ɛlɛ kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bɛkpa be osi sukuloo bɛkɔna fɔlɛtsa a akũ.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Ade ke liti ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ Israelfɔ, midã kadã wĩ nɛ lɛtsa kĩ miawɛ kĩ miakɛna nɛ bati kĩĩ akũ a!

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Ke liti, nɛ bati kakã lɔkɔ a, Yuda, Galileani a yayidza, ɛvɛlɛ bati kɔdabu ebumɔ ye akũ. Kĩ ye malo ɛyaku a bikĩ bitomɔ ye a flee yalɛ, ade ye fɔlɛtsa a lɛmɛ wɔ a.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Foesũ lɛtsa kĩ mabi mi nyɛ kĩ mifɔnɛ bati kĩĩ awɛ bɛnaa. Ntɛ be fɔtsa katsa a mɔ bati fude fɔanɛ ni, owi lɛláabuna mɔ fɔ́awɔ.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ futo kɔ̃ ni, bɔláapuli busua foe osi. Ntɛ mitádã kadã wĩ ni, luwi lidi míamɔ kĩ Baguma awɛ akũ buyidza bɔdza.” Katsɛnakɔ̃ bati a tɛ Gamaliel kalɛ a.

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛvɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a bimuniki bɛya ade bibo be a. Bisĩ be mbla kĩ bɛtabuki bɛlɛ kadima nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ dadaada, ade bɛfɔnɛ be awɛ a.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛdzakũ banaa a, bɛmɔ́ kawɔlɛ pii kitonɛkĩ Baguma bu be ndɛ bati kĩ bɛfɛta bɛkpa lubo kamɔ mɔ kɔnyɔãkado nɛ Yesu dĩ sũ.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ liti a, luwi daa bɛkatsa fɔtsa nɛ Asɔli kɔla kamɛ, mɔ bati tɔtɔ kamɛ, kade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ akũ kĩ Yesu nyɛ Kristo a.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kĩ fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a lidede lamɛna kapɔ kabu a, ɔdzɛ yawo nɛ Yudafɔ a kĩ balɛ Tigriki mɔ Yudafɔ mɔawɛ a kayite. Grikifɔ a tsa kĩ ntɛ bɛsɛ̃ funitsã ni, baákpa be bakunawa.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ fɔtɔ banyanɛ ewua-nviã a vɛ batɛ badonɛ a flee bɛkpɔta ade bibi be kĩ, “Fɔtáfɛta kĩ bɔakɔa lɔkɔ a kĩ bɔalɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa bɔkɛna funitsã kasɛ̃ lɔkɔ.

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a de bati a flee kawɔlɛ ade bɛnyɛ Stefano, oti kĩ edeĩ katɛkado, kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa lɛmɛ tɛ ye kamɛ, Filipo, Prokoro, Nikanora, Timona, Parmena mɔ Nikolao, kĩ ɛdani ɛkɛna Yudani. Eto Antiokia.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Bɛnyɛ bati evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛpau a bɛtsa fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ade bɛbɔa mpaɛ nɛ be lɔlɔ, bite awɛ nɛ be akũ a.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Bati pii tɛ Baguma kalɛ wĩ a. Fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ nɛ Yerusalem danɛ kapɔ ade Yudafɔ basɔfɔ a pii lɛmɛ dani bɛtɛ Yesu bido a. Yudafɔ Ɔmaĩ Banɔkɔɛ̃ a Pi Stefano.

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Stefano kĩ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ mɔ sɛmɛ kalɛ yi ye kamɛ a, kɛna awãwã mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa pii nɛ bati a kamɛ.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛnyɛ be nɛ awɔba kamɛ a kĩ bito Kirene mɔ Aleksandria bibumɔ bikĩ bito Kilikia mɔ Asia yidza nɛ Stefano akũ, bɛkavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ ye.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma Hũhũ kpa Stefano nyansã mɔ kadikanyi paa foesũ ntɛ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla ni, be kamɛ odima lápuli katɛ ye atsindze.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ badi kĩ bɛma fɔvã kĩ, “Bunu kĩ Stefano lɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Mose mɔ Baguma akũ.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Ye onukpɛ̃ katsua kĩĩ fɔ̃ bati a, banɔkɔɛ̃ a, mɔ mbla batsanɛ a na kalɔkpa paa, ade Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ a yapi Stefano bɛnamɛna Ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bitsua Stefano onukpɛ̃ a yama fɔvã bɛlɛ kĩ, “Oti kĩĩ alɛ kɔdzɛla katia Asɔli Kɔla Kpalɛwa mɔ Mose mbla a luwi nyɛ luwi.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Bunu lɛmɛ kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ Yesu Nazaretni a aaveli Asɔli kɔla kĩĩ lɔkɔa ɛdani tɛmaniɛ flee a kĩ Mose yi ɛkpa boe a.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ bati a flee kĩ bɛna nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a ke anɛ nɛ Stefano akũ ade bɛmɔ́ kĩ ye anɛ kamɛ dani ndɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ade aye a.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Ade Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a via Stefano kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ bɛlamɛna kalɛ nɛ nɔ akũ a ni, nuwĩ fɔanɛ?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Stefano kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nda amɛ mɔ nwãɛ amɛ, mitse katse wĩ! Fɔmɔ boe ɔwanyɔ Abraham aatu ɛnaadzi nɛ Haram tite akũ a, Baguma Kpalɛwa a nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye nɛ Mesopotamia tite akũ.

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: Ade awã ebi ye kĩ, ‘Yidza kafɔnɛ nɔ katete bati mɔ tite a na kanaa tite a kĩ maatsa nɔ a akũ.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: “Foesũ ɛdzakũ nɛ Kaldea tite akũ ɛnadzi nɛ Haram kɛnawo lɔkɔ a kĩ ɔlɛga ku. Ade ke Baguma kpã ye ɛyamɛna nɛ awã kĩ midzi nviã kĩĩ a.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Baguma tákpa Abraham tite kanyakɔ̃ sɛnɛta mɔa malo kĩ tɛnya ye tide. Lɛmɛ ɛlɛ eyi kĩ yaakɔa tite a na ɛkpa ye tɛnya ye mɔ ye babi mɔ bawa tide, titɔ kĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ malo ɛtáma obi odima!

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma bi ye nyɛ kĩ, ‘Nɔ bawa aanya banɔvɔɛ̃ nɛ tite bɛbã akũ, bɛnya abawa bɛkɛna be tɛnya futeli ɔha ɛna kpɔũ.’

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Maadzɛ mɔ bati a kĩ be baasum a. Ke liti baadzakũ nɛ tite a akũ bɛyaasum yĩ nɛ awĩ kĩĩ.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Ade ke Baguma kɛna lɛkpati katsua ndɛ apam aye nɛ Abraham mɔ Ye Baguma kayite a. Nɛ foesũ kĩ Abraham ma Isak a etsua ye lɛkpati kĩ ɛtɛ fuwi ɛlɛ̃ a. Isak lɛmɛ ma Yakob, Yakob lɛmɛ ma babi ewua-nviã kĩ bɛnyɛ Yudafɔ bawanyɔ ewua-nviã a.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: “Yakob babi kĩĩ yɔɔlɛ anɛ nɛ be ɔwaɛ̃ Yosef akũ ade bipi ye bɛnɔ bɛkpa bati badi bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna Egipte tite akũ a. Lɛmɛ Baguma dã ye akũ,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: ade ɛnyɛ ye etomɛna ye lubo kamɔ mɔ ayɔlɔhɔ kamɛ a. Baguma kpa ye nyansã mɔ kɔnyakamɔ baũ foesũ Egiptefɔ Lɛgã Farao wɛ ye kalɛ ade ɛkɔa ye ɛkɛna ye tite mɔ lɛgã tɔtɔ a akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: “Lɔkɔ lidi kamɛ ni, kɔlɛ kpale ya nɛ Egipte mɔ Kanan tite flee akũ kĩ fɔyamɛna lubo kamɔ beblebee kĩ boe bawanyɔ tána funitsã.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Yakob nu kĩ fugbanɔ na nɛ Egipte a, edo ye babi lɔtɔnyade a bɛnaaya funitsã bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yakob babi buki bɛnaa awã lɛyade kĩ banaaya funitsã a, Yosef nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa bawaɛ̃, ade ɛnyɛ be lɛmɛ ɛtsa Lɛgã Farao a.

english_verse: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
translation_verse: Yosef bi be kĩ ntɛ bɛnaa ni, bɛkpã ɔlɛga Yakob mɔ ye akũ bati flee a kĩ baana ndɛ bati aduesɔũ-elo bɛyamɛna nɛ Egipte.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Foesũ Yakob mɔ ye babi dzakũ bɛnaa Egipte bɛnadzi nɛ awã kɛnawo kĩ biku.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bɛkɔa be banɔkpa bɛyamɛna nɛ Sekem bɛyabila nɛ linikɛ a kĩ Abraham ya mɔ sika etomɛna Hamor babi sɛkɛ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Kĩ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma sui mɔ Abraham wo ɔtɔ kiyua a, Israelfɔ nɛ Egipte a ma bɛsa beblebee.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Fɔyaya lɛmɛ kĩ lɛgã vɔɛ̃ Farao a kĩ ɛnya Egipte akũ a tányi Yosef kɔlaa.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Farao kĩĩ tsã tɛdake kamɛ ɛkɛna boe bawanyɔ tɛnya, ɛfɔ̃ bɛnyɛ be babi bakɔkɔa nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ ayekĩ baaku.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “Ade lɔkɔ a na kamɛ bɛma Mose a. Obisɛ̃ kĩĩ kalɛ kadã paa. Nɛ foesũ ye bamanɛ kɔla ye nɛ kɔla kamɛ tidetɔ ɛlalɛ.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛnyɛ ye nɛ be akũ a, Farao obitsole namɔ ye ɛbɔɛ, ade ɛdɔɔla ye ndɛ ye mɔawɛ sɛmɛ kamɛ obi a.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Bɛtsa ye Egiptefɔ kadikanyi mɔ be kadzi kamɛ fɔkɛnatsa a flee, ade ɛyabo anɛ ɛkɛna oti a. Ɛna ovio nɛ kɔdzɛla kalɛ mɔ fɔkɛnatsa pii kamɛ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Kĩ ewo awã a, ɛmɔ́ kĩ Egipteni odi lamɛna Israelni odi awɔda kakɛna foesũ ɛnabumɔ Israelni a, ade ebo Egipteni a ɛkɔ a.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Mose kɔna kĩ bawaɛ̃ a aanu foe kayɔ kĩ Baguma lɛwɛ kĩ yatsã ye akũ ɛtɛ be lɛmɛ bɛtánu foe kayɔ lɔmɔ.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, ebuki ɛmɔ́ kĩ Israelfɔ bati nviã lamɛna kavũ. Ɛnaakpɔla lɛma a ade ebi be kĩ, ‘Midã, bawaɛ̃ mɔ bawaɛ̃ mianɛ, nde sũ miakɛna akũ awɔda lɔmɔ?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ ekĩ ɛwɛ sɔwã a du Mose ɛdzɛla nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ ade evia ye kĩ, ‘Fɔɔdabe tsɛla nɔ? Owei lɛkɛna nɔ boe akũ ɔnyanɛ mɔ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ?

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Mbɔɛ awɛ kĩ akɔ yĩ ndɛ ayekĩ ɔkɔ Egipteni a kadzɔ a?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Kĩ Mose nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, libe wo ye paa, foesũ elo ɛnaa Midianfɔ tite akũ, awã kĩ ɛnadzi ndɛ ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ aye, ɛma babinyole nviã.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Futeli tɛna liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Mose nɛ tuwĩ mɔ fu kĩ tɛkatɛ̃ nɛ kɔsa a kĩ kufuã Sinai ɔtɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Kĩ Mose mɔ́ fukĩĩ a, fɔkɛna ye saa foesũ efuã kĩ yanaadã lɛtsa a kadã wĩ. Ade Baguma vɛ ye ebi ye kĩ,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Yĩ nyɛ nɔ bawanyɔ a Baguma, Abraham mɔ Isak mɔ Yakob Baguma.’ Mose katila bliblibli mɔ libe foesũ ɛtápuli ebuki ɛdã awã kadã wĩ.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: “Ade Baguma bi ye kĩ, ‘Nyɛ nɔ mpabua nɛ abɔa, kitonɛkĩ awã kĩ ɔdzɛ̃ a ni, tite kpalɛwa tanɛ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali mmɔ́ yĩ bati a lubo kamɔ mɔ tɛnya a kĩ bakɛna be nɛ Egipte tite akũ a. Nnu be kɔnyakabe foesũ ĩya kĩ mayaanyɛ be. Finyaakĩĩ ya, lɔkɔa ndo nɔ fɔtɔ nɛ Egipte.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Ade Mose a kĩ bisĩ kĩĩ bivia kĩ, ‘Owei lɛkɔa nɔ ɛdzɛla ndɛ boe akũ ɔnyanɛ mɔ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ?’ Ade ye Mose kĩĩ a Baguma do kĩ ɛnaanya be akũ, lɔkɔa ɛnyɛ be nɛ ɔhau kamɛ fɔtsãmɛna ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye nɛ tuwĩ a kĩ tɛkatɛ̃ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Mose kpã bati a bɛdzakũ nɛ Egipte. Ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa pii nɛ Egipte mɔ Mpo Ngbã a abuĩ mɔ kɔsa kamɛ nɛ be osi katsã futeli tɛna a flee kamɛ.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: “Ade Mose kĩĩ mɔapɛ a libi Israelfɔ kĩ, ‘Baguma aanyɛ onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ odi ndɛ ayekĩ ɛnyɛ yĩ a etomɛna mimɔa a kamɛ.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Mose nyɛ oti kĩ ɛna nɛ boe bawanyɔ a sɛkɛ̃ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Israelfɔ na nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a. Ade ye mɔ Baguma fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ lɛmɔ kɔdzɛla nɛ Sinai ɔtɔ akũ, ɛtɛ Baguma nkpã kalɛ a ɛma boe a.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ boe bawanyɔ táwɛ kĩ batse Mose, mbom bisĩ ye, ade bɛwɛ kĩ bamuniki bɛnaa Egipte tite akũ a.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Ade ke bɛmɛ baviɔ̃ ndɛ ɔnantsue obi aye, bɛkabɔa afɔliɛ kakpa ye a. Bɛmɔ́ kawɔlɛ bɛnya luwi bɛkpa lɛtsa a kĩ be mɔa bɛkɔa awɛ bɛkɛna a.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ Baguma muniki nɛ be liti, ɛfɔ̃ bɛkasum suwa mɔ odetɔ mɔ fusibii, ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ Ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, ‘Israelfɔ, nnɛ́ yĩ mikɔa bayaɛ mibɔa afɔliɛ mikpa nɛ futeli tɛna a kĩ mina nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a.

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Mbom Molok pata mɔ Romfa fusibii baviɔ̃ a kĩ mikɛna a mikabɔa afɔliɛ kakpa. Foesũ maafɔ̃ bipili mi ndom bɛkpã mi bɛnamɛna mɔ lugo nɛ Babilonia liti flee.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Boe bawanyɔ na patampa kĩ ye kamɛ Baguma kanyɛ akũ katsa be nɛ kɔsa a kamɛ. Bike patampa kĩĩ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma nyɛ ɛtsa Mose kĩ ɛkɛna pɛpɛɛpɛ a.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Patampa kĩĩ, kĩ boe bawanyɔ tɛ bitomɛna be balɛga sɛkɛ̃ a na nɛ be awɛ, nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Yosua kpã be a, ade bɛtɛ bati a kĩ Baguma blɔmɛna nɛ be lɔtɔ a tite a. Patampa kĩĩ na nɛ be kamɛ kɛyawo Lɛgã Dawid lɔkɔ.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Dawid fɔkɛnatsa wo Baguma anɛ, ade evia Baguma kĩ ɛkpa ye osi eke kadzikɔ̃ wĩ ɛkpa Yakob Baguma a.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Salomo lɛyake kɔla a ɛkpa Baguma.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ Baguma Kpale ládzi nɛ fɔla kĩ batidziwa ke a kamɛ. Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ lɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Ode nyɛ yĩ lɛgãkpo, ade tite lɛmɛ nyɛ yĩ abɔa katekɔ̃ a. Kɔla kɔɔdabe odu míapuli mike mikpa yĩ, mbɔɛɛ kadzikɔ̃ mɔ nlá?

english_verse: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
translation_verse: Ade Stefano buki ebi be kĩ, “Bati kĩ mideĩ atɔ ɔsĩ mianɛ! Misĩ kĩ míatse Baguma kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ mi balɛga kɛna a. Daa miayidza kadza nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa akũ.

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ ɔɔdabe mɔ mi bawanyɔ tátomɔ ye liti? Bɛkɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kĩ bɛlɛ biyi kĩ Oti a Kĩ Bɛpau kĩ ye akũ kpalɛ a aaya. Oti Kĩ Bɛpau kĩĩ malo, minyɛ ye mikpa bɛkɔ ye.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ mi a kĩ mitɛ Baguma mbla mitomɛna ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ sɛkɛ̃ a, misĩ ye akũ kakɛna.” Bɛtalɛ Stefano Boe.

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Kĩ binu Stefano fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ nɛ be akũ a, be tɔwɔlɛ hulu, ade bɛkaba adɔ̃ dzadzadza mɔ kalɔkpa a.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Stefano kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa yi ye kamɛ, dzɛla anɛ ɛdã ode koee ade ɛmɔ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a mɔ Yesu kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma sɔmɔna a.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Ade ebi be kĩ, “Midã! Mamɔ kĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ binya ade Otidziwa Obi a dzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma sɔmɔna a!”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Bati a bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba, bisua atɔ ade biwula atsɛ bɛnapi ye a.

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: Bɛvɛlɛ ye bɛnɛmɛna nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ ade bɛtalɛ ye boe a. Bati a kĩ bɛvɛlɛ ye bɛnɛmɛna a nyɛ be tɛtadiɛ wulaa biyi nɛ opopo odi kĩ bavɛ Saulo sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkatalɛ Stefano boe a, ɛbɔa mpaɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate Yesu, tɛ yĩ hũhũ.”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Ade ke eke aduli ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, tase be tɔkpa kĩĩ akũ kɔtɔ!” Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ eyua a, ade eku a.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Ade Saulo kɔa ete nɛ ye akũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ yakɔ batɛ badonɛ a. Foesũ ɛkawo nɛ tɔtɔ tidi nyɛ tidi kamɛ kapili batsole mɔ banyole flee kavɛlɛ be kanaado nɛ kɔla kamɛ. Filipo De Lɛtsa Nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a Akũ Nɛ Samaria.

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bilo nɛ Yerusalem a tsã bimini bɛkalɛ kalɛ wĩ a nɛ Yesu akũ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Ade Filipo naa Samaria ɔmatɔ odi kamɛ ɛnabi bati a Kalɛ Wĩ nɛ Kristo akũ a.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Bati pii tse ye fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ a nɛ awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna bɛmɔ́ a sũ.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Ɛblɔmɛna hũhũ kpa etomɛna bati pii kamɛ. Hũhũ kpa a kabɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ntɛ banɛ nɛ bati a kamɛ. Ɛtsa bati kĩ biveliveli mɔ bikĩ bɛnɛ lɛkpɛ̃ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ Samaria ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bati mɔ kawɔlɛ paa!

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Kanɔkɔnɛ ɔdãnɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Simon na nɛ Samaria ɔmatɔ a kamɛ Ye kanɔkɔnɛ kadã a kakɛna bati wãwã kĩ fɔfɔ̃ yabu ye akũ kĩ oti mɔ kɔbɛ̃ odi odu yanɛ.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Samariafɔ batsole mɔ banyole flee abu ye nɛ fukĩĩ sũ, ade balɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ, “Ye nyɛ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ bavɛ Kɔbɛ̃ Kpalede a.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ ye kanɔkɔnɛ kadã awo be anɛ sũ ni, bati atse ye ɔlɛdo.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ kɔ̃, bɛtɛ Filipo fɔtsa katsa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanya akũ a nɛ Yesu Kristo dĩ akũ bido. Nɛ foesũ batsole mɔ banyole pii tɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Simon kĩĩ lɛmɛ tɛ edo ade ɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ a. Ɛtsã etomɔ Filipo bɛkamini ade Filipo awãwã fɔtsa kpale a kĩ ɛkakɛna a fɔ̃ akũ dzuli ye a!

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ nɛ Yerusalem nu kĩ Samariafɔ tɛ Baguma kalɛ a bido a, bido Petro mɔ Yohanes kĩ bɛnaasɛla be.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo awã a, bɛbɔa mpaɛ nɛ batɛ badonɛ vɔɛ̃ a lɔlɔ kĩ bɛtɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bɛkɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ a nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ malo ni, bɛtátɛ tɔ̃ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Foesũ Petro mɔ Yohanes te awɛ nɛ be akũ ade bɛtɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Kĩ Simon mɔ́ kĩ awɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ate nɛ bati akũ fɔmɔ batɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a sũ ni, ɛbɔɛ sika ɛkpa,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: ade ebi be kĩ, “Mikpa yĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩĩ odu ayekĩ ntɛ nte awɛ nɛ bati akũ ni, baana Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Ade Petro bi ye kĩ, “Mimɔ nɔ sika a flee míawɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɔkɔna kĩ aapuli ɔya Baguma futetsa mɔ sika.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Ɔláapuli ɔkɛna adzuma kĩĩ, kitonɛkĩ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɛ́waa yededeede nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ mmɔ́ kĩ anɛngbã mɔ titikpa kakɛna nya nɔ kamɛ kamɛ!”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Ade Simon bi Petro mɔ Yohanes kĩ, “Mike boe Ɔlate aduli mikpa yĩ ayekĩ kadumu fɔlɛtsa kĩ milɛ kĩĩ fudima lɛlaaya nɛ yĩ akũ.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro mɔ Yohanes lɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ wĩ a, bidi adansiɛ lɛmɛ nɛ ke akũ nɛ Samaria biyua a, bimuniki bɛnaa Yerusalem. Bɛnatsã Samaria tukula pii kamɛ bɛkade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ. Filipo Lɔmɛna Mɔ Etiopia Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Odi.

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Ade boe Ɔlate ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi yabi Filipo kĩ, “Yidza, kaka nɛ kayɔ, katsã kɔsa kamɛ osi a kĩ eto Yerusalem ɛnaa Gaza akũ a.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Ade ke eyidza epi osi a. Kĩ yanaa a, ɛnatsɛlamɛna onyole odi kĩ yaya. Onyole kĩĩ nyɛ Etiopia lɛgãtsole kĩ bavɛ Kandake, sika akũ fɔtsa ɔdãmɛnanɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃. Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ nabɔa mpaɛ nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: Emuniki yatsã, ade edzi nɛ ohilili kĩ bapɔnkɔ avɛlɛ kamɛ, ɛkakã Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya ovoli a.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a bi Filipo kĩ, “Tsã kanaapi ohilili a.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Foesũ Filipo wula atsɛ ɛnaa ye sɛkɛ̃ ade enu kĩ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kakã Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya ovoli a kĩ ɛtsɛlɛ a. Ade evia ye kĩ, “Anu fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɔlamɛna kakã a kayɔ?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oowo, nde maakɛna nnu foe kayɔ ntɛ odima tányɛ foe kamɛ ɛtsa yĩ?” Eke Filipo aduli kĩ ede ewo nɛ ohilili a kamɛ edzi nɛ ye ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ foe ɛkakã nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ nyɛ kĩ, “Bɛkpã ye ndɛ kuveli aye banamɛna kakɔkɔ̃. Edzɛ̃ dĩĩ ndɛ kavelibi nɛ ke tɛtsã bakpelinɛ anɛ ade ayea lɛmɛ ɛtábinya onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Nɛ ye akũ kadɔla sũ, bɛtádzɛ bɛkpa ye ɔtɛbɛ Owei aalɛ kadi etomɛna ye babi mɔ bawa akũ, Kitonɛkĩ bɛtɛ ye nkpã nɛ ye awɛ nɛ katinya kamɛ.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a via Filipo kĩ, “Bi yĩ. Owei akũ fɔlɛtsa onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ a lamɛna kɔdzɛla kalɛ, ye mɔa akũ mbɔɛɛ kĩ oti bɛbã?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Filipo yi kayɔ etomɛna fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ akũ, ade ɛkɔa fɔbɛbã pii ebu, ebi ye Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ Yesu akũ a.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Kĩ banaa nɛ osi a bɛnawo kakɔ kadi kĩ mui dzɛ̃ nɛ osi ɔwɛ̃. Fɔtsa akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a bi Filipo kĩ, “Aádã mui badi nɛ awĩ! Litsa sũ nláapuli ntɛ Baguma mui kawɔla amaniɛ a?” Ade Filipo bi ye kĩ, “Ntɛ ɔtɛ odo mɔ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ flee kɔ̃ ni, aapuli ɔtɛ”

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛnɛ nɛ mui a kamɛ a, Boe Ɔlate Hũhũ a bɔɛ Filipo ɛdzakũmɛna, ade ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a tabuki ɛmɔ ye a. Ɛtɔasu ye osi ɛnaa mɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Filipo mɔ eyi ɔsũ kĩ ɛyanɛ ɛta nɛ Asoto ɔmatɔ kamɛ. Ɛtsã emini eto awã ɛkalɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ tɛmatɔ a kĩ tideĩ nɛ osi akũ a flee kɛnawo Kaisarea ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Saulo fɔtsa katsa na kɔbɛ̃ ade ye fɔlɛtsa kamɛ kanyɛ kĩ Yesu nyɛ Kristo a táfɔ̃ Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛna nɛ Damasko a lɛtɛmɔ ye atsindze a.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Yidza finyaakĩĩ kanaa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ, lɔkɔa baabi nɔ lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ akɛna.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Ade yĩ mɔawɛ a maanyɛ lubo a kĩ yaamɔ nɛ yĩ sũ a ntsa ye a.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Kĩ Saulo naa Yerusalem a, ɛwɛ kĩ yanaawo nɛ batɛ badonɛ a kamɛ nɛ awã. Lɛmɛ be flee bivila ye kitonɛkĩ bɛtátɛ bido kĩ ɔtɛ odonɛ yanɛ.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Edeĩ nɛ awã ɛlamɛna mpaɛ kabɔa, kitonɛkĩ ĩnyɛ ntsa ye ɛmɔ nɛ hũhũ kamɛ kĩ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Anania aayate awɛ nɛ ye akũ ebuki ɛmɔ fɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ fɔkɛna Saulo ndɛ tupo lɛtsɛ nɛ ye anɛ tɛnɛ tɛtɔ, ade edo fɔkɔ kamɔ awɛ a. Eyidza ɛkpa osi bɛkɛna ye mui kawɔla amaniɛ.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ, Saulo kɔa kɔnɛyɔɔlɛ etomɔ batɛ badonɛ a liti kĩ yawɔla be. Ɛnaa Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: ɛnavia kĩ bɛkpa ye otumi tuvoli, lɔkɔa ɛnaa Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ nɛ Damasko, ɛnaapili bikĩ bɛtsɛ̃ nɛ boe Ɔlate osi vɔɛ̃ a akũ, batsole mɔ banyole flee, esuli be ɛyamɛna nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Saulo fuã Damasko ɔmatɔ a eyua mɔ kakɔna kĩĩ a ni, deedimɔapɛ ɔhaĩ odi mɔanɛ eto ode etsua esinya ye.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ɛka ɛtɔ ade enu ɔlɛdo odi kĩ, “Saulo, Saulo litsa sũ otomɔ yĩ liti?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ade Saulo via kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nɔ owei anɛ?” Ɔlɛdo kĩĩ kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yĩ nyɛ Yesu a kĩ ye liti otomɛna a.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Banyole a kĩ bɛmɔ Saulo bɛtsɛ̃ a fɔ̃ bɛdza lɛmɛ baámɔ lɛtsa a kĩ de balɛ. Binu ɔlɛdo a lɛmɛ bɛtámɔ oti odima.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Saulo yidza nɛ tite ebinya banebi a, ebuki yaámɔ fɔkɔ. Foesũ bipi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna Damasko ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Edzi fuwi ɛlalɛ kĩ yaámɔ fɔkɔ, yaánya, yaámɔa lɛmɛ.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ɔtɛ odonɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Anania na nɛ Damasko. Boe Ɔlate nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye ɛvɛ ye kĩ, “Anania!” Anania tunɔ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, amɔ yĩ nɛ awĩ!”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Ade boe Ɔlate bi ye kĩ, “Yidza katsã ɔblɔnu a kĩ bavɛ Ɔblɔnu Kpĩĩ a akũ, lɔkɔa ntɛ owo Yuda tɔtɔ kamɛ ni, via Saulo kĩ eto Tarso a akũ.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ade ke Anania kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nnu fɔlɛtsa pii ntomɛna oti kĩĩ akũ nɛ ayekĩ ɛkɛna nɔ bati awɔda nɛ Yerusalem a!

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Ade bunu kĩ ɛtɛ otumi tuvoli etomɛna Basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ sɛkɛ̃ kĩ epili nɔ batɛ badonɛ flee nɛ Damasko a.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe Ɔlate bi ye kĩ, “Naa nɔ kɔ̃ kanaakɛna ayekĩ mbi nɔ a, kitonɛkĩ mpau ye kĩ ɛyaanya yĩ ɔsumunɛ ɛlɛ kalɛ wĩ a ɛtsa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ, fɔgã, mɔ Israelfɔ flee.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Foesũ Anania pi osi ɛnawo nɛ tɔtɔ a kĩ te kamɛ Saulo na, ade ete awɛ nɛ ye akũ ebi ye kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ Saulo, Boe Ɔlate Yesu kĩ ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa nɔ nɛ osi a lido yĩ nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ kĩ buki kamɔ fɔkɔ, lɔkɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ɛtɛ nɔ kamɛ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ ɛnya eyua a, akũ de ye ɔsĩ. Saulo De Lɛtsã Nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ Akũ Nɛ Damasko. Saulo dzi nɛ batɛ badonɛ a sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Damasko fuwi fudi.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi a kamɛ a, ɛnaa Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade ɛnalɛ kɔdzɛla ɛtsa kĩ Yesu nyɛ Baguma Obi a.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna bati flee a kĩ binu ye fɔtsa katsa a saa, ade bivia be akũ kĩ, “Nnɛ́ oti kĩĩ tsɛ̃ yakɔ bikĩ bɛtɛ Yesu bido a nɛ Yerusalem a mbɔɛɛ? Nnɛ́ ye lɛmɛ lɛya nɛ awĩ mɔ botaɛ kĩ yayaapili batɛ badonɛ a ɛma basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Fuwi fudi liti ni, Yudafɔ a natsɛna ade bipi adzuni kĩ bakɔ ye a.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati bɔɛ bɛyabi Saulo. Lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee bɛkadzɔlɛ ɔmatɔ a seebi a kĩ bike bisinya a tunyukpɛ̃ ayekĩ baana ye bɛkɔ.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɛnyɛ mɔa kayite a, bikĩ bitomɔ Saulo a bɔɛ ye bido nɛ ɔtsɛtsɛ kamɛ bɛtsãmɛna seebi ɔkɛ kamɛ bɛkamɛna ye bɛdzɛla nɛ tite! Saulo Naa Yerusalem.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ade Barnaba bumɔ ye ɛkpã ye ɛnamɛna fɔtɔ banyanɛ a sɛkɛ̃ a. Ebi be ayekĩ Saulo mɔ́ boe Ɔlate a nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ yanaa Damasko, mɔ ayekĩ boe Ɔlate lɛmɔ ye kɔdzɛla lɛmɛ a. Ebi be lɛmɛ ayekĩ Saulo lɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ nɛ Damasko.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ade bɛtɛ ye ɛna nɛ be kamɛ a. Kito ke a, ɛna nɛ be kamɛ, emini nɛ Yerusalem flee ɛkalɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ mɔ kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Ade Yudafɔ badi kĩ balɛ Griki, kavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ Saulo a, lɛmɛ bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ ye.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Kĩ batɛ badonɛ a badi nu fukĩĩ a bɛkpã Saulo bɛnamɛna Kaisarea ade bɛnyɛ ye osi ɛnaa ye ɔtɔ Tarso a.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Fɔyaya kĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ, batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛna nɛ Yudea, Galilea mɔ Samaria na nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ. Nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kɔbɛ̃ akũ sũ ni, bɛna ɔwɔlɛ kado. Bɛpɔ bibu ade bɛkɔa obu bɛkpa boe Ɔlate a. Petro Naa Lida Mɔ Yopa.

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Petro mini ɛnaa fɔkɔ pii. Nɛ foe kamɛ a, ɛnasɛla batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ ɔmatɔ odi kĩ bavɛ Lida kamɛ.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Awã na ɛmɔ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Ainea. Ade futeli ɛlɛ̃ flee kĩ eveliveli kĩ yaápuli eyidza nɛ ɔkɛna akũ a.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro mɔ ye a, ebi ye kĩ, “Ainea! Yesu Kristo tsa nɔ fɔsɔ! Yidza kadza kabuta nɔ ɔkɛna.” Deedimɔapɛ Ainea yidza ɛdza, nɛ akũ ɔsĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Bati a flee kĩ bidzi nɛ Lida mɔ Saron a, mɔ́ Ainea foesũ bɛkɔa be akũ bɛkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, otsole odi kĩ bavɛ Tabita na nɛ Yopa. Nɛ Griki tide kamɛ ni, bavɛ ye Dorkas kĩ de kayɔ nyɛ kayisie. Ɛnyɛ ɔtɛ odonɛ kĩ yakɛna tuwĩ daa, kabumɛna ahiãfɔ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Fɔsɔ yabɔa ye ade eku a. Foesũ ye bati wɔla ye mui bihihiɛ ye bɛwɔla nɛ kɔla kamɛ nɛ abansulu ode.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Yopa mɔ Lida kayite távɛlɛ, foesũ kĩ batɛ badonɛ a nu kĩ Petro na nɛ Lida a, bido bati nviã mɔ ɔlɛdo nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ kĩ, “Wɛ sɔwa kaya nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Ade ke Petro yidza ɛmanɛ akũ etomɔ be a. Kĩ ewo awã a bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna kɔla kĩ koe kamɛ bɛwɔla Dorkas a. Bakunawa batsole dza bisinya Petro nɛ awã bɛkabe ade bɛtsa ye tɛtadie flee a kĩ Dorkas kapamu nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ edzi nkpã a.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Ade Petro nyɛ be flee nɛ kɔla a kamɛ, eke aduli ɛbɔa mpaɛ a. Ɛdani ɛtsɛlamɔ ɔnɔkpa a ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Tabita! Yidza!” Awã na ebinya anɛ ade kĩ ɛmɔ Petro a, eyidza edzi.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Ade Petro pi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ ɛdzɛla ye a. Foesũ Petro vɛ batɛ badonɛ mɔ bakunawa a, ade ɛkɔa ye nkpã ode edo nɛ be awɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ gba nɛ Yopa flee ade bati pii tɛ boe Ɔlate bido a.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Petro dzi nɛ Yopa fuwi pii ade ɛna nɛ Simon adi kĩ yakɔa bayaɛ tuvoli kapamu fɔtsa a sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ, onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Kornelio na nɛ Kaisarea ɔmatɔ a kamɛ. Ɛnyɛ Romafɔ kawũ ɔnɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ oti wĩ, ade ye mɔ ye kɔla flee basum Baguma a. Oti kĩĩ abumɔ Yudafɔ ahiãfɔ kĩ futo ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, ade yabɔa mpaɛ luwi daa lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Luwi a, kĩ kanaa olobe tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ a, ɛna hũhũ kamɛ kanyɛkatsa, ɛmɔ́ yededeede kĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi yabi ye kĩ, “Kornelio!”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Kornelio dã ye koee mɔ libe ade evia ye kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, litsa fɔanɛ?” Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Nɔ sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna mɔ mpaɛbɔ a wo Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Do bati bɛnaa Yopa bɛnaakpã onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Simon Petro bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Edeĩ nɛ Simon kĩ yakɛna bayaɛ tuvoli akũ adzuma nɛ mpo abuĩ a sɛkɛ̃.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Kĩ Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a dzakũ a, Kornelio vɛ ye kɔla basumunɛ nviã mɔ kawũ ɔnɔnɛ mɔa kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ kĩ yadzɔlɛ ye akũ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Ebi be lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a, ade edo be kĩ bɛnaa Yopa a.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a kĩ bɛkɔa bafuã Yopa a, Petro de ɛnaa lɛtsã ode nɛ awã kĩ ese a, ɛnaabɔa mpaɛ mɔ suwa tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã lɔkɔ a.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Kɔlɛ kakɔ ye ade ɛwɛ litsedi kĩ yanya a. Lɛmɛ kĩ bɛkatɔ funitsã a, ɛna kanyɛkatsa.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: Awã na ɛmɔ́ kĩ odetɔ́ binya, ade ɛmɔ́ kĩ bɛkayɔna litsedi ndɛ lima kpale kĩ bipini de nɛ atɔ ɛna a.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Bayaɛ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ, bɔɛ mɔ kɔvɔlabi lɛmɛ na nɛ lima a kamɛ.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ade ɔlɛdo odi bi ye kĩ “Petro Yidza, kɔ, kaba!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Oowo! Kusɛ! Boe Ɔlate yĩ onukpɛ̃ táta ɔyaɛ sɛkɛna kĩ boe Yudafɔ bɔakesĩ a da!”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɔlɛdo a buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma kpalɛ de akũ ni, tabu kĩ efĩĩ deĩ nɛ de akũ!”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Ɛna kanyɛkatsa kĩĩ titi ɛlalɛ, ade bɛvɛlɛ fɔtsa a bɛnamɛna ode a.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kakɛna Petro basa basa, ade evia ye akũ kĩ, “Litsa fukĩĩ atsa?” Ade ke kamɛ a mɔ bati a kĩ Kornelio do fɔtɔ a yawo ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a. Kĩ bɛmɔ́ tɔtɔ a kĩ bawɛ a, bɛnadza nɛ te onyukpɛ̃,

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: ade bɛkavia katse awã kĩ Simon Petro dzi a.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Petro buki edzimɔ lɔlɔ kamɛ kakɔna nɛ kanyɛkatsa a kayɔ kanu akũ, ade Baguma Hũhũ a bi ye kĩ, “Bati ɛlalɛ ya bɛlamɛna nɔ kawɛ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Yidza katsɛlamɔ be. Tavila be katomɛna kanaa kitonɛkĩ yĩ lido be.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Ade Petro duma ɛka ɛnabi be kĩ, “Yĩ nyɛ oti a kĩ milamɛna kawɛ a. Litsa sũ mitsɛ̃?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Kornelio kĩ ɛnyɛ Romafɔ kawũ ɔnɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ lido boe fɔtɔ. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ edzi kpĩĩ, yasum Baguma, ade Yudafɔ flee akpa ye obu a. Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi bi ye kĩ edo batɔ nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa etse nɔ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Foesũ Petro kpa be kasɛkɔ̃ nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ade ɛtɛ be ndɛ ye banɔvɔɛ̃ aye a. Kĩ kade nyina a, ye mɔ batɛ badonɛ badi kĩ bito Yopa mɔ batɔ a bipi osi.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Luwi lɛyade a, bɛnawo Kaisarea, awã kĩ Kornelio kpɔta ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati mɔ bakɔba badi bidzi bɛkadzɔlɛ Petro kaya a.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Kĩ Petro aawo nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ a, Kornelio natsɛlamɔ ye, eke aduli nɛ ye abɔa kayɔ ade ɛkɛna lɔlɔ a.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Petro dzɛla ye ebi ye kĩ, “Yidza kadza! Yĩ malo otidziwa manɛ.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkalɔmɛna a, Kornelio kpã ye ewomɛna nɛ awã kĩ bati nkaɛ a kpɔta bidzi badzɔlɛ be a.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Ade Petro bi be kĩ, “Minyina yededeede kĩ boe Yudafɔ mbla tákpa yĩ osi kĩ nnaa odi bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a sɛkɛ̃ ĩye nkɔa akũ mbɔamɔ be. Lɛmɛ Baguma nyɛ ɛtsa yĩ kĩ ntabu odima kĩ ye akũ tákpalɛ.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ kĩ batɔ a yavɛ yĩ a, ntásĩ. Litsa sũ odo bati kĩ bɛyaavɛ yĩ?”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Ade Kornelio bi ye kĩ, “Nviã fuwi ɛlalɛ, fɔ́ana ndɛ tɔdɔũ ɛlalɛ kamɛ, nkabɔa mpaɛ nɛ yĩ awã. Deedimɔapɛ onyole odi kĩ edeĩ nɛ tɛtadiɛ ovoe kĩ tata muamua kamɛ nɛ ɛta nɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ,

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: ade ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Kornelio, Baguma nu nɔ mpaɛbɔ, eyi nɔ sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna lɛmɛ ɔsũ. Do bati bɛnaa Yopa bɛnaakpã odi kĩ bavɛ Simon Petro. Ɛyase nɛ Simon kĩ yakɔa bayaɛ tuvoli kapamu fɔtsa nɛ mpo abuĩ a sɛkɛ̃.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ ndo batɔ nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ lɛtalɛta a, ade odi foe akũ ɔya mɔatsɛ lɛmɛ a. Foesũ boe flee budzi nɛ awĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ kamɛ kĩ yaaabi boe lɛtsa a kĩ ebi nɔ kĩ bi boe a.” Petro Kpalɛ Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ a Kamɛ.

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Ade Petro bi ye kĩ, “Finyaakĩĩ mmɔ́ ĩyi ɔsũ kĩ Baguma ládã bati anɛ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Mbom oti kĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ ɛkpa Baguma, yakɛna nɛ ye kawɛ akũ lɛmɛ ni, ade ye Baguma awɛ a.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Matɛ kado kĩ minu kalɛ a kĩ ɛma Israelfɔ kĩ fɔnyɛ lukudɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ Yesu Kristo akũ a. Ade ye lɛmɛ nyɛ katinya kamɛ bati flee Ɔlate a.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Ade minyina kalɛ kpale a kĩ fɔya nɛ Israelfɔ tite akũ, kĩ kiyi kayɔ nɛ Galilea, kito lɔkɔ a kĩ Yohanes de lɛtsã nɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ akũ a.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Minu lɛmɛ ayekĩ Baguma kɔa ye Hũhũ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ eko nɛ Yesu kĩ eto Nazaret a akũ. Ɛtsã emini nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ ɛkakɛna tuwĩ, ade ɛtsa bikĩ hũhũ kpa kaha be a, kitonɛkĩ Baguma tomɔ ye.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Boe bɔnyɛ adansifɔ bɔkpa fɔtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ Israelfɔ tite akũ mɔ Yerusalem a. Ade ke bɛkɔ ye nɛ ye kabɔa kamɛna nɛ asendua akũ a.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma dzɛla ye eto kanɔkpa nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ, ade ɛfɔ̃ ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa bati a.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ bati flee lɔmɔ ye, mbom boe a kĩ Baguma pau ndɛ adansifɔ a lɛmɔ́ ye. Budo awɛ bɔnya, bɔmɔa mɔ ye kĩ eyidza eto kanɔkpa liti a.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Ade boe edo kĩ bɔnaalɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, budi adansiɛ lɛmɛ kĩ Baguma kɛna Yesu batidziwa mɔ banɔkpa flee fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ flee lɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaatɛ ye edo ni, Baguma aakɔa ye tɔkpa ete ye nɛ Yesu dĩ akũ.” Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a Ka Eko Nɛ Bikĩ Bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a Lɛmɛ Akũ.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Petro kalɛ kɔdzɛla a, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ka eko nɛ bati a flee kĩ bidzi batsɛ ye fɔlɛtsa a akũ.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna Yudafɔ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bitomɛna Petro nɛ Yopa bɛyaa a saa, nɛ ayekĩ Baguma Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ka eko nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ lɛmɛ a akũ.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ binu kĩ bɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ ade bɛkamu Baguma Kpale a. Ade ke Petro lɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Ayekĩ bati kĩĩ tɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ndɛ ayekĩ boe malo bɔtɛ a sũ ni, bɔ́apuli busĩ kĩ bɔákɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛkpa osi kĩ bɛkɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo dĩ akũ. Ke liti a, bɛwã ye kĩ edzi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ fɔtɛ fuwi fudi.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Owi tábuna mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a mɔ batɛ badonɛ bɛbã nɛ Yudea nu kĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ tɛ Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ a.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Petro muniki ɛya nɛ Yerusalem a, Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛna nɛ awã a tátunɔ mɔ ye, ade bibi ye kĩ,

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: “Ɔnakɔa nɔ akũ ɔtsɛkamɔ banɔvɔɛ̃ kĩ bitómɛna Yudafɔ fɔtsa katsa a, ade odo awɛ ɔnyamɔ be malo a!”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Ade ke Petro nyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ ɛtsa be kĩ,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Lɔkɔ a kĩ nna nɛ Yopa nkabɔa mpaɛ a, nna kanyɛkatsa. Nɛ foe kamɛ a, mmɔ́ kĩ bɛkakɛna lima dzɛlɛwa baũ lidi kĩ bipini de nɛ atɔ ɛna, ade lɛka lɛyakpula nɛ yĩ ɔwɛ̃ a.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Bayaɛ kumɛkumɛ na nɛ lima kpale kĩĩ kamɛ, bayaɛ kĩ bideĩ abɔa ɛna, bikĩ bɛtsɛ̃ nɛ sɛmɛ akũ, mɔ kɔvɔlabi kĩ bɔába malo.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ade nnu ɔlɛdo odi ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Petro! Yidza kakɔ kaba!’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ nkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Oowo! Kusɛ! Boe Ɔlate, yĩ onukpɛ̃ táta ɔyaɛ kĩ boe Yudafɔ bɔakesĩ a da.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Ɔlɛdo a buki ɛlɛ kĩ, ‘Ntɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma kpalɛ de akũ ni, tabu kĩ efĩĩ deĩ nɛ de akũ.’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa kĩĩ kɛna lɔmɔ titi ɛlalɛ flee. Ke liti a, bɛvɛlɛ lima kpale kĩĩ mɔ bayaɛ a flee bɛnamɛna ode.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: “Ade lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ mɔ́ batɔ ɛlalɛ a kĩ bito Kaisarea bɛyaakpã yĩ a wo tɔtɔ a kĩ te kamɛ nse a.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Baguma Hũhũ a bi yĩ kĩ ntasĩ, mbom ntomɔ be bɔnaa. Batɛ badonɛ evũ a kĩ bito Yopa a, tomɔ yĩ bɔnawo oti a kĩ ye lido batɔ a tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ye malo ebi boe ayekĩ Baguma fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ nyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa ye, ebi ye kĩ, ‘Naawɛ odi kado ye nɛ Yopa ɛnaavɛ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Simon Petro.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Yaabi nɔ ayekĩ mimɔ nɔ tɔtɔ kamɛ bati flee míana katɛ.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: “Ke liti a, ĩyi kayɔ mɔ be Baguma fɔlɛtsa a kabi. Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ka eko nɛ be akũ ndɛ ayekĩ eko nɛ boe flee akũ nɛ kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Ade ke nte anɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate lɛ eyi kĩ, ‘Nuwĩnuwĩ, Yohanes kɔa mui ɛwɔla bati mui, lɛmɛ Baguma aakɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ɛwɔla mi mui.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Baguma lɛkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a futetsa kĩĩ ndɛ ayekĩ ɛkpa boe kĩ bɔtɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo budo sũ ni, yĩ nyɛ owei kĩ masua Baguma osi!”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Kĩ binu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a bɛfɔ̃ atsindze katɛ ade bimu Baguma bɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali, Baguma binya osi ɛkpa bati bɛbã lɛmɛ kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛna nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a!” Batɛ Badonɛ Nɛ Antiokia Pɔ Bibu.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
