translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ kĩ fukĩĩ bamɛ lamɛna kaya a, batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bilo tɛnya kakɛna nɛ Stefano wuda sũ a, lo bɛnaa Foenike, Kipro mɔ Antiokia tɛmatɔ a kamɛ. Bimini bɛlɛ Baguma kalɛ a bɛkpa Yudafɔ odi.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ batɛ badonɛ bɛbã a kĩ bito Kipro mɔ Kirene naa Antiokia bɛnabi Grikifɔ Baguma kalɛ a nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu akũ.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ boe Ɔlate kɔbɛ̃ na nɛ be kamɛ sũ bati pii tɛ bido.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Kĩ batɛ badonɛ nɛ Yerusalem nu lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ Antiokia a, bido Barnaba kĩ ɛnaa awã.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Kĩ ewo awã a, ɛmɔ́ ayekĩ Baguma bumɔ be a, kawɔlɛ de ye paa, ade ɛkpa be kɔdɛ kĩ bɛkɔa be tɔwɔlɛ flee bite nɛ boe Ɔlate akũ a.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Barnaba nyɛ oti wĩ, edeĩ katɛkado ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛmɛ tɛ ye kamɛ. Futo ye akũ sũ ni, bati pii tɛ boe Ɔlate bido.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Barnaba naa Tarso kĩ yanaawɛ Saulo nɛ awã a.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛmɔ ye a, ɛkpã ye bɛnaa Antiokia. Bɛna nɛ awã kateli mɔa flee, bɛkatsɛna bati pii katsa be fɔtsa. Ade Antiokia mɔ bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛvɛ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ Kristofɔ a.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ badi to Yerusalem bɛya nɛ Antiokia a.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Ade Agabo kĩ ɛnyɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa yidza ɛdza nɛ be katsɛnakɔ̃, ade nɛ Baguma Hũhũ kɔbɛ̃ onukpɛ̃ ni, ɛlɛ eyi kĩ kɔlɛ kpale aanɛ nɛ katinya flee kamɛ. Fukĩĩ yaya nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Klaudio nya Romafɔ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ a.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ batɛ badonɛ a yake otse kĩ baate fɔtsa fudi bɛkɔa bɛmɛna be bawaɛ̃ nɛ Yudea. Be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi te ayekĩ yaapuli.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛkpɔta fɔtsa kĩĩ a, bɛkɔa foe bɛkpa Barnaba mɔ Saulo kĩ bɛkɔa bɛma batɛ badonɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ a nɛ Yudea.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ, Lɛgã Herodes pi batɛ badonɛ a badi kĩ bɛkɔ be.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔ Yakobo kĩ ɛnyɛ Yohanes ɔwaɛ̃ a mɔ lɛpamɛ.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛmɔ kĩ kawɔlɛ de Yudafɔ a sũ ni, ɛfɔ̃ bipi Petro lɛmɛ. Ɛkɛna foe nɛ Abodoo Kĩ Ɛtáyila Fuwi Kanya a kamɛ.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa ye bido nɛ kɔla kamɛ, awã kĩ sodzafɔ ɛna ɛna na nɛ kutsu ɛna kamɛ kavɛmɛna akũ kadzɔlɛ ye akũ. Herodes nhihiɛ nyɛ kĩ yaakɔa ye ɛkpa kanade a bɛdzɛmɔ ye ntɛ bɛnya Wuda Katɛ̃ Luwi a biyua.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Foesũ sodzafɔ a kadzɔlɛ ye akũ kadzɔlɛ wĩ nɛ kɔla a kamɛ. Lɛmɛ batɛ badonɛ a kabɔa mpaɛ nɛ ye lɔlɔ kakpa Baguma mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Baguma Nyɛ Petro Nɛ Abãã Kɔla Kamɛ.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ a kĩ ke kade kanyina kamɛ Herodes aakɔa Petro ɛkpa bati a, Petro waa tida nɛ sodzafɔ nviã kayite. Bido ye nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ nviã ade sodzafɔ bɛbã dzɛ̃ nɛ opunu liti badzɔlɛ kɔla akũ a.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ɔhaĩ odi mɔanɛ nɛ kɔla a kamɛ. Ade ke ode Baguma fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi nɛ ɛdza nɛ Petro ɔwɛ̃ a. Ɛbɔa Petro nɛ lɔbɔtɛɛ enyina ye, ade ebi ye kĩ, “Yidza mɔatsɛ!” Lɛtalɛta nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ a nɛ́ nɛ ye akpɔ ɛtɔ nɛ tite.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Bɔɛ nɔ tɛtadiɛ, kado nɔ mpabua lɛmɛ.” Petro kɛna foe lɔmɔ, ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɔɛ nɔ atadiɛ kpale katsa akũ katomɔ yĩ.”

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Bɛtɛ̃ kawũ banɔnɛ katsu katɔnyade mɔ kayade a kĩ badzɔlɛ tunyukpɛ̃ akũ a. Kĩ biwo fɔbɔtsaɛ onyukpɛ̃ a kĩ babinya kawo nɛ ɔmatɔ kamɛ a, onyukpɛ̃ a mɔawɛ binya nɛ ye akũ akũ ade bɛtɛ̃ a. Kĩ bɛnaa lɔtɔ sukuloo a, Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ ye.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Kĩ anɛ kamɛ kpalɛ Petro a, ɛyanyi lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ ye akũ, ade ebi ye akũ kĩ, “Finyaakĩĩ, mmɔ́ kĩ ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ. Boe Ɔlate do ye ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ kĩ ɛyaanyɛ yĩ etomɛna Herodes awɛ kamɛ mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ Yudafɔ hihiɛ kĩ baakɛna yĩ a kamɛ!”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛmɔ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ ye akũ a, ɛdzakũ ɛnaa Maria kĩ ɛnyɛ Yohanes Marko ɔma awã. Bati pii tsɛna nɛ awã bɛkabɔa mpaɛ.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Petro dza ɛkabɔa kanya opunu a, katsolebi abawa kĩ bavɛ Roda yabinya kɔla a.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Kĩ eyi Petro odo ɔsũ a, kawɔlɛ de ye paa, foesũ ɛtábinya opunu a, mbom ewula atsɛ ɛnakpa bati a kanyi kĩ Petro dzɛ̃ nɛ opunu liti.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Bati a tátɛ bido foesũ bibi ye kĩ, “Kamɔ aamɔ mbɔɛɛ?” Lɛmɛ kĩ ɛdzɛmɛna kalɛ kĩ nuwĩ fɔanɛ sũ ni, bibi be akũ kĩ, “Ɔsa fɔ́anya ye Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa na flee Petro dzɛmɔ opunu a kabɔa. Kĩ bibinya opunu bɛmɔ́ ye a, akũ dzuli be.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Ete be awɛ kĩ bɛkɛna dĩĩ, ade ebi be ayekĩ boe Ɔlate nyɛ ye etomɛna kɔla a kamɛ a. Ebi be kĩ, “Mibi Yakobo mɔ batɛ badonɛ nkaɛ a lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a.” Ke liti a, ɛdzakũ nɛ awã ɛnaa kakɔ bɛbã.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina, kukũtila mɔ ɔtutũ kpale wo nɛ sodzafɔ a kamɛ nɛ kawakɔ̃ a, ade bɛkavia be akũ kĩ, “Ndia koũ Petro tsã?”

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Kĩ Herodes wɛ Petro kayɛla evia a, ɛfɔ̃ bipi sodzafɔ a bivia be onukpɛ̃ kamɛ tuwuli. Ɛkɔ be kɔtɔ ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔ be. Ke liti a, Herodes dzakũ nɛ Yudea ɛnaa Kaisarea, ɛnadzi nɛ awã fuwi fudi. Lɛgã Herodes Ku.

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ Herodes na kalɔkpa nɛ Tirofɔ mɔ Sidomfɔ akũ. Foesũ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃. Bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛnamɔ Blasto, kĩ ɛnyɛ Herodes tɔtɔ akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ, kĩ eke Herodes aduli ɛkpa be. Bɛwɛ kĩ tɛmatɔ nviã a tɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Herodes, kitonɛkĩ ye tite akũ bana funitsã katomɛna.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Nɛ luwi a kĩ Herodes yi ɛkpa be akũ a, edo ye fɔgãtsa edzi nɛ ye lɛgãkpo akũ, ade ɛkabi ɔmaĩ a fɔlɛtsa a.

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ yabɔa Herodes emuni, kitonɛkĩ ɛtákpa Baguma obu. Ewo batsɛ bɛba ye kamɛ kamɛ, ade ke eku a.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Kĩ Barnaba mɔ Saulo yua be adzuma a kĩ bido be a, bɛdzakũ nɛ Yerusalem ade bɛkpã Yohanes Marko bibumɔ be akũ a.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ mɔ fɔtsa batsanɛ badi na nɛ batɛ badonɛ a kamɛ nɛ Antiokia. Ade be nyɛ Barnaba mɔ Simeon kĩ bavɛ Oti Ɔɔwa, Lukio kĩ eto Kirene tite akũ, Manahen kĩ bɛmɔ Lɛgã Herodes bɛdɔɔla nɛ be tibisɛ̃ kamɛ, mɔ Saulo.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛna nɛ awã bɛkamu boe Ɔlate, bɛkasui onukpɛ̃ lɛmɛ a, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa bi be kĩ, “Minyɛ Barnaba mɔ Saulo miyi lite lɔkɔa bɛnaakɛna adzuma a kĩ foesũ nvɛ be a.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bisui onukpɛ̃ bɛbɔa mpaɛ, bite awɛ nɛ be akũ ade bɛnyɛ be osi a. Barnaba Mɔ Saulo Naa Kipro.

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa lido be sũ ni, bɛnaa ɔmatɔ a kĩ bavɛ Seleukia kamɛ. Awã na bidzi ɔkɔlɔ binyina bɛnaa Kipro tite kĩ mui tsua bisinya te a akũ.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Salami ɔmatɔ kamɛ a, bɛnalɛ Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃. Yohanes Marko lɛmɛ tomɔ be ndɛ be obumɛnanɛ aye.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtsã mpo akũ bɛnawo Pafo ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, bɛtsɛlamɔ Yudani odi kĩ yaveli tɔɔta. Bavɛ ye kĩ Yesuobi, ade ɛbɔɛ ye akũ kĩ ɛnyɛ onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ a.

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: Ɔwanɛ kĩĩ mɔ tite a akũ ɔdãnɛ kĩ bavɛ Sergio Paulo nyɛ bakɔba. Abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ tsua linebi. Ɛvɛ Barnaba mɔ Saulo bɛnaa ye awã kitonɛkĩ ɛwɛ kĩ yatse Baguma fɔlɛtsa a.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ bɛnaa awã a, ɔwanɛ a kĩ babuki kavɛ Elima nɛ Griki tide kamɛ a, tɛ atsindze mɔ be. Ɛwɛ kĩ yadani ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a adzuni nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Saulo mɔ Barnaba lɛ a akũ.

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kɔbɛ̃ tɛ Saulo kĩ babuki kavɛ Paulo kamɛ ade eke anɛ nɛ ɔwanɛ a akũ fɛɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nɔ Abonsam obi! Ɔnyɛ okesĩnɛ ɔkpa tuwĩ tidi nyɛ tidi kakɛna. Tɛdake mɔ tɔkpa yi nɔ kamɛ flee. Luwi daa abɔa mbɔdi kĩ adani boe Ɔlate ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a liti mɔ lɔtɔ!

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Dã, boe Ɔlate aavɛlɛ nɔ sɔtɔ finyaakĩĩ. Yaafɔ̃ oku anɛ, ade ɔláabuki ɔmɔ́ suwa kɛnaawo lɔkɔ lidi a.” Lɛtalɛta Elima anɛ kamɛ ke ɔtũ, ade ke ebuki yaámɔ fɔkɔ a. Ɛkatalɛ fɔkɔ kĩ yawɛ odi kĩ epi ye nɛ kɔkpɔ.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Kĩ abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a, ɛtɛ edo kitonɛkĩ fɔtsa katsa nɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ wĩ a akũ kɛna ye wãwã paa. Barnaba Mɔ Paulo Naa Antiokia Nɛ Pisidia.

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Paulo mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a wo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ bito Pafo bɛnaa Perge ɔmatɔ a kĩ edeĩ nɛ Pamfilia tite kamɛ. Ade awã mɔ Yohanes Marko dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ be emuniki ɛnaa Yerusalem a.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Kĩ bito Perge a, bɛyawo Antiokia kĩ edeĩ nɛ Pisidia. Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ a ni, bɛnaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a bɛnadzi nɛ bati a kamɛ.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɛkã fɔlɛtsa nɛ Mose mbla, mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ tuvoli kamɛ biyua a, katsɛnakɔ̃ banɔkɔɛ̃ a bi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ntɛ mideĩ kadi kĩ míalɛ mido boe kɔbɛ̃ ni, osi waa, milɛ faa.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Ade Paulo yidza ɛdza ete be awɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ Israelfɔ mɔ mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Israelfɔ lɛmɛ miasum Baguma a, mitse katse wĩ!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Israelfɔ Baguma lɛpau boe bawanyɔ, ade ɛkɛna be ɔmaĩ kpale nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛnya banɔvɔɛ̃ nɛ Egipte a. Ye mɔawɛ lɛnyɛ be etomɛna Egipte tɛsate kamɛ mɔ ye kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Foesũ midã kadã wĩ ayekĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ biyi a lɛlaaya nɛ mi akũ.

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Ɛwɔla fude evũkɔnɔ bati nɛ Kanan tite akũ, ade ɛkɔa te ɛkpa Israelfɔ kĩ tɛnya be adzapadiɛ a.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma dzɛla ye nɛ lukpo a akũ, ade ɛkɔa Dawid ɛkɛna lɛgã a. Ade lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ nɛ ye akũ nyɛ kĩ, ‘Yese obi Dawid nyɛ oti kĩ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ awɛ, kitonɛkĩ yaakɛna nɛ yĩ kawɛ akũ.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: “Ade Dawid kasinu a kamɛ Baguma nyɛ Ɔtɛnɛ a kĩ ɛnyɛ Yesu ɛkpa Israelfɔ ndɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ eyi a.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Fɔmɔ Yesu aayi ye adzuma kayɔ a, Yohanes mui ɔwɔlanɛ a de lɛtsã ebi bati Israelfɔ flee kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Kĩ Yohanes aawũna ye adzuma kakɛna a, evia bati a kĩ, ‘Mikɔna kĩ yĩ nyɛ Oti a Kĩ Bɛbɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a mbɔɛɛ? Oowo! Lɛmɛ etomɛna yĩ nɛ liti yaya, ade ntáfɛta kĩ mawũnya mpabua nɛ ye abɔa malo a.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Israelfɔ, Abraham babi mɔ bawa, mɔ mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ lɛmɛ miasum Baguma, bɛkɔa katɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ bɛma boe flee.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ Yerusalem mɔ be banɔkɔɛ̃ a tányi kĩ ye nyɛ Ɔtɛnɛ, ĩye binu Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bakã nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lidi nyɛ lidi akũ kayɔ sũ ni, fɔlɛtsa a ya nɛ foe kamɛ lɔmɔ, ade bɛkɔ ye kɔtɔ a.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɛkɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa biyua ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ eyi nɛ ye akũ a, bɛnyɛ ye nɛ asendua akũ bɛnabila nɛ linikɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna kanɔkpa ebuki edzi nkpã.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi pii na kamɛ ni, ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa bikĩ bɛmɔ ye bɛtsã bito Galilea bɛnaa Yerusalem. Ade be nyɛ ye adansifɔ bɛkpa Israelfɔ finyaakĩĩ a.

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: “Finyaakĩĩ bɔkɔa Kalɛ Wĩ a bɔama mi, kĩ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi nɛ boe bawanyɔ lɔkɔ a ya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ boe lɔkɔ. Baguma fɔ̃ Yesu yidza edzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ Dawid tsɛlɛ nɛ ɔnɔ ɔyade a kamɛ kĩ, ‘Nɔ nyɛ yĩ obi. Nviã kĩĩ nkɛna nɔ Ɔlɛga.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Ade lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi nɛ ye kadzɛla katomɛna kanɔkpa nyɛ kĩ ye sukɛna lɛláavɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ da. Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Maakɔa nhila kpalɛwa a kĩ nlɛ ĩyi nkpa Dawid a nkpa nɔ.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ nɛ Dawid ɔnɔ bɛbã kamɛ kĩ, ‘Ɔláafɔ̃ nɔ oti kpalɛwa a lɛvɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “Kĩ Dawid kɛna nɛ Baguma fɔwɛtsa akũ lɔkɔ a kĩ edzi nkpã eyua a, eku bɛbɔɛ ye bibila nɛ ye bawanyɔ ɔwɛ̃, ade ɛvɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ oti a kĩ Baguma dzɛla etomɛna kanɔkpa a sukɛna távɔ̃ nɛ linikɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bɔwɛ kĩ minyi kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ Yesu akũ mɔ bide lɛtsã nɛ Baguma tɔkpa kakɔakate a bɛkpa mi.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛ kĩ, ‘Midã mi a kĩ miadi kalɛ a nsekuo! Fɔkɛna mi saa lɔkɔa miwɔ. Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ makɛna nviã kĩĩ a ntɛ odi nyɛ foe kayɔ ɛtsa mi malo, miláatɛ foe mido!’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo mɔ Barnaba adzakũ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, bati a bi be kĩ bibuki bɛya nɛ Kawɛya Luwi a kĩ laayaa akũ, lɔkɔa bɛyaaabi be fɔlɛtsa a bibu.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛpansam nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, Yudafɔ pii mɔ bikĩ bɛdani bɛkɛna Yudafɔ bavila Baguma lɛmɛ a, tomɔ Paulo mɔ Barnaba. Fɔtɔ banyanɛ a dɛ be kĩ bɛdza pintii nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ a kĩ edeĩ ɛkpa be a sũ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lɛyade akũ a, kite sukuloo ɔmatɔ a flee yabɔa obũ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a kĩ batse boe Ɔlate kalɛ a.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔ́ bati lidede kĩĩ odu nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a, anɛ bɛɛlɛ be. Bɛvɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ Paulo nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de ɛlɛ a akũ ade bivũna ye a.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo mɔ Barnaba lɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ bɔnya lɔtɔ bubi mi Yudafɔ Baguma kalɛ kĩĩ. Lɛmɛ kĩ misĩ ke kĩ mitákpɔla mikpa nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a sũ ni, bɔkɔa ke bɔama bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ade ɔlɛdo kĩ Baguma kpa boe kĩ, ‘Nkɛna nɔ ndɛ ɔsɛdza nkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a ayekĩ katinya kamɛ bati flee aana katɛ.’ ”

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Kĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, kawɔlɛ de be, ade bimu boe Ɔlate kalɛ a. Ade bikĩ Baguma pau kĩ bɛna nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a, dani bɛkɛna batɛ badonɛ a.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Baguma Kalɛ a gba nɛ tite a na flee akũ.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Yudafɔ a fɔ̃ batsole badi kĩ obu deĩ nɛ be akũ kĩ basum Baguma lɛmɛ, mɔ ɔmatɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a biyidza bɛdza nɛ Paulo mɔ Barnaba akũ, ade bɛblɔmɛna be nɛ be kayite a.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Paulo mɔ Barnaba anɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a, bɛkpa be abɔa kudu biko ɔmatɔ a bɛkɔa batsa kĩ bisĩ be. Ke liti a, bɛdzakũ bɛnaa Ikonio.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ de batɛ badonɛ nɛ Antiokia ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛmɛ yi be kamɛ paa a.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Ikonio a, Paulo mɔ Barnaba naa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃. Ayekĩ bɛlɛ kalɛ wĩ a fɔ̃ Yudafɔ mɔ Grikifɔ pii tɛ bido.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Ade Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛtátɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ wĩ kĩĩ bido a kɔa ɔdzɛ bido nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a mɔ Paulo mɔ Barnaba kayite.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ kĩĩ kahɔ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ, bɛlɛ kalɛ boe Ɔlate akũ mɔ kɔbɛ̃. Ade ɛnyɛ ye kawɛ ɛtsa ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɛna awãwã mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa pii a.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Fɔyaya kĩ bati nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ sɛ̃ nɛ kutsu nviã kamɛ, badi tomɔ Yudafɔ a ade badi lɛmɛ tomɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Listra a bɛmɔ́ onyole odi kĩ eveliveli. Kito nɛ ye lɛmawi ɛtápuli eyidza ɛtsã da.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Edzi nɛ awã ɛkatse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Paulo kalɛ a. Kĩ Paulo mɔ eyi ɔsũ kĩ oti kĩĩ tɛ edo kĩ baapuli bɛtsa ye sũ ni,

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: ɛdã ɔsɔnɛ a koee ade ɛlɛ kɔɔba kĩ, “Yidza kadza ɔkpalɛ!” Onyole kĩĩ ta eyidza ade edo kato katɛ̃ awɛ a.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã batse kalɛ a mɔ́ lɛtsa a kĩ Paulo kɛna a, bɛbɔa fɔwɔla nɛ Likaonia tide kamɛ kĩ, “Baviɔ̃ banɛ, bɛka bɛya ndɛ batidziwa aye nɛ boe kayite!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Foesũ bite Barnaba dĩ kĩ Zeus. Bite Paulo lɛmɛ kĩ Herme, kitonɛkĩ ade ye lɛdza ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Adeke Zeus oviɔ̃late a kĩ ye kuviɔ̃la kpale dza nɛ ɔmatɔ ɔbaɛ̃ a, nakpã banantsue ɛbɔɛ flawasi lɛka lɛmɛ ɛyamɛna nɛ ɔmatɔ a kɔbã onyukpɛ̃ kĩ bayaabɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa fɔtɔ banyanɛ a.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo mɔ Barnaba nu lɛtsa kĩ bati a wɛ kĩ bakɛna a, bɛyɛlɛ be akũ tɛtadiɛ. Foesũ biwula atsɛ biwo nɛ bati a kamɛ, bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kĩ,

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: “Nda amɛ, nde sũ miakɛna fukĩĩ odu? Boe malo batidziwa bɔanɛ ndɛ mi aye. Bɔya nɛ awĩ kĩ bɔkɔa kalɛ wĩ a bɔma mi. Mifɔ̃ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔlá kayɔ kakɛna lɔkɔa midani mimu Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a. Ade ye lɛkɛna tite mɔ ode, mpo mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ foe kamɛ a flee a!

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Kɔkɔɛ a ɛnyɛ anɛ nɛ batidziwa flee akũ ɛfɔ̃ bɛkakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ bawɛ a.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ebuki yanyɛ ye fɔkɛlatsa katsa boe kĩ fɔatsa kĩ nuwĩ edzi nkpã. Lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ fɔtsa deĩ kĩ fɔakai boe ye tudzuma wĩ a. Yakpa boe oni lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ, fudutsa nɛ lɔkɔ wĩ akũ, funitsã mɔ kawɔlɛ lɛmɛ.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ lɔmɔ malo ni, fɔna ɔsĩ fɔmɔ bati a fɔ̃ afɔliɛ kabɔa kakpa be.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Ade Yudafɔ badi to Antiokia nɛ Pisidia, mɔ Ikonio bɛyadani bati a adzuni a. Bɛfɔ̃ bɛtalɛ Paulo boe bɛvɛlɛ ye bɛnɛmɛna nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ nɛ kĩ bɛkɔna kĩ eku sũ.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ batɛ badonɛ a yatsɛna nɛ ye akũ a, eyidza ɛdza ade emuniki ɛnaa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a. Kĩ kade nyina a, bɛmɔ Barnaba bɛdzakũ bɛnaa Derbe. Paulo Mɔ Barnaba Muniki Bɛnaa Antiokia Nɛ Siria Tite Akũ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Paulo mɔ Barnaba de lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nɛ Derbe, ade bɛna batɛ badonɛ pii nɛ awã a. Bimuniki bito awã bɛnaa Listra, Ikonio mɔ Antiokia nɛ Pisidia.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Awã na bɛdɛ batɛ badonɛ a, bido be kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ kĩ bidi ɔnɔkɔali nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ. Ade bibi be lɛmɛ kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ bɔtsã lubo kamɔ pii kamɛ fɔmɔ bɔawo nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ade ke Paulo mɔ Barnaba pau lɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛkpa asɔli adi nyɛ adi a. Bɛtsã mpaɛbɔ mɔ onukpɛ̃ kasui kamɛ bɛkɔa be bido nɛ boe Ɔlate a kĩ bɛtɛ bido a awɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bimini nɛ Pisidia tɛmatɔ kamɛ biyua a, bɛdani bɛnaa Pamfilia tite kamɛ.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ kalɛ wĩ a nɛ Perge fɔmɔ bɛtɛ̃ bɛnaa Atalia.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Ade awã binyina mpo bɛnaa Antiokia, awã kĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛkɔa be bido nɛ Baguma awɛ kamɛ nɛ adzuma a kĩ bɛkɛna biyua finyaa a.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Antiokia a, bɛkpɔta batɛ badonɛ a flee nɛ awã, bɛbɔa be osi akũ amandiɛ. Ade bibi be fɔtsa flee a kĩ Baguma kɛna ɛtsãmɛna be akũ mɔ ayekĩ ebinya katɛkado osi ɛkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Bidzi nɛ batɛ badonɛ nɛ Antiokia sɛkɛ̃ owi buna kɔlaa.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo mɔ Barnaba na nɛ Antiokia a, bati badi to Yudea bɛyatsa batɛ badonɛ nɛ awã fɔtsa kĩ, “Miláana katɛ mbɔɛntɛ bitsua mi lɛkpati nɛ Mose mbla a onukpɛ̃.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo mɔ Barnaba vɛlɛ foe mɔ be mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Nɛ foesũ bɛnyɛ batɛ badonɛ a badi kĩ bito Antiokia kĩ bitomɔ be bɛnaa Yerusalem lɔkɔa bɛmɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yerusalem a bɛwɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Foesũ batɛ badonɛ a yi be osi. Bɛnatsã Foenike mɔ Samaria bɛsɛla batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã, ade bibi be ayekĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kɔa be akũ bɛkpa Baguma a. Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ de be kawɔlɛ paa.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Yerusalem a, batɛ badonɛ a, fɔtɔ banyanɛ a mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a flee tɛ be mɔ kawɔlɛ. Paulo mɔ Barnaba bɔa be amandiɛ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma kɛna ɛtsãmɛna be akũ a flee.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Farisifɔ batɛ badonɛ badi yidza bɛdza ade bɛlɛ kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a bitsua fɔkpati lɔkɔa bɛkɛna nɛ Mose mbla a akũ.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ banɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a tsɛna bɛkɔna fɔlɛtsa a akũ.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛsa foe kamɛ fɛɛ a, Petro yidza ɛdza ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, miawɛ minyina kĩ lɔkɔ lidi Baguma pau yĩ etomɛna mi kamɛ kĩ nnaabi bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a Kalɛ Wĩ a, ayekĩ be malo baanu ke bɛtɛ bido lɛmɛ.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Ade Baguma kĩ enyina otidziwa kakɔna a nyɛ ye kawɛ ɛtsa nɛ kĩ ɛkpa be Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ndɛ ayekĩ ɛkpa boe malo a.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Ɛtátsa ntsitsemu adima nɛ bɔmɔ́ be kayite, kitonɛkĩ ɛtɛ be nɛ ayekĩ bɛtɛ bido sũ, ade ɛkɔa be tɔkpa ete be ndɛ boe aye a.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, nde sũ bɔasɔ Baguma bɔdã? Nde sũ bɔkɔa fɔlɔtsa a kĩ bɔmɔ́ boe bawanyɔ bɔtápuli bɔlɔ a bɔate nɛ batɛ badonɛ kĩĩ akũ?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Oowo! Ndɛ ayekĩ bɔtɛ budo kĩ boe a flee bɔna katɛ fɔtsã boe Ɔlate Yesu kɔnyakamɔ kamɛ a, ade ayea be malo bɛna katɛ a.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ade ke be flee bɛkɛna dĩĩ, bitse Paulo mɔ Barnaba amandiɛ kabɔa nɛ ayekĩ Baguma tsã be akũ ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa ɛkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛbɔa amandiɛ biyua a, Yakobo lɛmɛ yidza ɛdza ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mitse!

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon Petro bi boe ayekĩ Baguma nya lɔtɔ ɛnyɛ ye kawɛ ɛtsa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, ade ɛpau be badi kĩ bɛnya ye bade a.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Nɛ fukĩĩ liti ni, maamuniki ĩya. Maayalɔla Dawid lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kadzɛla, ade maalɔla fɔtsa a kĩ fubu kade nɛ awã kake a, lɔkɔa awã libuki kɛdza pintii.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Ade bati nkaɛ a aaya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kĩ nvɛ kĩ bɛnya yĩ bade a aatomɔ yĩ liti.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Foesũ nɛ yĩ, Yakobo, kakɔna onukpɛ̃ ni, kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔaha bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛkɔa be akũ bakpa Baguma a.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Mbom mifɔ̃ bɔtsɛlɛ ovoli bɔma be kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fukĩĩ bamɛ ndɛ bayaɛ kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ kaba, bɛfɔ̃ litsolewɛ mɔ linyolewɛ, bɛfɔ̃ ɔyaɛ kĩ bɛtátsua ye sɔmɔ kaba mɔ babɔanɛ kanya.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kito anɛ kɔkɔɛ a bakã Mose mbla a kĩ ɛtsɛlɛ eyi a nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lidi nyɛ lidi akũ nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ade bade lɛtsã nɛ ye fɔlɛtsa akũ nɛ ɔmatɔ nyɛ ɔmatɔ kamɛ a.” Bɛtsɛlɛ Ovoli Bɛma Bikĩ Bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ Batɛ Badonɛ a.

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Ade ke fɔtɔ banyanɛ, banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ batɛ badonɛ a flee kɛna onukpɛ̃ mɔa kĩ bɛnyɛ be kamɛ badi bibumɔ Paulo mɔ Barnaba bɛnaa Antiokia a. Bɛpau bati nviã kĩ batɛ badonɛ a akpa be obu paa. Ade be nyɛ Yuda kĩ bibuki bavɛ Barnaba mɔ Sila a.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Ovoli a kĩ bɛnɛɛ banamɛna a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa nyɛ kĩ, Ovoli kĩĩ to boe fɔtɔ banyanɛ, banɔkɔɛ̃, mɔ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yerusalem sɛkɛ̃. Bɔawɔ̃na mi, boe bawaɛ̃ kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kĩ mito Antiokia, Siria mɔ Kilikia.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Bunu kĩ batɛ badonɛ badi to boe kamɛ nɛ awĩ bɛyabi mi fɔlɛtsa fudi kĩ fɔha mi fudo ɔtutũ lɛmɛ nɛ mi kamɛ. Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ boe lido be.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Budo Yuda mɔ Sila kĩ be mɔa awɛ bibi mi fɔlɛtsa a kĩ foe bɔtsɛlɛ a.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a mɔ boe mɔawɛ a bɔmɔ́ buyi ɔsũ kĩ kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔakɔa fɔlɔtsa kpale bɔlɔla mi kɛba tɛbla sĩwa kĩĩ.

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: Ade te nyɛ kĩ mitanya funitsã kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃, mitanya babɔanɛ, mitaba ɔyaɛ kĩ bɛtátsua ye sɔmɔ, mipi mi akũ nɛ linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ kamɛ. Ntɛ mipi mi akũ limii ni, katinya kamɛ aalɛ kɛkpa mi. Midzi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛnyɛ batɔ kĩĩ osi bɛnaa Antiokia. Bɛnakpɔta batɛ badonɛ a flee nɛ be katsɛnakɔ̃ a, ade bɛkɔa ovoli a bɛkpa be a.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ de bati a nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛkã nɛ ovoli a kamɛ a sũ.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Yuda mɔ Sila kĩ bɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ nyɛ fɔlɛtsa pii kayɔ bɛtsa be nɛ lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ kamɛ, bido be kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Bidzi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ bɛtɛ fuwi fudi, ade batɛ badonɛ a nyɛ be osi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ kĩ bimuniki bɛnaa Yerusalem a.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Paulo mɔ Barnaba dzi nɛ Antiokia sukuloo. Bɛmɔ bati bɛbã pii bɛtsa fɔtsa ade bɛkade lɛtsã nɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ a akũ a. Paulo Mɔ Barnaba Sɛ̃ Tisi.

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi fudi liti ni, Paulo bi Barnaba kĩ, “Fɔ̃ bumuniki bɔnaasɛla boe bawaɛ̃ nɛ tɛmatɔ a kĩ bɔtsã te kamɛ bude lɛtsã nɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ a akũ a, lɔkɔa bɔdã ni nde bidzi.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Barnaba tunɔ ade ɛwɛ kĩ bakpã Yohanes Marko bibumɔ be akũ a.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo tátunɔ kɔlaa kitonɛkĩ, kĩ bɛnaa Pamfilia a, Yohanes Marko dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ be nɛ awã ade ɛtábumɔ be biyua adzuma a.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Bati nviã kĩĩ vɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa a mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ade fɔfɔ̃ bɛyalɛ a. Barnaba kpã Marko ade binyina mpo bɛnaa Kipro a.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Paulo lɛmɛ kpã Sila ade batɛ badonɛ a kɔa be bido nɛ boe Ɔlate awɛ kĩ ɛmɔ be kɔnya fɔmɔ bɛdzakũ a.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Ɛtsã Siria mɔ Kilikia tite kamɛ ɛkado tɛsɔli nɛ awã a kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ade Paulo dzakũ ɛnaa ɔmatɔ odi kĩ bavɛ Derbe, ade ɛtɛ̃ ɛnaa Listra lɛmɛ a. Ade awã ɛnamɔ ɔtɛ odonɛ odi kĩ bavɛ Timoteo a. Ɔma nyɛ Yudani kĩ ɛtɛ Kristo edo. Lɛmɛ ɔlɛga kɔ̃ nyɛ Grikini.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ flee a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Listra mɔ Ikonio bɔa Timoteo dĩ wĩ.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ade ye Paulo wɛ kĩ yakpã ɛnamɛna a, foesũ etsua ye lɛkpati, kitonɛkĩ Yudafɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ kayite a na nyina kĩ ɔlɛga nyɛ Grikini.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɛdzakũ a, bɛtsã bimini nɛ tɛmatɔ a kamɛ, bibi be tɛbla a kĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ nɛ Yerusalem ke otse nɛ te akũ a. Bɛdɛ be kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ te akũ.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Foesũ luwi daa bati a kapɔ ade be katɛkado lɛmɛ na ɔsĩ kibu a. Paulo Na Kanyɛkatsa Nɛ Troa.

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bɛtsã Frigia mɔ Galatia tite a kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a tákpa be osi kĩ bɛnaade lɛtsã kalɛ wĩ a akũ nɛ Asia tite akũ.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Misia tite ɔbaɛ̃ a, bɛwɛ kĩ bawo nɛ Bitinia tite kamɛ, lɛmɛ Yesu Hũhũ a tákpa be osi.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Foesũ bimuniki bɛtsã Misia bɛnawo Troa ɔmatɔ a kĩ ɛwaa nɛ mpo abuĩ a kamɛ.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Ke lɛnyɛ a, Paulo na kanyɛkatsa nɛ kɔda kamɛ. Nɛ kɔda a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ onyole odi kĩ eto Makedonia, ɛdza ɛlamɛna ye aduli kake kĩ, “Nyina mpo kaya nɛ Makedonia kayaabumɔ boe!”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo na kanyɛkatsa kĩĩ sũ ni, bɔmanɛ akũ bupi Makedonia osi, kitonɛkĩ fɔatsa kĩ Baguma lɛvɛ boe kĩ bɔnaade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nɛ awã. Lidia Dani Ɔwɔlɛ Kamɛ Nɛ Filipi.

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Ade ke budzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ nɛ Troa bunyina kpĩĩ bɔnaa Samotrake a. Kĩ kade nyina a, buwo Neapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Kĩ buto awã a, bɔnawo Filipi kĩ ɛnyɛ Makedonia ɔmatɔ kpale flee a kamɛ. Awã lɛmɛ nyɛ tite kĩ Romafɔ adãmɛna te akũ. Budzi nɛ awã fuwi fudi.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi akũ a, bɔnɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bɔnaa ɔwɔ abuĩ, awã kĩ bɔkɔna kĩ Yudafɔ atsɛna kabɔa mpaɛ. Ade budzi nɛ awã bɔkatsa batsole a kĩ bɛkpɔta nɛ awã Baguma kalɛ a.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Bavɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ eto Tiatira kĩ Lidia. Yanɔ tilima ngbã, ade ɛnyɛ oti kĩ yasum Baguma yededeede a. Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛna nɛ awã ɛkatse boe a, Baguma binya ye ɔwɔlɛ kĩ ɛtɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Paulo kalɛ a edo.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Ade kĩ ye mɔ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati flee bɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ a, eke boe aduli kĩ, “Ntɛ mitunɔ kĩ ntɛ boe Ɔlate ndo paa kɔ̃ ni, miyaadzi nɛ yĩ tɔtɔ kamɛ” Ɛdza nɛ boe akũ flee kɛnawo kĩ butunɔ bɔnaa ye sɛkɛ̃. Bido Paulo Mɔ Sila Nɛ Kɔla.

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Luwi mɔa lidi kĩ bɔanaa mpaɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a, bɔtsɛlamɔ oyitsɔ̃ abawa adi kĩ hũhũ kpa deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ, fɔafɔ̃ yapuli kadã kanɔkɔnɛ. Nɛ foesũ yakɛna sika baũ kakpa ye balate.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Oyitsɔ̃ kĩĩ tomɔ boe mɔ Paulo mɔ fɔwɔla kabɔa kĩ, “Baguma Kpale basumunɛ bati kĩĩ anɛ, ade bɛya nɛ awĩ kĩ bayaatsa mi nkpã kana osi a!”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ na nkɔsu fuwi pii. Ade kĩ fuwo Paulo odo a, ɛdani ebi hũhũ a kĩ etomɔ oyitsɔ̃ a kĩ, “Nɛ Yesu Kristo dĩ akũ, mabi nɔ kĩ nɛ́ nɛ oti a kamɛ!” Lɛtalɛta hũhũ kpa a nɛ́ nɛ ye kamɛ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Kĩ ye balate a mɔ́ kĩ osi a kĩ ye akũ batsã kana sika yɛla be a, bipi Paulo mɔ Sila bɛma fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ a nɛ kɔya.

english_verse: They told the officials, “These Jews are upsetting our city!
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ nɛ kɔya kĩ, “Bati kĩĩ nyɛ Yudafɔ kĩ bɛyaabula boe ɔmatɔ a kade.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: Batsa bati fɔtsa kĩ boe ndɛ Romafɔ bɔláapuli bɔtɛ foe.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Ade bati kɔdabu a yidza bɛdza bisinya Paulo mɔ Sila, bido be katsootsoo awɛ a. Fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a fɔ̃ bɛyɛlɛ tɛtadiɛ nɛ be akũ, ade bɛkpa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bibo be mɔ kutse a.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Kĩ bibo be kabo kpa biyua a, bɛnado be nɛ kɔla, ade bibi ekĩ yadãmɛna bikĩ bɛwaa kɔla akũ a kĩ ɛbalɛ Paulo mɔ Sila kabalɛ wĩ.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Kĩ bibi ye lɔmɔ sũ ni, ɛnado be nɛ kamɛ flee a, ade ɛbɔa be agba a.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ kayite a, kĩ Paulo mɔ Sila kabɔa mpaɛ mɔ tɔnɔ kasã kakpa Baguma a, kɔlabawanɛ nkaɛ a katse be.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Ade deedimɔapɛ tite kuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ade kɔla a flee kuna kɔnake a. Lɛtalɛta kɔla tupunu a flee binya ade nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ a wɔlɔ nɛ kɔlabawanɛ a flee akũ tɛtɔ a.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Kĩ kɔlabawanɛ akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a nyinya a, ɛmɔ kĩ kɔla a tunyukpɛ̃ flee waa luto. Ɛkɔna kĩ kɔlabawanɛ a nɛ bilo sũ ni, ɛbɔlɛ lɛpamɛ ɛnyɛ kĩ yatsɔɛ ye akũ ekɔ.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo bɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Takɛna nɔ akũ lɛtsama. Boe a flee budeĩ nɛ awĩ!”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Kɔla bawanɛ akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a fɔ̃ bɛma ye ɔsɛdza ade nɛ kukũtila kamɛ ewula atsɛ ɛnatɔ nɛ Paulo mɔ Sila abɔa kamɛ.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Ade ke ɛnyɛ be nɛ kɔla a kamɛ, evia be kĩ, “Nda amɛ, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna nna katɛ?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Bibi ye kĩ, “Tɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu kado lɔkɔa mimɔ nɔ tɔtɔ kamɛ bati flee míana katɛ.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ade ke bɛnaa bɛnade lɛtsã nɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ akũ bɛkpa ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati a flee a.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ a na, ɛkpã be ɛnasu be fɔkpa, ade ye mɔ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati bɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ a.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ɛkpã Paulo mɔ Sila ɛnamɛna ye awã, ade ɛkpa be funitsã bɛnya a. Ye mɔ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati a flee bɛmɔ́ kawɔlɛ paa kitonɛkĩ bɛtɛ Baguma bido.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, ɔmatɔ a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ do fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛnabi kɔlabawanɛ akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a kĩ, “Fɔnɛ bati a awɛ bɛnaa.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Kɔla bawanɛ akũ ɔdãmɛnanɛ a bi Paulo mɔ Sila kĩ, “Fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a bo nfɔnɛ mi awɛ midzakũ. Foesũ minaa faa nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo bi batɔ a kĩ, “Bibo boe nɛ kanade nɛ lɔkɔ kĩ bɛtádzɛmɔ boe bɛkɔ boe kɔtɔ, ade bido boe nɛ kɔla kamɛ a, titɔ kĩ Romafɔ bɔanɛ. Ade finyaa kɔ̃ bawɛ kĩ bɔdzakũ nɛ kabɛla kamɛ? Oowo! Ke kɔ̃ nviã nɛ awĩ! Fɔ̃ be mɔawɛ a bɛyaanyɛ boe!”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Ade ke fɔtɔ banyanɛ a muniki bɛnabi fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a ɔlɛdo a. Kĩ binu kĩ Paulo mɔ Sila nyɛ Romafɔ a, libe wo be paa.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛnaa be sɛkɛ̃ bɛnakpa be sɛmɛ bɔɛɛ, ade bɛnyɛ be nɛ kɔla a kamɛ a. Bike be aduli kĩ bɛdzakũ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bɛnaa kakɔ bɛbã.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Paulo mɔ Sila dzakũ bɛnaa Lidia awã. Ade awã bɛnamɔ batɛ badonɛ bɛbã a. Bido be kɔbɛ̃ bibu ade bɛdzakũ a.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Paulo mɔ Sila tsã bito Amfipoli mɔ Apolonia bɛyawo Tesalonika, awã kĩ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ kadi na.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Paulo awɛ kakɛna a, ɛnaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a Kawɛya Fuwi ɛlalɛ etomɔ akũ, ɛnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kayɔ ɛtsa bati a.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ lubo a kĩ bɛlɛ biyi kĩ Kristo aayamɔ, mɔ ayekĩ yaabuki eyidza edzi nkpã ɛtsa. Ebi be kĩ, “Oti a kĩ ye akũ fɔlɛtsa nlamɔ mi kabi a nyɛ Yesu Kristo a.”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ anɛ bɛɛlɛ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a, ade ke bɛnawɛ patapaafɔ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ bibumɔ be akũ bido ɔtutũ nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a. Bɛbɔa obũ bɛkɛna dede bɛnawo nɛ Yason tɔtɔ kamɛ kĩ bapi Paulo mɔ Sila bɛnamɛna ɔmatɔ a katsɛnakɔ̃.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛtamɔ be sũ ni, bɛvɛlɛ Yason mɔ batɛ badonɛ badi bɛnamɛna katsɛnakɔ̃ a. Awã na bɛkabɔa fɔwɔla kĩ, “Bati badi tsɛ̃mɔ ɔtutũ kado nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, ade bɛkɔa foe bɛyamɛna nɛ awĩ kĩĩ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: Ade Yason lɛmɛ tɛ be edo nɛ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ a. Bati kĩĩ yidza bɛdza nɛ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare tɛbla akũ. Ade batsa kĩ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Yesu nyɛ Lɛgã a.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Kĩ kɔdabu a mɔ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ nu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a fɔha be paa.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Banɔkɔɛ̃ a fɔ̃ Yason mɔ ye bade a se be akũ liti kadza kɔtɔ ade bɛfɔnɛ be awɛ a. Paulo Mɔ Sila Dzakũ Bɛnaa Beroia.

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade sila kɛdɔ pɛ, batɛ badonɛ a kpã Paulo mɔ Sila bilo bɛnaa Beroia. Kĩ biwo awã a, bɛnaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Beroia bati a tsua linebi bɛba bikĩ nɛ Tesalonika a. Biyi atɔ bitse kalɛ a. Luwi nyɛ luwi bɛkasa Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa kadã ntɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Paulo kabi be a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Foesũ be kamɛ bati pii tɛ bido. Grikifɔ batsole pii kĩ obu deĩ nɛ be akũ mɔ banyole pii lɛmɛ tɛ bido.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yudafɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Tesalonika nu kĩ Paulo ya ɛlamɛna Baguma kalɛ a katsa nɛ Beroia, bɛnaa awã bɛnado ɔtutũ nɛ bati a kamɛ.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã a kpã Paulo mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ bɛnamɛna mpo abuĩ. Lɛmɛ Sila mɔ Timoteo kɔ̃ kahɔ nɛ Beroia.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Bikĩ biyi Paulo osi a naa flee bɛnawo Atene. Ade awã bimuniki a, ade Paulo kpa ɔlɛdo kĩ bibi Sila mɔ Timoteo kĩ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ mɔatsɛ. Paulo Na Nɛ Atene.

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo na nɛ Atene ɛkadzɔlɛ Sila mɔ Timoteo a, ɛtɛ kalɔɛ nɛ ayekĩ ɛmɔ kĩ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ flee yi mɔ baviɔ̃ kasum.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛnanyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ ɛtsa Yudafɔ a mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kĩ basum Baguma a, mɔ bikĩ batɛ̃ nɛ kɔya luwi daa a.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Bati kutsu kudi kĩ bɛnyɛ Epikurofɔ mɔ Stoafɔ kĩ bideĩ katinya kamɛ kadikanyi kavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ Paulo. Badi via kĩ, “Litsa fɔlɛtsa koũ oti kĩĩ wɛ kĩ yalɛ?” Bati bɛbã lɛmɛ kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Fɔadã ndɛ kade bɛbã baviɔ̃ akũ fɔlɛtsa ɛlamɛna kalɛ.” Bɛlɛ foe lɔmɔ kitonɛkĩ binu kĩ Paulo de lɛtsã nɛ Yesu mɔ bati kayidza kato kanɔkpa akũ.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bɛkpã ye bɛnɛmɛna be ɔmatɔ a banɔkɔɛ̃ katsɛnakɔ̃ nɛ Areopago, ade bibi ye kĩ, “Bɔawɛ kĩ bɔanu fɔlɛtsa vɔɛ̃ a kĩ ɔlamɛna katsa a kayɔ.

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ ɔlamɛna kalɛ a kamɛ tákpalɛ boe sũ ni, bɔawɛ butse foe kamɛ.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Atenefɔ mɔ banɔvɔɛ̃ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã awɛ be lɔkɔ flee kakpa fɔlɛtsa vɔɛ̃ katse mɔ foe akũ kɔlɔmɛna.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Ade Paulo yidza ɛdza nɛ bati a anɛ kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Atenefɔ! Mmɔ́ kĩ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ ni, miapi mi ɔsum kadunamɔ akũ paa.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ ntsɛ̃ nɛ ɔmatɔ kamɛ ndã mi fɔtsa kumɛkumɛ a kĩ miasum a ni, mmɔ́ kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ afɔliɛ kabɔa de lidi akũ kĩ, ‘Bɔkɔa bɔakpa Baguma a kĩ bunyína a.’ Baguma a kĩ minyina, lɛmɛ miabɔa afɔliɛ kakpa ye a, ade ye akũ fɔlɛtsa nlamɛna mi kabi finyaakĩĩ a.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: “Baguma kĩĩ nyɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa Ɔlate, kĩ ɛbɔa tite mɔ ode mɔ fɔtsa flee a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ foe kamɛ. Yaádzi nɛ kɔla a kĩ batidziwa ke mɔ awɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: Ɛtási boe lɛtsama kĩ bɔkɔa awɛ bɔkɛna a, kitonɛkĩ ye mɔawɛ akpa oti nkpã mɔ kawɛya, mɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lisĩ oti.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ fude fude bati a a flee etomɛna otidziwa mɔapɛ kamɛ kĩ bidzi nɛ tite akũ. Ye mɔawɛ liyi lɔkɔ mɔ fudzikɔ̃ tɛbɛ̃ ɛkpa bati flee.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Baguma kɛna fukĩĩ ayekĩ boe batidziwa baawɛ ye liti osi bɔmɔ́ ye, titɔ kĩ ɛláa mɔ lugo nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃ malo.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ndɛ ayekĩ mi tɔnɔ bamɛnɛ badi alɛ kĩ, ‘Ade ye dzi fɔ̃ budzi, bɔtsɛ̃ budeĩ nkpã a.’ Ade bɛlɛ lɛmɛ kĩ, ‘Boe malo bɔnyɛ ye kasinu a kamɛ babi.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Nɛ kĩ bɔnyɛ Baguma babi sũ ni, kɛtáfɛta kĩ bɔakɔna kĩ Baguma kutitse la ndɛ litsedi kĩ batidziwa kɔa awɛ bɛmɛ mɔ sika ngbã, suwovoe ĩye futse.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Kito anɛ katinya kayɔkayi kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ a, Baguma távɛlɛ bati sɔtɔ kitonɛkĩ bɛtányi ye sũ. Lɛmɛ finyaakɔ̃ ɛkpa ɔlɛdo kĩ bati flee nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ehihiɛ luwi eyi kĩ de akũ oti a kĩ ɛpau a, aadzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ flee nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ. Kĩ yaakɛna fukĩĩ sũ ni, ɛdzɛla Oti a Kĩ Baguma bɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a etomɛna kanɔkpa ade ke ɛnyɛ ye ɛtsa bati flee a!”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Kĩ binu kĩ Paulo lɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ kayidza kato kanɔkpa akũ a, bati a badi di ye nsekuo, ade babɛbã lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ, “Bɔawɛ kĩ bɔabuki butse fɔlɛtsa fudi nɛ oti kabu kayidza a akũ.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔya lɔmɔ a, Paulo dzakũ ɛfɔnɛ be.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati a badi tomɔ ye bɛdani bɛkɛna batɛ badonɛ. Be kamɛ badi nyɛ Dionisio, kĩ ɛnyɛ Areopago ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, mɔ otsole odi kĩ bavɛ Damari, mɔ bati bɛbã.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, Paulo dzakũ nɛ Atene ɛnaa Korinto.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Nɛ awã a ɛmɔ Yudani odi kĩ bavɛ Akwila kĩ eto Ponto. Bɛmɔ ɔtsɔ̃ Priskila bito Italia bɛya nɛ awã owi tábuna, kitonɛkĩ lɛgã a kĩ bavɛ Klaudio si mbla kĩ Yudafɔ flee bɛnɛ nɛ Roma. Paulo naa ɛnamɔ be,

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: ade ɛna nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛkabumɔ be adzuma, nɛ kĩ bɛmɔ be bakɛna adzuma mɔa sũ. Bɛkɔa bayaɛ tuvoli bɛkapam tilima fɔla fɔtsa ndɛ ayekĩ Paulo lɛmɛ akɛna a.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi lidi nyɛ lidi akũ ni, Paulo kanaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃, kanaanyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ katsa Yudafɔ mɔ Grikifɔ, kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Kĩ Sila mɔ Timoteo to Makedonia bɛya a, Paulo na aya koũ ede lɛtsã nɛ kalɛ wĩ a akũ, ade edi adansiɛ ɛtsa Yudafɔ a kĩ Yesu nyɛ Kristo a.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yudafɔ a yidza bɛdza nɛ ye akũ bɛlɛ fuvũnatsa nɛ ye akũ sũ ni, ɛkpa ye akũ eko be ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ miyɛla ni, mi mɔawɛ aatɛ foe sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ. Yĩ akũ kpalɛ, ntáku kɔtɔ. Kito finyaakĩĩ kanaa ni, maanaa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a sɛkɛ̃ nnaabi be kalɛ wĩ kĩĩ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛdzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ a, ɛnadzi nɛ onyole odi kĩ ɛnyɛ Yudani kĩ bavɛ Tito Yusto tɔtɔ kamɛ. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ yasum Baguma ade ye awã lɛmɛ fuãmɔ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Katsɛnakɔ̃ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bavɛ Krispo mɔ ye akũ bati flee tɛ boe Ɔlate bido ade bɛtɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ a. Ade ayea bɛkɛna Korintofɔ pii a kĩ bɛtɛ bido a mui kawɔla amaniɛ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Luwi a lɛnyɛ ni, Paulo na kanyɛkatsa, ade boe Ɔlate bi ye kĩ, “Tavila. Tafɔ̃ kalɛ wĩ a kalɛ.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Ntomɔ nɔ, odima lɛláapuli ɛkɛna nɔ lɛtsakpa lidima, kitonɛkĩ bati pii kĩ bɛnyɛ yĩ bade lɛmɛ deĩ nɛ ɔmatɔ kĩĩ kamɛ.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Foesũ Paulo dzi nɛ awã kateli mɔa mɔ sɛkɛ ɛkatsa bati Baguma kalɛ a.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛyadzina Galio ndɛ Roma abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ nɛ Akaia tite akũ a, Yudafɔ badi tsa fɔlɔ biyidza bɛdza nɛ Paulo akũ, bipi ye bɛnamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Ade ke bibi ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ, “Obi kĩĩ lamɛna bati adzuni kadani kĩ bisum Baguma nɛ osi kĩ etia boe mbla akũ.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Paulo wɛ kĩ yanyɛ ye akũ onukpɛ̃ pɛ, Galio bi Yudafɔ a kĩ, “Yudafɔ mitse! Ntɛ mi kalɛ kĩ miyamɛna kĩĩ mɔ mfumsu baũ adi yanɛ kɔ̃ ni, kufɛ maana ɔwɔlɛ ntse mi.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ fɔnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ kanyɛ nɛ buĩ mɔ mi tɛmaniɛ akũ sũ ni, mi mɔawɛ minaasa foe kamɛ mimanɛ foe. Fɔnyɛ́ fɔlɛtsa kĩ madzi ndzɛ.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Foesũ ɛblɔmɛna be ɛnɛmɛna nɛ ye fɔlɛtsakadzɛkɔ̃ a.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Ade bati kɔdabu a napi Sostene kĩ ɛnyɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a bibo nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ kɔla kamɛ a. Lɛmɛ Galio tákpeti be kɔlaa. Paulo Muniki Ɛnaa Antiokia.

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Paulo dzi nɛ Korinto fuwi beblebee. Ke liti a, ɛkla batɛ badonɛ a ade bɛmɔ Akwila mɔ Priskila binyina mpo bɛnaa Siria a. Fɔmɔ Paulo aadzakũ nɛ Kenkrea a, ɛfɔ̃ bɛkpa ye lɔlɔ tsɔlitsɔli nɛ fubo a kĩ ɛlɔ nɛ Yudafɔ amaniɛ osi sũ.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo Efeso a, ɛdzakũ ɛfɔnɛ Priskila mɔ Akwila nɛ awã. Ɛdzakũ ɛnaa Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ a ɛnanyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ mɔ Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛna nɛ awã a.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Bati a ke ye aduli kĩ edzi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ sukuloo a, lɛmɛ ɛtátunɔ.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Kĩ ewo Kaisarea a, ɛnaa Yerusalem ɛnawɔ̃na batɛ badonɛ a, ade ebuki ɛtɛ̃ ɛnaa Antiokia a.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Edzi nɛ awã sukuloo ɛtsã emini nɛ Galatia mɔ Frigia tite a kamɛ, ɛkado batɛ badonɛ a kɛbɛ̃. Apolo Naa Efeso.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔa na kamɛ ni, Yudani odi ya nɛ Efeso. Bavɛ ye Apolo ade bɛma ye nɛ Aleksandria a. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ enyina kɔdzɛla kalɛ ade enyina Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bɛtsa ye fɔtsa nɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ wĩ a akũ, yalɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ Yesu akũ malo ni, Yohanes mui kawɔla amaniɛ akũ fɔlɛtsa odi enyina.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Ɛkalɛ kalɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a. Kĩ Priskila mɔ Akwila tse ye fɔlɛtsa a, bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna be awã ade bɛnanyɛ Baguma osi a bɛtsa ye yededeede a.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Kĩ Apolo yake otse kĩ yaanaa Akaia, batɛ badonɛ nɛ Efeso a yado ye kɔbɛ̃. Bɛtsɛlɛ ovoli bɛkpa bikĩ nɛ Akaia kĩ bɛtɛ ye atuu. Kĩ ewo awã a ebumɔ bati pii a kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ sũ.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Ade ke ɛnya Yudafɔ a akũ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kavɛlɛ kamɛ a, kitonɛkĩ ɛnyɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa ɛtsa yededeede kĩ Yesu nyɛ Oti a kĩ Bɛbɔa tɔdɔ̃ a.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Kĩ Apolo na nɛ Korinto a, Paulo tsã abɔa, ɛtsã fuyite fuyite a ɛnanɛ nɛ Efeso. Awã naa, ɛmɔ batɛ badonɛ badi.

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Ade evia be kĩ, “Kĩ mitɛ mido a ni, mitɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a?” Ade ke bɛkpa mbuayɛ kĩ “Oowo! Litsa nyɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a? Bɔtánu tɔ̃ foe akũ lɛtsama.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Ebuki evia be kĩ, “Litsa mui kawɔla amaniɛ mitɛ?” Ade bibi ye kĩ, “Yohanes mui kawɔla amaniɛ a kĩ ke akũ ede lɛtsã a.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Paulo nyɛ foe kayɔ ɛtsa be kĩ, “Yohanes kɛna bati mui kawɔla amaniɛ kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Ade ebi be kĩ bɛtɛ oti a kĩ yaaya nɛ ye Yohanes liti a bido. Ade oti a nyɛ Yesu a.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Kĩ binu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, bɛfɔ̃ Paulo kɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu dĩ akũ.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Paulo te awɛ nɛ be akũ a, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ka ɛyatɛ be flee kamɛ ade bɛlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ a, bɛlɛ Baguma kalɛ a lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Be lidede aawo ndɛ banyole ewua-nviã.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Paulo kanaa katsɛnakɔ̃ a nɛ tidetɔ ɛlalɛ kamɛ, ɛkabi bati kalɛ wĩ a mɔ ɔwɔlɛ kado. Ɛnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ ɛtsa be, ɛbɔa mbɔdi kĩ yavɛlɛ be adzuni ɛyamɛna nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanya a akũ a.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati a badi na atɔ ɔsĩ, bɛtátse kalɛ wĩ a, ade bɛlɛ tuwuli kpa nɛ boe Ɔlate Osi Wĩ a akũ nɛ bati a flee anɛ kamɛ a. Foesũ Paulo dzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃, ade ɛkpã bikĩ bɛtɛ bido a ɛnamɛna Tirano kadzikɔ̃. Ade awã ɛkatsa be fɔtsa luwi nyɛ luwi a.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Ɛkɔa futeli nviã ɛtsa be fɔtsa lɔmɔ, ade Yudafɔ mɔ Helafɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Asia kamɛ kamɛ a lɛmɛ nu boe Ɔlate kalɛ a. Skeva Babinyole Evũkɔnɔ Wɛ Kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Baguma kpa Paulo kɔbɛ̃ ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa pii.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɛkɔa ye tuduku mɔ tɛtadiɛ malo bɛtamɔ basɔnɛ ni, bana akũ ɔsi, ade fɔablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa katomɛna bikĩ edeĩ nɛ be kamɛ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Ade Yudafɔ bawanɛ badi tsɛ̃ nɛ fɔkɔ kĩ be malo bablɔmɛna hũhũ kpa. Bɛkɔna kĩ baapuli bɛkɔa Yesu dĩ bɛblɔmɛna hũhũ a. Bibi hũhũ kpa a kĩ, “Mablɔmɛna mi nɛ Yesu a kĩ ye fɔlɛtsa Paulo tsɛ̃ yalɛ a dĩ akũ!”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛkɛna fukĩĩ nyɛ bawaɛ̃nyole evũkɔnɔ kĩ bɛnyɛ Skeva, Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee babi.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ bɛnabi hũhũ kpa adi tuwuli kĩĩ a, ebi be kĩ, “Ĩnyina Yesu, ĩnyina Paulo malo. Lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃, ĩnyína mi! Bawei mianɛ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Ade oti a kĩ ye kamɛ hũhũ a deĩ a wo nɛ be akũ ebo be a! Bilomɔ mɔ ɔdzɔ mɔ akũ fɔkpa bɛnɛ nɛ tɔtɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔya a gba nɛ Efeso, ade Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ flee nu foe a. Nɛ foesũ libe wo bati paa, ade boe Ɔlate Yesu dĩ buki lide nɛ ode libu a.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Bati pii kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a, nɛ bɛya nɛ kanade bɛyanyɛ be tɔkpa bɛtsa.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛnyɛ bawanɛ ĩye madzikifɔ a lɛmɛ lɔ be fuwa, fɔ́bɛ mɔ foe akũ tuvoli bɛyamɛna bipila nɛ kanade. Tuvoli a kɔya aawo ndɛ sika ovoe mpim aduenum.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Fɔtsã osi kĩĩ akũ boe Ɔlate kalɛ a gba mɔ kɔbɛ̃, kɛtɛ awã lɛmɛ flee. Ɔtutũ Ya Nɛ Efeso.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
