translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ liti a Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a fɔ̃ Paulo kɛna adzuni kĩ yanatsã Makedonia mɔ Akaia ɛnaa Yerusalem. Paulo lɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ nto awã ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ntɛ̃ nnaadã Roma lama.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Edo ye babumɛnanɛ nviã Timoteo mɔ Erasto kĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛna Makedonia, ade ye mɔa edzi nɛ Asia kamɛ kamɛ fɛɛ a.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, ɔdzɛ kpale odi ya nɛ Efeso kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ Kristo katɛ kado osi a akũ sũ.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ yi kayɔ futo odi kĩ bavɛ Demetrio kĩ yakɔa sika ovoe katu Artemis asɔli kɔla akũ fɔtsa a. Bɛmɔ ye adzuma bakɛnanɛ a banɔ foe kakpa bikĩ bayaabɔa afɔliɛ kakpa baviɔ̃ a. Adzuma kĩĩ akpa be nfasɔ baũ paa.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Luwi a, Demetrio ve sika fɔtsa batunɛ a flee ɛkpɔta ade ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, minyina kĩ boe akũ kana to adzuma kĩĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Ade mi mɔawɛ minu, mimɔ́ yededeede ayekĩ Paulo do boe adzuma kĩĩ kɔnyɔã a. Epuli ebula bati a adzuni kade kĩ baviɔ̃ fɔtsa kĩ bɔatu a lɛnɛ́nɛ, nnɛ́ nɛ Efeso odi fukĩĩ lamɛna kaya, mbom nɛ Asia tite a flee akũ.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ boe adzuma odi aabu kade! Boe baviɔ̃ kpale Artemis dĩ mɔ obu flee lɛmɛ aawɔ nɛ Asia mɔ katinya kamɛ flee!”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Kĩ binu Demetrio fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a kalɔkpa wo be mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ade bido fɔwɔla kabɔa awɛ kɔɔba kĩ, “Boe Ɔma Artemis nyɛ Efesofɔ baviɔ̃ kpale!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Fɔwɔla kĩĩ tɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ flee. Nɛ foesũ ɔmatɔ a bɔa obũ ade bipi Paulo batomɛnanɛ nviã a kĩ be nyɛ Gaio mɔ Aristarko kĩ bito Makedonia, bɛnamɛna ɔmatɔ a katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Paulo lɛmɛ wɛ kufɛ kĩ yanaa awã, lɛmɛ batɛ badonɛ bɛbã sua ye osi.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Kayite a na banɔkɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛnyɛ Paulo bakɔba lɛmɛ do lɔtɔ nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃, bike ye aduli kĩ ɛtasuma katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a na ni, katsɛnakɔ̃ a flee kɛna yoo. Bati a badi lamɛna lɛtsa mɔa kalɛ, babɛbã lamɛna lɛtsa bɛbã kalɛ, ade be pii lɛmɛ ti binyína lɛtsa a kĩ de sũ bɛyatsɛna nɛ awã a.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Ade Yudafɔ a vɛlɛ Aleksandro bɛyadzɛla nɛ lɔtɔ kĩ ɛyaanyɛ lɛtsa kĩ de lɛya a onukpɛ̃. Ade Aleksandro dzɛla akpɔ nɛ ode ete be awɛ kĩ bɛfɔ̃ ebi be kadi.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a mɔ biyi ɔsũ kĩ Yudani yanɛ sũ ni, bibuki bɛbɔa fɔwɔla, fɔtɛ ndɛ ndɔũhuilɛ nviã. Bɛkalɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔma Artemis nyɛ Efesofɔ baviɔ̃ kpale!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Owi tábuna fɔmɔ abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi puli ɛyadina bati a ade ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Efesofɔ, mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi nyina kĩ Efeso nyɛ Artemis, baviɔ̃ kpale a kadzikɔ̃, ade ke de kpalɛwa a kĩ lito ode lɛka a lɛmɛ deĩ nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Odima lɛláapuli ɛlɛ kĩ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ lɛnyɛ́ ɔnɔkɔali, foesũ mina ɔwɔlɛ lɔkɔa mitakɛna lɛtsama mɔ kalɔkpa.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati a kĩ mipi miyamɛna nɛ awĩ a táye Artemis kuviɔ̃la fɔtsa fudima ĩye bɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kpa fudima bitia boe baviɔ̃ a.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Demetrio mɔ ye adzuma bakɛnanɛ a deĩ kalɛ mɔ odi kɔ̃ ni, fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ deĩ. Ade budeĩ fuwi kĩ foe akũ badzɛ fɔlɛtsa, kĩ baapuli bɛkɔa bɛtsa lɛtsa kĩ litia be a bɛnamɛna awã.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ fɔlɛtsa bɛbã minɛɛ kɔ̃ ni, mifɔ̃ bɔkɔa foe bɔnaayi nɛ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a anɛ bɛdzɛ nɛ mbla onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nviã ɔtutũ kĩĩ kɔ̃ kɛna ndɛ kayidza kadza nɛ abãã akũ kĩ baapuli bɛbɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ boe akũ. Ade ntɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ a via boe kadi ni, bɔláapuli bɔnyɛ foe onukpɛ̃. Baavɛlɛ boe sɔtɔ.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛlɛ fukĩĩ eyua, ɛnyɛ be flee a osi, ade bɛpansam a.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɔtutũ a onukpɛ̃ dɔ a, Paulo vɛ batɛ badonɛ a ɛkpɔta. Kĩ ɛna kadi ebi be, edo be kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ eyua a, evia osi ɛdzakũ ɛnaa Makedonia.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Paulo tsã kamɛ kamɛ na flee ebi bati boe Ɔlate fɔlɛtsa pii ɛkado be kɔbɛ̃, ɛkɔa ɛyawo Akaia.

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: Edzi nɛ awã tidetɔ ɛlalɛ. Lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛkamanɛ akũ kĩ yadzakũ ɛnaa Siria tite akũ, ɛyamɔ kĩ Yudafɔ a sui nɛ ye akũ. Nɛ foesũ eke otse kĩ yabuki ɛdani ɛtsã Makedonia kamɛ a.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Bɛmɔ bati pii lɛtsã bɛnaa. Ade be nyɛ Sopater, Piro obi kĩ eto Beroia, Aristarko mɔ Sekundo kĩ bito Tesalonika. Babɛbã nyɛ Gaio kĩ eto Derbe, Tihiko mɔ Trofimo kĩ bito Asia, mɔ Timoteo.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Bɛnya boe lɔtɔ bɛnadzɔlɛ boe nɛ Troa ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔnya Yudafɔ Abodoo kĩ Ɛtáyila Fuwi a buyua a, budzi ɔkɔlɔ nɛ Filipi ade bunyina bɔnaa be sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Troa a. Buwo awã nɛ fuwi elo liti, ade budzi nɛ awã fuwi evũkɔnɔ a. Paulo Naa Troa Nkaɛ.

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɛkwansieda luwi lɛtɔnyade akũ a, bɔtsɛna kĩ bɔa nya boe Ɔlate funitsã a. Nɛ kĩ Paulo aadzakũ ntɛ kade nyina sũ ni, ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla flee kɛnawo lɛnyɛ kayite.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Bɛtɛna tɛsɛdza nɛ abansulu kɔla a kamɛ.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo lɛ kɔdzɛla gɛlɛɛlɛ sũ tida bɔɛ Eutiko, opopo kĩ ede edzi nɛ nfansili. Eto abansulu kɔla kɔlalɛde a ɛyatɔ nɛ tite. Bikĩ bɛnabɔɛ ye a mɔ́ kĩ eku.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Paulo ka ɛnaa a, ɛkɛna akũ eboomu ye, ade ebi be kĩ, “Mitavila! Edeĩ nkpã!”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Ade be flee bibuki bide bɛnaa abansulu ode a bɛnanya boe Ɔlate funitsã a. Paulo buki ɛlɛmɔ be kɔdzɛla flee kɛnawo odenyinawi ade ɛdzakũ a.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ lɛtsama tákɛna opopo a sũ ni, bɛkpã ye bɛnamɛna kɔla, ade ɔwɔlɛ ka edzi be a. Paulo To Troa Ɛnaa Mileto.

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Paulo tsã abɔa ɛnaa Aso a. Lɛmɛ boe a kĩ butomɔ ye a budzi ɔkɔlɔ bɔnya ye lɔtɔ kĩ bɔ́anatsɛlamɔ akũ nɛ awã, lɔkɔa bɔbɔɛ ye ndɛ ayekĩ ɛtsa a.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛyamɔ boe nɛ Aso a, boe flee bɔdzakũ bɔnaa Mitilene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Odenyinawi a buwo Kio ntintisɔ. Luwi lɛyade akũ ni bɔnawo Samo, ade nɛ luwi lɛlade a buwo Mileto a.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Paulo fɔ̃ bɔtɛ̃ Efeso, kitonɛkĩ ɛtáwɛ kĩ yabula lɔkɔ pii kade nɛ Asia tite akũ. Eke otse lɛmɛ kĩ ntɛ fɔ́ana ye sɔwa ni, yaana nɛ Yerusalem nɛ Pentekoste luwi a akũ. Paulo Kla Efeso Batɛ Badonɛ Banɔkɔɛ̃ a.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ buwo Mileto a Paulo do batɔ nɛ Efeso batɛ badonɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ a sɛkɛ̃ kĩ bɛyaatsɛlamɔ ye nɛ awã.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɛyaa a, ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mi a flee minyina ayekĩ ndzi nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃, kito lɔkɔ a kĩ ĩya nɛ Asia tite akũ a kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ a.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Mimɔ́ ayekĩ ndɔla yĩ akũ nsum Boe Ɔlate, titɔ kĩ nna nɛ lubo kamɔ mɔ kabe kamɛ, nɛ ayekĩ Yudafɔ wɛ kĩ bakɔ yĩ sũ.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ minyina kĩ ntákɔla mi ɔnɔkɔali a. Fɔtsa a flee kĩ ĩnyina nɛ boe Ɔlate akũ a, ade foe ntsa mi a. Ntsa foe nɛ kanade flee anɛ mɔ mi tɔtɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ade kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ ntsã mmini ni, ndɛ Yudafɔ mɔ Helafɔ flee mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ bɛya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa bɛtɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu bido.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: “Finyaakĩĩ manaa Yerusalem nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kawɛ onukpɛ̃, nɛ kĩ ĩnyína lɛtsa kĩ laaya nɛ yĩ akũ nɛ awã a.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ɔmatɔ odi nyɛ odi kamɛ kĩ ntsã ni, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a fɔ̃ ĩnyi kĩ maawa kɔla nɛ lubo kamɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Yĩ nkpã lɛla nfasɔ adima, ntɛ ntápuli nkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate Yesu do yĩ kĩ nkɛna a. Lɛtsa kĩ edo yĩ kĩ nkɛna nyɛ kĩ nkaabi bati Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ a, mɔ ayekĩ edeĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ ɛkpa boe.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Finyaakĩĩ ĩnyina kĩ mi a flee kĩ minu kĩ ntsa mi fɔtsa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ akũ, bɔmɔ́ mi bɔlaabuki bɔmɔ́ akũ dadaada.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ mabi mi paa nviã kĩĩ, kĩ ntɛ odi yɛla ye nkpã ni, nnɛ́ yĩ nfumusu fɔanɛ.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, ntákɔla mi lɛtsama nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ. Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛpau mi ndɛ lɔtɔ banyanɛ kĩ mipi ye babi a kĩ edo mi nɛ awɛ a kapi wĩ. Mipi be kapi wĩ, kitonɛkĩ Baguma kɔa ye Obi babɔanɛ eko nɛ tite ɛnyɛ be.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Ade ĩnyina yededeede kĩ ntɛ ndzakũ ni, bati kpa aayawo nɛ mi kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛyalɛ mi ndɛ ayekĩ kulikii awo nɛ baveli kamɛ a.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nɛ mi mɔa awɛ kamɛ malo ni, bavãlɛvã aanɛ, lɔkɔa bɛkpã batɛ badonɛ pii bɛtsãmɛna fɔvã fɔtsa katsa a osi akũ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Foesũ midã kadã wĩ, lɔkɔa mite anɛ ayekĩ nkɔa anɛ mɔ babe ntsa mi fɔtsa lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee nɛ futeli ɛlalɛ a kĩ nna nɛ mi kamɛ a.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Finyaakĩĩ, nkɔa mi mado nɛ Baguma awɛ kĩ ɛdãmɛna mi akũ mɔ ye fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔabumɛna bati a, fudo mi katɛkado kɔbɛ̃ ayekĩ fɔ́akɛna mi ode fɔtsa banyanɛ ndɛ bikĩ ɛpau ɛkpa ye akũ a aye.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Anɛ tábɛɛlɛ yĩ nɛ odima sika ngbã ĩye suwovoe ĩye tilima akũ.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Mi awɛ minyina kĩ yĩ awɛ kĩĩ nkɔa nkɛna adzuma ndãmɛna yĩ mɔ bikĩ bitomɔ yĩ a lɛmɛ akũ a.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Nkɛna fukĩĩ bamɛ flee nnɛɛ matsa mi ayekĩ mikɛna adzuma kɔɔba mibumɛna ahiãfɔ, ade mite anɛ nɛ lɛtsa kĩ boe Ɔlate Yesu lɛ a kĩ, ‘Kakpa deĩ nhila kɛbɛ̃ katɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Ade ke kĩ Paulo lɛ kɔdzɛla eyua a, eke aduli ade bɛmɔ be flee bɔbɔa mpaɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛkakla be a, bɛkɔa anɛ mɔ babe bɛkatu kaboomu ye.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ de lɛfɔ̃ biwo ɔblɔ koũ a nyɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ kĩ bɛmɔ be bɛláabuki bɛmɔ akũ dadaada a. Biyi ye osi bɛnawo ɔkɔlɔ a ɔwɛ̃ nɛ ɔwɔ abuĩ.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔkla akũ buyua a, budzi ɔkɔlɔ bɔtsã kpĩĩ bunyina bɔnaa Kos. Kĩ kade nyina a buwo Rodo ade bɔtɛ̃ bɔnaa Patara a.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Awã naa budzi ɔkɔlɔ odi kĩ yanaa Foenike.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Bɔmɔ́ Kipro tite a nɛ lɔtɔ, ade bɔtsã te kayɔ kayɔ a bɔnaduma nɛ Tiro, nɛ Siria tite akũ, awã kĩ baanyɛ fɔtsa flee a kĩ ɔkɔlɔ a nɔnɛ a.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Kĩ boe kadzakũ wo ɔtɔ a, batɛ badonɛ a mɔ batsɔ̃ mɔ babi flee yi boe osi bɛnawo mpo abuĩ. Awã na boe flee buke aduli bɔbɔa mpaɛ.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔkla akũ buyua a, buwo nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ ade be malo bimuniki bɛnaa fɔla a.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Bɔbɔɛ buto Tiro ade bɔnadza nɛ Ptolemia a. Bɔnawɔ̃na batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã ade budzi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ luwi mɔa a.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, bɔdzakũ nɛ awã bɔnaa Kaisarea. Bɔnase nɛ Filipo, Kalɛ Wĩ a Lɛtsã odenɛ a awã. Ɛnyɛ babumɛnanɛ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛpau nɛ Yerusalem a kamɛ ɔmɔa.

english_verse: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
translation_verse: Edeĩ bayitsɔ̃ ɛna kĩ bidzí fɔnyɔ, ade bɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Bɔna nɛ awã fuwi fudi ade foe kamɛ a Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ a kĩ bavɛ Agabo to Yudea ɛya a.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ ɛya nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃ a ɛbɔɛ Paulo ɔwɛ̃ a kĩ yakɔa kaboomu kate a esuli ye mɔa awɛ akpɔ mɔ abɔa, ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a bo ni, ade aye Yudafɔ nɛ Yerusalem aasuli ɔwɛ̃ kĩĩ ɔlate bɛkɔa ye bido nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a awɛ a.”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kĩ boe flee bunu fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, bɔmɔ́ bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã flee a buke Paulo aduli kĩ ɛtanaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo bi boe kĩ, “Litsa nyɛ kabe a kĩ mikɔa miaveli yĩ akpɔ? Mmanɛ akũ nkpa nnɛ́ kĩ bisuli yĩ nɛ Yerusalem odi lɛmɛ kĩ maaku nɛ awã nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu sũ.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔmɔ́ kĩ bɔláapuli bɔdani ye adzuni sũ ni, bɔfɔnɛ ye awɛ ade bɔlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate kawɛ kɛya nɛ foe kamɛ.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Fuwi fudi liti ni, bɔmanɛ akũ ade bɔdzakũ bɔnaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ badi to Kaisarea bitomɔ boe bɔnaa. Kĩ buwo awã a, bɛkpã boe bɔnase nɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi kĩ bavɛ Mnason awã. Eto Kipro ade ɛnyɛ batɛ badonɛ batɔnyade a kamɛ ɔmɔa a.

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Kĩ buwo Yerusalem a batɛ badonɛ a tɛ boe mɔ kawɔlɛ. Paulo Namɔ Yakobo.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, Paulo kpã boe bɔnamɔ Yakobo mɔ batɛ badonɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ flee a nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɔnawɔ̃nɔ akũ buyua a, Paulo bɔa be amandiɛ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma kɛna nɛ ye kamini nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ kamɛ a akũ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Kĩ bitse fɔlɛtsa a biyua a, be flee bimu Baguma ade bibi ye kĩ, “Boe ɔwaɛ̃, dã ayekĩ Yudafɔ mpim mpim tɛ bido, ade bipini Mose mbla a mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ a.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Bati badi bi be kĩ ɔtsa Yudafɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ fude fude a kĩ bɛtakɛna nɛ Mose mbla mɔ boe tɛmaniɛ a akũ, ade obuki ɔtsa kĩ bɛtatsua be babi fɔkpati lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Baanu kokooko kĩ ɔya nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ litsa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna finyaa?”

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Ade ke bɛkpa ye adzuni kĩ “Budeĩ bati ɛna kĩ bido lidetɔ.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Kpã be kanamɛna Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ kabumɔ be minyɛ lɔlɔ kakpa lidetɔ a kase foe kɔtɔ. Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ odi nyɛ odi lɔmɔ kĩ fɔvã bɛma bite nɛ nɔ akũ, lɔkɔa fɔtsa lɛmɛ kĩ opini Mose mbla a kapini wĩ.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kɔ̃ ni, bɔtsɛlɛ be ovoli bubi be kĩ bɛtabuki bɛnya fɔtsa kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃, bɛfɔ̃ babɔanɛ kanya, bɛtaba bayaɛ kĩ bɛtátsua be fɔmɔ, lɔkɔa bɛfɔ̃ linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ kade nyina a, Paulo kpã banyole a, ade bɔnakɛna lɔlɔ kakpa tsɔli tsɔli amaniɛ a. Ke liti a, ɛnaa Yudafɔ afɔliɛ kabɔakɔ̃ a ɛnakpa basɔfɔ kanyi nɛ fuwi a kĩ tɔlɔ kakpa amaniɛ a aawũna, mɔ futetsa a lɛmɛ kĩ yaama be bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ a nɛ be lɔlɔ. Bipi Paulo Nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a Kamɛ.

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ kite sukuloo lɔkɔa fuwi evũkɔnɔ a aawo ɔtɔ a, Yudafɔ badi kĩ bito Asia kamɛ a yamɔ Paulo nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Bido ɔtutũ nɛ bati a badi kamɛ ade bipi Paulo a.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Bɛkabɔa fɔwɔla kalɛ kĩ, “Israelfɔ miyaatɛ mɔ boe tsoo! Ade oti kĩĩ tsɛ̃ yamini nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, yabi bati kĩ bɛtakɛna nɛ boe Yudafɔ mbla aku a. Ye lɛmɛ ɛkpã Grikifɔ ɛyawomɛna nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, kĩ fɔatsa kĩ ebula boe kakɔ kpalɛwa kĩĩ.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ foe lɔmɔ kitonɛkĩ luwi a bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛmɔ́ Paulo mɔ Trofimo kĩ eto Efeso a, kĩ bɛtsɛ̃ bamini nɛ Yerusalem. Nɛ foe sũ bɛkɔna kĩ ɛkpã ye ewomɛna nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Nɛ fɔwɔla kabɔa a sũ, akũ nyɛnɛ bati a flee ade biwula atsɛ yoo bɛnatsɛna nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Badi vɛlɛ Paulo bɛnɛmɛna ade bɛbalɛ tunyukpɛ̃ flee a.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ ye a, Romafɔ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi nu kĩ Yerusalem ɔmatɔ a flee deĩ nɛ ɔtutũ kamɛ mɔ fɔwɔla kabɔa.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ ɛkpã sodzafɔ badi bɛnaa awã kĩ bɛkakɛna hũnyɛhũnyɛ a. Kĩ bati kĩĩ mɔ sodzafɔ mɔ be ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a pɛ bɛfɔ̃ Paulo katuli.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Awã naa, sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a fɔ̃ bipi Paulo ade bido ye nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ nviã a. Ade evia etse oti a odu kĩ yanɛ mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ de ɛkɛna a.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Bati a flee kanyɛ tunukpɛ̃ futefute. Kĩ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a lanu lɛtsama kayɔ pɔtii etomɛna be sɛkɛ̃ nɛ fɔwɔla mɔ ɔsĩ a sũ ni, ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔa Paulo bɛnado nɛ kɔla a kamɛ.

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛkɔa ye bado nɛ kɔla kamɛ a, Paulo bi sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ. “Nwɛ kĩ malɔmɛna mɔ nɔ sukuloo.” Ade ke sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a via ye kĩ, “Yie! Alɛ Griki tide?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Ade Paulo kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yudani manɛ. Bɛma yĩ nɛ Tarso nɛ Kilikia tite akũ. Ade ɔmatɔ a na ni, nnɛ́ ɔmatɔ kabii yanɛ. Foesũ make nɔ aduli fɔ̃ nlɛmɔ bati kĩĩ kɔdzɛla.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Adeke ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ tunɔ kĩ Paulo ɛlɛmɔ be kɔdzɛla a. Paulo nɛ ɛyadza nɛ onyukpɛ̃ kadekɔ̃ a, ade ete awɛ nɛ bati kɔdabu akũ kĩ bɛkɛna dĩĩ a. Kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fɔwɔla kabɔa a, ɛlɛmɔ be kɔdzɛla nɛ Yudafɔ tide kamɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “Nda amɛ mɔ nwaɛ̃ amɛ, agoo! Mifɔ̃ ĩnyɛ yĩ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa mitse.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Kĩ binu kĩ Yudafɔ tide kamɛ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla a, bɛkɛna fɔwɔla kabɔa nɛ kayɔ bibu. Ade ke Paulo bi be kĩ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Yudani manɛ ade bɛma yĩ nɛ Tarso, nɛ Kilikia tite akũ a. Lɛmɛ Yerusalem nɛ awĩ bɛdɔɔla yĩ, nkɔlɛ fɔtsa nɛ Gamaliel kayɔ. Bɛtsa yĩ boe bawanyɔ mbla a mɔ foe akũ amaniɛ a flee yededeede. Ade nkɔa yĩ ɔwɔlɛ flee nkpa Baguma ndɛ ayekĩ mi malo miakɛna nviã kĩĩ a.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Kito lɔkɔ a na, ntomɔ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛtsɛ̃ osi vɔɛ̃ a liti, nwɛ kĩ bɛkɔ be. Ade nkapi batsole mɔ banyole kado nɛ kɔla a.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Boe Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee mɔ ɔmaĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ aapuli bidi adansiɛ nɛ fukĩ malɛ kĩĩ akũ, kitonɛkĩ bɛkatsɛlɛ tuvoli kakpa yĩ kĩ nkɔa nkɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ. Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ manaa Damasko a ni, bɛkpa yĩ tuvoli kĩ nkɔa mma Yudafɔ nɛ awã ndza nɛ foe akũ mpili batɛ badonɛ a nsuli be nkɔa ĩyamɛna nɛ Yerusalem bɛvɛlɛ be sɔtɔ.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Lɔkɔ a kĩ ntsɛ̃ manaa, kĩ kite sukuloo mawo Damasko, ndɛ suwa tɔdɔũ ewua-nviã kamɛ a ni, deedimɔapɛ ɔhaĩ mɔ ɔsĩ odi to ode ɛka eko nɛ yĩ akũ!

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Adeke nka ntɔ nɛ tite, ade nnu ɔlɛdo odi kĩ ɛkavia yĩ kĩ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, nde sũ oyidza ɔdza nɛ yĩ akũ lɔmɔ?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: “Ade nvia kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, nɔ owei anɛ?’ “Ebuki ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Yesu Nazaretni a kĩ ye akũ oyidza ɔdza a.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɔmɔ be tsɛ̃ a mɔ́ ɔhaĩ a lɛmɛ bɛtánu oti a kĩ bɔmɔ ye lɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a odo.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: “Ade nvia ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, litsa kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna?’ “Ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Yidza kanaa Damasko ɔmatɔ a kamɛ. Awã na maatsa nɔ lɛtsa a kĩ nwɛ kĩ kɛna a.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Ɔhaĩ kĩĩ kɔ yĩ anɛ foesũ bati a kĩ bɔmɔ be tsɛ̃ a lipi yĩ nɛ kɔkpɔ bɛkpã yĩ bɔdzakũ bɔnawo Damasko ɔmatɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Anania na nɛ awã. Ɛnyɛ oti kĩ yavila Baguma ade yakɛna nɛ boe mbla lɛmɛ akũ yededeede a. Yudafɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã flee a akpa ye obu.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Oti kĩĩ yadza nɛ yĩ ɔwɛ̃ ade ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Nwaɛ̃ Saulo bu kamɔ fɔkɔ!’ Lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ kpalɛ yededeede ade mmɔ́ ye a.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: “Ade ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Boe bawanyɔ Baguma lɛpau nɔ eyi kĩ nyi ye fɔwɛtsa, kamɔ ye Obi Kpalɛwa a kĩ Bɛpau a lɔkɔa kanu ye kɔdzɛla lɛmɛ.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ litsa adzɔlɛ? Yidza lɔkɔa katɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ. Bɔa mpaɛ nɛ ye dĩ akũ lɔkɔa ɛkɔa nɔ titikpa ete nɔ.’ Baguma Do Paulo Ɛnaa Bikĩ Bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a Sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Fukĩĩ liti a, mmuniki nnaa Yerusalem. Ade kĩ nna nɛ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ nkabɔa mpaɛ a, fɔkɔna ndɛ tida abɔɛ yĩ, ade nna kanyɛkatsa kadi a.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Ade awã na boe Ɔlate bi yĩ kĩ, ‘Wɛ sɔwa kadzakũ nɛ Yerusalem kitonɛkĩ awĩ bati lɛláatse nɔ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ aalɛ nɛ yĩ akũ a.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ nkpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, be flee binyina kĩ yĩ lɛkapili bikĩ bɛtɛ nɔ bido a nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a kabo be.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛkakɔ nɔ ɔdansini Stefano a, yĩ malo ntunɔ. Foesũ bɛnyɛ be tɛtadiɛ bɛkpa yĩ nlɛ ade bɛkatalɛ ye boe a.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: “Lɛmɛ boe Ɔlate bi yĩ kĩ, ‘Yidza kanaa, maado nɔ mɔ lugo nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kamɛ!’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ kĩĩ flee a, bati a dza dĩĩ bɛkatse ye kalɛ a. Lɛmɛ mɔ ye kalɛ nkaɛ kawũna pɛ, be flee bɔbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Aa! Kɛtáfɛta kĩ yadzi nkpã! Mikpã ye minamɛna minaakɔ ye!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ bɛna kalɔkpa paa sũ ni, bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ade bido be tɛtadiɛ kayɛlɛ, mɔ kudu kata nɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ awɛ a.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Ade ke kawũ banɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a fɔ̃ bɛkpã ye biwomɛna nɛ kɔla kamɛ kĩ bɛnaabo ye lɔkɔa binyi lɛtsa a sũ kĩ Yudafɔ a kpa biko nɛ ye akũ lɔmɔ.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛkasuli ye a, Paulo via kawũ banɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ odi kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ awã kĩ, “Osi waa kĩ babo Romani ntɛ bɛtádzɛ ye fɔlɛtsa tɔ̃?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ naa be lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a sɛkɛ̃ ade evia ye kĩ, “Litsa akɛna nɛ awĩ a? Onyole kĩĩ boni Romani yanɛ!”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Foesũ kawũ banɔnɛ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a ya nɛ Paulo sɛkɛ̃ ade evia ye kĩ, “Abo nde? Romani anɛ?” Ade ke Paulo kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Romani manɛ!”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Kawũ banɔnɛ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ malo Romani manɛ. Yĩ kɔ̃ nse kɔtɔ baũ fɔmɔ ndani nkɛna Romani!” Ade Paulo bi ye kĩ, “Yĩ kɔ̃ yĩ bamanɛ flee nyɛ Romafɔ.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Ade ke kĩ bati a kĩ bɛdza kĩ babo ye bivia ye onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa nu kĩ Romani yanɛ a, bɛvɛlɛ akũ bɛnaa liti. Kĩ be lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a nyi kĩ Romani yanɛ a, libe wo ye paa, kitonɛkĩ ye lɛkpa osi kĩ bisuli Paulo bibo ye. Bɛkpã Paulo Bɛyadzɛla Nɛ Fɔlɛtsa Badzɛnɛ Anɛ.

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina, kawũ banɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a wɛ kĩ yanyi lɛtsa a kĩ de sũ bɛbɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ Paulo akũ. Ɛvɛ Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ be fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ ɛkpɔta. Ɛfɔ̃ biwũnya tɔwɛ̃ nɛ Paulo akũ ade bɛkpã ye bɛyadzɛla nɛ be anɛ kamɛ kĩ bawɛ ye fɔlɛtsa a kamɛ a.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Paulo ko anɛ ɛdã Yudafɔ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a kĩ bikoni dĩĩ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a, ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Israelfɔ, yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kpalɛ nɛ ayekĩ ndzi nkpã nɛ Baguma anɛ kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ a.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Kĩ Anania, Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a kĩ ɛna nɛ awã nu ayekĩ Paulo kpa mbuayɛ a, ebi bikĩ bɛdza bifuãmɔ Paulo kĩ bɛbɔa ye nɛ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Ade awã mɔ Paulo bi ye kĩ, “Baguma lɛmɛ aabalɛ nɔ awɛ! Nɔ seebi kĩ bikputa se fɔtsa ovoe bɛdzɛla anɛ! Litsa fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ anɛ? Mbla akũ ɔtsɛ̃ adzɛmɔ yĩ, lɛmɛ ade ye mɔapɛ a ɔlamɛna kafum a, nɛ kĩ ɔkpa kɔbɛ̃ bɛbalɛ yĩ ɔlɔtɔ sũ!”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Ade ke bikĩ bɛdzɛ nɛ Paulo ɔwɛ̃ a via ye kĩ, “Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee ovũna lɔmɔ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Paulo kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ntɛ ĩnyi kĩ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee yanɛ ni, kufɛ ntádo ye onukpɛ̃ lɔmɔ. Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, ‘Talɛ tuwuli kpa nɛ bikĩ banya mi akũ a akũ.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Ade kĩ Paulo mɔ eyi ɔsũ kĩ bati a kĩ bɛkpɔta nɛ awã a be kamɛ badi nyɛ Zadukifɔ ade badi lɛmɛ nyɛ Farisifɔ a sũ ni, ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, Farisini manɛ! Farisifɔ lɛmɛ lɛma yĩ! Nviã kĩĩ badzɛmɔ yĩ kitonɛkĩ ntɛ banɔkpa kabuki kayidza kadzi nkpã a ndo sũ!”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo sila ɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ eyua pɛ, kavɛlɛ ya nɛ Farisifɔ mɔ Zadukifɔ a kayite, ade bɛsɛ̃ be akũ a.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Zadukifɔ alɛ kĩ banɔkpa lábuki kayidza, ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ lɛláa, ade hũhũ lɛmɛ lɛláa a. Lɛmɛ Farisifɔ kɔ̃ tɛ fukĩĩ bamɛ flee bido.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Ɔtutũ kpale wo nɛ be kamɛ. Farisifɔ mbla batsanɛ a dza nɛ Paulo liti kĩ ye fɔlɛtsa a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Ade ke bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Bɔtámɔ lɛtsa kpa lidima nɛ ye akũ! Ɔsa hũhũ adi ĩye ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ odi alɛmɔ ye kɔdzɛla!”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Fɔwɔla kabɔa a buki fude nɛ ode fubu. Ade bati a flee pini Paulo bɛkavɛlɛ kadu kanamɛna kayamɛna a. Kĩ sodza ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a vila kĩ baavɛlɛ ye bɛpããpãã sũ ni, ebi ye sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛka bɛnaatɛ ye bɛnaado nɛ kɔla.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Luwi a lɛnyɛ a, boe Ɔlate yadza nɛ Paulo ɔwɛ̃ ebi ye kĩ, “Paulo do ɔwɔlɛ. Ayekĩ odi adansiɛ nɛ yĩ akũ nɛ Yerusalem nɛ awĩ a, ade ayea kɛnɛ kĩ akɛna nɛ Roma lɛmɛ a.” Yudafɔ Tsa Fɔlɔ Kĩ Bakɔ Paulo.

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ lidede aana ndɛ be tɛna mɔ liti.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Ade ke bibuki bɛnaa basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ be lɔtɔ banyanɛ sɛkɛ̃ bibi be kĩ, “Bɔlɔ fubo kĩ bɔlaanya ĩye bɔmɔa kɛnaawo kĩ bɔkɔ Paulo.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Foesũ mimɔ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a mibi sodzafɔ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, ebuki ɛkɔa Paulo ɛyamɛna nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ nɛ awĩ. Mikɛna ndɛ miawɛ kĩ miavia ye fɔlɛtsa fudi mibu. Ntɛ bɛkɔa ye bayamɛna ni, bɔ́akɔ ye nɛ osi.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo ɔfanyole odi nu fɔlɛtsa a flee kĩ bɛlɛ a, ade ewula atsɛ ɛnaa kɔla kamɛ, ɛnabi Paulo lɛtsa a kĩ de bɛlɛ a.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Paulo vɛ sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã kamɛ ɔmɔa ebi ye kĩ, “Kpã obisɛ̃ kĩĩ minaa ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ sɛkɛ̃. Ɛnɛɛ kalɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ yaabi ye.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Foesũ sodzani a kpã kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a bɛnaa be lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a sɛkɛ̃, ade ebi ye kĩ, “Paulo a kĩ bido nɛ kɔla kamɛ nɛ awĩ a lɛvɛ yĩ ebi yĩ kĩ nkpã kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ kĩĩ mma nɔ, lɔkɔa kibi nɔ kalɛ kadi.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Ade sodzafɔ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a pi kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a nɛ kɔkpɔ bɛnaa ɔɔwɛ̃ ade evia ye kĩ, “Litsa awɛ kĩ abi yĩ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Ade ke mɔ ebi ye kĩ, “Yudafɔ badi kɛna onukpɛ̃ mɔa mɔ be banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bayaake nɔ aduli lɔkɔa kunya ɔkpã Paulo ɔyamɛna nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃, mɔ kakɔna kĩ bavia ye fɔlɛtsa fudi.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a si ye mbla kĩ, “Tafɔ̃ odima libuki enu lɛtsa a kĩ ɔyabi yĩ nɛ awĩ a dadaada.” Ade ke ɛnyɛ ye osi ɛdzakũ a. Bɛkpã Paulo Bɛma Abãã Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Felike.

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Miwɛ ɔpɔnkɔ mikpa Paulo edzi nɛ ye akũ lɔkɔa mikpã ye bɔɛɛ, mima abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Felike.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Ade ke ɛtsɛlɛ ovoli odi ɛkpa abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ,

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: Ovoli kĩĩ to yĩ Klaudio Lisia sɛkɛ̃. Mawɔ̃na nɔ, boe Abãã Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Felike.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ badi pi oti kĩĩ, ade bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ ye a. Lɛmɛ kĩ bɔmɔ sodzafɔ buwo awã a, nnu kĩ Romani yanɛ sũ ni, nfɔ̃ sodzafɔ a tɛ ye nɛ be awɛ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Foesũ nkpã ye ĩyamɛna nɛ be fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ kĩ manyi lɛtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna a.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Ĩyamɔ kĩ lɛtsa kĩ bɛbɔɛ nɛ ye akũ a nyɛ Yudafɔ ɔsum mbla akũ fɔlɛtsa kĩ fɔnyɛ́ fukĩ bado ye nɛ kɔla kamɛ ĩye bɛkɔ ye.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Adeke kĩ bibi yĩ kĩ bɛlamɔ nhihiɛ kakɛna kĩ baakɔ ye sũ ni, nkɔna kĩ makɔa ye mma nɔ. Mbi bikĩ bɛbɔɛ kalɛ nɛ ye akũ a ku kĩ bɛkɔa ke bɛma nɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Foesũ luwi a lɛnyɛ a sodzafɔ a kpã Paulo bɛnawo Antipatre.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina, sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛtsã abɔa a muniki bɛnaa kɔla kamɛ, ade bɛfɔ̃ bapɔnkɔ badzinɛ a kpã Paulo bɛnamɛna Kaisarea a.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Kĩ biwo awã a, bɛkɔa Paulo mɔ ovoli a kĩ bɛnɛɛ a bɛkpa abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkã ovoli a eyua a, evia Paulo amantam a kĩ eto. Ade kĩ enyi kĩ Paulo to Kilikia a, ebi ye kĩ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: “Maanaa nɔ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ yededeede ntɛ bikĩ mimɔ be mina kalɛ a ya nɛ awĩ.” Ade ke ɛkpa sodzafɔ a kĩ bɛdza nɛ awã a kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛkɔa Paulo bido nɛ kɔla a kĩ Lɛgã Herodes ke a kamɛ lɔkɔa bɛkaadã ye akũ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Fuwi elo liti a, Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee Anania, Yudafɔ lɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ kĩ bavɛ Tertulo naa Kaisarea. Bɛya nɛ Felike sɛkɛ̃ kĩ bayaanyɛ Paulo nfumusu bɛtsa ye.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛvɛ Paulo bɛyamɛna a, Tertulo yi Paulo onukpɛ̃ katsua kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nɛ nɔ nyansã mɔ adzuni kĩ ɔnɛɛ akpã boe sũ ni, bɔna lukudɔ nɛ lɔkɔ pii kamɛ. Ade kavɛmɛna pii lɛmɛ ya nɛ boe tite akũ a.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ nyɛ fɔtsa kĩ fuwo boe Yudafɔ flee anɛ.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Bɔake nɔ aduli kĩ tse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ maabi nɔ nɛ onyole kĩĩ akũ a.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɔmɔ́ ye buyi ɔsũ kĩ ɔdzɛ odonɛ yanɛ, ade ɛtsɛ̃ nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ yadani Yudafɔ kakɔna nɛ katinya kamɛ kĩ bɛkɛna fɔtsa basa bitia abãã a. Ɛnyɛ lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɛkpa Nazaretfɔ katsu a.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ovia oti kĩĩ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa ni, aapuli onyi fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɔbɔɛ nɛ ye akũ a otomɛna ye sɛkɛ̃.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Ade ke Yudafɔ a flee tunɔ mɔ onukpɛ̃ mɔa bɛlɛ kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Tertulo lɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Paulo Nyɛ Onukpɛ̃ Nɛ Felike Anɛ.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Kĩ abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a vɛ Paulo kĩ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla a, eyi kayɔ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlɛga, ĩnyina kĩ nɛ futeli pii kĩ fɔtɛ̃ kĩĩ kamɛ ni, nɔ adzɛ Yudafɔ fɔlɛtsa. Nɛ foesũ kawɔlɛ de yĩ kĩ maanyɛ yĩ onukpɛ̃ nɛ nɔ anɛ.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Aanu kĩ fɔ́ana ndɛ fuwi ewua-nviã kĩ ĩya nɛ Yerusalem ĩyaabɔa afɔliɛ nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Ade odima támɔ kĩ bɔmɔ odi bɔkɛna ɔdzɛ ĩye ndo ɔtutũ nɛ Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ, be fɔtsɛnakɔ̃, ĩye kakɔ kadi nɛ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Ade bati kĩĩ kamɛ odima lɛláapuli eke otse nɛ lɛtsama akũ pɔtii kĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ nɛ awĩ a ni, ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ ntunɔ nyɛ kĩ masum boe bawanyɔ Baguma, ade ntomɛna Osi a kĩ balɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ́ osi wĩ a. Ntɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ tuvoli a kamɛ a ndo.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Ndeĩ kɔnɛte nɛ Baguma akũ ndɛ ayekĩ be malo bideĩ, kĩ Baguma aadzɛla bati wĩ mɔ bati kpa flee etomɛna kanɔkpa.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɔnɛte kĩĩ sũ ni, mabɔa mbɔdi paa kĩ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ɛkpalɛ yededeede nɛ Baguma mɔ batidziwa anɛ.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “Ade futeli pii kĩ ndzakũ nɛ Yerusalem a. Finyaakĩĩ ĩyaate ahiãfɔ fɔtsa lɔkɔa mbɔa afɔliɛ nkpa Baguma.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Kĩ mayua lɔlɔ kakpa amaniɛ a, Yudafɔ badi yamɔ yĩ nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Nnɛ́ bɔmɔ́ bati kĩ banyɛ lɛma tsɛ̃. Ade ke ntádo ɔdzɛ odima lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ a kĩ bito Asia tite akũ a na nɛ awã, ade be kɛnɛ kĩ bɛyaadza nɛ nɔ anɛ kamɛ a, lɔkɔa ntɛ bideĩ kalɛ kadi nɛ yĩ akũ ni, baalɛ.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Ĩye afɔ̃ bati a kĩ bidzi nɛ awĩ a bɛlɛ kɔtɔ a kĩ bɛkɔ yĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Yudafɔ ɔmaĩ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a dzɛmɔ yĩ a.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Mbɔɛ ntɛ kalɛ mɔa a kĩ ndzɛla ɔlɛdo nlɛ kĩ, ‘Miadzɛmɔ yĩ nviã kĩĩ kitonɛkĩ ntɛ banɔkpa kayidza kadzi nkpã a ndo.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Ade Felike, oti kĩ enyina katɛkado osi vɔɛ̃ kĩĩ paa a bi Yudafɔ a kĩ, “Midzɔlɛ yĩ kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ Lisia aaya fɔmɔ maatsua fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Adeke ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔa Paulo bɛnado nɛ kɔla a. Lɛmɛ ebi bikĩ badãmɛna ye akũ a kĩ bɛtakɛna ye tɛnya. Ntɛ ye bakɔba badi ya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ ni, bɛkpa be osi lɔkɔa bɛkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ yawɛ a bɛkpa ye. Paulo Nyɛ Fɔlɛtsa Kamɛ Ɛtsa Felike Mɔ Drusila.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi fudi liti ni, Felike mɔ ɔtsɔ̃ Drusila kĩ ɛnyɛ Yudani ya. Ɛfɔ̃ bɛnyɛ Paulo bɛyamɛna ade ebi be kalɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo katɛkado fɔlɛtsa akũ a.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɛkanyɛ fɔlɛtsa kamɛ nɛ kadzi wĩ, akũ kapi, mɔ Baguma kadzɛ a kĩ kaaya nɛ kunya kamɛ a akũ ni, libe wo Felike paa. Adeke Felike bi ye kĩ, “Yidza kanaa tɔ̃. Ntɛ nna lɔkɔ wĩ ni, maafɔ̃ bɛvɛ nɔ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ ɛkɔna kĩ Paulo aakpa ye litsedi sũ ni, yado bavɛ ye mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ bɛmɔ ye balɔmɛna.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Nɛ futeli nviã liti a, bɛnyɛ Felike ade bɛkɔa Porkio Festo bɛyamɛna nɛ ye kadzakɔ̃ a. Lɛmɛ kĩ Felike wɛ kĩ Yudafɔ a bɛwɛ ye kalɛ sũ ni, ɛfɔ̃ Paulo buki ɛna nɛ kɔla nɛ awã.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kĩ Festo yawo kayite a kĩ yanya lɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a, ke fuwi ɛlalɛ liti a, eto Kaisarea ɛnaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Nɛ awã naa, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a mɔ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a yabi ye fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛbɔɛ nɛ Paulo akũ. Ade ke bike ye aduli kĩ,

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: ɛmɔ be kɔnya ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔa ye bɛyamɛna nɛ Yerusalem. Be kasui nyɛ kĩ baadzi ye osi lɔkɔa ntɛ yatɛ̃ a bɛkɔ ye.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Festo bi be kĩ, “Paulo waa kɔla nɛ Kaisare. Yĩ malo maamuniki naa awã nɛ fuwi lukũ kamɛ.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ mi banɔkɔɛ̃ a bɛya nɛ awã bɛyaanyɛ ye nfumusu a bɛtsa.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi ɛlɛ̃ ĩye foe ewua liti ni, Festo muniki ɛnaa Kaisare, ade ke luwi lɛyade a ɛvɛ Paulo fɔlɛtsa a.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Ade ke kĩ Paulo yawo fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a, Yudafɔ a kĩ bito Yerusalem a yadza bisinya ye. Bitsua fɔlɛtsa pii bite nɛ ye akũ lɛmɛ fudima tápi ye.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Paulo nyɛ onukpɛ̃ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ntáfum Yudafɔ mbla ĩye Yudafɔ Asɔli Kɔla a ĩye Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ Festo awɛ kĩ Yudafɔ a bɛwɛ ye kalɛ sũ ni, evia Paulo kĩ, “Ɔwɛ kĩ anaa Yerusalem lɔkɔa ndzɛ nɔ fɔlɛtsa a nɛ awã mbɔɛɛ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Ade ke Paulo bi ye kĩ, “Oowo. Romafɔ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ ndzɛ̃. Ade awĩ kɛfɛta kĩ bɛdzɛmɔ yĩ a. Onyina yededeede kĩ ntákɛna tɔkpa tidima nɛ Yudafɔ akũ.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Ntɛ nkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkɔ yĩ ni, maatunɔ kĩ bɛkɔ yĩ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ntákɛna tɔkpa ni, kɛtáfɛta kĩ nɔ ĩye oti bɛbã akɔa yĩ edo nɛ bati kĩĩ awɛ kamɛ kĩ bɛkɔ yĩ. Nɛ foesũ nkɔa yĩ fɔlɛtsa makpa Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare!”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, Festo mɔ ye fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ a tsa fɔlɔ ade bibi ye kĩ, “Kĩ ɔkɔa nɔ fɔlɛtsa akpa Kaisare sũ ni, Kaisare sɛkɛ̃ mɔ aanaa.” Lɛgã Agripa Mɔ Bernike Tse Paulo Fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Fuwi fudi liti a, Lɛgã Agripa mɔ Bernike ya nɛ Festo sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Kaisare kĩ bɛyaawɔ̃na ye.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Bidzi nɛ awã fuwi pii, ade Festo kɔa Paulo fɔlɛtsa a ebi lɛgã a kĩ, “Onyole odi waa kɔla nɛ awĩ kĩ Felike kɔa ye fɔlɛtsa ɛkpa yĩ kĩ ndzɛ.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: Kĩ nnaa Yerusalem a, basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ be banɔkɔɛ̃ a tsua ye onukpɛ̃ bɛkpa yĩ ade bɛwɛ kĩ nkɔ ye kɔtɔ a.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Nfɔ̃ binyi kĩ Romafɔ mbla tákpa osi kĩ bɛkɔ oti fɔmɔ baadzɛmɔ ye. Kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkpa ye osi bɛmɔ bikĩ bideĩ fɔlɛtsa a bɛkpɔta anɛ mɔ anɛ fɔmɔ bɛlɛ foe.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ bɛya nɛ awĩ a, ntátɛ̃ ndzi nɛ kalɛ kĩĩ akũ. Kĩ kade sila kinyina a, nfɔ̃ bɛkpã oti kĩĩ bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ bitomɛna ye akũ a vɛmɛna nɛ fukĩ nkɔna kĩ baalɛ a akũ.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ nɛ awĩ a to be kasum mɔ onyole odi kĩ bavɛ Yesu kĩ eku, lɛmɛ Paulo kɔ̃ tsɛ̃ yalɛ kĩ ebuki edzi nkpã akũ!

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Fɔkɛna yĩ basaa ade ĩnyína ayekĩ matsua fɔlɛtsa a kamɛ a. Foesũ nvia Paulo ntɛ ɛwɛ kĩ nkɔa ye nnamɛna Yerusalem lɔkɔa ɛnaanyɛ ye akũ onukpɛ̃ nɛ awã.

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo lɛ kĩ ɛwɛ kĩ bɛkɔa ye fɔlɛtsa bɛkpa Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare. Ade nfɔ̃ bimunikimɔ ye bɛnado nɛ kɔla kɛnawo kĩ maakɔa ye mma Kaisare a.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Lɛgã Agripa bi Festo kĩ “Yĩ mɔawɛ mawɛ kĩ matse ye onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa a.” Ade Festo bi ye kĩ, “Aatse ye kunya!” Paulo Dza Nɛ Agripa Anɛ Kamɛ.

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, Lɛgã Agripa mɔ Bernike hɛhɛ akũ mɔ fɔgãtsa, bɛnɛ bɛya nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a. Ade kawũ banɔnɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ ɔmatɔ a kamɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ a tomɔ be bɛya a. Festo nyɛ onukpɛ̃ kĩ bɛkpã Paulo bɛyamɛna.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Ade Festo lɛ kĩ, “Lɛgã Agripa mɔ mi a flee kĩ mikpɔta nɛ awĩ a, onyole kĩĩ nyɛ oti kĩ Yudafɔ nɛ Yerusalem mɔ Kaisare wɛ kĩ bɛkɔ!

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ yĩ kamɔ kamɛ ni, ɛtákɛna lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkɔ ye. Kĩ ye a lɛmɛ ɛlɛ kĩ ɛkɔa ye fɔlɛtsa yakpa boe Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ sũ ni, yĩ malo ntásĩ.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ litsa koũ maatsɛlɛ nkɔa mmɛna yĩ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a? Kitonɛkĩ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, ɛtákɛna nfumusu adima. Ade foesũ nkɔa ye ĩyamɛna nɛ mi anɛ kamɛ titiliwu nɔ Lɛgã Agripa kĩ ntɛ bɔdzɛ ye fɔlɛtsa ni lɔkɔa maana kadi ntsɛlɛ nɛ ye akũ mma ye.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ fɔnɛ́nɛ kĩ bakɔa odi kĩ ɛwaa kɔla bɛma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛtámɔ tɔkpa a kĩ ɛkɛna a bɛtsa.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Ade ke Agripa bi Paulo kĩ, “Osi waa kĩ apuli ɔlɛ kɔdzɛla.” Foesũ Paulo dɛla awɛ ade ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate Agripa, nna lɔlɔ wĩ kĩ maanyɛ yĩ akũ onukpɛ̃ nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ foesũ Yudafɔ bɔɛ kalɛ nɛ yĩ akũ a akũ, nɛ nɔ anɛ.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ onyina Yudafɔ tɛdzɛ mɔ be tɛmaniɛ a flee kanyina wĩ. Foesũ na ɔwɔlɛ katse yĩ fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ndzɛ̃ badzɛmɔ yĩ nɛ ayekĩ yĩ anɛ teĩ nɛ Baguma kalɛ a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi ɛkpa boe bawanyɔ a sũ.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ Yudafɔ futete ewua-nviã bɔa mbɔdi kĩ basum Baguma lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee lɔkɔa bɛna kɔnɛte kĩĩ odu a. Boe Ɔlate, ade fukĩĩ sũ Yudafɔ a bɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ yĩ akũ a.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Nde sũ fudeĩ mi ɔsĩ kĩ miatɛ mido kĩ Baguma adzɛla banɔkpa?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Lɔkɔ lidi kamɛ ni, nkɔa nte nɛ yĩ akũ kĩ mayidza ndza nɛ bikĩ bitomɔ Yesu kĩ ɛnyɛ Nazaretni a akũ.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kpa yĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ mpili bati kpalɛwa pii ndo nɛ kɔla kamɛ nɛ Yerusalem. Ade ke ntɛ bakɔ be lɛmɛ matunɔ nɛ foe akũ a.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Ntomɔ be nkakɛna be tɛnya flee kɛnawo tɛmatɔ bɛbã kĩ tideĩ mɔ lugo a malo kamɛ. Nfɔ̃ bɛvɛlɛ be sɔtɔ nɛ Yudafɔ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ flee, ndza nɛ be akũ kĩ bɛlɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Yesu akũ. Paulo Lɛ Ayekĩ Ɛdani Etomɔ Yesu.

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Ade kakɔna kĩĩ odu nnɛɛ manaa Damasko, mɔ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kpa yĩ a.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate, kĩ ntsɛ̃ nɛ osi manaa mɔ suwa ketee a, mmɔ́ ɔhaĩ odi kĩ ɛkpalɛ ɛba suwa, kĩ ɛmɔanɛ eto ode ɛyako nɛ bɔmɔ́ bikĩ bɔtsɛ̃ bɔanaa akũ.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Kĩ boe flee bɔka bɔtɔ a, nnu ɔlɛdo odi nɛ Yudafɔ tide kamɛ evia kĩ, ‘Saulo, Saulo nde sũ otomɔ yĩ liti? Ntɛ ɔta fu sɛnɛta ni, nɔ mɔawɛ akũ opila.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: “Ade ke nvia ye kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate, nɔ owei anɛ?’ “Ɔlɛdo a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Yĩ nyɛ Yesu a kĩ ɔlamɛna ye kanɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ a.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ yidza kadza! Ĩnyɛ yĩ akũ ntsa nɔ kitonɛkĩ mawɛ kĩ nkɔa nɔ nkɛna yĩ tudzuma ɔkɛnanɛ. Aabi katinya kamɛ bati flee lɛtsa kĩ ɔmɔ́ nɛ awĩ, mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ maanyɛ ntsa nɔ a.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Ade kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ maado nɔ ni, maadza nɛ nɔ liti nɛ nɔ awɛ bati mɔ kade bɛbã bati lɛmɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Ade aabinya be anɛ bɛnɛ nɛ Abonsam ɔtũ kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ bɛya nɛ ɔhaĩ mɔ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ a. Baguma aakɔa be titikpa ete be lɔkɔa ɛkɛna be ndɛ bati kĩ ɛnyɛ ɛkpa ye akũ, nɛ kĩ bɛtɛ ye bido sũ.’ Paulo Lɛ Ye Tudzuma Kakɛna Fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
translation_verse: “Foesũ, boe Ɔlate Agripa, ntátu kanyɛ katsa a kĩ mmɔ́ ntomɛna ode a atsɛlɛ.

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Ĩyi kayɔ mɔ kalɛ wĩ a kabi Damaskofɔ, Yerusalemfɔ, Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ bɛya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa bɛkaakɛna fɔtsa a kĩ foe aatsa kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ yededeede a.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ sũ Yudafɔ a yapi yĩ nɛ be Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ kĩ ndza nkalɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ, ade ke bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ yĩ a.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma nyɛ yĩ nɛ be awɛ kamɛ. Foesũ mbuki ndzi nkpã ade nlamɛna fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ kanyɛ katsa mi banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ bati flee a. Lɛtsa a kĩ de nlamɛna kalɛ nɛ awĩ a nyɛ fukĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ mɔ Mose lɛ biyi kĩ fɔ́aya nɛ foe kamɛ a.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ade foe nyɛ kĩ Kristo aayamɔ lubo. Ade ye aanya oti ɔtɔnyade kĩ yaayidza eto banɔkpa kamɛ, lɔkɔa ɛkɔa ɔsɛdza ɛma Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a flee a.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ Festo bɔa fɔwɔla ɛlɛ kĩ, “Paulo, nɔ lɔlɔ kamɛ bu kade! Nɔ fɔtsa beblebee kakɔlɛ fɔ̃ nɔ lɔlɔ kamɛ bu kade!”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Ade Paulo kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate Festo, yĩ lɔlɔ kamɛ tábu kade. Takɔa fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ ɔkɛna kagulo. Kalɛ wĩ paa nlamɛna kalɛ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali ni, Lɛgã Agripa nyina fɔlɛtsa kĩ malɛ a. Foesũ maapuli nlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ ye faa. Matɛ kado paa kĩ bɛtákɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ nɛ kabɛla kamɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Nda Agripa, ɔtɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a odo? Ĩnyina kĩ ɔtɛ be odo.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Ade Agripa bi Paulo kĩ, “Ɔkɔna kĩ aapuli ɔdani yĩ nkɛna okristoni nɛ lɔkɔ lukũ kĩĩ kamɛ?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Ade ke Paulo kpaa mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ lɔkɔ lukũ ĩye lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ malo ni, mabɔa mpaɛ nkpa Baguma kĩ, nviã kĩĩ mi a flee kĩ mideĩ nɛ awĩ a, midani mikɛna ndɛ ayekĩ nla. Lɛmɛ bɛtádo mi nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ ndɛ yĩ aye!”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Ade lɛgã, abãã ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ Bernike mɔ bati a flee yidza a.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Kĩ biyidza a, bɛnaa adzina ade bibi be akũ kĩ, “Oti kĩĩ tákɛna lɛtsa kpa lidima kĩ bakɔ ye ĩye bido ye nɛ kɔla.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ade Agripa bi Festo kĩ, “Ntɛ obi kĩĩ ɛtalɛ kĩ ɛkɔa ye fɔlɛtsa yama Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ ni, kufɛ bɔ́apuli bɔfɔnɛ ye awɛ.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Kĩ bike otse kĩ bɔanaa Italia a, bɛkɔa Paulo mɔ kɔla bawanɛ bɛbã bido nɛ Romafɔ sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩ bavɛ Yulio awɛ kamɛ. Ade ye adãmɛna sodzafɔ katsu a kĩ bavɛ Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Sodzafɔ Katsu akũ a.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Ade ke budzi ɔkɔlɔ kĩ eto Adramitio yanaa tɔkɔlɔ fɔdzakɔ̃ nɛ Asia kamɛ a. Bɔmɔ Makedoniani kĩ bavɛ Aristarko lidzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ. Eto Tesalonika.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Kĩ kade nyina a, bɔnadza nɛ Sidon mpo abuĩ. Ade Yulio kpa Paulo osi kĩ ɛnaa ɔmatɔ kamɛ ɛnaamɔ ye bakɔba, lɔkɔa bɛkpa ye fɔtsa a kĩ yaakɔa ɛtsã osi a.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔbɔɛ nɛ awã a nɛ kĩ ɔwɛ kabɔa katsɛlamɔ boe a onukpɛ̃ deĩ ɔsi sũ ni, bɔtsã Kipro kayɔ sɛmakɛ bɔbɛla ɔwɛ a.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔtɛ̃ awã a, bɔtsã Kilikia mɔ Pamfilia mpo abuĩ, ade bɔnadza nɛ Mira nɛ Likia a.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Awĩ a sodzafɔ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ yakpã boe a mɔ́ ɔkɔlɔ odi kĩ eto Aleksandria yanaa Italia tite akũ, ade ke ɛkɔa boe flee edo nɛ ye kamɛ a.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Osi a katsã kamɛ na boe ɔsĩ nɛ fuwi pii kamɛ fɔmɔ bupuli buwo Knido ɔmatɔ a kamɛ. Kĩ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kabɔa sũ ni, bɔtápuli bɔtɛ̃ bɔnaa kpĩĩ. Foesũ kĩ buwo Salmone a, bɔblɛ bɔtsã Kreta ɔmatɔ a ɔlɔtɔ, awã kĩ tite a sua ɔwɛ a kɔbɛ̃ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Ɔsĩ kamɛ bɔkɔa bɔtsã mpo abuĩ a bɔnawo kakɔ a kĩ bavɛ Tɔkɔlɔ Kadzakɔ̃ Wĩ a. Awã mɔ Lasea kayite lɛlá lugo.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Bubula fuwi kade, ade kĩ mui akũ katsã lɛmɛ na ɔsĩ sũ ni, Onukpɛ̃ Kasui Lɔkɔ a tɛ̃. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ Paulo kpa be kakɔna ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Nda amɛ, mmɔ́ ĩyi ɔsũ kĩ boe mui anɛ osi katsã aawo nɛ mbɛtahu kamɛ, kĩ fɔtsa pii mɔ bati malo aawɔ.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kawũ banɔnɛ ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ tɛ ɔkɔlɔ ɔtanɛ mɔ ye ɔlate a kakɔna ɛba lɛtsa kĩ Paulo lɛ a.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ tɔkɔlɔ kadzakɔ̃ a lɛnɛ́nɛ kadzi gɛlɛɛlɛ nɛ osili lɔkɔ sũ ni, osi batsãnɛ a wɛ kĩ bɛdzakũ ɔsa baawo Foenike. Baapuli bidzi nɛ awã nɛ osili lɔkɔ, kitonɛkĩ Foenike nyɛ ɔmatɔ nɛ Kreta tite akũ. Edeĩ tɔkɔlɔ fɔdzakɔ̃ kĩ futsua ɔwɛ onukpɛ̃ nɛ suwa katomukɔ̃ a. Ɔwɛ Mɔ Ɔsĩ Vũ Nɛ Mpo Akũ.

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Kĩ ɔwɛ bɔɛɛ do kabɔa kademɛna awɛ a, fɔkɛna ndɛ fɔ́alɛ fɔkpa osi a akũ kanaa. Foesũ bɛmanɛ akũ ade bɛtsã Kreta mpo abuĩ a.

english_verse: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ owi tábuna mɔ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ odi vũ eto Kreta sɛba sɛmakɛ.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Ɛvũ ɛtsɛlamɔ ɔkɔlɔ a lɔtɔ foesũ bɛtápuli bɛdani ɔkɔlɔ a anɛ bɛtsɛlamɔ mpo abuĩ. Nɛ foesũ bɛfɔnɛ ɔkɔlɔ a awɛ ade ɔwɛ a bɔa ye ɛkanamɛna a.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Ke liti kɔlaa, bɔyana kablakɔ̃ kadi nɛ Kauda tite kĩ mui tsua bisinya te liti. Kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ fɔmɔ bupuli busuli boe ɔkɔlɔ kabii a budo nɛ ɔkpale a kĩ ye kamɛ bɔna a kamɛ.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛmanɛ ye biyua a, bɔkɔa tɔwɛ̃ bisuli bɔtɛ̃mɛna bupimɔ ɔkɔlɔ kpale a kitonɛkĩ bivila kĩ ɔwɛ aabɔa ye ɛnaabãã nɛ dã kamɛ nɛ Libia mpo abuĩ. Foesũ bɛkɛna tilima nɛ ɔkɔlɔ titse a akũ. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ɔwɛ a kabɔa ye kanamɛna bɔɛɛ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Kade nyina, lɛmɛ kĩ ɔwɛ a táfɔ̃ kavũ sũ ni, bido fɔtsa a kĩ ɔkɔlɔ a nɔnɛ a kanyɛ kawula nɛ mui kamɛ awɛ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ade nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde a, bɛyatalɛ ɔkɔlɔ a akũ fɔtsa pii lɛmɛ biwula.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩĩ vũ fuwi beblebee kĩ bɛtámɔ suwa ĩye fusibii malo. Ade bɔtátɛ budo kĩ bɔ́ana nkpã a.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Kĩ bidzi fuwi beblebee kĩ bɛtánya sũ ni, Paulo yidza ɛdza nɛ be anɛ kamɛ, ade ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ntɛ mitse yĩ kalɛ ni, kufɛ bɔtábɔɛ nɛ Kreta, kufɛ fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ flee taya nɛ boe akũ, bɔyɛla boe akũ fɔtsa.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, make mi aduli kĩ mina ɔwɔlɛ. Ade mabi mi yededeede kĩ ɔkɔlɔ kĩĩ aayɛla lɛmɛ mi kamɛ odima lɛláaku.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kadzɔ lɛnyɛ ni, Baguma a kĩ ye ode manɛ, mɔ ekĩ masum a, ye ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ yadza nɛ yĩ ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: Ade ke ebi yĩ kĩ, ‘Paulo, tavila. Kɛnɛ kĩ akɔa nɔ kalɛ oyi nɛ Kaisare anɛ kamɛ! Nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ sũ ni, nɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛtsɛ̃ osi a flee kamɛ odima lɛláaku.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Foesũ, nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mina ɔwɔlɛ! Ntɛ Baguma ndo kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa aaya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ eyi a.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɔwɛ aabɔa boe ɛnamɛna tite tidi kĩ mui tsua bisinya te a akũ.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Foesũ bikpeli fɔbɔtsaɛ mɔ ɔlɔmɛ kĩ fupini ɔkɔlɔ a tɔwɛ̃ bido nɛ mui kamɛ, ade biwũnya otse a kĩ ye atsa ɔkɔlɔ a osi a. Ke liti bɛdzɛla ɔkɔlɔ a tilima ayekĩ ɔwɛ aabɔa ye ɛnamɛna.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
