translation_verse: Kĩ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ adzuma bakɛnanɛ wɛ kĩ balo sũ ni, bɛkɛna ɔkɔlɔ kabii a, nɛ tɛdake kamɛ kĩ banaakɛna fɔbɔtsaɛ mɔ ɔlɔmɛ a nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kpale a lɔtɔ.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ bivila kĩ ɔwɛ a aabɔa ɔkɔlɔ a ɛnaasãmɔ boe kpale kpale sũ ni, bɛkɔa fɔbɔtsaɛ mɔ ɔlɔmɛ ɛna kĩ bido ɔwɛ̃ bipimɔ ɔkɔlɔ a nɛ liti bido nɛ mui a kamɛ. Fɔbɔtsaɛ a nabãã nɛ mui ɔda fɔfɔ̃ ɔkɔlɔ a dza. Ade ke bɛkadã anɛ kĩ kade wĩ kinyina a.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Paulo bi sodzafɔ a mɔ be lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, “Ntɛ bati kĩĩ flee tána nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kĩĩ kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, míaku.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Foesũ sodzafɔ a tsua ɔwɛ̃ a kĩ epini ɔkɔlɔ kabii a, ade ɛka ewo nɛ mui a kamɛ a.

english_verse: Just before daylight Paul begged the people to eat something. He told them, “For
translation_verse: Odenyinawi a, Paulo ke be flee aduli kĩ bɛnya. Ebi be kĩ, “Ade nviã nyɛ fuwi ewuana kĩ mi kamɛ odima táta lɛtsama onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ, make mi aduli, minya litsedi lɔkɔa mina kɔbɛ̃ ayekĩ miláaku. Kitonɛkĩ odima sɔlɔ mɔapɛ malo lɛláakamu.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Ade ke Paulo bɔɛ abodoo adi, ade kĩ ɛbɔa mpaɛ ɛkpa Baguma nɛ be flee anɛ a, ebuli adi ɛba.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Ade ke ɔwɔlɛ ka edzi odi nyɛ odi bido kanya awɛ a.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Kĩ bati a nya biwĩ a, bɛnyɛ funitsã mɔ fuvũ a flee nɛ ɔkɔlɔ a kamɛ biko nɛ mui kamɛ ade ɔkɔlɔ a nɛ́ ɔlɔmɛ a. Ɔkɔlɔ a Yɛlɛ.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Kĩ fɔkɔ kpalɛ a, ɔkɔlɔ batanɛ a támɔ awã mpo abuĩ a biyi ɔsũ. Lɛmɛ bɛmɔ́ kadzakɔ̃ kadi kĩ bɛkɔna kĩ awã aalɛ bɛdza. Foesũ bɛwɛ kĩ ntɛ fɔ́alɛ ni bata ɔkɔlɔ a bɛnamɛna awã.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɛyɛlɛ banaa a, ɔkɔlɔ a nabãã nɛ dã kamɛ ade ye anɛ sɛmakɛ tábuki epuli ekuna akũ a. Ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ a lɛmɛ yabo ye nɛ liti sɛmakɛ a ɛyɛlɛ ye fɔkɛ fɔkɛ.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Sodzafɔ a ke otse kĩ bakɔ kɔla bawanɛ a flee ayekĩ bɛláato mui bilo.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ be lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ wɛ kĩ yatɛ Paulo nkpã, foesũ ɛtátunɔ bɛkɛna nɛ be kawɛ akũ. Ade ɛkpa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ ntɛ bikĩ baapuli bito mui ni, bɛnya lɔtɔ bɛvɛnya biwo bito mui bɛnaa mpo abuĩ.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bite a lɛmɛ bɛbɔɛ titse fɔkɛ mɔ fɔtsa bɛbã kĩ fɔ́apuli fɔtsãmɔ be nɛ mui anɛ lɔkɔa biwo ɔwɔ abuĩ. Ade aye bɔkɛna buwo ɔwɔ abuĩ sɔũ a.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Ade kĩ bɔnɛ nɛ mbɛtahu kamɛ a, bɔyamɔ kĩ tite a kĩ mui tsua bisinya te a bavɛ Malta.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Bati nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ a tɛ boe mɔ kawɔlɛ paa a. Nɛ kĩ oni kako osili lɛmɛ na sũ ni, bɛkpɔta fi kpale nɛ mpo abuĩ a bɛkpa boe.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Kĩ Paulo fila tuvotsei kĩ yako nɛ fi kamɛ a, nɛ fi a ɔwɛ ɔbɔ̃ sũ kɔɛ kudi nɛ nɛ avo a kamɛ kɔyadɔ̃ ye kɔvanɛ nɛ ye kɔkpɔ.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Kĩ awã bati a mɔ kɔɛ a kĩ kɔvanɛ nɛ ye kɔkpɔ a, bibi akũ kĩ, “Owudini oti kĩĩ anɛ. Ade foesũ boe baviɔ̃ lɛláafɔ̃ edzi nkpã a, titɔ kĩ elo nɛ wuda awɛ kamɛ nɛ mpo kamɛ malo.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Ade Paulo wunya kɔɛ a edo nɛ fi a kamɛ a, lɛmɛ lɛtsama tákɛna ye.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Ade bati a dza bɛkadã anɛ kĩ yaawumi ĩye yaaka ɛtɔ deedimɔapɛ eku. Owi buna kĩ bɛdza bɛkadã, ade kĩ lɛtsama tákɛna ye a, bɛdani be adzuni nɛ ye akũ, ade bɛlɛ kĩ, baguma adi yanɛ.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Awã kĩ bɔnadza a, fuãmɔ Publio kĩ ɛnyɛ tite a lɛgã. Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ kĩĩ lɛmɛ tɛ boe mɔ kawɔlɛ ade ɛdã boe akũ fuwi ɛlalɛ flee a.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Nɛ fuwi kĩĩ kamɛ ni, Publio ɔlɛga kasɔ atilili, ade ɛkayo kɔbɔa mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ a. Paulo naa ye sɛkɛ̃, ɛnabɔa mpaɛ ete awɛ nɛ ye akũ, ade ɛsaɛ a.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Kĩ binu fɔsɔ katsa kĩĩ a, basɔnɛ nɛ tite akũ flee nɛ bɛya, ade Paulo tsa be a.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Nɛ lɛkɛnana kĩĩ sũ bɛkpa boe fɔtsa pii. Ade kĩ boe kadzakũ wo ɔtɔ a, bɛma boe fɔtsa a kĩ bɔ́akɔa bɔtsã osi. Bɛtɛ̃ Malta Bɛnaa Roma.

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Bɔtɛ tidetɔ ɛlalɛ nɛ awã fɔmɔ bɔdzakũ. Kĩ bɔadzakũ a budzi ɔkɔlɔ kĩ bavɛ Nta Baviɔ̃ kĩ eto Aleksandria ɛyadza nɛ kasekɔ̃ a nɛ osili lɔkɔ.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔbɔɛ buto awã a, bɔnaa Sirakuse ɔmatɔ a, ade bɔna nɛ awã fuwi ɛlalɛ a.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Ade ke luwi lɛyade buto awã bunyina bɔnaa Regio a. Ɔwɛ wĩ odi bɔa boe nɛ liti, ade bɔyawo Puteoli a.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Awã naa, bɔmɔ́ batɛ badonɛ badi, ade bike boe aduli kĩ budzi nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ ɛkwansieda mɔa. Nɛ ke liti a, buto awã bɔnaa Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Kĩ boe bawaɛ̃ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Roma nu kĩ bɔaya a, bɛyatsɛlamɔ boe nɛ awã kĩ bavɛ Apia Kɔyaatɔ a. Badi lɛmɛ yatsɛlamɔ boe nɛ Fusekɔ̃ Ɛlalɛ a. Kĩ Paulo mɔ́ be a, ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ ade ke ebuki ɛna ɔwɔlɛ kado a. Paulo Wo Roma.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Kĩ buwo Roma a, bɛkpa Paulo osi kĩ edzi ye akũ akũ nɛ awã kĩ ɛwɛ a, lɛmɛ bɛfɔ̃ sodzafɔ a kamɛ ɔmɔa kadãmɛna ye akũ.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Fuwi ɛlalɛ liti a, Paulo vɛ Yudafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã a ɛkpɔta. Kĩ bɛtsɛna a, ebi be kĩ, “Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bipi yĩ nɛ Yerusalem bɛkɔa bɛma Roma abãã kĩ ɛdzɛmɔ yĩ, titɔ kĩ ntáfum odima, ĩye nfum boe bawanyɔ amaniɛ adima malo.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Romafɔ a dzɛmɔ yĩ, ade ke bɛwɛ kĩ bafɔnɛ yĩ awɛ kufɛ a, kitonɛkĩ bɛtámɔ lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ baadza bɛkɔ yĩ wuda kɔtɔ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yudafɔ a tátunɔ nɛ foe akũ sũ ni, fɔyasĩ kĩ nkɔa yĩ kalɛ mma Lɛgãnɔkɔɛ̃ Kaisare, titɔ kĩ ntábɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ yĩ kade bati akũ malo.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ sũ nvɛ mi a, kitonɛkĩ Oti a kĩ Bɛbɔa Tɔdɔ̃ a, kĩ ye akũ Yudafɔ ate anɛ a, ade ye ntɛ ndo sũ nkɔsɔnkɔsɔ kĩĩ pini yĩ a.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Ade bibi ye kĩ, “Boe kɔ̃ bɔtánu kadima butomɛna nɔ akũ. Ade bɔtátɛ ovoli ĩye ɔlɛdo kpa odima nɛ nɔ akũ butomɛna bikĩ bito Yudea bɛya nɛ awĩ a sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔawɛ kĩ bɔatse litsedi butomɛna lɛtsa a kĩ de ɔtɛ odo a akũ. Kitonɛkĩ baálɛ kalɛ wĩ kadima nɛ katɛkado katsu a kĩ ke kamɛ odeĩ a akũ nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Biyi luwi ade ke bati pii yatsɛna nɛ Paulo awã a. Awã naa, ebi be fɔlɛtsa nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ. Ade ɛtsa be fɔtsa nɛ Yesu akũ etomɛna Mose mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a tuvoli kamɛ a. Ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla kito ɔnɔnɛ kɛnawo olobe.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Be kamɛ badi tɛ fɔlɛtsa a bido, badi lɛmɛ tátɛ bido.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ dani kavɛlɛ nɛ be kamɛ, ade bɛsɛ̃ be akũ a. Paulo bi be kĩ, “Hũhũ Kpalɛwa lɛ ɔnɔkɔali ɛtsã Yesaya akũ ebi boe bawanyɔ kĩ,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ‘Naa kanaabi Yudafɔ a kĩ, “Míanu yĩ fɔlɛtsa lɛmɛ miláanu foe kayɔ, míako anɛ midã lɛmɛ miláamɔ foe miyi ɔsũ.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati kĩĩ tɔwɔlɛ deĩ ɔsĩ, ade be atɔ sĩ a, ade bisua anɛ lɛmɛ a. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, kufɛ be anɛ aamɔ ade be atɔ aanu, be adzuni kamɛ lɛmɛ aakpalɛ, ade baadani bɛya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ lɔkɔa ntsa be fɔsɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Paulo wũna kɔdzɛla kalɛ mɔ be kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ minyi kĩ, bɛkɔa Baguma bati nkpã katɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a bɛma bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ. Be ni, baatɛ foe!”

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Paulo dzi nɛ awã futeli nviã nɛ kɔla kĩ ɛwã a kamɛ. Ade awã ɛkatɛ bati a flee kĩ bɛkaya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Ɛkalɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ akũ fɔlɛtsa mɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ flee kakpa be, ɛtsa be fɔtsa etomɛna boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo akũ, ade odima tásua ye osi a.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Ovoli kĩĩ to yĩ, Paulo, Yesu Kristo ɔsumunɛ sɛkɛ̃. Baguma lɛpau yĩ, ɛvɛ yĩ kĩ ĩyadani ye fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ, nde lɛtsã nɛ ye Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Baguma lɛ Kalɛ Wĩ kĩĩ eyi anɛ kɔkɔɛ ɛtsãmɛna ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ akũ, ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ a.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Nɛ kakpalɛ nɛ hũhũ kamɛ ni, Baguma nyɛ ye ɛtsa mɔ kɔbɛ̃ ndɛ ye obi, lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛdzɛla ye etomɛna wuda kamɛ a.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Baguma tsã Yesu Kristo akũ ɛmɔ́ boe kɔnya bɔyadani fɔtɔ banyanɛ, ayekĩ fɔ́atsã ye dĩ akũ, bɔ́akpã fude fude bati flee bɛtɛ ye bido, bitse ye.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Mi Romafɔ lɛmɛ, mideĩ nɛ bati a kĩ Baguma vɛ kĩ bɛyaakɛna Yesu Kristo bade a kamɛ.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Ade foesũ ntsɛlɛ ovoli kĩĩ mama mi flee a kĩ mideĩ nɛ Roma, kĩ Baguma awɛ mi kalɛ, ade ɛvɛ mi kĩ miyaakɛna bati kĩ ɛkpalɛ be akũ a. Baguma boe Ɔlɛga mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo, bɛmɔ mi kɔnya, bɛkpa mi lukudɔ lɛmɛ! Paulo Nyɛ Baguma Kɔlɛ.

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyaa lɔtɔ nyɛ kĩ, nkɔa Yesu Kristo manyɛ yĩ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ mi flee lɔlɔ, kitonɛkĩ balɛ ayekĩ mitɛ Kristo mido a fɔlɛtsa nɛ katinya kamɛ flee.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ masum mɔ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ flee, nɛ lɛtsã a kĩ made nɛ ye Obi Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ a, nyɛ yĩ adansiɛ kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ malɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Baguma nyina kĩ mate anɛ nɛ mi akũ,

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ mabɔa mpaɛ. Mawã Baguma kĩ ntɛ fɔnyɛ ye kawɛ ni, ɛkpa yĩ osi ĩyaasɛla mi finyaa.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ mawɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bɔmɔ akũ, lɔkɔa nkɔa hũhũ kamɛ futetsa fudi mma mi nkɔa ndo mi kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ matsa nyɛ kĩ bɔmɔ mi bɔ́ado akũ kɔbɛ̃. Yĩ katɛkado ado mi kɔbɛ̃, ade mi kade lɛmɛ aado yĩ kɔbɛ̃ a.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mawɛ kĩ minyi kĩ nkɛna nhihiɛ lɔkɔ pii kĩ mayaasɛla mi kufɛ, lɛmɛ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ litsedi asua yĩ osi. Mawɛ kĩ mana bati kĩ bɛtɛ Kristo bido nɛ mi kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ nna nɛ bati bɛbã a kĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Ade foesũ akũ asĩ yĩ kĩ made lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nkpa mi a kĩ mideĩ nɛ Roma lɛmɛ a. Kalɛ Wĩ a Deĩ Kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kalɛ Wĩ a lákɔ yĩ kɔnyɔã, kitonɛkĩ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ kɔanɛ. Kakpa bati a kĩ bɛtɛ ke bido a nkpã, Yudafɔ nyaa lɔtɔ fɔmɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Kalɛ Wĩ a kamɛ banyɛ ayekĩ Baguma abu bati kĩ bɛkpɔla a katsa. Fɔnyɛ katɛkado fɔlɛtsa kĩto kayɔkayi kɛnawo kawũna. Ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Oti kĩ Baguma abu kĩ yadã kpĩĩ nɛ ye katɛkado sũ ni, yaana nkpã.” Odima Lɛnyɛ́ Oti Kpĩĩ Nɛ Baguma Anɛ.

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Baguma anyɛ ye kalɔkpa katomɛna ode kako nɛ bati a kĩ bakɔa tɔkpa mɔ ablɔ fɔtsa kasua ɔnɔkɔali a kanyi osi a akũ.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Baguma avɛlɛ bati a sɔtɔ kitonɛkĩ binyina fɔtsa a kĩ otidziwa aapuli enyi nɛ ye akũ a. Baguma mɔawɛ a lɛnyɛ foe ɛtsa be yededeede.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Kito anɛ kĩ Baguma bɔa katinya a, ye kutitse kĩ baámɔ mɔ anɛ a, mɔ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ kudeĩ daa mɔ ye Baguma kutidu a, anyɛ foe akũ katsa nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ ɛbɔa a kamɛ. Foesũ bati a lɛláapuli bɛnyɛ onukpɛ̃ kĩ binyína Baguma.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ binyina Baguma malo ni, baákpa ye obu a kĩ ɛfɛta ye, ade baányɛ ye kɔlɛ a. Mbom fɔtsa a kĩ fɔlá nfasɔ akũ bakɔna, ade be adzuni kamɛ ke ɔtũ a.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Babu be akũ kĩ binyina kadi, lɛmɛ bɛdani badzimbifɔ.

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ bɛkɔa obu bɛkpa Baguma a kĩ edzi nkpã daa a ni, basum fɔtsa a kĩ bɛmɛ mɔ awɛ kutidu, kĩ fɔla ndɛ oti kĩ yaku, ĩye kɔvɔlabi, ĩye bayaɛ, ĩye bayaɛ kĩ baboli nɛ sɛmɛ akũ.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ Baguma fɔnɛ be awɛ kĩ bɛkɛna efĩĩ fɔtsa kĩ be tɔwɔlɛ awɛ a, kĩ bɛkɔa be sukɛna bakɛna fɔnyɔãtsa mɔ be akũ akũ.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Bakɔa Baguma akũ ɔnɔkɔali a kavɛmɛna mɔ fɔvã. Basum fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa a kaba Baguma mɔawɛ a kĩ ɛbɔa foe a, kĩ kɛfɛta kĩ bɛkaamu daa a! Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩ bakɛna a sũ, Baguma fɔnɛ be awɛ ɛkpa sukɛna fɔwɛtsa kĩ fɔado akũ kɔnyɔã kakɛna. Be batsole dani bawamɔ bakɔba batsole.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Ade ayea banyole lɛmɛ fɔ̃ bɛmɔ batsole kawa ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma kɛna eyi a, mbom bakɔba banyole akɔ be odo. Foesũ banyole awamɔ akũ kĩ kɔnyɔã lɛláa foe kamɛ. Nɛ ɔbla kpa kĩ bɛbɔa kĩĩ sũ ni, Baguma vɛlɛ be sɔtɔ a kĩ sɛfɛta be a, nɛ be mɔawɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ bati a sĩ kĩ baátɛ kadikanyi wĩ nɛ Baguma akũ a bido nɛ be kamɛ sũ ni, Baguma fɔnɛ be awɛ ɛkpa be adzuni kĩ bibula kade a, kĩ bɛkɛna fɔtsa a kĩ fɔtáfɛta a.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Be kamɛ yi mɔ tɔkpa tidi nyɛ tidi kakɛna, ablɔ, anɛngbã mɔ bati tɔkpa kawɛ. Fɔbɛbã kĩ fudeĩ nɛ be kamɛ nyɛ lineke kanɔ, awudi, lɛma kavũ, bati kakɛ mɔ likesĩ. Fukĩ fubu nyɛ kĩ balɛ bati akũ kɔdzɛla,

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: bɛnyɛ bati buĩ banyɛnɛ, mɔ Baguma bakesĩnɛ. Bɛnyɛ bati bavũnanɛ, akũ batanɛ, akũ bapianɛ, bawɛ tɔkpa tisi bɛbã pii kayamɛna mɔ bati kĩ baábu be bamanɛ a.

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: Bɛnyɛ bati kĩ be tɔwɔlɛ lákɔ be kɔtɔ, baákɛna nɛ bɔhɛ a akũ a, bɛlá sɛmɛ kalɛ ade baámɔ oti kɔnya a.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ binyina pefee kĩ nɛ Baguma mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, bikĩ bidzi nkpã nɛ osi kĩĩ odu akũ a fɛta bɛkpa wuda malo ni, bɛkakɛna foe. Nnɛ́ foe odi bakɛna, mbom bɛdza nɛ bikĩ bakɛna fɔtsa kpa kĩĩ odu a liti.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃, nɔ akɔ bati bɛbã kɔtɔ? Ntɛ litsa oti anɛ malo ɔláapuli ɔnyɛ onukpɛ̃ kĩ nɔ kɔ̃ oti kpĩĩ anɛ. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ adzɛmɔ bati bɛbã adeke akɛna lɛtsa mɔapɛ a kĩ de bakɛna a ni, nɔ mɔawɛ akũ akɔ kɔtɔ.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ Baguma deĩ ɔtɔbɛ ntɛ ɛdzɛmɔ bati a kĩ bakɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ a.

english_verse: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nwaɛ̃, nɔ a kĩ akɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ a lɛmɛ akɔ bati bɛbã kɔtɔ nɛ foe akũ a ni, abu kĩ aapuli olo Baguma kɔtɔkakɔ?

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ ɔbɔɛ foe kĩ Baguma deĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ, ɔwɔlɛ kado mɔ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ sũ ɔtsɛ́la lɛtsama? Onyina kĩ nuwĩ Baguma deĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ, kitonɛkĩ yawɛ kĩ yakpã nɔ ɔdani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ nɔ atɔ ɔsĩ kĩ aádani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ sũ ni, ɔfɔ̃ nɔ sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ aana ɔsɛ̃ nɛ luwi a kĩ Baguma aanyɛ ye kalɔkpa mɔ ye fɔlɛtsa kadzɛ kpĩĩ akũ a kanya.

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma aase odi nyɛ odi kɔtɔ nɛ ye adzuma a kĩ ɛkɛna a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bakɛna tuwĩ kĩ bawɛ kukũkpalɛ, obu mɔ daa nkpã a, Baguma aakpa be nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati bɛbã a kĩ be odi fude bawɛ, kĩ bisĩ ɔnɔkɔali a, bitomɔ tɔkpa a, Baguma aakɔa kalɔkpa ɛvɛlɛ be sɔtɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Bati flee a kĩ bakɛna tɔkpa a, aamɔ lubo mɔ kalɔɛ, Yudafɔ aanya lɔtɔ, ke liti bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma aakpa bikĩ bakɛna tuwĩ a kukũkpalɛ, obu mɔ lukudɔ, Yudafɔ aanya lɔtɔ bɛna, fɔmɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma ládã oti anɛ nɛ ye fɔlɛtsa kadzɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a tána Mose mbla a, lɛmɛ kĩ bɛkɛna titikpa ni, Baguma aavɛlɛ be sɔtɔ, kĩ ɛláakɔa mbla a bɛdzɛmɔ be. Yudafɔ a na Mose mbla a, lɛmɛ bɛkɛna titikpa, Baguma aakɔa mbla a ɛdzɛ mɔ be.

english_verse: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ bati a kĩ batse mbla a nyɛ bati kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, mbom bati a kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ mbla akũ a, adã kpĩĩ.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Be fɔkɛlatsa atsa kĩ Baguma tsɛlɛ mbla a nɛ be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Be tɔwɔlɛ lɛmɛ atsa kĩ fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, kitonɛkĩ fɔkɔ fudi be tɔwɔlɛ akɔ be kɔtɔ ade yadza nɛ be liti a.

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Avɛ nɔ akũ kĩ Yudani, akɔa anɛ kate nɛ mbla akũ, ade apia nɛ Baguma akũ a.

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: Onyina lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma awɛ kĩ kɛna, ade ɔkɔlɛ otomɛna mbla a kamɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ fɔtsa wĩ a.

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: Atɛ kado kĩ ɔnyɛ banɛkunɛ ɔkpãnɛ, ɔnyɛ ɔhaĩ ɔkpa bikĩ bideĩ nɛ ɔtũ kamɛ a.

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: Ɔkɔa nɔ akũ ɔkɛna bakɔasia ɔvɛlɛnɛ mɔ bikĩ bɛtábo anɛ tɔ̃ fɔtsa ɔtsanɛ. Onyina yededeede lɛmɛ kĩ kadikanyi mɔ ɔnɔkɔali deĩ nɛ mbla a kamɛ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Nɔ a kĩ atsa bati bɛbã fɔtsa a, nde sũ aátsa nɔ mɔawɛ akũ? Ade lɛtsã kĩ bati bɛtaye, lɛmɛ nɔ mɔawɛ a aáye?

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: Abi bati kĩ bɛtabɔa adɔpaa, lɛmɛ nɔ mɔawɛ a aábɔa adɔpaa? Aáwɛ baviɔ̃ fɔlɛtsa, lɛmɛ aáye kuviɔ̃la kamɛ fɔtsa?

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Abo ɔwɔlɛ nɛ Baguma mbla a akũ, lɛmɛ afum ye mbla a kakɔado Baguma kɔnyɔã.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Mi Yudafɔ ɔbla kpa sũ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a alɛ fɔlɛtsa kpa nɛ Baguma dĩ akũ.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Ntɛ akɛna nɛ mbla a akũ ni, kekɔ̃ nɔ lɛkpati katsua a akũ deĩ nfasɔ, lɛmɛ ntɛ aákɛna nɛ mbla a akũ kɔ̃ ni, oketimɔ bikĩ bɛtátsua fɔkpati a.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ Yudani, kĩ ɛtátsua lɛkpati akɛna nɛ mbla a akũ ni, Baguma lɛláabu ye ndɛ oti kĩ etsua lɛkpati aye?

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Foesũ, mi Yudafɔ, oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ Yudani a kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ mbla a akũ aakɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ miafum mbla a, titɔ kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ ye biyi bɛkpa mi, ade miatsua lɛkpati a.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: mbom Yudani kpanunuunu nyɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ Yudani nɛ ye kamɛkamɛ, ade foe nyɛ kĩ bitsua ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ lɛkpati. Lɛkpati katsua kĩĩ nyɛ Baguma Hũhũ adzuma, fɔtáto mbla a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ biyi a kamɛ. Baguma amu oti kĩĩ odu, nnɛ́ batidziwa.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Litsa koũ Yudani deĩ ɛbɛ̃ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ Yudani, ĩye litsa nfasɔ deĩ nɛ lɛkpati katsua kamɛ?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali nfasɔ a pɔnɛ nɛ tisi pii akũ. Katɔnyade nyɛ kĩ Yudafɔ awɛ Baguma kɔa ye fɔlɛtsa a edo.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nde ntɛ be kamɛ badi tátɛ bido a? Katsa fɔatsa kĩ be katatɛkado aafɔ̃ Baguma lɛláadi ɔnɔkɔali?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Oowo! Kɛnɛ kĩ Baguma adi ɔnɔkɔali, titɔ kĩ oti nyɛ oti ama fɔvã malo, ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ ɔlɛ kɔdzɛla ni, nɔ fɔlɛtsa fɔnya ɔnɔkɔali. Ntɛ bɛdzɛmɔ nɔ ni, aanya akũ.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ boe titikpa kakɛna anyɛ fɔtsa wĩ a kĩ Baguma akɛna a katsa yededeede a ni, bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ kĩ Baguma akɛna tɔkpa ntɛ ɛvɛlɛ boe sɔtɔ? Ade kavia kĩ otidziwa aavia a.

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Oowo! Ntɛ Baguma ládã kpĩĩ ni, nde yaakɛna ɛdzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Ntɛ yĩ fɔvã kama anyɛ Baguma ɔnɔkɔali a katsa, kĩ bamu ye a, nde sũ bakɔ yĩ kɔtɔ ndɛ tɔkpa ɔkɛnanɛ?

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Nde sũ bɔálɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bɔkɛna titikpa ayekĩ tuwĩ aanɛ tito foe kamɛ?” Ɔnɔkɔali bati badi lamɛna fɔvã kama kate nɛ yĩ akũ kĩ ade aye nlɛ a. Baguma aakɔ be kɔtɔ ndɛ ayekĩ kɛfɛta. Odima Lɛnyɛ́ Oti Kpĩĩ.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ finyaa, boe Yudafɔ kadzi kamɛ nɛnɛ kɛbɛ̃ bikĩ bɛnyɛ Yudafɔ a? Oowo! Kitonɛkĩ ĩnya lɔtɔ ntsa kɔkɔɛ kĩ tɔkpa nya Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a flee akũ,

english_verse: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Oti odima lɛláa kĩ yadã kpĩĩ. Midi oti mɔapɛ malo lɛláa.

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: Odima lɛláa kĩ yanu fɔlɛtsa kayɔ, ĩye kĩ yawɛ Baguma liti osi.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Bati flee muniki nɛ Baguma liti, be flee bɛyɛla osi, ade odima lɛláa kĩ yakɛna tuwĩ a, oti mɔapɛ malo lɛláa.

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: Be tudomɛna binya tɛwaa ndɛ linikɛ kĩ lɛwaa luto aye, bakɛ bati mɛnɛmɛnɛ mɔ be fɔmɛ. Be onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa la ndɛ kɔɛ kadɔ̃ aye.

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: Fudumutsa mɔ fɔlɔɛwotsa yi be tunukpɛ̃ kamɛ.

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: Bawɛ sɔwa kĩ bapila bati bɛkɔ be.

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: Kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ banaa ni, kawɔla mɔ fɔtsa kade kabula ate nɛ liti.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Binyína kadzi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ,

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: ade Baguma kavila kadima lɛláa be kamɛ a.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Finyaa, bunyina kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa nɛ mbla a kamɛ a atamɔ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ mbla a kayɔ, ayekĩ baasua batidziwa onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ osi, lɔkɔa Baguma lɛdzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ flee.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odima lɛnyɛ́ oti kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, nɛ ayekĩ yakɛna nɛ mbla a akũ sũ. Lɛtsa a kĩ mbla a akɛna nyɛ kĩ yafɔ̃ oti anyi kĩ ɛkɛna tɔkpa. Lɛtsa a Kĩ Lɛfɔ̃ Bɔkpɔla Nɛ Baguma Anɛ.

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa, bɛnyɛ osi a kĩ Baguma atsã kakɛna bati kpĩĩ nɛ ye anɛ a bɛtsa. Fukĩĩ lɛtsɛ́la mbla a akũ, titɔ kĩ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ di foe akũ adansiɛ malo.

english_verse: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Baguma akɛna bati badã kpĩĩ katsãmɛna be Yesu Kristo katɛkado kamɛ. Yakɛna fukĩĩ kakpa bati a kĩ bɛtɛ Kristo bido a flee, kĩ ntsitsemu adima lɛláa foe kamɛ.

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kɛna tɔkpa, ade edeĩ mɔ lugo nɛ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a ɔwɛ̃ a.

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ kɔnya a kĩ Baguma amɔ faa sũ ni, ɛtsã Kristo Yesu a kĩ ese kɔtɔ nɛ boe lɔlɔ a akũ, ɛkɛna boe bati kpĩĩ.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: lɛmɛ finyaa kɔ̃ yate anɛ nɛ be tɔkpa akũ ɛkɔa yatsa kĩ yadã kpĩĩ. Nɛ osi kĩĩ akũ, Baguma atsa kĩ ye mɔawɛ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ ade yakɛna odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ Yesu edo oti kpĩĩ a.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Finyaa ni, litsa bɔ́akɔa bubo ɔwɔlɛ? Bɔlá lɛtsamama! Mɔ litsa sũ? Ayekĩ bɔakɛna nɛ mbla a akũ sũ mbɔɛɛ? Oowo! Mbom ayekĩ bɔtɛ budo sũ.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔ́awũna kĩ katɛkado odi akũ fɔtsã mɔ oti adã kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, kĩ nnɛ́ mbla a akũ kakɛna sũ.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ Baguma a ni, Yudafɔ odi ode yanɛ? Nnɛ́ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ Baguma yanɛ? Oo, be malo Baguma yanɛ!

english_verse: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
translation_verse: Baguma mɔapɛ deĩ, ade yaakɛna Yudafɔ bati kpĩĩ ɛtsãmɛna be katɛkado akũ a, ɛkɛna bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ bati kpĩĩ ɛtsãmɛna be katɛkado kamɛ.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Katsa fɔatsa kĩ katɛkado sũ bɔfɔnɛ mbla a awɛ? Oowo! Fɔlá lɔmɔ kɔlaa, mbom bupini mbla a mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
translation_verse: Litsa bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ nɛ boe kade ɔwanyɔ Abraham akũ? Litsa ɛmɔ́ eyi ɔsũ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ akũ?

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Ntɛ tudzuma a kĩ ɛkɛna a akũ fɔtsã sũ Baguma bu Abraham kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ ni, kekɔ̃ yaana litsedi kĩ yakɔa ebo ɔwɔlɛ, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Abraham tɛ Baguma edo, ade nɛ ye katɛkado sũ Baguma tɛ ye kĩ yadã kpĩĩ.”

english_verse: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
translation_verse: Oti kĩ yakɛna adzuma ni, base ye kɔtɔ, lɛmɛ baábu kɔtɔkase a ndɛ futetsa, mbom fɔnyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ fɔfɛta fɔkpa ye adzuma a kĩ ɛkɛna a.

english_verse: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi tátɛ dĩ nɛ adzuma kakɛna kamɛ, mɔ ɛtɛ edo kĩ Baguma akɛna bikĩ baávila ye bati kpĩĩ ni, nɛ ye katɛkado sũ Baguma aabu ye kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Ade foe Lɛgã Dawid lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ bihila oti a kĩ Baguma bu ye kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ, kĩ nnɛ́ ye adzuma wĩ a kĩ ɛkɛna a sũ. Dawid lɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Baguma hila oti a kĩ Baguma nyɛ anɛ nɛ ye tɔkpa akũ.”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Nhila fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Dawid lɛ nɛ awĩ a ni, bikĩ bitsua fɔkpati a odi fude yanɛ, mbɔɛɛ kĩ mɔ bikĩ bɛtátsua fɔkpati a? Oowo! Ɛnyɛ bikĩ bɛtátsua fɔkpati a lɛmɛ ade, kitonɛkĩ bɔlɛ butomɛna Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Nɛ Abraham katɛkado sũ, Baguma bu ye oti kpĩĩ.”

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Ke liti kɔlaa fɔmɔ bitsua Abraham lɛkpati. Ye lɛkpati katsua lɛmɛ nyɛ nsɛntsɛlɛni kĩ Baguma tɛ ye kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ nɛ ye katɛkado sũ, titɔ kĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ malo bɛtátsua tɔ̃ ye lɛkpati. Foesũ Abraham yadani bati a kĩ bɛtɛ Baguma bido a flee hũhũ kamɛ ɔlɛga. Titɔ kĩ bɛtátsua fɔkpati malo ni, bɛtɛ be ndɛ bati kpĩĩ.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Ade ayea Abraham nyɛ bikĩ bitsua fɔkpati a lɛmɛ ɔlɛga a. Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ nɛ ayekĩ bitsua be fɔkpati a odi sũ, mbom nɛ ayekĩ bidzi nkpã nɛ katɛkado kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ boe ɔlɛga Abraham dzi fɔmɔ bitsua ye lɛkpati a sũ. Baguma Kpa Abraham Bɔhɛ.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Baguma kpa Abraham mɔ ye kasinu kamɛ babi bɔhɛ kĩ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ aanya be adzapadiɛ. Ɛkɛna foe nnɛ́ nɛ ayekĩ Abraham kɛna nɛ mbla a akũ sũ, mbom nɛ ayekĩ ɛtɛ edo kĩ Baguma bu ye oti kpĩĩ sũ.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bikĩ bakɛna nɛ mbla a akũ odi aanya fɔtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a ni, kekɔ̃ katɛkado lɛnyɛ́ lɛtsama ade Baguma kalɛkayi a lɛmɛ nyɛ fɔtsa pupulidza a.

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: Mbla a avɛlɛ Baguma kalɔkpa kayamɛna, lɛmɛ awã kĩ mbla lɛláa ni, mbla kafum lɛláa.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔhɛ a dzɛ̃ nɛ katɛkado akũ, ayekĩ bɔhɛ a aatim ndɛ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma mɔ kɔnya ɛkɔa ete Abraham kasinu a kamɛ babi. Nnɛ́ bikĩ bakɛna nɛ mbla a akũ odi fɔwaa fɔkpa, mbom mɔ bikĩ bɛtɛ bido ndɛ ayekĩ Abraham tɛ edo a, kitonɛkĩ Abraham nyɛ boe flee hũhũ kamɛ ɔlɛga,

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Nkɔa nɔ nkɛna fude fude pii ɔlɛga.” Foesũ bɔhɛ a nɛnɛ nɛ Baguma anɛ. Ade ye nyɛ Baguma a kĩ Abraham tɛ edo kĩ yadzɛla banɔkpa bayidza kadzi nkpã, kĩ yafɔ̃ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔláa kɔkɔɛ fɔanɛ katinya.

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ anɛ kate adima buki ɛláa malo ni, Abraham tɛ edo ɛkadã anɛ, ade foesũ ɛkɛna fude pii ɔlɛga, ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma bi ye a kĩ, “Nɔ babi mɔ bawa aama bɛsa beblebee ndɛ fusibii aye.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Ɛtátɛ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaakɛna ɛkpa ye a akũ atsindze, ade ɛtáfɔnɛ ye katɛkado awɛ a, mbom ye katɛkado a do ye kɔbɛ̃, edo Baguma obu.

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: Ɛtɛ edo pipiipi kĩ Baguma aapuli ɛkɛna nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a akũ.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ kĩ, “Baguma bu ye kĩ yadã kpĩĩ” a, nnɛ́ Abraham odi sũ bɛtsɛlɛ foe,

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: mbom bɛtsɛlɛ foe bɛkpa boe lɛmɛ. Boe a kĩ bɔ́atɛ oti a kĩ ɛdzɛla boe Ɔlate Yesu etomɛna kanɔkpa a budo a, baabu boe kĩ bɔnyɛ bati kpĩĩ.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Boe titikpa sũ Baguma fɔnɛ Kristo awɛ ɛkpa kĩ bɛkɔ, ade ɛdzɛla ye edzi nkpã, ayekĩ yaabu boe bati kpĩĩ.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Finyaa, kĩ Baguma bu boe bati kpĩĩ nɛ boe katɛkado sũ ni, bɔtsɛ̃ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo akũ bɔna lukudɔ nɛ bɔmɔ Baguma kayite.

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: Ye akũ lɛmɛ bɔtsã mɔ osi binya ɛkpa boe kĩ Baguma aamɔ boe kɔnya, budzi nɛ ye kɔnyakamɔ a kamɛ. Nɛ foesũ bɔapia nɛ kɔnɛte a kĩ budeĩ kĩ bɔ́ana tsɛfã nɛ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔhau pii kamɛ malo bɔamɔ kawɔlɛ, kitonɛkĩ bunyina kĩ ɔhau afɔ̃ bɔakɔlɛ ɔwɔlɛ kado,

english_verse: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
translation_verse: ɔwɔlɛ kado afɔ̃ bɔadza pintii, ade kadza pintii afɔ̃ bɔana kɔnɛte a.

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Ade kɔnɛte láfɔ̃ kɔnyɔã awo boe, kitonɛkĩ Baguma kɔa ye ɔdɔ edo nɛ boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ɛtsãmɛna ye Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kĩ ɛkpa boe a akũ.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ bubuki bɔlá kɔbɛ̃ kudima a, ade lɔkɔ a na kamɛ Kristo ku nɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ lɔlɔ, ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma mɔawɛ yi ɛkpa a.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ lɛtsa mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ oti odi aku nɛ oti kpĩĩ lɔlɔ. Fɔ́apuli fɔya malo kĩ oti odi aado ɔwɔlɛ kĩ yaku nɛ oti wĩ lɔlɔ.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma tsa boe ayekĩ eti yawɛ boe kalɛ, kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ a kĩ bubuki bɔnyɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ a, Kristo ku nɛ boe lɔlɔ.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Ɛtsã ye wuda kamɛ ɛkɛna bati boe kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ. Wuda kĩĩ sũ yaanyɛ boe etomɛna Baguma kalɔkpa a kamɛ.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Bɔnya Baguma bakesĩnɛ lɛmɛ ɛkɛna boe ye bakɔba ɛtsãmɛna ye Obi wuda a kĩ ɛkɔa ɛpata ye a kamɛ. Finyaa kĩ ɛkpɔla Baguma ɛkpa boe kĩĩ ni, yaatɛ boe ɛba, kitonɛkĩ edzi nkpã.

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ fukĩĩ odi, mbom bɔamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma tsã boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo akũ ɛlɔla bɔmɔ ye kayite a sũ. Ntsitsemu a Kĩ Edeĩ Nɛ Adam Mɔ Kristo Kayite a.

english_verse: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
translation_verse: Tɔkpa tsã oti mɔa akũ tɛya nɛ katinya kamɛ, ade wuda tsã ye akũ ɛya a. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ni, wuda yalɛ ewo nɛ batidziwa flee kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kɛna tɔkpa.

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, tɔkpa na nɛ katinya kamɛ fɔmɔ mbla a ya, lɛmɛ awã kĩ mbla lɛláa ni, baábu lɛtsama kĩ lɛnyɛ tɔkpa.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kito Adam lɔkɔ kɛyawo Mose lɔkɔ a ni, wuda nya batidziwa flee akũ, mɔ bikĩ bɛtákɛna tɔkpa ndɛ tikĩ Adam kɛna a malo. Adam kĩĩ dzɛ̃ ndɛ oti a kĩ yaaya a.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ be bati nviã a bɛtáketi, kitonɛkĩ Baguma futetsa faa a lɛlá ndɛ Adam tɔkpa a. Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ bati pii ku nɛ oti mɔa tɔkpa sũ. Lɛmɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ bɛ̃ kɔlaa, foesũ ade ayea ye futetsa a kĩ yakpa bati pii faa katsãmɛna oti mɔa kĩ ɛnyɛ Yesu Kristo akũ a la a.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Futesa a kĩ Baguma kpa faa a lɛlá ndɛ lɛtsa a kĩ oti mɔa tɔkpa vɛlɛ tɛyamɛna a, kitonɛkĩ tɔkpa a kĩ oti mɔa a kɛna a yamɔ kadzɛ mɔ kɔtɔkaku. Lɛmɛ futetsa a kĩ Baguma kpa faa a fɔ̃ bibu bati beblebee tɔkpa a kĩ bɛkɛna a kĩ badã kpĩĩ.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ oti mɔa tɔkpa a kĩ ɛkɛna sũ wuda yaanya akũ. Lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ oti mɔa a kĩ ɛnyɛ Yesu Kristo kɛna a bɛ̃ foe flee. Bikĩ bɛna Baguma kɔnyakamɔ baũ kĩĩ flee a, kĩ ɛkɛna be bati kpĩĩ lɛmɛ a, aanya akũ nɛ nkpã kamɛ bɛtsãmɛna Kristo akũ.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Foesũ ndɛ ayekĩ oti mɔa tɔkpa a kĩ ɛkɛna fɔ̃ bɛkɔ batidziwa flee kɔtɔ a, ade ayea oti mɔa tuwĩ kĩ ɛkɛna a kpa batidziwa flee ɔtɔbɛ, bɛna nkpã lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Ade ndɛ ayekĩ oti mɔa atɔ ɔsĩ kakɛna fɔ̃ bati flee dani tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ a ni, ade ayea oti mɔa Baguma ɔlɛdo akũ kakɛna aafɔ̃ bɛkɛna bati pii kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ a.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Bɛyamɛna mbla a ayekĩ tɔkpa kakɛna aapɔ ebu, lɛmɛ awã kĩ tɔkpa kakɛna pɔnɛ ni, Baguma kɔnyakamɔ lɛmɛ apɔ kabu.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Foesũ ayekĩ tɔkpa nya akũ nɛ wuda kamɛ a ni, ade ayea Baguma kɔnyakamɔ anya akũ katsãmɛna kadzi kpĩĩ kamɛ, kakpã boe kawomɛna nkpã a kĩ kɛlá kawũna a kamɛ, nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo dĩ akũ a.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Foesũ, finyaa litsa bɔ́alɛ? Bɔ́ana nɛ tɔkpa kakɛna kamɛ ayekĩ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ aapɔ kibu mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Oowo! Kĩ buku bɔkpa tɔkpa kɔkɔɛ a, nde bɔabuki budzi nkpã bɔkakɛna tɔkpa?

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ minyína kĩ, kĩ bɛwɔla boe mui, bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Yesu Kristo a, bɛwɔla boe mui bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye wuda a lɛmɛ?

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Nɛ boe mui kawɔla kĩĩ kamɛ a, bɔna tsɛfã nɛ ye wuda a kamɛ, ade bibila bɔmɔ ye a. Ndɛ ayekĩ Ɔlɛga a tsã ye kukũkpalɛ kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ ɛdzɛla Kristo etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ a, ade aye boe malo bɔ́ayidza budzi nkpã vɔɛ̃ a.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ayekĩ bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye nɛ ye wuda kamɛ a, ade ayea bɔ́abuki bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye nɛ boe kadzɛla kadzi nkpã kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ bɛdzɛla ye a.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ, kĩ bɛbɔa boe kutitse kɔɛ a nɛ asendua akũ mɔ Kristo ayekĩ boe sukɛna kĩ sawɛ tɔkpa kakɛna a aawɔ, ayekĩ bɔláabuki bɔnya tɔkpa basate.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ oti sila eku ni, bɛnyɛ ye nɛ tɔkpa kɔbɛ̃ kayɔ.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Ntɛ buku mɔ Kristo ni, bɔtɛ budo kĩ bɔ́adzi nkpã lɛmɛ mɔ ye.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ, kĩ Baguma dzɛla Kristo etomɛna kanɔkpa sũ ni, ɛláabuki eku da. Wuda buki lɛláabuki ɛna kɔbɛ̃ kudima nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Wuda a kĩ Kristo ku a, eku ye deedimɔapɛ ɛkpa tɔkpa, lɛmɛ nkpã a kĩ edzi finyaa, edzi ye ɛkpa Baguma.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Ade ayea mi malo mibu mi akũ kĩ miku mikpa tɔkpa, lɛmɛ Baguma nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo Yesu kamɛ.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ tɔkpa tibuki tɛnya mi sukɛna a kĩ saku a akũ, ayekĩ míakakɛna nɛ se fɔwɛtsa akũ.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Mitafɔnɛ mi sukɛna kakɔma awɛ mikpa tɔkpa tɛkɔa tɛkɛna ablɔ adima, mbom mikɔa mi akũ mikpa Baguma ndɛ bati kĩ ɛnyɛ etomɛna wuda kamɛ ɛyamɛna nɛ nkpã kamɛ. Mifɔnɛ mi akũ flee awɛ mikpa Baguma fɔnya fɔtsa kĩ yaakɔa ɛkɛna tuwĩ.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ tɔkpa tɛnya mi akũ, kitonɛkĩ miláa mbla kayɔ, mbom mideĩ nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ kayɔ. Tuwĩ Kakɛna Basate Mianɛ.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Foesũ litsa bɔalɛ? Bɔ́akɛna tɔkpa kitonɛkĩ bɔláa mbla kayɔ, mbom budeĩ nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ kayɔ sũ? Oowo!

english_verse: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
translation_verse: Minyina pefee kĩ ntɛ mikɔa mi akũ mikɛna odi basumunɛ ni, minyɛ ɔlate a kĩ ye kayɔ mideĩ a basate. Ntɛ mikɔa akũ mikpa tɔkpa kakɛna ni, lɛtsa a kĩ laanɛ nɛ foe kamɛ nyɛ wuda, lɛmɛ ntɛ mikɛna nɛ Baguma ɔlɛdo akũ ni, foe nfasɔ nyɛ kadzi kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Bɔanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ paa, kitonɛkĩ titɔ kĩ minya tɔkpa basate miyatɛ̃ malo ni, finyaa mitɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔtsa katsa a mɔ mi tɔwɔlɛ, milamɔ foe akũ kakɛna pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: Baguma nyɛ mi etomɛna tɔkpa kamɛ, midani fɔtsa wĩ kakɛna basate.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Malɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ otidziwa aye nɛ ayekĩ otidziwa adɔ nɛ sukɛna kamɛ sũ. Nɛ lɔkɔ lidi kĩ lɛtɛ̃ kamɛ ni, mifɔnɛ mi akũ flee awɛ ndɛ basate mikpa efĩĩ mɔ ablɔ fɔtsa kakɛna. Ade ayea kɛnɛ kĩ finyaa miafɔnɛ mi akũ flee awɛ mikpa ndɛ tuwĩ kakɛna basate a.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Kĩ minya tɔkpa kakɛna basate a, tuwĩ kakɛna tánya ɔhɛ fɔkpa mi.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Litsa nfasɔ mina mitomɛna fɔtsa a kĩ mikakɛna a kĩ finyaa fɔakɔ mi kɔnyɔã a kamɛ? Fɔtsa a na bamɛ flee kawũna nyɛ wuda.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa, Baguma nyɛ mi nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ midani ye basate. Nfasɔ a kĩ míana nyɛ kĩ miakɔa mi akũ flee mikpa Baguma ɛkpalɛ ye akũ, lɔkɔa nɛ foe kawũna mina nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ tɔkpa kɔtɔkase nyɛ wuda, lɛmɛ Baguma futetsa faa nyɛ nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a, nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ boe Ɔlate Kristo Yesu kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mi a kĩ minyina Mose mbla a, míanu lɛtsa a kĩ mawɛ kĩ malɛ nɛ awĩ a kayɔ. Mbla anya oti akũ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ edzi nkpã a odi.

english_verse: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
translation_verse: Foe nfantuhũ nyɛ kĩ mbla sui otsole kĩ edzi kɔnyɔ edo nɛ ɔnyɔ kayɔ nɛ onyole a nkpã kamɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ onyole a ku ni, otsole a nɛ́ nɛ mbla a kĩ esui ye ɛmɛna mɔ onyole a kamɛ.

english_verse: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ otsole a naa onyole bɛbã sɛkɛ̃ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ ɔnyɔ a buki edzi nkpã ni, baavɛ ye ɔdɔpaani. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔnyɔ a ku kɔ̃ ni, mbla kĩĩ tásui ye, ade ntɛ ɛnadzi mɔ onyole bɛbã ni, ɛtábɔa adɔpaa.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ade aye fɔla fɔkpa mi malo a. Nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, mi malo miku, kitonɛkĩ minyɛ Kristo sukɛna a sɛkɛ sidi. Ade finyaa minyɛ ye a kĩ Baguma dzɛla etomɛna kanɔkpa a bade, ayekĩ bɔ́anɛ libi bɔkpa Baguma.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ budzi nkpã nɛ sukɛna kamɛ a ni, sukɛna fɔwɛtsa kpa a kĩ mbla a dzɛla nɛ boe kamɛ a, kɛna adzuma nɛ boe kamɛ, ɛmanɛ boe ɛkpa wuda.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa bɔnɛ́ nɛ mbla a kayɔ, kitonɛkĩ buku bɔkpa fɔtsa a kĩ fusuli boe ndɛ kɔla bawanɛ a. Bɔláabuki busum nɛ mbla kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ osi kɔɛ a akũ, mbom bɔ́asum nɛ Hũhũ kamɛ osi vɔɛ̃ a akũ. Mbla Afɔ̃ Oti Akɛna Tɔkpa.

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Finyaa litsa bɔ́alɛ? Kalɛ bɔ́alɛ kĩ mbla nyɛ tɔkpa? Oowo! Fɔlá lɔmɔ. Lɛmɛ mbla a lɛfɔ̃ ĩnyi lɛtsa a kĩ tɔkpa anɛ. Ntɛ mbla a ɛtalɛ kĩ, “Tayɔɔla anɛ nɛ oti bɛbã fɔtsa akũ” ni, kufɛ ĩnyína anɛngbã kawɛ a na.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔtsã mbla a akũ, tɔkpa na osi tinyina odo kakɔ fɔtsa kpa flee nɛ yĩ kamɛ. Ntɛ mbla kalaa ɛláa ni, kufɛ tɔkpa lɛmɛ ku.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Yĩ mɔawɛ a ndzi tɔ̃ nkpã, kĩ mbla a tána, lɛmɛ kĩ mbla a ya a, tɔkpa yidza tidzi nkpã,

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: ade nku a. Ade mbla a kĩ kufɛ ɛma yĩ nkpã a, yĩ kɔ̃ wuda ɛkɔa ɛma yĩ.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Tɔkpa tsã mbla akũ tɛna osi tɛkɛ yĩ, ade tɛkɔ yĩ a.

english_verse: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
translation_verse: Foesũ mbla mɔawɛ a akũ kpalɛ, ade ayea ye nhihiɛ a adã kpĩĩ, ɛnɛnɛ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa lɛtsa wĩ a kĩ lɛnɛnɛ a dani wuda lɛkpa yĩ? Oowo! Fɔlá lɔmɔ. Tɔkpa tsã tuwĩ akũ tɛkɔa wuda tɛma yĩ, ayekĩ taanyɛ te kutitse paa a tɛtsa. Foesũ fɔtsã mbla akũ bɛnyɛ tɔkpa bɛtsa ndɛ lɛtsa kpa kĩ liti lɛnɛ́nɛ koũ. Kawũkanɔ a Kĩ Kideĩ Nɛ Oti Kamɛ.

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ mbla a nyɛ hũhũ kamɛ fɔtsa, lɛmɛ yĩ kɔ̃ ĩnyɛ otidziwa, kĩ bɛnɔ ɔsate bɛkpa tɔkpa.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Maánu yĩ fɔtsa kɛnawa a kayɔ, kitonɛkĩ maákɛna lɛtsa a kĩ mawɛ kĩ makɛna a, mbom makɛna lɛtsa a kĩ makesĩ a.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Ntɛ lɛtsa a kĩ maáwɛ a makɛna kɔ̃ ni, kekɔ̃ ntunɔ kĩ mbla a nɛnɛ.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Foesũ nnɛ́ yĩ mɔawɛ akɛna fɔtsa a, mbom tɔkpa a kĩ tideĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ a akɛna foe.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina kĩ tuwĩ tidima lidzí nɛ yĩ kamɛ ayekĩ ĩnyɛ otidziwa sũ. Kitonɛkĩ titɔ kĩ kawɛ a kĩ makɛna tuwĩ deĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ malo ni, maápuli nkɛna te.

english_verse: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
translation_verse: Tuwĩ a kĩ mawɛ kĩ makɛna a, maákɛna te, mbom makɛna tɔkpa a kĩ maáwɛ kĩ makɛna a.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Ntɛ lɛtsa a kĩ maáwɛ a makɛna ni, kekɔ̃ nnɛ́ yĩ mɔawɛ a akɛna foe, mbom tɔkpa a kĩ tideĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ a akɛna foe.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Foesũ mmɔ́ mbla kĩĩ fɔlɛtsa ĩyi ɔsũ, kĩ ntɛ mawɛ kĩ makɛna tuwĩ ni, tɔkpa mbom makɛna.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Nɛ yĩ kamɛkamɛ ni, mamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ Baguma mbla a akũ.

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mamɔ mbla bɛbã nɛ yĩ sukɛna kamɛ kĩ yanɔ lɛma mɔ yĩ lɔlɔ kamɛ tuwĩ kĩ mawɛ a. Ɛkɛna yĩ kɔla ɔwanɛ ɛkpa tɔkpa mbla a kĩ edeĩ nɛ yĩ sukɛna kamɛ.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Nkɛna oti kĩ ye akũ akɔ kɔnya. Owei aatɛ yĩ etomɛna sukɛna kĩ sɛkpã yĩ sanamɛna wuda kamɛ a awɛ?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Kɔlɛ bɔanyɛ Baguma bɔtsãmɛna boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo akũ. Finyaa, ayekĩ nla nyɛ kĩ nɛ yĩ mɔa akũ akũ ni, nnɛɛ yĩ adzuma makɛna nɛ Baguma mbla a akũ, lɛmɛ sukɛna a kɔ̃ ni, nnɛ masum tɔkpa mbla a.

english_verse: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
translation_verse: Foesũ finyaa kɔtɔkakɔ lɛláa kɛkpa bikĩ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Kristo Yesu a.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ mbla a kĩ yakpa oti nkpã nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna kamɛ mɔ Kristo Yesu a, nyɛ yĩ etomɛna tɔkpa mɔ wuda mbla a kamɛ.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ mbla a tápuli ɛkɛna a, ayekĩ sukɛna a tɛ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ ye awɛ sũ ni, Baguma kɔ̃ kɛna foe. Edo ye mɔawɛ obi ɛya nɛ otidziwa sukɛna kĩ sakɛna tɔkpa kamɛ, ade ɛyakɔ tɔkpa kɔtɔ, ɛnyɛ te a.

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: Baguma kɛna fukĩĩ ayekĩ boe a kĩ budzi nɛ Hũhũ kamɛ, kĩ nnɛ́ nɛ sukɛna kamɛ a, bɔ́apuli budzi nɛ tuwĩ kakɛna a kĩ ye mbla awɛ a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
translation_verse: Bati kĩ bidzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ be sukɛna awɛ a onukpɛ̃ ni, fɔtsa a kĩ be sukɛna awɛ a akũ bakɔna. Lɛmɛ bikĩ bidzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ Hũhũ a awɛ a onukpɛ̃ ni, fɔtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a awɛ a akũ be adzuni ana.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Oti kadzi nɛ sukɛna fɔwɛtsa tsɛ̃mɔ wuda, lɛmɛ oti kĩ Hũhũ a akpã ye a, ana nkpã mɔ lukudɔ.

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bidzi nkpã nɛ be sukɛna kawɛ onukpɛ̃ a fɔkɛlatsa lɛláapuli fuwo Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ midzí nɛ sukɛna kawɛ onukpɛ̃, mbom midzi ndɛ ayekĩ Hũhũ a awɛ kĩ midzi a. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ Kristo Hũhũ lɛláa ye kamɛ lɛnyɛ́ Kristo ode.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Kristo deĩ nɛ nɔ kamɛ a, Hũhũ a nyɛ nkpã ɛkpa nɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɛkɛna nɔ oti kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, titɔ kĩ nɔ sukɛna ayaku malo nɛ tɔkpa sũ.

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ midzi nkpã nɛ sukɛna kawɛ onukpɛ̃ ni, míaku. Lɛmɛ ntɛ mitsã Hũhũ kamɛ mikɔ mi fɔkɛlatsa kpa a, míana nkpã.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ Baguma Hũhũ akpã a flee nyɛ Baguma babi.

english_verse: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ a kĩ Baguma kpa mi a tákɛna mi basate mikavila, mbom Hũhũ a akɛna mi Baguma babi. Ade Hũhũ a kɔbɛ̃ afɔ̃ bɔbɔa fɔwɔla kavɛ Baguma kĩ, “Abba!” kĩ foe kayɔ nyɛ kĩ “Ao Nda!”

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Baguma Hũhũ a mɔawɛ lɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ boe hũhũ yadi adansiɛ kĩ Baguma babi bɔanɛ.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɔnyɛ ye babi ni, bɔnyɛ ye adzapadiɛ lɛmɛ banyanɛ, ade bɔmɔ Kristo bɔ́anya adzapadiɛ a kĩ Baguma kɔa eyi ye a. Foesũ ntɛ bɔna tsɛfã nɛ Kristo lubo kamɔ a kamɛ ni, bɔ́ana tsɛfã lɛmɛ nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ a kamɛ a. Kukũkpalɛ a Kĩ Kɔaya a.

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Makɔna kĩ bɔláapuli bɔkɔa lubo a kĩ bɔamɔ finyaa a bɔkɔnamɔ Baguma kukũkpalɛ a kĩ baanyɛ bɛtsa boe a kɔlaa.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa a flee dzi fɔadzɔlɛ mɔ akũ kasĩ kĩ Baguma ɛnyɛ ye babi ɛtsa.

english_verse: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɛkɔ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa a flee kɔtɔ kĩ fɔnyɛ fɔtsa pupulidza, nnɛ́ nɛ foe kawɛ akũ, mbom Baguma lɛwɛ kĩ fɔna lɔmɔ. Lɛmɛ kɔnɛte a deĩ kĩ,

english_verse: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
translation_verse: luwi lidi ni, fɔtsa a kĩ bɛbɔa flee aanyɛ foe akũ nɛ tɛsate ɔsum kĩ yabu kade a kamɛ, fɔyaawo nɛ akũ akũ kadzi mɔ kukũkpalɛ a kĩ Baguma aakpa ye babi a kamɛ.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bunyina kĩ kɛyawo nviã lɔkɔ kĩĩ a, fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa a flee abe ayɔlɔhɔ ndɛ ɔmatsɔ̃ kĩ sɛmɛ pi ye aye.

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ fɔtsa kĩ bɛbɔa a odi abe ayɔlɔhɔ, mbom boe a kĩ Baguma kpa ye Hũhũ ndɛ ye futetsa fɔtɔnyade a lɛmɛ bɔabe ayɔlɔhɔ nɛ boe kamɛ, kadzɔlɛ Baguma kĩ ɛkɛna boe ye babi a, ɛnyɛ boe kutitse a flee.

english_verse: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ade kɔnɛte kĩĩ sũ bɔna katɛ a, lɛmɛ ntɛ bɔamɔ kɔnɛte fɔtsa kɔkɔɛ ni, fubuki fɔnyɛ́ kɔnɛte. Kitonɛkĩ owei akɔa anɛ kate nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ yamɔ a akũ?

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ boe anɛ teĩ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɔámɔ akũ ni, bɔana ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kadzɔlɛ foe.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Hũhũ a lɛmɛ aayabumɔ boe a kĩ bɔdɔ a, kitonɛkĩ bunyína ayekĩ kɛfɛta kĩ bɔabɔa mpaɛ bɔkpa Baguma. Hũhũ mɔawɛ a abe ayɔlɔhɔ kapili boe lɔlɔ nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ mɔ fɔlɛtsa kĩ onukpɛ̃ lɛláapuli ɛlɛ.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Ade Baguma a kĩ yamɔ bati tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a nyina lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ Hũhũ a adzuni, kitonɛkĩ Hũhũ a ake Baguma aduli nɛ ye bade lɔlɔ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma awɛ a.

english_verse: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ Baguma akɛna fɔtsa flee fɔaboomu kakɛna fuwĩ kakpa bati a kĩ bawɛ ye kalɛ a, kĩ ɛvɛ nɛ ye nhihiɛ onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ Baguma nyi ɛnyamɛna lɔtɔ a, ɛpau be eyi kĩ bɛyaakɛna ndɛ ye Obi aye, ayekĩ ye Obi a aanya be ɔtɔnyade nɛ bawaɛ̃ pii kamɛ.

english_verse: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
translation_verse: Foesũ bati a kĩ Baguma nyɛ eyi a, ade be ɛvɛ a, ade bikĩ ɛvɛ a, ɛkɛna be bɛkpɔla ade ɛkpa be tsɛfã nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ a kamɛ a. Yesu Kristo Nyɛ Baguma Ɔdɔ a Ɛtsa Boe.

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɔkɔna fukĩĩ flee akũ a, litsa bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ? Ntɛ Baguma dzɛ̃ nɛ boe liti a ni, owei aapuli eyidza ɛdza nɛ boe akũ?

english_verse: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
translation_verse: Ntɛ Baguma a kĩ ɛtásĩ ye mɔawɛ Obi kakɔa kakpa, mbom ɛkɔa ye ɛkpa faa nɛ boe flee lɔlɔ a ni, nde fɔ́anya kĩ ɛláakɔa fɔtsa bɛbã flee faa ebu Obi ɛkpa boe?

english_verse: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
translation_verse: Owei aapuli ɛbɔɛ fɔlɛtsa nɛ bati a kĩ Baguma pau a akũ? Baguma mɔawɛ di adansiɛ kĩ bɛtáku kɔtɔ.

english_verse: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
translation_verse: Foesũ owei aakɔ be kɔtɔ? Nnɛ́ Kristo Yesu a kĩ eku ĩye kĩ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ, edzi nkpã nɛ Baguma sɔmɔna, yake ye aduli nɛ boe lɔlɔ a!

english_verse: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
translation_verse: Kekɔ̃ owei ĩye litsa aapuli etsua bɔmɔ Kristo ɔdɔ a kayite? Lubokamɔ aapuli, mbɔɛɛ ɔhau, mbɔɛɛ bati liti katomɛna, mbɔɛɛ kɔlɛkakɔ ĩye ohiã, mbɔɛɛ ɔdzɔ kadzi, mbɔɛɛ wuda libe, mbɔɛɛ kĩ wuda?

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Nɔ sũ, bakɔ boe lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ. Ade babu boe ndɛ baveli kĩ bɛkpã banaakɔ aye.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ flee kamɛ a, bɔtsã Kristo kĩ yawɛ boe kalɛ a akũ bɔba ayekĩ bɔkɔna malo.

english_verse: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ ndo kĩ lɛtsama lɛláapuli lɛnyɛ boe nɛ boe Ɔlate Kristo ɔdɔ a kamɛ. Fɔláanya wuda ĩye nkpã, ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ ĩye hũhũ kpa kɔbɛ̃ kudi, lɔkɔ kĩĩ ĩye lɔkɔ kĩ laaya,

english_verse: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
translation_verse: katinya kĩ kideĩ nɛ ode ĩye katinya kĩ kideĩ nɛ tite kayɔ ĩye fɔtsa a kĩ bɛbɔa a kamɛ fudima. Lɛtsama koũ lɛláa katinya kamɛ flee kĩ láapuli lɛnyɛ boe nɛ Baguma ɔdɔ a kĩ bɔna bɔtsãmɛna boe Ɔlate Kristo Yesu akũ a kamɛ!

english_verse: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ malɛ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, Kristo ode manɛ ade maáma fɔvã a. Yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa anya ye akũ a akpa yĩ katɛkado kĩ nnɛ́ fɔvã mama.

english_verse: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
translation_verse: Mabe ayɔlɔhɔ, ade ntɛ kalɔɛ kĩ kaádɔ nɛ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ,

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: kakpa yĩ bati a kĩ bɔmɔ be bɔnyɛ sukɛna mɔ babɔanɛ mɔa. Nɛ be sũ maawɛ kĩ Baguma edumu yĩ faa, ɛsɛ̃ bɔmɔ Kristo kayite malo kufɛ.

english_verse: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ade be nyɛ Israel ɔmaĩ a kĩ Baguma pau, ɛkɔa ɛkɛna ye bade, ɛnyɛ ye kukũkpalɛ a ɛtsa be a. Esui onukpɛ̃ mɔ be, ɛkpa be ye mbla a lɛmɛ. Be nɛɛ Baguma ɔsum osi wĩ a, ade be lɛmɛ ɛkɛna bɔhɛ ɛkpa a.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Be kasinu to Hebrifɔ bawanyɔ a kĩ bɛtɛ buĩ a kamɛ, ade be kade kamɛ Kristo lɛmɛ nɛ eto ndɛ otidziwa aye ɛya a. Mifɔ̃ bumu Baguma a kĩ yanya fɔtsa flee akũ daa daa a! Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Nlámɔ kalɛ kĩ Baguma kalɛkayi a táya nɛ foe kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ bati a kĩ bito Israel a flee nyɛ Israelfɔ kpanunu.

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Foe kayɔ nyɛ kĩ nnɛ́ babi a kĩ Abraham ma ndɛ bati ayea nyɛ Baguma babi, mbom bikĩ ɛma nɛ ye bɔhɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma bɔhɛ a nyɛ, “Kateliva, lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ a, maamuniki ĩya ade Sara aama obinyole a.”

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ fukĩĩ odi a. Babinyole nviã a kĩ Rebeka ma a ɔlɛga nyɛ boe ɔwanyɔ Isak.

english_verse: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ayekĩ babinyole nviã kamɛ ɔmɔa kapau fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ Baguma nhihiɛ onukpɛ̃ sũ ni, Baguma bi Rebeka kĩ, “Obinɔkɔɛ̃ a aasum obisɛ̃de a.” Ebi ye eyi kɔkɔɛ fɔmɔ ɛyama babi a. Foesũ Baguma kapau a kĩ ɛkɛna a lɛdzɛ́ nɛ tuwĩ ĩye tɔkpa a kĩ nta babi a kamɛ odi kɛna a akũ, mbom Baguma kawɛ fɔanɛ.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Nwɛ Yakob kalɛ, lɛmɛ nkesĩ Esau.”

english_verse: Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa bɔ́alɛ kĩ Baguma lɛmɛ ládã kpĩĩ? Oowo! Fɔlá lɔmɔ kɔlaa.

english_verse: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
translation_verse: Foesũ Baguma oti kapau lɛdzɛ́ nɛ ye mbɔdibɔ ĩye lɛtsa a kĩ yawɛ a akũ, mbom lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa dzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ odi akũ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa kamɛ a, Baguma bi Egipte lɛgã kĩ, “Ndzina nɔ lɛgã ayekĩ maatsã nɔ akũ ĩnyɛ yĩ kɔbɛ̃ ntsa, lɔkɔa yĩ dĩ lɛgba nɛ katinya kamɛ flee.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Foesũ odi nyɛ odi kĩ Baguma awɛ kĩ yamɔ kɔnya a, ade ye yamɔ kɔnya a, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ yawɛ kĩ ɛna atɔ ɔsĩ a, yafɔ̃ yana atɔ ɔsĩ. Baguma Kalɔkpa Mɔ Kɔnyakamɔ a.

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Mi kamɛ odi aavia yĩ kĩ, “Ntɛ fɔla lɔmɔ a, nde sũ Baguma buki yanyɛ nfumusu kakpa odi? Owei aapuli eyidza ɛdza nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ?”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Nɔ otidziwa, owei anɛ kĩ akpa Baguma mbuayɛ lɔmɔ? Lubui lávia de ɔmɛnɛ kĩ, “Nde sũ ɔmɛ yĩ lɔmɔ?”
