english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Ade lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma kɛna a kayɔ nɛ awĩ a. Ntɛ Baguma wɛ kĩ yanyɛ ye kalɔkpa kanya, ɛfɔ̃ bɛmɔ ye otumi, lɛmɛ ebuki ɛna ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ epini bikĩ be akũ ɛna kalɔkpa, kĩ bidum be bɛkpa kawɔla a ni, litsa odeĩ kĩ aalɛ?

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ Baguma wɛ lɛmɛ kĩ yanyɛ ye kukũkpalɛ baũ a kĩ biko nɛ boe a kĩ ɛmɔ kɔnya akũ a, boe a kĩ ɛmanɛ eyi ɛkpa ye kukũkpalɛ a,

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ Baguma lɛ nɛ Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Hosea ovoli kamɛ kĩ, “Bati kĩ bɛnyɛ́ yĩ ɔmaĩ bati a, maavɛ be kĩ, ‘Yĩ bati.’ Kade a kĩ ntáwɛ ke kalɛ a, maavɛ ke ‘Yĩ ɔdɔ bati.’

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kakɔ mɔapɛ a kĩ bibi be kĩ, ‘Minyɛ́ yĩ bati’ a Ade awã baavɛ be kĩ ‘Baguma Kĩ Edzi Nkpã Daa a babi.’ ”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Baguma Onukpɛ̃ Ɔnyɛnɛ Yesaya bɔa fɔwɔla ɛlɛ nɛ Israelfɔ akũ kĩ, “Ntɛ Israelfɔ nɛ lidede ndɛ mpo abuĩ dã malo ni, be kamɛ bati sukuloo badi aana katɛ,

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ boe Ɔlate aadzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ bati pɛpɛɛpɛ mɔatsɛ, etsua lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa lukũ.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Yesaya buki ɛlɛ eyi kĩ, “Ntɛ boe Ɔlate Otumifɔ Flee a ɛtatina boe babi mɔ bawa a badi ni, kufɛ bɔdani bɔkɛna ndɛ Sodom, buketimɔ Gomora!” Kalɛ Wĩ a Nyɛ Israelfɔ Lɛmɛ Kade.

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Foesũ litsa koũ bɔ́alɛ? Matsa kĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Israelfɔ a kĩ bɛtápili kĩ badzi kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ a, bɛkɛna be bati kĩ bɛkpɔla bɛtsãmɛna be katɛkado akũ.

english_verse: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
translation_verse: Litsa sũ bɛtápuli? Kitonɛkĩ bɛtákɔa katɛkado bɛwɛ foe, mbom bɛkɔa anɛ bite nɛ be mɔawɛ mbɔdibɔ akũ. Nɛ foesũ bitula nɛ Kristo kĩ ɛla ndɛ de a kĩ bati atula nɛ de akũ a akũ,

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Midã! Nkɔa de lidi mayi nɛ Zion, de kĩ bati aatula nɛ de akũ bɛnɛ bɛtɔ. Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ ye edo ni, kɔnyɔã lɛláawo ye.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, lɛtsa a kĩ mawɛ nɛ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ flee, mɔ mpaɛ kĩ mabɔa nɛ de akũ kakpa Baguma nyɛ kĩ, ɛfɔ̃ yĩ awɛ kade bati bɛna nkpã katɛ!

english_verse: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
translation_verse: Bɛtányi osi a kĩ Baguma atsã kabu bati kĩ bidzi kpĩĩ nɛ ye anɛ, foesũ bɛwɛ be mɔawɛ tisi kĩ batsã bɛkɛna fɔtsa biwo Baguma anɛ, ade foesũ bɛtákɔa be akũ bɛkpa Baguma osi a kĩ yatsã kakɛna bati kpĩĩ a.

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Kristo nyɛ Mose mbla a kawũna, ayekĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ ye edo ni, baabu ye oti kpĩĩ. Baguma Aakpa Odi Nyɛ Odi Nkpã.

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: Mose tsɛlɛ eyi ɛtsa ayekĩ oti adzi kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ ntɛ edi mbla a akũ. Ɛlɛ kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ mbla a akũ pɛpɛɛpɛ ni, yaana nkpã.”

english_verse: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ nɛ ayekĩ oti aadã kpĩĩ nɛ katɛkado kamɛ nyɛ kĩ, “Tavia nɔ akũ kĩ, ‘Owei aade ɛnaa ode?’ kĩ fɔatsa kĩ ɛnaakpã Kristo eto kanɔkpade ede ɛya.

english_verse: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
translation_verse: Ĩye tavia kĩ, ‘Owei aaka ɛnaa kayɔ?’ Foe nyɛ kĩ owei aanaa kanɔkpade ɛnaakpã Kristo ɛyamɛna.”

english_verse: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ yatsa a nyɛ kĩ, “Baguma kalɛ a fuã nɔ plɛplɛplɛ, kideĩ nɛ nɔ onukpɛ̃ mɔ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.” Ade katɛkado ɔlɛdo a kĩ bɔade lɛtsã nɛ ke akũ a nɛ awĩ a.

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɔkɔa onukpɛ̃ ɔlɛ kĩ Yesu nyɛ boe Ɔlate, mɔ ɔtɛ odo nɛ nɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kĩ Baguma dzɛla ye etomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ ni, Baguma aakpa nɔ nkpã.

english_verse: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ɔwɔlɛ bakɔa katɛ kado kana kadzi kpĩĩ, ade onukpɛ̃ bakɔa kanyɛ foe kanya mɔ Baguma akpa boe nkpã.

english_verse: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtɛ ye edo lɛláatɛ kɔnyɔã.”

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi deĩ nɛ foe kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ ntsitsemu adima lɛláa Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kayite. Baguma mɔapɛ a nyɛ bati flee Ɔlate, ade yakpa akũ kana fɔtsa nhila pii bikĩ babɔa mpaɛ kavɛ ye a.

english_verse: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ a, “Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaavɛ boe Ɔlate a dĩ ni, yaana nkpã.”

english_verse: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nde bati aakɛna bɛvɛ oti a kĩ bɛtátɛ ye bido a? Nde baakɛna bɛtɛ oti a kĩ bɛtánu ye fɔlɛtsa a bido? Ade ntɛ bɛtáde lɛtsã nɛ fɔlɛtsa a akũ a, nde baakɛna binu ye dĩ?

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Ade nde baakɛna bide lɛtsã Baguma fɔlɛtsa ntɛ bɛtádo fɔtɔ banyanɛ? Ade foe akũ mɔ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Batɔ a kĩ bɛyaade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ a kaya alɛ kadã paa!”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ bati flee lɛtɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a, kitonɛkĩ Yesaya lɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, owei lɛtɛ boe kalɛ a edo?”

english_verse: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Foesũ katɛkado ato kalɛ a kanu kamɛ, ade fɔlɛtsa a kanu ato lɛtsã kade nɛ Kristo akũ a.

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mavia kĩ ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ bɛtánu Baguma fɔlɛtsa a kɔlaa? Ɔnɔkɔali binu foe, kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsã badenɛ a odo tsɛna katinya kamɛ flee, ade be fɔlɛtsa a gba fɔnawo katinya kawũnakɔ̃ a flee.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Mavia bio kĩ lɔkɔa kanu Israelfɔ a tánu fɔlɛtsa a kayɔ? Mose mɔawɛ lɛnya lɔtɔ ɛlɛ kĩ, Baguma lɛ kĩ, “Maafɔ̃ minɔ lineke mɔ bati kĩ bɛnyɛ́ ɔmaĩ a. Maakɔa ɔmaĩ a kĩ ɛtábinya anɛ a ndo mi kalɔkpa.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesaya do ɔwɔlɛ ɛba ebi be kĩ, Baguma lɛ kĩ, “Bati a kĩ bɛtáwɛ yĩ liti osi a, ade be lɛmɔ́ yĩ a bati a kĩ bɛtávia yĩ akũ a, ade be ĩnyɛ yĩ akũ ntsa a.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Baguma buki ɛlɛ nɛ Israelfɔ akũ kĩ, “Luwi mɔa flee ndzɛlɛ yĩ akpɔ kamɛ nkpa bati a kĩ bideĩ atɔ ɔsĩ, kĩ biyidza bɛdza nɛ yĩ akũ lɛmɛ a.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Foesũ mavia kĩ Baguma pu ye mɔawɛ bati a flefle? Oowo! Yĩ mɔawɛ Israelni manɛ, ĩnyɛ Abraham kasinu ɔwa, kĩ nto Benyamin katete a kamɛ.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Baguma tápu ye ɔmaĩ a kĩ ɛpau eyi kito kayɔkayi a. Mbɔɛɛ minyína lɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ, awã a kĩ Eliya ke Baguma aduli nɛ Israelfɔ lɔlɔ a? Ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate, bɛkɔ nɔ onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ, ade bidzuli nɔ afɔlibɔ tɛtɛnyɛ a. Yĩ odi keĩ lite ade bawɛ kĩ bakɔ yĩ malo a!”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Foesũ nde? Israelfɔ a tána lɛtsa a kĩ bɛkawɛ a. Lɛmɛ be kamɛ badi sukuloo a kĩ Baguma pau a lɛna foe, ade be nkaɛ kɛna atɔ ɔsĩ nɛ Baguma kavɛ akũ a.

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Ade aye fɔla nɛ finyaa lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ malo a. Israelfɔ sukuloo badi te kĩ Baguma mɔ be kɔnya ɛpau eyi a.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Baguma mɔ be kɔnya, ɛpau be, nnɛ́ nɛ be adzuma a kĩ bɛkɛna a sũ. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bati kapau dzɛ̃ nɛ be lɛkɛnana akũ ni, kekɔ̃ ye kɔnyakamɔ a buki kɔnyɛ́ kɔnyakamɔ.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Baguma fɔ̃ be adzuni mɔ be tɔwɔlɛ dɔ kĩ baánu fɔlɛtsa kayɔ, Ade kito nɛ ke kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ a, baápuli bɛmɔ́ biyi ɔsũ ĩye binu foe kayɔ.”

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Ade Dawid lɛ kĩ, “Baguma ɛfɔ̃ be kanyakɔ̃ opunu ɛdani kpɛĩ epi be, mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ baatula bɛtɔ fɔnya be sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ.

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: Ɛfɔ̃ be anɛ eke ɔtũ ayekĩ bɛláamɔ fɔkɔ, ɛfɔ̃ bikũũkũ nɛ be lubokamɔ kayɔ daa daa.”

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Finyaa mavia kĩ, kĩ Yudafɔ tula bɛnɛ bɛtɔ a, bɛkahɔ kɔlaa nɛ awã? Oowo! Ayekĩ bɛkɛna tɔkpa sũ ni, nkpã katɛ a ya nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ Yudafɔ a aanɔ be lineke.

english_verse: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
translation_verse: Ntɛ Yudafɔ tɔkpa yamɛna akũ kana nhila baũ nɛ katinya kamɛ, mɔ ntɛ be hũhũ kamɛ katɔ a ma bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a nhila pii ni, kekɔ̃ nhila a aablusu paa ntɛ Yudafɔ a flee dani bɛkɛna Baguma bade. Bati Kĩ Bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a Malo Na Nkpã Katɛ.

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Ade mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a mabi fɔlɛtsa finyaa a. Ayekĩ ĩnyɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ sũ ni, maapia nɛ adzuma a akũ.

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: Ɔsa fɔ́atsã ye kakɛna kamɛ ni, maapuli nfɔ̃ yĩ kade bati lɛnɔ lineke ayekĩ maapuli ntɛ badi nkpã.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ Baguma fɔnɛ be awɛ a, ɛkpɔla batidziwa kĩ bɛnyɛ ye bakesĩnɛ a ɛkɛna be ye bakɔba. Foesũ nde fɔ́ana ntɛ Baguma buki ɛtɛ be? Fɔ́ana ndɛ nkpã ɛkpa banɔkpa!

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Ntɛ abodoo sɛkɛ sɛtɔnyade a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkpa Baguma akũ kpalɛ ni, kekɔ̃ ye nkaɛ a lɛmɛ akũ kpalɛ. Ade ayea ntɛ bɛkɔa kutse tunu bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa Baguma ni, koe tɛnanɛ a lɛmɛ nyɛ ye tide.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Bitsua kugua kutse kĩ bidu a tɛnanɛ tidi bɛnyɛ, ade bɛkɔa lupo kamɛ kugua kutsede tɛnanɛ bɛyatalɛ nɛ koe akũ a. Mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a la ndɛ lupo kamɛ kutse tɛnanɛ a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛyatalɛ nɛ kukĩ bidu akũ a, ade finyaa míana kutse a tunu kamɛ funitsã a fudi.

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: Foesũ mitasupa bikĩ bitsua bɛnyɛ ndɛ tɛnanɛ aye a. Nde sũ miapia akũ mitsa be? Mite anɛ kĩ tɛnanɛ kɛkɛ mianɛ, nnɛ́ mi nyɛ kutse tunu a, mbom tunu a pini mi.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ɔsa nɔ aalɛ kĩ, “Bitsua tɛnanɛ a bɛnyɛ kĩ yĩ nna nɛ te kadokɔ̃.”

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali. Bitsua te kitonɛkĩ bɛtátɛ bido, ade mina kadzakɔ̃ nɛ mi katɛkado sũ a, foesũ mitapia nɛ foe akũ, mbom mikavila.

english_verse: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ Baguma támɔ Yudafɔ a kĩ bɛla ndɛ kutse kpĩĩ a tɛnanɛ a kɔnya ni, ɔkɔna kĩ yaamɔ nɔ kɔnya?

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: Dã ayekĩ Baguma deĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ, mɔ ayekĩ edeĩ ɔsĩ. Baguma deĩ ɔsĩ nɛ bikĩ bɛnɛ bɛtɔ akũ, lɛmɛ edeĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ ɛkpa nɔ ntɛ osila odzi nɛ ye sɛmɛ kalɛ a kamɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔtádzi lɔmɔ ni, Baguma aatsua nɔ malo ɛnyɛ nɛ kutse a akũ.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ Yudafɔ a muniki bɛyatɛ Baguma bidoi ni, Baguma aakɔa be ɛdzɛla nɛ be kadzakɔ̃ kɔɛ a, kitonɛkĩ Baguma aapuli ɛkɛna foe.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: Mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, mila ndɛ lupo kamɛ kugua kutse tɛnanɛ a kĩ bitsua bɛyatalɛ nɛ kugua kutse wĩ a akũ a aye. Yudafɔ a la ndɛ kugua kutse a kĩ bidu a, foesũ fɔlá ɔsĩ fɔkpa Baguma kĩ yaabuki ɛkɔa kugua kutse mɔawɛ tɛnanɛ a kĩ bitsua bɛnyɛ a ɛtalɛ nɛ kutse mɔawɛ a akũ. Baguma Amɔ Bati Flee Kɔnya.

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa bɛlawa fudi deĩ kĩ mawɛ kĩ minyi, ayekĩ miláabu mi akũ anyansãfɔ. Ade foe nyɛ kĩ Israelfɔ badi atɔ ɔsĩ a aana kɛnaawo kĩ, bati a kĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, kakã flee ya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ fuyua a lɔkɔa Yudafɔ a flee aana katɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Ɔtɛnɛ a aato Zion ɛya. Yaanyɛ ablɔ fɔtsa flee ɛnɛmɛna nɛ Yakob katete a kamɛ a.

english_verse: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
translation_verse: Maakɛna bɔhɛ kĩĩ mɔ be, ntɛ ĩnyɛ be tɔkpa nnamɛna.”

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ kɔ̃ ni, Yudafɔ a dani Baguma bakesĩnɛ nɛ mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a sũ. Lɛmɛ nɛ Baguma be kapau sũ ni, bɛnyɛ Baguma bakɔba nɛ be bawanyɔ sũ.

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma ládani ye adzuni nɛ oti a kĩ ɛpau ĩye ehila a akũ.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Kɔkɔɛ a, mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, mitákɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ, lɛmɛ finyaa Baguma mɔ mi kɔnya kitonɛkĩ Yudafɔ a na atɔ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
translation_verse: Ade ayea kɔnya a kĩ Baguma mɔ mi a sũ ni, finyaa Yudafɔ lɛmɛ lákɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ ayekĩ Baguma aamɔ be malo kɔnya.

english_verse: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ batidziwa flee sĩ kĩ baátse Baguma sũ ni, ɛfɔ̃ bati flee kɛna fɔkɔ katabu kɔla bawanɛ, ayekĩ yaamɔ be flee kɔnya. Fɔfɛta Kĩ Bumu Baguma.

english_verse: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
translation_verse: Baguma akũ kana fɔtsa nɛ lidede paa! Ye nyansã mɔ kadikanyi kamɛ dzũ paa! Owei aapuli ɛnyɛ ye kɔtɔkakɔ kamɛ? Owei aapuli enu ye tisi kayɔ?

english_verse: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Owei nyina boe Ɔlate a adzuni? Owei aapuli ɛkpa ye kɔdɛ?

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: Owei lɛkpa Baguma litsedi tɔ̃, ayekĩ yaakɛna ese ye kɔtɔ?”

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛbɔa fɔtsa flee, ye kɔbɛ̃ akũ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa dzi nkpã, ade ye kamɛ lɛmɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa aawũna a. Obu mɔ kamu nyɛ Baguma fude daa. Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
translation_verse: Foesũ nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ayekĩ Baguma amɔ boe kɔnya sũ ni, make mi aduli paa kĩ mikɔa mi akũ mikpa Baguma ndɛ afɔliɛ fɔtsa kĩ fudeĩ nkpã, kĩ foe akũ kpalɛ, fɔawo ye anɛ. Ade fukĩĩ nyɛ ayekĩ miasum Baguma nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ mɔ mi tɔwɔlɛ a.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Mitadzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ bati adzi nɛ tɔkpa kakɛna kamɛ a, mbom mifɔ̃ Baguma ɛdani mi adzuni ɛkɛna avɔɛ̃ lɔkɔa midani ɔbla. Fɔ́afɔ̃ minyi Baguma kawɛ, fɔtsa a kĩ fɔnɛnɛ, kĩ fɔawo ye anɛ, fɔkpɔla lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
translation_verse: Nɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ futetsa baũ a kĩ ɛkpa yĩ sũ ni, mabi mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛtabu ye tɔnɔkɔɛ̃ ɛba ayekĩ kɛfɛta. Nɛ foe kadokɔ̃ a, ɛkɔna fɔtsa a kĩ fɔatsa akũ kadɔla, lɔkɔa ɛdzɛmɔ ye akũ ɛdã nɛ katɛkado a kĩ Baguma kpa ye a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ budeĩ sukɛna fɔkɔ kumɛkumɛ pii nɛ oti mɔawɛ a kamɛ, kĩ fɔakɛna tudzuma futefute a,

english_verse: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
translation_verse: ade ayea titɔ kĩ bati pii bɔanɛ malo ni, oti mɔa bɔanɛ nɛ Kristo kamɛ, kĩ bɔtsamɔ akũ ndɛ sukɛna fɔkɔ futefute nɛ oti mɔa kamɛ.

english_verse: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
translation_verse: Baguma mɔ boe kɔnya, ɛkpa boe futetsa futefute, foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔkɛna foe akũ adzuma nɛ ye kɔnyakamɔ a onukpɛ̃. Ntɛ boe futetsa nyɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bɔakɛna nɛ katɛkado a kĩ budeĩ a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
translation_verse: Ntɛ boe futetsa nyɛ kĩ busum bati ni, kɛnɛ kĩ busum be ayekĩ kɛfɛta, ntɛ futetsa nyɛ fɔtsa katsa ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bɔtsa fɔtsa.

english_verse: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔnyɛ bati ɔwɔlɛ kɔbɛ̃ kado ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna foe. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakpa bati fɔtsa ni, ɛkɛna foe mɔ ɔwɔlɛ faa. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ bati onukpɛ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkɛna foe mɔ anɛ kakpalɛ.

english_verse: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
translation_verse: Miwɛ akũ kalɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ. Mikesĩ tɔkpa, lɔkɔa mipi fɔtsa wĩ midomɔ akũ.

english_verse: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
translation_verse: Miwɛ akũ akũ kalɛ ndɛ bawaɛ̃ nɛ Kristo kamɛ aye, ade odi nyɛ odi ɛbɔa mbɔdi ɛkɔa obu ɛkpa ɔwaɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
translation_verse: Mikɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ ade mitana ɔnyɔnɛ a. Misum boe Ɔlate a mɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ mi kɔnɛte a kɔkpã mi nɛ kawɔlɛ kamɛ. Mina ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ nɛ mi ɔhau kamɛ, mikaabɔa mpaɛ daa daa.

english_verse: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
translation_verse: Mibumɔ asɔli bati a kĩ badi ahiã a, ade mitɛ banɔvɔɛ̃ faa nɛ mi tɔtɔ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
translation_verse: Miwã Baguma kĩ ehila bikĩ bitomɔ mi liti a. Mivia kĩ ehila be, ɛtadum be.

english_verse: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
translation_verse: Mimɔ kawɔlɛ mɔ bikĩ bamɔ kawɔlɛ, ade mibe mɔ bikĩ babe lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
translation_verse: Mina nɛ adzuni mɔapɛ kamɛ mikpa odi nyɛ odi. Mitapia akũ, mbom mikɛna tɔkɔba mɔ bikĩ bɛdɔla be akũ kĩ bati lábu nɛ lɛtsama kamɛ a. Mitabu mi akũ kĩ minyina kadi.

english_verse: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti odi fum nɔ ni, takɔa tɔkpa ose ye kɔtɔ. Mibɔa mbɔdi mikɛna lɛtsa kĩ odi nyɛ odi abu kĩ lɛnɛnɛ a.

english_verse: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔ́alɛ kakɛna, mɔ fɔ́ato mi sɛkɛ̃ ni, mimɔ odi nyɛ odi midzi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mitakɛna odima tɔkpa mise kɔtɔ da, mbom mifɔ̃ Baguma kalɔkpa kɛkɛna foe. Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, Ɔlate a lɛ kĩ, “Yĩ aavɛlɛ sɔtɔ, yĩ aakɛna nse kɔtɔ.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ kɔlɛ akɔ nɔ okesĩnɛ ni, kpa ye funitsã ɛnya. Ntɛ ɔmɔado akɔ ye ni, kpa ye mui ɛmɔa. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ ɔkɛna fukĩĩ ni, aafɔ̃ kɔnyɔã liwo ye paa.”

english_verse: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_verse: Tafɔ̃ tɔkpa lɛnya nɔ akũ, mbom kɔa tuwĩ kanya tɔkpa akũ.

english_verse: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ odi nyɛ odi ebu ɔmaĩ akũ banyanɛ, kitonɛkĩ otumi odima lɛláa kĩ ɛtáto Baguma sɛkɛ̃, ade ɔmaĩ akũ banyanɛ a kĩ bidzi finyaa a, Baguma lidzina be.

english_verse: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bakɛna tuwĩ a, lávila bati akũ banyanɛ, mbom bikĩ bakɛna tɔkpa a avila be. Ɔwɛ kĩ bati akũ ɔnyanɛ ɛtahũnahũna nɔ? Kekɔ̃ kɛna lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnɛnɛ, lɔkɔa yaamu nɔ.

english_verse: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati akũ ɔnyanɛ a nyɛ Baguma ɔsumunɛ kĩ yakɛna adzuma ɛkpa nɔ kalɛ. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔkɛna tɔkpa kɔ̃ ni, kekɔ̃ vila ye, kitonɛkĩ edeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ yavɛlɛ sɔtɔ ndɛ ayekĩ kɛfɛta. Baguma fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ yanɛ, ade yadã kĩ bɛkpa bikĩ bɛkɛna tɔkpa a Baguma sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a.

english_verse: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩ sũ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ oti adɔla akũ ɛkpa ɔmaĩ akũ banyanɛ a, nnɛ́ nɛ Baguma sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ odi sũ, mbom ayekĩ oti ɔwɔlɛ fɔ̃ enyina tuwĩ a sũ.

english_verse: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ lɛmɛ sũ miase ɔmaĩ tɔgɔlɛ a, kitonɛkĩ ɔmaĩ akũ banyanɛ a akɛna adzuma kakpa Baguma ntɛ bɛkɛna be tudzuma tɛkpɔla.

english_verse: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
translation_verse: Mitadzimɔ odima kɔtɔ, kɔtɔ a kĩ miadzimɛna a nyɛ kĩ miwɛ mi akũ akũ kalɛ. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛwɛ ɔwaɛ̃ kalɛ ni, ɛkɛna nɛ Baguma mbla a flee akũ.

english_verse: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Mbla a kĩ Baguma kpa a lɛ kĩ, “Tabɔa adɔpaa, takɔ oti, taye, tayɔɔlɛ anɛ nɛ nɔ ɔkɔba fɔtsa akũ,” mɔ tɛbɛbã a kĩ tibu a flee boomu nɛ mbla mɔapɛ kamɛ kĩ, “Wɛ nɔ ɔkɔba kalɛ ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ nɔ akũ kalɛ a.”

english_verse: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
translation_verse: Ntɛ awɛ odi kalɛ ni, ɔláakɛna tɔkpa nɛ ye akũ, foesũ ɔdɔ nyɛ mbla a flee akũ kakɛna.

english_verse: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ miakɛna fukĩĩ, kitonɛkĩ minyina kĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ mianyinya nɛ tida kamɛ. Kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma aatɛ boe a nkpã fuã finyaa lɛba lɔkɔ a kĩ bɔnya lɔtɔ bɔtɛ Kristo budo a.

english_verse: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
translation_verse: Lɛnyɛ lɔkɔ tɛ̃ liyua, kade lamɛna kanyina. Mifɔ̃ bɔfɔ̃ ɔtũ kamɛ fɔtsa kakɛna, lɔkɔa bɔbɔɛ kawũ kanɔ fɔtsa bɔbla bɔkpa kanɔ nɛ ɔhaĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bupi boe akũ kapi wĩ ndɛ bati kĩ bidzi nɛ ɔhaĩ kamɛ. Mitafɔ̃ bɔna nɛ kanya mɔ futo kawɔlɛ mɔ bavɛ kawuga kamɛ, ĩye adɔpaa kabɔa, ĩye ablɔ kadi, ĩye kapaa mɔ anɛngbã kakɛna kamɛ.

english_verse: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_verse: Mbom mibɔɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo kawũ fɔtsa mido ndɛ atadiɛ aye, lɔkɔa mifɔ̃ mi sukɛna akũ kadã kakɔna miyaadani se odo fɔtsa akũ bakɛnanɛ.

english_verse: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
translation_verse: Mitɛ oti a kĩ ɛtátim nɛ katɛkado kamɛ a mido nɛ mi kamɛ faa, mitavɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa mɔ ye nɛ lɛtsa kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkɛna mɔ likĩ kɛtáfɛta kĩ ɛkɛna a akũ.

english_verse: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
translation_verse: Oti odi katɛkado kpa ye osi kĩ ɛnya funitsã fudi nyɛ fufudi, lɛmɛ oti a kĩ ye katɛkado tátim a kɔ̃ anya kɔba kamɛ funitsã odi.

english_verse: But you should not criticize others for eating or for not eating. After all, God welcomes everyone.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ yanya lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a, ɛtasupa oti a kĩ yaánya foe. Ade oti a kĩ yanya kɔba funitsã odi a, ɛtadzɛmɔ ekĩ yanya lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa, kitonɛkĩ Baguma tɛ ye.

english_verse: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
translation_verse: Owei anɛ kĩ adzɛmɔ oti bɛbã ɔsumunɛ? Ye mɔawɛ ɔlate aamɔ ntɛ ɛdzɛ̃ pintii ĩye ɛtɔ. Lɛmɛ yaanya akũ kitonɛkĩ boe Ɔlate a aapuli ɛkɛna ye ɛdza pintii.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
translation_verse: Oti odi abu kĩ luwi lidi sĩ lɛba fuwi bɛbã a, ade odi lɛmɛ adã kĩ fuwi a flee keti a. Nɛ fukĩĩ odu akũ a, odi nyɛ odi ɛdza pintii nɛ kakɔ mɔa.

english_verse: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ebu luwi lidi paa ni, ɛkɛna foe kĩ yado boe Ɔlate a obu. Ade ayea odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaánya funitsã fudi a, kɛna foe kĩ yado boe Ɔlate a obu, ade yanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ foe akũ a.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
translation_verse: Boe kamɛ odima lidzí nkpã ɛkpa ye akũ odi, ade boe kamɛ odima láku lɛmɛ kakpa ye akũ odi a.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
translation_verse: Ntɛ budzi nkpã ni, boe Ɔlate a budzi bɔkpa, ade ntɛ buku a, boe Ɔlate a buku bɔkpa. Foesũ ntɛ budzi nkpã o-o ĩye buku o-o, boe Ɔlate a bade bɔanɛ.

english_verse: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ sũ mɔ Kristo ku, ade eyidza edzi nkpã ayekĩ yaanya bikĩ bidzi nkpã mɔ bikĩ biku a Ɔlate a.

english_verse: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
translation_verse: Ade nɔ kɔ̃, nde sũ akɔ awaɛ̃ kɔtɔ? Ĩye nɔ malo nde sũ asupa awaɛ̃? Boe a flee bɔ́adza nɛ Baguma anɛ kamɛ lɔkɔa ɛdzɛmɔ boe.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Ɔlate a lɛ kĩ, ‘Kĩ ndzi nkpã kĩĩ a, odi nyɛ odi aake aduli ɛkpa yĩ, ade odi nyɛ odi aabinya onukpɛ̃ ɛlɛ kĩ yĩ nyɛ Baguma a.’ ”

english_verse: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
translation_verse: Foesũ boe kamɛ odi nyɛ odi aakpa Baguma ye mɔawɛ akũ akunta. Tafɔ̃ Awaɛ̃ Okristoni Lɛkɛna Tɔkpa.

english_verse: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔfɔ̃ akũ akũ kadzɛmɛna, kakɔ kɔtɔ. Fukĩ miakɛna nyɛ kĩ mikɛna adzuma kĩ miláakɛna lɛtsama kĩ fɔ́afɔ̃ awaɛ̃ lɛtɔ ĩye fɔfɔ̃ ɛkɛna tɔkpa.

english_verse: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
translation_verse: Yĩ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu sũ, ntɛ ndo kĩ funitsã fudima nɛ foe akũ akũ lɛnyɛ́ efĩĩ fɔtsa. Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi tɛ edo kĩ funitsã fudi akũ tákpalɛ ni, kekɔ̃ fɔkɛna efĩĩ fɔtsa fɔkpa ye.

english_verse: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Ntɛ nɔ funitsã kĩ anya sũ awaɛ̃ tɛ kalɔɛ ni, kekɔ̃ nnɛ́ ɔdɔ kamɛ ɔtsɛ̃mɔ ye. Tafɔ̃ funitsã a kĩ anya a lɛwɔla oti a kĩ ye lɔlɔ Kristo ku a.

english_verse: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
translation_verse: Foesũ mitafɔ̃ bati bɛbã lisupa fɔtsa a kĩ mi miakɔna kĩ fɔnɛnɛ a.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛgãkanya a lɛnyɛ́ kanya mɔ kamɔa, mbom fɔnyɛ tuwĩ kakɛna mɔ lukudɔ, mɔ kawɔlɛ kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a akpa a.

english_verse: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ odi asum Kristo nɛ osi kĩĩ akũ a, yawo Baguma anɛ ade bati lɛmɛ akpa ye obu a.

english_verse: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ boe botaɛ ɛnya kĩ bɔakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ layamɛna lukudɔ, titiliwu mɔ fukĩ fɔ́abumɛna akũ akũ.

english_verse: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
translation_verse: Mitabula Baguma adzuma kade nɛ funitsã sũ. Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, funitsã flee akũ kpalɛ fɔkpa kanya, lɛmɛ fɔnɛ́nɛ kĩ banya lɛtsa kĩ laha odi fɔfɔ̃ ɛkɛna tɔkpa.

english_verse: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa wĩde nyɛ kĩ taba sɛkɛna ĩye ɔmɔa waĩ ĩye akɛna lɛtsa bɛbã lidima kĩ fɔ́afɔ̃ awaɛ̃ lɛkɛna tɔkpa.

english_verse: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
translation_verse: Fɔ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ ɔtɛ odo nɛ fɔtsa kĩĩ akũ a, fɔna nɛ mimɔ nɔ Baguma odi kayite. Baguma hila oti a kĩ ɛtákɔ ye akũ kɔtɔ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna, ɛmɔ kĩ lɛnɛnɛ a sũ.

english_verse: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi anya lɛtsa kĩ ye ɔwɔlɛ tákpa ye osi a, Baguma akɔ ye kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna a táto katɛkado kamɛ. Lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ oti akɛna kĩ fɔtáto katɛkado kamɛ ni, tɔkpa tanɛ.

english_verse: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ boe a kĩ budeĩ ɔsĩ nɛ katɛkado kamɛ a, bɔabumɔ bati a kĩ be katɛkado lɛlá ɔsĩ a nɛ be ɔhau kamɛ. Kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ laawo boe mɔawɛ anɛ.

english_verse: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ boe kamɛ odi nyɛ odi ɛkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ laawo boe bakɔba anɛ ayekĩ fɔ́alɛ fɔkpa be, bitim nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ.

english_verse: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Kristo malo tákɛna fɔtsa a kĩ fuwo ye anɛ, mbom ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, Kristo bi Baguma kĩ, “Fuvũnatsa a kĩ bɛta bɛkpa nɔ a, yako nɛ yĩ akũ.”

english_verse: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ye nyɛ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ kado katokɔ̃ a, ɛfɔ̃ mina onukpɛ̃ mɔa nɛ mi kamɛ, mitomɔ Kristo Yesu osi a kĩ ɛtsa a,

english_verse: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
translation_verse: ayekĩ mi flee míakɔa ɔwɔlɛ mɔa mɔ odo mɔa mimu Baguma, kĩ ɛnyɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo Ɔlɛga a. Paulo Kɔa Kalɛ Wĩ a Ɛma Bikĩ Bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a Lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
translation_verse: Foesũ mitɛ mi bawaɛ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ Kristo tɛ mi a, fɔnya obu fɔkpa Baguma.

english_verse: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
translation_verse: Mabi mi kĩ Kristo yakɛna Yudafɔ a ɔsumunɛ, ɛkɔa ɛtsa kĩ Baguma adi ɔnɔkɔali, lɔkɔa ete de nɛ bɔhɛ a kĩ Baguma kɛna mɔ Yudafɔ bawanyɔ a akũ,

english_verse: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
translation_verse: ayekĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ aamu Baguma nɛ ye kɔnyakamɔ sũ. Ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Nɛ foesũ maamu nɔ nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a kamɛ. Maasã kamu tɔnɔ ndɛla nɔ dĩ nɛ ode.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
translation_verse: Ade bibuki bɛtsɛlɛ biyi kĩ, “Mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, mimɔ kawɔlɛ mɔ Baguma bade a.”

english_verse: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ bɛbã lɛ kĩ, “Mi a kĩ minyɛ́ Yudafɔ a flee mimu boe Ɔlate a. Mi fude fude flee mimu ye!”

english_verse: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
translation_verse: Ade Yesaya lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ, “Isai kasinu a kamɛ obi odi aanɛ ɛta, ye aayanya bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a akũ. Ade ye akũ baakɔa anɛ bite a.”

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ye akũ bɔate anɛ a ɛfɔ̃ kawɔlɛ mɔ lukudɔ lɛtɛ mi kamɛ nɛ mi ye katɛkado sũ, ayekĩ mi kɔnɛte a aakabo anɛ nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kɔbɛ̃ a kamɛ. Paulo Nyɛ Lɛtsa a Sũ Kĩ Ɛtsɛlɛ Fɔlɛtsa Kĩĩ a Kayɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, yĩ mɔawɛ a ntɛ ndo paa kĩ fɔtsa wĩ kakɛna tɛ mi kamɛkamɛ, ade minyina kadi pii kĩ míapuli midɛ akũ akũ.

english_verse: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ovoli kĩĩ kamɛ a, ndo ɔwɔlɛ ntsɛlɛ fɔtsa fudi faa kĩ mabuki nkɔa nkai mi foe. Ndo ɔwɔlɛ kitonɛkĩ Baguma mɔ yĩ kɔnya ɛkɔa adzuma kĩĩ edo yĩ nɛ awɛ,

english_verse: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
translation_verse: kĩ ĩnya Kristo Yesu ɔsumunɛ nkɛna adzuma nkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a. Nkɛna adzuma ndɛ ɔsɔfɔ nkalɛ Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ a, ayekĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a aakɛna ndɛ afɔlibɔ wĩ kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kpalɛ ye akũ ɛkpa Baguma.

english_verse: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
translation_verse: Foesũ yĩ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Yesu Kristo sũ ni, maapuli mpia nɛ adzuma a kĩ nkɛna nkpa Baguma a akũ.

english_verse: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ odi maado ɔwɔlɛ nlɛ nyɛ kĩ Kristo tsã yĩ akũ ɛfɔ̃ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ na osetie nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa akũ. Ɛkɛna fukĩĩ ɛtsãmɛna yĩ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ nlɛ, mɔ adzuma a kĩ nkɛna a kamɛ.

english_verse: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ ya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ awãwã mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔtsa a kĩ nkɛna, nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kɔbɛ̃ a kamɛ. Foesũ ntsã mmini nto Yerusalem nnawo Iliriko tite akũ a, nde lɛtsã nɛ Kristo Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ ntsɛna tɛmatɔ a flee.

english_verse: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
translation_verse: Yĩ kawɛ mɔ ɔsĩ nyɛ kĩ made lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nɛ awã kĩ bati tánu tɔ̃ Kristo dĩ, ayekĩ nlánake nte nɛ litekũ kĩ odi nya lɔtɔ eke a akũ.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Bati a kĩ bɛtábi be kadima nɛ ye akũ tɔ̃ a, aamɔ ye, ade bikĩ bɛtánu tɔ̃ kadima nɛ ye akũ a, aanu foe kayɔ.” Paulo Kawɛ Kĩ Yanaa Roma.

english_verse: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ nkawɛ lɔkɔ pii kĩ maya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃, mɔ ntána osi a.

english_verse: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa kĩ ĩyua yĩ adzuma nɛ fuyite kĩĩ, mɔ ayekĩ nkawɛ futeli pii kĩ mayaasɛla mi a sũ ni,

english_verse: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
translation_verse: matɛ kado kĩ maapuli nkɛna foe finyaa. Mawɛ kĩ ntɛ ĩyaatɛ̃ nnnaa Spania, maadza nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ sukuloo budo akũ kawɔlɛ, lɔkɔa mibumɛna minyɛ yĩ osi nnaa.

english_verse: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Makedonia mɔ Akaia tɛsɔli a te fɔtsa faa kĩ bakɔa bibumɛna ahiãfɔ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Baguma bade a kamɛ nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Be mɔawɛ a adzuni fɔanɛ, lɛmɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, be adzuma fɔanɛ kĩ bibumɔ be. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a na tsɛfã nɛ Yudafɔ hũhũ nhila fɔtsa a kamɛ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a lɛmɛ akɔa be akũ kana fɔtsa bibumɛna Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ nkɛna adzuma kĩĩ ĩyua, mɔ nkɔa fɔtsa a kĩ bite a nkpa be ni, maatsã mi sɛkɛ̃ ntɛ̃ nnaa Spania.

english_verse: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina kĩ ntɛ maya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ni, maakɔa Kristo Kalɛ Wĩ kamɛ nhila baũ mma mi.

english_verse: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, nnɛɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo mɔ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a ɔdɔ kĩ yakpa a, make mi aduli kĩ mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ yĩ bɔbɔa mpaɛ mɔ ɔsĩ bɔkpa Baguma nɛ yĩ lɔlɔ.

english_verse: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
translation_verse: Mibɔa mpaɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ yĩ etomɛna bati a kĩ bɛtátɛ bido nɛ Yudea a awɛ. Mibɔa mpaɛ lɛmɛ kĩ yĩ kasum a kĩ manaakɛna nɛ Yerusalem a kiwo Baguma bade a anɛ.

english_verse: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔnyɛ Baguma kawɛ ni, maaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ mɔ kawɔlɛ ĩyaadzi nwɛya.

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
translation_verse: Baguma kĩ yakpa lukudɔ a, ɛnamɔ mi flee. Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
translation_verse: Mawɛ kĩ mabɔa boe ɔwaɛ̃tsole Foibe kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔsumunɛ paa nɛ Kenkrea asɔli a kamɛ dĩ ntsa mi.

english_verse: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
translation_verse: Mitɛ ye nɛ boe Ɔlate a dĩ akũ ndɛ ayekĩ fɔfɛta Baguma bade a aye. Mikpa ye kabumɛna kadi nyɛ kadi kĩ yaasĩ etomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃, kitonɛkĩ ɛnyɛ bati pii mɔ yĩ mɔawɛ malo obumɛnanɛ.

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Priskila mɔ Akwila kĩ bɛnyɛ yĩ bakɔba adzuma bakɛnanɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo adzuma kamɛ.

english_verse: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa be nkpã bɛwãnɛ yĩ lɔlɔ. Nnɛ́ yĩ odi anyɛ be kɔlɛ, mbom mɔ tɛsɔli a kĩ tideĩ nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ kamɛ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na asɔli bati a kĩ batsɛna nɛ be tɔtɔ kamɛ a lɛmɛ. Mawɔ̃na yĩ ɔwɛnɛ Epaineto kĩ ɛnya oti ɔtɔnyade kĩ ɛtɛ Kristo edo nɛ Asia amantam a kamɛ.

english_verse: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Maria kĩ ɛkɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ ɛkpa mi a.

english_verse: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Androniko mɔ Yunia, yĩ akũ bati, kĩ bɔmɔ be bɔwa kɔla a. Bɛtɛ dĩ nɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ, ade bɛnya yĩ lɔtɔ nɛ Kristo katɛ kado kamɛ a.

english_verse: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Ampila kĩ mawɛ kalɛ paa, kĩ edeĩ nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna kamɛ mɔ boe Ɔlate a.

english_verse: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Urbana lɛmɛ, boe ɔkɔba nɛ Kristo adzuma a kamɛ, mɔ yĩ ɔkɔba paa Staki.

english_verse: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Apele kĩ bɛmɔ́ kĩ etim nɛ Kristo kamɛ a. Mawɔ̃na Aristobulo tɔtɔ kamɛ bati a flee.

english_verse: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na yĩ kade oti Herodion mɔ Narkiso tɔtɔ kamɛ bati a kĩ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Kristo a.

english_verse: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Trifaina mɔ Trifosa kĩ bakɛna boe Ɔlate a adzuma a, mɔ yĩ ɔwɛnɛ Persi kĩ ɛkɛna adzuma paa ɛkpa boe Ɔlate a.

english_verse: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Rufo kĩ ɛtɛ dĩ nɛ boe Ɔlate adzuma kamɛ a, mɔ ɔma kĩ epi yĩ ndɛ obi aye a.

english_verse: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Asinkrito, Flegon, Herme, Patroba, Herma mɔ be bawaɛ̃ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
translation_verse: Mawɔ̃na Filologo, Yulia, Nerea mɔ ye ɔwaɛ̃tsole, mɔ Olipa mɔ Baguma bade a kĩ bideĩ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
translation_verse: Miwɔ̃na akũ mikɛna atuu nɛ Kristo ɔdɔ kamɛ. Kristo tɛsɔli bati a flee awɔ̃na mi! Paulo Dɛ Batɛ Badonɛ Nɛ Roma a.

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, make mi aduli kĩ mifɔ̃ mi anɛ ɛna nɛ bati a kĩ bayamɛna kasɛ̃, kakpã badi bayɛla be katɛkado a akũ. Mivɛlɛ akũ nɛ be akũ!

english_verse: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati a kĩ bakɛna fukĩnaa bamɛ odu a lásum Kristo, boe Ɔlate a, mbom be mɔawɛ lupu basum. Bakɔa sɛmɛ mɛnɛmɛnɛ mɔ fɔvã fɔlɛtsa kamu bati, ade bakɛ bati a kĩ binyína tɔkpa a adzuni a.

english_verse: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ odi nyɛ odi nu ayekĩ miayi atɔ katse Kalɛ Wĩ a, nɛ foesũ kawɔlɛ ade yĩ paa nɛ mi akũ. Lɛtsa a kĩ mawɛ nyɛ kĩ minyi kadi nɛ lɛtsa wĩ kakɛna kamɛ, lɛmɛ nɛ tɔkpa kakɛna kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, minya bati kpĩĩ.

english_verse: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Owi lɛláabuna, Baguma a kĩ yakpa lukudɔ a, aabubuli Satana kpɛtɛkpɛtɛkpɛtɛ nɛ mi abɔa kayɔ. Boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo ɛmɔ mi kɔnya.

english_verse: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
translation_verse: Timoteo yĩ adzuma kamɛ ɔkɔba awɔ̃na mi flee. Ade aye a Lukio lɛmɛ, mɔ Yason mɔ Sosipatro kĩ bɛnyɛ yĩ kade bati awɔ̃na mi a.

english_verse: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
translation_verse: Yĩ Tertio, kĩ ntsɛlɛ ovoli kĩĩ nkpa Paulo a, lɛmɛ mawɔ̃na mi nɛ boe Ɔlate a dĩ akũ.

english_verse: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bumu Baguma. Yaapuli edo mi katɛkado kɔbɛ̃ nɛ ayekĩ ɛfɔ̃ nde lɛtsã Kalɛ Wĩ a nɛ Yesu Kristo akũ a, mɔ ayekĩ ɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa bɛlawa a kito anɛ kɔkɔɔkɔɛ a ɛtsa sũ.

english_verse: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa, bɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali a na bɛtsa fude flee bɛtsãmɛna Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa katsɛlɛ kayi a kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã daa hihiɛ eyi a, ayekĩ bati flee aatɛ bido, bɛkɛna nɛ ye ɔlɛdo a akũ.

english_verse: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ye mɔapɛ nyɛ ɔnyansãni a, bɔakpa kamu bɔtsãmɛna Yesu Kristo akũ kanaa daa daa. Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
translation_verse: Yĩ Paulo kĩ Baguma vɛ nɛ ye kawɛ onukpɛ̃, kĩ nkɛna Kristo Yesu fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ, mɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃ Sostene sɛkɛ̃ ovoli kĩĩ to.

english_verse: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
translation_verse: Bɔtsɛlɛ ovoli kĩĩ bɔakpa batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Korinto, mɔ bati a kĩ Baguma vɛ kĩ bɛkɛna ye bati kpalɛwa, kĩ bɛnyɛ ye bade nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo Yesu a. Bɛvɛ mi bati kĩĩ mɔ bati a flee kĩ basum boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ. Yesu kĩĩ nyɛ be mɔ boe flee Ɔlate.

english_verse: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Mawã Baguma boe Ɔlɛga mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo kĩ bɛmɔ mi kɔnya, bɛkpa mi lukudɔ lɛmɛ. Nhila Kĩ Bɔana Katomɛna Yesu Kristo Kamɛ.

english_verse: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Luwi daa manyɛ yĩ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ mi lɔlɔ kĩ ɛtsã Yesu Kristo akũ ɛmɔ mi kɔnya.

english_verse: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
translation_verse: Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ Kristo akũ a sipi nɛ mi kamɛ paa,

english_verse: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
translation_verse: kĩ foesũ mitɛ hũhũ kamɛ futetsa fudi nyɛ fudi kĩ fusĩ mi, nɛ kĩ midzi miadzɔlɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo kaya a.

english_verse: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
translation_verse: Yaapi mi edomɔ akũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kɛnaawo kawũna, ayekĩ nfumsu adima lɛláana nɛ mi akũ nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo Luwi a akũ.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ɛvɛ mi kĩ mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye Obi, boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo a, nyɛ oti kĩ yadi ɔnɔkɔali. Kasɛ̃ Kĩ Kɛna Nɛ Asɔli a Kamɛ.

english_verse: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ndzɛ̃ nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo otumi akũ make mi a flee aduli kĩ mikɛna ɔmɔa nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ mialɛ a akũ ayekĩ kasɛ̃ lɛláaya nɛ mi kayite. Mina onukpɛ̃ mɔa nɛ mi kakɔna mɔ fukĩ foe akũ mike otse a kamɛ,

english_verse: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ, nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bati badi to Kloe tɔtɔ kamɛ bɛyabi yĩ pefee kĩ ɔdzɛ deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ.

english_verse: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ nlɛ foe limii, bati a lɛ kĩ mi kamɛ badi alɛ kĩ, “Yĩ kɔ̃ Paulo ntomɛna.” Ɔbɛbã lɛmɛ alɛ kĩ, “Ntomɛna Apolo.” Odi lɛmɛ bo, “Ntomɔ Petro.” Ade odi lɛmɛ bo, “Ntomɔ Kristo.”

english_verse: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
translation_verse: Mavia mi ni, bɛsɛ̃ Kristo nɛ kutsukutsu kamɛ mbɔɛɛ? Paulo bɛbɔa nɛ asendua akũ nɛ mi lɔlɔ mbɔɛɛ? Mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɛkɛna mi kamɛ odi mui kawɔla amaniɛ ndɛ Paulo fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ aye?

english_verse: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
translation_verse: Manyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ kĩ ntákɛna mi kamɛ odima mui kawɔla amaniɛ kɛba Krispo mɔ Gayo odi pɛ.

english_verse: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
translation_verse: Foesũ odima lɛláapuli ɛlɛ kĩ bɛkɛna ye mui kawɔla amaniɛ nɛ yĩ dĩ akũ.

english_verse: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
translation_verse: Nuwĩ mate anɛ kĩ nkɛna Stefana mɔ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati mui kawɔla amaniɛ, lɛmɛ maáte anɛ kĩ nwɔla oti bɛbã odima.

english_verse: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
translation_verse: Kristo tádo yĩ kĩ ĩyaakɛna mui kawɔla amaniɛ nkpa bati. Edo yĩ kĩ ĩyaade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ, kĩ ntákɔa batidziwa kadikanyi nlɛ ke ayekĩ Kristo wuda nɛ asendua akũ kɔbɛ̃ a lɛláakɛna fɔtsa pupulidza. Kristo Fɔlɛtsa a Nyɛ Baguma Kɔbɛ̃ Mɔ Nyansã.

english_verse: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
translation_verse: Kristo wuda nɛ asendua akũ fɔlɛtsa a nyɛ fudzimbitsa fɔkpa bati a kĩ bɛtsɛ̃ nɛ kawɔ osi akũ, lɛmɛ boe a kĩ bɛlamɔ katɛ a, bɔmɔ́ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ buyi ɔsũ ndɛ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ aye.

english_verse: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
translation_verse: Nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa kamɛ a, Baguma lɛ kĩ, “Maawɔla kadi banyinɛ a kadikanyi, ade maanyɛ tuvoli banyinɛ kayɔkanu a ĩyi nɛ ɔɔwɛ̃ a.”

english_verse: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
translation_verse: Foesũ finyaa ndia kadi banyinɛ a dzɛ̃? Ndia tuvoli banyinɛ fɔtsa batsanɛ a mɔ fɔlɛtsa bavɛlɛnɛ a deĩ? Baguma kɔa foe ɛtsa kĩ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kadikanyi nyɛ fudzimbitsa.

english_verse: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma nɛ ye kadikanyi onukpɛ̃ ni, ɛkɛna foe kĩ batidziwa bɛtatsã be kadikanyi kamɛ binyi ye. Mbom lɛtsa kĩ bavɛ boe tidzimbi lɛtsã kade fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ a, Baguma kɔa foe ɛnyɛ bikĩ bɛtɛ ye bido a.

english_verse: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ awɛ awãwã fɔtsa bɛmɔ́, ade Grikifɔ lɛmɛ tomɛna katinya kadikanyi liti a.

english_verse: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe kɔ̃ bɔade lɛtsã nɛ Kristo wuda nɛ asendua akũ. Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ aba Yudafɔ tuwũna, ade fɔnyɛ fɔkɔasiatsa fɔkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a.

english_verse: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ be a kĩ Baguma vɛ a kĩ bɛnyɛ Yudafɔ, mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ Helafɔ a ni, fɔlɛtsa a nyɛ Kristo kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ mɔ ye kadikanyi.

english_verse: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔamɔ ndɛ Baguma tidzimbi nhihiɛ a bɛ̃ otidziwa kadikanyi kɔlaa, ade ke awã kĩ fɔkɛna ndɛ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ dɔ nɛ ye lɛkɛnana kamɛ a, bɛ̃ otidziwa kɔbɛ̃ kɔlaa.

english_verse: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mikɔna mi akũ midã ayekĩ mina fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ mi a. Nɛ batidziwa kamɔ onukpɛ̃ ni, mi badi sukuloo nyɛ kadi banyinɛ, ĩye bikĩ bideĩ kɔbɛ̃, ĩye kĩ mi akũ deĩ obu.

english_verse: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
translation_verse: Baguma bua ɛpau katinya kamɛ fɔtsa kĩ foe akũ lɛlá nfasɔ, kĩ yakɔa foe edo kadi banyinɛ kɔnyɔã, ade ɛpau fɔtsa a kĩ fɔlá akũ ɔsĩ kĩ yakɔa foe edo bikĩ bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ a kɔnyɔã.

english_verse: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
translation_verse: Baguma pau fɔtsa a kĩ bati lábu foe kado nɛ lɛtsama kamɛ, kasupa foe lɛmɛ a, kĩ yakɔa foe ɛwɔla fukĩ bati akɔna kĩ fɔtɛ kɔya a.

english_verse: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
translation_verse: Baguma kɛna fukĩĩ ayekĩ otidziwa odima lɛláapuli epia ye akũ nɛ ye anɛ.

english_verse: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma kĩĩ lɛfɔ̃ mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Yesu Kristo, ade ɛfɔ̃ Kristo nyɛ boe kadikanyi a. Baguma tsã ye akũ ɛkɛna boe bati kpĩĩ, ɛkpalɛ boe akũ, ɛfɔ̃ bɔna nkpã.

english_verse: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
translation_verse: Foesũ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Oti kĩ yawɛ kĩ yapia ye akũ ni, epia nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate kɛna a akũ.”

english_verse: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ade foesũ lɔkɔ a kĩ ĩya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ kĩ matsa Baguma ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ kɔlawa a, ntákɔa onukpɛ̃ mɛnɛmɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa kalɛ baũbaũ mɔ nyansã dzununuunu ntsa mi foe.

english_verse: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kĩ nna nɛ mi kamɛ a, nke otse kĩ manyɛ anɛ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa akũ mbɔɛntɛ Yesu Kristo mɔ ye wuda nɛ asendua akũ a.

english_verse: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ ĩya nɛ awã a, ndã kpɛtɛɛ, nna nɛ libe mɔ kukũtila kamɛ,

english_verse: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
translation_verse: ade yĩ fɔtsa katsa mɔ yĩ ɔlɛdo a tánya batidziwa kɔdzɛla kalɛ baũ baũ ĩye nyansã tisi akũ fɔlɛtsa, mbom nde lɛtsã a kayɔ kanu a kĩ Baguma Hũhũ kɔbɛ̃ a nyɛ ɛtsa a.

english_verse: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ mi katɛkado lɛdzɛ̃ nɛ batidziwa nyansã akũ, mbom kɛdzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ akũ. Baguma Hũhũ a Nyina Lɛtsa Nyɛ Lɛtsa.

english_verse: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ made kadikanyi lɛtsã kakpa bikĩ bibo anɛ nɛ hũhũ kamɛ a. Lɛmɛ fɔnyɛ́ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kadikanyi ĩye ke kɔbɛ̃ kĩ kanya katinya akũ. Kɔbɛ̃ a akũ ɔlɔmɛ lamɛna katinya.

english_verse: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
translation_verse: Kadikanyi fɔlɛtsa a kĩ matsa a nyɛ Baguma kadikanyi kɔlawa, kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla batidziwa, lɛmɛ Baguma nyɛ foe eyi ɛkpa boe kadɛla nɛ ode fɔmɔ ɛbɔa katinya malo.

english_verse: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
translation_verse: Katinya kamɛ kĩĩ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ odima tányi kadikanyi kĩĩ. Ntɛ kanyi binyi ke kufɛ bɛtábɔa boe Ɔlate kpalɛwa a nɛ asendua akũ.

english_verse: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Lɛtsa a kĩ linebi támɔ tɔ̃, sɔtɔ tánu tɔ̃, lɛtsa a kĩ odima tákɔna tɔ̃ kĩ laapuli lɛya a, ade foe pɛpɛɛpɛ Baguma manɛ eyi ɛkpa bati a kĩ bawɛ ye a.”

english_verse: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ade boe Baguma tsã ye Hũhũ Kpalɛwa akũ ɛnyɛ foe ɛtsa a. Hũhũ a awɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ yededeede, ade yafiifii Baguma fɔtsa a kĩ ɛkɔla a dzununuunu kamɛ malo a.

english_verse: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
translation_verse: Odima lɛláapuli enyi lɛtsa a kĩ oti bɛbã akɔna mbɔɛntɛ oti a mɔawɛ hũhũ a kĩ edeĩ nɛ ye kamɛ a. Ade ayea odima lɛláapuli enyi Baguma kakɔna mbɔɛntɛ ye mɔawɛ Hũhũ odi.

english_verse: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
translation_verse: Bɔtátɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ hũhũ, mbom bɔtɛ Baguma Hũhũ a kĩ edo a, ayekĩ bɔ́anyi fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma kpa boe a flee.

english_verse: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
translation_verse: Foesũ nnɛ́ batidziwa kadikanyi kĩ batsa bɔnɛɛ bɔalɛ kalɛ, mbom bɔnɛɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a tsa boe a bɔkɔa bɔanyɛ hũhũ kamɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔtsa a katsa bikĩ Hũhũ deĩ nɛ be kamɛ a.

english_verse: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ Hũhũ a deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ aapuli enu fukĩĩ flee kayɔ. Lɛmɛ odima lɛláapuli ɛdzɛmɔ ye.

english_verse: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Owei nyina lɛtsa a kĩ de boe Ɔlate akɔna? Owei aapuli ɛkpa ye kɔdɛ?” Lɛmɛ boe kɔ̃ Kristo kakɔna a deĩ nɛ boe kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ ntápuli mbi mi fɔlɛtsa ndɛ ayekĩ maabi bikĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa deĩ nɛ be kamɛ aye, mbom mbi mi fɔlɛtsa ndɛ bati kĩ bidzi nɛ sukɛna kamɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ babisɛ̃ nɛ Kristo katɛkado kamɛ.

english_verse: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
translation_verse: Tɛmɛ mui nkpa mi, nnɛ́ funitsã kɔkɔlɔkɔ kitonɛkĩ mitábo anɛ tɔ̃ kĩ mianya foe. Finyaakĩĩ malo mitáwo tɔ̃ kĩ mianya foe,

english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ mibuki midzi ndɛ ayekĩ katinya kamɛ kĩĩ bati dzi nkpã. Ntɛ mianɔ lineke, kapaa mɔ akũ ni, nnɛ́ katsa fɔatsa kĩ minyɛ katinya kamɛ bati kĩ bidzi nɛ ke tisi akũ?

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Ntɛ mi kamɛ ɔmɔa lɛ kĩ, “Ntomɔ Paulo,” mɔ ɔbɛbã bo, “Ntomɔ Apolo,” nnɛ́ katsa fɔatsa kĩ miakɛna fɔtsa ndɛ katinya kamɛ bati aye?

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe kamɛ a, owei nyɛ Apolo? Owei lɛmɛ nyɛ Paulo? Baguma adzuma bakɛnanɛ kɛkɛ bɔanɛ, ade mitsã boe akũ mitɛ mido a. Boe kamɛ odi nyɛ odi akɛna adzuma a kĩ Baguma kpa ye a.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Yĩ lidu kutse libi a, ade Apolo ko de mui a, lɛmɛ Baguma lɛfɔ̃ kutse a bo anɛ.

english_verse: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edu mɔ ekĩ eko de mui a tásĩ. Baguma lisĩ, kitonɛkĩ ade ye lɛfɔ̃ kutse a bo anɛ a.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Kavɛmɛna kadima lɛláa ekĩ edu mɔ ekĩ eko mui a kayite. Baguma aase be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kɔtɔ nɛ ye adzuma a kĩ ɛkɛna onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: Boe, buboomu bɔakɛna adzuma bɔkpa Baguma, ade mi nyɛ Baguma kɔba a kĩ koe kamɛ bɔakɛna adzuma a. Minyɛ Baguma kɔla a kĩ bɔake a.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ Baguma mɔ yĩ kɔnya sũ ni, nkɛna adzuma ndɛ kɔla okenɛ wĩ. Ĩyi litekũ lɔyɔde a kayɔ ade oti bɛbã lamɛna kake kadɛla nɛ ode a. Lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ ekĩ ɛlamɛna kake kadɛla ɛdã kadã wĩ nɛ ayekĩ yake a akũ.

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ baanyɛ kɔla a kĩ odi nyɛ odi lamɛna kake a kutitse bɛtsa nɛ Kristo Luwi a akũ. Kitonɛkĩ nɛ Luwi a na akũ fi aanyɛ odi nyɛ odi adzuma kĩ ɛkɛna a kanya. Baakɔa fi kĩĩ bɛsɔ tudzuma a bɛdã, fɔtsa te kɔyakatɛ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi fɔtsa vio ni, ɛláana kɔtɔkase, lɛmɛ ye mɔawɛ a yaana katɛ ndɛ kalo elo ɛnɛ nɛ fi a kamɛ aye. Minyɛ Baguma Asɔli Kɔla Kpalɛwa a.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Minyína kĩ Baguma Asɔli Kɔla mianɛ, kĩ Baguma Hũhũ a dzi nɛ mi kamɛ?

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ odi bula Baguma Asɔli Kɔla a kade ni, Baguma lɛmɛ aawɔla ye, kitonɛkĩ Baguma Asɔli Kɔla akũ kpalɛ. Ade mi nyɛ ye Asɔli Kɔla a.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Odima ɛtakɛ ye akũ. Ntɛ mi kamɛ odi kɔna kĩ enyina kadi nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kamɔ onukpɛ̃ ni, kɛfɛta kĩ ɛdani ɛkɛna odzimbifɔ mbom ayekĩ yaakɛna ɔnyansãni nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ bati abu ndɛ kadikanyi a ni, fɔnyɛ fudzimbitsa nɛ Baguma anɛ. Ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa lɛ a, “Baguma api anyansãfɔ nɛ be mɔawɛ kadikanyi kamɛ.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: Ade bibuki bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa kamɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate nyina kĩ, bikĩ binyina kadi a be adzuni nyɛ fɔtsa pupulidza.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: Paulo, Apolo, Petro, katinya kĩĩ, nkpã ĩye wuda, lɔkɔ kĩĩ ĩye, likĩ laaya o-o, foe flee fɔnyɛ mi fude.

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ minyɛ Kristo bade, ade Kristo lɛmɛ nyɛ Baguma ode a.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Mibu boe ndɛ Kristo basumunɛ a kĩ be awɛ Baguma kɔa ɔnɔkɔali fɔtsa kɔlawa a edo.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Lɛtsa kĩ bawɛ bitomɛna adzuma ɔkɛna kĩĩ odu awɛ nyɛ kĩ, yadi ɔnɔkɔali ɛkpa ɔlate.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ntsɛ́la kɔlaa ntɛ miadzɛmɔ yĩ, ĩye odi bɛbã adzɛmɔ yĩ. Ade maádzɛmɔ yĩ akũ malo a.

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: Yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ kpalɛ, lɛmɛ fɔátsa kĩ tɔkpa lɛláa yĩ akũ, kitonɛkĩ boe Ɔlate nyɛ oti kĩ yaadzɛmɔ yĩ.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Foesũ odima ɛtadzɛmɔ odi kɛnaawo kĩ lɔkɔ wĩ a wo ɔtɔ. Kɛnɛ kĩ kadzɛ nkaɛ a kɛdzɔlɛ boe Ɔlate a kaya. Ade ye aanyɛ ɔtũ kamɛ fɔtsa bɛlawa mɔ bati kakɔna a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla a kanya a. Ade lɔkɔ a na Baguma aakɔa kamu ɛkpa odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛfɛta ɛkpa kamu a.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, nɛ mi kalɛ sũ, nkɔa bɔmɔ Apolo fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ nkpa nfantuhũ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ matsa a akũ, ayekĩ míanu fɔlɛtsa a kĩ balɛ a kayɔ kĩ, “Mitsã nɛ osi kpĩĩ a akũ.” Mi kamɛ odima ɛtapia nɛ oti mɔa akũ lɔkɔa esupa ɔbɛbã.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Owei lɛkɛna mi bati kĩ bɛtɛ kɔya bɛba babɛbã? Nnɛ́ Baguma lɛkpa mi lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ mideĩ a? Lɔkɔa nde sũ miadɛla akũ ndɛ nnɛ́ Baguma lite mi fɔtsa a na?

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Mi awɛ ta lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lisĩ mi a kɔkɔɛ mbɔɛɛ? Midani asikafɔ kɔkɔɛ mbɔɛɛ? Midani fɔgã titɔ kĩ boe bɔnyɛ́ fɔgã malo? Awoo. Mawɛ kĩ minyɛ fɔgã lɔkɔa boe malo bɔnya fɔgã bubumɔ mi kufɛ.

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: Fɔadã yĩ ndɛ Baguma kɔa boe fɔtɔ banyanɛ a ɛdzɛla nɛ kadzakɔ̃ nkaɛ. Bɔkɛna ndɛ bati kĩ biku kɔtɔ kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkɔ be nɛ kanade anɛ, ade bɔkɛna ndɛ fɔtsa dãwa lɛmɛ bɔkpa katinya kamɛ bati mɔ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ flee a.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kristo sũ bɔnyɛ badzimbifɔ, lɛmɛ mi minyɛ kadi banyinɛ nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo kamɛ! Bɔlá kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ mideĩ kɔbɛ̃! Bati lábu boe kado nɛ lɛtsama kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi kɔ̃ bakpa mi obu!

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Kito kɛyawo nviã lɔkɔ kĩĩ malo budzi kɔlɛ mɔ ɔmɔado, budeĩ nɛ tɛtadiɛ kpeliwa kamɛ, babo boe, ade bɔtsɛ̃ bɔato katɛ̃ kĩ bɔlá kadzikɔ̃.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Bɔakɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kaveli akũ. Ntɛ bati dumu boe bɔakpa be nhila, ade ntɛ bitomɔ boe liti bɔado ɔwɔlɛ katɛ foe.

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bivũna boe a, bɔakɔa ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa be mbuayɛ. Bɛkɛna boe ndɛ katinya kamɛ fukluku kĩ bɔnyɛ tite akũ fukluku kawulakɔ̃ kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: Maátsɛlɛ mi fukĩĩ kĩ mado mi kɔnyɔã, mbom makɔa foe ndɛ mi ndɛ yĩ babi kĩ mawɛ be kalɛ aye.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ mideĩ bati mpim ewua kĩ batsa mi fɔtsa nɛ Kristo akũ malo ni, ɔlɛga mɔapɛ mideĩ. Ntsã Kalɛ Wĩ a kalɛ kamɛ ndani mi ɔlɛga, kitonɛkĩ mikɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Yesu nɛ mi nkpã kamɛ.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Foesũ make mi aduli kĩ mikɔlɛ yĩ ɔbla.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ mado Timoteo kĩ ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃. Ɛnyɛ yĩ mɔawɛ obi nɛ Kristo kamɛ, kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ ade ɛnyɛ oti kĩ yadi ɔnɔkɔali nɛ ye kadzi kamɛ a. Yaate mi anɛ nɛ tisi vɔɛ̃ a kĩ te akũ ndzi nkpã nɛ Kristo kamɛ a akũ. Ade foe lɛmɛ matsa nɛ tɔsɔli a flee kamɛ nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ a.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Mi kamɛ badi miata akũ, kitonɛkĩ mikɔna kĩ nláabuki ĩyaasɛla mi.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ boe Ɔlate tunɔ kɔ̃ ni, owi lɛláabuna kɔlaa mɔ maaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃. Ntɛ ĩya ni, maawɛ kĩ manyi kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ bikĩ bata akũ deĩ, nnɛ́ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsɛ̃ balɛ a odi.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛgãkanya a lɛnyɛ́ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa kalɛ kɛkɛ, mbom fɔnyɛ kɔbɛ̃ fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Fɔɔdabe miawɛ? Ĩya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ mɔ katsei, mbɔɛɛ kĩ ĩya nɛ ɔdɔ mɔ akũ kadɔla bɔɛɛ?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ bɛlamɛna kalɛ kokooko kĩ adɔpaa deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ ye odu lɛláa amamufɔ malo kamɛ. Bɛlɛ kĩ onyole odi mɔ ɔlɛga ɔtsɔ̃ dzi.

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔa nde míapuli midɛla mi akũ nɛ ode? Kanɛ kɛnɛ kĩ ayɔlɔhɔ api mi lɔkɔa minyɛ oti a kĩ ɛkɛna fɔtsa kpa kĩĩ odu a nɛ mi asɔli katsu a kamɛ.

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: mikɔa oti a mido nɛ Abonsam awɛ lɔkɔa ɛwɔla ye sukɛna a, ayekĩ ye hũhũ a aapuli ɛna katɛ nɛ boe Ɔlate Luwi a akũ.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Fɔnɛ́nɛ fɔkpa mi kĩ miata akũ! Minyina lɛgbã a kĩ, “Oyila kabii nɛ bodobodo mawɛ kamɛ afɔ̃ ye flee yavuya.”

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Minyɛ oyila kɔɛ a kĩ ɛnyɛ tɔkpa a nɛ mi kamɛ lɔkɔa mi akũ ɛkpalɛ. Míadani mikɛna onuvoe lɔbɔ vɔɛ̃ kĩ oyila lɛláa ye kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ ĩnyina kĩ mila a. Kitonɛkĩ bɛkɔ Kristo kĩ ɛnyɛ boe Wuda Katɛ̃ kavelibi bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ nɛ boe lɔlɔ.

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔnya boe Wuda Katɛ̃ luwi a, nnɛ́ mɔ abodoo kĩ edeĩ oyila kɔɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ tɔkpa mɔ ablɔ kakɛna, mbom bɔnya abodoo kĩ ɛlá oyila kĩ ɛnyɛ tuwĩ kakɛna mɔ ɔnɔkɔali kadi.
