english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: Nɛ ovoli a kĩ ntsɛlɛ nkpa mi kamɛ a, mbi mi kĩ mitakɔa akũ mibɔamɔ bati kĩ bideĩ adɔpaa.

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ni, nnɛ́ amamufɔ a kĩ bideĩ adɔpaa, ĩye bideĩ anɛ ngbã, ĩye baye, ĩye basum baviɔ̃ a malɛ. Ntɛ miwɛ kĩ mianyɛ mi akũ nɛ be flee kamɛ ni, kekɔ̃ kɛnɛ kĩ mianɛ nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kɔlaa.

english_verse: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
translation_verse: Lɛtsa kĩ matsa a nyɛ kĩ mitakɔa akũ mibɔamɔ oti kĩ yavɛ ye akũ kĩ ɔtɛ odonɛ lɛmɛ yabɔa adɔpaa, ĩye edeĩ anɛ ngbã, yasum baviɔ̃, ĩye yavɛ bati buĩ kpa, ĩye yawuga bavɛ ĩye yaye. Mimɔ oti a na odu mitado awɛ minya malo.

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɔdzɛ wo nɛ mi kamɛ odi mɔ ɔkɔba ɔtɛ odonɛ kayite ni, nde sũ yakɔa kalɛ a ɛma amamufɔ fɔlɛtsa badzɛnɛ, mɔ yaáfɔ̃ Baguma bati lɛdzɛ kalɛ a?

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Minyína kĩ boe a kĩ bɔnyɛ Baguma bati a aayadzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ? Ntɛ mi aayadzɛmɔ katinya kamɛ a, nde sũ miláapuli midzɛ fɔlɛtsa kubii kubii?

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Minyína kĩ boe aadzɛmɔ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ lɛmɛ mbɔɛɛ? Lɔkɔa nde sũ mɔ miláapuli midzɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ fɔtsa?

english_verse: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔlɛtsa a na odu ya ni, miákɔa foe mikpa bati kĩ bɛla fɔdzakɔ̃ nɛ asɔli a kamɛ malo kĩ bɛdzɛ foe?

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: Kɔnyɔã kuwo mi! Matɛ kado kĩ oti mɔapɛ aye kĩ enyina kadi deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ kĩ yaapuli ɛdzɛ mi batɛ badonɛ a kayite fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Kĩ miakɛna foe lɔmɔ mbom a ni, ɔtɛ odonɛ akpã ɔkɔba kanamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ kafɔ̃ bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido a adzɛ fɔlɛtsa a!

english_verse: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
translation_verse: Kalɛ ɔnɔkɔali a nyɛ kĩ nɛ ayekĩ miti miakɔa fɔlɛtsa kanamɛna fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a atsa kĩ, obu salɛ nɛ mi akũ. Fɔláalɛ fɔba ntɛ bɛkɛna tɔkpa nɛ mi akũ lɔkɔa miálɛ kadima? Fɔláalɛ fɔba ntɛ mifɔnɛ be awɛ bisisi mi mbom?

english_verse: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
translation_verse: Minyina pefee kĩ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ tɔkpa a na odu lɛláana kanyakɔ̃ nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ. Mitakɛ mi akũ, kitonɛkĩ bati kĩ babɔa adɔpaa, ĩye basum baviɔ̃, ĩye banɛ kɔnyɔ mɔ kɔtsɔ̃ liti, batsole kĩ bawamɔ batsole mɔ banyole kĩ bawamɔ akũ,

english_verse: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
translation_verse: ĩye bati kĩ baye, ĩye bikĩ bideĩ anɛ ngbã, ĩye bikĩ bawuga bavɛ, ĩye bikĩ banyɛ bati buĩ kpa ĩye bablɔfɔ bayekpanɛ ni, be kamɛ odima lɛláana kanyakɔ̃ nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Ade aye mi kamɛ badi mina miyatɛ̃ a, lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ bɛvɔlɛ titikpa nɛ mi akũ. Bɛkɔa mi bido nɛ Baguma awɛ, bɛkɛna mi bati kpalɛwa nɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo dĩ mɔ Baguma Hũhũ akũ. Mikɛna Mi Akũ Yedede, Mikɔa Obu Mikpa Baguma.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: Mi kamɛ badi aalɛ kĩ, “Bɛkpa yĩ osi kĩ nkɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.” Oo! Nuwĩ fɔanɛ. Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa nɛnɛ lɛkpa oti. Maapuli nlɛ kĩ, “Bɛkpa yĩ osi kĩ nkɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.” Lɛmɛ nláafɔ̃ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa lɛnya yĩ akũ.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: Oti bɛbã lɛmɛ aalɛ kĩ, “Funitsã waa fɔkpa lupu ade lupu lɛmɛ waa lɛkpa funitsã a.” Oo! Nuwĩ fɔanɛ. Lɛmɛ Baguma aawɔla foe nviã a. Kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔakɔa boe sukɛna bɔkpa linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ, mbom kĩ bɔakɔa sukɛna busum boe Ɔlate ade boe Ɔlate lɛmɛ adãmɛna se akũ a.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma dzɛla boe Ɔlate etomɛna kanɔkpa a, ade ayea yaakɔa ye kɔbɛ̃ ɛdzɛla boe lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Minyina kĩ mi sukɛna nyɛ Kristo sukɛna a sidi. Fɔnɛnɛ kĩ makɔa Kristo sukɛna sɛkɛ sidi nkɛna otsole ɔdɔpaani sukɛna sukɛna a sidi? Kɛtánɛ kĩ fɔna lɔmɔ koũ!

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ minyína kĩ ntɛ odi kɔa akũ ɛta otsole kĩ yabɔa adɔpaa ni, bɛmɔ ye bɛkɛna sukɛna mɔa? Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Be bati nviã a baakɛna sukɛna mɔa.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ oti kĩ ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛmɛnamɔ boe Ɔlate ni, bɛmɔ ye bɛkɛna ɔmɔa nɛ hũhũ kamɛ.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Mivɛlɛ akũ nɛ linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ tɔkpa ɔwɛ̃! Titikpa bɛbã kĩ oti akɛna látamɔ ye sukɛna, lɛmɛ oti kĩ ɛbɔa adɔpaa titikpa a tamɔ ye mɔawɛ sukɛna.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Minyína kĩ mi sukɛna nyɛ asɔli kɔla kɔkpa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kĩ Baguma kpa mi, kĩ edeĩ nɛ mi kamɛ lɛmɛ a? Foesũ nnɛ́ mi awɛ la mi sukɛna mbom Baguma side sanɛ.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: Baguma se kɔtɔ kpale nɛ mi lɔlɔ, foesũ mikɔa mi sukɛna mido ye obu.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ maalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ mitsɛlɛ mikpa yĩ a akũ. Fɔnɛnɛ ntɛ onyole odi tákɔã ɔtsɔ̃.

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ kĩ linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ bɔ paa sũ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ onyole nyɛ onyole ɛkɔã ɔtsɔ̃, ade kɛnɛ kĩ otsole nyɛ otsole lɛmɛ edzi kɔnyɔ a.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Otsole a sukɛna lɛnyɛ́ ye odi side, mbom ɔnyɔ side sanɛ lɛmɛ. Ade ayea onyole a sukɛna lɛnyɛ́ ye odi side, mbom sɛnyɛ ɔtsɔ̃ lɛmɛ side.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ ɔnyɔ bɛtakɛna be kawa akũ lɔlɔ ɔsĩ, mbɔɛntɛ binu be akũ kayɔ ade bɛwɛ lɛmɛ kĩ bakɔa lɔkɔ lidi bɛkpa mpaɛbɔ. Lɛmɛ ke liti a, kɛnɛ kĩ bamuniki bidzi tɔtsɔ̃ mɔ tɔnyɔ, ayekĩ Abonsam lɛláafɔ̃ biwo nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ ntɛ bɛtápuli bɛnya be akũ akũ.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Nlɛ fukĩĩ ndɛ osi kakpa aye nɛ mi kadzi kamɛ, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ mbla yanɛ makpa mi.

english_verse: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
translation_verse: Kufɛ maawɛ kĩ bati flee bidzi ndɛ ayekĩ ndzi a. Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi deĩ futetsa fudi kĩ ɛtɛ etomɛna Baguma sɛkɛ̃. Odi nyɛ odi fude vɛmɛna nɛ ɔkɔba fude akũ.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Finyaa, mabi bakutse mɔ bakunawa kĩ fɔnɛnɛ fɔkpa be fɔbɛ̃ ntɛ bidzi be odi ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ odi ndzi a.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛláapuli bɛnya be akũ kɔ̃ ni, bɛkɔã batsɔ̃ ĩye bidzi fɔnyɔ, kitonɛkĩ kɔtsɔ̃ mɔ kɔnyɔ kadzi nɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ kĩ sukɛna kawɛ aakaha be.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Ndeĩ mbla nkpa batsɔ̃ mɔ banyɔ. Mbla a lɛnyɛ́ yĩ ade, mbom boe Ɔlate sɛkɛ̃ eto. Ade ye nyɛ kĩ otsole ɛtasĩ ɔnyɔ,

english_verse: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
translation_verse: lɛmɛ ntɛ esĩ kɔnyɔ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ edzi ye odi, ntɛ nnɛ́ aye emuniki ɛnaakpɔla ɔnyɔ a. Ade ayea kɛtáfɛta kĩ onyole lɛmɛ asĩ ɔtsɔ̃ a.

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Mi bati nkaɛ a kɔ̃ ni, yĩ lamɔ be kabi, nnɛ́ boe Ɔlate. Ntɛ okristoni deĩ ɔtsɔ̃ kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔmamuni, kĩ etunɔ kĩ badzimɔ akũ ni, onyole a ɛtasĩ ye.

english_verse: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ otsole okristoni dzimɔ ɔnyɔ kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔmamuni, mɔ etunɔ kĩ bɛmɔ ye badzi ni, otsole a ɛtasĩ ye.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ fɔya kĩ be kamɛ odi kĩ ɛtátɛ edo tátunɔ kĩ yadzimɔ ɔtɔ odonɛ ni, yapuli ɛdzakũ faa. Ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ a ɔtɛ odonɛ a deĩ osi kĩ yabɔɛ ye osi, kitonɛkĩ Baguma wɛ kĩ midzi nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
translation_verse: Mi batɛ badonɛ batsole a mite anɛ kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ mi akũ mi banyɔ aapuli bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Ade ayea mi batɛ badonɛ banyole mite anɛ kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ mi akũ mi batsɔ̃ lɛmɛ aapuli bɛdani be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a. Midzi Ndɛ Ayekĩ Mina Fɔmɔ Baguma Vɛ Mi a.

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ odi nyɛ odi edzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ boe Ɔlate kpa ye, ndɛ ayekĩ ɛna fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ ye a. Ade mbla kĩ matsa nɛ tɛsɔli flee a kamɛ a.

english_verse: If you are already circumcised, don't try to change it. If you are not circumcised, don't get circumcised.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti tsua lɛkpati fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ ye ni, fɔ̃ ɛna lɔmɔ. Ntɛ ɛtátsua lɛkpati fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ ye ni, ɛtalɛ kĩ yanaatsua lɛkpati.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ otsua lɛkpati ĩye ɔtátsua ni, fɔtsɛ́la. Lɛtsa a kĩ lisĩ nyɛ kĩ akɛna nɛ Baguma mbla a akũ.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Foesũ odi nyɛ odi ɛna ndɛ ayekĩ ɛla fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ ye a.

english_verse: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
translation_verse: Ɔsate anɛ fɔmɔ Baguma vɛ nɔ? Fɔtsɛ́la. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔna osi kĩ aanyɛ nɔ akũ nɛ tɛsate kamɛ ni tsã ye akũ,

english_verse: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ ɔsate a kĩ boe Ɔlate vɛ a nɛ́ nɛ tɛtsate kamɛ. Ade ayea oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ ɔsate fɔmɔ bɛvɛ ye ni, ye malo ɛdani ɔsate ɛkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Baguma se kɔtɔ kpale nɛ mi lɔlɔ, foesũ mibuki minyɛ́ basate mikpa batidziwa.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ mitsɛlɛ nɛ bakutse akũ a ni, ntátɛ mbla ntomɛna boe Ɔlate sɛkɛ̃. Lɛmɛ ade yĩ kakɔna a, kitonɛkĩ nɛ ayekĩ boe Ɔlate mɔ yĩ kɔnya sũ miapuli mitɛ yĩ mido.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Nɛ yĩ kamɔ onukpɛ̃ mɔ ayekĩ nviã katinya lamɛna ɔsĩ kana sũ ni, fɔnɛnɛ kĩ oti edzi ndɛ ayekĩ ɛla a.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɔkɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni talɛ kĩ asĩ ye. Ntɛ ɔtákɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni, talɛ kĩ awɛ odi ɔkɔã.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔkɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni ɔtákɛna titikpa, ade ayea ntɛ odi dzi kɔnyɔ ni, ye malo ɛtákɛna titikpa a. Lɛmɛ ntáwɛ kĩ awo nɛ ɔhau a kĩ ɔtsɔ̃ mɔ ɔnyɔ amɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, lɛtsa a kĩ matsa nyɛ kĩ lɔkɔ pii buki lɛwáa nɛ boe lɔtɔ, foesũ kito finyaa kanaa ni, banyole kĩ bɛkɔã batsɔ̃ bidzi ndɛ bɛtákɔã batsɔ̃.

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: Bikĩ babe ni, bidzi ndɛ bati kĩ be akũ lákɔ kɔnya, bikĩ bamɛmɛ ni, bɛkɛna ndɛ kawɔlɛ láde be. Bikĩ bɛya fɔtsa ni bɛkɛna ndɛ nnɛ́ be la fɔtsa a kĩ bɛya a.

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bitomɛna katinya kamɛ fɔtsa liti kĩ bawɛ kĩ foe flee fɔnya be fude ni, bidzi ndɛ bɛtsɛ́la katinya kamɛ fɔtsa akũ, kitonɛkĩ katinya kĩĩ lɛláana lɔmɔ flee ndɛ ayekĩ kɛla finyaa.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: Maáwɛ kĩ ana nɛ ɔhau odima kamɛ. Kitonɛkĩ oti kĩ ɛtákɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni, ɛkɔa ye akũ flee ɛkpa Baguma adzuma kakɛna, ade yabɔa mbɔdi kĩ yakɛna adzuma a edo Baguma kawɔlɛ a.

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ onyole kĩ ɛkɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni, yakɔna katinya kamɛ fɔtsa akũ, kitonɛkĩ yawɛ kĩ yado ɔtsɔ̃ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ ye adzuni sɛ̃ tisi nviã. Otsole kĩ ɛtádzi kɔnyɔ ĩye oyitsɔ̃ kĩ enyína onyole ni, ɛkɔa Baguma adzuma ete nɛ ye akũ, kitonɛkĩ ɛwɛ kĩ yakɔa ye sukɛna mɔ hũhũ flee edzi nkpã yedede. Otsole kĩ edzi kɔnyɔ ni, ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛkpa katinya kamɛ fɔtsa kitonɛkĩ yawɛ kĩ yado ɔnyɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: Malɛ fukĩĩ bamɛ flee kitonɛkĩ mawɛ kĩ makpa mi kabumɛna. Ntáwɛ kĩ miana nɛ́ kasuli kamɛ, mbom mawɛ kĩ mikɛna lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnɛnɛ mɔ likĩ lɛfɛta, lɔkɔa mikɔa mi akũ mikpa Baguma ɔsum, kĩ kavia katse lɛláa foe akũ.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Ntɛ onyole tinya oyitsɔ̃ mɔ ɛmɔ́ kĩ yaápuli epi ye akũ, ĩye ɛmɔ kĩ lɔkɔ lamɛna katɛ̃ ni, baapuli bɛkɔã akũ. Fɔnyɛ́ titikpa ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: But it is better to have self-control and to make up your mind not to marry.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ onyole tinya oyitsɔ̃, mɔ ɛkɔna nɛ ye kamɛ kĩ yaapuli epi ye akũ, kĩ baadzimɔ akũ kokooko ni, ɛkɛna foe kakɛna wĩ.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Foesũ onyole kĩ ɛkɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni, ɛkɛna kakɛna wĩ, lɛmɛ onyole kĩ ɛtákɔã ɔtsɔ̃ ni, ye fude nɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃ kɔlaa.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Otsole kĩ edzi kɔnyɔ ni ebuki ɛlá osi kĩ yadzi kɔnyɔ bɛbã. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔnyɔ a ku ni, edeĩ osi kĩ yadzimɔ onyole a kĩ yawɛ. Kɛnɛ kĩ onyole a ɛnya ɔtɛ odonɛ.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ edzi ndɛ okunawa aye ni, yaamɔ kawɔlɛ ɛba. Ade yĩ kakɔna a, ade matɛ kado kĩ ade aye nyɛ Baguma Hũhũ a kakɔna kĩ ɛkpa yĩ a.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Finyaa, mawɛ kĩ mabi mi litsedi nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ mitsɛlɛ nɛ funitsã a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ akũ. Boe flee bɔalɛ kĩ bunyina kadi. Lɛmɛ bunyina kĩ kadikanyi kĩĩ afɔ̃ oti adɛla ye akũ nɛ ode. Lɛtsa a kĩ lakpa boe kɔbɛ̃ nyɛ ɔdɔ.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi kɔna kĩ enyina fɔtsa nɛ litsedi akũ paa ni, ɛtámɔ foe kamɔ wĩ ndɛ ayekĩ kɛnɛ kĩ fɔna a.

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi awɛ Baguma ni, Baguma lɛmɛ nyina ye.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Foesũ ade lɛtsa a kĩ mabi mi nɛ funitsã kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ akũ a. Bunyina kĩ baviɔ̃ nyɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa awɛ bɛmɛ, kĩ fɔlá nkpã, kitonɛkĩ Baguma odi pɛ deĩ nkpã.

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bati alɛ kĩ baviɔ̃ deĩ nɛ ode mɔ tite akũ, kĩ baviɔ̃ kumɛkumɛ kĩĩ mɔ be balate deĩ malo ni,

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: boe kɔ̃ bunyina kĩ Baguma mɔapɛ deĩ. Ade ye nyɛ boe Ɔlɛga kĩ ɛbɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa, ade budzi bɔkpa ye a. Boe Ɔlate kĩ ɛnyɛ Yesu Kristo mɔapɛ lɛmɛ deĩ. Ade ye akũ Baguma tsã ɛbɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa, kĩ fɔtsɛ̃ ye akũ budzi nkpã.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ odi nyɛ odi nyina ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ. Baviɔ̃ akũ fɔlɛtsa nya bati badi kamɛkamɛ limii kĩ kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ ntɛ bɛnya funitsã kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ pɛ bakɔna kĩ baviɔ̃ funitsã fɔanɛ. Nɛ kĩ bɛtábo anɛ nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ sũ ni, bakɔna kĩ funitsã a do be efĩĩ.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: Funitsã lɛláapuli fɔlɔla bɔmɔ Baguma kayite. Bɔláayɛla lɛtsama ntɛ bɔtánya ade ayea bɔláana nfasɔ adima ntɛ bɔnya lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ midã kadã wĩ lɔkɔa mitafɔ̃ mi lɛkɛnana nɛ akũ akũ kadzi kamɛ lɛfɔ̃ bikĩ bɛtábo anɛ nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ liwo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Bɔɛ foe kĩ oti kĩ ɛtábo anɛ nɛ ye katɛkado kamɛ mɔ́ kĩ nɔ a kĩ onyina kadi a odzi anya nɛ kuviɔ̃la. Nnɛ́ kɔbɛ̃ ado ye kĩ ɛnya funitsã kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃?

english_verse: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
translation_verse: Miafɔ̃ mi bawaɛ̃ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bɛtábo anɛ nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ a, akɛna titikpa nɛ Kristo akũ, kitonɛkĩ miado be kɔbɛ̃ bakɛna lɛtsa kĩ bɛtɛ bido kĩ fɔnɛ́nɛ.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ funitsã fudi kanya aafɔ̃ nwaɛ̃ lɛkɛna titikpa ni, nláanya funitsã a na da, ayekĩ ɛláawo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Miakɔna kĩ ĩnyɛ́ oti kĩ edzí ye akũ akũ mbɔɛɛ? Miakɔna kĩ ĩnyɛ́ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ mbɔɛɛ? Miakɔna kĩ ntákɔa anɛ mmɔ́ boe Ɔlate Yesu mbɔɛɛ? Nnɛ́ mi nyɛ adzuma a kĩ nkɛna nkpa boe Ɔlate a foe akũ nfasɔ?

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bati bɛbã támɔ yĩ biyi ɔsũ ndɛ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ aye ni, mi kɔ̃ mitɛ yĩ. Kitonɛkĩ ayekĩ miawɛ midzi nkpã nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ a atsa kĩ ĩnyɛ ye fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ.

english_verse: When people question me, I tell them
translation_verse: Ntɛ bati atɛ yĩ atsindze ni, ade limii manyɛ yĩ akũ onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Bɔlá osi kĩ bakpa boe funitsã mɔ mui nɛ adzuma a kĩ bɔakɛna a sũ mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Bɔlá osi kĩ bakpã boe batsɔ̃ batɛ badonɛ bɔnamɛna osi akũ ndɛ ayekĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛbã, Petro mɔ boe Ɔlate bawaɛ̃ a akɛna mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔmɔ Barnaba odi pɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkɛna adzuma bɔkɔa bɔdãmɛna boe akũ?

english_verse: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
translation_verse: Sodzani ɔdabe adãmɛna ye mɔawɛ akũ ntɛ edeĩ nɛ kawũkanɔ adzumamu? Okuani ɔdabe anɛ kɔba kĩ yaánya funitsã a kĩ fɔnɛ a? Ĩye owei adɔɔla ɔnantsue kĩ yaámɔa ye tɛmɛ mui badi?

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Mose mbla ovoli a kamɛ kĩ, “Mitasui ɔyaɛ kĩ minɛɛ miakɛna adzuma nɛ kɔba a onukpɛ̃.” Baguma tsɛla ɔyaɛ odi akũ mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ boe sũ ɛlɛ kalɛ a na mbɔɛɛ? Ɔnɔkɔali ade boe kalɛ sũ bɛtsɛlɛ foe a. Kɛnɛ kĩ ekĩ yadu mɔ ekĩ yakpɔta a flee bakɛna adzuma a mɔ kɔnɛte kĩ baana be tsɛfã nɛ funitsã a kĩ fɔ́anɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Budu hũhũ kamɛ fɔtsa nɛ mi kamɛ. Ntɛ mi malo mikpa boe katinya kamɛ fɔtsa a ni, miyɛla osi?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bati bɛbã deĩ osi kĩ bana nfasɔ adi bitomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃ ni, kekɔ̃ kɛnɛ kĩ boe bɔana foe butomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃, fublusu malo. Lɛmɛ bɔtákɔa boe osi kawa a na bɔkɛna adzuma. Mbom bɔkɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa bute nɛ boe mɔawɛ akũ ayekĩ lɛtsama lɛláanya mbɛtahu fɔkpa boe nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a kalɛ nɛ Kristo akũ.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: Minyina yededeede kĩ be a kĩ bakɛna adzuma nɛ Baguma Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ ana be funitsã katomɛna Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, ade bikĩ babɔa afɔliɛ nɛ afɔliɛ de akũ a lɛmɛ ana afɔliɛ fɔtsa a foe tsɛfã a.

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Ade aye boe Ɔlate lɛmɛ lɛ eyi kĩ be a kĩ bade lɛtsã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa akũ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ bɛna be kanya bitomɛna lɛtsã kade a kamɛ.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ yĩ kɔ̃ ntákɛna osi kĩĩ akũ adzuma, ade nlámɛna mi ovoli kĩĩ katsɛlɛ kĩ mikɛna nɛ foe akũ mikpa yĩ a. Ntɛ foe lɔmɔ sũ ni, maaku mbom! Kitonɛkĩ ntáwɛ kĩ odi ɛkɔa lɛtsa kĩ mapia nɛ de akũ a ɛkɛna fɔtsa pupulidza.

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Nlá osi kĩ mapia nɛ kĩ made lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ sũ. Fɔnyɛ ɔhɛ fɔkpa yĩ kĩ ndɛ lɛtsã a, ade Baguma aavɛlɛ yĩ sɔtɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ntɛ ntálɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: Ntɛ yĩ mɔawɛ kawɛ onukpɛ̃ makɛna adzuma kĩĩ ni, maadã kĩ bise yĩ foe kɔtɔ. Lɛmɛ makɛna foe ndɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bihihiɛ bɛkpa yĩ kĩ nkɛna, kitonɛkĩ fɔnyɛ adzuma kĩ Baguma hihiɛ ɛkpa yĩ.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Foesũ litsa kɔtɔkase maana? Yĩ kɔtɔkase nyɛ kĩ made lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ faa, ade maáte anɛ kĩ maatsã osi odi akũ nna foe akũ kɔtɔkase kudi a.

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Ndzi yĩ akũ akũ ade ĩnyɛ́ ɔsate nkpa odima a. Lɛmɛ nkɔa yĩ akũ nkɛna oti kĩ yasum odi nyɛ odi ayekĩ maapuli ĩnyɛ bati pii nkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ nkɛna adzuma nɛ Yudafɔ kamɛ a, ndzi nkpã ndɛ Yudani ayekĩ maavɛlɛ be mma boe Ɔlate. Titɔ kĩ nláa Mose mbla kayɔ malo ni, ndzi nkpã ndɛ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ mbla kayɔ, ayekĩ maanyɛ bikĩ bideĩ nɛ mbla a kayɔ nkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Ade ayea kĩ makɛna adzuma nɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ kamɛ a, ndzi nkpã ndɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ Yudani. Ntákɛna nɛ Yudafɔ mbla akũ ayekĩ maana bati nkpa boe Ɔlate. Lɛmɛ fukĩĩ látsa kĩ mpu Baguma mbla a. Ɔnɔkɔali a nyɛ kĩ ndeĩ nɛ Kristo mbla a kayɔ.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ndeĩ nɛ bikĩ be katɛkado tabo anɛ kamɛ ni, makɛna yĩ akũ ndɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔa aye, ayekĩ maavɛlɛ be nkpa boe Ɔlate. Matsã osi nyɛ osi akũ kakɛna ɔmɔa mɔ odi nyɛ odi ayekĩ maavɛlɛ be kamɛ badi nkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Makɛna fukĩĩ bamɛ flee nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a sũ, ayekĩ yĩ malo maana foe akũ nhila.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: Minyina yededeede kĩ bati pii adã akũ nɛ atsɛ kalo kamɛ, lɛmɛ be kamɛ ɔmɔapɛ anya akũ katɛ foe akũ lɔbɔɛ. Foesũ miwula atsɛ ayekĩ míapuli minya akũ mitɛ foe akũ kɔtɔkase a.

english_verse: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛmanɛ akũ ɛkpa atsɛ kalo ni, yakɔa ye akũ kado nɛ mbla mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kayɔ ayekĩ yaana lɛgãbɔɛ a kĩ de kɔ̃ laawɔ. Lɛmɛ boe kɔ̃ bɔamanɛ akũ ayekĩ bɔ́ana lɛgãbɔɛ a kĩ lɛláawɔ da.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Foesũ mawula atsɛ kanaa kpĩĩ kĩ manaatsua ɔwɛ̃ a. Ade foesũ nla ndɛ fukpeni ɔbɔanɛ kĩ yaáta fukpeni nɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ aye aye a.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Mafɔ̃ fukpeni awo yĩ akũ ayekĩ yĩ sukɛna aapuli sɛdza sɛkpa lubo kamɔ. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ made lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ kakpa bati bɛbã ni, ntáwɛ kĩ bɛyaanyɛ yĩ nɛ boe a kĩ bɔadã akũ nɛ atse kalo kɔdza a kamɛ.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mawɛ kĩ mikai lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya nɛ boe bawanyɔ a kĩ bitómɔ Mose a akũ. Be flee bɛna nɛ luflututu a kɔbɛ̃ kayɔ ade bɛtsã Mpo Ngbã a kamɛ bɛnɛ yededeede a.

english_verse: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Baguma kɔa luflututu mɔ mpo mui ɛkɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ ndɛ bati kĩ bitomɔ Mose aye.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Be flee bɛnya hũhũ kamɛ funitsã mɔapɛ a,

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: ade bɛmɔa hũhũ kamɛ bavɛ mɔapɛ a lɛmɛ a. Bɛmɔa mui bitomɛna awãwã hũhũ de a kĩ litomɔ be a kamɛ. Ade de kĩĩ nyɛ Kristo awɛ a.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ ni, Baguma támɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ be pii akũ. Nɛ foesũ biku nɛ kɔsa kamɛ.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ni, fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ flee nyɛ nfantuhũ fɔkpa boe ayekĩ fɔ́abɔa boe kɔkɔ, kĩ bɔláatomɔ fɔtsa kpa liti ndɛ ayekĩ bɛkɛna,

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: ĩye bɔakɔa boe akũ bɔkpa baviɔ̃ kasum ndɛ ayekĩ be kamɛ badi kɛna a. Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Bati a dzi banya luwi, lɛmɛ be kanya mɔ kamɔa dani fɔkɛna bavɛ kawuga, mɔ litsolewɛ mɔ linyolewɛ kakɛna.”

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: Babɔa lɛgbã kĩ, “Bɛkpa boe osi kĩ bɔkɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.” Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa nɛnɛ lɛkpa boe. “Bɛkpa boe osi kĩ bɔkɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa.” Lɛmɛ nnɛ́ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa deĩ nfasɔ lɛkpa boe.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ bɔasɔ boe Ɔlate bɔdã ndɛ ayekĩ be kamɛ badi kɛna a, nɛ foesũ bɔɛ dɔ̃ be biku.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Mitafɔ̃ buyidza bɔdza nɛ Baguma akũ ndɛ ayekĩ be kamɛ badi kɛna, nɛ foesũ Wuda Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ yawɔla be.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ bamɛ flee ya nɛ be akũ ayekĩ bati bɛbã aakɔlɛ fɔtsa bitomɛna foe akũ. Bɛtsɛlɛ foe biyi ayekĩ bɔ́akɔa bɔkpã boe akũ, kitonɛkĩ budeĩ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ katinya kamɛ kawũna fuã kiyua a kamɛ.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɔna kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ pintii ni, ɛdã kadã wĩ ayekĩ ɛláatɔ.

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Kasɔ kadã kadi nyɛ kadi kĩ kɛya nɛ nɔ akũ ni, ade foe odu aya nɛ bati bɛbã akũ a. Lɛmɛ Baguma akɛna nɛ ye kalɛ akũ, ade ke ɛláafɔ̃ kasɔ a na lɛba nɔ kɔbɛ̃ a. Nɛ kasɔ a kamɛ ni, yaakpa mi kɔbɛ̃ ayekĩ míapuli midza nɛ foe kamɛ, mipuli minya foe akũ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Foesũ, nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mivɛlɛ mi akũ nɛ baviɔ̃ kasum ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Mabi mi fɔlɛtsa ndɛ bati kĩ binyina kadi, foesũ mikɔna fɔlɛtsa a kĩ nlɛ a akũ midã.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Kɔɔpu a kĩ bɔalɛ kanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ a nyɛ boe Ɔlate babɔanɛ. Ntɛ buhila kayei a na, bɔmɔa bavɛ a ni, bɔmɔ Kristo bɔkɛna ɔmɔa. Ntɛ bɔanya boe Ɔlate funitsã kpalɛwa ni, fɔatsa kĩ bɔmɔ Kristo bɔkɛna ɔmɔa.

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ bɔpɔnɛ malo ni bɔkɛna oti mɔa, kito nɛ abodoo mɔapɛ a kĩ boe flee bɔsɛ̃ bɔanya a sũ.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Mikɔna Israelfɔ midã. Be a kĩ bɛnya funitsã a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa Baguma a, bɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Baguma.

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Foesũ katsa matsa kĩ baviɔ̃ ĩye fɔtsa a kĩ bakɔa kabɔa afɔliɛ kakpa be a nyɛ fɔtsa sĩwa fudi mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Oowo! Lɛtsa a kĩ matsa a nyɛ kĩ fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛbɔa afɔliɛ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ ni, bɛkɔa foe bɛkpa hũhũ kpa, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ Baguma bɛbɔa bɛkpa. Ntáwɛ kĩ miakɛna ɔmɔa mɔ hũhũ kpa.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ miamɔa mui nɛ boe Ɔlate kɔɔpu kamɛ lɔkɔa mibuki minaamɔa nɛ hũhũ kpa kɔɔpu lɛmɛ kamɛ, ĩye minya nɛ boe Ɔlate opunu akũ lɔkɔa minaanya nɛ hũhũ kpa opunu akũ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Miawɛ kĩ boe Ɔlate ɛna kalɔkpa, ɛnɔ lineke lɛmɛ mbɔɛɛ? Bɔakɔna kĩ budeĩ kɔbɛ̃ bɔbɛ̃ ye mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ oti akɔna ye odi kalɛ, mbom kɛnɛ kĩ yakɔna bati bɛbã kalɛ akũ.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Mideĩ osi kĩ mianya sɛkɛna sidi nyɛ sidi kĩ banɔ nɛ kɔyaatɔ. Mitafɔ̃ fɔha mi tɔwɔlɛ ĩye mivia ntɛ se akũ kpalɛ ĩye sɛtákpalɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Baguma la katinya mɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lideĩ nɛ ke kamɛ a.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ́ ɔtɛ odonɛ vɛ nɔ kĩ yaanya mɔ́ otunɔ ɔnaa ni, nya funitsã a kĩ ete opunu ɛkpa nɔ a, lɛmɛ tavia ye otse ntɛ foe akũ kpalɛ ĩye fɔtákpalɛ.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ nɔ a kĩ ɔnya a ɔwɔlɛ fɔ́aha, mbom fɔ́aha oti a kĩ ebi nɔ a. Odi aavia kĩ, “Nde sũ ntɛ ndeĩ osi kĩ makɛna litsedi mɔ fɔaha odi ɔwɔlɛ?

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Ntɛ ĩnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ funitsã a kĩ ɛkpa yĩ a akũ ni, nde sũ odi alɛ kɔdzɛla etia yĩ?”

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mite anɛ kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ miakɛna, ntɛ fɔnyɛ kanya ĩye kamɔa malo ni mikɛna foe mikɔa mimu Baguma.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Midzi nkpã ayekĩ miláavɛlɛ ɔhau adima mikpa Yudafɔ ĩye bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ mɔ be a kĩ bɛnyɛ Baguma asɔli bati a.

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: Mikɛna ndɛ ayekĩ nlamɛna kakɛna. Mbɔa mbɔdi kĩ makɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa fuwo odi nyɛ odi anɛ. Maákɔna yĩ akũ mbom makɔna bati flee be tuwĩ akũ ayekĩ baana nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Mikɔlɛ yĩ ɔbla mikɛna ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ malo makɔlɛ Kristo ɔbla kakɛna a. Kristo Nyɛ Lɔlɔ Ɛkpa Odi Nyɛ Odi.

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Mamu mi nɛ ayekĩ mite anɛ nɛ yĩ akũ luwi daa mɔ ayekĩ mitomɔ fɔtsa flee a kĩ ntsa mi a.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mawɛ kĩ minyi kĩ Kristo nyɛ lɔlɔ ɛkpa odi nyɛ odi. Onyole nyɛ ɔtsɔ̃ lɔlɔ ade Baguma lɛmɛ nyɛ Kristo lɔlɔ a.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ onyole kĩ yabɔa mpaɛ ĩye yalɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa nɛ asɔli kɔla kamɛ sua ye lɔlɔ ni, edo Kristo kɔnyɔã.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ otsole kĩ yabɔa mpaɛ ĩye yalɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa nɛ asɔli kɔla kamɛ, tásui duku nɛ lɔlɔ ni, edo ɔnyɔ kɔnyɔã. Ntsitsemu lɛláa bɛmɔ otsole kĩ ɛkpa lɔlɔ tsɔlitsɔli kayite.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Ntɛ otsole tásui duku nɛ lɔlɔ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ekpeli tɔlɔ. Lɛmɛ nɛ kĩ fɔnyɛ kɔnyɔã ntɛ otsole kpeli tɔlɔ ĩye ɛkpa lɔlɔ sũ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ esui duku nɛ lɔlɔ.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Fɔtásĩ kĩ onyole asua lɔlɔ kitonɛkĩ ɛla ndɛ Baguma kutitse mɔ ye kukũkpalɛ. Lɛmɛ otsole kɔ̃ nyɛ onyole kukũkpalɛ.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma tábɔa onyole etomɛna otsole kamɛ, mbom ɛbɔa otsole etomɛna onyole kamɛ.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Bɛtábɔa onyole bɛkpa otsole nɛ ye otsole a kalɛ sũ, lɛmɛ bɛkɛna otsole bɛkpa nɛ onyole a kalɛ sũ.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ sũ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ otsole esui duku nɛ lɔlɔ ɛkɔa ɛtsa kĩ edeĩ nɛ ɔnyɔ kɔbɛ̃ kayɔ.

english_verse: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ fɔla lɔmɔ malo nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ boe Ɔlate sũ ni, otsole lídzi ye akũ akũ, ade onyole lɛmɛ lídzi ye akũ akũ a.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ndɛ ayekĩ bɛbɔa otsole bitomɛna onyole kamɛ a, ade aye a bɛma onyole bitomɛna otsole kamɛ a. Lɛmɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa to Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Mi mɔawɛ mikɔna midã ntɛ fɔnɛnɛ kĩ otsole abɔa mpaɛ ɛkpa Baguma nɛ kanade, kĩ ɛtásui duku nɛ lɔlɔ.

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ tɔlɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ nyɛ akũ obu fɔkpa otsole. Bɛkpa ye tɔlɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ kĩ ɛkɔa ɛkɛɛlɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi awɛ kĩ yatɛ atsindze nɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ akũ ni, lɛtsa a kĩ maalɛ nɛ foe akũ nyɛ kĩ boe ĩye Baguma tɛsɔli a buki tɛlá amaniɛ bɛbã adima. Osi a Akũ Kĩ Kɛnɛ Kĩ Bɔnya Boe Ɔlate Funitsã.

english_verse: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɔdɛ a kĩ makpa mi finyaa kamɛ ni, maámu mi kitonɛkĩ ntɛ mikpɔta kĩ miasum Baguma ni, miakɛna mi akũ tɔkpa kaba tuwĩ.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Kalɛ katɔnyade ni, nnu kĩ ntɛ mikpɔta ndɛ asɔli bati aye ni, miasɛ̃ mi akũ. Ntɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ fudi ndo.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Fusĩ kĩ kasɛ̃ kɛna nɛ mi kamɛ ayekĩ bɔ́amɔ yededeede bikĩ badi ɔnɔkɔali nɛ mi kamɛ.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ mianya ni, odi nyɛ odi abɔɛ ye funitsã kanya. Fɔfɔ̃ badi lána kanya, ade badi lɛmɛ awuga bavɛ.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Fɔatsa kĩ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ mianya abodoo, miamɔa bavɛ nɛ kɔɔpu kĩĩ kamɛ lɛmɛ ni, milamɛna lɛtsã kade nɛ boe Ɔlate wuda akũ, kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ yaabuki ɛya.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ odi táyi boe Ɔlate babɔanɛ mɔ sukɛna ɔsũ kĩ fɔnyɛ fɔtsa kpalɛwa fɔmɔ ɛnya ĩye ɛmɔa foe nɛ osi kpa akũ ni, ɛvɛlɛ kadzɛ eko nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Ade ayea kĩ bɛnya biyua a, ɛbɔɛ kɔɔpu ade ɛlɛ kĩ, “Kɔɔpu kĩĩ nyɛ nhihiɛ vɔɛ̃ kĩ bɛkɔa yĩ babɔanɛ bisui. Lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ miamɔa bavɛ kĩĩ ni, mikɛna foe mikai yĩ.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa a kĩ ntɛ ntomɛna boe Ɔlate sɛkɛ̃ a, ade foe ntsa mi a. Lɛnyɛ a kĩ ke mɔ bɛnyɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu bɛkpa a, ɛbɔɛ abodoo,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: ade ɛnyɛ kɔlɛ, ɛyɛlɛ ye ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ sukɛna kĩ nkɔa mabɔa afɔliɛ nkpa mi a. Mikɛna fukĩĩ mikɔa mikai yĩ.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ odi nya boe Ɔlate abodoo, ɛmɔa kɔɔpu kĩĩ nɛ osi kpa akũ ni, baakɔ ye kɔtɔ kĩ efum nɛ boe Ɔlate sukɛna mɔ ye babɔanɛ akũ.

english_verse: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ odi nyɛ odi ɛkɔna ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ fɔmɔ yaanya funitsã, ɛmɔa bavɛ nɛ kɔɔpu a lɛmɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ mi pii miasɔ ade milá akũ ɔsĩ a. Mi kamɛ bati pii lɛmɛ ku.

english_verse: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɔnya lɔtɔ bɔnaa boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ ni, Baguma lɛláadzɛmɔ boe.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ boe Ɔlate adzɛmɔ boe, ɛvɛlɛ boe sɔtɔ lɛmɛ ni, yalɔla boe ayekĩ Baguma lɛláakɔ bɔmɔ bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido a flee kɔtɔ ɛkpɔta.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Foesũ nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ntɛ miyakpɔta kĩ mianya ni, midzɔlɛ mi bawaɛ̃.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kɔlɛ akɔ odi ni, ɛnya nɛ kɔla ayekĩ ntɛ miyatsɛna nɛ Baguma kɔla kamɛ ni owula lɛláana kadzɛ ɛkpa ye akũ. Ntɛ ĩya ni, maadɛ mi nɛ fɔlɛtsa bɛbã a kĩ fute a akũ.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Minyina kĩ fɔmɔ midani mikɛna batɛ badonɛ a, mitsã tisi pii akũ ade mikɔa mi akũ mikpa baviɔ̃ kĩ báalɛ kɔdzɛla kasum a.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Foesũ mafɔ̃ minyi kĩ ntɛ odi kɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kɔbɛ̃ yalɛ kɔdzɛla ni, ɛláalɛ kĩ, “Yesu, kɔa nɛ ke a!” ade odima lɛmɛ lɛláapuli ɛlɛ kĩ, “Yesu nyɛ boe Ɔlate,” ntɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kɔbɛ̃ lɛláa ye kamɛ.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Futetsa futefute deĩ lɛmɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ a lɛkpa foe flee.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Tisi pii deĩ kĩ te akũ bɔatsã kakɛna Baguma adzuma, lɛmɛ ade boe Ɔlate mɔapɛ a bɔasum a.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Ade ayea tisi pii deĩ kĩ Baguma atsã kakpa bati kɔbɛ̃ bakɛna fɔtsa, lɛmɛ Baguma mɔapɛ akpa kɔbɛ̃ a flee.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Baguma kɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa ɛtsã bati akũ bakɛna tuwĩ fɔnɛ libi fɔkpa bati flee.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a apuli kakpa oti mɔa kɔbɛ̃ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ nyansã, ade Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ a akpa oti bɛbã lɛmɛ kɔbɛ̃ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ kadikanyi a.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ a akpa oti mɔa katɛkado baũ. Ye mɔapɛ a yakpa oti bɛbã fɔsɔ katsa kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a akpa oti bɛbã kɔbɛ̃ kĩ ɛkɛna awãwã fɔtsa, odi bɛbã lɛmɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃. Yakpa oti mɔa kɔbɛ̃ kĩ epuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ, ade yakpa odi bɛbã lɛmɛ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ yapuli ɛnyɛ tide a kayɔ, oti bɛbã kɔbɛ̃ kĩ epuli enyi ntɛ futetsa kĩĩ to Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a sɛkɛ̃ ĩye fɔtáto ye sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ kĩĩ a akɛna fɔtsa a flee, ade yakpa odi nyɛ odi futetsa ndɛ ayekĩ yawɛ a. Oti Deĩ Akũ Fɔtsa Kĩ Fɔakɛna Fɔtsa Futefute.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Boe badi bɔnyɛ Yudafɔ ade badi lɛmɛ nyɛ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a. Badi nyɛ basate ade badi lɛmɛ dzi be akũ akũ a. Lɛmɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a wɔla boe flee mui nɛ Kristo kamɛ, bɔkɛna ɔmɔa nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ a kamɛ. Boe flee bɔtɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔapɛ a mui bɔmɔa.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ otidziwa kutitse a lɛnyɛ́ fɔtsa fɔmɔapɛ, mbom kudeĩ fɔkɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Ntɛ kɔbɔa lɛ kĩ, “Nnɛ́ awɛ manɛ sũ ĩnyɛ́ kutitse a sɛkɛ sidima” a, foe kalɛ lɔmɔ lɛláafɔ̃ kĩ kɔbɔa buki kɔnyɛ́ kutitse a sɛkɛ sidi.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Ntɛ sɔtɔ lɛmɛ lɛ kĩ, “Nnɛ́ linebi manɛ sũ ĩnyɛ kutitse a sɛkɛ sidima,” foe kalɛ lɔmɔ lɛláafɔ̃ kĩ sɔtɔ buki sɛnyɛ́ kuti a sɛkɛ sidi.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kutitse a flee nyɛ linebi odi ni, kɔláapuli kunu fɔkɔ. Ntɛ kutitse a flee nyɛ sɔtɔ odi ni, kɔláapuli kɔtɛ fɔtsa nfiã.

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma bɔa boe kutitse mɔ foe akũ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ, ade edo fudi nyɛ fudi nɛ awã kĩ yawɛ a.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Nde otidziwa kuti a aana ndɛ akũ fɔtsa mɔapɛ fɔanɛ?

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Oti akũ fɔkɔ futefute deĩ lɛmɛ kutitse mɔapɛ kɔanɛ.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Foesũ linebi lɛláapuli libi awɛ kĩ, “Ntásĩ nɔ!” Lɔlɔ lɛmɛ lɛláapuli libi sɛnɛta kĩ, “Ntásĩ nɔ!”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Oti akũ fɔkɔ a kĩ fɔadã ndɛ fɔlá akũ ɔsĩ a, ade foe mbom akũ lisĩ boe a.

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: Ade ke fɔkɔ a kĩ bɔakɔna kĩ fɔtátɛ kɔya a, ade foe bɔakpa obu kaba a. Fukĩ fɔálɛ kadã a, ade foe bɔakɔla kaba a.

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: Boe akũ fɔkɔ a kĩ fɔalɛ kadã a tásĩ akũ kadãmɛna. Baguma awɛ boomu akũ fɔkɔ a flee ade edo obu nɛ fukĩ obu lɛláa foe akũ,

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: ayekĩ boe kutitse a lɛláasɛ̃ koe akũ. Baguma wɛ kĩ fɔkɔ futefute a fɔdãmɛna fɔkɔba akũ pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti kakɔ kadi asɔ ni, fɔkɔ nkaɛ a lɛmɛ amɔ lubo kabumɔ foe. Ntɛ bido kakɔ kadi anunyam ni, fɔkɔ nkaɛ a lɛmɛ amɔ kawɔlɛ kabumɔ foe.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ mi a flee mikpɔta minyɛ Kristo kutitse a, ade mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi nyɛ kutitse a sɛkɛ sĩwa sidi a.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Baguma kɔa badi ɛdzɛla nɛ ye batɛ badonɛ onukpɛ̃. Bɛkɔa kadzakɔ̃ katɔnyade bɛkpa fɔtɔ banyanɛ, kayade bɛkpa bikĩ bɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ, ade kalalɛde a naa fɔtsa batsanɛ sɛkɛ̃ a. Ke liti Baguma kɔa kadzakɔ̃ bɛbã ɛkpa bikĩ bakɛna awãwã fɔtsa, bikĩ bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ batsa fɔsɔ, bikĩ babumɛna bati, bikĩ banya bati lɔtɔ nɛ tudzuma kakɛna kamɛ, mɔ bikĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ bati flee nyɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ ĩye kĩ bɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ ĩye fɔtsa batsanɛ. Ade nnɛ́ bati flee deĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bakɛna awãwã fɔtsa a.

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ bati flee deĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ batsa fɔsɔ, ĩye balɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ ĩye banyɛ tide a kayɔ.

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe flee kamɛ a, mitomɔ futetsa a kĩ fɔtɛ kɔya fɔba flee a liti. Lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ maatsa mi fukĩ fɔbɛ̃ a flee.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mapuli kalɛ batidziwa mɔ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ flee tide, mɔ bati kawɛ lɛláa yĩ kamɛ ni, kekɔ̃ yĩ kɔdzɛla kalɛ la ndɛ lɔwɔda ĩye ɔdɔũ kĩ yanɛ kɔɔba kasĩna atɔ.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ndeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ manyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃, ĩye ĩnyina fɔtsa kɔlawa mɔ kadikanyi flee, ĩye ntɛ ndeĩ katɛkado kĩ maapuli ndzɛla ɔtɔ nɛ ye kadzakɔ̃, mɔ́ bati kawɛ lɛláa yĩ kamɛ ni, kekɔ̃ ĩnyɛ́ lɛtsama.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Ntɛ nkɔa yĩ akũ fɔtsa flee nte ahiãfɔ, ĩye ntɛ nkɔa yĩ akũ mbɔa afɔliɛ kĩ bipila mɔ ntɛ bati kawɛ lɛláa yĩ kamɛ ni, nlá nfasɔ adima.

english_verse: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
translation_verse: Ɔdɔ deĩ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ edeĩ sɛmɛ kalɛ lɛmɛ. Ɔdɔ lɛlá anɛngbã, yaátui ɔwɔlɛ ade yaáta akũ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔ lákɛna fɔnyɔãtsa, yaáwɛ ye odi fude, yaána kalɔkpa, ade yaápi tɔkpa kado nɛ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔ lámɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ tɔkpa akũ, mbom yamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔ lɛlá kawũna da. Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ aayatɛ̃, kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ mɔ kadikanyi deĩ, lɛmɛ lɔkɔ aaya kĩ foe flee fɔ́atsua,

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ boe kadikanyi mɔ boe Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ a lɛmɛ tákpɔla fuyua a.

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔkpɔla yawo ɔtɔ ni, futetsa kĩĩ flee aatɛ̃.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ nna nɛ tibisɛ̃ kamɛ a, nlɛ kɔdzɛla, mbɔɛ adzuni, mbu lɔlɔ kamɛ ndɛ obisɛ̃ aye. Lɛmɛ kĩ mbo anɛ ndani ɔnɔkɔɛ̃ ni, nfɔnɛ tibisɛ̃ kamɛ fɔtsa flee awɛ.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ bɔamɔ fɔtsa futufutu ndɛ ayekĩ fɔla nɛ ahuhɔɛ kamɛ. Lɛmɛ ke liti ni bɔ́ayamɔ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa anɛ mɔ anɛ. Lɛtsa a kĩ ĩnyina finyaa ni, fɔtákpɔla fuyua, lɛmɛ liti maamɔ foe yededeede ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma nyina yĩ a.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe kamɛ a, fɔtsa ɛlalɛ deĩ kĩ foe kɔ̃ fɔ́awa daa. Ade foe nyɛ katɛkado, kɔnɛte mɔ ɔdɔ, lɛmɛ fukĩ fɔbɛ̃ flee nyɛ ɔdɔ.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Foesũ ɔdɔ ɛnya fɔtsa a kĩ foe liti míatomɛna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, lɛmɛ miwɛ hũhũ kamɛ futetsa liti osi, titiliwu Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ odi deĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ yalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ ni bɛmɔ Baguma odi alɛ kɔdzɛla, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ bɛmɔ bati kitonɛkĩ bɛláanu foe kayɔ. Fɔnyɛ fɔlɛtsa kɔlawa kĩ futo Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ni, yabi bati kalɛ ade yabumɔ be, yado be kɔbɛ̃, kadɔla be tɔwɔlɛ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Oti kĩ yalɛ kalɛ nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ bati lánu foe kayɔ ni, ye odi akũ yabumɛna. Lɛmɛ odi kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ni, yabumɛna batɛ badonɛ a flee.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Nwɛ kĩ mipuli milɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ mipuli minyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ni fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ odi nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ni, ebumɔ asɔli bati a ɛba odi kĩ yalɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ, mbɔɛntɛ odi bɛbã nyɛ foe kayɔ fɔnya kabumɛna fɔkpa bati a flee.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ntɛ ĩya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ĩyalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ kĩ odima tánu foe kayɔ a ni, nfasɔ wɔdabe deĩ nɛ foe kamɛ fɔkpa mi? Lɛmɛ ntɛ nkɔa kanyɛkatsa kadi, kadikanyi kadi, Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ kadi ĩye kadikanyi, Baguma fɔlɛtsa, fɔtsa katsa fudi mma mi ni, ade foe aakpa mi kabumɛna a.

english_verse: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
translation_verse: Ade aye kagbulɛɛ ĩye ɔsankuo, titɔ kĩ fɔlá nkpã malo ni, foe odo deĩ. Ntɛ bɛtávɔ̃ ĩye bɛbɔa foe nɛ osi odi akũ ni, ndɛ baakɛna biyi odo a ɔsũ?

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ oti a kĩ yavɔ̃ kagbulɛɛ távɔ̃ ke kavɔ̃ wĩ ni, nde kawũ banɔnɛ aakɛna binyi kĩ bɛpai be bɛkpa kawũkanɔ?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Ade ayea ntɛ ɔlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide bɛbã kamɛ ni, nde bati aakɛna binu nɔ fɔlɛtsa a kayɔ? Fɔatsa kĩ ɔlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi lánu yĩ tide ni, ndani oti kĩ mapɔtɔ nkpa ye, ade ke ye malo ɛdani ɔpɔtɔni ɛkpa yĩ a.

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Nɛ kĩ miawɛ kĩ miana hũhũ kamɛ futetsa mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ sũ ni, mivia Baguma ɛkpa mi fukĩ fɔ́anya kabumɛna fɔkpa asɔli bati a flee.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ edeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ yalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ ni, eke Baguma aduli ɛkpa ye foe kayɔkanyɛ kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ, lɔkɔa ɛnyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de ɛlɛ a kayɔ yededeede.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ mbɔa mpaɛ nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ ni, yĩ hũhũ kɔ̃ bɔa mpaɛ lɛmɛ yĩ adzuni lɛláa foe kamɛ.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Foesũ litsa kɛnɛ kĩ nkɛna? Maabɔa mpaɛ nɛ hũhũ kamɛ ade maabɔa mpaɛ lɛmɛ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩ manu foe kayɔ kamɛ a. Maasã tɔnɔ nɛ hũhũ kamɛ ade maasã tɔnɔ lɛmɛ mɔ fɔlɛtsa kĩ manu foe kayɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Ntɛ amu Baguma nɛ hũhũ kamɛ ni, nde oti a kĩ ɛtánu nɔ fɔlɛtsa kayɔ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a aapuli etunɔ kĩ, “Fɔya lɔmɔ” nɛ nɔ kɔlɛ kanyɛ a onukpɛ̃?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Ntɛ nɔ kɔlɛkanyɛ mpaɛbɔ a nɛnɛ malo ni, fɔtábumɛna oti bɛbã nɛ osi odima akũ.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Manyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ kĩ mapuli kalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ kaba mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ boe katsɛna kamɛ ni, mawɛ kĩ malɛ fɔlɛtsa sukuloo kĩ bati aanu foe kayɔ bɛkɔa bɛkɔlɛ fɔtsa, kɛba kĩ maalɛ fɔlɛtsa pii nɛ tide bɛbã kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, mitato tibisɛ̃ nɛ mi kakɔna kamɛ. Mikɛna ndɛ babisɛ̃ ntɛ fɔnyɛ tɔkpa lɛmɛ mibo anɛ nɛ kadikanyi kamɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Maalɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ yĩ kade bati ntsã bati kĩ balɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ akũ. Ade maabi be kalɛ ntomɛna banɔvɔɛ̃ tunukpɛ̃, lɛmɛ bɛláatse yĩ kalɛ.”

english_verse: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
translation_verse: Foesũ miamɔ kĩ kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ nyɛ nsɛntsɛlɛni fɔkpa bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido, nnɛ́ bikĩ bɛtɛ bido a. Lɛmɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ nyɛ nsɛntsɛlɛni ɛkpa bikĩ bɛtɛ bido a, nnɛ́ bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido a.

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ asɔli bati flee tsɛna mɔ odi nyɛ odi lamɛna kɔdzɛla kalɛ nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ, mɔ fɔya kĩ bati pupulidza ĩye bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ a, nnɛ́ kalɛ baalɛ kĩ mi fɔlɔ kamɛ bu kade?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi nyɛ odi lamɛna Baguma onukpɛ̃ kanyɛ mɔ oti pulidza ĩye ekĩ ɛtátɛ edo ya nɛ awã ni, lɛtsa kĩ yanu aafɔ̃ ɛdani ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Fɔlɛtsa a kĩ yanu a aadzɛmɔ ye,

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: ade lɛtsa kĩ ɛkɔa ɛkɔla nɛ ye kamɛ aanɛ fɔwa pefee a. Yaadɔla ye akũ emu Baguma ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔali, Baguma deĩ nɛ boe kamɛ nɛ awĩ.” Bɛkɛna Mbla Bɛkpa Asɔli a.

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ade fukĩĩ mawɛ kĩ mikɛna a. Ntɛ mitsɛna kĩ miasum Baguma ni, mi kamɛ odi deĩ kĩ yasã ɔnɔ, odi atsa fɔtsa, odi deĩ kanyɛkatsa kĩ futo Baguma sɛkɛ̃, odi alɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide bɛbã kamɛ, ade odi lɛmɛ anyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ a kayɔ a. Kɛnɛ kĩ miakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ fɔado asɔli bati a flee kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi aalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide bɛbã kamɛ ni, bati nviã ĩye be ɛlalɛ bɛlɛ bitomɔ akũ, lɛmɛ kɛnɛ kĩ oti bɛbã anyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛlɛ a kayɔ.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odima lɛláa kĩ yaanyɛ foe kayɔ ni, kekɔ̃ oti a kĩ yalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide bɛbã kamɛ a ɛkɛna dĩĩ ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla ɛkpa ye akũ mɔ Baguma.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Bati nviã ĩye be ɛlalɛ bɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ lɔkɔa bati nkaɛ a bɛsa fɔlɛtsa a kĩ baalɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi deĩ nɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ kĩ ɛtɛ kanyɛkatsa etomɛna Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ni, owula kĩ ɛlamɛna kɔdzɛla kalɛ a ɛfɔ̃ kalɛ.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Mi a flee míapuli milɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa ɔmɔa ɔmɔa ayekĩ odi nyɛ odi aakɔlɛ fɔtsa ɛkɔa foe edo ye akũ kɔbɛ̃.

english_verse: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
translation_verse: Bikĩ banyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ a bipuli bipi be akũ nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ balɛ a kamɛ,

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ Baguma táwɛ kĩ bɔna nɛ ɔtutũ kamɛ mbom budzi nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ lukudɔ kamɛ. Ade aye kɛnɛ kĩ fɔna nɛ Baguma tɛsɔli a flee kamɛ a.

english_verse: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ batsole bɛkɛna dĩĩ nɛ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃. Bɛtákpa be osi kĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla, ade Yudafɔ mbla lɛmɛ tsa kĩ bɛtanya lɔtɔ nɛ adzuma adima kamɛ a.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɛwɛ kĩ banyi litsedi kamɛ ni bivia be banyɔ nɛ kɔla. Fɔnyɛ kɔnyɔã fɔkpa otsole kĩ yalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ batɛ badonɛ katsɛnakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
translation_verse: Mi sɛkɛ̃ Baguma kalɛ a nɛ kito mbɔɛɛ? Mbɔɛɛ kĩ mi odi bɛkɔa ke bɛma?

english_verse: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mi kamɛ odi kɔna kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ, ĩye edeĩ hũhũ kamɛ futetsa ni, enyi kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ ntsɛlɛ mamɛna mi a nyɛ boe Ɔlate mbla kĩ ɛkpa.

english_verse: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi tátse fukĩĩ ni, mi malo mitatse ye kalɛ.

english_verse: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
translation_verse: Foesũ nwaɛ̃ amɛ, fɔnya mi kawɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ mianyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃, lɛmɛ mitasua bikĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ tide ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ kamɛ a osi.

english_verse: But do everything properly and in order.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ miakɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa nɛ de osi akũ mɔ obu kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, nwɛ kĩ mate mi anɛ nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a kĩ nde lɛtsa nkpa mi a akũ. Mitɛ ɔlɛdo a mido ade mi katɛkado dzɛ̃ nɛ foe akũ pintii a.

english_verse: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
translation_verse: Ade fɔlɛtsa wĩ a kĩ nde foe akũ lɛtsã nkpa mi a. Míana katɛ ntɛ mipi fɔlɛtsa wĩ kĩĩ midomɔ akũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, kekɔ̃ katɛ mitɛ mido pupulidza.

english_verse: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa titiliwu a kĩ ntɛ a, ade foe nkɔa nkpa mi a. Ade foe nyɛ kĩ Kristo ku nɛ boe tɔkpa lɔlɔ, ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ a.

english_verse: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Bibila ye ade ke nɛ luwi lɛlalɛde akũ ni, Baguma dzɛla ye edzi nkpã ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ a.

english_verse: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Petro, mɔ fɔtɔ banyanɛ ewua-nviã a flee.

english_verse: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa bikĩ bitomɔ ye kĩ bɛbɛ̃ bati ɔha elo nɛ lɔkɔ mɔa kamɛ, ade be kamɛ badi pii dzi nkpã kɛyawo nviã kĩĩ lɛmɛ be pii tɛ̃ kuwa liti.

english_verse: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
translation_verse: Ɛyanyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa Yakobo, ade ke liti ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa fɔtɔ banyanɛ a flee a.

english_verse: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
translation_verse: Nɛ ke kawũna a, ɛnyɛ ye akũ ɛtsa yĩ malo, titɔ kĩ nkɛna ndɛ oti kĩ bɛtáma ye nɛ lɔkɔ wĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ yĩ nyɛ obisɛ̃ kɔlaa nɛ fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ, ade ntáfɛta nkpa kĩ bavɛ yĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ malo, kitonɛkĩ ntomɔ Baguma asɔli bati a liti.

english_verse: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ kĩ Baguma mɔ yĩ kɔnya sũ ni, ɛkɛna yĩ oti a kĩ ye manɛ finyaakĩĩ. Ye kɔnyakamɔ kĩĩ tákɛna fɔtsa pupulidza. Nkɛna adzuma paa mba fɔtɔ banyanɛ bɛbã a, kĩ nnɛ́ yĩ mɔawɛ kɔbɛ̃ nkɔa nkɛna, mbom Baguma kɔnyakamɔ a libumɔ yĩ.

english_verse: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ fɔlɛtsa a to yĩ sɛkɛ̃ o-o, futo be sɛkɛ̃ o-o, ade foe akũ boe flee bɔade lɛtsã a. Fɔnyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ mi malo mitɛ mido. Baguma Dzɛla Kristo Etomɛna Kanɔkpa.

english_verse: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ boe ɔlɛdo nyɛ kĩ Baguma dzɛla Kristo etomɛna kanɔkpa. Nde sũ mi kamɛ badi alɛ kĩ bɛláadzɛla banɔkpa bidzi nkpã?

english_verse: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ banɔkpa láyidza ni, kekɔ̃ fɔatsa kĩ Kristo táyidza.

english_verse: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
translation_verse: Ntɛ Baguma ɛtadzɛla Kristo eto kanɔkpa ni, kekɔ̃ bɔlá lɛtsama kĩ bɔade lɛtsã nɛ de akũ, ade mi malo lɛtsama lɛláa kĩ miatɛ mido a.

english_verse: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ lɛbɛ̃ nyɛ kĩ fɔadã ndɛ bɔma fɔvã bɔate nɛ Baguma akũ, kitonɛkĩ bɔlɛ kĩ ɛdzɛla Kristo etomɛna kanɔkpa. Lɛmɛ ntɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ kĩ banɔkpa lɛláayidza ni, kekɔ̃ Baguma tádzɛla Kristo.

english_verse: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ Baguma ɛtadzɛla banɔkpa ni, ade aye a ɛtádzɛla Kristo lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
translation_verse: Ntɛ Baguma ɛtadzɛla Kristo ni, kekɔ̃ mi katɛkado nyɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ fɔtsa ade ke míabuki mina nɛ mi tɔkpa kamɛ a.

english_verse: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
translation_verse: Foe kayɔ lɛmɛ atsa kĩ bikĩ bɛtɛ Kristo bido fɔmɔ biku ni, bɛtána katɛ.

english_verse: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
translation_verse: Ntɛ boe anɛ teĩ nɛ Kristo akũ fɔkpa katinya kĩĩ odi kɔ̃ ni, kekɔ̃ boe akũ akɔ kɔnya paa kaba odi nyɛ odi nɛ katinya flee kamɛ.

english_verse: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kalɛ ɔnɔkɔali a nyɛ kĩ Baguma dzɛla Kristo etomɛna kanɔkpa ade fɔnyɛ kasipi kĩ bikĩ biku a ni, yaadzɛla be malo.

english_verse: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ndɛ ayekĩ wuda tsã oti akũ ɛya nɛ katinya kamɛ a, ade aye bati kayidza kato kanɔkpa lɛmɛ tsã oti akũ a.

english_verse: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ bati flee aaku nɛ kĩ bɛnɛ bito Adam kamɛ sũ a, ade ayea Baguma aadzɛla bati flee nɛ ayekĩ bɛkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Kristo sũ a.

english_verse: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma aadzɛla odi nyɛ odi nɛ ye nhihiɛ a kĩ ɛkɛna eyi akũ. Kristo nyɛ oti ɔtɔnyade, ke liti lɔkɔ a kĩ yaabuki ɛya a, yaadzɛla bikĩ bɛnyɛ ye bade a.

english_verse: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, katinya kawũna aaya. Kristo aanya hũhũ flee kɔbɛ̃, katinya kamɛ otumifɔ mɔ bikĩ bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ akũ, lɔkɔa ɛkɔa lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a edo nɛ Baguma boe Ɔlɛga awɛ.

english_verse: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ Kristo ɛnya lɛgã kɛnawo kĩ Baguma nya bakesĩnɛ flee akũ, ɛkɔa ye abɔa ete nɛ be akũ.

english_verse: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
translation_verse: Okesĩnɛ nkaɛ kĩ Baguma aanya ye akũ nyɛ wuda.

english_verse: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Baguma fɔ̃ ɛkɔa ye abɔa ete nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa akũ.” Fɔwaa yedede kĩ ntɛ bɛlɛ kĩ “lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa” a, fɔátsa kĩ Baguma awɛ a kĩ ɛfɔ̃ Kristo kɔa abɔa ete nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa akũ a tsɛka nɛ foe kamɛ.

english_verse: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛkɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa bido nɛ Kristo lɛgãkanya kayɔ ni, ye mɔawɛ kĩ ɛnyɛ Obi a, aakɔa ye akũ edo nɛ Baguma a kĩ ɛkɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa edo nɛ ye kayɔ a kayɔ. Baguma aanya lɛgã nɛ fɔtsa flee akũ.

english_verse: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, nde nyɛ bikĩ bɛkɛna be mui kawɔla amaniɛ nɛ bikĩ biku lɔlɔ a? Litsa nyɛ likĩ baamɔ nɛ foe kamɛ? Ntɛ ɔnɔkɔali fɔanɛ ndɛ ayekĩ badi alɛ kĩ Baguma lɛláadzɛla bati bidzi nkpã a, nde sũ bɛfɔ̃ bɛkɛna boe be mui kawɔla amaniɛ?

english_verse: And why do we always risk our lives
translation_verse: Nde sũ boe fɔtɔ banyanɛ a malo budeĩ nɛ nkpã mɔ wuda kayite lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ?

english_verse: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ndeĩ nɛ wuda awɛ kamɛ luwi daa. Mapia nɛ mi akũ nɛ kĩ bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo sũ. Ade foe lɛfɔ̃ manyɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kanya a.

english_verse: What do you think I gained by fighting wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead are not raised to life, “Let's eat and drink. Tomorrow we die.”
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔnyɛ otidziwa kakɔna onukpɛ̃ sũ mɔ nvũ lɛma mɔ bayaɛ kpa kĩ bɛla ndɛ bati aye nɛ Efeso nɛ awĩ ni, litsa nfasɔ nna? Lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛláadzɛla banɔkpa bidzi nkpã ni, kekɔ̃ fuketi mɔ kalɛ a kĩ balɛ kĩ, “Mifɔ̃ bɔnya bɔmɔa, kitonɛkĩ kunya bɔ́aku.”

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
translation_verse: Mitafɔ̃ bɛkɛ mi. “Tɔkɔbato abula oti kĩ edeĩ ɔbla wĩ kade.”

english_verse: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
translation_verse: Milɔla adzuni wĩ kabɔɛ, lɔkɔa mifɔ̃ tɔkpa kakɛna. Mabi mi kĩ kɔnyɔã fɔanɛ fɔkpa mi kĩ mi kamɛ badi linyína Baguma. Paulo Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Banɔkpa Kabuki Kayidza Akũ.

english_verse: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
translation_verse: Odi aavia kĩ, “Nde banɔkpa aapuli biyidza bidzi nkpã? Sukɛna sɔwɔdabe odu baana?”

english_verse: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
translation_verse: Odzimbifɔ anɛ! Ntɛ odu kutse libi, libi a lɛláamɛ ntɛ lɛtávɔ̃ nɛ tite kamɛ.

english_verse: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa kĩ odu a nyɛ kutse libi pupulidza, ndɛ ligbanɔbi ĩye fudutsa bɛbã. Fɔnyɛ́ kutse munumunu kĩ ɔbɔɛ odu fɔanɛ.

english_verse: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
translation_verse: Baguma kpa kutse libi a na sukɛna a kĩ yawɛ, ade ɛkpa libi nyɛ libi de sukɛna a.

english_verse: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa kĩ fudzi nkpã flee lɛlá sukɛna mɔa odu, batidziwa deĩ be sukɛna odu, bayaɛ deĩ be side, kɔvɔlabi deĩ be side, ade kɔfɔabi lɛmɛ deĩ be side a.

english_verse: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
