translation_verse: Ode kamɛ fɔtsa deĩ be sukɛna, fɔmɔ tite akũ fɔtsa lɛmɛ deĩ be sukɛna. Ayekĩ ode kamɛ bati alɛ kadã a vɛmɛna nɛ ayekĩ tite akũ bati alɛ kadã a akũ.

english_verse: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
translation_verse: Suwa deĩ se kakpalɛ, odetɔ deĩ ye kakpalɛ. Fusibii lɛmɛ deĩ foe kakpalɛ lite lɛmɛ nɛ foe kamɛ malo fudeĩ foe kakpalɛ futefute.

english_verse: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
translation_verse: Ade limii fɔ́ana ntɛ Baguma aadzɛla banɔkpa bidzi nkpã a. Ntɛ bibila ɔnɔkpa ni, sukɛna a aavɔ̃, lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛdzɛla ye ni ɛláabuki eku.

english_verse: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bibila ɔnɔkpa ni, ɛnyɛ sukɛna pupulidza, lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛdzɛla ye ni, yaana hũhũ kamɛ sukɛna. Fɔwaa pefee kĩ oti sukɛna deĩ ade hũhũ kamɛ sukɛna lɛmɛ deĩ a.

english_verse: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Baguma bɔa oti ɔtɔnyade a kĩ ɛnyɛ Adam ndɛ otidziwa aye.” Lɛmɛ Adam nkaɛ a nyɛ Hũhũ kĩ yakpa nkpã.

english_verse: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ hũhũ a lɛnya lɔtɔ ɛya, mbom otidziwa a ya fɔmɔ hũhũ a lɛmɛ yaya.

english_verse: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
translation_verse: Baguma bɔa Adam ɔtɔnyade a etomɛna tite kamɛ, lɛmɛ Adam ɔyade a kɔ̃ to ode.

english_verse: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bɛnyɛ katinya kamɛ bade a la ndɛ oti a kĩ bɛbɔa ye bitomɛna tite kamɛ a. Bikĩ bɛnyɛ ode bade lɛmɛ la ndɛ okĩ eto ode a aye.

english_verse: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ budeĩ nɛ ekĩ bɛbɔa bitomɛna tite kamɛ kutitse odu kamɛ a, ade aye bɔ́ana nɛ Otidziwa a kĩ eto ode kutitse a kamɛ a.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, lɛtsa kĩ matsa nyɛ kĩ lɛtsa kĩ lideĩ sukɛna mɔ babɔanɛ ni, lɛláapuli lɛna kanyakɔ̃ nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ. Lɛtsa a kĩ laaku ni, lɛláapuli lɛna nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔláawɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
translation_verse: Mitse ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ kĩĩ kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla boe. Nnɛ́ boe flee aaku, lɛmɛ ntɛ bɛvɔ̃ kagbulɛɛ nkaɛ a ni, boe flee bɔ́adani deedimɔapɛ. Fɔ́aya nɛ sinepo kabɔa mɔa kamɛ. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bɛvɔ̃ kagbulɛɛ a pɛ, Baguma aadzɛla banɔkpa flee lɔkɔa bɛláabuki biku dadaada, yaadani be bɛkɛna bati vɔɛ̃.

english_verse: Our dead and decaying bodies will be changed into bodies that won't die or decay.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa kĩ laabu kade ni, kɛnɛ kĩ lɛdani lɛkɛna lɛtsa kĩ lɛláabu kade, ade lɛtsa a kĩ laaku ni, kɛnɛ kĩ lɛdani lɛkɛna lɛtsa kĩ lɛláaku.

english_verse: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ fɔya kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ laapuli libu kade dani lɛkɛna lɛtsa a kĩ lɛláabu kade ni, lɔkɔa fɔlɛtsa nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kĩ, “Bɛwɔla wuda, bɛnya ye akũ.”

english_verse: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
translation_verse: Wuda tɛ ye kɔbɛ̃ kĩ ɛnɛɛ yapila boe a etomɛna titikpa kakɛna kamɛ, ade titikpa kakɛna lɛmɛ tɛ te kɔbɛ̃ titomɛna mbla a kamɛ a.

english_verse: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ kĩ ɛkpa boe anunyam ɛtsãmɛna boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo akũ.

english_verse: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
translation_verse: Foesũ, nwaɛ̃ amɛ kĩ maátunɔ kalɛ, midza pintii, mitakpã nɛ mi adzuma kakɛna kamɛ. Midanɛ boe Ɔlate adzuma kakɛna, kitonɛkĩ minyina kĩ adzuma adi nyɛ adi kĩ mikɛna lɛnyɛ́ adzuma pupulidza ɛkpa boe Ɔlate.

english_verse: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, nɛ sika a kĩ miawɛ kĩ miate mikɔa mibumɛna Baguma bati nɛ Yudea a akũ ni, mikɛna ndɛ ayekĩ mbi Galatiafɔ batɛ badonɛ kutsu a kĩ bɛkɛna a.

english_verse: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
translation_verse: Ɛkwansieda nyɛ́ Ɛkwansieda ni, mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi ɛmanɛ sika nɛ ye kɔtɔkase onukpɛ̃ eyi, lɔkɔa ntɛ ĩya ni, fɔláasĩ kĩ miate tɔgɔlɛ.

english_verse: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ĩya ni, maakpa bati a kĩ mite awɛ nɛ be akũ a tuvoli, lɔkɔa bɛkɔa mi futetsa bɛnamɛna Yerusalem.

english_verse: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
translation_verse: Ntɛ fɔ́asĩ kĩ yĩ malo nnaa ni, bɔmɔ be bɔ́atsã kɔbɔa mɔa.

english_verse: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
translation_verse: Maatsã Makedonia kamɛ fɔmɔ maaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃, kitonɛkĩ nke otse kĩ maatsã awã lɛmɛ.

english_verse: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
translation_verse: Ɔsa maana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ sukuloo nɛ osili lɔkɔ flee a, lɔkɔa ntɛ madzakũ ni, míapuli mibumɔ yĩ nɛ yĩ osi akũ kanaa kamɛ nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ.

english_verse: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
translation_verse: Ntáwɛ kĩ yĩ kasɛla a fɔdã lukũ lɔkɔa ntɛ̃ nnaa lɔtɔ. Nwɛ kĩ mana nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ ntɛ boe Ɔlate kpa osi.

english_verse: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
translation_verse: Maadzi nɛ Efeso nɛ awĩ kɛnaawo Pentekoste luwi a akũ,

english_verse: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ osi waa paa ɛkpa adzuma sĩwa baũ kakɛna nɛ awĩ, titɔ kĩ bati pii yidza bɛdza nɛ yĩ akũ malo.

english_verse: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
translation_verse: Ntɛ Timoteo ya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ni, mitɛ ye atuu kitonɛkĩ yakɛna boe Ɔlate adzuma ndɛ yĩ aye.

english_verse: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
translation_verse: Odima ɛtadã ye ndɛ oti kĩ ɛla kadzakɔ̃ kadima, lɛmɛ mikpa ye kabumɛna lɔkɔa ɛnaa ye osi akũ nɛ lukudɔ kamɛ, emuniki ɛya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃, kitonɛkĩ madã anɛ kĩ bɛmɔ boe bawaɛ̃ a aamuniki bɛya.

english_verse: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ, kalɛ nɛ boe ɔwaɛ̃ Apolo akũ nyɛ kĩ, lɔkɔ pii mado ye kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bɛmɔ bawaɛ̃ bɛbã a bɛyaasɛla mi, lɛmɛ fɔnyɛ́ ye kawɛ kɔlaa kĩ yaya nɛ awã lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ. Ntɛ ɛna osi ni, yaaya ke liti. Paulo Kɔdɛ Nkaɛ Mɔ Kawɔ̃na.

english_verse: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
translation_verse: Mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ, midza pintii nɛ mi katɛkado kamɛ. Mido ɔwɔlɛ, mido akũ kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Show love in everything you do.
translation_verse: Mikɛna mi tudzuma flee nɛ ɔdɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
translation_verse: Minyina kĩ Stefana mɔ ye tɔtɔ kamɛ bati a nyɛ bati batɔnyade kĩ bɛdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ nɛ Akaia, kĩ bɛkɔa be akũ bɛkpa Baguma adzuma kakɛna. Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, make mi aduli kĩ,

english_verse: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
translation_verse: mitomɔ lɔtɔ banyanɛ kĩĩ odu ɔbla liti mɔ bati bɛbã kĩ bakɛna adzuma kabumɔ boe.

english_verse: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ deĩ yĩ kĩ Stefana, Fortunato mɔ Akaiko ya bideĩ nɛ awĩ nɛ mi kadokɔ̃.

english_verse: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
translation_verse: Bido yĩ kɔbɛ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ bido mi kɔbɛ̃ a. Kɛnɛ kĩ buyi bati kĩĩ odu ɔsũ.

english_verse: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Asia kamɛkamɛ a awɔ̃na mi. Akwila mɔ Priskila mɔ asɔli bati a kĩ batsɛna nɛ be tɔtɔ kamɛ a awɔ̃na mi nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ flee a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awĩ bo nwɔ̃na mi nkpa be tsoo! Mitu miboomu akũ nɛ tɔwaɛ̃ kamɛ.

english_verse: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
translation_verse: Yĩ Paulo mɔawɛ lɛkɔa awɛ ntsɛlɛ kawɔ̃na kĩĩ.

english_verse: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaáwɛ boe Ɔlate kalɛ ni, ɛkɔa nɛ ke a! O boe Ɔlate, ya!

english_verse: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate Yesu kɔnyakamɔ kitomɔ mi.

english_verse: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Yĩ ɔdɔ ɛnamɔ mi flee nɛ Kristo Yesu dĩ akũ. Fɔya lɔmɔ.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
translation_verse: Ovoli kĩĩ to yĩ, Paulo, kĩ Baguma pau ndɛ Kristo Yesu fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ nɛ ye kawɛ onukpɛ̃, mɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃ Timoteo sɛkɛ̃. Bɔtsɛlɛ ovoli a bɔama Baguma asɔli a kĩ edeĩ nɛ Korinto mɔ Baguma bade a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Akaia tite flee a akũ.

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
translation_verse: Bɔawã kĩ Baguma boe Ɔlɛga mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo, bɛmɔ mi kɔnya bɛkpa mi lukudɔ. Paulo Nyɛ Baguma Kɔlɛ.

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bɔnyɛ Baguma kĩ ɛnyɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo Ɔlɛga, kĩ yamɔ bati kɔnya, kĩ ye sɛkɛ̃ kabumɛna flee lɛmɛ ato a, kɔlɛ.

english_verse: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
translation_verse: Yado boe ɔwɔlɛ nɛ boe ɔhau flee kamɛ, ayekĩ bɔ́apuli bɔdɔla bikĩ bideĩ nɛ ɔhau adi nyɛ adi kamɛ a tɔwɔlɛ, mɔ kabumna a kĩ boe mɔawɛ bɔtɛ butomɛna Baguma sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ bɔamɔ lubo beblebee odu a kĩ Kristo mɔ́ a, ade aye fɔtsɛ̃ Kristo akũ bɔana tsɛfã katomɛna Baguma kabumɛna baũ a kamɛ a.

english_verse: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bɔamɔ lubo ni, fɔnyɛ kabumɛna mɔ nkpã katɛ fɔkpa mi. Ntɛ Baguma bumɔ boe ni, ebumɔ mɔ mi malo, ɛkpa mi kɔbɛ̃ kĩ míapuli mina ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ mimɔ́ lubo odu a kĩ de bɔamɔ a.

english_verse: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
translation_verse: Foesũ boe kɔnɛte nɛ mi akũ dzɛ̃ kpli. Kitonɛkĩ bunyina kĩ ayekĩ miamɔ lubo ndɛ boe ayea, ade aye mi malo miana kabumɛna a kĩ bɔana a.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bɔawɛ kĩ minyi ɔhau a kĩ bɔna nɛ Asia kamɛkamɛ a. Fɔlɔtsa a kĩ bite nɛ boe akũ pɔnɛ paa, fɔna ɔlɔmɛ fɔba boe kalɔ kĩ bɔtákɔna kĩ bɔ́abuki budzi nkpã.

english_verse: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
translation_verse: Bɔmɔ́ nɛ boe kamɛ kĩ bɛkɔ boe kɔtɔ bɛkpa wuda. Lɛmɛ fukĩĩ ya nɛ boe akũ ayekĩ bɔláakɔa anɛ bute nɛ boe mɔawɛ akũ, mbom nɛ Baguma kĩ yadzɛla banɔkpa a akũ.

english_verse: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
translation_verse: Ɛnyɛ boe etomɛna wuda tisi kĩ tideĩ libe kamɛ, ade yaabuki ɛnyɛ boe a. Bɔkɔa anɛ bute nɛ ye akũ kĩ yaabuki ɛnyɛ boe,

english_verse: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
translation_verse: ndɛ ayekĩ miabumɛna boe nɛ mi mpaɛbɔ kabɔa kakpa boe kamɛ a. Foesũ fɔ́aya kĩ Baguma aatse mi mpaɛbɔ pii a kĩ mibɔa mikpa boe a, lɔkɔa ehila boe, ade bati pii aakɔa osee bɛnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ boe lɔlɔ a. Paulo Lɛ Lɛtsa a Kĩ De Sũ Ɛdani Ye Nhihiɛ.

english_verse: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa mɔa kĩ de akũ bɔapia nyɛ kĩ boe tɔwɔlɛ abi boe kĩ budzi kadzi wĩ nɛ katinya kamɛ, titiliwu nɛ bɔmɔ mi kadzi kamɛ. Bɔkɛna foe nnɛ́ nɛ batidziwa kadikanyi kamɛ, mbom Baguma kɔnyakamɔ kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ futo.

english_verse: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
translation_verse: Ĩnyina fɔtsa kĩĩ flee yededeede, ade foesũ nkɛna nhihiɛ kĩ maanya lɔtɔ ĩyaasɛla mi kufɛ, ayekĩ míana kasɛla titi nviã kamɛ nfasɔ.

english_verse: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nkɛna nhihiɛ kĩ maayasɛla mi ntɛ manaa Makedonia, ade ntɛ mmuniki maya lɛmɛ maatsã awã, lɔkɔa maatɛ osi katsã kabumɛna ntomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃ nnaa Yudea.

english_verse: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
translation_verse: Kĩ nkakɛna nhihiɛ kĩĩ a, ntáke otse paa nɛ foe akũ mbɔɛɛ? Mbɔɛɛ kĩ nkɛna nhihiɛ a ni, nkɛna ye nɛ batidziwa kawɛ onukpɛ̃ kĩ maapuli nlɛ “Oo” mɔ “Oowo” nɛ lɔkɔ mɔa kamɛ?

english_verse: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ Baguma akɛna nɛ ye kalɛ akũ sũ ni, yĩ lɛtsa a kĩ nlɛ ĩyi nkpa mi a lɛnyɛ́ “Oo” mɔ “Oowo” fɔlɛtsa.

english_verse: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Yesu Kristo, Baguma Obi, kĩ ye akũ fɔlɛtsa Sila, Timoteo mɔ yĩ bude lɛtsã a lɛnyɛ́ oti kĩ yalɛ “Oo” mɔ “Oowo” oti, mbom ye nyɛ Baguma a kĩ yalɛ “Oo” lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ.

english_verse: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ade ye nyɛ oti kĩ ɛnyɛ Baguma kĩ yalɛ “Oo” kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ ɛkpa ye fɔlɛtsa flee a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a. Ade foesũ bɔamu Baguma katsãmɛna Yesu Kristo akũ kalɛ kĩ, “Fɔya lɔmɔ” a.

english_verse: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
translation_verse: Baguma mɔawɛ lɛkɛna kĩ bɔmɔ mi nkpã esipi nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna nɛ Kristo kamɛ. Ade Baguma a mɔawɛ lɛpau boe eyi lite,

english_verse: and put his Spirit in our hearts to show that we belong only to him.
translation_verse: eyi boe ɔsũ kĩ ye la boe, ade ɛkɔa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa edo nɛ boe kamɛ ndɛ kasipi nɛ fɔtsa flee a kĩ yaakpa boe a akũ a.

english_verse: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
translation_verse: Baguma nyɛ yĩ ɔdansini, kitonɛkĩ enyina yĩ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Fɔnɛnɛ kĩ mmɔ mi kɔnya, ade foesũ ntábuki ĩya nɛ Korinto a.

english_verse: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
translation_verse: Bɔtáwɛ kĩ bɔahɛ mi nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ mitɛ mido a akũ, kitonɛkĩ bunyina kĩ midzɛ̃ pintii nɛ mi katɛkado kamɛ. Mbom bɔakɛna adzuma mɔ mi ayekĩ míamɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ nkɛna adzuni kĩ nláaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ayekĩ míawo ɔblɔ.

english_verse: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ nfɔ̃ miwo ɔblɔ ni, bawei deĩ kĩ baado yĩ kawɔlɛ? Ade mi mɔapɛ a kĩ nfɔ̃ miwo ɔblɔ a.

english_verse: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ ntsɛlɛ ovoli a na mma mi a. Ntáwɛ kĩ maya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ lɔkɔa mi a kĩ kɛfɛta kĩ mido yĩ kawɔlɛ a miyaafɔ̃ nwo ɔblɔ. Ĩnyina yededeede kĩ ntɛ kawɔlɛ ade yĩ ni, mi malo kawɔlɛ ade mi.

english_verse: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
translation_verse: Ntsɛlɛ ovoli a nɛ ɔhau, ɔwɔlɛ kabubuli mɔ babe pii kamɛ. Nnɛ́ yĩ kawɛ nyɛ kĩ mado mi kɔnyɔã, mbom mawɛ kĩ mimɔ miyi ɔsũ ayekĩ mawɛ mi a flee kalɛ. Paulo Kɔa Tɔkpa Ɔkɛnanɛ a Tɔkpa Ete Ye.

english_verse: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ oti odi fɔ̃ odi wo ɔblɔ ni, nnɛ́ yĩ odi ɛfɔ̃ nwo ɔblɔ, mbom ntɛ nláasɛsɛ foe ni, mi badi kɔ̃ miwo ɔblɔ. Ntáwɛ kĩ mana ɔsĩ beblebee nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
translation_verse: Sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a kĩ mi bati pii mikpa ye a kpɔla.

english_verse: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ni, mikɔa tɔkpa a mite ye, mido ye kɔbɛ̃ ayekĩ ɛláatɛnɛ ɔblɔ kawo beblebee, ɛyaatɔ flefle.

english_verse: You should make them sure of your love for them.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ make mi aduli kĩ mifɔ̃ ɛmɔ kĩ miawɛ ye kalɛ paa.

english_verse: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
translation_verse: Ntsɛlɛ ovoli a na mma mi, kitonɛkĩ nwɛ kĩ manyi ayekĩ midzɛ̃ pintii nɛ mi sɔhuiɛ kamɛ, mɔ ayekĩ miakɛna nɛ yĩ fɔtsa katsa a akũ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ.

english_verse: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mikɔa odi tɔkpa mite ye ni, yĩ malo nkɔa nte ye. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ nkɔa oti tɔkpa nte, mɔ fusĩ malo kĩ nkɔa litsedi nte kɔ̃ ni, makɛna foe nɛ Kristo anɛ kamɛ nɛ mi sũ.

english_verse: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
translation_verse: Nkɛna foe ayekĩ Abonsam lɛláakɔa tɛdake ɛnya mi akũ, kitonɛkĩ bunyina ye tisi a flee. Paulo Wɛ Kĩ Yanu Kalɛ Etomɛna Tito Sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
translation_verse: Kĩ ĩyaade lɛtsã nɛ Kristo Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nɛ Troa a, mmɔ́ kĩ boe Ɔlate binya osi ɛkpa ye adzuma kakɛna nɛ awã.

english_verse: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ fɔha yĩ paa, kitonɛkĩ ntámɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃ Tito nɛ awã. Foesũ nkla bati a ntɛ̃ nnaa Makedonia. Kristo Fɔ̃ Bɔnya Akũ.

english_verse: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ! Kitonɛkĩ nɛ boe ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo kamɛ ni, Baguma akpã boe ndɛ bati kĩ bipili be ndom aye nɛ Kristo akũ kanya kɔdza kamɛ. Baguma tsã boe akũ ɛfɔ̃ Kristo akũ fɔlɛtsa gba nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ ndɛ ayekĩ kugua mɔ ɔlɛ̃ abɔa kanaa mɔ lugo.

english_verse: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
translation_verse: Boe kɔ̃ bɔlá ndɛ bati bɛbã pii kĩ bɛnɛɛ Baguma kalɛ a ndɛ kɔyaatɔ fɔtsa kĩ fɔlá kɔya mɔ kanɔ aye. Lɛmɛ nɛ ayekĩ Baguma do boe sũ ni, bɔalɛ kalɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ye anɛ kamɛ, ndɛ Kristo basumunɛ aye.

english_verse: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
translation_verse: Katsa fɔatsa kĩ bubuki bɔapia boe akũ mbɔɛɛ? Kanɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔkpa mi boe akũ adansiɛ tuvoli, mbɔɛɛ kĩ bɔtɛ te butomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃ ndɛ ayekĩ bati badi akɛna a?

english_verse: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
translation_verse: Ade mi mɔawɛ a nyɛ tuvoli kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, kĩ odi nyɛ odi aapuli enyi ɛkã lɛmɛ.

english_verse: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
translation_verse: Foe kamɛ kpalɛ kĩ Kristo mɔawɛ lɛtsɛlɛ ovoli kĩĩ ɛkpa boe bɔkɔa bɔma mi. Bɛtákɔa ovoli katsɛlɛ mui bɛtsɛlɛ ye, mbom bɛkɔa Baguma kĩ edeĩ nkpã a Hũhũ bɛtsɛlɛ foe. Bɛtátsɛlɛ ovoli a nɛ sileti akũ, mbom bɛtsɛlɛ ye nɛ batidziwa du akũ. Baguma Kpa Boe Nkpã Nɛ Apam Vɔɛ̃ a Kamɛ.

english_verse: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
translation_verse: Bɔalɛ fukĩĩ kitonɛkĩ nɛ Kristo sũ, budeĩ kadzakɔ̃ nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
translation_verse: Lɛtsama lɛláa boe kamɛ kĩ laafɔ̃ bɔlɛ kĩ bɔ́apuli bɔkɛna adzuma kĩĩ. Kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ budeĩ to Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
translation_verse: Ade ye lɛfɔ̃ kĩ bupuli bɔnya apam vɔɛ̃ basumunɛ, kĩ ɛnyɛ́ mbla a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ biyi a, mbom bɔasum ekĩ Hũhũ kamɛ edeĩ a. Mbla a kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ a tsɛ̃mɔ wuda lɛmɛ Hũhũ ade a kɔ̃ akpa nkpã.

english_verse: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma kɔa mbla a kĩ ɛtsɛlɛ nɛ boe tratra akũ yakpa Mose a ni, Baguma nyɛ ye kukũkpalɛ ɛtsa. Mose anɛ kamɛ kata muamua mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, mɔ kukũkpalɛ paa kĩ Israelfɔ tápuli bike anɛ nɛ ye akũ, titɔ kĩ kɔkadĩ malo. Ntɛ mbla a kĩ ɛtsɛ̃mɔ wuda a nɛ́ mɔ kukũkpalɛ mɔ ɔsĩ a ni,

english_verse: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
translation_verse: kekɔ̃ ntɛ bɛkɔa Hũhũ a bakpa a, kukũkpalɛ a aana ɔsĩ kɔba paa.

english_verse: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
translation_verse: Ntɛ osi a kĩ batsã ye akũ kana kɔtɔkaku ayamɔ kukũkpalɛ, kekɔ̃ osi a kĩ ɛtsɛ̃mɔ nkpã lɛláana kukũkpalɛ baũ ɛba!

english_verse: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
translation_verse: Bɔalɛ foe lɔmɔ kitonɛkĩ finyaa kukũkpalɛ a bɛ̃ kɔtɔnyade a, ade kukũkpalɛ a kĩ kɔna ɔsĩ kɔkɔɛ a yɛla a.

english_verse: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ kukũkpalɛ na nɛ lɛtsa kĩ lɛtábuna owi akũ ni, kekɔ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ laawa daa a kukũkpalɛ aana ɔsĩ kɔba.

english_verse: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ budeĩ kɔnɛte kĩĩ sũ ni, budeĩ ɔwɔlɛ kado paa.

english_verse: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
translation_verse: Boe kɔ̃ bɔlá ndɛ Mose kĩ ɛkɔa lima esua anɛ kĩ Israelfɔ bɛtamɔ ye anɛ kamɛ kata muamua kĩ fɔlamɛna kadĩ a.

english_verse: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, be adzuni kamɛ tábinya kɛyawo nviã. Ntɛ bakã tuvoli kɔɛ a kamɛ fɔlɛtsa a malo ni, lima mɔapɛ a buki lasua be anɛ. Ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo kamɛ odi mɔ baanyɛ lima a na.

english_verse: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
translation_verse: Nɛ nviã fuwi kĩĩ kamɛ malo ni, ntɛ bɛkã Mose ovoli kamɛ fɔlɛtsa a, anɛ kasua fɔtsa a buki fɔasua be adzuni.

english_verse: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi muniki ɛya nɛ Ɔlate a sɛkɛ̃ ni, baanyɛ limakũ a nɛ ye anɛ.

english_verse: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ ni, “Ɔlate a” nɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ nyɛ Hũhũ a, ade awã kĩ boe Ɔlate Hũhũ deĩ ni, kawɛya lɛmɛ deĩ nɛ awã.

english_verse: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛnyɛ limakũ a nɛ boe flee anɛ, ade bɔanyɛ boe Ɔlate kukũkpalɛ a katsa yededeede a. Ɛlamɛna boe kadani kanamɛna ye kutidu odu a kamɛ, ade boe kukũkpalɛ lɛmɛ ade nɛ ode kabu a.

english_verse: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ Baguma mɔ́ boe kɔnya, ɛkɔa ɔsum adzuma kĩĩ edo nɛ boe awɛ sũ ni, akpɔ láveli boe.

english_verse: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
translation_verse: Bɔfɔnɛ fɔtsa bɛlawa mɔ fɔnyɔãtsa flee awɛ buyi nɛ ɔɔ́wɛ̃. Bɔákɛna lɛtsama nɛ kakɛ osi akũ, ade bɔádani Baguma fɔlɛtsa a kalɛ a. Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, budzi nkpã wĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ, ade bɔabɔa mbɔdi kĩ boe kadzi kamɛ kiwo odi nyɛ odi anɛ a.

english_verse: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a kĩ bɔalɛ a nyɛ fɔtsa kɔlawa ni, fɔnyɛ fɔtsa kɔlawa fɔkpa bikĩ bɛtsɛ̃ nɛ kayɛla osi akũ a.

english_verse: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
translation_verse: Katinya kĩĩ kamɛ oti kpa a kɔ bati a kĩ bɛtátɛ bido a adzuni. Esua be anɛ kĩ bɛtamɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a ɔhaĩ a nɛ Kristo kukũkpalɛ akũ a, kĩ kɔla ndɛ Baguma kutidu pɛpɛɛpɛ a kĩ kɔako nɛ be akũ a.

english_verse: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bɔáde lɛtsã nɛ boe mɔawɛ akũ, mbom bɔade lɛtsã nɛ Yesu Kristo boe Ɔlate akũ, ade boe kɔ̃ bɔnyɛ mi basumunɛ nɛ Yesu sũ a.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Baguma a kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ɔhaĩ ɛvɔlɛ eto ɔtũ kamɛ” a, ade ye nyɛ Baguma mɔapɛ a kĩ ye ɔhaĩ a ko nɛ boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, ɛkpa boe kadikanyi kĩ bɔmɔ Baguma kukũkpalɛ nɛ Yesu anɛ kamɛ a buyi ɔsũ.

english_verse: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe a kĩ boe kamɛ hũhũ fɔtsa mɔ kɔya kĩĩ deĩ a, bɔla ndɛ tibute fubui aye. Ɛkɛna fukĩĩ yatsa kĩ kɔbɛ̃ kpale fle a nyɛ ye Baguma kude, nnɛ́ boe kude kɔanɛ.

english_verse: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
translation_verse: Lɔkɔ pii bawɛ boe kado nɛ ɔhau kamɛ lɛmɛ baánya boe akũ, ade fɔkɔ fudi bakɛna boe adzuni basabasa lɛmɛ akpɔ láveli boe da.

english_verse: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
translation_verse: Bakesĩnɛ pii deĩ nɛ boe akũ, lɛmɛ budeĩ ɔkɔba odi kĩ yaálo kafɔnɛ boe, ade fɔkɔ fudi babɔa boe kamuni kapila boe lɛmɛ baákɔ boe.

english_verse: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
translation_verse: Nɛ boe nkpã flee kadzi kamɛ ni, wuda ado boe libe daa nɛ Yesu sũ, ayekĩ baamɔ ye nkpã nɛ boe sukɛna kĩ saaku a kamɛ.

english_verse: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ atsa kĩ wuda lamɛna adzuma kakɛna nɛ boe kamɛ, ade nkpã lɛmɛ lamɛna adzuma nɛ mi kamɛ a.

english_verse: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Nlɛ kɔdzɛla kitonɛkĩ ntɛ ndo.” Nɛ hũhũ mɔapɛ a katɛkado onukpɛ̃ ni, boe malo bɔalɛ kɔdzɛla kitonɛkĩ bɔtɛ budo.

english_verse: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
translation_verse: Bunyina kĩ Baguma a kĩ ɛdzɛla boe Ɔlate Yesu edzi nkpã a aadzɛla boe malo, lɔkɔa ɛkpã bɔmɔ mi lodo ɛnamɛna Yesu anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Bɔakɛna fukĩĩ bamɛ flee nɛ mi kalɛ sũ. Ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma sɛmɛkalɛ lamɛna bati pii sɛkɛ̃ kawo a, ade aye baakɔa kɔlɛkanyɛ mɔ mpaɛbɔ bɛkpa Baguma nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ kamɛ a. Bɔ́ana Obu Mɔ Kawɔlɛ Baũ.

english_verse: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ni, akpɔ láveli boe dadaada. Titɔ kĩ boe sukɛna kĩĩ lamɛna kavɔ̃ malo ni, boe hũhũ a kɔ̃ ana nkpã vɔɛ̃ luwi daa.

english_verse: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
translation_verse: Lubokamɔ kabii lukũ kĩĩ aakpa boe kukũkpalɛ kĩ fɔláana kawũna da, ade fɔ́aba lubo a kĩ bɔamɔ a.

english_verse: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Boe anɛ líteĩ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bɔamɔ a akũ, mbom futeĩ nɛ fukĩ bɔámɔ mɔ anɛ a akũ. Lɛtsa a kĩ bɔapuli kamɔ mɔ anɛ waa fɔkpa nɛ lɔkɔ lukũ kamɛ, lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔláapuli bɔmɔ́ mɔ anɛ a aana daa.

english_verse: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bunyina kĩ ntɛ biveli boe tite akũ lɛpɛ kĩ budzi nɛ de kamɛ, kĩ de nyɛ boe sukɛna a, Baguma aakpa boe kɔla vɔɛ̃ kĩ ye mɔawɛ eke nɛ ode budzi nɛ koe kamɛ daa.

english_verse: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
translation_verse: Finyaa bɔawɛya kake, ade boe kawɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ nyɛ kĩ Baguma ɛkɔa boe ode tɔtɔ sukɛna a esua boe akũ.

english_verse: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ɛkɔa se esua boe akũ ni, bɔláadzi ɔdzɔ.

english_verse: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
translation_verse: Kĩ budzi nɛ tite akũ lɛpɛ kĩĩ kamɛ sũ ni, bɔawɛya kake kĩ boe fɔlɔtsa tɛ́nɛ ɔlɔmɛ. Nnɛ́ kawɛ bɔawɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ tite akũ sukɛna kĩĩ nɛ boe akũ, mbom bɔawɛ kĩ Baguma ɛkɔa ode side a edo boe ayekĩ sukɛna a kĩ saaku a, aadani flefle sɛkɛna sikĩ saana nkpã.

english_verse: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
translation_verse: Baguma nyɛ oti a kĩ ɛmanɛ boe ɛkpa kadani kĩĩ, ade ɛkɔa ye Hũhũ ɛkpa boe ndɛ kasipi nɛ fɔtsa flee a kĩ ɛmanɛ eyi boe a akũ a.

english_verse: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔado boe akũ kɔbɛ̃ luwi daa. Bunyina kĩ ntɛ busila budzi nɛ tɔtɔ kĩ tɛnyɛ sukɛna tɔtɔ kamɛ kɔ̃ ni, bɔvɛlɛ akũ nɛ boe Ɔlate ɔwɛ̃.

english_verse: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ katɛkado bɔkɔa budzi nkpã, nnɛ́ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔamɔ mɔ anɛ a.

english_verse: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
translation_verse: Budeĩ ɔwɔlɛ, ade bɔawɛ mbom kĩ bɔanɛ nɛ boe sukɛna tɔtɔ kamɛ bɔnaadzi nɛ tɔtɔ kamɛ nɛ boe Ɔlate a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛbɛ̃ flee nyɛ kĩ, bɔawɛ kĩ bɔakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ laawo boe Ɔlate anɛ, ntɛ budzi nɛ boe tɔtɔ kamɛ nɛ awĩ ĩye bɔnaa ye sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ boe flee bɔ́anadza nɛ Kristo anɛ kamɛ, ɛdzɛmɔ boe. Odi nyɛ odi aatɛ kɔtɔkase nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛkɛna a onukpɛ̃, ntɛ fɔnyɛ tuwĩ ĩye tɔkpa a kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ ye katinya nkpã kadzi onukpɛ̃. Bɔtsã Kristo Akũ Bɔkɛna Tɔkɔba Mɔ Baguma.

english_verse: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
translation_verse: Bunyina ayekĩ kɛnɛ kĩ buvila boe Ɔlate, foesũ bɔabɔa mbɔdi kado bati bɛbã kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bivila ye. Baguma kɔ̃ nyina ayekĩ bɔla yededeede, ade matɛ kado kĩ mi malo minyina yĩ nɛ mi tɔwɔlɛ fii kamɛ a.

english_verse: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
translation_verse: Bɔlámɛna boe akũ kabuki kamu katsa mi, mbom bɔawɛ kĩ bɔakpa mi osi minyi lɛtsa sũ kĩ mita akũ nɛ boe akũ. Bɔkɛna foe lɔmɔ ayekĩ míapuli mina mbuayɛ mikpa bikĩ badã oti kutitse kapia nɛ ye akũ, kĩ nnɛ́ ye ɔbla badã a.

english_verse: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bati badi alɛ kĩ boe fɔlɔ kamɛ bu kade ni, Baguma sũ fubu kade. Lɛmɛ ntɛ boe fɔlɔ kamɛ waa yedede ni, mi kalɛ sũ fɔla lɔmɔ.

english_verse: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
translation_verse: Kristo ɔdɔ anya boe akũ, kitonɛkĩ finyaa bɔmɔ́ buyi ɔsũ kĩ oti mɔapɛ liku nɛ bati flee lɔlɔ, kĩ fɔatsa kĩ bati flee liku nɛ ye wuda a kamɛ.

english_verse: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
translation_verse: Eku nɛ bati flee lɔlɔ, ayekĩ bikĩ bidzi a lɛláabuki bidzi nkpã bɛkpa be akũ, mbom baadzi bɛkpa ye odi pɛ a kĩ eku, kĩ bɛdzɛla ye edzi nkpã nɛ be kalɛ sũ a.

english_verse: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
translation_verse: Foesũ kito finyaa kanaa ni, bɔláabuki bɔdzɛmɔ odima bɔkɔ ye kɔtɔ nɛ otidziwa kakɔna onukpɛ̃. Titɔ kĩ bɔdzɛmɔ Kristo tɔ̃ nɛ bati adzuni onukpɛ̃ malo ni, bubuki bɔákɛna foe.

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi kɛna ɔmɔa mɔ Kristo ni, ɛdani oti vɔɛ̃, oti kɔɛ a naa, ɔvɔɛ̃ lɛya.

english_verse: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
translation_verse: Baguma lɛkɛna fukĩĩ flee, ɛtsã Kristo akũ ɛdani boe, ye bakesĩnɛ a ɛkɛna bakɔba, ade ɛkpa boe adzuma kĩ boe malo bɔkɛna bati bɛbã ye bakɔba a.

english_verse: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
translation_verse: Boe fɔlɛtsa nyɛ kĩ Baguma tsɛ̃ Kristo akũ yalɔla bɛmɔ batidziwa flee kayite bɛdani ye bakɔba. Baguma tátɛ batidziwa tɔkpa eyi, mbom ɛkpa boe ɔlɛdo kĩ bude lɛtsã kĩ ɛlɔla bɛmɔ batidziwa flee kayite.

english_verse: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
translation_verse: Foesũ bido boe kĩ bɔlɛ kalɛ bɔkpa Kristo. Fɔkɛna ndɛ Baguma awɛ lamɛna bati kadɛ katsãmɛna boe akũ. Bɔake mi aduli nɛ Kristo dĩ akũ kĩ mifɔ̃ Baguma ɛdani mi ndɛ ye bakesĩnɛ a ɛkɛna ye bakɔba.

english_verse: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ Kristo tákɛna tɔkpa tidima malo ni, Baguma fɔ̃ ɛlɔ boe tɔkpa ɛnamɛna ayekĩ ntɛ bɔkɛna ɔmɔa mɔ ye ni, boe malo bɔ́anya bati kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ bɔmɔ Baguma bɔakɛna adzuma sũ ni, bɔake mi aduli kĩ mitafɔ̃ kɔnya a kĩ Baguma mɔ́ mi a lɛkɛna fɔtsa pupulidza.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
translation_verse: Mitse lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ, “Kĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ mmɔ mi kɔnya a, ntse mi, kĩ luwi a wo ɔtɔ kĩ matɛ mi nkpã a, mbumɔ mi.” Mitse! Ade finyaa kɛnɛ kĩ bɔatɛ Baguma kɔnyakamɔ a, nviã nyɛ luwi a kĩ ɛtɛ boe nkpã! Paulo Lubokamɔ.

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
translation_verse: Bɔáwɛ kĩ odima amɔ nfumusu nɛ boe adzuma akũ, foesũ bɔáwɛ kĩ bɔakɛna lɛtsa kpa lidima kĩ laasua odi osi.

english_verse: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
translation_verse: Mbom nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔakɛna kamɛ ni, bɔatsa kĩ Baguma basumunɛ bɔanɛ. Nɛ foesũ bɔado ɔwɔlɛ katɛ ɔhau, kamɔ lubo mɔ ohiã mɔ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ.

english_verse: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
translation_verse: Bibo boe, bido boe nɛ kɔla, bido ɔtutũ nɛ boe kamɛ, bɛfɔ̃ bɔkɛna adzuma bɔtɛ̃ ɔbɛ̃, ade budzi tida katawa mɔ katanya malo a.

english_verse: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ ni, bɔbɔa mbɔdi kĩ bɔanya bati kpalɛwa nɛ Baguma anɛ. Bɔkɔa boe kadikanyi, ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ mɔ boe sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna bɔatsa kĩ Baguma basumunɛ bɔanɛ. Fukĩ fubu nyɛ kĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔ ɔdɔ dzununuunu,

english_verse: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
translation_verse: boe ɔnɔkɔali ɔlɛdo a, mɔ Baguma kɔbɛ̃ abumɔ boe. Bɔnɛɛ kadzi kpĩĩ nɛ Baguma anɛ a ndɛ boe kawũkanɔ fɔtsa kĩ bɔkɔa bɔanɔ kawũ, ade bɔakɔa foe bɔapili boe lɔlɔ lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
translation_verse: Bakpa boe obu, badi asupa boe, badi avũna boe badi lɛmɛ amu boe. Badi adã boe ndɛ bavãlɛvã bɔanɛ, lɛmɛ bɔalɛ ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a.

english_verse: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
translation_verse: Bakɛna boe ndɛ bati kĩ binyína, lɛmɛ bati flee nyina boe. Babu boe ndɛ bati kĩ bɛlá nkpã lɛmɛ budeĩ nkpã. Titɔ kĩ bavɛlɛ boe sɔtɔ malo ni, bɛtákɔ boe,

english_verse: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
translation_verse: titɔ kĩ bido boe nɛ ɔbɔdɔ kamɛ malo ni, bɔamɔ kawɔlɛ luwi daa. Bɔadã ndɛ ahiãfɔ lɛmɛ bɔafɔ̃ bati pii akɛna asikafɔ. Bɔkɛna ndɛ bɔlá lɛtsama lɛmɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, ade boe deĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a.

english_verse: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Korintofɔ, bubinya boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ mɔ ɔdɔ bɔkpa mi, ade bubi mi ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa a yededeede.

english_verse: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ boe lɛtányɛ boe ɔdɔ kanya, mbom mi lɔkɔa mi ɔdɔ mikɔla boe.

english_verse: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
translation_verse: Foesũ finyaa mabi mi tuwuli ndɛ yĩ babi aye. Minyɛ mi tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ fɔlɛtsa mitsa boe ndɛ ayekĩ boe malo bɔakɛna kakpa mi a. Mitakɛna Ɔmɔa Mɔ Bikĩ Bɛtátɛ Bido a Nɛ Lɛtsama Kamɛ.

english_verse: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
translation_verse: Mitakɛna ɔmɔa mɔ bikĩ bɛtátɛ bido a. Tuwĩ kakɛna mɔ tɔkpa kakɛna aapuli tɛkpɔta tɛkɛna lɛtsa mɔa mbɔɛɛ? Nde ɔhaĩ mɔ ɔtũ aapuli bidzi bɛkpɔta?

english_verse: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
translation_verse: Nde Kristo mɔ Belial baviɔ̃ a aapuli bɛkɛna onukpɛ̃ mɔa? Litsa nyɛ lɛtsa mɔa kĩ lideĩ nɛ ɔtɛ odonɛ mɔ ekĩ ɛtátɛ edo kayite?

english_verse: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
translation_verse: Nde Baguma asɔli kɔla mɔ kuviɔ̃la aapuli bɛkɛna ɔmɔa? Kitonɛkĩ boe nyɛ Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a asɔli fɔla! Baguma mɔawɛ lɛ kĩ, “Maake tɔtɔ nɛ yĩ bati sɛkɛ̃, lɔkɔa maadzi nɛ be kamɛ. Maanya be Baguma lɔkɔa bɛnya yĩ bati.”

english_verse: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
translation_verse: Foesũ boe Ɔlate a lɛ kĩ, “Midzakũ nɛ be sɛkɛ̃, minyɛ mi akũ nɛ be kamɛ. Mitakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ tákpalɛ, lɔkɔa maatɛ mi atuu.

english_verse: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Maanya mi ɔlɛga ade mi míanya yĩ babinyole mɔ babitsole a. Ade aye boe Ɔlate Mɔ Kɔbɛ̃ Flee a lɛ a.”

english_verse: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, ayekĩ bɛkpa boe kɔnɛte nɛ fukĩĩ flee akũ sũ ni, mifɔ̃ bɔkpalɛ boe akũ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lado sukɛna mɔ hũhũ efĩĩ a kamɛ. Mifɔ̃ boe kadzi kamɛ flee kɛdã yedede nɛ Baguma kavila kamɛ. Paulo Mɔ́ Kawɔlɛ Nɛ Ayekĩ Korintofɔ Dani Tɔwɔlɛ Kamɛ Sũ.

english_verse: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
translation_verse: Minyɛ mi ɔdɔ mitsa boe. Bɔtáfum odima, bɔtábula odima kadzi kamɛ kade, ade bɔtásisi odima lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
translation_verse: Ntálɛ fukĩĩ kĩ makɔ mi kɔtɔ, kitonɛkĩ ndɛ ayekĩ nlɛ ĩnyamɛna lɔtɔ a, mitɛ kɔya mikpa boe paa kĩ ntɛ fɔnyɛ wuda malo ni, bɔmɔ mi aaku, ade ntɛ fɔnyɛ nkpã lɛmɛ ni, bɔmɔ mi aadzi nkpã.

english_verse: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mi ndo paa ade mapia akũ nɛ mi akũ lɛmɛ a. Ɔwɔlɛ ka edzi yĩ. Nɛ boe lubo kamɔ flee kamɛ ni, mbuki ndeĩ ɔwɔlɛ paa, ade kawɔlɛ ade yĩ paa a.

english_verse: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
translation_verse: Kĩ bɔyawo Makedonia malo ni, bɔtána kawɛya kadima. Bɔmɔ́ lubo nɛ kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ, bati do boe tɛdzɛ ade libe na nɛ boe kamɛ a.

english_verse: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma kĩ yado bikĩ akpɔ veli be kɔbɛ̃ a, do boe ɔwɔlɛ nɛ Tito kaya kamɛ.

english_verse: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ Tito kaya a odi lido boe ɔwɔlɛ, mbom ye adansiɛ a kĩ edi nɛ mi akũ kĩ mido ye ɔwɔlɛ a lɛmɛ bu. Ebi boe ayekĩ miwɛ paa kĩ miamɔ yĩ, ayekĩ fuwo mi kalɔɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛya a akũ, mɔ ayekĩ mimanɛ akũ midzi kĩ míadza nɛ yĩ liti mipili yĩ lɔlɔ. Nɛ foesũ finyaa mamɔ kawɔlɛ kaba kɔkɔɛ a.

english_verse: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ ovoli a kĩ ntsɛlɛ mma mi a fɔ̃ miwo ɔbɔdɔ malo ni, fɔtáwo yĩ kalɔɛ kĩ ntsɛlɛ ye. Fɔ́apuli fuwo yĩ kalɔɛ kitonɛkĩ mmɔ́ kĩ lɔkɔ lukũ odi kamɛ mɔ miwo ɔbɔdɔ.

english_verse: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa, kawɔlɛ ade yĩ, nnɛ́ kĩ ndo mi ɔbɔdɔ a sũ, mbom mi ɔbɔdɔ kawo a fɔ̃ midani mi kadzi kamɛ. Baguma kawɛ fɔanɛ kĩ miwo ɔbɔdɔ, foesũ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔkɛna a nyɛ nfasɔ mbom fɔkpa mi.

english_verse: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ɔbɔdɔ kawo a kĩ fɔnyɛ Baguma kawɛ afɔ̃ oti adani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, ade fɔakpã ye kanamɛna daa nkpã a kamɛ a. Fukĩnaa láwo kalɔɛ! Lɛmɛ ntɛ oti wo ɔbɔdɔ nɛ katinya kamɛ kawɛ onukpɛ̃ kɔ̃ ni, wuda fɔavɛlɛ kayamɛna.

english_verse: Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter.
translation_verse: Midã ayekĩ ɔbɔdɔ kĩĩ odu, kĩ Baguma awɛ a akɛna adzuma nɛ mi kamɛ! Fɔfɔ̃ miayili akũ kakɛna fɔtsa wĩ, ade fɔfɔ̃ miakpalɛ mi akũ a. Fɔfɔ̃ miana kalɔkpa nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔtákpɔla akũ, fɔfɔ̃ miavila ade fɔfɔ̃ miadã anɛ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ a. Fɔfɔ̃ miana ovio nɛ fɔtsa kakɛna kamɛ, ade miawɛ kĩ bɛvɛlɛ oti a kĩ ɛkɛna tɔkpa a sɔtɔ a. Nɛ foe flee kamɛ ni, minyɛ mi akũ miatsa kĩ mitádi mfumsu nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kamɛ.

english_verse: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
translation_verse: Foesũ titɔ kĩ ntsɛlɛ ovoli a na malo ni, nnɛ́ oti a kĩ efum, ĩye ekĩ ye bifum a sũ ntsɛlɛ ye. Mbom ntsɛlɛ ye matsa ayekĩ miakɔa mi akũ flee kakpa boe a nɛ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ bɔkado ɔwɔlɛ a. Nnɛ́ ɔwɔlɛ kado odi bɔna, kawɔlɛ de boe baũ nɛ kawɔlɛ a kĩ Tito mɔ́ nɛ mi akũ a sũ, kitonɛkĩ ebi boe ayekĩ mi flee mifɔ̃ ɔwɔlɛ ka edzi ye a.

english_verse: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
translation_verse: Nkɔa mi mpia ntsa ye, ade mitádo yĩ kɔnyɔã lɛmɛ a. Bubi mi ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a, ade ayea kapia a kĩ bupia bɔtsa Tito a lɛmɛ yanya ɔnɔkɔali a.

english_verse: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
translation_verse: Ye ɔdɔ nɛ mi akũ ade nɛ ode kabu, ntɛ ete anɛ nɛ ayekĩ mi flee miwɛ kĩ míakɛna nɛ ye fɔtsa katsa akũ, mɔ ayekĩ mitɛ ye atuu nɛ obu mɔ kukũtila kamɛ a.

english_verse: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
translation_verse: Kawɔlɛ ade yĩ, kitonɛkĩ maapuli nkɔa anɛ nte nɛ mi akũ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ.

english_verse: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bɔawɛ kĩ minyi lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma tsãmɛna ye kɔnya kamɔ kamɛ ɛkɛna nɛ Makedonia tɛsɔli a kamɛ.

english_verse: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
translation_verse: Bɛsɔ be bɛdã nɛ lubo kamɔ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kamɛ, lɛmɛ fude be kawɔlɛ baũ, foesũ titɔ kĩ ahiãfɔ banɛ malo ni, bite fɔtsa mɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
translation_verse: Maapuli nlɛ pipiipi kĩ bite fɔtsa ndɛ ayekĩ baapuli, malo kɔlaa ni, bite fɔba be kɔbɛ̃. Nɛ be mɔawɛ kawɛ onukpɛ̃ bite fɔtsa faa.

english_verse: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
translation_verse: Bike boe aduli mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ bɔkpa be osi be malo bibumɛna Baguma bade a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yudea bakɛna adzuma a.

english_verse: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
translation_verse: Fukĩ bɛkɛna kĩĩ bɛ̃ lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ bute anɛ. Lɛtsa lɛtɔnyade nyɛ kĩ bɛkɔa be akũ bɛkpa boe Ɔlate, ade ke liti a, bɛkɔa be akũ bɛkpa boe nɛ Baguma kawɛ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
translation_verse: Foesũ budo Tito, kĩ ye liyi adzuma kĩĩ kayɔ a kɔbɛ̃, kĩ ɛnaa lɔtɔ mɔ ye adzuma, lɔkɔa ebumɛna mi miwũna ɔdɔ adzuma titiliwu kĩĩ.

english_verse: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
translation_verse: Finyaa minyɛ bati kĩ bɛnyaa lɔtɔ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ. Mi katɛkado, mi kɔdzɛla kalɛ, mɔ mi kadikanyi awo anɛ. Miayili akũ nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kamɛ ade miawɛ boe kalɛ a. Nɛ foesũ bɔawɛ kĩ minya lɔtɔ nɛ kɔnyakamɔ adzuma kĩĩ lɛmɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ mbla adima masui nkpa mi, mbom nɛ ayekĩ bati bɛbã lamɛna akũ kayili kĩ bakpa kabumɛna sũ ni, mawɛ ĩnyi ntɛ mi ɔdɔ a kĩ mideĩ a lɛmɛ kpɔla.

english_verse: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
translation_verse: Minyina boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo kɔnya kamɔ a kĩ edeĩ ɛkpa boe, ade titɔ kĩ ɛnyɛ osikani malo ni, ɛkɛna ye akũ ndɛ ohiãni nɛ mi kalɛ sũ, ayekĩ yaakɛna boe asikafɔ nɛ ye tihiã a kamɛ.

english_verse: A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice.
translation_verse: Yĩ kakɔna nyɛ kĩ fɔnɛnɛ kĩ miwũna adzuma a kĩ miyi kayɔ katukɔɛ a. Mi nyɛ́ bati batɔnyade kĩ biyi kayɔ, ade minya batɔnyade malo kĩ bitunɔ kĩ baakpa a.

english_verse: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
translation_verse: Finyaa mikɛna adzuma a minamɛna lɔtɔ lɔkɔa miwũna ye! Miyili akũ miwũna ye ndɛ ayekĩ mikɔa miyi ye kayɔ a. Mikɔa futetsa a kĩ mideĩ a mikɛna adzuma a.

english_verse: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
translation_verse: Ntɛ futo odi ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ paa kĩ yaate fɔtsa ni, Baguma aatɛ fukĩ ɛna ɛkpa a, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ fukĩ ɛlá a.

english_verse: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
translation_verse: Nlámɛna bati bɛbã fɔlɔtsa akũ katinya kĩ makɔa ndo mi fude ɔlɔmɛ,

english_verse: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
translation_verse: lɛmɛ ayekĩ mideĩ fɔtsa pii nɛ lɔkɔ kĩĩ kamɛ sũ ni, fɔnɛnɛ kĩ miabumɔ bikĩ fusĩ be a. Ke liti ntɛ kɛyaasĩ mi, mɔ bideĩ fɔtsa pii ni, baabumɛna mi. Nɛ osi kĩĩ akũ ni, mi akũ kabumɛna a aaketi.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
translation_verse: Bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Ekĩ ɛkpɔta fɔtsa beblebee tána fublusu, ade ekĩ ɛkpɔta kabii lɛmɛ tásĩ lɛtsama a.” Bido Tito Mɔ Ye Bakɔba Nɛ Korinto.

english_verse: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
translation_verse: Bɔanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ kĩ ɛfɔ̃ Tito bɔa mbɔdi yabumɔ mi ndɛ ayekĩ boe malo bɔawɛ kĩ bɔakpa mi kabumɛna.

english_verse: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
translation_verse: Kĩ bubi ye kĩ ɛya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ a, etunɔ mɔ kawɔlɛ. Lɛmɛ fɔnyɛ ye kawɛ kɔkɔɛ kĩ yaya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
translation_verse: Budo bɛmɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃nyole, kĩ tɛsɔli bati a flee akpa obu paa nɛ ye Baguma fɔlɛtsa kalɛ kamɛ a kĩ etomɔ ye.

english_verse: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ libu nyɛ kĩ tɛsɔli a lɛnyɛ ye, bitunɔ lɛmɛ kĩ ɛtsã etomɔ boe bɔkɛna ɔdɔ adzuma a fudo boe Ɔlate obu, lɔkɔa fɔtsa lɛmɛ kĩ bɔwɛ kĩ bɔakpa bati bɛbã kabumɛna.

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
translation_verse: Bɔadã kadã wĩ kĩ odima lɛláayavia boe kadi etomɛna ayekĩ bɔakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ futetsa kĩĩ akũ adzuma.

english_verse: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
translation_verse: Boe botaɛ nyɛ kĩ bɔakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, nnɛ́ nɛ boe Ɔlate odi anɛ, mbom mɔ batidziwa lɛmɛ anɛ.

english_verse: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
translation_verse: Foesũ budo boe ɔwaɛ̃ kĩ etomɔ be. Bɔsɔ ye bɔdã lɔkɔ pii ade bɔmɔ́ kĩ yabɔa mbɔdi nɛ fɔtsa pii kakɛna kamɛ a. Ade finyaa, kĩ ɛtɛ mi edo paa sũ ni, akũ asĩ ye paa kĩ yayaakpa mi kabumɛna.

english_verse: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
translation_verse: Tito kɔ̃ ni, ɛnyɛ yĩ ɔkɔba paa kĩ bɔakɛna adzuma bubumɔ mi. Boe bawaɛ̃nyole bɛbã a kĩ bitomɔ ye a kɔ̃ ni, ade be dzɛ̃ nɛ tɛsɔli a kadzakɔ̃ bɛkɔa kamu bakpa Kristo a.

english_verse: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
translation_verse: Minyɛ mi ɔdɔ mitsa be, ayekĩ tɛsɔli a flee aatɛ bido, binyi lɛmɛ kĩ boe kukũpia nɛ mi akũ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali.

english_verse: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
translation_verse: Fɔtásĩ kɔlaa kĩ matsɛlɛ mi ovoli nɛ futetsa a kĩ miakɔa kama Baguma bade a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Yudea tite akũ a akũ.

english_verse: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina kĩ miawɛ kĩ miakpa kabumɛna, foesũ nkɔa mi mpia ntsa bati a kĩ bideĩ nɛ Makedonia tite akũ a. Nlɛ kĩ, “Boe bawaɛ̃ nɛ Akaia tite akũ a manɛ akũ kĩ bakpa kabumɛna anɛ katukɔɛ.” Mi mbɔdi nɛ foe akũ fɔ̃ fi wo nɛ be sɛkɛ mɔa pii kamɛ.

english_verse: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ mado boe bawaɛ̃ kĩĩ nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ baamɔ kĩ boe kukũpia nɛ mi akũ nɛ futetsa akũ a lɛnyɛ́ kɔdzɛla pupulidza. Lɛmɛ ndɛ ayekĩ mbi be kĩ mimanɛ akũ mɔ mi fubumɛnatsa a, mawɛ kĩ mina nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ lɔmɔ.

english_verse: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ Makedoniafɔ aatomɔ yĩ bɛyaamɔ kĩ mitámanɛ akũ mikpa futetsa a kɔ̃ ni, kɔnyɔã aawo boe, lɛmɛ kɔnyɔã baũ aawo mi kɔba, kitonɛkĩ baakɔna kĩ bɔtɛ́nɛ anɛ kate nɛ mi akũ.

english_verse: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
translation_verse: Foesũ nkɔna kĩ fusĩ kĩ mapiã boe bawaɛ̃ kĩĩ bɛnya yĩ lɔtɔ lɔkɔa bɛyaamanɛ futetsa a kĩ milɛ miyi kĩ míakpa a. Futetsa kĩĩ aakpɔta ntɛ ĩya, ade fɔ́atsa kĩ mi ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ futo sũ mikpa, fɔnyɛ́ fukĩ bɛhɛ mi kĩ mikpa. Baguma Awɛ Oti Kĩ Yate Fɔtsa Kĩ Futo Ye Ɔwɔlɛ Kamɛ.

english_verse: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
translation_verse: Mite anɛ kĩ oti kĩ edu funitsã kabii ni, funitsã sukuloo yaana, ekĩ edu funitsã pii aanyɛ funitsã baũ.

english_verse: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
translation_verse: Foesũ odi nyɛ odi ɛkpa ndɛ ayekĩ ɛwɛ futo ye kamɛ, ɛtakɛna foe mɔ kalɔɛ ĩye kĩ fɔnyɛ ɔhɛ kĩ ɛkɛna sũ, kitonɛkĩ Baguma awɛ oti a kĩ yakpa fɔtsa mɔ kawɔlɛ.

english_verse: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
translation_verse: Baguma aapuli ɛkpa mi fɔtsa ɛba fukĩ misĩ, ayekĩ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ míana fɔtsa a kĩ misĩ mikpa mi akũ a, mina fublusu mikɔa mikɛna tudzuma wĩ tidi nyɛ tidi.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
translation_verse: Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Yakpa ahiãfɔ fɔtsa faa ade ye sɛmɛ kalɛ kakɛna waa daa a.”

english_verse: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
translation_verse: Baguma kĩ yakpa okuani fudutsa mɔ funitsã kĩ yanya, aakpa mi fudutsa flee a kĩ misĩ a, ɛfɔ̃ fubo anɛ fɔkpa mi funitsã beblebee ayekĩ míakɔa mikɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ tudzuma pii.

english_verse: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
translation_verse: Baguma aafɔ̃ mina akũ fɔtsa daa, mipuli mikakɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ, ayekĩ bati pii aanyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ futetsa a kĩ bɛtɛ bitomɛna boe sɛkɛ̃ a sũ.

english_verse: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kabumɛna a kĩ mikpa a ni, nnɛ́ Baguma bati a odi kasĩ onukpɛ̃ mipi, mbom fɔfɔ̃ bati pii anyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ paa.

english_verse: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
translation_verse: Lɛtsa a kĩ laato mi ɔsum kĩĩ kamɛ aafɔ̃ bati pii limu Baguma, kitonɛkĩ mitɛ Kristo Kalɛ Wĩ a mido, mikɛna nɛ ke akũ, ade mikɛna sɛmɛ kalɛ kĩ mite fɔtsa mikpa be mɔ bati bɛbã lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
translation_verse: Foesũ baakɔa ɔdɔ kĩ eto ɔwɔlɛ dzununuunu kamɛ bɛbɔa mpaɛ bɛkpa mi, kitonɛkĩ Baguma mɔ́ mi kɔnya pii.

english_verse: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bɔnyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ ye futetsa a kĩ ɛkpa boe, kĩ bɔláapuli bɔnyɛ foe kayɔ sũ a!

english_verse: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
translation_verse: Yĩ Paulo mɔawɛ lamɛna mi aduli kake. Ade yĩ bati alɛ kĩ ĩnyɛ oti dɔwa ntɛ ndeĩ nɛ mi kamɛ, lɛmɛ ntɛ nláa mi sɛkɛ̃ kɔ̃ ni, ndeĩ awũni. Ntsɛ̃ Kristo akũ kadɔla mɔ ye sɛmɛ kalɛ kamɛ,

english_verse: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
translation_verse: make mi aduli kĩ ntɛ ĩya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ ni, mitafɔ̃ ĩyaana awũni, kitonɛkĩ matɛ kado kĩ maapuli nna awũni paa nɛ bikĩ balɛ kĩ katinya liti katomɛna sũ bɔakɛna adzuma kĩĩ akũ a.

english_verse: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ katinya kamɛ budzi, lɛmɛ bɔánɔ kawũ nɛ katinya kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
translation_verse: Kawũkanɔ fɔtsa a kĩ bɔnɛɛ bɔanɔ a lɛnyɛ́ katinya kamɛ fude, mbom fɔnyɛ Baguma fude a kĩ fudeĩ kɔbɛ̃, kĩ bɔkɔa bɔabubuli okesĩnɛ fudzikɔ̃ mɔ ɔsĩ a.

english_verse: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
translation_verse: Bɔabula fɔvã kama fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bati avɛlɛ a kade. Bɔawɔla lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ latsa kapia kĩ fɔasua Baguma kanyi osi. Bɔapi adzuni adi nyɛ adi ndɛ ayekĩ bapili bati nɛ kawũ kamɛ kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ Kristo kawɛ akũ.

english_verse: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mikɔa mi akũ flee mikpa boe, mɔ miatse boe ɔlɛdo ni, boe malo bɔmanɛ akũ bɔkpa kĩ bɔ́avɛlɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yakɛna atɔ ɔsĩ a sɔtɔ.

english_verse: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
translation_verse: Miadã oti anɛ kakɛna fɔtsa. Odi deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ kĩ yabu ye akũ kĩ ɛnyɛ Kristo ode mbɔɛɛ? Ntɛ aye ni, mifɔ̃ owula ebuki ɛkɔna ye akũ ɛdã, kitonɛkĩ ndɛ ayekĩ ɛnyɛ Kristo ode a, ade aye boe malo bɔnyɛ Kristo bade a.

english_verse: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kɔnyɔã lákɔ yĩ, ntɛ ntɛ́nɛ kapia beblebee nɛ otumi a kĩ boe Ɔlate kpa boe a sũ. Ɛkpa boe otumi kĩ bɔkɔa bɔdɔɔla mi mibo anɛ, nnɛ́ kĩ bɔkɔa bubula mi kade.

english_verse: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
translation_verse: Ntáwɛ kĩ fɔkɛna mi ndɛ mado mi libe mɔ tuvoli kĩ ntsɛlɛ a.

english_verse: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
translation_verse: Badi aalɛ kĩ, “Paulo tuvoli kamɛ fɔlɛtsa deĩ ɔsĩ, fudeĩ kɔbɛ̃ malo, lɛmɛ ntɛ ye mɔawɛ a edeĩ nɛ boe sɛkɛ̃ kɔ̃ ni, ɛlá ɔsĩ ade ye kɔdzɛla kalɛ lɛmɛ lányɛnɛ bati akũ!”

english_verse: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
translation_verse: Bati a na odu binu foe kayɔ, kĩ kavɛmɛna kadima lɛláa lɛtsa a kĩ bɔatsɛlɛ kado nɛ boe tuvoli kamɛ ntɛ budeĩ mɔ lugo, mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔ́akɛna ntɛ budeĩ nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ a kamɛ.

english_verse: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe kɔ̃ ni, boe kukũpia lɛláanyina ɔbɛ̃. Mbom kɔ́ana nɛ awã kĩ Baguma yi ɛkpa nɛ boe adzuma kakɛna kamɛ, ade boe adzuma nɛ mi kamɛ lɛmɛ bu a.

english_verse: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ mideĩ nɛ kakɔ a kĩ Baguma tsua ɛkpa boe a kamɛ sũ ni, kĩ bɔkɔa Kalɛ Wĩ nɛ Kristo akũ a bɔama mi a, bɔtányina bɔnaa tɛbɛ̃ a liti.

english_verse: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔápia nɛ adzuma a kĩ bati bɛbã kɛna binyina tɛbɛ̃ a kĩ Baguma yi ɛkpa boe a akũ. Mbom bɔadã anɛ kĩ mi katɛkado aabo anɛ, lɔkɔa bɔ́apuli bɔkɛna adzuma baũ bɔnaa lɔtɔ nɛ mi kamɛ, nɛ awã kĩ Baguma kpa boe ekpula a.

english_verse: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bɔ́apuli bunyina mi bɔnaade lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a akũ nɛ fude bɛbã kamɛ, ade fɔláasĩ kĩ bɔapia nɛ adzuma a kĩ bati bɛbã kɛna kɔkɔɛ nɛ be kakɔ akũ a.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ odi awɛ epia ni, kɛnɛ kĩ epia nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate kɛna a akũ.”

english_verse: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ oti a kĩ yamu ye mɔawɛ akũ a nyɛ oti wĩ, mbom oti a kĩ boe Ɔlate amu nyɛ oti wĩ.

english_verse: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
translation_verse: Manɔ lineke nɛ mi lɔlɔ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma lɛmɛ anɔ lineke a. Mila ndɛ oyitsɔ̃ kĩ enyína onyole kĩ ntinya nkpa onyole mɔapɛ odi kĩ ɛnyɛ Kristo mɔawɛ a.

english_verse: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
translation_verse: Mavila kĩ bati aabula mi adzuni kade ndɛ ayekĩ kɔɛ kɔa tɛdake kɔkɛ Eva, kĩ miláakɔa tɔwɔlɛ kpalɛwa misum Kristo ndɛ ayekĩ mikakɛna a.

english_verse: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ miatɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yade lɛtsã nɛ Yesu bɛbã kĩ nnɛ́ ye akũ bude lɛtsã bɔkpa mi a mɔ kawɔlɛ mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ. Ade ke miatɛ hũhũ mɔ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔvɛmɛna kɔlaa nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa mɔ Kalɛ Wĩ a kĩ mitɛ mitomɛna boe sɛkɛ̃ a lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
translation_verse: Maábu kĩ mi fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kĩ miakɔna kĩ bɛnyɛ bati titiliwu badi odu a tɛ kɔya bɛba yĩ nɛ osi odima akũ.

english_verse: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
translation_verse: Ɔsa ĩnyɛ oti kĩ yaápuli kalɛ kɔdzɛla kalɛ wĩ, lɛmɛ mabi mi pipiipi kĩ nnɛ́ mɔ kadikanyi kamɛ. Bɔnyɛ foe bɔtsa mi pefee nɛ lɔkɔ a flee kamɛ nɛ osi nyɛ osi akũ.

english_verse: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
translation_verse: Kĩ ĩyalɛ Baguma Kalɛ Wĩ a ntsa mi a, ntátɛ lɛtsama ntomɛna mi sɛkɛ̃. Lɛmɛ ndɔla yĩ akũ ayekĩ baadɛla mi nɛ ode. Tɔkpa tanɛ kĩ nkɛna mbɔɛɛ?

english_verse: I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you.
translation_verse: Kĩ nkakɛna adzuma nɛ mi kamɛ a, tɛsɔli bɛbã bati lise yĩ kɔtɔ. Bɔ́abɔɛ foe kĩ nkaye be kĩ makɔa mbumɛna mi.

english_verse: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
translation_verse: Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ nna nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃ a, ntáha mi mɔ kabumɛna ntɛ nsĩ sika malo. Boe bawaɛ̃ batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bito Makedonia bɛya a lɛma yĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lisĩ yĩ. Ndɛ ayekĩ fɔna kɔkɔɛ a ade aye fɔ́ana nɛ kunya kamɛ lɛmɛ a, kĩ nláakɔa yĩ akũ nkɛna fɔlɔtsa nte nɛ mi akũ da.

english_verse: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
translation_verse: Kĩ Kristo ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ a deĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ sũ ni, lɛtsama lɛláasua yĩ osi nɛ kakɔma, kĩ ntapia nɛ fukĩĩ akũ nɛ Akaia tite flee akũ.

english_verse: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
translation_verse: Kalɛ nlɛ fukĩĩ kitonɛkĩ maáwɛ mi kalɛ sũ mbɔɛɛ? Baguma nyina kĩ mawɛ mi kalɛ!

english_verse: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
translation_verse: Maakakɛna lɛtsa a kĩ makɛna finyaa, kĩ nláakɔa yĩ fɔlɔtsa nte nɛ mi akũ, ayekĩ fɔtɔ banyanɛ babɛbã a lɛláana osi bipia akũ bɛlɛ kĩ bɔmɔ be akɛna adzuma nɛ osi mɔapɛ akũ.

english_verse: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
translation_verse: Bati a na lɛnyɛ́ fɔtɔ banyanɛ wĩ, mbom bɛnyɛ fɔvã fɔtɔ banyanɛ kĩ bakɛ bati nɛ be adzuma a kĩ bakɛna a kamɛ. Bakɛna be akũ ndɛ Kristo fɔtɔ banyanɛ mɔawɛ paa banɛ.

english_verse: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ tákɛna wãwã, kitonɛkĩ Abonsam malo apuli kadani ye akũ kadã ndɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ aye nɛ ɔhaĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
translation_verse: Foesũ fɔnyɛ́ lɛtsa baũ lidima ntɛ ye basumunɛ lɛmɛ dani be akũ bɛkɛna ndɛ Baguma basumunɛ kĩ bakɛna fɔtsa nɛ osi kpĩĩ akũ. Nɛ ke kawũna a, baana lɛtsa a kĩ be lɛkɛnana fɛta a pɛpɛɛpɛ. Lubo a Kĩ Paulo Mɔ Ndɛ Fɔtɔ Ɔnyanɛ Aye.

english_verse: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
translation_verse: Mbuki malɛ kĩ odima ɛtakɔna kĩ ndzimbi. Lɛmɛ ntɛ mibu yĩ lɔmɔ kɔ̃ ni, mitɛ yĩ ndɛ odzimbifɔ aye lɔkɔa yĩ malo mia sukuloo.

english_verse: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali kalɛ kĩ malɛ kĩĩ lɛnyɛ́ lɛtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate aawɛ kĩ nlɛ. Nɛ kukũpia fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ ni, malɛ kɔdzɛla koũ ndɛ odzimbifɔ aye.

english_verse: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ayekĩ bati pii apia akũ nɛ katinya kamɛ fɔtsa akũ sũ ni, yĩ malo maakɔa foe mpia.

english_verse: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
translation_verse: Mi mɔawɛ a minyɛ bati kĩ binyina kadi paa, foesũ miana ɔwɔlɛ katɛ bikĩ bidzimbi a nɛ kawɔlɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi akpã mi nɛ ɔhɛ kamɛ, ĩye yasisi mi, ĩye yadã mi nɛ kayɔ kayɔ ĩye yabalɛ mi nɛ anɛ kamɛ malo ni, miatɛ foe mɔ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ.

english_verse: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
translation_verse: Kɔnyɔã fɔanɛ fɔkpa yĩ kĩ bɔtána ɔwɔlɛ bɔkɛna fukĩna bamɛ odu. Mbɔɛɛ fɔfɛta kĩ bɔkɛna lɔmɔ? Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi do ɔwɔlɛ kĩ yapia nɛ litsedi akũ ni, yĩ malo maado ɔwɔlɛ ndɛ ye aye, lɛmɛ finyaa malɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ odzimbifɔ aye.

english_verse: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
translation_verse: Balɛ kĩ Hebrifɔ banɛ. Oo, yĩ malo Hebrini manɛ. Balɛ kĩ Israelfɔ banɛ. Yĩ malo Israelni manɛ. Balɛ kĩ Abraham babi mɔ bawa banɛ. Yĩ malo nto Abraham kasinu a kamɛ.

english_verse: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
translation_verse: Balɛ kĩ Kristo basumunɛ banɛ. Malɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ ɔbɔndamfɔ manɛ, lɛmɛ ĩnyɛ ɔsumunɛ wĩ mbɛ̃ be! Nkɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ mba be, bido yĩ nɛ kɔla mba be, bibo yĩ mɔ kutsei fɔba be, ade nkawo nɛ wuda awɛ kamɛ mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ.

english_verse: Five times my own people gave me
translation_verse: Titi elo mɔa kĩ Yudafɔ bo yĩ kutsei taalɛ-avedi.

english_verse: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
translation_verse: Titi ɛlalɛ kĩ Romafɔ bo yĩ mɔ mpile ade bɛnɔ yĩ boe deedi a. Ndzi nɛ ɔkɔlɔ kamɛ titi ɛlalɛ kĩ ɔwɛ bubuli ye, luwi mɔa mɔ lɛnyɛ flee nkahɔ nɛ mui anɛ.

english_verse: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nɛ yĩ tisi pii katsã kamɛ ni, tɔwɔ kayili, bayekpanɛ kado yĩ libe ade ayea Yudafɔ mɔ bikĩ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ kawɛ kĩ bakɔ yĩ a. Nna nɛ nkpã mɔ wuda kayite nɛ tɛmatɔ tɛkpale tɛkpale kamɛ, nɛ fupo kamɛ, nɛ mpo anɛ mɔ fɔvã lɛtsã badenɛ awɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
translation_verse: Ĩyili akũ nkɛna adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ, fɔkɔ fudi ndzi kĩ maána tida nwa. Kɔlɛ mɔ ɔmɔado kakɔ yĩ, fɔkɔ fudi ndzi maána funitsã ĩnya nwĩ, ade maána kawakɔ̃ ĩye tɛtadiɛ kĩ mado a.

english_verse: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ flee liti ni, luwi nyɛ luwi tɛsɔli a akũ kakɔna kɛna fɔlɔtsa kĩ futeĩ nɛ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ.

english_verse: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
translation_verse: Ntɛ akpɔ veli odi nɛ ye katɛkado kamɛ ni, akpɔ aveli yĩ kaba. Ntɛ bɛfɔ̃ odi wo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ ni, mana foe akũ kalɔkpa baũ.

english_verse: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kɛnɛ kĩ mpia akũ ni, maapia nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔatsa kĩ nlá kɔbɛ̃ a akũ.

english_verse: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
translation_verse: Baguma, kĩ ɛnyɛ boe Ɔlate Kristo Ɔlɛga, kĩ bamu ye dĩ daa a, nyina kĩ nnɛ́ fɔvã mama.

english_verse: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
translation_verse: Kĩ nna nɛ Damasko a, Lɛgã Areta fɔ̃ bikĩ badã ɔmatɔ a akũ a dza nɛ ɔmatɔ a seebi kɔbã onukpɛ̃ kĩ bipi yĩ.

english_verse: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bati do yĩ nɛ ɔtsɛtsɛ kamɛ bɛtsãmɛna nɛ seebi nfansili kamɛ bɛkamɛna bɛdzɛla, ade nlo nfɔnɛ ye a.

english_verse: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ nfasɔ adima lɛláa kapia kamɛ malo ni, kɛnɛ kĩ mpia akũ. Lɛmɛ finyaa maalɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ mmɔ́ nɛ anɛ ketee fɔda kamɛ, mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ boe Ɔlate nyɛ ɛtsa yĩ a akũ.

english_verse: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
translation_verse: Finyaa ade lɛlalɛde kĩ mmanɛ akũ kĩ mayaasɛla mi a, lɛmɛ nláawã mi lɛtsama. Mi mɔawɛ a mawɛ, nnɛ́ mi sika. Malo kɔlaa ni, nnɛ́ babi kɛnɛ kĩ bɛmanɛ fɔtsa biyi be bamanɛ, mbom bamanɛ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkpɔta fɔtsa biyi be babi.

english_verse: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
translation_verse: Foesũ maapia nɛ onyole kĩĩ akũ, lɛmɛ nláapia nɛ yĩ mɔawɛ akũ, mbɔɛntɛ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔatsa kĩ nlá kɔbɛ̃ a akũ.

english_verse: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kawɛ nwɛ kĩ mapia akũ malo ni, nláakɛna foe ndɛ odzimbifɔ aye, kitonɛkĩ maakalɛ ɔnɔkɔali a. Lɛmɛ nláapia akũ, kitonɛkĩ ntáwɛ kĩ odima ebu yĩ ɛba ayekĩ enyina kĩ nla a, nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛmɔ́ kĩ nkɛna mɔ likĩ enu kĩ nlɛ a sũ.

english_verse: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ayekĩ nláadɛla yĩ akũ nɛ ode nɛ awãwã fɔtsa pii a kĩ Baguma nyɛ ɛtsa yĩ a sũ ni, bido fɔsɔ ndɛ su kĩ sawo paa nɛ yĩ sukɛna kamɛ. Fɔsɔ a kɛna ndɛ Abonsam fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ kĩ fɔkaaha yĩ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ayekĩ nláatɛnɛ yĩ akũ kadɛla nɛ ode.

english_verse: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
translation_verse: Mbɔa mpaɛ titi ɛlalɛ nkpa boe Ɔlate kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔsɔ kĩĩ nɛ yĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mbuayɛ kĩ ɛkpa yĩ nyɛ kĩ, “Yĩ kɔnyakamɔ nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ de osĩ, kitonɛkĩ yĩ kɔbɛ̃ ana ɔsĩ paa ntɛ nɔ ɔlá kɔbɛ̃.” Foesũ kawɔlɛ ade yĩ paa kĩ mapia akũ nɛ yĩ ɔsĩ katana a akũ, ayekĩ maamɔ Kristo kɔbɛ̃ a nɛ yĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
translation_verse: Foesũ kawɔlɛ ade yĩ nɛ ɔsĩ katana, fuvũnatsa, lubo kamɔ, liti katomɛna, mɔ ɔhau kamɔ akũ nɛ Kristo sũ. Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ nlá kɔbɛ̃ ni, Kristo ado yĩ kɔbɛ̃. Paulo Sĩ Akũ Nɛ Korintofɔ Batɛ Badonɛ a Ɔbla Akũ.

english_verse: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
translation_verse: Malɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ odzimbifɔ aye, lɛmɛ mi lɛfɔ̃ nkɛna foe, kitonɛkĩ mi nyɛ bati kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bimu yĩ kufɛ. Kitonɛkĩ titɔ kĩ ĩnyɛ́ lɛtsama malo ni, mi fɔtɔ banyanɛ titiliwu a lɛbɛ̃ yĩ nɛ osi odima akũ.

english_verse: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
translation_verse: Awãwã mɔ kɔbɛ̃ fɔtsa, mɔ nsɛntsɛlɛni a kĩ nkɔa ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ nkɛna nɛ mi kamɛ a atsa pefee kĩ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ wĩ manɛ.
