translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ odu abɔa ɔwɔlɛ kĩ binyina Baguma lɛmɛ be fɔkɛlatsa atsa kĩ bisĩ ye. Bɛnyɛ bati kĩ be akũ deĩ libe, kĩ baábu fɔkɔ ade bɛtáfɛta bɛkpa lɛtsa wĩ lidima kakɛna a.

english_verse: Titus, you must teach only what is correct.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɔ kɔ̃ ni, tsa fɔtsa a kĩ fuketimɔ katɛkado fɔtsa katsa wĩ a.

english_verse: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
translation_verse: Tsa banyolenɔkɔɛ̃ a kĩ bidzi mɔ anɛ yedede nɛ kadikanyi mɔ be akũ kapi wĩ kamɛ. Bɛnya bati kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ pintii nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ, bati kĩ bideĩ bati akũ ɔdɔ mɔ bikĩ bado ɔwɔlɛ.

english_verse: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
translation_verse: Ade ayea batsole banɔkɔɛ̃ a lɛmɛ ni, tsa be kĩ bidzi nkpã kĩ ɛfɛta batsole kĩ be akũ kpalɛ. Kɛtánɛ kĩ banyɛ bati buĩ, ĩye bɛkawuga bavɛ, mbom kɛnɛ kĩ bɛtsa bati lɛtsa kĩ lɛnɛnɛ,

english_verse: so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers.
translation_verse: bɛtsa bayitsɔ̃ kĩ bidzi fɔnyɔ ayekĩ baawɛ be banyɔ mɔ be babi kalɛ.

english_verse: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ binyi ayekĩ bapi be akũ, bɛkɛna be akũ ɛkpalɛ lɔkɔa bɛnya batsɔ̃ wĩ kĩ badɔla be akũ kakpa be banyɔ. Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ odima lɛláalɛ tuwuli kpa nɛ Baguma kalɛ a akũ.

english_verse: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
translation_verse: Ade osi mɔapɛ a akũ atsã ɔdɛ bapopo lɛmɛ bɛnya be akũ akũ a.

english_verse: Always set a good example for others. Be sincere and serious when you teach.
translation_verse: Nɛ foe flee kamɛ a, kɛnɛ kĩ nɔ mɔawɛ a anya oti kĩ ye ɔbla wĩ bati akɔlɛ. Kɔa anɛ ovio katsa fɔtsa nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ.

english_verse: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Bi bati tuwuli nɛ osi kpĩĩ akũ ayekĩ bɛláanyɛ nɔ nfumusu. Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ kɔnyɔã liwo nɔ bakesĩnɛ nɛ ayekĩ bɛláana lɛtsa kpa bɛlɛ nɛ boe akũ sũ.

english_verse: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
translation_verse: Bati kĩ basum badi ni, bɛdɔla be akũ bɛkpa be balate, bɛkɛna fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́awo balate a anɛ. Kɛtánɛ kĩ bakpa onukpɛ̃ bɛkpa be.

english_verse: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
translation_verse: Kɛtánɛ kĩ baye be lɛmɛ, mbom bɛtsa be akũ kĩ bɛnyɛ bati wĩ, ade badi ɔnɔkɔali kĩ baapuli bɛtɛ be bido a. Fukĩĩ aafɔ̃ bɛkɔa obu bɛkpa boe fɔtsa katsa nɛ Baguma boe Ɔtɛnɛ a akũ.

english_verse: God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma nyɛ ye kɔnyakamɔ a kĩ edeĩ ɛkpa katinya kamɛ bati nkpã katɛ a ɛtsa.

english_verse: He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world.
translation_verse: Kɔnyakamɔ kĩĩ atsa boe kĩ bɔfɔnɛ nkpã kpa kadzi mɔ katinya kamɛ fɔtsa kpa liti katomɛna awɛ. Kɛnɛ kĩ bɔadzi nkpã kĩ bɔ́anya boe akũ, budzi nkpã wĩ nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Ade osi kĩĩ akũ bɔ́atsã bɔkadzɔlɛ Luwi kpale a kĩ bɔadã anɛ a. Ade luwi kĩĩ akũ mɔ boe Baguma Kpale, mɔ boe Ɔtɛnɛ Yesu Kristo aanɛ nɛ kukũkpalɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
translation_verse: Ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛkpa nɛ boe lɔlɔ kĩ yanyɛ boe etomɛna tɔkpa flee kamɛ, ɛkɛna boe bati kpalɛwa kĩ bɛnyɛ ye odi bade a kĩ bɔawɛ kĩ bakakɛna tuwĩ.

english_verse: Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
translation_verse: Tsa fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ Baguma kpa nɔ, kado bikĩ batse nɔ a kɔbɛ̃. Kã bikĩ bitómɔ osi kĩĩ akũ a anɛ. Tafɔ̃ be kamɛ odima lɛmɔ nɔ ndɛ oti kĩ ɛtákpɔla.

english_verse: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
translation_verse: Te be anɛ kĩ bɛdɔla be akũ bɛkpa abãã mɔ bati a kĩ banya be akũ a, bitse be kalɛ, bɛna nɛ kukũmanɛ kamɛ bɛkɛna tuwĩ nɛ osi nyɛ osi akũ.

english_verse: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
translation_verse: Bi be kĩ bɛtalɛ tɔkpa nɛ odima akũ, mbom bidzi nɛ lukudɔ mɔ tɔkɔba kakɛna kamɛ. Bɛkaakɔa obu kakpa odi nyɛ odi.

english_verse: We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ kɔkɔɛ a, boe mɔawɛ malo bɔkɛna fɔkɔasiatsa mɔ atɔ ɔsĩ, ade bɔyɛla osi wĩ a. Kɔkɔɛ lɛmɛ a sukɛna fɔwɛtsa kpa mɔ kawɔlɛkamɔ futefute nya boe akũ. Bɔkɔa boe lɔkɔ bɔkpa fɔtsa kpa kakɛna mɔ anɛ kayɔɔla. Bati badi kesĩ boe ade boe malo bukesĩ be a.

english_verse: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Baguma boe Ɔtɛnɛ nyɛ ye sɛmɛ kalɛ mɔ kawɛ ɛtsa boe a ni,

english_verse: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
translation_verse: ɛtɛ boe nkpã. Nnɛ́ boe tuwĩ a kĩ bɔkɛna sũ, mbom ye mɔawɛ kɔnyakamɔ sũ ɛtɛ boe. Ɛfɔ̃ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa yako nɛ boe akũ, ɛlɔla boe kama, ɛwɔla boe mui ade ɛkpa boe nkpã vɔɛ̃ a.

english_verse: God sent Jesus Christ our Savior to give us his Spirit.
translation_verse: Baguma tsã Yesu Kristo boe Ɔtɛnɛ akũ ɛkɔa ye Hũhũ Kpalɛwa kɔbɛ̃ pii a eko nɛ boe akũ,

english_verse: Jesus treated us much better than we deserve. He made us acceptable to God and gave us the hope of eternal life.
translation_verse: ayekĩ nɛ ye sɛmɛ kalɛ sũ bɔmɔ ye kayite kɛlɛ, lɔkɔa boe malo bɔna nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a, kĩ ye akũ lɛmɛ budeĩ kɔnɛte a.

english_verse: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
translation_verse: Kalɛ kĩĩ nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali paa. Mawɛ kĩ lɛ kasisa fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ bamɛ ayekĩ bikĩ bɛtɛ Baguma bido a aakɔa be lɔkɔ bɛkpa tuwĩ kakɛna kĩ fudeĩ nfasɔ fɔkpa odi nyɛ odi.

english_verse: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
translation_verse: Tado onukpɛ̃ nɛ fɔkɔasiatsa kavɛlɛ kamɛ, nɛ bati kasinu katokɔ̃ kakã akũ. Tawɛ sɔwa ɔkɛna ɔdzɛ ĩye ɔtɛ atsindze nɛ Yudafɔ mbla a akũ. Foe flee fɔlá nfasɔ adima.

english_verse: Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them.
translation_verse: Ntɛ oti odi ayamɛna kasɛ̃ nɛ bati kamɛ ni, sua ye osi deedi ĩye titi nviã. Nɛ ke liti a, tabuki odo ye onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
translation_verse: Nyi kĩ oti a na odu ni, tɔkpa nya ye kamɛkamɛ ade ye titikpa aakɔ ye kɔtɔ a. Kɔdɛ Nkaɛ Kakpa Mɔ Kakla.

english_verse: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
translation_verse: Ntɛ ndo Artema ĩye Tihiko nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ ni, bɔa mbɔdi kayawo yĩ sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Nikopoli, kitonɛkĩ ade awã mawɛ kĩ maadzi nɛ osili lɔkɔ a.

english_verse: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
translation_verse: Bɔa mbɔdi kanyɛ Sena kĩ ɛnyɛ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ a mɔ Apolo osi bɛnaa be osi akũ. Dã kĩ bɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ laasĩ be.

english_verse: Our people should learn to spend their time doing something useful and worthwhile.
translation_verse: Kɛnɛ kĩ batɛ badonɛ a bɛkɔlɛ ayekĩ baakɔa be lɔkɔ flee bɛkpa tudzuma wĩ kakɛna, kĩ baapuli bɛkabumɔ bati bɛbã lɛmɛ. Bɛtadzi nkpã kĩ ɛlá nfasɔ.

english_verse: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Bati flee a kĩ bideĩ nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awĩ a awɔ̃na nɔ! Wɔ̃na batɛ badonɛ a kĩ bideĩ nɛ awã kakpa boe. Baguma ɛmɔ mi flee kɔnya.

english_verse: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
translation_verse: Yĩ, Paulo kĩ bido nɛ kɔla nɛ Kristo Yesu sũ, mɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃ Timoteo sɛkɛ̃ ovoli kĩĩ to. Bɔtsɛlɛ ye bɔama nɔ, Filemon boe ɔkɔba adzuma ɔkɛnanɛ kĩ bɔawɛ kalɛ paa.

english_verse: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
translation_verse: Bɔtsɛlɛ ye lɛmɛ bɔama batɛ badonɛ a kĩ batsɛna nɛ nɔ tɔtɔ kamɛ, mɔ boe ɔwaɛ̃tsole Apia, mɔ boe ɔkɔba paa Arkipo kĩ yakɛna boe Ɔlate adzuma mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ ndɛ sodzani aye a.

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
translation_verse: Baguma boe Ɔlɛga mɔ boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo bɛmɔ mi kɔnya, bɛkpa mi lukudɔ lɛmɛ. Filemon Deĩ Ɔdɔ Mɔ Katɛkado.

english_verse: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ Filemon, lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ mabɔa mpaɛ ni, mate anɛ nɛ nɔ akũ, ade manyɛ Baguma kɔlɛ nɛ nɔ lɔlɔ,

english_verse: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ nnu ayekĩ awɛ Baguma bati kalɛ, mɔ ayekĩ ɔtɛ boe Ɔlate Yesu odo a sũ.

english_verse: As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us.
translation_verse: Mabɔa mpaɛ kĩ ɔmɔa a kĩ bɔakɛna ndɛ batɛ badonɛ aye a ɛna ɔsĩ ebu, ayekĩ bɔ́apuli bunu fɔtsa wĩ flee a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ ɔmɔa kakɛna mɔ Kristo kayɔ a dzununuunu.

english_verse: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃, mmɔ́ kawɔlɛ ade ɔwɔlɛ ka edzi yĩ a, kitonɛkĩ nɔ ɔdɔ a kĩ odeĩ ɔkpa Baguma bati a fɔ̃ bɛna kawɛya. Paulo Ke Aduli Nɛ Onesimo Lɔlɔ.

english_verse: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ mawɛ nɔ kalɛ sũ ni, nkɔa kudulike makpa nɔ mbom. Makɛna fukĩĩ midi kĩ yĩ, Paulo, Kristo Yesu fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ, nwaa kɔla nɛ ye sũ.

english_verse: I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ nkɔa kudulike makpa nɔ nɛ Onesimo lɔlɔ. Ɛnyɛ yĩ mɔawɛ obi nɛ Kristo kamɛ. Ndani hũhũ kamɛ kɔlɛga nkpa ye nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ nwaa kɔla a.

english_verse: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ ye dĩ kayɔ nyɛ nfasɔ malo a, kɔkɔɛ a, ɔtána ye akũ nfasɔ adima, lɛmɛ finyaakĩĩ a, ye akũ deĩ nfasɔ ɛkpa bɔmɔ nɔ flee.

english_verse: Sending Onesimus back to you makes me very sad.
translation_verse: Nfɔ̃ emuniki yaya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃, ade fɔakɛna yĩ ndɛ yĩ ɔwɔlɛ flee tomɔ ye a.

english_verse: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
translation_verse: Kĩ nwaa kɔla nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a sũ ni, nwɛ kĩ ɛkahɔ nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awĩ kufɛ, ɛkaabumɔ yĩ adzuma nɛ nɔ kadokɔ̃.

english_verse: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntáwɛ kĩ mahɛ nɔ kĩ kpa yĩ kabumɛna, mbom mawɛ kĩ kɛna foe futo nɔ ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Foesũ nláakɛna lɛtsama mbɔɛntɛ otunɔ nɛ foe akũ.

english_verse: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
translation_verse: Ɔsa Onesimo dzakũ nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ nɛ lɔkɔ lukũ kamɛ, ayekĩ yaabuki emuniki ɛya nɛ nɔ sɛkɛ̃ ɛyaadzi flee.

english_verse: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Finyaakĩĩ nnɛ́ ɔsate odi ebuki yanɛ, mbom ɛnyɛ oti kĩ ɛbɛ̃ ɔsate kɔlaa. Ɛnyɛ ɔwaɛ̃nyole kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ nɛ Kristo kamɛ. Dã ayekĩ ɛtɛ kɔya ɛkpa yĩ! Lɛmɛ yaabuki ɛtɛ kɔya ɛkpa nɔ ɛba ndɛ ɔsate aye, ade yaabuki ɛnya ɔwaɛ̃nyole nɛ boe Ɔlate dĩ akũ a.

english_verse: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ ɔbɔɛ yĩ ndɛ nɔ ɔkɔba adzuma ɔkɛnanɛ aye ni, tɛ ye mɔ kawɔlɛ ndɛ ayekĩ aatɛ yĩ.

english_verse: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
translation_verse: Ntɛ edi nfumusu adi nɛ nɔ akũ, ĩye edzimɔ nɔ kɔtɔ kudi malo ni, bu foe akũ akunta kakɔa kakpa yĩ.

english_verse: With my own hand I write: I, PAUL, WILL PAY YOU BACK. But don't forget you owe me your life.
translation_verse: Yĩ Paulo mɔawɛ lɛkɔa awɛ matsɛlɛ kĩ maase nɔ kɔtɔ. Lɛmɛ ntáwɛ kĩ mate nɔ anɛ kĩ nɔ mɔawɛ a odzimɔ yĩ nɔ nkpã kɔtɔ.

english_verse: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
translation_verse: Foesũ nwaɛ̃, mawɛ kĩ kpa yĩ kabumɛna kĩĩ nɛ boe Ɔlate sũ. Nɛ Kristo sũ kɛna foe ayekĩ ɔwɔlɛ aaka edzi yĩ.

english_verse: Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
translation_verse: Fukĩ fubu nyɛ kĩ wɛ kɔla kayi yĩ, kitonɛkĩ matɛ kado kĩ Baguma aatse mi flee mpaɛbɔ lɔkɔa etunɔ ĩya nɛ mi sɛkɛ̃. Kawɔ̃na Nkaɛ.

english_verse: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
translation_verse: Epafra, kĩ bɔmɔ ye waa kɔla nɛ Kristo sũ a bo yawɔ̃na nɔ!

english_verse: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Marko, mɔ Aristarko, mɔ Dema, mɔ Luka, yĩ bakɔba kĩ babumɔ yĩ adzuma kĩĩ kakɛna a, awɔ̃na nɔ.

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate Yesu Kristo ɛmɔ mi hũhũ flee kɔnya.

english_verse: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɔkɔɛ a, Baguma lɛmɔ boe bawanyɔ a kɔdzɛla lɔkɔ pii nɛ tisi ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ akũ, ɛtsãmɛna ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ akũ.

english_verse: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ fuwi nkaɛ kĩĩ kamɛ ni, Baguma lɛmɔ boe kɔdzɛla ɛtsãmɛna ye Obi akũ. Ade Obi kĩĩ akũ ɛtsã ɛbɔa katinya flee, ɛpau ye kĩ ɛnya lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa ɔlate a.

english_verse: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
translation_verse: Obi a lɛnyɛ ayekĩ Baguma kukũkpalɛ la ɛtsa, ade Baguma kutidu a pɛpɛɛpɛ ye malo edeĩ a. Obi a kɔa Baguma kalɛ a kɔbɛ̃ epini katinya flee. Kĩ ɛvɔlɛ bati tɔkpa eyua a, ɛnadzi nɛ Baguma Kpale nɛ ode flee a sɔmɔna. Baguma Obi a Nyɛ Ode Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ a Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃.

english_verse: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
translation_verse: Baguma kɛna ye Obi a ɛbɛ̃ Baguma ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a, kitonɛkĩ dĩ a kĩ Baguma te ye a tɛ kɔya lɛba be bade a.

english_verse: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma tábi ye fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ odima tɔ̃ kĩ, “Nɔ nyɛ yĩ Obinyole. Nviã luwi kĩĩ mma nɔ.” Bio Baguma tálɛ nɛ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ odima akũ kĩ, “Maanya ye Ɔlɛga, ade ye malo ɛnya yĩ Obi a.”

english_verse: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
translation_verse: Ade lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma wɛ kĩ yado ye obinyole ɔtɔnyade nɛ katinya kamɛ a ni, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a flee, misum ye.”

english_verse: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a akũ ni, Baguma lɛ kĩ, “Baguma kɛna ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ ndɛ ɔwɛ, ade ɛkɛna ye ode basumunɛ ndɛ fi fɔmɛ a.”

english_verse: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ye Obi akũ ni, Baguma lɛ kĩ, “O Baguma, nɔ lɛgãkanya aawa daa daa. Nɔ bati akũ kanya tsɛ̃ osi kpĩĩ akũ.

english_verse: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
translation_verse: Awɛ tuwĩ kakɛna lɛmɛ akesĩ tɔkpa a. Ade foesũ Baguma, nɔ Baguma a pau nɔ a, ɛkpa nɔ obũ kadzikɔ̃ kĩ kawɔlɛ deĩ kɛbɛ̃ nɔ bakɔba flee kade a.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
translation_verse: Baguma buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, nɔ lɛbɔa tite nɛ kayɔkayi a, ade ɔkɔa nɔ mɔa awɛ ɔkɛna ode lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa kĩĩ flee aawɔ, lɛmɛ nɔ kɔ̃ aana daa, foe flee fɔ́adɛ fɔtsɛ ndɛ tɛtadiɛ.

english_verse: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
translation_verse: Aabuta foe ndɛ atadiɛ, ade fɔ́adani ndɛ tɛtadiɛ. Lɛmɛ nɔ kɔ̃ ade ayea aana daa a, ade nɔ nkpã kadzi lɛlá kawũna a.”

english_verse: God never said to any of the angels, “Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you!”
translation_verse: Baguma tábi ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kamɛ odima tɔ̃ kĩ, “Dzi nɛ yĩ sɔmɔna nɛ awĩ, kɛnaawo kĩ nkɔa nɔ bakesĩnɛ nkɛna nɔ abɔa katekɔ̃.”

english_verse: Angels are merely spirits sent to serve people who are going to be saved.
translation_verse: Kekɔ̃ baáde nyɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a? Ade be nyɛ hũhũ a kĩ basum Baguma, ade yado be lɛmɛ kĩ bɛnaabumɛna bati a kĩ baana nkpã katɛ a.

english_verse: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ bɔapi ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bunu a budomɔ akũ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ayekĩ bɔláayɛla osi.

english_verse: The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved.
translation_verse: Bɛtsa kĩ ntɛ Baguma ɔlɛdo a kĩ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ kɔa bɛma boe bawanyɔ a nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali, mɔ odi nyɛ odi kĩ efum ĩye ɛtákɛna nɛ ye akũ a tɛ foe sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ kĩ sɛfɛta a ni,

english_verse: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
translation_verse: nde bɔ́apuli bulo sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ a kĩ baakpa boe a, ntɛ bɔtátɛ nkpã katɛ kpale kĩĩ a? Boe Ɔlate mɔawɛ lɛnya lɔtɔ ɛlɛ nkpã katɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ, ade bikĩ binu foe a di adansiɛ kĩ fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali a.

english_verse: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ mɔapɛ a kamɛ a, Baguma mɔawɛ kɔa nsɛntsɛlɛni mɔ awãwã fɔtsa futefute kakɛna edi adansiɛ nɛ nkpã katɛ a akũ, ade ɛsɛ̃ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa futetsa a ɛkpa bati nɛ ye kawɛ onukpɛ̃ a. Yesu Kpã Boe Yanamɛna Nkpã Katɛ Kamɛ.

english_verse: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
translation_verse: Baguma tákɔa katinya a kĩ kaaya, kĩ ke akũ fɔlɛtsa bɔlamɛna kalɛ a akũ kanya edo nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a awɛ.

english_verse: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
translation_verse: Mbom bɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kakɔ kadi kamɛ kĩ, “Litsa nyɛ otidziwa obi kĩ akɔna ye akũ? Litsa koũ nyɛ otidziwa kĩ api ye kadunamɔ akũ?

english_verse: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
translation_verse: Ɔkɛ́na ye nɛ kayɔ lɔkɔ lukũ lidi ɔba ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a. Lɛmɛ ɔkɔa kukũkpalɛ mɔ obu ɔtsa ye ndɛ lɛgãbɔɛ aye.

english_verse: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
translation_verse: Ade ɔkɛna ye kĩ ɛnya fɔtsa flee akũ a.” Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ ɔkɛna ye kĩ ɛnya fɔtsa flee akũ, foesũ lɛtsama tábuki lite kĩ ɛlá kɔbɛ̃ nɛ de akũ. Lɛmɛ finyaa bɔámɔ kĩ otidziwa deĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ fɔtsa flee akũ.

english_verse: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɔmɔ́ Yesu a kĩ bɛkɛna nɛ kayɔ nɛ lɔkɔ lukũ kamɛ ɛba ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a, ayekĩ fɔtsã Baguma kɔnyakamɔ kamɛ ɛyaaku nɛ odi nyɛ odi lɔlɔ a, finyaa kɔ̃ bɔmɔ́ ye kĩ Baguma kɔa obu mɔ kukũkpalɛ ɛtsa ye ndɛ lɛgãbɔɛ aye, nɛ wuda lubo a kĩ ɛmɔ́ a sũ.

english_verse: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Fɔnɛnɛ kĩ Baguma kĩ ɛbɔa fɔtsa flee, epini foe a, ɛkɛna Yesu ɛkpɔla ɛtsãmɛna lubo kamɔ kamɛ, ayekĩ yaakpã bati beblebee bɛyaana tsɛfã nɛ ye kukũkpalɛ a kamɛ. Kitonɛkĩ oti a kĩ yakpã be kanamɛna nkpã katɛ kamɛ a nyɛ Kristo.

english_verse: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ oti a kĩ yakpalɛ bati akũ a, mɔ bati a kĩ bɛkpalɛ be akũ a flee deĩ Ɔlɛga mɔapɛ. Ade foesũ kɔnyɔã lákɔ Yesu kĩ yavɛ be ye bawaɛ̃ a.

english_verse: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
translation_verse: Yabi Baguma kĩ, “Maade nɔ dĩ akũ lɛtsã nkpa awaɛ̃ amɛ, ade maasã tɔnɔ mmu nɔ nɛ be katsɛnakɔ̃ a.”

english_verse: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Maakɔa anɛ nte nɛ Baguma akũ.” Ade ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Amɔ yĩ mɔ babi a kĩ Baguma kɔa ɛkpa yĩ a nɛ awĩ.”

english_verse: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ babi a kĩ yawɛ a nyɛ batidziwa, kĩ bideĩ sukɛna mɔ babɔanɛ sũ ni, Yesu malo dani ɛkɛna otidziwa, edzi nkpã ndɛ be aye. Ɛkɛna fukĩĩ fɔtsã ye wuda kamɛ, ɛwɔla Abonsam a kĩ ye nɛɛ wuda kɔbɛ̃ a.

english_verse: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
translation_verse: Ke liti lɛmɛ, Yesu kɛna fukĩĩ kĩ bati a kĩ wuda kavila nɛ be nkpã kadzi flee kamɛ fɔ̃ bɛdani basate a, bɛfɔ̃ wuda kavila.

english_verse: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, nnɛ́ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a yakpa kabumɛna, mbom Abraham babi mɔ bawa yabumɛna.

english_verse: He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ atsa kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ bɛkɛna ye ndɛ ye bawaɛ̃ aye nɛ osi nyɛ osi akũ, ayekĩ yaapuli ɛnya be Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee kĩ yamɔ bati kɔnya. Yaakasum Baguma nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ lɛmɛ, ayekĩ yaafɔ̃ bɛnyɛ tɔkpa nɛ bati akũ.

english_verse: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ ye mɔawɛ ɛmɔ lubo, ɛtsã sɔhuiɛ kamɛ lɛmɛ sũ ni, yaapuli ebumɔ bikĩ be malo bideĩ nɛ sɔhuiɛ kamɛ a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest!
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ Kristofɔ, kĩ Baguma vɛ etomɛna ode lɛmɛ a, mikɔna Yesu a kĩ Baguma do kĩ ɛnya Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee nɛ katɛkado a kĩ bɔnɛɛ a midã.

english_verse: Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people.
translation_verse: Edi ɔnɔkɔali ɛkpa Baguma a kĩ ye lɛpau ye kĩ ɛkɛna adzuma kĩĩ, ndɛ ayekĩ Mose lɛmɛ di ɔnɔkɔali ɛkɛna adzuma nɛ Baguma tɔtɔ kamɛ a.

english_verse: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
translation_verse: Ndɛ ayekĩ babu kɔla okenɛ kaba kɔla mɔawɛ a ni, ade aye kɛfɛta kĩ Yesu ɛna obu baũ ɛba Mose a.

english_verse: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
translation_verse: Kɔla nyɛ kɔla ni, oti ake koe. Lɛmɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, Baguma nyɛ oti a kĩ eke fɔtsa a flee.

english_verse: Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future.
translation_verse: Mose di ɔnɔkɔali ndɛ ɔsumunɛ aye nɛ Baguma tɔtɔ kamɛ, ade edi adansiɛ nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma aanyɛ ɛtsa nɛ kunya kamɛ a akũ a.

english_verse: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Kristo kɔ̃ dã Baguma tɔtɔ akũ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ndɛ Obi aye. Boe nyɛ ye kɔla a, ntɛ budo ɔwɔlɛ, bɔna katɛkado nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ boe anɛ teĩ nɛ de akũ a akũ. Kawɛyakɔ̃ Deĩ Kɛkpa Baguma Bade a.

english_verse: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ ndɛ ayekĩ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛ a, “Ntɛ minu Baguma odo nɛ nviã ni,

english_verse: don't be stubborn! Don't rebel like those people who were tested in the desert. *
translation_verse: mitakɛna atɔ ɔsĩ, ndɛ ayekĩ mi bawanyɔ kɛna a, kĩ biyidza bɛdza nɛ Baguma akũ luwi a nɛ kɔsa kamɛ, bɛsɔ ye bɛdã a.

english_verse: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
translation_verse: Ade bɛsɔ yĩ bɛdã nɛ awã bɛwɛ fɔlɛtsa lɛmɛ nɛ yĩ akũ, titɔ kĩ bɛmɔ́ fɔtsa a kĩ nkɛna nɛ futeli tɛna kamɛ a malo.

english_verse: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ nna kalɔkpa nɛ bati a akũ ade nlɛ kĩ, ‘Bafum ade bayɛla osi a, ade bisĩ baákɛna nɛ yĩ mbla a akũ a.’

english_verse: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
translation_verse: Nna kalɔkpa ade nlɔ fubo nlɛ kĩ, ‘Bɛláake kɔbɔa nɛ tite a kĩ kufɛ maakpa be kawɛya a akũ da!’ ”

english_verse: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ batɛ badonɛ, midã kadã wĩ kĩ mi kamɛ odima ɛtana ɔwɔlɛ kpa mɔ katatɛkado lɔkɔa emuniki nɛ Baguma a kĩ edzi nkpã a liti.

english_verse: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
translation_verse: Mbom mikaabumɛna akũ akũ luwi nyɛ luwi, kĩ nviã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa kamɛ a tsɛla boe akũ, ayekĩ tɔkpa lɛláakɛ mi, ĩye mina atɔ ɔsĩ. Tsɛla boe akũ.

english_verse: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ boe flee budeĩ tsɛfã mɔ Kristo ntɛ bupini boe katɛkado mɔ ɔsĩ kɛnawo kawũna a, ndɛ ayekĩ buyi kayɔ a.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
translation_verse: Bɛlɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ, “Ntɛ minu Baguma odo nviã ni, mitakɛna atɔ ɔsĩ ndɛ ayekĩ mi bawanyɔ kɛna a, kĩ biyidza bɛdza nɛ Baguma akũ a.”

english_verse: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
translation_verse: Bawei linu Baguma odo mɔ biyidza bɛdza nɛ ye akũ a? Ade be nyɛ bati flee a kĩ Mose kpã etomɛna Egipte tite akũ a.

english_verse: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
translation_verse: Bawei akũ Baguma lɔ fubo ɛlɛ kĩ, “Bɛláake kɔbɔa nɛ tite a kĩ kufɛ maakpa be kawɛya a akũ da”? Ade bati a kĩ biyidza bɛdza nɛ Baguma akũ a.

english_verse: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔmɔ́ kĩ bɛtápuli biwo tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ bɛtátɛ Baguma bido.

english_verse: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
translation_verse: Foesũ kĩ Baguma buki ebinya osi eyi nɛ ye kawɛya a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a kamɛ kawo sũ ni, mifɔ̃ bɔdã kadã wĩ ayekĩ bɛláamɔ kĩ mi kamɛ odi tápuli ɛna kawɛya a kamɛ kawo.

english_verse: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bibi boe Kalɛ Wĩ a ndɛ ayekĩ bibi be malo a. Binu fɔlɛtsa a, lɛmɛ bɛtána foe akũ nfasɔ, kitonɛkĩ kĩ binu foe a, bɛtátɛ foe bido.

english_verse: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe a kĩ bɔtɛ budo a, buwo nɛ kawɛya a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi a kamɛ. Ndɛ ayekĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nna kalɔkpa ade nlɔ fubo kĩ, ‘Bɛláake kɔbɔa nɛ tite a kĩ kufɛ maakpa be kawɛya a akũ da!’ ” Baguma lɛ fukĩĩ titɔ kĩ eyua ye mɔawɛ adzuma kito lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛbɔa katinya a malo.

english_verse: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kakɔ kadi nɛ luwi luvũkɔnɔde a akũ kĩ, “Nɛ luwi luvũkɔnɔde akũ a, Baguma dzi ɛwɛya, ade ɛtábuki ɛkɛna adzuma adima a.”

english_verse: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ kalɛ mɔapɛ a kĩ, “Bɛláake kɔbɔa nɛ tite a kĩ kufɛ maakpa be kawɛya a akũ da!”

english_verse: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
translation_verse: Bikĩ bɛnya lɔtɔ binu Kalɛ Wĩ a tápuli biwo nɛ kawɛyakɔ̃ a, kitonɛkĩ bɛtátɛ bido, lɛmɛ osi buki ebinya kĩ badi biwo.

english_verse: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
translation_verse: Baguma yi luwi bɛbã kĩ bavɛ Nviã, nɛ futeli pii liti. Ke liti a, Lɛgã Dawid lɛ kɔdzɛla ɛtsãmɛna fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɔlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ foe akũ bɔyatɛ̃ a kĩ, “Ntɛ minu Baguma odo nviã ni, mitakɛna atɔ ɔsĩ.”

english_verse: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ Yosua ɛkpã be ewo nɛ kawɛyakɔ̃ a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi a ni, kufɛ Baguma lɛláabuki ɛlɛ kalɛ nɛ kawɛya luwi bɛbã akũ ke liti a.

english_verse: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
translation_verse: Foesũ kawɛya luwi buki lideĩ lɛkpa Baguma bade a, ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma awɛya nɛ luwi luvũkɔnɔde akũ a

english_verse: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ewo nɛ Baguma kawɛyakɔ̃ a, aatɛ kawɛya nɛ be adzuma kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma lɛmɛ fɔ̃ ye mɔawɛ adzuma kakɛna, ɛwɛya a.

english_verse: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔbɔa mbɔdi buwo nɛ kawɛyakɔ̃ a kamɛ, ayekĩ boe kamɛ odima lɛláatomɛna be ɔbla kpa a, ɛtɔ nɛ atɔ ɔsĩ sũ.

english_verse: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma kalɛ a deĩ nkpã ade kɛlamɛna adzuma a. Kideĩ ɔbɔ̃ kɛbɛ̃ atugbe adi nyɛ adi, ade katsɔɛ kanaawo awã kĩ kasɛ̃ ɔkla mɔ hũhũ kayite. Kawo kanaasɛ̃ fɔtsakɔ̃ fɔtsakɔ̃ mɔ fɔtsɔ̃ kamɛ tɔbɔ̃ kayite. Kalɛ a adzɛmɔ oti adzuni mɔ ye ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ fɔwɛtsa yededeede.

english_verse: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
translation_verse: Lɛtsama lɛláa kĩ baapuli bɛkɔa bɛkɔla Baguma, kitonɛkĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ ɛbɔa nɛ katinya kamɛ waa ɔdzɔ nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ. Ade ye mɔ bɔ́akɛna akunta nɛ boe kadzi kamɛ akũ bɔkpa a. Yesu Nyɛ́ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a.

english_verse: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ bupi boe katɛkado a kĩ bɔanyɛ katsa a budomɔ akũ mɔ ɔsĩ, kitonɛkĩ budeĩ Yesu, Baguma Obi kĩ ɛnyɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a kĩ ɛtsã ode flee kamɛ ɛnɛ́ edzi nɛ Baguma ɔwɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin!
translation_verse: Nnɛ́ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee kĩ ɛláapuli enu boe katɛkado onukpɛ̃ kadɔ kayɔ budeĩ, mbom oti kĩ ye malo bɛsɔ ye bɛdã nɛ osi nyɛ osi akũ ndɛ boe aye, lɛmɛ ɛtákɛna tɔkpa tidima a budeĩ.

english_verse: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ budo ɔwɔlɛ bufuã Baguma kɔnyakamɔ lɛgãkpo a, ayekĩ bɔ́ana Baguma sɛmɛkalɛ mɔ kɔnyakamɔ fubumɔ boe nɛ kasĩ lɔkɔ.

english_verse: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
translation_verse: Bapau Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a odi nyɛ odi katomɛna ye bakɔba kamɛ, kate awɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ ɛdza nɛ be onukpɛ̃ esum Baguma. Ye adzuma nyɛ kĩ ɛkɔa bati futetsa ɛkpa Baguma, ɛbɔa afɔliɛ nɛ be tɔkpa akũ.

english_verse: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ ye mɔawɛ a ɛlá kɔbɛ̃ nɛ tisi pii akũ sũ ni, yana ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ kakpa bikĩ binyína kadi mɔ bikĩ bɛyɛla osi a.

english_verse: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛbɔa tɔkpa afɔliɛ nɛ ye mɔawɛ lɔlɔ mɔ bati bɛbã tɔkpa flee lɛmɛ akũ a.

english_verse: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
translation_verse: Odima ládzina ye akũ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee kado ye akũ foe obu, mbom Baguma avɛ ye ndɛ ayekĩ ɛvɛ Aron a.

english_verse: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
translation_verse: Ade ayea Kristo lɛmɛ tádo ye akũ obu kĩ yanya Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee, mbom Baguma lidzina ye ebi ye kĩ, “Yĩ Obinyole anɛ. Nviã luwi kĩĩ mma nɔ.”

english_verse: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ nɛ kakɔ bɛbã kĩ, “Aanya ɔsɔfɔ daa, nɛ Melkizedek osi a akũ.”

english_verse: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
translation_verse: Kĩ Yesu dzi nkpã nɛ tite akũ a, ɛbɔa mpaɛ, eke aduli mɔ fɔwɔla mɔ babe ɛkpa Baguma a kĩ ye lipuli ɛnyɛ ye nɛ wuda awɛ kamɛ a. Baguma tse ye, kitonɛkĩ ɛdɔla ye akũ ɛkpa Baguma ɔsum a.

english_verse: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
translation_verse: Titɔ kĩ Baguma Obi yanɛ malo ni, ɛkɔlɛ osetie etomɛna ye lubokamɔ kamɛ.

english_verse: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
translation_verse: Ade ye nyɛ oti a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaanya Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee nɛ Melkizedek osi a akũ a. Mitafɔnɛ Mi Katɛkado a Awɛ.

english_verse: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa pii deĩ kĩ bɔ́alɛ nɛ fukĩĩ akũ kufɛ, lɛmɛ foe kayɔkanyɛ deĩ ɔsĩ, kitonɛkĩ miápuli kanu fɔlɛtsa kayɔ mɔatsɛ.

english_verse: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali kĩ titɔ kĩ mɔ lɔkɔ kĩĩ kɛnɛkĩ minya fɔtsa batsanɛ malo ni, mibuki misĩ odi kĩ ɛtsa mi Baguma fɔlɛtsa kayɔkayi fude a. Mila ndɛ akɔkɔa amɛ kĩ bisĩ tɛmɛ mui, lɛmɛ nnɛ́ ndɛ bikĩ bisĩ funitsã kĩ fudeĩ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ yamɛmɛ ni, akɔkɔa ebuki yanɛ, foesũ enyína lɛtsama nɛ tɔkpa mɔ tuwĩ akũ.

english_verse: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_verse: Banɔkɔɛ̃ anya funitsã kĩ fudeĩ ɔsĩ, kitonɛkĩ bɛvɛlɛ be adzuni nɛ fɔtsa pii kamɛ, kĩ foesũ bapuli kamɔ tɔkpa mɔ tuwĩ kayi ɔsũ.

english_verse: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
translation_verse: Foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔnaa lɔtɔ bɔkɔlɛ banɔkɔɛ̃ fɔtsa katsa, lɔkɔa bɔfɔnɛ kayɔkayi fɔtsa a kĩ bɛtsa nɛ Kristo akũ a awɛ buyi. Mitafɔ̃ bumuniki bɔnaayi kayɔ mɔ fɔtsa katsa nɛ ayekĩ bɔdani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, bɔfɔnɛ tudzuma kĩ tɛla nfasɔ, mɔ ayekĩ bɔtɛ Baguma budo,

english_verse: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
translation_verse: mɔ fɔtsa katsa nɛ mui kawɔla amaniɛ, awɛ kate nɛ bati akũ, kayidza kato kanɔkpa mɔ kadzɛmɛna nkaɛ a akũ.

english_verse: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
translation_verse: Baguma ahila tite a, kĩ tamɔa oni mui kafɔ̃ futse amɛ nɛ te akũ kana nfasɔ kakpa bikĩ be sũ banɛ fɔba nɛ te akũ a.

english_verse: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ntɛ fu mɔ tuwĩ kpa mɛ nɛ te akũ ni, nfasɔ adima lɛláa te akũ. Baguma aapuli edumu te ade nɛ kawũna kamɛ a, baapila te mɔ fi.

english_verse: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
translation_verse: Nwaɛ̃ amɛ, titɔ kĩ bɔlɛ foe lɔmɔ malo ni, mi kɔ̃ bɔtɛ budo kĩ minyina fɔtsa wĩ a kĩ fɔnɛnɛ fɔbɛ̃, kĩ fɔ́afɔ̃ mina nkpã katɛ.

english_verse: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Baguma lɛ́nyɛ oti kĩ yaádã kpĩĩ, kĩ yaálɛla adzuma a kĩ mikɛna mɔ ɔdɔ a kĩ minyɛ mitsa nɛ yĩ dĩ sũ. Mibumɛna mi bakɔba Kristofɔ ade milamɛna be kabumɛna finyaa malo a.

english_verse: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
translation_verse: Boe kawɛ mɔ ɔsĩ nyɛ kĩ mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi epi ye mbɔdi kabɔa kamɛ lɔmɔ kɛnaawo kawũna, ayekĩ mi awɛ aata lɛtsa a kĩ de akũ miate anɛ a.

english_verse: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
translation_verse: Bɔtáwɛ kĩ mikɛna ɔnyɔnɛ, mbom mikɛna fɔtsa ndɛ bati kĩ bɛtɛ bido, kĩ bideĩ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ, kĩ foesũ bɛna fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaakpa a. Lɛtsa a Kĩ Baguma Lɛ Eyi a Lávɛmɛna.

english_verse: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
translation_verse: Kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaakpa Abraham fɔtsa a, ɛtani ye mɔawɛ akũ ɛkpa ye, kitonɛkĩ odima lɛbɛ̃ ye Baguma kĩ yaatani.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ kĩ, “Maahila nɔ ade maakpa nɔ babi mɔ bawa beblebee lɛmɛ a.”

english_verse: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
translation_verse: Abraham na ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ, foesũ ye awɛ ta lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma ke otse kĩ yaakpa ye a.

english_verse: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
translation_verse: Bati atani fɔtsa kavɛ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛbɛ̃ be a kakɔa katsua fɔlɛtsa kavɛlɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ Baguma tani fɔtsa nɛ ye bɔhɛ a akũ a, kitonɛkĩ ɛwɛ kĩ foe kamɛ fɔkpalɛ bikĩ baatɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a kĩ ye nhihiɛ lɛláavɛmɛna da.

english_verse: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
translation_verse: Foesũ Baguma kɛna fɔtsa nviã kĩ fɔnyɛ bɔhɛ mɔ katani, kĩ fɔláapuli fɔdani, ade Baguma lɛmɛ lɛláapuli ɛma fɔvã nɛ foe akũ a. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ni, boe a kĩ bulo bɔnatani ye a bubuki bɔana ɔwɔlɛ, bɔate anɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi kĩ yaakpa boe a akũ.

english_verse: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
translation_verse: Budeĩ kɔnɛte kĩĩ bɔkpa boe akũ nɛ boe kadzi kamɛ, ndɛ ayekĩ kutse tunu api koe kadzɛla kpli a. Kɔnɛte a akpã boe katsãmɛna lima a kĩ litsua kɔla a kamɛ kawomɛna nɛ kakɔ kpalɛwa flee a.

english_verse: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_verse: Ade awĩ Yesu nya lɔtɔ ewo ndɛ boe lɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ aye, ɛdani daa Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee nɛ Melkizedek osi a akũ a.

english_verse: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
translation_verse: Melkizedek kĩĩ nyɛ Salem lɛgã, ade ɛnyɛ Baguma Kpale a ɔsɔfɔ a. Kĩ Abraham to kawũ a kĩ ke kamɛ ɛnya fɔgã ɛna akũ yaya a, Melkizedek natsɛlamɔ ye ehila ye.

english_verse: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
translation_verse: Awã naa, Abraham kɔa kawũ kamɛ fɔtsa a kĩ ɛna a flee katsu kawuade a ɛkpa ye. Dĩ Melkizedek kayɔkanyɛ katɔnyade nyɛ kĩ Lɛgã, fɔtsa kpĩĩ akũ ɔkɛnanɛ. Kayade nyɛ Salem lɛgã kĩ fɔatsa kĩ Lukudɔ Lɛgã.

english_verse: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
translation_verse: Bɛtálɛ lɛtsama nɛ ɔlɛga ĩye ɔma akũ. Bɛtálɛ lɛtsama nɛ ye babi mɔ bawa akũ, bɛtátsa lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛma ye, ĩye lɔkɔ a kĩ eku a. Melkizedek kĩĩ la ndɛ Baguma Obi a ade ɛnyɛ ɔsɔfɔ daa daa a.

english_verse: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
translation_verse: Midã ayekĩ Melkizedek tɛ dĩ! Boe ɔwanyɔ Abraham ndɛ otinɔkɔɛ̃ malo a kpa ye kawũ kamɛ fɔtsa a kĩ ɛna a kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kawuade a.

english_verse: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
translation_verse: Nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, Levi babi mɔ bawa kĩ banya basɔfɔ a deĩ osi kĩ batɛ fɔtsa kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kawuade nɛ Israelfɔ awɛ, titɔ kĩ be malo bɛnyɛ Abraham kasinu kamɛ babi malo.

english_verse: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
translation_verse: Ade Melkizedek táto Levi kasinu a kamɛ a, lɛmɛ ɛtɛ fɔtsa katsu kawuade etomɛna Abraham sɛkɛ̃, ade ehila ye oti a kĩ Baguma kpa bɔhɛ a.

english_verse: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
translation_verse: Atsindze adima lɛláa foe kamɛ kĩ oti a kĩ yakpa nhila a bɛ̃ oti a kĩ ye bihila a.

english_verse: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
translation_verse: Nɛ basɔfɔ sɛmakɛ ni, batidziwa kĩ baku a atɛ fɔtsa katsu kawuade, lɛmɛ nɛ Melkizedek sɛmakɛ kɔ̃ ni, oti kĩ bidi adansiɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ edzi nkpã daa, ndɛ ayekĩ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a lɛ a, lɛtɛ fɔtsa a.

english_verse: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
translation_verse: Foesũ bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ malo kĩ, Levi kĩ yatɛ fɔtsa kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kawuade a malo kpa fɔtsa ɛtsãmɛna Abraham akũ,

english_verse: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ bɛtáma tɔ̃ Levi, lɛmɛ ntɛ bɔ́alɛ ni, ɛna nɛ ɔwanyɔ Abraham kate kamɛ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Melkizedek natsɛlamɔ ɔwanyɔ a.

english_verse: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Levifɔ a tɔsɔfɔ kanya kamɛ fɔtsã mɔ bɛkpa Israelfɔ mbla a. Ade finyaa ntɛ Levifɔ tɔsɔfɔ kanya a kakpɔla kɛkpɔla ni, kufɛ fɔtásĩ kĩ ɔsɔfɔ bɛbã ndɛ Melkizedek aye ebuki ɛnɛ, kĩ nnɛ́ Aron kasinu a kamɛ eto.

english_verse: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bɛvɛmɛna tɔsɔfɔ kanya a ni, kɛnɛ kĩ kavɛmɛna kɛya nɛ tɔsɔfɔ kanya mbla a lɛmɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
translation_verse: Boe Ɔlate kĩ ye akũ fɔlɛtsa bɔlamɛna kalɛ a to katete bɛbã kamɛ, ade ke katete a kamɛ obi odima tánya tɔ̃ ɔsɔfɔ a.

english_verse: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
translation_verse: Fɔnɛ fɔwaa pefee kĩ Yuda katete kamɛ bɛma ye, lɛmɛ Mose tábɔa katete kĩĩ dĩ ɛlɛ tɔsɔfɔ kanya lɛtsama. Yesu La Ndɛ Ɔsɔfɔ Melkizedek Aye.

english_verse: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛ kpalɛ fɔba kĩ ɔsɔfɔ bɛbã kĩ ɛla ndɛ Melkizedek aye ya.

english_verse: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
translation_verse: Oti kĩĩ nya ɔsɔfɔ nnɛ́ nɛ batidziwa mbla onukpɛ̃, mbom ɛtsã nkpã kĩ ɛla kawũna a kɔbɛ̃ akũ ɛya.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ, “Aanya ɔsɔfɔ daa, nɛ Melkizedek osi a akũ.”

english_verse: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
translation_verse: Foesũ bɛnyɛ mbla kɔɛ a biyi nɛ ɔɔ́wɛ̃, kitonɛkĩ ɛtána kɔbɛ̃ ĩye nfasɔ adima.

english_verse: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Mose mbla a tápuli ɛkɛna lɛtsama lɛkpɔla. Finyaa budeĩ kɔnɛte kĩ kɔnɛnɛ kɔbɛ̃, kĩ bɔtsɛ̃ koe akũ bɔafuã Baguma.

english_verse: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yesu yanya ɔsɔfɔ a, Baguma lɔ fubo ɛkpa ye ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate a lɔ fubo ade ɛláadani ye adzuni a, ‘Aanya ɔsɔfɔ daa.’ ”

english_verse: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
translation_verse: Fubo kĩĩ sũ Yesu dani apam a kĩ ɛnɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ kɔlaa a liti ɔdzanɛ.

english_verse: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
translation_verse: Ntsitsemu bɛbã buki edeĩ. Kɔkɔɛ a basɔfɔ nɛ lidede, kitonɛkĩ bɛkaku mɔatsɛ mɔatsɛ kafɔnɛ adzuma a.

english_verse: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Yesu kɔ̃ dzi nkpã daa sũ ni, ye tɔsɔfɔ kanya a lɛláato ye akũ tɛnaa odima akũ.

english_verse: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
translation_verse: Foesũ yaapuli ɛtɛ bati a kĩ batsã ye akũ kaya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ, kitonɛkĩ edzi nkpã daa, ade yake Baguma aduli kakpa be a.

english_verse: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
translation_verse: Foesũ Yesu nyɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a kĩ busĩ. Ɛnyɛ oti kpalɛwa kĩ nfumusu adima lɛláa ye akũ, ade tɔkpa tidima lɛláa ye akũ a. Bɛnyɛ ye nɛ tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ kamɛ, ade bɛdɛla ye flee ɛtɛ̃ ode a.

english_verse: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛlá ndɛ basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ nkaɛ a, ade fɔtásĩ kĩ yabɔa afɔliɛ luwi nyɛ luwi nɛ ye mɔawɛ tɔkpa akũ, lɔkɔa ke liti ɛbɔa nɛ bati bɛbã tɔkpa lɔlɔ. Ɛbɔa afɔliɛ deedimɔapɛ ɛkpa fɔkɔ a flee, nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ a.

english_verse: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_verse: Mose mbla a apau banyole kĩ badɔ kadzina be basɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ flee, lɛmɛ Baguma tsã ye fubo kalɔ akũ nɛ mbla a liti a, ɛpau Obi a kĩ ɛkɛna ye ɛkpɔla a yanya ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ flee daa.

english_verse: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
translation_verse: Kalɛ titiliwu nɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɔlamɛna kalɛ a kamɛ nyɛ kĩ budeĩ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee kĩĩ odu, kĩ edzi nɛ Obu Lɛgã Kpale lɛgãkpo a sɔmɔna nɛ ode.

english_verse: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
translation_verse: Yasum ndɛ ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ aye nɛ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee a kĩ kɛnyɛ lima patampa wĩ a kamɛ, kĩ nnɛ́ otidziwa lɛbɔa de, mbom boe Ɔlate a lɛbɔa de.

english_verse: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
translation_verse: Badzina ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ flee kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ ɛkɔa afɔliɛ fɔtsa ɛkpa Baguma ɛbɔa afɔliɛ lɛmɛ, foesũ kɛnɛ kĩ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee kĩĩ lɛmɛ ɛna afɔliɛ litsedi ɛkpa Baguma.

english_verse: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ tite akũ edzi ni, kufɛ ɛláanya ɔsɔfɔ kɔlaa, kitonɛkĩ basɔfɔ a kĩ be akɔa futetsa kakpa Baguma nɛ Yudafɔ mbla a onukpɛ̃ a deĩ kɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
translation_verse: Adzuma kĩ bakɛna ndɛ basɔfɔ aye nɛ kasumkɔ̃ la ndɛ fukĩ fudeĩ nɛ ode a kuyo odi. Ade foesũ kĩ Mose aabɔa Lima Patampa Kpalɛwa a ni, Baguma bi ye kĩ, “Dã kĩ ɔkɛna nɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsa nɔ nɛ ɔtɔ akũ a onukpɛ̃.”

english_verse: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ finyaa Baguma kpa Yesu ɔsɔfɔ adzuma kĩ ɛnɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ basɔfɔ a tide kɔlaa, ndɛ ayekĩ apam a kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ Baguma mɔ ye bati kayite a nɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ a, kitonɛkĩ bɛkɛna ye nɛ bɔhɛ a kĩ ɛnɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ a akũ.

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Ntɛ mfumsu adima ɛtana nɛ apam atɔnyade a akũ ni, kufɛ fɔláabuki fusĩ kĩ bakɛna ayade.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Baguma amɔ mfumsu nɛ ye bati akũ kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate a lɛ kĩ, fuwi fudi aya kĩ maakɛna apam vɔɛ̃ mɔ Israel kade a mɔ Yuda kade a lɛmɛ.

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: Apam kĩĩ lɛláana ndɛ ekĩ nkɛna mɔ be bawanyɔ nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ mpi be nɛ akpɔ nkpã be nnɛmɛna nɛ Egipte tite akũ a. Kitonɛkĩ bɛtákɛna nɛ apam a kĩ nkɛna mɔ be a akũ, foesũ yĩ malo mbɔa kanɛ nkpa be.”

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Ɔlate a buki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade apam kĩ maakɛna mɔ Israel bati nɛ fuwi a kĩ fɔ́aya a liti a. Maakɔa yĩ mbla ndo nɛ be adzuni kamɛ, ade maatsɛlɛ ye nɛ be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ a. Maanya be Baguma, ade be baanya yĩ bati a.

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Odima lɛláabuki ɛtsa ɔkɔba fɔtsa ĩye ebi ɔwaɛ̃ kĩ, ‘Nyi Baguma,’ kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi aanyi yĩ kito babisɛ̃ akũ kɛnawo banɔkɔɛ̃ akũ.

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: Maamɔ be kɔnya nɛ be ɔbla kpa a kĩ bɛbɔa akũ, ade nláabuki nte anɛ nɛ be tɔkpa akũ kɔlaa a.”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Kĩ Baguma lɛ kĩ apam vɔɛ̃ a, fɔatsa kĩ ɛkɛna atɔnyade a akɔɛ. Ade finyaakĩĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lɛkɛna lɔkɔɛ ni, owi lɛláabuna mɔ laatɛ̃ lɛnaa.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Apam atɔnyade a na tɛbla kĩ te akũ batsã kasum, mɔ tite akũ kɔla, awã kĩ basum lɛmɛ.

english_verse: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
translation_verse: Bike patampa bɛkɔa lima bɛsɛ̃ ye kamɛ. Bavɛ kakɔ katɔnyade a kĩ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa, ade awã lɛmɛ bike ɔsɛdza otse mɔ opunu a kĩ ye akũ bate abodoo kakpa Baguma a.

english_verse: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɔla kamɛ katsua lima a liti ni, patampa bɛbã deĩ kĩ bavɛ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ awã nyɛ sika ngbã atɛnyɛ a kĩ ye akũ bate lifii ɔnyɔ, apam adakã kĩ bidue mɔ sika ngbã. Adakã a kamɛ fɔtsa a nyɛ sika ngbã okluwa kĩ funitsã kĩ bavɛ mana deĩ nɛ ye kamɛ, Aron otse kĩ yake katsã, kĩ ɛnyɛ tɔɔta, mɔ boe tratra nviã kĩ be akũ bɛtsɛlɛ mbla ewua a bite a.

english_verse: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
translation_verse: Bɛmɛ Kerubifɔ kutidu bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ adakã a akũ, kĩ banyɛ Baguma kukũkpalɛ katsa, ade bɛdzɛlɛ be fupoki bɛtsa awã kĩ bakɔa bati tɔkpa kate a. Lɛmɛ bɔláapuli bɔlɛ kɔdzɛla nɛ fɔtsa a na flee akũ dzununuunu finyaa.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Ade ntɛ bɛmanɛ fɔtsa a biyi lɔmɔ a, basɔfɔ awo nɛ Patampa atɔnyade a kamɛ kakɛna be tɔsɔfɔ adzuma.

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a odi awo nɛ kɔla kɔyade a kamɛ deedimɔapɛ nɛ kateli kamɛ. Ntɛ yanaa a, kɛnɛkĩ yalɛ bayaɛ babɔanɛ kĩ yaakɔa ɛbɔa afɔliɛ ɛkpa Baguma nɛ ye mɔawɛ tɔkpa mɔ tɔkpa a kĩ bati kɛna nɛ katanyi kamɛ a lɔlɔ.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Nɛ nhihiɛ kĩĩ onukpɛ̃ ni, Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a nyɛ ɛtsa kĩ, nɛ ayekĩ patampa atɔnyade a buki ɛdzɛ̃ sũ ni, osi tábinya kĩ bati biwo nɛ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee a kamɛ.

english_verse: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ nyɛ nfantuhũ fɔtsa katsa fɔkpa nviã fuwi kĩĩ. Fɔatsa kĩ afɔliɛ mɔ futetsa a kĩ bakpa Baguma a lápuli kafɔ̃ basumunɛ a ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ akpalɛ flefle,

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ be ɔsum a manɛ mɔ funitsã, bavɛ mɔ akũ kakpalɛ tɛmaniɛ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ odi. Fukĩĩ flee nyɛ katinya kamɛ tɛbla kĩ bakɛna kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma aalɔla foe.

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Kristo ya kɔkɔɛ ndɛ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee ɛkpa fɔtsa wĩ a kĩ fɔya kɔkɔɛ a. Patampa Kpalɛwa a kĩ ye kamɛ yasum a pɔnɛ lububu, ade ɛkpɔla ɛba akɔɛ a. Ade ye kɔ̃ nnɛ́ patampa kĩ batidziwa like ye, ade foe nyɛ kĩ ɛtáto katinya kĩĩ kamɛ.

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: Kĩ Kristo wo nɛ patampa a kamɛ ɛnaa Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee kamɛ deedimɔapɛ a, ɛtákɔa kutukpabi mɔ banantsue babi babɔanɛ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ, mbom ye mɔawɛ babɔanɛ ɛkɔa ɛvɛlɛ nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna ɛma boe.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Kutukpabi, banantsue babɔanɛ mɔ ɔnantsue obi kĩ bipila ye batɔ bakɔa kasã nɛ oti a kĩ efĩĩ deĩ nɛ ye akũ, lɔkɔa ye akũ akpalɛ.

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ fukĩĩ nyɛ ɔnɔkɔali sũ ni, nde mɔ Kristo babɔanɛ lɛláakɛna fɔtsa kpale kɔlaa? Kristo tsã daa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kɔbɛ̃ akũ, ɛkɔa ye a kĩ efĩĩ adima lɛláa ye akũ a, ɛbɔa afɔliɛ ɛkpa Baguma. Ye babɔanɛ a akpalɛ boe tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, ɛnyɛ boe tɛmaniɛ kĩ tɛlá nfasɔ ayekĩ bɔ́apuli busum Baguma a kĩ edzi nkpã a.

english_verse: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
translation_verse: Ade fukĩĩ sũ Kristo kɛna apam vɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔkpɔlanɛ, ayekĩ bati a kĩ Baguma vɛ a, aana daa adzapadiɛ a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a. Fukĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ kitonɛkĩ oti liku, ade fɔnyɛ tɔkpa a kĩ bati kɛna nɛ apam atɔnyade a kamɛ a.

english_verse: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
translation_verse: Awã kĩ adzapadiɛ akũ ovoli deĩ ni, baákɛna ye akũ adzuma ntɛ bɛtámɔ́ foe kamɛ kĩ ovoli a ɔkɔnanɛ ku,

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: kitonɛkĩ adzapadiɛ akũ ovoli lɛnyɛ́ lɛtsama ntɛ oti a kĩ ɛkɛna ye a dzi nkpã. Mbɔɛntɛ oti a ku fɔmɔ baapuli bɛkɛna ye akũ adzuma.

english_verse: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ apam atɔnyade a malo ni, bɛtánɛ babɔanɛ liti bɛkɛna ye adzuma.

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: Mose nya lɔtɔ ede lɛtsã nɛ tɛbla flee a kĩ tideĩ nɛ mbla ovoli kamɛ a ɛkpa bati a. Ke liti a, ɛkɔa mui ɛtsɛka mɔ kutukpabi mɔ banantsue babɔanɛ, ɛkɔa kuveli tɛtsã mɔ tuwĩ mɔ ɔlɛ̃ ɛta ade ɛnyɛ ɛsã nɛ mbla ovoli a mɔ bati a flee akũ.

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade apam a kĩ Baguma kɛna mɔ mi a kĩ mikɛna nɛ ye akũ a babɔanɛ nɛ awĩ a.”

english_verse: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Mose lɛmɛ akɔa babɔanɛ kasã nɛ Patampa Kpalɛwa a mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ foe akũ adzuma bakɛna nɛ kasum kamɛ a flee akũ a.

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Ɔnɔkɔali, bɔ́apuli bɔlɛ kĩ nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, babɔanɛ bakɔa kakpalɛ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa akũ, ade ntɛ bɛtáko babɔanɛ nɛ tite ni, tɔkpa kakɔa kate láya. Kristo Kɔa Ye Akũ Ɛbɔa Afɔliɛ Ɛnyɛ Tɔkpa.

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: Foesũ fusĩ kĩ bɛkpalɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔla ndɛ ode fude kuyo a akũ nɛ osi kĩĩ akũ. Lɛmɛ ode fɔtsa a mɔawɛ kɔ̃ afɔliɛ kĩ ɛnɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ tite akũ ade bɛkɔa bɛkpalɛ foe akũ.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Kristo táwo ɛnaa Patampa Kpalɛwa a kĩ batidziwa like, kĩ ɛnyɛ ode fude a odi kuyo a kamɛ. Ewo ɛnaa ode mɔawɛ a, awã kĩ finyaa ɛnanyɛ ye akũ nɛ Baguma anɛ nɛ boe onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ Kristo táwo nɛ awã kĩ yakɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ ebuli ndɛ ayekĩ Yudafɔ Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a akɔa ɔyaɛ babɔanɛ kawomɛna nɛ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee a kamɛ kateli nyɛ kateli a.

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ aye fɔla ni, kufɛ kɛnɛ kĩ ɛkaamɔ lubo lɔkɔ pii, kito anɛ katinya kayɔkayi a. Lɛmɛ finyaa kĩ katinya anaa kawũna a, Kristo ya deedimɔapɛ kĩ yakɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ ɛnyɛ tɔkpa.

english_verse: We die only once, and then we are judged.
translation_verse: Baguma hihiɛ kĩ otidziwa eku deedimɔapɛ, ade nɛ wuda liti a, yaadzɛmɔ ye.

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: Ade ayea Kristo lɛmɛ kɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ deedimɔapɛ, ɛnyɛ bati pii tɔkpa a ɛnamɛna a. Yaabuki ɛya lɛyade, lɛmɛ ɛláabuki ɛkɛna lɛtsama nɛ tɔkpa akũ, mbom yaatɛ bati a kĩ bidzi badã ye anɛ a bɛna nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ mbla a lɛnyɛ́ fɔtsa wĩ a kutidu, mbom ɛla ndɛ fɔtsa wĩ a kĩ fɔ́aya a kuyo kɛkɛ. Nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ ni, kateli nyɛ kateli basɔfɔ a abɔa afɔliɛ mɔapɛ a kakpa Baguma. Ade foesũ ntɛ fɔtsã mbla kĩĩ akũ ni, bɛláapuli bɛkɛna bati a kĩ baya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ a bɛkpɔla da a.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bipuli bɛkpalɛ bati a kĩ basum Baguma a akũ deedimɔapɛ ni, kufɛ be tɔwɔlɛ lɛláabuki tɛkakɔ be kɔtɔ bio kĩ tɔkpa deĩ nɛ be akũ, lɔkɔa bɛfɔ̃ afɔlibɔ flee kabɔa.

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ kĩ Kristo aaya nɛ katinya kamɛ a, ebi Baguma kĩ, “Aáwɛ afɔlibɔ mɔ futetsa, mbom ɔkɛna sukɛna ɔkpa yĩ.

english_verse: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
translation_verse: Ɔyaɛ kĩ bipila ye lodo nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ akũ ĩye tɔkpa kanyɛ afɔliɛ láwo nɔ anɛ.

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Ade ke nlɛ kĩ, ‘O Baguma, amɔ yĩ nɛ awĩ. Ĩya kĩ mayaakɛna nɔ kawɛ ndɛ ayekĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ yĩ akũ nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ a.’ ”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Aáwɛ afɔlibɔ, mɔ futetsa, ĩye ɔyaɛ kĩ bapila ye nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ akũ, mɔ tɔkpa kanyɛ afɔliɛ láwo nɔ anɛ.” Ɛlɛ fukĩĩ titɔ kĩ babɔa afɔliɛ kĩĩ flee nɛ mbla a onukpɛ̃ malo.

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ade ke ɛlɛ kĩ, “O Baguma, amɔ yĩ nɛ awĩ. Ĩya kĩ mayaakɛna nɔ kawɛ.” Nɛ osi kĩĩ akũ a, Baguma fɔnɛ afɔlibɔ kɔɛ a flee awɛ ɛkɔa Kristo afɔliɛ a edo nɛ te kadokɔ̃.

english_verse: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
translation_verse: Ayekĩ Yesu Kristo kɛna Baguma kawɛ akũ, ɛkɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ deedimɔapɛ, ɛkpa fɔkɔ flee ɛkpalɛ boe akũ.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Yudafɔ ɔsɔfɔ odi nyɛ odi akɛna ye ɔsɔfɔ adzuma luwi nyɛ luwi, kabɔa afɔliɛ mɔapɛ a kabuli, titɔ kĩ afɔlibɔ kĩĩ lɛláapuli ɛnyɛ tɔkpa malo.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kĩ Kristo kɔa ye akũ ɛbɔa afɔliɛ mɔapɛ nɛ tɔkpa akũ eyua a, ɛnadzi nɛ Baguma sɔmɔna,

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: awã kĩ edzi yadzɔlɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ Baguma aakɔa ye bakesĩnɛ ɛkɛna ye abɔa katekɔ̃.

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: Kristo kɔa afɔliɛ mɔapɛ a kĩ ɛbɔa a, ɛkɛna bati a kĩ bɛkpalɛ be akũ a bɛkpɔla daa.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a lɛmɛ adi adansiɛ nɛ foe akũ. Ɛnya lɔtɔ ɛlɛ kĩ,

english_verse: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate lɛ kĩ, ‘Ade apam vɔɛ̃ kĩ maakɛna mɔ be, nɛ lɔkɔ kĩ laya a kamɛ a. Maakɔa yĩ mbla a ndo nɛ be tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Ade maatsɛlɛ ye nɛ be adzuni kamɛ a.’ ”

english_verse: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
translation_verse: Ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Nláabuki nte anɛ nɛ be tɔkpa mɔ be ɔbla kpa akũ.”

english_verse: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
translation_verse: Foesũ ntɛ bɛkɔa fukĩĩ flee bite ni, tɔkpa kanyɛ afɔliɛ tábuki esĩ. Mifɔ̃ Bufuã Bɔya Nɛ Baguma Sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
translation_verse: Foesũ nwaɛ̃ amɛ, bɔtsã Yesu wuda akũ, bɔna osi faa kĩ bɔ́apuli buwo nɛ Kakɔ Kpalɛwa Flee a kamɛ.

english_verse: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
translation_verse: Ebinya nkpã osi vɔɛ̃ ɛkpa boe ɛtsãmɛna lima a kĩ lɛsɛ̃ patampa kamɛ a kĩ lɛdza lɛkpa ye sukɛna a.

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: Budeĩ Ɔsɔfɔ Kpale kĩ ye adãmɛna Baguma tɔtɔ a akũ,

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: foesũ mifɔ̃ bɔkɔa ɔnɔkɔali ɔwɔlɛ, mɔ katɛkado kĩ kitim, mɔ tɔwɔlɛ kĩ tɛkpalɛ kĩ kɔtɔkaku lɛláa, mɔ sukɛna kĩ bɛwɔla se mui klɛĩklɛĩ bufuã Baguma.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bupi kɔnɛte fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bɔanyɛ katsa a budomɔ akũ pintii nɛ boe katɛkado kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ bɔ́apuli bɔtɛ Baguma budo kĩ yaakɛna nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a akũ.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Mifɔ̃ bɔkɔna ayekĩ bɔ́ado boe bawaɛ̃ kɔbɛ̃ bibumɛna akũ akũ, bɛwɛ akũ kalɛ, bɛkɛna tudzuma wĩ lɛmɛ.

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: Mitafɔ̃ bɔfɔ̃ boe katsɛna kanaa ndɛ ayekĩ mi kamɛ badi akɛna a, mbom mifɔ̃ budo akũ kɔbɛ̃ bubu, kitonɛkĩ mimɔ́ kĩ boe Ɔlate Luwi a lamɛna kafuã.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ ntɛ bunyi ɔnɔkɔali a buyua, mɔ bubua pa bɔakɛna tɔkpa ni, afɔliɛ adima buki ɛláa kĩ yaapuli ɛnyɛ tɔkpa.

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: Mbom lɛtsa a kĩ lite nyɛ kĩ bɔadzi bɔkadã Kadzɛ mɔ libe a kĩ laaya a anɛ, mɔ fi gɛĩgɛĩ a kĩ fɔ́awɔla Baguma bakesĩnɛ a!

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi fum Mose mbla a ni, baámɔ ye kɔnya, kakɔ bakɔ ye ntɛ bɛdza nɛ bati nviã ĩye bati ɛlalɛ adansiɛ kadi akũ.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Kekɔ̃ mikɔna midã ni, litsa sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ aaya nɛ oti a kĩ esupa Baguma Obi a, kĩ ebu Baguma apam babɔanɛ a kĩ ɛkɔa ɛvɔlɛ tɔkpa nɛ ye akũ a fɔtsa pupulidza? Litsa lɛmɛ miakɔna kĩ laaya nɛ oti a kĩ yasupa Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a kĩ yamɔ bati kɔnya a akũ? Mikɔna midã sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ mɔ ɔsĩ a kĩ baakpa ye a!

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bunyina oti a kĩ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Yĩ aavia tɔkpa kɔtɔkase, yĩ aase te kɔtɔ.” Ebuki ɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate a aadzɛmɔ ye bati.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Fɔnyɛ libe paa kĩ odi aawo nɛ Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a kalɔkpa kamɛ!

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: Fɔkɔ fudi bavũna mi nɛ kanade, kakɛna mi awɔda. Fɔkɔ bɛbã lɛmɛ miakɔa mi akũ kabumɛna bikĩ bamɔna be lubo kĩĩ odu a.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, mimɔ́ lubo ndɛ bati a kĩ bɛwaa kɔla, ade titɔ kĩ bɛkɔa kɔbɛ̃ bɛtɛ mi akũ fɔtsa flee nɛ mi awɛ malo ni, mido ɔwɔlɛ mitɛ foe mɔ kawɔlɛ, kitonɛkĩ minyina kĩ mibuki mideĩ lɛtsa a kĩ lɛnɛnɛ lɛbɛ̃, kĩ laana daa a.

english_verse: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
translation_verse: Foesũ akpɔ ɛtaveli mi, kitonɛkĩ fɔ́ama mi kɔtɔkase kpale.

english_verse: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
translation_verse: Fusĩ kĩ miana ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ, ayekĩ míapuli mikɛna nɛ Baguma kawɛ akũ, lɔkɔa mi awɛ aata lɛtsa a kĩ ɛlɛ eyi a.

english_verse: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a lɛ kĩ, “Kite sukuloo oti a kĩ yaaya a, aaya, ade ɛláabuna owi a.

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Yĩ oti kpĩĩ aatɛ yĩ edo, edzi nkpã. Lɛmɛ ntɛ odi muniki liti ni, ɛláabuki ewo yĩ anɛ.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ boe kɔ̃ bɔnyɛ́ bati a kĩ bamuniki liti kanaa kayɛla kamɛ, mbom budeĩ katɛ kado ade bɔna nkpã katɛ a.

english_verse: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
translation_verse: Katɛkado nyɛ fɔatsa kĩ nɔ anɛ teĩ nɛ foe akũ a anɛ kadã mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ aámɔ mɔ anɛ a foe katɛkado kĩ fudeĩ.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Katɛkado akũ fɔtsã mɔ Baguma mɔ́ kawɔlɛ nɛ boe atitifɔ bawanyɔ a akũ a.

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Katɛkado kamɛ fɔtsã mɔ bunu foe kayɔ kĩ Baguma kalɛ a ɛkɔa ɛbɔa katinya, ayekĩ lɛtsa a kĩ bɔamɔ a, ɛkɛna de etomɛna lɛtsa a kĩ bɔámɔ mɔ anɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
translation_verse: Katɛkado lɛfɔ̃ Abel bɔa afɔliɛ kĩ ɛnɛnɛ ɛbɛ̃ Kain ade ɛkpa Baguma. Ye katɛkado kamɛ ɛtsã ɛna Baguma adansiɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ, kitonɛkĩ Baguma mɔawɛ lɛtɛ ye futetsa a. Titɔ kĩ Abel ku malo ni, ɛtsã ye katɛkado kamɛ ebuki yalɛ kɔdzɛla nviã.

english_verse: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
translation_verse: Katɛkado lɛfɔ̃ bɛbɔɛ Henok bɛnamɛna ode kĩ ɛtáku. Odima tábuki ɛmɔ́ ye, kitonɛkĩ Baguma yabɔɛ ye ɛnamɛna ode. Fɔmɔ ɛbɔɛ ye ɛnamɛna ode a ni, Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a di adansiɛ nɛ ye akũ kĩ ye fɔlɛtsa wo Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi lɛlá katɛkado ni, ye fɔlɛtsa lɛláawo Baguma anɛ, kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaya nɛ Baguma sɛkɛ̃ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ɛna katɛkado kĩ Baguma deĩ, ade ɛnyɛ bikĩ bawɛ ye liti osi a kɔtɔ osenɛ a.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Katɛkado lɛfɔ̃ Noa tɛ Baguma kɔdɛ nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ fɔ́aya kĩ ɛláapuli ɛmɔ́ a akũ. Ebu Baguma ade ke ɛmɛ mui anɛ adakã kpale kĩ ɛnyɛ ye mɔ ye tɔtɔ bati a. Nɛ fukĩĩ sũ ni, ye katɛkado nyɛ kɛtsa kĩ katinya kamɛ bati ku kɔtɔ ade Baguma bɔɛ ye kĩ ɛnyɛ oti kpĩĩ a.

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Katɛkado lɛfɔ̃ Abraham bu Baguma, kĩ ɛvɛ ye kĩ ɛdzakũ ɛnaa tite a kĩ taanya ye adzapadiɛ akũ a. Ade ɛdzakũ nɛ ye mɔawɛ tite akũ ɛnaa awã kĩ enyína malo a.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Katɛkado ɛkɔa ɛnanya ɔnɔvɔɛ̃ nɛ tite a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi yaakpa ye a akũ. Edzi nɛ patampa kamɛ, ade ayea Isak mɔ Yakob lɛmɛ dzi nɛ tɛpatampa kamɛ a, titɔ kĩ bɛkpa be tite mɔapɛ a bɔhɛ a.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Abraham kɛna foe lɔmɔ, kitonɛkĩ ɛkadã anɛ kakpa ɔmatɔ kpale a kĩ Baguma awɛ tsua ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ kayɔkayi futekũ mɔ ɔsĩ akũ.

english_verse: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
translation_verse: Katɛkado lɛfɔ̃ Sara lɛmɛ na kɔbɛ̃ epuli ɛtsãmɔ kɔma, titɔ kĩ ɛkɛna katsɔkpa malo, kitonɛkĩ ebu Baguma a kĩ ɛkpa ye bɔhɛ a ɔnɔkɔalini.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Foesũ kito Abraham mɔapɛ a kĩ eku eyua malo a kamɛ babi mɔ bawa kĩ bɛnɛ lidede ndɛ fusibii, mɔ mpo abuĩ dã kĩ bɛláapuli bɛkã a nɛ́ bito a.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ flee ku nɛ be katɛkado kamɛ, kĩ be awɛ táta lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma lɛ eyi kĩ yaakpa be a. Lɛmɛ bɛmɔ́ foe mɔ lugo, bɛtɛ foe mɔ kawɔlɛ ade bitunɔ nɛ foe akũ kĩ banɔvɔɛ̃ mɔ basitsãnɛ banɛ nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ a.

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bati a kĩ balɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ odu a, nyɛ bɛtsa pefee kĩ bɛlamɔ be mɔawɛ tite kawɛ.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Ntɛ tite a kĩ te akũ bɛdzakũ a bɛkakɔna ni, kufɛ bɛna osi bimuniki bɛnaa te akũ.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Mbom bɛkawɛ tite kĩ tɛnɛnɛ tɛbɛ̃, kĩ tɛnyɛ ode tide a lɛmɛ. Nɛ foesũ fɔákɔ Baguma kɔnyɔã kĩ bavɛ ye kĩ be Baguma, kitonɛkĩ ɛlɔla ɔmatɔ kpale odi eyi be.

english_verse: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
