translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse lɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli a kabi, Oti a kĩ ɛnya akũ ni, maakpa ye nkpã kutse a kĩ kɔdzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma kɔbã a kĩ bavɛ Paradiso a kamɛ babi badi ɛnya.” Ɔlɛdo a Kĩ Ɛkpa Smirna Asɔli a.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kama Smirna Asɔli ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a. Ade ɔlɛdo kĩ eto oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ Ɔtɔnyade mɔ Owũnade, oti a kĩ eku lɛmɛ ebuki edzi nkpã a sɛkɛ̃ nɛ awĩ a.

english_verse: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina nɔ ɔhau flee. Ĩnyina kĩ ohiãni anɛ lɛmɛ nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, osikani anɛ! Ĩnyina tuwuli kpa a kĩ bikĩ bavɛ be akũ Yudafɔ lɛmɛ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a, alɛ nɛ nɔ akũ. Bɛnyɛ Abonsam katsu a kamɛ bati.

english_verse: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
translation_verse: Tavila lubo lidi nyɛ lidi kĩ laaya nɛ nɔ akũ a kamɔ. Dã! Abonsam aafɔ̃ bipili mi kamɛ badi bido nɛ kɔla ɛsɔ mi ɛdã, ade míamɔ lubo fuwi ewua a. Di ɔnɔkɔali kakpa yĩ ntɛ fɔ́anya wuda malo, lɔkɔa maakpã nɔ nkpã ndɛ akũ kanya lɛgãbɔɛ aye.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse lɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli bati a kabi! Bati a kĩ baanya akũ ni, wuda ayade a lɛláatamɔ be.” Ɔlɛdo a Kĩ Ɛkpa Pergamo Asɔli a.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kama Pergamo Asɔli a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, Ade oti a kĩ ɛnɛɛ atugbe mɔ ɔbɔ̃ a fɔlɛtsa nɛ awĩ a.

english_verse: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina nɔ kadzikɔ̃ nɛ awã kĩ Abonsam lɛgãkpo a deĩ a. Opini yĩ dĩ mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ. Lɔkɔ a kĩ bɛkɔ Antipa, yĩ ɔdansini mɔ ɔnɔkɔalini nɛ mi kamɛ nɛ awã kĩ Abonsam dzi malo ni, ɔtámuniki nɛ yĩ liti, mbom ɔtɛ yĩ odo.

english_verse: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ ndeĩ nɔ akũ fɔlɛtsa sukuloo. Bati badi deĩ nɛ mi kamɛ kĩ bitomɔ Balam fɔtsa katsa. Ade Balam kĩĩ lɛtsa Balak tisi ɛkpã Israelfɔ edo nɛ tɔkpa kamɛ, ade ɛfɔ̃ bɛnya funitsã kĩ bɛtɔ bɛkpa baviɔ̃, ade bɛbɔa adɔpaa a.

english_verse: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
translation_verse: Ade ayea odeĩ bati badi nɛ mi kamɛ kĩ bitomɔ Nikolatiafɔ fɔtsa katsa liti a.

english_verse: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
translation_verse: Finyaa, dani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ! Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, maaya nɛ nɔ akũ mɔatsɛ nkɔa lɛpamɛ a kĩ lideĩ nɛ yĩ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ a nnɔ mɔ bati a.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
translation_verse: Oti kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse lɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli bati a kabi. Oti a kĩ ɛnya akũ ni, maakpa ye mana a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛkɔla a adi ɛnya. Bio maakpa ye de fitaa kĩ bɛtsɛlɛ dĩ vɔɛ̃ bite nɛ de akũ. Odima linyína dĩ a, mbɔɛntɛ owula a kĩ ɛtɛ de a odi.” Ɔlɛdo a Kĩ Ɛkpa Tiatira Asɔli a.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛna Tiatira Asɔli a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ kĩ, Ade fɔlɛtsa a kĩ futo Baguma Obi, kĩ ye anɛ atɛ̃ ndɛ fi luwotu aye, ade ye abɔa lɛmɛ amɔanɛ ndɛ kɔɔble kĩ bɛvɔlɛ tsɔlitsɔli a nɛ awĩ a.

english_verse: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina nɔ adzuma a kĩ ɔkɛna. Ĩnyina nɔ ɔdɔ, nɔ katɛkado, nɔ ɔsum, mɔ ayekĩ odeĩ ɔwɔlɛ bɔɛɛ. Ĩnyina kĩ ɔlamɛna adzuma kakɛna finyaa kaba ayekĩ ɔkɛna nɛ kayɔkayi a.

english_verse: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ kalɛ a kĩ ndeĩ nɛ nɔ akũ a nyɛ kĩ ɔkpa otsole Isabel a kĩ yavɛ ye akũ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ osi, kĩ yatsa yĩ basumunɛ fɔtsa, ade yakɛ be kĩ bɛbɔa adɔpaa, bɛnya funitsã kĩ bɛtɔ bɛkpa baviɔ̃ a.

english_verse: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
translation_verse: Nkpa ye lɔkɔ kĩ ɛdani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ, lɛmɛ yaáwɛ kĩ yafɔ̃ adɔpaa kabɔa.

english_verse: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
translation_verse: Foesũ maafɔ̃ ɛkahɔ nɛ ɔkɛna akũ, awã kĩ bɛmɔ bikĩ babɔa adɔpaa a, ntɛ bɛtádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ finyaa ni, baamɔ lubo mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ.

english_verse: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
translation_verse: Maakɔ bikĩ bitomɔ ye a flee lɔkɔa asafu a flee linyi kĩ yĩ nyɛ oti a kĩ enyina oti ɔwɔlɛ mɔ ye lɔlɔ kamɛ yededeede. Maase mi kamɛ odi nyɛ odi kɔtɔ nɛ ye adzuma a kĩ ɛkɛna a onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mi Tiatira bati nkaɛ a kĩ mitátɛ Isabel fɔtsa katsa a, mɔ mi a kĩ mitákɔlɛ fɔtsa a kĩ bavɛ Abonsam kadikanyi dzununuunu a kɔ̃ ni, minu kĩ nláalɔla mi fɔlɔtsa bɛbã.

english_verse: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mipi lɛtsa a kĩ mideĩ a mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kɛnaawo kĩ maaya.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Baguma Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli bati a kabi.”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kamɛna Sardis Asɔli a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, Oti a kĩ ɛnɛɛ Baguma Hũhũ evũkɔnɔ mɔ fusibii evũkɔnɔ a fɔlɛtsa nyɛ fukĩĩ, Ĩnyina nɔ adzuma a kĩ ɔkɛna a. Ĩnyina kĩ ɔtɛ dĩ kĩ odzi nkpã, lɛmɛ oku.

english_verse: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ nyinya, kado lɛtsa a kĩ lite nɛ nɔ kamɛ a kĩ laaku a kɔbɛ̃. Kitonɛkĩ mmɔ́ kĩ lɛtsa a kĩ ɔkɛna a tákpɔla tɔ̃ nɛ yĩ Baguma anɛ.

english_verse: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
translation_verse: Foesũ te anɛ nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ bɛtsa nɔ, mɔ lɛtsa a kĩ onu a akũ, kakɛna nɛ foe akũ, kadani ɔwɔlɛ kamɛ. Ntɛ ɔtányinya ni, maayawo nɛ nɔ akũ ndɛ oyekpanɛ aye, ade ɔláanyi lɔkɔ a kĩ maawo nɛ nɔ akũ a malo a.

english_verse: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ mi kamɛ badi sukuloo deĩ nɛ Sardis kĩ bɛtádo be tɛtadiɛ tuduli. Baado tɛtadiɛ ovoe bɔmɔ be bɔtsã, kitonɛkĩ bɛkpɔla bɛkpa foe.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
translation_verse: Owula a kĩ ɛnya akũ ni, yaado ye tɛtadiɛ ovoe lɔmɔ, ade nláawɔla ye dĩ ĩnyɛ nɛ nkpã badzinɛ ovoli a kamɛ. Maanyɛ ye ntsa nɛ kanade nɛ Nda mɔ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ anɛ kĩ yĩ ode yanɛ.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli bati a kabi.” Ɔlɛdo a Kĩ Ɛkpa Filadelfia Asɔli a.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kama Filadelfia Asɔli a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a. Ade fɔlɛtsa a kĩ futo oti kpalɛwa mɔ ɔnɔkɔalini a kĩ ɛnɛɛ Lɛgã Dawid safi a nɛ awĩ a. Ntɛ ebinya kɔla ni, odima lɛláapuli ɛbalɛ koe, ade ntɛ ɛbalɛ koe lɛmɛ ni, odima lápuli kabinya koe.

english_verse: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina nɔ adzuma a kĩ ɔkɛna a. Mbinya opunu ĩyi nɛ nɔ lɔtɔ kĩ odima lɛláapuli ɛbalɛ ye. Ĩnyina kĩ odeĩ kɔbɛ̃ kabii, lɛmɛ otomɔ yĩ fɔtsa katsa, ade ɔtámuniki nɛ yĩ liti a.

english_verse: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
translation_verse: Dã! Bati katsu kadi deĩ kĩ kɛnyɛ Satana kade, bavãlɛvã a kĩ balɛ kĩ bɛnyɛ Yudafɔ lɛmɛ bɛnyɛ́ Yudafɔ a. Maafɔ̃ bɛyaake aduli nɛ nɔ anɛ, lɔkɔa be flee binyi kĩ mawɛ nɔ kalɛ.

english_verse: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
translation_verse: Nɛ ayekĩ odo ɔwɔlɛ ɔkɛna nɛ yĩ kalɛ akũ, ɔmɔ lubo sũ ni, yĩ malo maanyɛ nɔ ntomɛna sɔhuiɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ laaya nɛ katinya kamɛ flee, kĩ baasɔ bati flee bɛdã a.

english_verse: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
translation_verse: Maaya mɔatsɛ. Pi lɛtsa a kĩ lideĩ nɛ nɔ awɛ a mɔ kɔɔba, ayekĩ odima lɛláatɛ nɔ akũ kanya lɛgãbɔɛ a nɛ nɔ awɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
translation_verse: Maakɛna oti a kĩ ɛnya akũ a ndɛ luku ɛdza nɛ Baguma Asɔli kɔla a kamɛ. Yaadza nɛ awã daa kĩ ɛláabuki ɛnɛ́ nɛ awã da. Maatsɛlɛ yĩ Baguma dĩ, mɔ yĩ Baguma ɔmatɔ dĩ kĩ lɛnyɛ Yerusalem vɔɛ̃ a kĩ ɛka eto ode a nte nɛ ye akũ. Maatsɛlɛ yĩ mɔawɛ dĩ vɔɛ̃ lɛmɛ nte nɛ ye akũ.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli bati a kabi.” Ɔlɛdo a Kĩ Ɛkpa Laodikea Asɔli a.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Tsɛlɛ fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ kakɔa kamɛna Laodikea Asɔli a ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ, Ade oti a kĩ ɛnyɛ Fɔya lɔmɔ, kĩ ɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali ɔdansini wĩ a, kĩ ye nyɛ fɔtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa katokɔ̃ a fɔlɛtsa nɛ awĩ kĩ,

english_verse: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
translation_verse: Ĩnyina nɔ adzuma a kĩ ɔkɛna a. Ĩnyina kĩ ɔtádɔ, ĩye ɔlá ovio. Mawɛ kĩ adɔ, ĩye ɔna ovio kufɛ!

english_verse: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ nɛ ayekĩ adã blɔblɔ, kĩ ɔtádɔ ĩye ɔlá ovio lɛmɛ sũ ni, maatua nɔ ntomɛna yĩ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ nwula!

english_verse: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
translation_verse: Alɛ kĩ, ‘Nna akũ, ndeĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa, ade ntásĩ lɛtsama lɛmɛ a.’ Lɛmɛ onyína ayekĩ ɔkɛna pasapasa, mɔ ayekĩ nɔ akũ akɔ kɔnya. Ɔnyɛ ohiãni, odzi ɔdzɔ, ade ɔnyɛ ɔnɛkunɛ a.

english_verse: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
translation_verse: Foesũ madɛ nɔ kĩ ya sika ngbã kĩ binyinyina nɛ fi kamɛ katomɛna yĩ sɛkɛ̃, ayekĩ aadani osikani. Ya lima ovoe lɛmɛ kasua akũ, ayekĩ bɛláamɔ nɔ kɔnyɔã ɔdzɔ a. Ya anɛ kugua kado nɛ banebi kamɛ, ayekĩ aamɔ fɔkɔ.

english_verse: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ mawɛ kalɛ a ni, makã be anɛ, ade mavɛlɛ be sɔtɔ a. Foesũ mibɔa mbɔdi midani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
translation_verse: Mitse! Ndzɛ̃ nɛ onyukpɛ̃ mabɔa awɛ. Ntɛ odi nu yĩ odo mɔ ebinya kɔla a ni, maawo nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bɔmɔ ye bɔnya, ade ye malo yaanya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ a.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ ɛnya akũ ni, maafɔ̃ edzi nɛ yĩ ɔwɛ̃ nɛ yĩ lɛgãkpo akũ, ndɛ ayekĩ yĩ malo ĩnya akũ ndzi nɛ Nda ɔwɛ̃ nɛ ye lɛgãkpo akũ a aye.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Hũhũ a lamɛna tɛsɔli a kabi!”

english_verse: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, nna kanyɛkatsa bɛbã kadi ade mmɔ́ kĩ bibinya opunu odi biyi nɛ ode a. Odo a kĩ ĩnya lɔtɔ nnu ndɛ kagbulɛɛ aye a, kĩ ebi yĩ tuwuli tɔ̃ a lɛ kĩ, “De kaya nɛ ode nɛ awĩ, lɔkɔa ntsa nɔ fɔtsa a kĩ kɛnɛ kĩ fɔya nɛ foe kamɛ nɛ fukĩĩ liti a.”

english_verse: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
translation_verse: Deedimɔapɛ Hũhũ wo nɛ yĩ kamɛ. Nɛ ode naa, mmɔ́ kĩ bike lɛgãkpo lidi kĩ odi dzi nɛ de akũ.

english_verse: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
translation_verse: Lukpo a akũ odzinɛ akũ ata muamua ndɛ ahuhɔɛ boe bawovoe mɔ bangbã kĩ bɛtɛ kɔya, ade odetɔ́ kamɛ sinigbɛnɛ kĩ se kalɛkadã la ndɛ tɔɔta gɔ̃ sinya lɛgãkpo a.

english_verse: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
translation_verse: Fɔgãkpo tɛya-ana bɛbã keni fusinya lɛgãkpo a, ade banɔkɔɛ̃ tɛya-ana kĩ bido tɛtadiɛ ovoe, bɛtsa sika ngbã fɔgãbɔɛ dzi nɛ foe akũ a.

english_verse: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Ɔdetɔ́ kamɛ kamɔanɛ, ɔplala kakpɛnɛ, mɔ fɔwɔla kɔɔba kanɛ kato lɛgãkpo a kamɛ. Tibia evũkɔnɔ kĩ tatɛ̃ dzɛ̃ nɛ lɛgãkpo a anɛ kamɛ. Ade te nyɛ Baguma Hũhũ evũkɔnɔ a.

english_verse: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
translation_verse: Litsedi kĩ ladã ndɛ mpo kĩ bɛkɔa ahuhɔɛ bɛkɛna deĩ nɛ awã nɛ lɛgãkpo a anɛ kamɛ. Ye kamɛ kpalɛ klɛĩklɛĩklɛĩ. Fɔtsa ɛna kĩ fudzi nkpã, kĩ fudeĩ banebi nɛ liti mɔ lɔtɔ tsua fusinya lɛgãkpo a nɛ fɔkɛ ɛna a akũ.

english_verse: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kĩ fudzi nkpã a fɔtɔnyade a la ndɛ dzata, fɔyade a la ndɛ ɔnantsuenyole, fɔlalɛde a deĩ anɛ ndɛ otidziwa, ade fɔnade a anɛ la ndɛ ɔkɔliɛ kĩ yayidza a.

english_verse: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
translation_verse: Fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a fudi nyɛ fudi deĩ fupoki evũ, ade banebi tsɛna foe kamɛ mɔ foe akũ flee a. Baáfɔ̃ tɔnɔ kasã lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate Baguma Kpale, nɔ akũ kpalɛ! Nɔ akũ kpalɛ, nɔ akũ kpalɛ! Ɔna anɛ kɔkɔɛ, odeĩ finyaa, ade aabuki ɔya a.”

english_verse: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a sã kamu, obu, mɔ kɔlɛkanyɛ tɔnɔ kakpa oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo akũ, kĩ edzi nkpã daa daa a. Ntɛ bɛsã te lɔmɔ a,

english_verse: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
translation_verse: “Boe Ɔlate mɔ boe Baguma! Nɔ kĩĩ kukũkpalɛ, obu mɔ otumi fɛta fɔkpa, kitonɛkĩ nɔ lɛbɔa fɔtsa flee. Ɔbɔa foe ndɛ ayekĩ awɛ a, ade fudzi nɛ nɔ kawɛ onukpɛ̃ a.”

english_verse: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, mmɔ́ ovoli butawa odi nɛ oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo akũ a sɔmɔna awɛ. Bɛtsɛlɛ fɔtsa nɛ ye kamɛ mɔ liti ade bɛkɔa kɔma fɔtsa evũkɔnɔ bɛtalɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a.

english_verse: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
translation_verse: Mmɔ́ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ odi kĩ ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba nɛ ode, evia kĩ, “Owei lɛkpɔla kĩ yaapuli ɛwai kɔma a, ebinya ovoli butawa a?”

english_verse: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ odima lɛláa nɛ ode, nɛ tite akũ, ĩye nɛ tite ɔda kĩ yaapuli ebinya ovoli a, ĩye ɛdã ye kamɛ.

english_verse: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Mbe paa, kitonɛkĩ bɛtámɔ odima kĩ ɛkpɔla kĩ yabinya ovoli a, ĩye ɛdã ye kamɛ.

english_verse: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
translation_verse: Ade banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔmɔa bi yĩ kĩ, “Fɔ̃ kabe! Dã! Dzata a kĩ eto Yuda katete kamɛ a, kĩ ɛnyɛ Dawid kasinu kamɛ obi a nya akũ, kĩ ɛkpɔla kĩ yawai kɔma fɔtsa evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛtalɛ ovoli a onukpɛ̃, ebinya ye lɛmɛ.”

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
translation_verse: Kĩ ndã a, mmɔ́ Kavelibi kadi kĩ kɛdzɛ̃ nɛ lɛgãkpo a kayite nɛ fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a kamɛ, ade banɔkɔɛ̃ tɛya-ana a sinya ye a. Fɔdã ndɛ kakɔ bɛkɔ Kavelibi a. Kavelibi a deĩ tɛlɛ evũkɔnɔ mɔ banebi evũkɔnɔ kĩ fɔnyɛ Baguma hũhũ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ edo nɛ tite flee akũ a.

english_verse: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
translation_verse: Kavelibi a naa kɛnatɛ ovoli a etomɛna oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo a akũ a sɔmɔna awɛ.

english_verse: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
translation_verse: Bɛsã ɔnɔ vɔɛ̃ odi kĩ, “Nɔ kĩĩ lɛkpɔla kĩ atɛ ovoli a lɔkɔa ɔwai kɔma evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛtalɛ ye onukpɛ̃ a. Kitonɛkĩ nɔ bɛkɔ, ade ɔkɔa nɔ babɔanɛ, ɔya bati ɔkpa Baguma a. Bati kĩ bito fude nyɛ fude, tide nyɛ tide, ɔmaĩ nyɛ ɔmaĩ kamɛ, mɔ batidziwa ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ a kamɛ.

english_verse: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
translation_verse: Ɔkɛna be lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ basɔfɔ kĩ bisum boe Baguma, ade baanya fɔgã nɛ tite akũ a.”

english_verse: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
translation_verse: Ade kĩ mbuki ndã a, nnu ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ mpim mpim kĩ bɛláapuli bɛkã odo. Bɛdza bisinya lɛgãkpo a, mɔ fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a, mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ tɛya-ana a.

english_verse: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
translation_verse: Ade bɛsã ɔnɔ kɔɔba kĩ, “Kavelibi a kĩ bɛkɔ a, lɛkpɔla kĩ katɛ otumi, mɔ akũ kana, mɔ nyansã, mɔ kɔbɛ̃, mɔ obu, mɔ kukũkpalɛ mɔ kamu!”

english_verse: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
translation_verse: Adeke nnu kĩ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ Baguma bɔa nɛ tite mɔ ode, mɔ tite ɔda, mɔ mpo kamɛ, mɔ fɔtsa a kĩ fudeĩ nɛ foe kamɛ a flee lamɛna ɔnɔ kasã kĩ, “Kamu, mɔ obu, mɔ kukũkpalɛ mɔ otumi fɔna daa fɔkpa, oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo akũ a mɔ Kavelibi a!”

english_verse: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a tɛ futunɔ kĩ, “Fɔya lɔmɔ!” Ade banɔkɔɛ̃ a tsa anɛ nɛ tite bisum Baguma mɔ Kavelibi a.

english_verse: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ kĩ Kavelibi a wai kɔma evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛkɔa bɛtalɛ ovoli a foe fɔtɔnyade. Ade nnu kĩ fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a kamɛ fɔmɔa lɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ ɔplala lɛkpɛnɛ kĩ, “Ya!”

english_verse: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
translation_verse: Ndã fɔkɔ ade mmɔ́ ɔpɔnkɔ ovoe odi a. Oti a kĩ edzi nɛ ye akũ a nɛɛ kɔta nɛ awɛ, ade bɛkpa ye lɛgãbɔɛ lidi a. Edzi ye ɛnɛ ndɛ kawũ akũ ɔnyanɛ aye kĩ yanaanya akũ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Kavelibi a wai kɔma kɔyade a, ade nnu kĩ fɔtsa a kĩ fudzi nkpã a fɔyade a lɛ kĩ, “Ya!”

english_verse: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
translation_verse: Ɔpɔnkɔ bɛbã kĩ ɛyɔɔlɛ nɛ́. Bɛkpa oti a kĩ edzi nɛ ye akũ a kɔbɛ̃ kĩ ɛnyɛ lukudɔ nɛ tite akũ, lɔkɔa bati bɛkɔ be akũ akũ. Bɛkpa ye kɔpã kpale kudi.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
translation_verse: Kavelibi a wai kɔma kɔlalɛde a, ade nnu kĩ fɔtsa a kĩ fudzi nkpã a fɔlalɛde a lɛ kĩ, “Ya!” Kĩ ndã fɔkɔ a, mmɔ́ ɔpɔnkɔ tũũtũ odi. Oti a kĩ edzi nɛ ye akũ a nɛɛ sɔbɔtsaɛ a kĩ bakɔa kakali fɔtsa a nɛ awɛ.

english_verse: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
translation_verse: Nnu odo odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a kayite ɛlɛ kĩ, “Kamɔnɛ lɔɔda mɔa lɛtɛ luwi mɔa kɔtɔkase, ade fugbanɔ tsensi futukũ ɛlalɛ fɔtɛ luwi mɔa kɔtɔkase, lɛmɛ mitabula kugua mɔ waĩ kade!”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, Kavelibi a wai kɔma fɔnade a, ade nnu kĩ fɔtsa a kĩ fudzi nkpã a fɔnade a lɛ kĩ, “Ya!”

english_verse: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
translation_verse: Kĩ ndã fɔkɔ a, mmɔ́ ɔpɔnkɔ odi kĩ ye akũ la ndɛ batɔ aye. Oti a kĩ edzi nɛ ye akũ a dĩ nyɛ Wuda ade Kanɔkpade tomɔ ye nɛ liti a. Bɛkpa be kɔbɛ̃ nɛ katinya kamɛ kasɛ̃ kanade a akũ, kĩ bɛkɔa kawũkanɔ, kɔlɛ, fɔsɔ kpa mɔ bayaɛ kpa bɛkɔ bati.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
translation_verse: Kĩ Kavelibi a wai kɔma kulode a, mmɔ́ bati a kĩ bɛkɔ, nɛ ayekĩ bide lɛtsã nɛ Baguma kalɛ a akũ, mɔ ayekĩ bidi adansiɛ nɛ ke akũ sũ a tɛkla nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kayɔ.

english_verse: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
translation_verse: Bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Baguma Kpale, oti kpalɛwa mɔ ɔnɔkɔalini anɛ! Lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe koũ aadzɛmɔ tite akũ bati, lɔkɔa ɔvɛlɛ bikĩ bɛkɔ boe a sɔtɔ?”

english_verse: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
translation_verse: Bɛkpa be kamɛ odi nyɛ odi atadiɛ ovoe wulaa ade bibi be kĩ bibuki bidzi bɛwɛya sukuloo kɛnaawo lɔkɔ a kĩ baakɔa be bakɔba basumunɛ mɔ be bawaɛ̃ a kĩ baakɔ ndɛ be aye a lidede bibumɔ be, ayekĩ kakã a onukpɛ̃ aakpɔla.

english_verse: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
translation_verse: Mmɔ́ Kavelibi a kĩ ɛwai kɔma kuvũde a. Tite kuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ade suwa dani ndɛ lima ɔɔwɔ tsutsulitsui, odetɔ lɛmɛ dani gbãã ndɛ babɔanɛ aye a.

english_verse: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
translation_verse: Fusibii ka fɔtɔ nɛ tite ndɛ ayekĩ ɔwɛ mɔ ɔsĩ awili kutse babi bũũ kawula a.

english_verse: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
translation_verse: Odetɔ́ kamɛ yɛla ndɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kĩ bibuta aye, ade bɛbɔlɛ tɔtɔ mɔ tite kĩ mui sinya a tidi nyɛ tidi nɛ te kadzakɔ̃ a.

english_verse: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
translation_verse: Tite akũ fɔgã, abãã banɔkɔɛ̃, sodzafɔ banɔkɔɛ̃, asikafɔ, otumifɔ, bati flee, basate mɔ bikĩ bidzi be akũ akũ a, bɛla nɛ tite tɛkɛ mɔ tɔtɔ akũ boe tɛkɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
translation_verse: Ade bɛbɔa fɔwɔla bibi tɔtɔ a kĩ, “Midzuli miko nɛ boe akũ, lɔkɔa mikɔla boe nɛ oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo akũ a anɛ, mɔ Kavelibi a kalɔkpa onukpɛ̃.

english_verse: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ be kalɔkpa luwi mɔ libe a wo ɔtɔ. Owei aapuli ɛdza nɛ de onukpɛ̃?”

english_verse: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
translation_verse: ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mitakɛna tite, mpo ĩye futse lɛtsamanɔ kɛnaawo kĩ bɔ́akɔa ɔsũkayi fɔtsa buyi boe Baguma basumunɛ a ɔsũ nɛ be nnyɛ.”

english_verse: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
translation_verse: Ade nnu kĩ bati a kĩ biyi be ɔsũ mɔ Baguma ɔsũkayi a kĩ bito Israelfɔ futete ewua-nviã kamɛ a lidede nyɛ bati mpim ɔha tɛna-ana (

english_verse: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
translation_verse: bɛlɛ kĩ, “Fɔya lɔmɔ! Kamu, mɔ kukũkpalɛ, mɔ nyansã, mɔ kɔlɛkanyɛ, obu, kɔbɛ̃ mɔ otumi nyɛ boe Baguma fude daa daa! Fɔya lɔmɔ!”

english_verse: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
translation_verse: Fukĩĩ liti a, mmɔ́ bati kɔdabu baũ kudi kĩ odima lɛláapuli ɛkã. Bito batidziwa ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ a, kade nyɛ kade, ɔmaĩ nyɛ ɔmaĩ, mɔ tide nyɛ tide flee kamɛ, ade bɛdza bɛtsɛlamɔ lɛgãkpo mɔ Kavelibi a. Be flee bido tɛtadiɛ ovoe wulaa, ade bɛnɛɛ tukuo nɛ awɛ a.

english_verse: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Bɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Nkpã katɛ a to boe Baguma a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo a akũ, mɔ Kavelibi a sɛkɛ̃!”

english_verse: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a flee dza bisinya lɛgãkpo a, mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a, mɔ fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a, ade bɛka bɛtsa anɛ nɛ tite nɛ lɛgãkpo a anɛ bisum Baguma,

english_verse: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
translation_verse: Banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔmɔa odi via yĩ kĩ, “Bawei nyɛ bikĩ bido tɛtadiɛ ovoe wulaa kĩĩ a? Nɛ ndia bito?”

english_verse: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
translation_verse: Nkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Yĩ Ɔlate, ĩnyína. Nɔ nyina.” Ade ebi yĩ kĩ, “Ade be nyɛ bati a kĩ bɛtsã lubo kamɔ kpale kamɛ bɛnɛ sɔũ a. Bɛvɔlɛ be tɛtadiɛ tivoe fitaa nɛ Kavelibi babɔanɛ a kamɛ.

english_verse: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
translation_verse: Ade foesũ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma lɛgãkpo a anɛ basum ye lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa flee nɛ ye Asɔli Kɔla kamɛ a. Oti a kĩ edzi nɛ lɛgãkpo a akũ a, aafɔ̃ lɛtsama lɛláakɛna be nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
translation_verse: Kɔlɛ ĩye ɔmɔado lɛláabuki ɛkɔ be da. Suwa lɛláapila be ĩye ovio libuki ɛkɔ be da.

english_verse: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ Kavelibi a kĩ kideĩ nɛ lɛgãkpo a kayite a nyɛ be ɔkpãnɛ. Yaakpã be ɛnamɛna awã kĩ nkpã mui anɛ kato a, ade Baguma aayɔ babe flee nɛ be anɛ a.”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
translation_verse: Kĩ Kavelibi a wai kɔma kuvũkɔnɔde a, ode kɛna dĩĩ fɔ́ana ndɛ dɔũhuilɛ sɛkɛ.

english_verse: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ kĩ badza nɛ Baguma anɛ a, ade bɛkɔa kugbulɛɛ evũkɔnɔ bido be nɛ awɛ a.

english_verse: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã kĩ ɛnɛɛ sika ngbã ɔnyɔ kate lubui a nɛ́ ɛyadza nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a ɔwɛ̃. Bɛkpa ye tutsonu mɔ ɔlɛ̃ beblebee kĩ ɛkɔa ebumɔ Baguma bati flee mpaɛbɔ, ɛkɔa ɛbɔa afɔliɛ nɛ sika ngbã afɔliɛ atɛnyɛ a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ lɛgãkpo a anɛ a akũ.

english_verse: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
translation_verse: Tutsonu a kĩ bite fi a ɔnyɔ, mɔ Baguma bati mpaɛbɔ a nɛ́ nɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ a awɛ fude fɔnaa Baguma sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a nyɛ fi nɛ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kamɛ eyula ɔnyɔ kate lubui a, ade ɛta foe eko nɛ tite akũ a. Ɔplala kpɛnɛ kɔɔba, odetɔ́ kamɛ mɔanɛ ade tite kuna a. Ode Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ a Vɔ̃ Be Kugbulɛɛ.

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛnɛɛ kugbulɛɛ a manɛ akũ kĩ baavɔ̃ be kugbulɛɛ a.

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔtɔnyade a vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a. Lɛtalɛta lɛdãglɛglɛ mɔ fi kĩ babɔanɛ tsɛka nɛ foe kamɛ ka fɔyako nɛ tite akũ. Tite kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde vio, tite akũ futse foe kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde vio mɔ tuwĩ gɔ̃ flee vio.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔyade a lɛmɛ vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a. Bɛta litsedi kĩ ladã ndɛ ɔtɔ kpale kĩ ɛkatɛ̃ mɔ fi bido nɛ mpo kamɛ, ade mpo a kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde dani babɔanɛ a.

english_verse: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
translation_verse: Fɔtsa a kĩ fudzi nkpã nɛ mpo kamɛ a foe kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde ku, ade tɔkɔlɔ a kĩ titeĩ nɛ mui anɛ a te kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde bu kade a.

english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔlalɛde a vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a, ade lisibii kpale kĩ latɛ̃ ndɛ ɔbia aye to ode lɛyatɔ nɛ tɔwɔ kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde mɔ mui kanɛkɔ̃ kamɛ a.

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: Bavɛ lisibii kĩĩ kĩ tuwĩ mɔ ɔnyɛ, ade mui kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde a dani mɔ ɔnyɛ mɔ ɔnyɛ, ade bati beblebee ku a, kitonɛkĩ mui a dani otsonu kpa.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔnade a lɛmɛ vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a. Deedimɔapɛ bɛbɔa suwa kasɛ̃ kamɛ kalalɛde, odetɔ kasɛ̃ kamɛ kalalɛde mɔ fusibii kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde a. Fukĩĩ fɔ̃ kade kanyina kasɛ̃ kamɛ kalalɛde mɔ lɛnyɛ kasɛ̃ kamɛ kalalɛde tána ɔhaĩ.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ndã fɔkɔ ade nnu ɔkɔliɛ odi kĩ ɛlamɛna kayidza nɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ flee ɛlɛ mɔ odo kɔɔba kĩ, “Bɔbɔɛ, bɔbɔɛ, bɔbɔɛ makpa bati a kĩ bidzi nkpã nɛ tite akũ ntɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ ɛlalɛ a kĩ bite a yavɔ̃ be kugbulɛɛ a.”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ olode a vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a. Mmɔ́ lisibii lidi kĩ lɛka lɛtɔ nɛ tite akũ, ade bɛkɔa lɛkɛ dzununuunu a safi bido nɛ ye awɛ a.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Lisibii a binya lɛkɛ dzununuunu a ade ɔnyɔ tugbluu ndɛ fi luwotu kpale kamɛ ɔnyɔ a nɛ́ eto de kamɛ a. Ɔnyɔ nɛ lɛkɛ dzununuunu a kamɛ fɔ̃ suwa anɛ mɔ odetɔ́ kamɛ ke ɔtũ.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Koolo nɛ́ eto ɔnyɔ a kamɛ ɛyako nɛ tite akũ, ade bɛkpa koolo kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ kakpɔ̃na deĩ a odu.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Bibi be kĩ bɛtabula tuwĩ, futse ĩye tuwĩ blɔblɔ tidima kade, mbom bɛkɛna bati a kĩ Baguma ɔsũkayi lɛláa nɛ be nnyɛ a ablɔ.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Bɛtákpa koolo a osi kĩ bɛkɔ bati kĩĩ, mbom kĩ bɛkɛna be awɔda tidetɔ elo. Ntɛ bɛta oti a, fɔawo ndɛ ayekĩ kakpɔ̃na kata aye.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Nɛ tidetɔ elo a na kamɛ ni, bati aawɛ wuda, lɛmɛ bɛláana ye, baawɛ kĩ baku lɛmɛ wuda aalo be.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Koolo a la ndɛ bapɔnkɔ kĩ bɛmanɛ be bɛkpa kawũkanɔ. Litsedi teĩ nɛ be fɔlɔ ndɛ sika ngbã lɔbɔɛ aye, ade be anɛ la ndɛ otidziwa ade aye a.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Be tɔlɔ la ndɛ batsole tide, ade be adɔ̃ la ndɛ dzata ade a.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa litsedi kĩ ladã ndɛ babĩĩ katɛ sɔbɔtsaɛ bɛtsa nɛ tɔwɔlɛ. Be fupoki kamɛ kakuna atunɔ ndɛ ayekĩ bapɔnkɔ mɔ kawũkanɔ tihilili beblebee wula atsɛ banaa kawũ a.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Bideĩ tɛkã mɔ fubĩĩ ndɛ kakpɔ̃na aye, ade be tɛkã kĩĩ kamɛ kɔbɛ̃ a kĩ baakɔa bɛkɛna bati awɔda a deĩ a.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Bideĩ lɛgã kĩ lanya be akũ. Ade ye nyɛ fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ yadãmɛna lɛkɛ dzununuunu a akũ a. Ye dĩ nɛ Hebri tide kamɛ nyɛ Abadon, ade bavɛ de nɛ Hela tide kamɛ kĩ Apolion.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Lubokamɔ kpale lɛtɔnyade a yatɛ̃, lɛmɛ lubo kamɔ nviã buki fɔaya.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũde a vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a, ade nnu odo odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto sika ngbã afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ Baguma anɛ a atɔ ɛna a kamɛ a.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Odo a bi Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũde a kĩ ɛnɛɛ kagbulɛɛ a kĩ, “Wũnya Baguma ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ ɛna a kĩ bisuli bɛdzɛla nɛ Frat ɔwɔ kpale abuĩ a!”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Ade biwũnya Baguma ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ ɛna a, kĩ bɛmanɛ biyi badzɔlɛ ɔdɔũ a, mɔ luwi a, mɔ odetɔ a, mɔ kateli a kĩ bɛkɔ batidziwa be kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde a.

english_verse: By listening, I could tell there were more than
translation_verse: Bibi yĩ kĩ bapɔnkɔ badzinɛ a lidede nyɛ mpim mpim ɔha nviã.

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Ade ayekĩ mmɔ́ bapɔnkɔ mɔ be badzinɛ a nɛ kanyɛkatsa kamɛ a nɛ awĩ a. Bɛbla ɔwɔlɛ babĩĩ katɛ fɔbɔtsaɛ kĩ fɔadã gbãã ndɛ fi katɛ̃ aye, fubuki fɔadã ndɛ safir de aye, fɔyɔɔlɛ lɛmɛ ndɛ sulfa de aye. Bapɔnkɔ a fɔlɔ la ndɛ dzata fɔlɔ aye, ade fi, ɔnyɔ mɔ fukpokpo gɛĩgɛĩ nɛ́ futo be tunukpɛ̃ kamɛ a.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔa lubo kamɔ fɔtsa ɛlalɛ kĩ fɔnyɛ fi, ɔnyɔ, mɔ fukpokpo gɛĩgɛĩ kĩ futo bapɔnkɔ a tunukpɛ̃ a bɛkɔ batidziwa kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: Kitonɛkĩ bapɔnkɔ a kɔbɛ̃ deĩ nɛ be tunukpɛ̃ mɔ tɛkã kamɛ. Be tɛkã a la ndɛ bɔɛ aye, kĩ tideĩ fɔlɔ. Ade te bakɔa kakɛna bati ablɔ a.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Bati a kĩ bite bidzi nkpã a flee, kĩ bɛtákɔ be nɛ lubo kamɔ kĩĩ kamɛ a, támuniki nɛ fɔtsa a kĩ be mɔawɛ bɛkɔa awɛ bɛmɛ a kasum liti. Bɛtáfɔ̃ hũhũ kpa, baviɔ̃ kĩ bɛkɛna mɔ sika ngbã, sika ovoe, kɔɔble, de, ĩye otse, kĩ fɔámɔ, fɔánu ĩye fɔátsã a kasum!

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Bɛtádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ nɛ bati kakɔ, kanɔkɔnɛ kadã, linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ, mɔ kaye kamɛ.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ mɔ kɔbɛ̃ bɛbã odi kĩ eto ode yaka. Edeĩ nɛ luflututu kamɛ, ade sinigbɛnɛ sinya ye lɔlɔ a. Ye anɛ ata ndɛ suwa aye, ade ye abɔa la ndɛ fi fɔmɛ fuku aye kĩ fɔade nɛ ode a.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Ɛnɛɛ kavolibi kadi kĩ bibinya a nɛ awɛ. Ɛkɔa ye sɔmɔna kɔbɔa eke nɛ mpo akũ ade ɛkɔa sɛba kɔbɔa lɛmɛ eke nɛ tite akũ a.

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: Ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ndɛ dzata amɛ kaklumɛ aye. Kĩ ɛbɔa fɔwɔla a, ɔplala evũkɔnɔ a nyɛ tunukpɛ̃.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Kĩ ɔplala evũkɔnɔ a nyɛ tunukpɛ̃ a, nwɛ kĩ matsɛlɛ fɔtsa ĩyi, lɛmɛ nnu odo odi kĩ eto ode ɛlɛ kĩ, “Talɛ lɛtsa a kĩ ɔplala evũkɔnɔ a lɛ a onukpɛ̃, tatsɛlɛ foe oyi.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ mmɔ́ kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ mpo mɔ tite akũ a dzɛla ye sɔmɔna awɛ nɛ ode,

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: ɛlɔ fubo nɛ Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã daa a, kĩ ɛkɛna tite mɔ te akũ fɔtsa, ode mɔ ye kamɛ fɔtsa, mpo mɔ ye kamɛ fɔtsa a dĩ akũ kĩ, “Liti kakpa lɛláabuki lɛna!

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũkɔnɔde a aavɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ ni, Baguma nhihiɛ kɔlawa a aaya nɛ foe kamɛ ndɛ ayekĩ ɛnyɛ ɛtsa ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kĩ bɛnyɛ basumunɛ a.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, odo a kĩ nnu ntomɛna ode a buki ebi yĩ kĩ, “Naatɛ kavolibi a kĩ bibinya a nɛ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ mui mɔ tite akũ a awɛ.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Nnaa ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a sɛkɛ̃ mbi ye kĩ ɛkɔa kavolibi a ɛkpa yĩ. Ebi yĩ kĩ, “Tɛ ke kaba ke. Fɔ́akɛna mɔ ɔnyɛ mɔ ɔnyɛ nɛ nɔ lupu kamɛ, lɛmɛ fɔ́ana ɔmɛnɛ nɛ nɔ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ ndɛ kɔsã aye.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Ntɛ kavolibi a nɛ ye awɛ mba ke. Fɔna ɔmɛnɛ ndɛ kɔsã aye nɛ yĩ onukpɛ̃ kamɛ, lɛmɛ kĩ mmɛna foe a, fɔna ɔnyɛ nɛ yĩ lupu kamɛ.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ebi yĩ kĩ, “Kɛnɛ kĩ abuki ode lɛtsã Baguma fɔlɛtsa a nɛ bati, tɛmaĩ, tide mɔ fɔgã pii akũ.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ takɔna ablanda nɛ Asɔli Kɔla akũ a, kitonɛkĩ bɛkɔa awã bɛkpa amamufɔ. Ade be aasumini Ɔmatɔ Kpalɛwa a nɛ tidetɔ tɛna-nviã kamɛ a.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Adansifɔ nviã kĩĩ nyɛ kugua futse nviã mɔ ɔsɛdza titse nviã a kĩ tɛdzɛ̃ nɛ tite Ɔlate a anɛ kamɛ a.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Ntɛ odi wɛ kĩ yakɛna foe ablɔ ni, fi anɛ kato foe tunukpɛ̃ kamɛ kawɔla be mɔ bakesĩnɛ a. Ntɛ odi wɛ kĩ yakɛna be ablɔ ni, baakɔ ye.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ basua ode osi kĩ oni ɛtako nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ bade lɛtsã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa akũ a kamɛ. Bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ lɛmɛ kĩ badani mui bɛkɛna babɔanɛ, ade bideĩ kɔbɛ̃ kĩ bayamɛna lubo kamɔ lidi nyɛ lidi nɛ tite akũ nɛ lɔkɔ nyɛ lɔkɔ kĩ bawɛ.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Ntɛ bide lɛtsã nɛ Baguma fɔlɛtsa a akũ biyua a, ɔyaɛ kpa a kĩ eto lɛkɛ dzununuunu kamɛ ɛnɛ a, aanɔ kawũ mɔ be. Yaanya be akũ, ɛkɔ be.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Be banɔkpa aawa nɛ ɔmatɔ kpale a ɔblɔnu akũ nɛ awã kĩ bɛbɔa be Ɔlate nɛ asendua akũ a. Ɔmatɔ a dĩ kɔlawa nyɛ Sodom ĩye Egipte.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Bati aato ɔmaĩ nyɛ ɔmaĩ, kade nyɛ kade, tide nyɛ tide mɔ katinya kamɛ bati flee kamɛ bɛyaadã banɔkpa a nɛ fuwi ɛlalɛ mɔ sɛkɛ kamɛ, ade bɛláakpa osi kĩ bibila be a.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Tite akũ bati flee aamɔ kawɔlɛ nɛ bati nviã kĩĩ wuda akũ. Baanya luwi bɛmɛna akũ futelitsa kitonɛkĩ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ nviã kĩĩ vɛlɛ lubo kamɔ baũ bɛma batidziwa.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Fuwi ɛlalɛ mɔ sɛkɛ a liti a, nkpã kawɛya to Baguma sɛkɛ̃ kɛyawo nɛ be kamɛ, ade biyidza bɛdza a. Libe paa wo bati a kĩ bɛmɔ́ be a flee.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Adeke Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ nviã a nu odo mɔ ɔsĩ odi kĩ eto ode ebi be kĩ, “Mide miya nɛ awĩ!” Kĩ be bakesĩnɛ a dza bɛkadã a, bide bɛnaa ode nɛ luflututu kamɛ.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ a, tite kuna mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ, ɔmatɔ kpale a ye kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kawuade a bubuli bubloo, ade bati mpim evũkɔnɔ ku a. Libe wo bati nkaɛ a, ade bimu ode Baguma a.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Lubokamɔ mɔ libe lɛyade a yatɛ̃, lɛmɛ fɔlalɛde mɔ libe a aaya finyaa. Bɛvɔ̃ Kagbulɛɛ Kavũkɔnɔde a.

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũkɔnɔde a vɔ̃ ye kagbulɛɛ a, ade tido kɔɔba nɛ́ nɛ ode tɛlɛ kĩ, “Katinya kamɛ lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a dani boe Ɔlate mɔ ye Kristo a fude, ade yaanya akũ daa daa a!”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: bɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate otumifɔ, kĩ edeĩ, kĩ ɛna anɛ kɔkɔɛ. Bɔanyɛ nɔ kɔlɛ kĩ ɔtɛ nɔ otumi kpale a, oyi lɛgãkanya a kayɔ!

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Amamufɔ na kalɔkpa, kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ a wo ɔtɔ kĩ anyɛ nɔ kalɔkpa ɔtsa. Lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ adzɛmɔ banɔkpa. Lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ ase nɔ basumunɛ onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ a kɔtɔ, mɔ nɔ bati kpalɛwa a kĩ bakpa nɔ obu, mɔ bati a kĩ bavila nɔ dĩ a, banɔkɔɛ̃ mɔ babisɛ̃. Lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ, kĩ awɔla bati a kĩ babula tite akũ kade a!”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, bibinya Baguma Asɔli Kɔla nɛ ode a biyi, ade bɛmɔ́ ye apam adakã a nɛ koe kamɛ a. Deedimɔapɛ, odetɔ́ kamɛ mɔanɛ, tido nɛ́, ɔplala kpɛnɛ, tite kuna ade oni ko mɔ fɔdãglɛglɛ baũbaũ a.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Ɛtsɛ̃mɔ kɔma. Ade kĩ sɛmɛ pi ye sũ a, ɛkatsimtsim akũ mɔ fɔwɔla kɔɔba nɛ foe kawo sũ.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Nsɛntsɛlɛni bɛbã nɛ́ nɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ. Dã, fɔnyɛ kɔɛ kpale ngbã, kĩ kudeĩ fɔlɔ evũkɔnɔ, mɔ tɛlɛ ewua, ade kɔtsa fɔgãbɔɛ evũkɔnɔ nɛ fɔlɔ a akũ a.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Kɔkɔa koe ɔkã kɔlɔ fusibii kasɛ̃ kamɛ katsu kalalɛde a kutomɛna odetɔ́ kamɛ kɔta kuwula nɛ tite akũ. Finyaa kɔɛ kpale a yidza kɔdza nɛ ɔmatsɔ̃ a kĩ sɛmɛ pi ye a anɛ kamɛ, kĩ ntɛ esila ɛma pɛ kɔ́abɔɛ obisɛ̃ a kɔmɛna.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Otsole a má obinyole kĩ yaakɔa sɔbɔtsaɛ otse ɛnya fude fude bati flee akũ. Lɛmɛ bɛbɔɛ kabisɛ̃ɛ̃ a lɛtalɛta bɛnamɛna Baguma mɔ ye lɛgãkpo a sɛkɛ̃.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Otsole a lo ɛnaa kɔsa kamɛ, awã kĩ Baguma manɛ eyi ɛkpa ye kĩ bɛdã ye akũ fuwi apim mɔa, ɔha nviã mɔ aduesia.

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, kawũ nɛ́ nɛ ode. Mikael mɔ ye ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ nɔ kawũ mɔ kɔɛ kpale a, ade kɔɛ a mɔ koe fɔtɔ banyanɛ a nɔ bɛtsɛlamɔ be a.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛnya kɔɛ kpale a akũ, ade bɛtákpa koe mɔ koe basumunɛ a osi kĩ bibuki bidzi nɛ ode a.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Bɛta kɔɛ kpale a bimuni nɛ tite akũ. Ade koe nyɛ anɛ kɔkɔɛ kɔɛ a kĩ bavɛ kĩ Abonsam ĩye Satana, kĩ kɔkɛ katinya kamɛ bati flee a. Koe mɔ koe fɔtɔ banyanɛ a flee bɛta bimuni nɛ tite akũ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Ke liti a, nnu odo kpaa odi nɛ ode ɛlɛ kĩ, “Finyaakɔ̃ Baguma oti katɛ ya! Baguma nyɛ ye kɔbɛ̃ ɛtsa ndɛ Lɛgã aye! Finyaa ye Kristo a nyɛ ye otumi kanya! Kitonɛkĩ ɛta oti a kĩ ɛdza nɛ Baguma anɛ ɛkatsua boe bawaɛ̃ onukpɛ̃ kakpa Baguma lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa a emuni.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Ade be lɛtsã Kavelibi a babɔanɛ, mɔ ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa a kĩ bidi adansiɛ nɛ ye akũ a bɛnya ye akũ. Bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔa be nkpã bɛkpa faa ntɛ fɔ́anya wuda malo.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ ni, kawɔlɛ kide mi ode, mɔ mi a kĩ mideĩ nɛ awã a flee! Lɛmɛ tite mɔ mpo, bɔbɔɛ makpa mi, kitonɛkĩ Abonsam duma ɛya nɛ mi kamɛ mɔ kalɔkpa, ade enyina kĩ fuwi sukuloo lite ye.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Kĩ kɔɛ kpale a mɔ́ kĩ biɛta koe bimuni nɛ tite akũ a, kutomɔ otsole a kĩ ɛma obinyole a liti.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ bɛkpa otsole a ɔkɔliɛ kpale fupoki nviã a eyidza ɛnaa awã kĩ Baguma manɛ eyi ɛkpa ye nɛ kɔsa kamɛ a. Baadãmɛna ye akũ nɛ awã futeli ɛlalɛ mɔ sɛkɛ, lɔkɔa ɛwɛya nɛ kɔɛ kpale a awɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Ade kɔɛ kpale a vũma mui kĩ bitini ndɛ ɔwɔ kutomɛna koe onukpɛ̃, bitomɔ otsole a kĩ bɛbɔɛ ye bɛnamɛna.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Lɛmɛ tite bumɔ otsole a. Tite binya onukpɛ̃ tɛmɔa mui a kĩ kɔɛ kpale a vũma etomɛna otsole a flee.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Kɔɛ kpale a na kalɔkpa mɔ ɔsĩ nɛ otsole a akũ, ade kɔdzakũ kĩ kɔnaanɔ kawũ mɔ ye babi mɔ bawa nkaɛ a. Ade be nyɛ bati a kĩ bakɛna nɛ Baguma mbla a akũ, kĩ bipini ɔnɔkɔali fɔlɛtsa a kĩ Yesu nyɛ ɛtsa lɛmɛ a.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Ade kɔɛ kpale a nɛ́ kɔnadza nɛ mpo abuĩ a.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ ɔyaɛ kpa odi kĩ ɛnɛ yato mpo kamɛ. Edeĩ fɔlɔ evũkɔnɔ mɔ tɛlɛ ewua, ade lɛgãbɔɛ tsanɛ nɛ ɔlɛ odi nyɛ odi akũ a. Bɛtsɛlɛ mbusuo dĩ kĩ lasupa Baguma bite nɛ lɔlɔ lidi nyɛ lidi akũ.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Ɔyaɛ kpa a la ndɛ dzakpa aye. Ye abɔa la ndɛ kalikii abɔa, ade ye onukpɛ̃ lɛmɛ la ndɛ dzata onukpɛ̃ a. Kɔɛ kpale a kɔa ye mɔawɛ kɔbɛ̃, ye lɛgãkpo mɔ ye lɛgãkanya otumi kpale a ɛkpa ɔyaɛ kpa kĩĩ.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Fɔdã ndɛ ɔyaɛ kpa a lɔlɔ mɔa tɛ wuda kaakpa nɛ de akũ, lɛmɛ kaakpa a ku. Fɔkɛna katinya kamɛ bati flee wãwã, ade bitomɔ ye a.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Bati flee sum kɔɛ kpale a, kitonɛkĩ ɛkɔa ye kɔbɛ̃ ɛkpa ɔyaɛ kpa a. Bisum ɔyaɛ kpa a lɛmɛ bɛlɛ kĩ, “Owei la ndɛ ɔyaɛ kpa kĩĩ? Owei aapuli ɛnɔ kawũ mɔ ye?”

english_verse: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ɔyaɛ kpa a osi kĩ ɛlɛ kukũpia mɔ fubusuotsa pii ɛnya akũ tidetɔ tɛna-nviã.

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Eyi kayɔ mɔ Baguma, mɔ ye dĩ, mɔ awã kĩ edzi, mɔ bati flee a kĩ bideĩ nɛ ode a kadumu.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ye osi kĩ ɛnɔ kawũ mɔ Baguma bade a ɛnya be akũ, ade bɛkpa ye otumi lɛmɛ nɛ fude nyɛ fude, ɔmaĩ nyɛ ɔmaĩ, tide nyɛ tide mɔ tite bati flee akũ a.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Tite akũ bati flee aasum ye, mbɔɛntɛ bikĩ be buĩ bɛtsɛlɛ nɛ Kavelibi a kĩ bɛkɔ a nkpã ovoli kamɛ, kito anɛ kĩ bɛbɔa katinya a.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Foesũ oti a kĩ edeĩ atɔ ni, etse!

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Odi nyɛ odi kĩ bihihiɛ biyi kĩ bipi ye ndom ni, baapi ye ndom, ade odi nyɛ odi kĩ bihihiɛ biyi kĩ yaatɔ nɛ kɔpã onukpɛ̃ ni, yaatɔ nɛ kɔpã onukpɛ̃ kokooko. Ade fukĩĩ kamɛ mɔ kɛnɛ kĩ Baguma bade a bɛna ɔwɔlɛ kado mɔ katɛkado a.

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ ɔyaɛ kpa bɛbã kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto tite kamɛ. Edeĩ tɛlɛ nviã ndɛ kavelibi aye, ade yalɛ kɔdzɛla ndɛ kɔɛ kpale a.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛna kɔbɛ̃ kpale a kĩ ɛtɛ etomɛna ɔyaɛ kpa ɔtɔnyade a sɛkɛ̃ a akũ adzuma nɛ ye anɛ kamɛ. Ɛhɛ tite mɔ te akũ bati flee kĩ bisum ɔyaɛ kpa ɔtɔnyade a kĩ ye kaakpa ku a.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Ɔyaɛ kpa ɔyade kĩĩ kɛna awãwã fɔtsa pii. Ɛfɔ̃ fi malo nɛ futo ode fɔyatɔ nɛ tite akũ nɛ bati anɛ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛ tite akũ bati flee mɔ awãwã fɔtsa a kĩ bɛkpa ye osi kĩ ɛkɛna nɛ ɔyaɛ kpa ɔtɔnyade a anɛ sũ. Ɔyaɛ kpa a bi be kĩ bɛmɛ ɔyaɛ kpa a kĩ bipila mɔ kɔpã a lɛmɛ ebuki edzi nkpã a kutidu bɛdzɛla.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Bɛkpa ɔyaɛ kpa ɔyade a osi kĩ ɛwɛya nkpã edo nɛ ɔyaɛ kpa ɔtɔnyade kutidu a kĩ bɛmɛ a kamɛ, ayekĩ yaapuli ɛlɛ kɔdzɛla, ɛfɔ̃ bɛkɔ bati a kĩ baasĩ kĩ bɛláasum ye a.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Ɔyaɛ kpa kĩĩ hɛ bati flee, babisɛ̃ mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃, asikafɔ mɔ ahiãfɔ, basate mɔ akũ akũ badzinɛ, kĩ bite sɔkpɔ̃na nɛ be sɔmɔna kɔkpɔ ĩye nɛ be nnyɛ.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Odima lɛláapuli ɛya fɔtsa ĩye ɛnɔ lɛtsama ntɛ sɔkpɔ̃na kĩĩ liteĩ nɛ ye akũ. Sɔkpɔ̃na kĩĩ nyɛ ɔyaɛ kpa a dĩ, ĩye kakã katsɛlɛ kĩ fɔdzɛ̃ fɔkpa dĩ a.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Fɔlɛtsa kĩĩ akũ kakɔna sĩ nyansã. Odi nyɛ odi kĩ enyina kadi aapuli ebu ɔyaɛ a dĩ kakã katsɛlɛ a akũ akunta, enu foe kayɔ, kitonɛkĩ kakã katsɛlɛ a dza fɔkpa oti dĩ. Ye kakã katsɛlɛ a nyɛ ɔha evũ mɔ taavũ-evũ (

english_verse: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ndã fɔkɔ ade mmɔ́ Kavelibi a kĩ ɛdzɛ̃ nɛ Zion ɔtɔ a akũ a. Bati mpim ɔha tɛna-ana (

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Ade nnu odo odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto ode kĩ ɛla ndɛ ayekĩ mui ato ɔtɔ akũ kaka kako, ĩye ndɛ ayekĩ ɔplala kakpɛnɛ aye. Odo a buki ɛla ndɛ tɔnɔ kĩ babɔa nɛ tɛsankuo akũ.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Bati mpim ɔha tɛna-ana a dza bɛkasã ɔnɔ vɔɛ̃ nɛ lɛgãkpo a, mɔ fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a, mɔ banɔkɔɛ̃ a anɛ. Otidziwa odima lɛláapuli ɛkɔlɛ ɔnɔ a na, mbɔɛntɛ bati mpim ɔha tɛna-ana a kĩ be odi lɔlɔ kɔtɔ bise nɛ tite akũ a.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Ade be nyɛ bati a kĩ bɛkpalɛ be akũ, kĩ binyína batsole, bɛnyɛ badedzaa. Ade be atomɔ Kavelibi a kanaa kakɔ nyɛ kakɔ kĩ yanaa a. Ade be lɛmɛ nyɛ bati a kĩ bise be lɔlɔ kɔtɔ nɛ batidziwa kasinu a kamɛ, bɛkɛna be bati batɔnyade lɛmɛ bɛkpa Baguma mɔ Kavelibi a.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Bɛtámɔ tɔ̃ kĩ bɛma fɔvã, ade nfumusu adima lɛmɛ lɛláa be akũ a. Ode Fɔtɔ Banyanɛ Ɛlalɛ.

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã kĩ yayidza nɛ tite mɔ ode kayite. Ɛnɛɛ Kalɛ wĩ a kĩ kɛlá kawũna a, kĩ yaade lɛtsã nɛ ke akũ ɛkpa tite akũ bati, batidziwa ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ, fude nyɛ fude, tide nyɛ tide mɔ ɔmaĩ nyɛ ɔmaĩ.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ɛlɛ kĩ, “Mivila Baguma, mikpa ye obu, kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ kĩ yadzɛmɔ batidziwa. Misum ye a kĩ ɛkbɔa tite mɔ ode, mpo mɔ mui kĩ banɛ nɛ tite kamɛ a!”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔyade tomɔ ɔtɔnyade a ɛkalɛ kĩ, “Ɛtɔ o-o! Babilonia a kĩ ɛtɛ dĩ a tɔ tsoo! Ɛfɔ̃ fude fude flee mɔa ye waĩ mɔ ɔsĩ a kĩ fɔnyɛ linyolewɛ mɔ litsolewɛ a!”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔlalɛde a tomɔ be nviã a ɛkabɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba kĩ, “Ntɛ odi sum ɔyaɛ kpa mɔ ye kutidu a kĩ bɛmɛ a, mɔ ɛtɛ ye sɔkpɔ̃na nɛ ye nnyɛ ĩye kɔkpɔ ni,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: yaamɔa Baguma kawũnaba waĩ a kĩ ɛkɔa eko nɛ ye kalɔkpa kɔɔpu kamɛ a. Bati flee a kĩ bɛkɛna fukĩĩ odu a, Baguma aamɔna be lubo nɛ fi mɔ fukpokpo gɛĩgɛĩ kamɛ nɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ kpalɛwa a mɔ Kavelibi a anɛ kamɛ.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Be lubo kamɔ fi ɔnyɔ a aakade nɛ ode daa daa. Bati a kĩ basum ɔyaɛ kpa a mɔ ye kutidu a kĩ bɛmɛ a, mɔ bikĩ ye dĩ sɔkpɔ̃na a teĩ nɛ be akũ a, lɛláana kawɛya lɛnyɛ mɔ suwa da.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Ade awĩ mɔ Baguma bade a kĩ bakɛna nɛ ye mbla a akũ, bɛtɛ Yesu bido lɛmɛ a, sĩ ɔwɔlɛ kado a.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Ke liti a, nnu odo odi kĩ eto ode ɛkalɛ kĩ, “Tsɛlɛ kayi kĩ, Kito finyaa kanaa ni, Baguma hila bati a kĩ biku nɛ Kristo katɛkado kamɛ a!” Hũhũ a tɛ etunɔ kĩ, “Nuwĩ, Baguma hila be! Baawɛya bitomɛna be lubo kamɔ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ be tudzuma kakɛna tomɔ be!” Tite Akũ Bati Kakpɔta.

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ndã fɔkɔ ade mmɔ́ luflututu fitaa lidi, kĩ litsedi ndɛ otidziwa aye dzi nɛ de akũ. Ɛtsa sika ngbã lɔbɔɛ, ɛnɛɛ lɛsɛga mɔ ɔbɔ̃ nɛ awɛ.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Ade Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã nɛ́ eto Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ, ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ebi oti a kĩ edzi nɛ luflututu akũ a kĩ, “Kɔa nɔ lɛsɛga a kanɛna fudutsa, kitonɛkĩ lɔkɔ wo ɔtɔ, tite akũ fudutsa bo fɔkpa kakpɔta!”

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Ade ke oti a kĩ edzi nɛ luflututu akũ a wũnya ye lɛsɛga nɛ tite akũ ɛkpɔta fudutsa a flee a.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto ode Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Ye malo ɛnɛɛ lɛsɛga mɔ ɔbɔ̃ lidi.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ bɛbã odi kĩ edeĩ otumi nɛ fi akũ a nɛ́ eto afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kamɛ a. Ɛbɔa fɔwɔla kɔɔba ebi ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ ɛnɛɛ lɛsɛga mɔ ɔbɔ̃ a kĩ, “Kɔa nɔ lɛsɛga a kakpalɛ waĩ babi a nɛ tite akũ kɔbã a kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ waĩ babi a yɔɔlɛ!”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Foesũ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a wũnya ye lɛsɛga a nɛ tite akũ ade ɛna waĩ babi a nɛ tɔwɛ̃ akũ a. Ɛta be eko nɛ waĩ kakã lɛkɛ kpale, kĩ lɛnyɛ Baguma kalɔkpa a kamɛ.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Binuna waĩ babi a mɔ fɔnɛta nɛ ɔmatɔ a ɔbaɛ̃, ade babɔanɛ tini bito waĩ kakã lɛkɛ a kamɛ a, bɛtɛ̃ ndɛ ɔwɔ a. Babɔanɛ a kadzũ kamɛ aana ndɛ fɔnɛta elo ĩye ɔpɔnkɔ akũ kadzɛla, ade bitini bɛnaa ndɛ fusikũ ɔha nviã a.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ awãwã fɔtsa kpale bɛbã kĩ fɔanyɛnɛ akũ paa nɛ odetɔ́ kamɛ. Ade foe nyɛ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ kĩ bɛnɛɛ lubokamɔ evũkɔnɔ a. Lubokamɔ kĩĩ nyɛ nkaɛ flee a kĩ foe aanyɛ Baguma kalɔkpa nkaɛ a fɔtsa.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Ade mmɔ́ litsedi ndɛ mpo kĩ ladã ndɛ ahuhɔɛ kĩ fi tsɛka mɔ ye. Mmɔ́ bati a kĩ bɛnya ɔyaɛ kpa, mɔ ye kutidu a kĩ bɛmɛ́, mɔ ye kakã katsɛlɛ dĩ akũ a, kĩ bɛdzɛ̃ nɛ ahuhɔɛ mpo a abuĩ, bɛnɛɛ tɛsankuo kĩ Baguma kpa be.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Bɛkasã Baguma ɔsumunɛ Mose mɔ Kavelibi a ɔnɔ kĩ, “O boe Ɔlate Baguma Kpale, nɔ tudzuma pɔnɛ, ade takɛna wãwã a! Nɔ fude fude flee Lɛgã, Nɔ tisi adã kpĩĩ, ade tɛnyɛ ɔnɔkɔali a!

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: O boe Ɔlate, owei koũ lɛláavila nɔ? Owei aasĩ kĩ ɛláado nɔ dĩ obu? Kitonɛkĩ nɔ odi nyɛ oti kpalɛwa, fude fude flee a aayasum nɔ, kitonɛkĩ bɛnyɛ nɔ fɔtsa kakɛna kpĩĩ a bɛtsa bati flee.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ndã fɔkɔ ade mmɔ́ kĩ bibinya Asɔli Kɔla nɛ ode a kĩ Lɛpɛ Kpalɛwa a deĩ nɛ koe kamɛ a biyi.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ bɛnɛɛ lubo kamɔ evũkɔnɔ a nɛ́ bito Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ. Bido aklala fitaa kĩ yata muamua tɛtadiɛ, ade bɛkɔa sika ngbã abɔsɔ bɛbɔa nɛ tɔwɔlɛ a.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Ade fɔtsa ɛna a kĩ fudzi nkpã a kamɛ fɔmɔa kɔa sika ngbã tɔkɔɔpu evũkɔnɔ kĩ tiyi tsɔnɔnɔɔnɔ mɔ Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã daa a kalɔkpa, ɛkpa ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ a.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Ade ɔnyɔ a kĩ eto Baguma kukũkpalɛ mɔ ye kɔbɛ̃ kamɛ a yi Asɔli Kɔla a flee kamɛ a. Odima tápuli ewo nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ kɛnawo kĩ lubo kamɔ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ a yamɛna a onukpɛ̃ tsua.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Ade nnu odo kɔɔba odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ ɛkabi ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ evũkɔnɔ a kĩ, “Minaatsokui Baguma kalɔkpa nɛ tɔkɔɔpu kamɛ a miko nɛ tite akũ!”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Adeke Baguma ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔtɔnyade a natsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ tite akũ a. Bati a kĩ bite be ɔyaɛ kpa sɔkpɔ̃na a, mɔ be a kĩ bisum ye kutidu mɛwa a nɛ́ fɔɔkpa kĩ fɔawo, kĩ fɔakũ lɛmɛ nɛ akũ.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔyade a naa ɛnatsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ mpo kamɛ a. Mui a dani ndɛ ɔnɔkpa babɔanɛ aye, ade lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ lidzi nkpã nɛ mpo kamɛ a ku a.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔlalɛde a lɛmɛ tsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ tɔwɔ mɔ mui kanɛkɔ̃ kamɛ, ade mui a dani babɔanɛ a.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Nnu kĩ ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ a kĩ yadãmɛna mui akũ a lɛ kĩ, “O Oti Kpalɛwa, nɔ kĩ odeĩ daa, kĩ ɔna anɛ kɔkɔɛ a, akɔ ɔtɔ nɛ osi kpĩĩ akũ!

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Bati kĩĩ ko Baguma bade a mɔ ye onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ babɔanɛ nɛ tite, nɛ foesũ ɔkpa be babɔanɛ kĩ bɛmɔa. Nuwĩ, sɔtɔ kavɛlɛ kĩĩ fɛta be!”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Ade nnu odo odi kĩ ɛnɛ́ eto afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kamɛ kĩ, “O boe Ɔlate Baguma, nɔ otumifɔ adi ɔnɔkɔali, ade akɔ kɔtɔ nɛ osi kpĩĩ akũ a.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ɔnade a tsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ suwa akũ, ade bɛkpa se osi kĩ sipila bati mɔ se fi a.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Fi a otutuo mɔ ɔsĩ a pila bati ade bɛlɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Baguma a kĩ ye deĩ otumi nɛ lubo kamɔ kĩĩ akũ a, lɛmɛ bisĩ kĩ baádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ bimu ye.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ olode a tsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ ɔyaɛ kpa a lɛgãkpo akũ. Adeke ɔtũ ke nɛ ɔyaɛ kpa a lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a, ade bati dɔ̃ fɔmɛ nɛ ayekĩ fɔawo be a sũ.

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: Nɛ foesũ bɛlɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Baguma kĩ edeĩ nɛ ode a akũ. Titɔ kĩ fɔɔkpa a kĩ bɛnɛ́ a awo be malo ni, bisĩ kĩ baádani tɔwɔlɛ kamɛ nɛ bɛ fɔtsa kpa kakɛna kamɛ.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Ade ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũde a tsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ Frat ɔwɔ kpale a kamɛ a. Ɔwɔ a viɔ̃ ade ɛnyɛ osi ɛkpa fɔgã a kĩ futo suwa kadekɔ̃ a.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, mmɔ́ hũhũ fĩĩ ɛlalɛ kĩ bɛla ndɛ fɔpɔtrɔlɔ, bɛnɛ bito kɔɛ kpale a onukpɛ̃, mɔ ɔyaɛ kpa a onukpɛ̃ mɔ fɔvã onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ a onukpɛ̃ kamɛ.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Be nyɛ hũhũ kpa a hũhũ kĩ yakɛna awãwã fɔtsa a. Hũhũ ɛlalɛ kĩĩ anɛ kanaa katinya kamɛ fɔgã flee sɛkɛ̃, kakpɔta be kakpa kawũkanɔ nɛ Baguma Otumifɔ Flee Luwi kpale a akũ.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: “Mitse! Maya ndɛ oyekpanɛ aye! Baguma hila oti a kĩ esi lipe yadãmɛna ye tɛtadiɛ akũ, ayekĩ ɛláatsã ɔdzɔ, kɔnyɔã liwo ye nɛ kanade!”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Ade hũhũ kpa a kpɔta fɔgã a nɛ awã kĩ bavɛ nɛ Hebri tide kamɛ kĩ Armagedon a.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ ɔnyanɛ ovũkɔnɔde a natsokui ye kɔɔpu kamɛ fɔtsa a eko nɛ ɔwɛ kamɛ. Ade odo kɔɔba odi nɛ́ eto lɛgãkpo a nɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kamɛ kĩ, “Fuwoli!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Ɔmatɔ kpale a bɛ̃ nɛ fɔkɛ ɛlalɛ kamɛ, ade fude flee tɛmatɔ tɛkpale tɛkpale bubuli a. Baguma te anɛ nɛ Babilonia kĩ ɛtɛ dĩ a akũ, ade ɛfɔ̃ ɛmɔa ye kalɔkpa waĩ mɔ ɔsĩ a nɛ ye kɔɔpu a kamɛ a.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Ke liti a, ode fɔtɔ banyanɛ a kĩ bɛlɛ tɔkɔɔpu evũkɔnɔ a kamɛ ɔmɔa ya nɛ yĩ sɛkɛ̃ ebi yĩ kĩ, “Ya, ntsa nɔ ayekĩ bɛvɛlɛ adɔpaani a kĩ ɛtɛ dĩ, kĩ edzi nɛ tɔwɔ pii akũ a sɔtɔ.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Tite akũ fɔgã bɔa adɔpaa mɔ ye, ade ye adɔpaa kabɔa waĩ a wugɛla tite akũ bati a.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
